12/04/24, 07:53 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘ He sets aside the first to establish the second’ (Hebrews 10:9)

What was this first that was set aside by the Lord Jesus? These were all the rites, rituals of burnt offerings, sin offerings, and sacrifices that were prescribed by the Laws of Moses. These were to be ardently followed to be justified by the Law. The Merciful Father (God) willed that mankind be purged from their sins, and be saved without these burdensome offerings made frequently because these could not suffice for the transgressions and cleanse them from their sins. Here is where the second was established by Jesus Christ, through his sacrifice on the Cross. By that only offering of Himself in Eternity for all mankind, the will of God for the Salvation of all men was fulfilled. The second which reinstated us to our former glory was the blood of Jesus (the innocent Lamb of God). For all that we have been bequeathed in Jesus, how could we ever be thankful to the Father? By doing His will in our lives, as Jesus did. The will of God for us is this – as verse 22 of the same chapter explains to us – to draw near to God with a sincere heart, filled with assurance of faith, with a clear conscience and in the purity of the body. And our unwavering faith would support this gesture in us, for we are reminded that the One who has called us is ‘faithful’ to all that He has promised us. Let us therefore honor God by living that sacrifice of praise, let us pray for a heart that is obliging to God, that is thankful and filled with the assurance of His promises for us. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

13/04/24, 09:10 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘What no eye has seen, what no ear has heard, and what no human mind has conceived, the things God has prepared for those who love Him’ (1 Corinthians 2:9)

It is the Holy Spirit given to us in Baptism that enlightens our Spirit to understand Who God is and What He does for us every moment in life. Each one of us is unique and endearing in the eyes of God. And therefore, whenever we ask the Father in Jesus’ name, according to His Will, our wishes would surely be granted. The Holy Spirit reminds us to delight in the Lord, in His presence, in His Mighty name (Psalm 37:4) that our intentions and prayers may be answered; to worthily approach His Throne of Mercy for grace (Hebrews 4:16) in times of need. This understanding is what the Bible terms as ‘Wisdom’. Wisdom is a Divine gift bestowed upon all who ask of it to the Father, trusting in God’s greatness and admitting of one’s unworthiness. King Solomon once asked God for this gift; he not only was given wisdom but was also complemented with all needed gifts for a content life. So it would be for us who approach God with an unwavering heart that in Jesus we would be filled with all that would build us up. As it was prophesied by Isaiah, re-proclaimed by Paul to the Corinthians, ‘What no eye has seen, no ear has heard, no human mind conceived, all these things would be given to the one who loves God’. Believe and it would be yours. Amen

15/04/24, 08:27 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I commend you to God and to the Word of His Grace, which is able to build you up and to give you the inheritance among all those who are sanctified’ (Acts 20:32)

Paul was on his way to Jerusalem when he got a chance to meet with the Ephesian believers with whom he had a great connection. Paul had been living in Ephesus for almost 3 years when he preached to them the Gospel and from there to all surrounding regions his ministry spread. The number of miracles, signs and works of power that occurred in this region was tremendous and extraordinary. He also had to suffer much persecution, contempt, jealousy and severe setbacks while ministering there. Now when he knew that he hadn’t much time ahead and was to go to Jerusalem, he prayed for the Ephesians whom he dearly loved. What best could we do for those close to our hearts, but to commend them to the Lord and into the protection of His Word? God’s Word wields the power to build up, protect, support, rejuvenate and restore relationships, people, situations; and even hopeless turnarounds. Contrary to common course thinking that it is human skills, wit, empathy, and words that make a change in lives, God’s Word revives and elevates one to a higher pedestal in grace to receive from above and give more to those around. It doesn’t matter what you are or what you do, without God’s grace, you and I are powerless, with nothing worthy at hand. Let the Holy Spirit teach us to offer all that is ours to the Lord and the sanctification of His Word. May the Risen Jesus work miracles in your life, especially in those most unexpected and forgotten areas of life. Amen

16/04/24, 08:31 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But Jesus remained silent and gave no answer’ (Mark 14:61)

Jesus was brought before the Council of the Sanhedrin, which consisted of the priests, Chief priests, Elders and Scribes. Now this was the first time in history that a trial was made by the Sanhedrin at night and outside their usual meeting place. These had gathered that night in Jerusalem to try Jesus (after He had been arrested, bound and taken captive) to find ‘solid evidence’ against Him that He could be put to death. Numerous false testimonies were raised against Him, but due to dissensions and contradictions in them, none of them could be taken up. Jesus was aware of their intentions, that their hearts would never go with the truth that was He; not far from now, even His disciples would play a safe game distancing themselves from Him. He was prepared for the greatest purpose of His incarnation – the Crucifixion. Therefore, He uttered no reply and gave no explanations for the allegations imposed upon Him. The Council was surprised as they expected Jesus to disagree and prove His point. What at times proves most difficult for us is to submit to the wrong, especially when we know the reality is otherwise. Before people who fail to listen to us or accept what we say, no matter how much we try to accurately communicate or convince them, it might always be wise to remain silent, to let God intervene for us. Jesus asks us to emulate Him, in situations of mistrust, and in the events of arguments. It is in moments of silent surrender, that Jesus takes control; He the silent Lamb, knew the pain of loneliness, of being falsely accused and rejected. He will fight our cause. Be our way, our Light and resurrection today, Jesus. Amen

17/04/24, 08:08 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘How can one enter a strong man’s house and carry off his possessions unless he first binds the strong man? Then he can plunder his house’ (Matthew 12:29)

You and I could be this strong man; the plunderer the evil one. We are strong because God’s Word dwells in us and we are aware of His presence in us. What the evil plunders from us are the virtues of a lifetime – love, compassion, humility, generosity, chastity, patience, temperance and diligence. It’s not that knowingly one gives in to be confiscated of these virtues, but unknowingly. Gradually he infiltrates into the heart of this one, by which his God-given faculties are bound and enslaved by the evil one. Now how this evil one, the one who doesn’t gather with the Lord (Matthew 12:30) intrudes into the innermost being of the strong man is through 3 ways – lust of the flesh, pride of the eyes, and the pride of life. As soon as he gains entry into the otherwise strong person, he destroys, devastates and plunders as he wills. Now, how do we regain all that is lost? By empowering ourselves with the Spirit of Jesus, who gathers with Jesus. With the Holy Spirit, You and I are restored to our original state of power, of freedom of unity with God and man. He, the Holy Spirit is stronger, attacks the evil, takes away his armour and divides his spoil, thereby releasing the victim with strength, vigour and enthusiasm to fervently serve in God’s kingdom. Let us pray for being anointed with power from above, the Holy Spirit. Amen

18/04/24, 08:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For you know that it was not with perishable things such as silver or gold that you were redeemed from the empty way of life handed down to you from your ancestors’ (1 Peter 1:18)

Everyone who is with Jesus is taught by the Heavenly Father. We are taught of what is the truth and of the Spirit, as God is Spirit, He leads us to a true and perfect worship that confirms us to His likeness and image. We are assured of our inheritance of the city with Eternal foundations, ‘the New Jerusalem’, whose designer and maker is God. And this we have obtained by grace – by Jesus, through His precious blood, the Lamb sacrificed spotless and blameless. You and I have received this call, the vocation to obey the truth, that was revealed to us by Jesus, not to fall back to the ways of old, the ways that have been handed down to us from our ancestors. We know that our former ways led to the death of the Spirit in us, but now that we have been redeemed in Christ, we are alive. We have hope that as Christ was risen and glorified, our mortal bodies would receive glorification to surmount all that defeats, deprives and depresses. There is supernatural hope in us to tackle all hopelessness in life, there is joy that transcends every moment of agony in life, and there is peace that supersedes the emptiness within us, for what Jesus has gifted us is unmatched and priceless. Nothing can compare to the Spirit of God possessing you. Ask Him all that you need, all that you are deprived of today, ask Him for strength and power to make your signature to the world ‘Christ in You’ Amen

19/04/24, 09:16 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And you know the way to where I am going’ (John 14:4)

Jesus knew He was on His way to fulfill His purpose in life. He had told his disciples of the suffering, humiliation, death and resurrection he was to undergo soon in Jerusalem. He knew that how much ever he spoke, they were not prepared to comprehend or make sense of what He intended to tell them. But He continued encouraging them and exhorting them to believe in what He had told them. This is what the Lord keeps reminding us each day – to trust Him, He will make sure that all is well, regardless of how adverse our current situations may seem. The reality may be that we may not be knowing the way ahead or where we are heading. The skies may have been forever yellow and disenchanting that we fail to recognize ourselves. Then we must ask the Lord, as one of his disciples Thomas asked: Lord we do not know where you are going (taking us) nor do we know the way. He will surely then give us a reply – one of assurance, conviction and comfort. Speak to us Jesus as we stray away from the path you take us through, remind us of your ongoing presence in our lives, especially in moments of despair, agony and fear. Be the Way, the Light and Life in our lives. Amen

20/04/24, 09:04 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Great is the Lord, who delights in the welfare of His servant!’(Psalm 35:27)

The eyes of the Lord watch over all Creation – to see if there are those among men who honor Him, obey His Commandments, and hope in His compassion and forgiveness. These are graced with the Light of God’s favor. In all that they do, they prosper; they are blessed in abundance that too in the sight of all. It is He who protects them from the intrigues of men and lying tongues. We are children of a God who delights in our uplift, our well-being and our welfare. Not that there will not be rough patches in life as we keep moving; there might come situations, people and authorities who disown or fail to approve of our motives and ways. But the one who has formed us so special and distinct knows the length, breadth and dimensions of our life. Let us entrust ourselves to the Lord, seek His intervention in our lives, open our hearts to delight in His presence, to praise and thank Him for all goodness in life, to emit His mercy and love to all that is cold and fallen. I thank you Lord for being my refuge and extending your arms of protection for me. Yes there is no one like the God of Jeshurun, who secures his beloved to ride over the heights! Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

21/04/24, 10:04 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For surely it is not angels that he helps, but he helps the offspring of Abraham’ (Hebrews 2:16)

God wanted mankind to realize their worth – for His Son was sent to redeem mankind. Not to save Angels, did Jesus take the form of a man, but to reclaim the lost you and me to the Father. To restore us to our original selves, He became fully human in every way, to become that merciful and faithful High Priest in service to God, that atonement for the sins of all men would be performed in its fullness. He himself was tempted by the evil one, therefore He is able to understand us whenever we reach out to Him for solace and support. Not a mere understanding, but also wields the authority to transform every creation to submit to His plan. None can supersede His plans or purpose for our lives. He, the Good Shepherd knows His sheep, their strengths and weaknesses, that they tend to go astray, that He would need to seek after them. Let us pray to this Good Shepherd Jesus, who tends to us day and night, provides for our needs and protects us from all that is hazardous to life. A battered reed He would not break, a smoldering wick He will not extinguish until He leads justice to victory for you and me. In trust and confidence in Him will be our peace. Amen

22/04/24, 10:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘If you ask me anything in my name, I will do it’ (John 14:14)

To ask in Jesus’s name would mean more than adding it to the end of our prayers. It would infer a humble submission to the will of the Father for us; accepting that we are inadequate due to our sinfulness; believing that Jesus has claimed for us a treasure highly esteemed, the position as sons and daughters of God. In no other name is our Salvation – in no person, relationship, authority or power, but in Jesus. Therefore, being born again in Jesus and His Spirit, we are called towards an extraordinary lifestyle – one that follows after Jesus, imitates His ways and transforms into His likeliness each day. It is in our calling to hold firmly to what we have heard in the beginning, what has drawn us to faith in Jesus, to love Him with a pure heart, and to let go of ways, practices and ideals that are contrary to proclaiming Jesus as the Lord. It is thus that when we ask in Jesus’s name, we are healed, we are delivered from the bondages of this world, and we enjoy the fruits of His Spirit in us. To all who thirst to seek the truth, He gives this privilege – To dwell in His Word and keep asking for in His name. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

23/04/24, 07:47 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘ You have disposed all things by measure and number and weight’ (Wisdom 11:20)

Orderliness and Peace were foundational to this earth when it was established and set by the Spirit of God. Into this Orderliness was mankind sent, to live in harmony with nature and ensure its upkeep. God invited mankind into His Creativity to ordain peace and tranquility in all that they do and speak; to thwart all chaos and uncertainty that may emerge in the course of his living. Now in our lives today, if things that occur seem to create disruptions and disturbance, it’s time we turn to God who has disposed all things in their right measure and number, to ask Him for a turnaround. And this we are assured that it is not in mere human effort and desire that things change for the better but in God’s mercy and compassion. Jesus calls us to a life of peace by surrendering all that is ours to Him, who sustains the world at large. Towards harmony, peace and joy in the Holy Spirit, You and I have been called and set apart for by the Lord. Where else can our hope emerge from apart from the great promise that Jesus has given us? Wishing you a Spirit filled day! Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
23/04/24, 09:27 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Pavanathmave Nee Varename
 Kester

ഞാൻ അഞ്ചാറു മാസം ആയിട്ട് ഒരുപ്പാട്‌ വേദനയിലും വിഷമത്തോടും പോയിക്കൊണ്ട് ഇരിക്കുക ആണ്. കാരണം എന്റെ സ്വന്തം ഭാര്യ (Richu) ഒര് ചെറിയ വാക്കിന്റെ പേരിൽ എന്നെ വിളിക്കാതെ ഇരുന്നിട്ട് അഞ്ചാറു മാസം ആയി. ഞങളുടെ വിവാഹം കഴിഞ്ഞിട്ട് 6 വർഷം ആയി ഒര് 4 വയസ്സുള്ള കുട്ടിയും ഉണ്ട്. ഞാൻ dubai യില് ആയിരുന്നു ജോലി ചെയ്തിരുന്നത് ഞാൻ എട്ടു കൊല്ലം ജോലി ചെയ്തതും എന്റെ ഭാര്യക്ക് വേണ്ടി ആയിരുന്നു അന്ന് അവൾക്ക് ജോലിയോ ഒന്നും ഇല്ലാ. ഇന്ന് ഇപ്പോൾ അവൾക്ക് ജോലി ആയി uk യില്. അവിടെ എത്തിയപ്പോ മുതൽ അവൾക്ക് മൊത്തത്തിൽ എല്ലാം കൂടെ മാറ്റം ആയി. അവൾ എന്നെ വിളിക്കാതെ ആയി. നേരത്തെ വിളിക്കും ആയിരുന്നു ചെന്നപ്പോ പക്ഷെങ്കിൽ അവൾക്ക് എല്ലാം ഒര് അവടെ തായ timming ആയിരുന്നു. Just വിളിച്ച് പെട്ടന് പോകും. അങ്ങനെ ഇരുന്നപ്പോ ആണ് ചെറിയ ഒര് വാക്കിന്റെ പേരിൽ അവൾ എന്നെ മൊത്തത്തിൽ വിളിക്കാതെ ആയി. അവൾ അവളുടെ സ്വന്തം പപ്പയെ പോലും വിളിക്കുന്നില്ല കാരണം അവളുടെ pappa എന്നെ വിളിക്കാതെ അവടെ പപ്പയെ വിളിക്കരുത് എന്ന് പറഞ്ഞത് കൊണ്ട് ആ വാശിയിൽ അവളുടെ പപ്പയെ വിളിക്കുന്നില്ല. അവൾ അവളുടെ അമ്മയെ മാത്രം വിളിക്കും. അവളുടെ ഹൃദയത്തിൽ പിശാഷിക കൊണ്ട് ആയിരിക്കാം എന്നോടും എന്റെ അപ്പനോടും അമ്മയോടും വേണ്ടാത്ത വേദനിക്കുന്ന വാക്കുകൾ അവൾ പറയുകയും ചെയ്തു. അവൾ ഇപ്പൊ അവൾക്ക് അവടെ ലോകം എന്ന് പറയുന്നത് അവളുടെ friends, കറങ്ങി അടിച്ചു നടക്കണം അതിൽ കുടുബം എന്നോ ഒന്നും അവൾ ചിന്തിക്കുനില്ല. എനിക്ക് അവളെ ഉപേക്ഷിക്കാനും പറ്റില്ല. എനിക്ക് അവളെ അത്രയ്ക്ക് ഇഷ്ട്ടം ആണ്. എനിക്ക് പറയാൻ ഉള്ളത് അവളിലെ ഹൃദയത്തിൽ ഈ അരൂപീകളുടെ ബന്ധനത്തിൽ നിന്നും അവളെ മോചിത ആക്കി നല്ല സ്നേഹം നിറഞ്ഞവളായി അവൾ തിരിച് വന്ന് വേഗം ഞങ്ങളെ എല്ലാരേയും അവളുടെ സ്വന്തം പപ്പയെ യും എന്നെയും വിളിക്കാൻ വേണ്ടി പ്രാർത്ഥിക്കണം. അവളെ വേഗം കേരളത്തിൽ എത്തിച്ചു divine ധ്യാന കേന്ദ്രത്തിൽ അവിടെ ഏഴു ദിവസം നിന്ന് എല്ലാർക്കും അവിടെ വന്ന് പ്രാർത്ഥിക്കാൻ വഴി ഒരുക്കണം എന്നും . എന്നിട്ട് സ്നേഹത്തോടെ ഒത്തൊരുമിച്ചു ജീവിക്കുവാൻ വേണ്ടി പ്രാർത്ഥിക്കണം അഭ്യർത്ഥിക്കുന്നു. 24/04/24, 08:50 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Since they did not know the righteousness of God and sought to establish their own, they did not submit to God’s righteousness’ (Romans 10:3)

‘They’ could be ‘You’ and ‘me’, or anyone ignorant of Christ Jesus. The One who believes in Jesus is convinced of the Cloak of righteousness that Jesus has wrapped him in, not one that he deserves, but bequeathed to Him by the Son of God. Dressed appropriately in this Garb of Righteousness of Jesus, we make ourselves worthy to feast at the Table of God, the Father, who hosts a grand banquet of His mercy and compassion for all mankind. This is why this parable of the Wedding Feast ends with the heartrending message that ‘Many are invited but few are chosen’ (Matthew 22). What essential condition would secure our worthiness to feast with God in the Banquet He has individually invited us to? The likeness of Jesus. And this is what we receive when we believe in Jesus, that He is the culmination of the Law so that righteousness is opened to all. This is the essence, the crux of our belief, that through Jesus, and only through HIM, there is Salvation. When we obey what He tells us, we are redeemed and wrapped again with the righteous blood of the Lamb of God – the Lamb that was sacrificed but yet is alive, reigning in Glory with the Father. The Lamb which only could open the seal of the Heavenly scroll, that reconciliation to the Father was made a reality for all mankind. In this is God’s righteousness revealed, that though we were unworthy, sinful and lost, the Blood of Jesus rescued, renewed and reclaimed us to Him. Why then do we need to settle scores and establish our rights, when we are a Royal kingdom, priestly race and God’s own people. Amen

25/04/24, 07:32 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Let us who are of the day, be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love; and for a helmet, the hope of Salvation’ (1 Thessalonians 5:8)

We who have experienced the Light of Jesus are called to preserve the light and its intensity in us to the end. God has destined us for Salvation through Jesus Christ and this is not a one-shot-stop effort. It calls for continuity, renewal of strength and power in the Holy Spirit each day. And that which keeps us on track is these 3 Theological virtues – faith, Hope and Love – It is these that pose the yardstick for our assessment, as to whether we have fallen from the Light. If faith in the Lord Jesus, which inevitably would mean ‘trust in Him’ has dwindled, if love for the Lord has become cold in us, if hope of the Glory of ‘Being Saved’ has lost its severity in us, it is sure test that we have deteriorated in our soberness of being the Children of Light. In case, we are, where do we turn to? God’s Word and His promises. These hold immense power to rejuvenate and reignite us in the Holy Spirit. Ask yourself, how much have we longed to follow the Lord, after all that He has done for us. Jesus asks us of nothing more than to trust in Him. Amen

26/04/24, 09:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘When He comes, He will prove the world to be in the wrong about sin and righteousness and judgment’ (John 16:8)

No matter how convincingly articulative we may be, or how successful our lives may be, we will never be able to make a change in another’s life by way of speech. Time and again, our experiences may have taught us so, but we never let go of a chance to convince another that his ways are wrong. How different and comfortable would living in this world seem to be, if everyone did listen to us? Through His Word of life today, Jesus gives a new perspective, on how our lives could pitch for the better, with ease and comfort. By asking for the power of the Holy Spirit, the power from above, that can change even the most ordinary situations to one of triumph and glory. It is He the Holy Spirit, the power and strength of Jesus, who would prove the world of what is sin, what is righteousness and what the ultimate judgment would be. We would not need to interfere here, but to pray for an outpouring of this Spirit of ‘truth’. He would do all that would need to be done to bring in repentance, return, conversion of hearts, and a conviction of Who God is and of our relationship with Him to Eternity. Where God does the work of speaking, why would we need to bother, be hesitant, or complain? Ask for today’s anointing (a special one) of the Holy Spirit to be your best and do your best to outsmart yesterday with all its flaws. Amen

27/04/24, 08:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Jesus replied: They need not go away. You give them something to eat.’(Matthew 14:16)

Jesus is our provider; who opens wide doors of possibilities in our life. Even when things appear stagnant in life, it is He who shows us the way. When the people heard of Jesus crossing the shore onto a desolate place, they did not hesitate to follow after Him, knowing well that It was far from their towns and homes. Jesus felt compassion for them not just because they followed Him, but also because of seeing the faith that prompted them to overcome all hassles to meet with Jesus. For us a promising lesson, the Holy Spirit teaches us is - it is faith that has to drive us ahead in life. The faith that no matter how difficult things may seem, to obey what the Lord tells us, to comply with the way He shows us (though it may seem a farce). The courage needed to listen close to Jesus, and deafen ourselves to the voices of the people around us is what He graces us compassionately. It reminds us that we are obliged to do all in our care to support others, those the Lord brings before us, those whom the Lord wills to help through us -  in ways He shows us. Let us look into our immediate world, into the hearts that need our love, and encouragement, and pray that God’s will may be fulfilled through us and for them through the outpouring of His Spirit in us. Amen

29/04/24, 07:19 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The authorities that exist have been established by God. Consequently, whoever rebels against the authority is rebelling against what God has instituted, and those who do so will bring judgment on themselves (Romans 13:1,2)

It’s not that the virtue of ‘being subject to authority’ has been forgotten, but that people today are unaware of whom to obey and be subject to.  It is just like many followers of Christ today do, who practically are unaware of the Gospel and whose lives contradict its essence. Jesus explains to us that there is no authority except that which God has established. The family, the Church, and Governance in nations are all divinely instituted, therefore we are instructed to abide by those at the helm of affairs in each of these. In families, it is the parent or the husband who is authorized to take the lead, the Bishops and clergy in the Church take important decisions on behalf of all, and for a nation, it is the Political Leader/ the Government who is authorized to rule over all affairs. What all this means is that You and I are called to obey and respect decisions taken by these authorities as dictated by God. This also means that we are obliged to pray for those who lead us, that they may be led by the Holy Spirit in all the decisions they make. At times their human natures take head-on to suppress their charism to serve and induce progress for all. But even then, this Word from God assures us that Grace has the power to propitiate the good for all. It is not in rebellion and opposition that good comes to prevail over evil but in humility and patient understanding of human limitations, God’s will comes to fulfillment. The Spirit of God propagates peace, love and generosity, and encourages a broader perspective to accept the Will of God, no matter how illogical it may seem to the human intellect. Therefore, let us ask ourselves how well could we obey the Word of God to be subject to Divinely instituted authorities, to humbly pray for them that all they do and say may turn out for greater good of all. Amen

01/05/24, 07:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I, the Lord, the First, and with the last; I am He’ (Isaiah 41:4)
With love and authority, the Lord God asks you and me, who sometimes wallow in regret and sorrow – The One who was stirred up from the East, with whom victory meets at every step; who He is? – Jesus Christ. We are asked to approach Him, speak to Him and preserve ourselves to stay the final judgment, He would make on our behalf. He, the First in Heaven and Earth, would always love to stand with those who are the last, those who have experienced failures over failure and those who have always been left in solitude and agony.  With these ‘the last’, ‘the least’ He is the I am, in whatever ways they would need His presence – be it material needs, be it spiritual or even in the realm of lost relationships, He is the Am for us all. Whenever You and I battle with the storms of life, whenever our hearts are troubled, approach the Lord, the First and ask Him to be the I Am for you. What no human can provide, He fills with peace, rejuvenates with strength, encourages with confidence to be what we are called to be. It may be difficult for our frail hearts to acknowledge this truth, especially when situations and people turn hostile and all that was in place just falls apart or gets distorted. And remember, whenever so, free yourself from all that keeps you engaged and busy to find time to approach and speak with the Lord. He acknowledges every gesture of love and sincerity from you and me. Lord Jesus, be with me always, be the First to deliver me and let me believe that You are all that I need each moment in life. Amen

02/05/24, 10:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Our Gospel came to you not simply in word, but also with power, with the Holy Spirit, and deep conviction’ (1 Thessalonians 1:5)

Every time the Word of God is read, heard, or preached, a four-fold blessing descends upon the one who listens earnestly and the one who spreads the Word. The grace to speak with God’s approval, the truth and not to please men; power from God that unveils in boldness to speak amid adversities; to wield power in the Holy Spirit to love, care and gently share; with assurance that it is God who would intervene in lives of people to equip them for His Kingdom. To Paul, He witnessed to this Word which was fulfilled in the Thessalonians believers. They believed in the Lord Jesus and held firm to faith even when persecutions came their way. They imitated the good works of Paul as he went about toiling and exhorting them like a father to his children. Rest was the work of the Holy Spirit, who fulfilled God’s purpose about the Thessalonians. In the absence of Paul and his co-workers, who had to flee due to persecution there, the community flourished like anything, with hope and love. Let us pray for God’s Word to penetrate deep into our hearts, change our old ways and prepare us for newer things in life. Amen

03/05/24, 09:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I have given them your Word’ (John 17:14)

The Word by which the Universe was created, which sustains everything in order in accordance with the perfect plan of the Father, the Word of power, of truth and that which delineates a path through the wilderness – is what Jesus gives to you and me today. Deep within our hearts He exists and desires to be manifest by our word and deed. In humility, in compassion, in obedience, in serenity He becomes revealed to the world through us. We are the Light of this world, that knows not what is beneficial, what builds and upholds. It is not in permissibility and liberty that human beings attain creativity and success but in the right discernment and right choices that elevate one from where He exists to where He is called to be. Before going to the Father, Jesus gave Himself to us symbolically in bread and wine, which He fulfilled for us on the Cross the very next day. Whenever we receive this Eucharistic Lord, He dwells, enlightens, and inspires us for the better, He leads us to our destiny – to be one with the Trinity. The Trinitarian love gets poured into our hearts whereby we obtain the power to do greater things – to forgive where impossible, to love even when ignored, to pray in hope for all things even when the future seems bleak and dark. This is the Word that Jesus instills in your heart today – receive and accept it with reverence and joy. Amen

'In His name, the nations will place their hope '(Matthew 12:21)

The nations would imply anyone who has endorsed Jesus as Lord and Master in their hearts. To this one Jesus has promised the overflowing rivers of grace sourced from within, the power of the Holy Spirit. Thus he becomes a conqueror of hearts and situations with the love of the Lord. With the anointing of this power from His Heavenly Father , Jesus set forth through Galilee, Judea and Samaria, healing the sick, delivering the afflicted and proclaiming the Good news of His Kingdom. 07/05/24, 09:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For if we have been united with Him in a death like His, we will certainly also be united with Him in a resurrection like His’ (Romans 6:5)

There can be no greater goal in one’s life than to be like Jesus, to transform into His likeness in the course of our short-lived span on earth. His Spirit nurtures us – our selfish old beings into the new creation, the masterpiece we have been called to embrace. This training is life-long, pivoted on the realization of God’s master plan for creation. Each one is however drilled according to his uniqueness, individual roles, responsibilities, and characteristic traits meaning on a case-to-case basis, with no one suit fits all. The multifaceted experiences one may be let through in life can be exciting and rejuvenating, but not without disappointments, rejections, criticisms and setbacks. To be united with Jesus in a death like His is to endure persecution, solitariness and doubts as He did on the Cross. And God promises us that as Christ was raised and glorified after the excruciating death, we who follow closely with Him are sure to receive our reward for all the sufferings in life, provided we have surrendered ourselves to His grace and committed to sin no more. What Jesus asks of you is a sincere desire and commitment to live His way and truth, to reconcile with God and not to the passions of this world. Even though we may be weak, in Him is our strength, it is He who establishes us firm for His Kingdom; so let us rely no more on our human instincts and thoughts, trusting in Him always and in His Word. Amen

08/05/24, 10:03 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘If anyone hears my words and does not keep them, I do not judge him, for I did not come to judge the world but to save the world’ (John 12:47)

No one can understand us as well as the Lord does! And thus, from the depths of His compassion and love, the Lord reiterates His promises for all who ardently receive His Word and believe in Him. He knows well that in our frailty, we may be unable to perfect His Word in our lives. He never judges the one who has fallen (who has failed to keep His Word) but encourages him to retract from the lost ground. What Jesus longs to see in us is the desire to listen to Him, to seek counsel and support from Him, when we fail or plummet in our orientation and drive. He is the Light in us, that works in Creation for regeneration. Even if You and I are surmounted by uncertainties, when the soil underneath may seem as thin as never, be assured that - We have a stronghold on which we can place our hopes, dreams, and possibilities for a take-off. In Jesus, the Son of God, we have our redeemer, our Good Shepherd, the restorer of our pride and lost glory. With a God so merciful, always ready to shade us beneath His mighty wings, to reclaim us from the storms of yesterday and raging winds that disturb our peace today, we have everything in us to keep moving ahead. Jesus asks You and me to stop listening to the voices around and to focus on Him. If God is with us, who can be against us? He will endow us with the strength, agility, and endurance to keep His Word and follow Him today as in the past. Amen

09/05/24, 10:41 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me’ (Psalm 51:10)

In this, I see the steadfast love, creativity, and faithfulness of the Lord towards me, that there is always something anew to learn from my world. The incredibility of nature, the versatility in people who surround me, the change of seasons, mood swings, disruptions, and change – remind me of how simplicity is so neatly woven into needed intricacies by the Creator. A grateful heart is open to exploring and imploring God about what creates contentment in life. Smothered with difficulties, challenges, trials and intrigues in life, the Great King David knew that power from the Lord was all that he needed to make each day fruitful for him and for the people he reigned over. He thus implored God to create in him a pure heart, one that followed closely after the heart of His Creator. In addition, he pleaded for steadfastness in spirit, for the boldness to make the right decisions at the right time and without fret or fear. If our hearts are likewise created pure, and our Spirits revived for action each day, how easy would even the most burdening tasks in life turn out to be for us? Let us pray for the broadening of our hearts, to expand our productivity, creativity and purpose of life; and to execute every task entrusted to us in thankfulness always. Amen

10/05/24, 11:10 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Greater love has no one than this; to lay down one’s life for one’s friends’ (John 15:13)

The expediency of ‘love’ is its supernatural power to bring in perfection. God the Father loved us and for our sake, He sent His only Son, Jesus, to reclaim mankind to its lost glory. Jesus likewise loved us, and thus incarnated as one among men in the course of history. It was Jesus who delineated the contours of love, broadening its dimensions to more than what the world could have imagined of. While ‘to love’ would mean to express a sincere sense of affection, interest or likeness to someone or something, as the world defined it to be, Jesus perfected love by offering Himself for the sake of His loved ones. To us, He gives this commandment ‘to love’ one another, which is the first and best commandment a Christian ought to follow. And in our lives, the Lord has given many who lean on us, who journey with us through the same crossroads, our dear and near, those in need of support, to whom a kind word or encouragement would make a big difference. It is to these that the Lord expects us to manifest our love first, while also loving the world at large. At times our love expressed is direly conditional and overtly relational. There may have been many who were disappointed by our ways, words and inaction; these were specific opportunities given to us to love and learn to care for as Jesus did. Where we have let go of them, we can make up by asking to be filled with the power of the Spirit of Jesus. To be led by the inspirations of the Holy Spirit, is where our lives would find perfection and fulfilment. Let us pray for the grace to be filled with God’s love and His compassion. Amen

11/05/24, 09:43 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For through the Spirit we eagerly await by faith the righteousness for which we hope’ (Galatians 5:5)

In Jesus, the righteousness of God has been manifested for all mankind, apart from the Law that judges based on works. This is the gift God gives to all who believe in Jesus. Jesus, the face of the merciful Father, reconciles us to the glory of the Father, by the propitiating sacrifice of His on the Cross. Therefore, if one who has received Jesus as Lord and Master, retreats to the state of justifying himself by works and deeds of the Law (human or divinely given), he severs his relationship with Christ, thereby falling away from grace. You and I are being reminded of what it means to be a true follower of Christ – to humble yourself to be filled with power by His Spirit each day. It is not in complying with rules or rituals of piety that one sanctifies himself, but by letting himself to be empowered and led by the Holy Spirit. Therefore, in this is faith, that in all our sinfulness, distress and inadequacies, God’s Spirit in us takes control. Not against flesh and blood we wrestle, but against Principalities, Powers, against the Spirits of Darkness of this world, that deter us from strengthening ourselves in Jesus. We who wait in the Lord are sure to see Him intervene for us, for the better, and for miracles to unravel in everyday living. Lord, let the power of Your Spirit rain upon us - for our most needed anointing for the day. Come Holy Spirit come! Amen

12/05/24, 07:42 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘To Him, who is able to do immeasurably more than all we ask or imagine, according to His power that is in work within us..’(Ephesians 3:20)

The Spirit of ‘Truth’ the Holy Spirit enables us to seek and find the real person You and I are – the Inner being. Personality development begins with this first step of knowing who we are and strengthening ourselves from this perspective. The Inner being takes nourishment from its Creator, from the love poured into our hearts by the Holy Spirit. From here comes the drive and caliber in our personalities, to excel, to harbour ideas, and thoughts in the arena of the sublime, to endure hardships and work towards growth, surpassing all limitations. It is Christ in us, His love that motivates the Inner person to outdo inhibitions, to garner peace, joy and serenity amid turmoil. To fight the odds and oddities of life’s tribulations, people and circumstances, Jesus challenges us to believe and more than all ‘trust’ in Him. Where there is trust in Jesus, in His immeasurable power in work within us, there is no fear, no room for despair or dejection. Even what we fail to ask for in our human understanding the Spirit discerns and offers as prayers before the Throne of the Most High. We praise and thank you Jesus for enlightening our hearts to understand who we are (however little) and what we could do within all the inhibitions and flaws of our human nature, with the immeasurable power of Your Spirit in us. Amen

13/05/24, 09:40 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Let us fall into the hands of the Lord and not into the hands of mortals, For equal to his majesty is his mercy; and equal to his name are his works’ (Sirach 2:18)

Today’s Word from the Lord insists on the need to always trust the Lord. To trust in the Lord would mean being convinced that He would take care of everything in its due time. But this comes with certain conditions being fulfilled from our end. Firstly, there is a need for this one to fear the Lord. Fear of the Lord is a gift that God’s Spirit pours into one’s heart by which he is able to distinguish between the ordinary and the holy, to revere the consecrated and humbly submit to God’s will. The second condition we would need to practice in our lives is ensuring righteousness in our ways and deeds. To wait for God is the third element that one who trusts in the Lord would need to imbibe in himself. Waiting unto the Lord is the most difficult among the aspects that constitute ‘trust’. For it is so likely for us men (with all our flaws and weaknesses) to sway, to stray when situations prove adverse and inconducive to a peaceful existence. In this juncture, God’s Word through this Chapter of the Book of Sirach reminds us of generations of old – if anyone who has trusted in the Lord has ever been disappointed or forsaken by Him? Men may reject, dislike or even deceive us on account of their emotions and perceptions, but which the Lord can never do to His beloved. Our Lord is a compassionate, merciful God, who forgives, upholds and saves at times of distress. Therefore, as Kind David did once resolve to offer himself to the Lord than trust in the goodwill of men, let us also learn to place our hearts and be strengthened in the Lord, to bequeath of His great works of mercy and majesty for us. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

14/05/24, 10:05 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For not from the east or from the west and not from the wilderness comes lifting up, but it is God who executes judgment, putting down one and lifting up another’ (Psalm 75:6,7)

The Risen Lord brings to our comprehension a basic truth – that it is He who lifts or elevates man. And when He does so, this man is charged with a higher responsibility to bear fruit. Bearing fruit would mean a gratuitous life, that reflects God’s love in word, deed and attitude. Such a one would refrain from judging or despising another who is less privileged in all dimensions of living (material and spiritual). Jesus expects this man to be merciful and to be a living example of ‘The Beatitudes’ outlined as central in His Sermon on the Mount. The Psalm hints at two sinful aspects that have become common course in daily human living – boastfulness (pride) and haughtiness (arrogance). These being part of the list of Cardinal sins have repercussions even more far-fetched. First, these invite the wrath of God upon those who exhibit it and deprive them of their worthiness to receive mercy. Second, because of these, they prove themselves untrustworthy servants or stewards of God’s grace. It is God who elevates one and puts down another. If so, we all owe a primary responsibility to stay away from sins of this kind that are detrimental to our welfare and create disapproval from God. Let us pray for the virtue of humility which prepares us for greater works of charity, In Jesus name we ask. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

15/05/24, 09:45 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For not by their own sword did they win the land, nor did their own arm save them, but your right hand and your arm, and the light of your face, for you delighted in them’ (Psalm 44:3)

Your Right hand and arm would refer to Jesus Christ who sits at the right hand of the Father and the Holy Spirit who performs wonders and mighty deeds to fulfill God’s plan for human salvation. The Israelites were sure that it was God who willed their prosperity, that they must inherit the Promised land. They were merely nomads, who reared cattle; how could they equip themselves for war against men who used the most advanced artillery and weapons and who were trained for warfare? Therefore not by their might, strength or tactics was this land handed over to the Israelites, but because of God’s love and favour towards them. This impeccable experience of God’s glory canopying them (by day and night, through the wilderness and green pastures) was handed down to their posterity; who were reminded of their ultimate refuge at times of affliction and difficulties. To us, this serves an emulative example, of the need to transmit to our generations the experiences of the past, how when we felt all was lost, the Hands of the Lord kept working close by to help us persevere, to resiliently endure the challenge and rebound with renewed strength. It is this legacy that we owe to our generations, of how to thrive in an ever-evolving dynamic world with clear focus and unsurpassed vigor because of the living presence of God in us. This is the needed life-skills training our generations need to imbibe while decking themselves with material possessions and prowess in life. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
16/05/24, 09:46 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But if through my lie God’s truth abounds to his glory, why am I still being condemned as a sinner?’ (Romans 3:7)

The Created – the Universe, Paradise, and its upkeep was given to mankind. The Law, the prophets and the Oracles of God were given to the Jews, but they were unable to manifest obedience to the given Word in fullness. God knew this flaw in mankind, that in themselves they were unable to demonstrate fidelity to the truth; they were easily swayed towards evil and what was unbecoming to God. Therefore, He decided to extend Himself to them to reinstate them to their needed perfection. This God did through sending His Only-begotten Son, Jesus to save mankind from its sinfulness and reclaim them as adopted Sons into His Kingdom. Man’s waywardness became a cause for God’s mercy to be unveiled in abundance. Therefore, perceiving the will of God, St Paul reminds us, as he spoke to the believers in Rome, that even one’s state of helplessness, sinfulness or blame cannot be an obstacle for God’s grace to outpour on him, provided he repented of his sinfulness. A sense of contriteness or remorse for having offended God because of our misdeeds or omission to do the right is what God expects of You and me when we turn to Him for mercy. It is not towards self-hatred or condemnation or guilt that the Holy Spirit inspires us to renew ourselves today, but in increased love for the Lord, for what He has done for our behalf and how better we could reform our ways to please the Lord. Lord we pray that our lives be true witnesses to your glory to all men. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

17/05/24, 11:17 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The one who escapes from the sword of Hazael shall Jehu put to death, and the one who escapes from the sword of Jehu shall Elisha put to death’ (1 Kings 19:17)

Most extraordinarily, God reveals to Elijah (the prophet) His plan to bring back His people from their present state of wretchedness and Idolatry. The restoration of justice and righteousness among the people will occur over time through the varied experiences of tumult, resurgence and uncertainty that would be permitted in their lives. Elijah was to anoint these three persons as instruments of God’s works to be performed in due time. While Hazael was anointed as the King of Syria, Jehu would take over Israel from the hands of Ahab, the corrupt Idolatrous King. Elisha would succeed Elijah in his ministry of leading and teaching God’s people as their prophet. On the whole, these three had a predestined and individually distinct role to play in fulfilling God’s will and plan for the future. As we read through the Books of Kings we see how things take a turn, when Ahab and his family (who were unfaithful to God and the people) were wiped out in a series of wars. Ben Hadad, Ahab’s counterpart in Syria is killed by Hazael who takes over the reins of the empire shortly. And the people are revived into the worship of Yahweh through Elishah’s fiery miracles and signs worked by God for His people. You and I are likewise vested with destined and defined responsibilities to be performed without fail to ensure that His master plan for all mankind is fulfilled through us. If not us, God does have a way of securing things in place undoubtedly, but what about us, who would have then lost a grand opportunity as His prophets and ambassadors in history to make a mark? Let us ask the Lord for the grace to make use of every opportunity, every rare situation as these come knocking at our doors, waiting for us to act upon them. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

18/05/24, 09:03 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Answer me, O Lord, for your steadfast love is good; according to your abundant mercy, turn to me’ (Psalm 69:16)

To raise our complaints, the agony/pain in our hearts to the Almighty would be the most appropriate prayer for comfort and consolation. While many may doubt if we could ever voice our discontent before God, the Bible is replete with instances of prophets, Kings, and Godly ones raising their supplications, anguish and distress before God. Some of these were offered to the Lord as a plea for grace and power from the Lord (David, Isaiah and Jeremiah’s prayers), while others were the outpouring of the negative emotions that plagued the well-being of one’s mind, body and soul (as Job prayed from the depths of his afflictions). Whenever these prayers were offered to the Most High, there was always an answer from Him, irrespective of the worthiness of the one who prayed. We have a God, filled with compassion, with His steadfast love hovering above us who is concerned and always cares for our well-being. He knows every step in our life, the destiny we are moving towards and what it takes to transform us as doers of His Will. We must be convinced that there have been no coincidences or occurrences of mere chance in life. These were but the invisible works of the Lord drawing us closer to His plan and towards our purpose in life. So let us with confidence approach our loving Lord, pleading mercy, seeking His interference in our lives for deliverance, counsel and support. Answer me, O Lord, when I call upon your name, Your name and presence is my strength,refuge and comfort for a lifetime, no matter what. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

19/05/24, 07:24 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For as we share abundantly in Christ’s sufferings, so through Christ we share abundantly in comfort too’ (Acts 1:5)

Difficulties and afflictions are part of everyone’s life. You and I come across adversities in life but in varied ways. Today’s Word from the Lord teaches us how to get along with these unwelcome situations – by offering our sufferings to the sufferings of Jesus Christ. It is then that we share in Christ’s sufferings so that over time we would share in the comfort He gives us in its abundance. The Spirit of Jesus reasons out to us why sufferings have been given to each – so that we may learn the skill of forbearance.  Forbearance comes from grace that helps us not to rely on ourselves but on the risen Lord, the One who can transform everything untoward into an advantage for You and me. if then, why do we needlessly carry the burdens within? St Paul exhorted the Corinthians (and us) to pray for the grace to share in Christ’s sufferings, to obtain strength not just for our solace, but to comfort and encourage many around us who are unaware of the truth. The truth being that where there is Jesus, there is power, encouragement, resurrection and every possibility for transformation. On this day of the Feast of the Pentecost, let us pray for being with Christ, being drawn to His Word and seeking solutions to all our afflictions through oneness with Him. Lord, fill us with your power, the Holy Spirit to be witnesses to the world to recreate when all seems lost, to generate the hope that is needed to empower our world and to restart from where things have fallen. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
20/05/24, 09:16 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today
'All His precepts are trustworthy. They are established forever and ever, to be performed with faithfulness and uprightness' (Psalm 111:7,8)
Irrespective of time, space and technological progress, that which remains constant is the Divine law. His precepts that teach mankind how to live as per their call in life were established for Eternity. It is from these precepts that wisdom is imparted into the human heart. These were given to His own - Jacob/ Israel, and through them to the ends of the world through Jesus, His Son. That we may understand them, honour and obey these in faithfulness and in its fullness. These protect one from being evaded by evil and strengthens His Inner being to fight the battle with courage and conviction. For our meditation today, our Good Lord asks how determined we are in keeping His Word. For the Heavens and earth may pass by but not a single word that He has dictated.Let us seek help from the Holy Spirit to revere Jesus and abide by His Word always. Amen

21/05/24, 09:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘So is the one who lays up treasure for himself and is not rich toward God’ (Luke 12:21)

It is to lay up treasure for oneself bodily and in the Spirit, that You and I have been privileged with life on earth. The way of the world teaches us to accumulate possessions that purport richness here on earth. But God’s Word enlightens us to understand that ‘man does not live by bread alone.’ One’s life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions, so we have been asked to guard ourselves from covetousness. The question that may arise is how does one become rich towards God? the very question to which hardly a few have answers. Jesus tells us that the moment we feel the urge to be filled in our Spirit/soul, we are blessed. Blessed are the poor in Spirit for theirs is the Kingdom of God. It is when we accept the depravities of our Inner Being, when we begin to implore God to fill us with needed strength and when we make concerted efforts towards receiving grace that we become ‘rich in the soul’ or rich towards God. The moment pride takes over, self-sufficiency rules the roost, when we begin to rely on our intellect and understanding, we have limited our potential to expand towards God in richness. It is then that we become like this ‘rich fool’ who failed to experience poverty in him beyond his earthly possessions. Let us examine ourselves in the light of this Word from God, and desire to enrich ourselves both in body and soul. If God is for us, no bodily deprivation can overcome us. Lord Jesus, we believe that by the poverty you undertook on our behalf unto the cross, we have inherited the prosperity of yours and the glory of Your kingdom. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
22/05/24, 12:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Do not present your members to sin as instruments of unrighteousness, but present yourselves to God as those who have been brought from death to life’ (Romans 6:13)

Jesus the Righteous One surrendered himself to death, taking upon Him the sin and blame of all mankind, from Adam to those of end times. This he did, so that we may partake in His righteousness to be worthy of Eternity with the Trinitarian God. In this is God’s immense love for us revealed; that in all our wickedness, He willed that we be reclaimed and reconciled. It is by grace through faith that we have been saved, not by our works or designs. For by our works and ways, we have obtained the price of sin – death and this has unplugged us from engaging with the Father. God’s Word reminds us of how we could reciprocate God’s regard for us and His marvelous acts of mercy towards us – By presenting our faculties to the Lord for sanctification by His blood, by offering ourselves to be used by Jesus for greater works of mercy. We are called to enlighten and expand the horizons of our understanding of Jesus and how we could resemble Him through our lives – to look at things above, letting go of things of this world. Jesus asks us to learn to surrender every moment, situation and person in life to His Sacred heart, to trust that He will always be there for us, instilling the needed confidence and courage to make our next signature move through the twists and turns in life. May you be empowered to do your best today with the Lord and for Him. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
23/05/24, 10:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘….For He that is higher than the highest regardeth; and there be higher than they’ (Ecclesiastes 5:8)

To us who keep harping on the varied types of injustice, oppression and iniquities that crop up then and there in societal living, the Spirit of Jesus gives us counsel and comfort. We tend to get perplexed and deterred by odds and oddities in life, especially when they seem to emerge from the most sober and subtle avenues of daily living. Through each of these experiences, the Lord has great regard for us. Firstly because it was He who had permitted these to pay unexpected visits in our otherwise peaceful living. And these tend to leave significant imprints as aftereffects in our personality – intellectual, motivational, spiritual - and multidimensional. Yes, we must accept that some of these did forcefully drag us to and fro, derailing our course in life’s journey, some were highly detested due to the negative tinge and colour they instilled into a relaxed living. But every time we felt we were ignored or that we deserved more than what was given, the Lord took note of the same. And today He urges us to take heart, to console ourselves – to find peace in the Peace He gives us, to let go of the memories of yester years and experience the restoration, renewal and love He fills us for this day. For the hierarchies of this earth may fail to prove just, but He, the Highest above all, longs to ensure that we are found lacking in nothing, He who had come for giving us abundance in life, will be our shield, hiding place and refuge every moment in time. Amen

24/05/24, 08:24 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today
' After this I will return and I will rebuild the dwelling of David which has fallen; from its ruins, I will rebuild it and I will set it up' (Acts 15:16)

To understand the context of God's amazing promise to His people, given to us today, we must rewind to the times of the Babylonian exile. To the Judean inhabitants, never had they imagined of having had to succumb to defeat by a foreign power, considering the many instances prior when the City of Jerusalem was miraculously rescued through God's Providence. While trying to make sense of this unbelievable plight of theirs, God sends prophet Jeremiah to reveal to them the magnificent plan He works out for them under the cover of defeat and slavery. The dwelling of David would be restored to its prior glory. All that the people needed to do was to trust in God's works for them, to accept their present humiliation, to be subservient to the Babylonian King and to wait in hope for God's marvellous wonder works yet to be unveiled for them though in exile. Even with all our understanding, intellect and discernment of today, we might be struggling to make peace with situations at hand at times in life. But these are testing times for us, just as Jeremiah the prophet explained to the people who were shattered, that they were to merely trust in God's mighty Hands and His gracious deeds to be fulfilled in the desolate moments of life's journey. So be it to us, the  message of hope from the Most High, the choice is ours to believe and receive from above. Amen 
Dr. Mini Sherino

25/05/24, 09:15 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For this is why the Gospel was preached even to those who are dead, that though judged in the flesh the way people are, they might live in the Spirit in the way God does’ (1 Peter 4:6)

In Christ Jesus, we have been called to live an exemplary life in the Spirit, by obeying every Word that comes from the mouth of God. This is what Peter meant when he spoke of a life in Christ to be lived in the flesh not to satiate bodily passions but the will of God. The will of God is that we must each day transform into the image and likeness of Jesus. It is easy to live according to the ways of men, what the world teaches us, but to dare to be different, to forfeit sensuality and pleasures of the things around us would require courage and conviction. This is what the Spirit of the Lord imbibes in us, whenever we approach Jesus and seek help and support from Him. To be considered righteous during the Day of Final judgment, when all men would be brought before the Throne of Grace, we must have secured for us His favour, during this short earthly existence. As King David referred to ‘ to be the one whom the Lord considers blameless, is the one who is blessed, not the one whom the world extols’. This is why the truth was preached even to the dead, that men may realize – not by power or might or superior skills can the Kingdom of God be obtained, but by force, which comes from a Spirit-filled living. Let us pray for this grace of being forgiven and reclaimed by Jesus through mercy each day in life, to be loved by the Lord, and to be regarded for by Him always. Amen

26/05/24, 08:46 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Trust in the Lord, and do good; dwell in the land and befriend faithfulness’ (Psalm 37:3)

Faithfulness and abundance come from the Lord. Jesus is the fountain and from Him, we partake in the Living waters, the Holy Spirit that enlightens us to see the Light, the Truth and the way ahead in life’s journey. Contrary to logic or common reason (that we have obtained from the ways of the world) surety, calm, abundance and delights are what percolates deep into a soul that trusts in the Lord. Where the Spirit of the Lord dwells, there is freedom and hope. This freedom creates in us an aversion to sinfulness; while hope reminds us of God’s great promises to those who delight in Him. Therefore, this Sunday morning, Jesus teaches us what it means to ‘delight in Him’ – to rest in Him, to wait patiently for Him. By this, we befriend ourselves to the Father, because He has given us Jesus, that we may inherit Eternity. We become one with the Holy Trinity, the great mystery Jesus revealed to us through His Baptism. On this day of the Feast of the Holy Trinity, may God be our share, our fortress, refuge and hope for a better tomorrow. Over all that is transient, Jesus has given us mastery to conquer, because of His steadfast love and faithfulness for us. Amen

27/05/24, 09:40 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Lord is your keeper; the Lord is your shade on your right hand’ (Psalm 121:5)

The door for His Sheep, the Lord is, as He introduced Himself to the Pharisees and the crowds (John 10:7) - That Door which leads one to his destiny – Eternity. The One who holds the Keys of David, who opens that none would be able to shut and If He shuts none would be able to open (Rev 3:7) This is our Lord Jesus and His steadfast love towards us reminds us that however unworthy You and I are because of our sin and iniquities, He waits at the door of our hearts to embrace and restore us. Jesus is our keeper, the Shepherd of His flock who seeks after them, those who have lost their way, those wounded and those in need of strength, His hands comfort them. Having understood the mystery of Jesus in his life, about the perfect patience through mercy and forgiveness that recreated Peter from Simon, St Peter offers himself an example to all who believe in Jesus Christ. All who trust in Jesus would be judged faithful and chosen as instruments for God’s great works on earth. Therefore with St Paul let us accept and proclaim that none can separate us from the love of the Lord – neither death nor life, nor angels nor rulers, nor things present nor things to come, nor powers, nor height or depth, nor anything else in all creation. With the Lord at our right hand, we are more than conquerors over all that inhibits and disturbs a peaceful existence on earth. Amen

28/05/24, 09:33 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The meek shall obtain fresh joy in the Lord, and the poor among mankind shall exult in the Holy One of Israel’ (Isaiah 29:19)

The love of the Lord is new every morning bringing fresh joy into the hearts of all who cherish to receive in fullness. Elijah was once amid great agony, threatened for life, he had to flee into the wilderness; there God visited him. Elijah is strengthened in body and soul, to wait for the Lord. Into his desolate spirit, as a gentle breeze, he experiences joy and communion with the Most High. In His Sermon on the Mount, Jesus upheld the virtue of meekness; that the meek would inherit the earth; that they were blessed. To a nation that had turned deaf ears to God’s commandments and His will, Isaiah prophesies to them what the Lord desired of His people. That if they refrained from hardening of the heart, if they humbled themselves before their God, with a contrite heart, God would pour His Spirit of awakening and power. They would turn into God’s own, the invincible and protected by the Most High. For Lebanon (a wilderness land) to turn into a fruitful field and the field into a forest, would that be impossible for the Lord? The Lord asks His people. This grace of meekness adds to the soul a Heavenly joy, that which is not diminished by any earthly power. Meekness comes with its by-products - prudence and redemptive suffering. The power to endure hardships for greater glory and to evade sinfulness in all situations. To those among us who have mastered this skill, they will enjoy peaceful living which is a gift from above. Yes, in all our shortcomings, Jesus is our reason for rejoicing, hopefulness and encouragement. Who else is our rock, except the Lord, who arms us with strength and secures our way each new day. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

29/05/24, 08:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Lord looked down from his sanctuary on High, from Heaven he viewed the earth, to hear the groans of the prisoners and release those condemned to death’ (Psalm 102:19,20)

Whenever we try to make sense out of suffering, this Word from the Lord should cause us to understand that throughout the earth many suffer likewise or even more intensely. How could that condone one’s individual hardships and struggles? Jesus wants us to know the truth that suffering was not the plan of God for all mankind. And that is why the Lord from His Holy Sanctuary looks down on the earth, to see the struggles of people. All the unrighteous pain and hardships were those bestowed by the evil, that through these obstacles in life, men may defer God’s purpose for them. Their spirits cowed and strengths failing, they may attribute the cause of these to God, thereby abandoning Him. The Holy Spirit enlightens us that God in all His mercy and compassion, looks on to these people, offering His mighty Hands of support and provision. He sent His Son – Jesus, that all those fixed plans of evil may be curbed, reversed and utterly destroyed. What the Son of God did was to teach us how to love and respect human dignity and serve mankind. Jesus did not do anything out of His greatness or to demonstrate His superiority, but did it with a servant heart, with compassion and love, to redeem and reclaim for His Father. Whenever You and I find people around us going through tough times, it is not through our meaningless sympathising and advises that we emulate the work of God, but by ardently loving them and praying for their recovery. Who knows if we would also find ourselves entrapped in a similar kind of anomaly as theirs? And if so, what would be our plight? Instead of reasoning this way, we go on judging, expressing our superiority and condemning those who seem unfit according to our human standards. Let mercy and compassion prevail over judgment, Lord give us this grace. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

30/05/24, 08:23 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Thanks be to God for His indescribable gift’ (2 Corinthians 9:15)

For the unsurpassable gift of Jesus in our lives, let us join St Paul to thank God. Grace as we refer to each day is the ‘Power of God’ that supplies all our needs. He enriches us in every way that we reciprocate the generosity of God to those who are in deprivations – physical, emotional and spiritual. God loves a generous giver because he has proven himself as ‘one’ upholding the image and likeness of God. It becomes a reparation for the ingratitude, sacrileges and transgressions that men unknowingly do towards their fellow beings and God. What thus proves righteous before God is our belief in Jesus Christ; in the Sacraments (He has established for His Church – You and me) and in devoting ourselves as humble vessels to receive this grace that enriches one’s living. Into the Holy of Holies, Jesus leads us to be one with the Trinity, to be His Chosen Ones. Every time You and I approach God, with Jesus and through His Sacrifice reenacted on our behalf, we are blessed and elevated as Sons and daughters of the Most High. How can we not thank God for this indescribable gift on this day of the Feast of the Holy Eucharist? Lord Jesus, may all our deeds and words today be a gratuitous offering to you; that our lives progress as living Gospels to your praise and goodwill of mankind! Amen

31/05/24, 10:20 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He said to me’ Fear not, Daniel, for from the first day that you set your heart to understand and humbled yourself before your God, your words have been heard...’ (Daniel 10:12)

Daniel had a vision of ‘a great conflict.’ Having understood that it meant a period of prolonged suffering for the Israelites he prayed and fasted there in Babylon for three weeks. At the close of the third week, there appeared to him in another vision a glorious man, whose sight was frightening. Daniel is strengthened by this God-man, encouraged by the message from God. In fact, the moment Daniel had begun to pray in earnest, this messenger from God had been sent with God’s Word. But He was opposed by some other power that ruled the City of Babylon. It took him twenty-one days to prevail upon this power (an evil one) and reach out to Daniel with the Word that God is pleased with Him and willing to deliver His people – to fulfill His promises for them. However weak we are, dispirited and sinful, every prayer that emerges from a humble sincere soul, does touch the heart of God. God immediately listens to our prayers and wills to come to our aid. This must be our confidence when we approach God, that God alone wills to help, deliver, and make fruitful every endeavor of ours. If not today, over time, all those supplications we have been pouring out before the Lord, will find an answer to. As was the case of the Persian Demon that prevented God’s message from being cascaded to Daniel, every hindrance in our lives brought forth by evil will be cast out by Jesus. A prayer from a righteous heart has the power to negate all forces of the untoward and enforce peace. You and I are called to be peace-makers, not to wrestle with men. This we do, by surrendering ourselves and situations to the Lord. Jesus, Be our Light! Amen

01/06/24, 09:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Listen to me, You who pursue righteousness, you who seek the Lord: Look to the rock from which you were hewn, and to the quarry from which you were dug’ (Isaiah 51:1)

The prophet addresses not all of Israel but specifically those who are in pursuit of the truth – the Righteous. Those who are keen on following after the footsteps of the law, the Word of God, and those who desire to live His Word. These may not be successful, as they may seem to be direly distinct, not appealing to the good sense of the crowds, and may seem dull and unintelligible. But God promises them the joy and peace of a lifetime when they place their trust in the ‘Rock’ from which they were hewn, in the ‘quarry’ from which they were dug out. This Rock is Jesus, who changed the face of mankind pleasing to the Most High and This Quarry is the Word of life, that brought them to life from the curse/misery of sin. In no other does the Salvation of mankind rest, other than in the name of Jesus Christ and the power of His Cross. To all who wish to be filled with the love of God, Jesus is your way. He will lead you to the truth, you desired for all these years, to the communion of the ‘righteous’ before the Throne of Mercy. And ask of Jesus all that you desire, for He will open the doors of blessings, those that mankind has not seen, heard or even thought of. Because He is the beloved of His Father and so are you who are with Him. Amen

03/06/24, 08:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But I trust in you, O Lord; I say, You are my God. My times are in your hand’ (Psalm 31:14,15)

Just like the great King David, let us repose our confidence and our ways to the Lord. Our life’s journey may have encountered innumerable sorrows, there may have been years of sighing and dismay we have gone through, because of iniquities in us and in those around, our strength may have failed us, even our body may have been weighed down owing to the pressure of daily afflictions, those unbearable for it to support….Jesus was always there at our side, comforting and strengthening us each day. This King David was reminded, as he too went through the most debilitating circumstances in life. To those who are near to the Lord, there will be trials, so will there be comfort simultaneously, that they experience. And this Jesus referred to as the peace He gives, which the world can never offer, Even with all the possessions of this world intact, have men been able to obtain the real self-aggrandizement, they thought these material things would provide for them? God’s Word teaches us that every moment in life has been just a situation permitted by the Lord, to ensure we are His and we remain so. Therefore, however dark or indelible things may seem today, Jesus tells you – Do not worry! The One who holds the world in his arms, who sustains everything, knows what next and what best fits you today and tomorrow. Like St Paul let us admit, Jesus Christ has possessed me, therefore all my times are in His hands. Amen

‘Now to Him who is able to establish you in accordance with my Gospel, the message I proclaim about Jesus Christ, in keeping with the revelation of the mystery hidden for long ages….so that all the Gentiles might come to the obedience that comes from faith’ (Romans 16:25,26)

May God be praised and revered in all our deeds and works this day. Amen

05/06/24, 08:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Those whom I love, I reprove and discipline, so be zealous and repent’ (Revelations 3:19)

To the Church of Laodicea, the Lord opens His heart of love. His heart which was pierced for the sins and sacrileges of mankind, still throbs for the pitiest of sinners. However unworthy, wretched, blind, and naked they were in the Spirit, Jesus calls them to repentance. They need not do anything, but offer themselves to Him, so that they may be clothed with His righteousness and anointed with His oil. This message was not just to the Laodicean believers, but also to You and me. Those whom the Lord loves would need to undergo rigorous training that leads to the transformation of their lives and attitudes. This He permits not to punish them, but to retrieve them to their lost glory, to the purpose of their lives. Look into yourself, into the call that the Lord has called you to – Ask of Jesus, what He desires of you in life, what are the plans He has for you to undertake, that You alone can fulfil. Pray for the strength, drive and courage to fulfil this with zeal and commitment. There may be trials that drive one to lean on himself, his expediencies and traits, forfeiting the promptings of God’s Spirit within. Even when we fail, Jesus never fails in His commitment to us, so return to the Lord, be zealous and repent, and Permit Grace to outdo all that human fallacy has contrived. Let us thus walk today with the Lord, learn from Him to do those works that He inspires us to do with zeal and die hard commitment. Amen

06/06/24, 08:57 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He forgave us….by canceling the record of debt that stood against us with its legal demands. He set aside, nailing it to the Cross. He disarmed the rulers and authorities and put them to open shame, by triumphing over them in Him’ (Colossians 2:13-15)

Our Loving and Merciful God gifts us today with ‘the richness of understanding and knowledge of God’s mystery.’ This is where we have been asked to be rooted and built up in, to receive, to walk with, and to be established in faith – Jesus Christ. The reason has been so explicit that Jesus through His passion and death on the Cross, did what was totally beyond the imagination of any human being. He cleansed us from our transgressions and sins; canceled the record of debt that stood against us by bearing it all upon Himself and nailing it on to His Cross. The rulers and authorities (Spirits and dominions) of this world were thus disarmed, made a public spectacle of, and defeated by His Selfless love for us on the Cross. Death has power no more, nor does evil. Now that to us - this great mystery of the Crucifixion been unveiled, what more befitting can we offer the Lord in thanksgiving, than our lives rooted and established in Him. Paul exhorted all believers to be encouraged to live for Christ, to be united in love for one another and obtain the fullness of wisdom, Jesus for themselves. In this is one’s fullness in Spirituality, when One learns to unlearn what He has been taught by the world and blindly obey the Words that the Lord asks Him each day. Whoever we are, whatever our priorities in life, there can be no priority above ‘Jesus’ and without Him as the centre of our lives, the Spirit of the Lord in us testifies each day. Amen
