This secure home or place of refuge is the Sacred Heart of Jesus - which was pierced for the transgressions of all mankind. The ‘blood and water’ as was revealed to St Faustina were the signs of His mercy and grace towards us. I will dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, was what David resolved to do, as He knew of the surety of goodness and mercy that would overcome the unworthiness of His life. At times of turmoil, it would be wise to reflect on what the Ecclesiasticus concurs to us in chapter 2 – Think back to ancient generations and consider this: Has the Lord ever disappointed anyone who put his hope in Him? Has the Lord ever abandoned anyone who held Him in constant reverence? Has the Lord ignored anyone who prayed to Him? The author sums up his conviction in verse 11 (and this is what You and I are reminded of today) The Lord is kind and merciful, He forgives our sins and keeps us safe in times of trouble. Offer yourself, all that you value in life, your dear and near to the Lord and into His Sacred Heart, rest assured: You will know that Your home is safe; You will take stock of your property and find nothing amiss’ Amen

' And Simeon blessed them and said to Mary, his mother, Behold this child is appointed for the fall and rising of many in Israel, and for a sign that is opposed and a sword will pierce through your own soul also' (Luke 2:34-35)

The heart pierced by a sword, representative of the Immaculate Mother Mary is what we are called to ponder upon this day. The feast of the Immaculate Heart of Mother Mary follows the day of the feast of the Sacred Heart of our Lord. What set aflame the Lord's heart was His immense love for the world, this same love surmounted or overwhelmed His mother's heart. But what set her apart to be commended by the prophet Simeon in the temple was that this heart that was a Treasure trove for all mankind would be wounded for all its atrocities too. The Mother who accepted all circumstances in her life as God's intervention for mankind's redemption, had to bear the pain these inflicted on her dreams and aspirations as an individual. She was thus raised as a figure for veneration and emulation for all times. She who knew what it meant to sacrifice her wishes, would essentially know how to console you and me who approach her in our times of pain. She who endured every trial from the moment of Jesus's conception to the  point of His Cross, would be our sure refuge. Each day when we begin, understanding all the intricacies and complexities we would have to face, the Blessed Mother would be at our beck and call to help and raise us, the way she accompanied Jesus to fulfil His Divine purpose. Let us seek intercession from the Mother of Grace to straighten and strengthen our way today and ever. Amen

‘As it is written: What no eye has seen, what no ear has heard, and what no human mind has conceived – the things God has prepared for those who love Him’ (1 Corinthians 2:9)

And what are those things that God has prepared for those who love Him? The Spirit of the Lord reveals to us these things through the mouth of the prophets – He sets up our Salvation as walls and bulwarks (that no oppressor may intrude or infiltrate); He opens the Gates for the righteous to enter (His faithfulness that is experienced by the one who is pure in heart); In perfect peace, He sustains those whose minds are set upon Him (the inner peace that He instills in us even when things go haywire). And all our works were those that He had worked on our behalf (for the sake of His love for us); that the nations may know that we are His vineyard; The Lord its keeper, who nourishes and sustains it day and night. Would there be thorns and briers, He would battle them out and Jacob shall blossom and bear fruit. This is God’s message for you and me today – That when the Lord is foremost in our lives, we will be preserved as His cherished vineyard. Let us pray to be filled with the mind of Jesus Christ, to be led by His Spirit; to embrace the freedom that Jesus’s Spirit gives us. May this Word bear fruit in your lives, especially in the darkest corners of your mind. May you be renewed in the hope that comes from the Lord. Amen

    that you may take it into your hands;

    you have been the helper of the fatherless. (Psalm 10:14)

Contrary to the assumptions of men, that intellect and might would always prevail, God’s Word today reminds us that every act of man is subject to scrutiny by God. He notes the oppressor and his wrongs, and the vexation of helplessness reaches His ears. God is the helper of the orphaned. Whenever men abstain from fulfilling their responsibilities, the Lord steps in for the victim. More than what men would do, the favour of the Lord fulfills and upholds. If ever You and I have felt dejected by the ways of men, we are called to offer our supplications to the Lord, who waits to generously provide. That the Lord would give victory to His anointed, no matter how numerous were his adversaries and however mightier they seemed to be. We have a God who understands us so personally, who desires our victory and who has rewritten all those records of indebtedness that stood against us on His Cross. Through the Cross of Jesus, we have been redeemed, set apart for greater works that God intents to be fulfilled through us. Let us, therefore, strive to be led always by the Spirit of Christ, to be doers of the Word and not the ways of men. Wishing you a blessed Sunday. Amen

‘Maintain justice and do what is right, for my Salvation is close at hand and my righteousness will soon be revealed’ (Isaiah 56:1)

The righteousness of the Lord, His Salvation that girdles us with strength, that anoints one with gladness of heart, that wraps one in His righteousness is at work for You and me. This is not earned by one by way of his works, but conferred to the humble and upright who trust in His name. The pre-condition that God puts forth for man’s redemption is to maintain justice and do what is right to those given to our care. But justice has a deeper meaning when spoken from the mouth of the Lord. It would mean complying with God’s will and His character, rather than leaning on one’s intellectual inclination and reasoning. For what may seem right in one’s sight, may have a different version and perspective in another’s bend of mind. It is love and mercy that would need to outplay the limitations of laws, regulations, and the ways of the world. Today, Jesus reminds us of the triviality of the opinions of the crowds, to pick up the courage and conviction to think beyond and with the Lord. So, let us stop judging, but praying for those around us, offering them to the love of Jesus and rest assured, He will take care. The Salvation of the Lord and His righteousness that shines brighter than daylight is at hand for you and me. This is God’s message of love and hope for the new day. May You and I triumph in God’s Salvation, His love for us, surrendering ourselves for His greater works. Amen

18/06/24, 08:34 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘As a deer pants for flowing streams, so pants my soul for you, O God. My soul thirsts for God, for the Living God’ (Psalm 42:1,2)

I thought to myself why does this deer pant for flowing streams as it moves through the forest? And this was what the Spirit of the Lord caused me to understand – Just as You and I may have to put up with trials in life, temptations we would need to flee from, difficulties we would need to endure, the deer is chased by predators mightier than him. The animal would always need to be on high alert, defend itself from dangerous situations and therefore would pant for streams of flowing waters, where it expects to see animals of its kind. You and I are this deer that thirsts for the Living Waters that flow from the heart of the Lord – the Holy Spirit. It is here that we are filled, replenished, recharged and reset to take our next move in life’s onerous crossroads. The Lord is our stronghold, our protection and our powerhouse. Let us learn to lean on Him this day, asking from Him the power to quench all worldly deprivations in us and to gear up for today’s new tasks he desires us to take hands-on, to succeed with Him for His glory. 19/06/24, 08:22 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But You, our God are good and true. You are very patient. You govern everything in your mercy’ (Wisdom 15:1)

At times we err desperately in understanding ‘who’ God is and His true nature. It is this that leads and shuns us into darkness and the negativity that shrouds our minds. Whenever You and I are in low spirits, when things don’t seem right for us, when we tend to lose control of situations and people, it is then that the Lord wants us to recognize ‘Who’ we are and ‘Who’ He is to us. We have a God who is always good, faithful in His promises and the ‘One’ who always desires our ultimate good. To us there can be no human being as patient, as considerate and as merciful as Jesus is. Who can compare to your loving goodness, O Lord! The psalmist acclaimed of God’s steadfast love; that it is He who governs the Universe in His graciousness. After having described ‘God’s’ true nature, the author of Wisdom continues His prayer in Chapter 15, acknowledging his sinfulness, seeking forgiveness from God. ‘Even if we sin, we still belong to You, Lord. And we will not sin because You consider us as Your own. All perfection in our life stems from knowing You. May this be our prayer too this morning, Jesus, To know you is the beginning of transformation, the beginning of perfection in our life. Fill our minds, intellect and understanding with Your Wisdom and Your inspirations, that we may know what pleases you, refrain from sin and glorify Your Holy name. Amen

‘Pursue love, and earnestly desire the Spiritual gifts, especially that you may prophesy’ (1 Corinthians 14:1)

After having outlined in detail the varied traits of ‘love’, St Paul asks the Corinthian Believers (not just to them but to all who believe in Jesus) to follow after ‘love’. To experience Jesus would mean to experience His love, mercy and compassion. The fullness of all revelations, knowledge and gifts is in Jesus Christ. It is when one is filled with this love from Jesus, that one draws nearer to His purpose of life. You and I have been called to live in this world as His Chosen, those His Father has given to Him, as the branches of the Vine. So easily said, but hard to do, especially when strangled by the cares and worries of this world. Here again, through Psalm 4, the Lord comes to our comfort, that ‘more than what they have when their grain and wine abound, You have put joy in my heart’. This joy prepares us to desire and pray for the 9 Spiritual gifts - the gift of Wisdom, knowledge, faith, healing, to work miracles, to prophesy, to discern of Spirits, to speak in tongues and interpreting tongues. Without the love of the Lord in us, neither would we be able to receive these gifts given by the Holy Spirit, nor would we be able to desire for these. Spiritual gifts are to be spiritually discerned and not by the knowledge that the world has given us. Let us therefore humble ourselves, to open our hearts to receive from Jesus, to strive to be like Him, to do all things with greater love. Lord Jesus, stay with me this day, enlighten my mind, heart and Spirit to be Your Light in the world. Amen

Today’s Word from the Lord is that of strength and empowerment. Whenever we learn to be exultant in God, when we sing praises to His Holy name, we rejoice in Him, He comes down to our midst from His Sanctuary. The Sanctuary of God is a refuge for the Fatherless, the widow and the destitute – those who are oppressed and have none to heed to their cries. With abundance and joy, God fills the hearts of those who seek after Him. God rebukes the beasts, the Evil that exist in our midst that chokes and deprives us from enjoying sheer abundance. 23/06/24, 07:22 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For I am not aware of anything against myself, but I am not thereby acquitted. It is the Lord who judges me’ (1 Corinthians 4:4)

Faithfulness and Righteousness for a believer stem from the ‘pioneer’ and the crux of our faith – Jesus Christ. It is therefore that the examination of one’s conscience is not restricted merely to the realm of Moral and Natural laws alone, but to compliance with every Word that comes from the mouth of Jesus. The world has always marveled at the Gospel and its profound teachings which have touched every aspect of human living. As stewards to the Gospel of Christ, as followers of Christ, You and I have been set apart for an extraordinary way of life. In abiding with the ‘mind of Christ’ we express our allegiance to Jesus and His Gospel. Many times, the perspective of Jesus – His love and compassion, may prove contradictory to the usual ways of this world. One may have to deny himself, as Jesus once said of His disciples, pick up crosses (that go against our bend of mind), expose oneself to ridicule and scorn of the public (on specific pretexts) so that he may be acceptable to the Lord. It is thus that St Paul explains, ‘Not that he is aware of having transgressed a Divine Commandment, but that doesn’t prove him innocent either’; It is the Lord who is to justify him, condone and favour him. It doesn’t matter what one has learned or acquired over the years from life, but it is in simple surrender to Jesus and willingness to accept His choices that our faith and righteousness lies in. Let us, therefore, do everything today with the mind of Christ; that in everything we do and say, Let Christ be glorified. Amen

24/06/24, 08:41 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I will go before you and level exalted places, I will break in pieces the doors of bronze and cut through the bars of iron’ (Isaiah 45:2)

God promises His protection this new day to you and me, who keenly await His Word. The Lord will go before You, He will cast away all obstacles in our path, and level all winding roads, making them easily accessible for our success. Isaiah’s prophesy of old was meant for King Cyrus, God’s chosen one. He was instrumental in delivering the exiled Judeans from Babylon after having taken over the city and its fortifications in conquest. King Cyrus, who knew not of the God of Israel became God’s great witness before the nations. In the post-exilic era, the King is said to have proclaimed this Divine intervention in his life that which he experienced, which made him supernaturally powerful before the greatest empires of the times. This Word from the Lord is for our remembrance and strength today. When distraught by the torments and trials of the world, by hindrances and insecurities that render us timid, we must declare this Word of power to our circumstances and ourselves. That It is God who goes before us each day, with the intent of making our paths smooth. All that may emerge in the course of the day, that which oppresses He will not permit to overpower us. Cyrus, the King was victorious in fulfilling His God-given task of Israel’s deliverance, so would You and I emerge successful in all that we have been entrusted with. With Jesus at our side, we are more than conquerors. Stay with us Lord, through the day. Amen

24/06/24, 11:14 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Schedules in Indian Constitution

First Schedule of Indian Constitution	It contains the name of States and Union Territories
Territorial Jurisdiction of states is also included

Second Schedule of Indian Constitution	The provisions in relation to allowances, privileges, emoluments of:
President of India
Governors of Indian States
Speaker of Lok Sabha & Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha
Chairman of Rajya Sabha & Deputy Chairman of Rajya Sabha
Speaker and Deputy Speaker of Legislative Assemblies of Indian States
Chairman and Deputy Chairman of Legislative Councils of the Indian States
Supreme Court Judges
High Court Judges
Comptroller & Auditor General of India (CAG)
Third Schedule	It contains the forms of oath and affirmation for:
Union Ministers of India
Parliament Election Candidates
Members of Parliament (MPs)
Supreme Court Judges
Comptroller and Auditor General
State Ministers
State Legislature Elections’ Candidates
State Legislature Members
High Court Judges
Fourth Schedule	It contains the provisions in relation to the allocation of seats for States and Union Territories in the Rajya Sabha
Fifth Schedule	It contains provisions in relation to the administration and control of scheduled areas and scheduled tribes
Sixth Schedule	It contains provisions in relation to the administration of tribal areas in the states of Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram
Seventh Schedule	This schedule deals with the three legislative lists:
Union
State
Concurrent
Eighth Schedule	It deals with the 22 official languages recognized by the Constitution of India:
Assamese
Bengali
Bodo
Dogri (Dongri)
Gujarati
Hindi
Kannada
Kashmiri
Konkani
Mathili (Maithili)
Malayalam
Manipuri
Marathi
Nepali
Oriya
Punjabi
Sanskrit
Santhali
Sindhi
Tamil
Telugu
Urdu
Ninth Schedule	It deals with the state acts and regulations of that deal with land reforms and
abolition of the zamindari system. It also deals with the acts and regulations of the Parliament dealing with other matters.

Note:

1st Amendment Act 1951 added the Ninth Schedule to protect the laws included in it from judicial scrutiny on the ground of violation of fundamental rights.

However, in 2007, the Supreme Court ruled that the laws included in this schedule after

April 24, 1973, are now open to judicial review

Tenth Schedule	It contains provisions relating to disqualification of the members of Parliament and State Legislatures on the ground of
defection.

Note:

This schedule was added by the 52nd Amendment Act of 1985, also known as Anti-defection Law

Eleventh Schedule	It contains the provisions that specify the powers, authority and responsibilities of Panchayats. It has 29 matters.
Note:

This schedule was added

by the 73rd Amendment Act of 1992

Twelfth Schedule	It deals with the provisions that specify the powers, authority and responsibilities of Municipalities. It has 18 matters.
Note:

This schedule was added

by the 74th Amendment Act of 1992 <This message was edited>

25/06/24, 08:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And in that day his burden will depart from your shoulder, and his yoke from your neck, and the yoke will be broken because of the fat’ (Isaiah 10:27)

The Yoke here symbolizes the failures in one’s life, the fruitless toil, struggles and pain of a lifetime, that burden and dwindle his strength and confidence. The misery of mankind, his predisposition to hard labour, and the heavy yoke that is placed on the Sons of Adam is a curse that mankind bequeathed due to their disobedience in the Garden of Eden. But this is not all – the Book of Sirach offers us the remedy open to all men to lessen this burden imposed on them – by turning to the ‘Mother’ of all; the Second Eve, who redeemed mankind. Now placing the prophesy within its context; Isaiah spoke these Words of comfort to God’s people at a time when they bemoaned their ill fate and slavery under the Assyrian oppression.  It was their infidelity towards God and His Covenant that landed these people into a despicable situation under the Assyrians. However, the moment they turned away from their iniquities and ill ways, repented and placed their trust in God, He would work all things in their favour. He who in His wisdom did cause the people to succumb to oppression, would by all means come to rescue them, deliver and uphold them victorious. We have a God who created all things, reigns over the Universe and dictates the norms of nature. Therefore, however weak and overwhelming our problems in life may be, just one touch from the Lord, a favoured look from Him is all that would make a lifetime change. And this is the one thing, You and I would need to receive each day in life, from the Throne of Mercy. Let us entrust all our cares, worries and anxieties to the Most High, believing that He has the most viable solution to all perplexities of living. Because He is with us, the burden of evil, the yoke cast on us will no longer prevail on us. Amen

‘O Lord, You hear the desire of the afflicted; You will strengthen their heart; You will incline Your ear’ (Psalm 10:17)

God is in charge of the Universe; we must believe. Though the ways of the wicked may seem to have been condoned for long, though the unrighteous seem to prevail over the weak, God knows it all. God loves justice, but at the same time is compassionate to all. He understands the frailties of men and how they are so susceptible to sin. There is none righteous and so he gave us His only Son, Jesus – the most righteous of all. That through His sacrificial offering for all mankind and for all times, we may obtain the adornment of being children of God, of a Just Father. You and I are called to reminisce on God’s greatness – of His loving kindness to all, especially those who are sick, afflicted, oppressed and voiceless. The Lord is their stronghold, the only One whom all can trust at life’s agonizing moments. Let us pray for encouragement and strength to take on this new day. Let us offer our heartfelt prayers and supplications for ourselves and for those given to our care to Him, who sustains. Be assured that God would stand up for you at the loneliest moments of your life. He who has tasted what it meant to be rejected, despised and condemned is merciful to all who wallow in situations of pain. Thank you, Jesus, for being my strength, my encouragement, light and purpose for this day. May I glorify you today. Amen

28/06/24, 08:51 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘In the Spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children and the disobedient to the wisdom of the Just, to make ready for the Lord a people prepared’ (Luke 1:17)

St John, the Baptiser had a mission in life. Today’s Word from the Lord summarises his purpose of living on earth as to bring about a people prepared for the Lord. It was a highly inclusive group that included every section of society – the young, children, the elderly, and people from all walks of life. What God envisaged for mankind’s Salvation was always ‘inclusive’, ‘equalitarian’ and ‘just’. It called for the turning of hearts of parents towards their children in a way the Lord desired of; the repentance of children and their honoring their parents in a way emulative to all and glorious to God; the return of humility in place of all arrogance, hatred, divisiveness and selfish ambitions. A Just order in the world is what God envisioned about the human race, but what defied this marvelous plan of God – disobedience and lack of trustworthiness in men. When men began to figure out what they felt would suit them the best, to define their modus operandi to get things done, to imbibe a personality they felt was alluring and desirous to their hearts, they abandoned God’s purpose about themselves and invited numerous troubles, those unheeded of to anyone. What we often tend to forget is that our ways and thoughts are much lower than the ways and wisdom of the Lord. His vision about us and His plan for our well-being are above board and beyond compare. He sees us in our most perfect selves, unlike the way we look into ourselves or the way others think of us. If so, If the God of the Universe has such a distinct, remarkable plan for us, why not we adhere to his set of commandments, His way of love, and the truth that He has taught us – that apart from Him, we are nothing. May the Spirit of Jesus enlighten us today, with the vigour and power of Elijah, of the prophets, to put on the mind of Jesus Christ and do the will of God always, Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

29/06/24, 10:45 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘We pray very hard night and day that we may see you again so that we can supply whatever you still need for your faith’ (1 Thessalonians 3:10)

Today’s Word from the Lord imprints in our minds the two most essential elements that are decisive in Christian living. The need for ‘watchfulness’ by which our faith is affirmed and the need for ‘constant prayer.’ St Paul feared that his efforts in evangelizing the Thessalonians would go in vain because he could not remain with them for long; he was afflicted by several hindrances, persecutions, and sufferings (personally) that deterred him from revisiting the Thessalonians. What distressed him more was whether these people would be hard pressed to let go of faith, due to the numerous temptations from the tempter. For this, he prayed day and night, that these people may be strengthened in their faith to continue to live according to their call in Christ Jesus. They needed to be reminded of their specific call first of all and be duly supplied with encouragement to discipline themselves per the tenets of the Gospel preached to them. Paul’s sincere love for the people of God forced him to send his coworker Timothy to the Thessalonians, to support them. Timothy’s report about the Thessalonians was comforting and delightful to Paul. Paul attributes this fervence and zeal in the Thessalonians to his unceasing prayers for them. This revelation of Paul is a life-lesson for You and me. The kind of problems, challenges, and temptations that our current era, the society at large; our culture may impose upon us may be hazardous to faith, but Paul gives us the remedy to take on the crisis – the habit of prayer, that empowers one to defeat all strongholds, progress in faith and in one’s specific call. May the Spirit of Jesus inspire our hearts to pray, converse with the Lord, seek His support on daily challenges, to move ahead in life with a firm resolve. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

30/06/24, 07:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For freedom Christ has set us free; stand firm therefore, and do not submit again to a yoke of slavery’ (Galatians 5:1)

WE have been led to freedom through the One and only One sacrifice of Jesus Christ for us. He has ransomed us from the bondage of sin, of the evil, of tendencies of the flesh and here lies the crux of our liberty. This freedom to which You and I have been called is not to be used as an opportunity to satiate the desires of the flesh. And what are the desires of the flesh – that which is opposed to the Spirit of God – Idolatry, Sorcery, strife, enmity, jealousy, fits of anger, rivalries, dissensions, envy and so on…Those who do such things will disqualify themselves from the inheritance of God’s Kingdom. Jesus, the sum and substance of all Law and the Prophets, has given us the ‘New command’, that to love Your neighbour as yourself’. Indeed, the whole law has been fulfilled in this Word from Him. We have been called into the freedom as ‘Sons and daughters’ of the Kingdom to serve one another in love. It is not in abiding by the inclinations of the reason, intellect or mind that one is led by the Spirit of God, but by the knowledge that we are not our own – we were redeemed for a price, not to sin anymore, but to live holy and worthier lives in the way Jesus envisions our personalities to progress into. You and I have a perfect call, predestined before the foundations of the world, to be like Christ. May this Sunday bring in the rejuvenation of our Spirits, the awareness of who we are and what our purpose on earth is – To live for Christ and by His Spirit. Amen

01/07/24, 07:40 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He made known to us the mystery of His will according to His good pleasure, which He purposed in Christ’ (Ephesians 1:9)

In Christ Jesus, was revealed to us the greatest of all mysteries – that for all the earth, Salvation was planned and perfected by the Father to be fulfilled in His Son, Jesus. There is forgiveness of all sins through Jesus, through grace that flowed from the Cross where He sacrificed Himself as the Lamb of God. Through His blood mankind is redeemed and the fruits of this redemption was made available in the course of time and space of mankind, to all who believed in Him. And this was so destined that all creation in Heaven and on the earth may acknowledge, bow down and give honour to Jesus. Jesus is Your way, the only Truth for You and the abundance of life for you. What is therefore our thanksgiving, reciprocation for this gift of Jesus? To live a holy and blameless life before the Father. This would elevate us to the personality of Jesus, that for which we were created and predestined to transform into before the foundations of the earth. Behold, what love the Father has shown us, that we were made His Sons and daughters in Jesus. Lord, we thank you for the grace we receive each day through our love for Jesus, fill us with gifts that adorn the Heavenly realms, today to live our call. Amen

02/07/24, 09:09 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘To be ready for every good work, to speak evil of no one, to avoid quarreling, to be gentle, and to show perfect courtesy toward all people.’ (Titus 3:1-2)

To devote ourselves to good works – this is why we have been saved by Jesus. We who were once unworthy, because of the works of the flesh in us (disobedience, faithlessness, malice, envy, hatred, sinful passions, etc.) were redeemed, washed, regenerated, and renewed in the Holy Spirit through the love of the Lord. We have been justified by His Grace, not by our effort or desire, and have been made heirs of His Kingdom, replenished in the hope of ‘Eternity. After having received countless blessings from the Lord, what He asks of us is just this – To speak evil of no one, to avoid quarreling, to be gentle, to be courteous to others, to forebear patiently the shortcomings of others, to indulge in no worthless talk of genealogies, in creating dissensions or quarrels. This is the Gospel message that Jesus inspires You and me today – Do to others, what you desire others to do to you. How different and incredible a place to live in would the world turn out to be, if we did obey this commandment of the Lord. Let us sincerely yearn to love one another, be a bit more merciful to the other, and avoid divisions and discontentful talk among us. The Good Lord opens His heart to shower upon us His choicest blessings, those we never thought of asking from Him. May His Spirit inspire us to be a better version of our self today. Amen

03/07/24, 09:23 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘In all their affliction, He was afflicted, and the Angel of His presence saved them; in His love and in His pity, He redeemed them; He lifted them up and carried them all the days of old’ (Isaiah 63:9)

The Revised Standard Version of the Bible has penned down this Word from God as ‘It was no messenger or angel but His presence that saved them’. Jesus took to Himself all our afflictions. In understanding this mystery is our healing and deliverance; That we were at all times escorted by His mighty presence. In His love and mercy, we were carried through the days of struggles of old. No doubt, we did experience the most agonizing moments of life, there were times when we were all alone to deal with our deep wounds, confusion and distress. There were times when the fact that we had no shoulder to lean on to - did overtly impact our self-esteem and self-confidence. But He did cause us to overcome them all. He gave us a sure footing to stand up on, and protected us with His host of Angels, He was our rock and stronghold, the source of our strength when things around were in twilight. Jesus understood us so personally as no other; He knew the strongholds that deprived us of being ourselves and called the shots; till we were reclaimed to the Glory of Sons of the Kingdom. Let us thank the Lord for what we are today, ask Him for strength for another new beginning, keep to His commandments, and live lives worthy of the Glory He has bestowed on us. Even with all the gains of this world, without being able to see the Light in the Lord, how abrupt and meaningless would our lives turn out to be. Jesus, My Lord and My God be the power in me today. Amen

04/07/24, 09:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Therefore, while the promise of entering his rest is still open, let us take care that none of you should seem to have failed to reach it’ (Hebrews 4:1)

The promise of entering into His rest – ‘The Paradise’, a life of communion and joy with the Lord – is before us. But today’s Word discloses to us the reverse possibility, of failing to reach this coveted opportunity. We must thus acquaint ourselves with the truth of God’s Word, as to those factors that deny one from making himself capable of a glorious life. An unbelieving and hardened heart, one that wrestles with the gift of the Holy Spirit and thereby tests God. Those who believe in God, must essentially demonstrate the trust that God the Almighty exists and He will reward all those who seek Him. Without the fundamental trait of ‘trusting the Lord, ‘of waiting for the Lord’ one disqualifies himself from obtaining the promise of Eternity, that which has already been given us through Jesus. It is this growth in faith that Jesus looks upon us each day, as we call upon His name. So it doesn’t matter how intricate our life’s situations may seem as of today, how intense our struggles of the day may seem, how much men have approved of us as of today, what all matters is whether the Lord Jesus is pleased because of what we do and say. And this is what has the power to triumph the heart of the Lord, to persist Him into acting on our behalf – our faith and love for Him. What Abel could offer, what Enoch obtained, what Abraham set an example for, what Sarah was empowered for, what Noah was chosen to do; what the Israelites in the wilderness forgot to uphold – Let us imbibe in ourselves this tremendous indescribable power to change our world – our Faith in Lord Jesus and a thankful heart for all that He has done for our behalf. Let us make ourselves worthy of the rest that has been promised to us. Amen

05/07/24, 08:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For he says, In a favorable time I listened to you, and in a day of Salvation I have helped you. Behold now is the favorable time; Behold now is the Day of Salvation’ (2 Corinthians 6:2)

That favorable time and Day of Salvation is now for You and me, when we decide to accept Jesus as the Lord of our lives. The prophet Isaiah was consoled by God that all his prayers for the People of God were heard and accepted, they would be fulfilled in its due time. The due time was the Day of the Messiah, when He incarnated as man, took all the transgressions of men upon Himself and offered the most pleasing sacrifice on the Cross. Whenever this Sacrifice is reenacted anywhere in the world, grace descends upon mankind, overtaking all human shortcomings and renewing the face of the earth. Whoever is in Christ is a new Creation, St Paul reminds us of the immense power that permeates our Soul whenever we receive the Lord in true worthiness. Our worthiness is not our righteousness, but His mercy, our confession that we are what we are, because of Him who loved us. God who loved mankind so dearly, has heard the cries, supplications and ardent desires of every human heart, that He gave His Son for us. Through Him we are saved, redeemed, delivered and sanctified for those greater tasks of glory. If ever you feel tired of the life situations you are currently going through, hold on to Jesus, and ask of Him what He desires out of the situations He has put you into. The favorable time for your prayers to be answered is this day. Do not belittle the grace of God that has urged you into prayer, into conversing with the Lord. He will reveal to you the most hidden, unknown traits of your life, your Call - that which He can use for greater glory. Thank you Jesus. Amen

06/07/24, 08:50 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For whatever does not proceed from faith is sin’ (Romans 14:23)

Jesus Christ died for the sins of all mankind: This is the crux of our faith - that through this Divine mystery of His death and resurrection, everyone who believes in Him becomes an inheritor to the Kingdom of God. You and I have a master, our Lord, who upholds us all. To Him, human parameters of weakness, strength, wisdom, nobility and wealth are of lesser regard, what matters to Him is the openness of a man’s heart; his genuineness, humility and ability to love. Therefore, St Paul asks us ‘Who are we to judge the servant of another? Is it not before his own master that he stands or falls? And his master who is willing to strengthen him, will do all in His care to uphold him. Therefore, whoever we are, we have no right to evaluate another, for we all belong to the Lord whose kingdom is the fulfillment of righteousness, peace and joy in the Holy Spirit. This folly of ‘appraising another’ is one that we often than not, commit knowingly and unknowingly. This could be the reason why we have dwindled in our faith over time. Today, Jesus wants to let us know the need for us to change.  We who have been called to serve Jesus Christ in His body (The Church) and in Spirit (personal lives) are called to relent from this transgression of ours that distances us from the Lord. Some of these behavioural traits are so deep-rooted that they require healing and deliverance from above. And what is impossible for our Lord? Let us pray for the grace to change and prove more acceptable to the Lord. Amen

07/07/24, 08:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And I saw no temple in the city, for its temple is the Lord God the Almighty and the Lamb’ (Revelations 21:22)

The magnanimity of our God is that He is ‘Emmanuel’, a God who accompanies His people. To the Israel of Old, He was with them as the Pillar of Cloud and Fire, by day and night. Through the wilderness, He led them, they felt no dearth or want for their necessities even in the most adverse circumstances of their journey, and He gave to them the inheritance of a land flowing with milk and honey. There was a temple erected by these Chosen people symbolic of His presence and power with them. Whenever they raised their voices in prayer in this temple, they received support and deliverance. They were taught to depend on the Lord for not merely their provisions but also to overcome challenges, difficulties and tribulations in life. The new generations were not skilled in warfare, but their trust in God helped them prevail over their stronger opponents. Jesus envisioned the unveiling of a new Heaven and Earth, one that was distinct from that which existed. It was when the people of God worshipped God in Spirit and truth, to them would rain down graces unheard of. It is the predisposition of their hearts, the consecration of themselves that mattered to God, than any place of Divinity. Not to mean that institutions or places consecrated to God (Churches) were of lower significance, but that these could ignite the presence of the Holy Spirit in those who approached them with the sanctity of the heart. You and I are that temple of God, in which dwells God’s presence. It is when our hearts are enflamed with love (for God and man) that graces flow unceasingly into our lives and societies. It is not the place but into our hearts that the Lord looks to, that each time we partake in those Divine mysteries of His death and resurrection, we are renewed in our call and capability to be like Him. Then the Lord God the Almighty and the Lamb will reign in us. Amen

1. Early phase of Malayalam Language	1.1 Various theories: Origin from proto-Dravidian, Tamil, Sanskrit. 
1.2 Relation between Tamil and Malayalam: Six nayas of A. R. Rajarajavarma. 

1.3 Pattu School—Definition, Ramacharitam, later pattu works—Niranam works and Krishnagatha.

2. Linguistic features of	2.1 Manipravalam—definition. Language of early manipravala works—Champu, Sandesakavya, Chandrotsava, minor works. Later manipravala works—medieval Champu and Attakkatha. 
2.2 Folklore—Southern and Northern ballads, Mappila songs. 

2.3 Early Malayalam Prose—Bhashakautaliyam, Brahmandapuranam, Attaprakaram, Kramadipika and Nambiantamil.

3. Standardisation of Malayalam	3.1 Peculiarities of the language of Pana, Kilippattu and Tullal. 
3.2 Contributions of indigenous and European missionaries to Malayalam. 

3.3 Characteristics of contemporary Malayalam; Malayalam as administrative language. Language of scientific and technical literature—media language.

Section B: Literary History
4. Ancient and Medieval Literature	4.1 Pattu—Ramacharitam, Niranam Works and Krishnagatha. 
4.2 Manipravalam—early and medieval manipravala works including attakkatha and champu. 

4.3 Folk Literature. 

4.4 Kilippattu, Tullal and Mahakavya.

5. Modern Literature—Poetry	5.1 Venmani poets and contemporaries. 
5.2 The advent of Romanticism—Poetry of Kavitraya i.e., Asan,     Ulloor and Vallathol. 

5.3 Poetry after Kavitraya. 

5.4 Modernism in Malayalam Poetry.

6. Modern Literature—Prose	6.1 Drama. 
6.2 Novel. 

6.3 Short story. 

6.4 Biography, travelogue, essay and criticism.

UPSC Malayalam Literature Optional UPSC Syllabus For Paper 2
UPSC Malayalam Literature Paper 2 will test the candidates’ critical thinking skills and knowledge regarding the selected collection of Malayalam literature. Candidates will have to read all the assigned Malayalam literature texts properly to score good marks in the examination.

Section A
Unit 1	1.1 Ramacharitam—Patalam Kannassaramayanam—Balakandam first 25 stanzas.

Unit 2	2.1 Kumaran Asan—Chintavisthayaya Sita.

2.3 G. Sankara Kurup—Perunthachan.

Unit 3	3.1 O. N. V.—Bhumikkoru Charamagitam. 
3.2 Ayyappa Panicker—Kurukshetram. 

3.3 Akkittam—Pandatha Messanthi.

Section B

08/07/24, 09:02 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And raised us up with Him and seated us with Him in the Heavenly places in Christ Jesus, so that in the coming ages He might show the immeasurable riches of His Grace in kindness towards us in Christ Jesus’ (Ephesians 2:6,7)

What we have become in Christ Jesus is but the immeasurable riches of His Grace. We were elevated to ‘Sonship’ in Jesus, by God the Father, though we were dead in our trespasses. He gave us life – a dignified state of life. We have access to the Father in His Spirit; we are therefore no longer strangers and aliens, but fellow citizens with the Saints and members of God’s household. Here is where we identify ‘Who’ we are and ‘how we ought to conduct ourselves in our real identity?’ Going by the standards of this world would not work for us, for the protocols of this world are inapplicable to God’s Kingdom. The tenets of the Sermon on the Mount of Jesus are our abiding principles, the foundation we build up for generations to come. Today, God invites us to look into ourselves, to perceive how special we are in the eyes of God, of the uniqueness of roles we play in our world, those that were predestined in Jesus, before the foundations of the world – those good deeds to be fulfilled through our lives alone. In Jesus, each day, You and I are being built into a dwelling place for God by the Holy Spirit. Let us thank God for this indescribable gift (love and mercy) towards us, that which has seated us with Him in the Heavenly places through Jesus, so that we would learn to channelize this same mercy to those who seek of us. Amen

09/07/24, 09:08 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I have put my words in your mouth and covered you with the shadow of my hand – I who set the Heavens in place, who laid the foundations of the earth, and who say to Zion, ‘You are my people’ (Isaiah 51:16)

The Almighty God, the One who created the Heavens and laid the foundations of the earth assures His people today of His presence with them. It was during the Babylonian exile of Judea when they felt all was lost and nothing could be done anymore to retrieve their pride, their heritage, their kingdom, riches, their glorious name “God’s people”, everywhere around were setbacks and humiliations. It was then that Isaiah was sent to proclaim this message of comfort to these people. God reminds His people of ‘Where’ they were to look to, when they were evaded of ‘hope’, when reproach and reviling from men overwhelmed their hearts. It was to the rock that was hewn for them from times Eternal – Jesus Christ. ‘Righteousness’ and ‘Salvation’, He would bring forth, that none can induce into our troubled hearts. His Word will be our comfort, hope for revival and strength for pursuit forward. And as we read His Words, our Inner Being will be assured of the shadowing presence of our Lord with us. We are a people so privileged, possessed and treasured by Jesus, that He gave up His life to reclaim us from the bondages of sin and evil. Abraham who was but one when called by this Great God was blessed, and multiplied into a Nation, because of his trust in the One who called Him. God’s people are given the example of Abraham, who looked forward to the City with foundations built, architected, and established by God, and to Him was fulfilled all the promises God made to Him. So to us, Hope can never disappoint us because it has been poured into our hearts through the love of Jesus, the Holy Spirit given us. After all the present ordeal You and I are destined to go through in life, there is this shore of peace and joy that the Lord promises to us, not far from now. Let us wait for the Lord. Amen

10/07/24, 09:23 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Blessed is that slave whom His master will find at work when he arrives. …. From the one to whom much has been entrusted even more will be demanded’ (Luke 12:43-48)

The Lord was teaching His disciples the cost of discipleship. To whom, more has been entrusted from above, those whom He has called, those entrusted with authority, there is more that will be demanded from them. Jesus was not merely referring to the Service levels of His Church, but to every rank in society where authority has been conferred for serving people - it could be a family, an institution or any other. Authority comes with responsibilities and therefore would leave no scope for defiance, lacklustre attitude, indifference, or self-aggrandizement. As the Son of Man, came to serve and not to be served, to give up His life as a ransom for mankind, so would His followers need to do. Even with the most renowned, innovative set of management principles applied for administration (autocratic, democratic, transformational, Visionary, Inspirational, etc.) in the world, issues like poverty, scarcity of resources, inequalities – gender, economic and devastation of the ecology still persist as realities, because of the lack of the soothing touch of love. The work Jesus has entrusted to each of us, in our own vocations is founded on love, on compassion, on mercy and sincerity. There is no ‘one size fits all’ kind of approach that would apply when it comes to those entrusted to our care. At times even the type of subsidization (segregation for support) we create may turn meaningless to perpetrating growth unless approved and empowered by the Holy Spirit. Let us therefore ask the Lord to direct our words and deeds to be expressive of our faith in Christ through the signature of love for one another. This is what the Lord desires to see us at ‘work’ on, to be rewarded for Eternity. God bless you. Amen

11/07/24, 09:14 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Lord is righteous in all His ways and faithful in all He does’ (Psalm 145:17)

Righteousness and faithfulness are two Divine traits that the Holy Spirit inspires us to meditate upon this day. Our Lord is trustworthy in all that He has promised and faithful in all His works. He is the True Way to Life. And If you and I claim to be His followers, we are expected to portray the same Divine traits that characterized the Way of the Lord – Righteousness and faithfulness. How faithful we are does not merely depend upon our circumstances; our righteousness is not what we perceive following human standards of the conscience. The Bible tells us that all of us have fallen short of the Grace of God and therefore no one can claim to have attained perfection in the Divine sense of this term. The benchmarks set by God’s kingdom are much higher and sometimes seem impossible to the eyes of the world. But God has set these with all mercy and compassion. He upholds the one who is falling and raises the one who is discouraged from past failures; He gives each one what is due to Him as and when they call upon His name. When Jesus has asked us to be faithful as His Heavenly Father is, He meant what He said and we are called just to obey Him. To indulge in all our chores of the day with utmost diligence and commitment, to leave no stone unturned, to sow our seeds in peace, that we may reap a harvest of righteousness as God’s Word promises us. Let us pray for this grace to be righteous and faithful in all that we do and say today. Amen

12/07/24, 08:51 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He has raised up a horn of Salvation for us in the house of His servant David…..Salvation from our enemies, from the hand of all who hate us, to show mercy to our ancestors, to remember His holy Covenant, …to rescue us from the hand of our enemies and to enable us to serve Him’ (Luke 1:69-74)

God in His infinite wisdom predestined our Salvation; He chose the Family of David to execute this salvation into human history (time and space) through His only Son, Jesus. This was a massive plan, to encompass the defeat of our enemies, to deliver us from the hands of all who oppress us (who hate us), to rescue us from the hands of our enemies to serve God. The most likely question that may arise in our minds is ‘Who this enemy is?’ St Paul in his Letter to the Ephesians makes it clear that this enemy is not flesh and blood, but the rulers, authorities, and powers of the Dark, the Spiritual forces of Evil in the Heavenly realms. (Ephesians 6:12) Knowingly or unknowingly You and I slip into unheeded sufferings, afflictions and pain, all due to this enemy who prowls around looking for whom to devour and destroy. Where can our deliverance come from? Today’s Word from the Spirit of God enlightens us to look to ‘Jesus’, who was sent to reign in complete deliverance to mankind. On His Cross, He challenged and defeated the authority of the Prince of this world, who had till then enslaved mankind into death. Powers and authorities of the world were disarmed and made public ridicule on the Cross at Calvary. Through the Holy Spirit, the world was sensitized about sin, of righteousness and judgment, that the Ruler of this world has already been judged. Let this be an eye opener for you and me to call upon the name of ‘Jesus’ our horn of Salvation, the King of Kings, the Son of David and receive peace. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

13/07/24, 09:18 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Yes I will rejoice, for I know that through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ this will turn out for my deliverance’ (Philippians 1:19)

There was optimism in Paul’s words, He was joyful; not because he was free and expected to be liberated soon. He was in home arrest in Rome and thus his liberties were curtailed. What brought him joy was the strength, generosity, and faith of the Church of Philippi. The Philippians who were Gentile Christians accepted Paul’s message of the gospel preached to them with an open heart, were fervent in their love for Jesus, were a model community to other Churches – in matters of unity, their prayers, love for one another and cooperation in Paul’s evangelic mission to the other Churches and in their financial support to their counterparts in Jerusalem. The cue for us to imitate is this attitude with which the Philippians proclaimed the Gospel. The Spirit of Jesus reigned in their hearts, leading them to consistently pray for all intentions of theirs as well as those of the Church at large. The message is clear for us – the need to pray, interact and seek the Lord’s interference in all our needs and to be filled with His Holy Spirit each day. This will turn out for our deliverance, as Paul vouched through his appreciation for the Believers in Philippi. Wishing you a joyful Saturday with newer inspirations from the Lord. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
14/07/24, 07:30 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Note then, the kindness and the severity of God: severity toward those who have fallen, but God’s kindness to you, provided you continue in His kindness. Otherwise you too will be cut off’ (Romans 11:22)

It is the disposition of your mind that God looks to, (your criteria to receive God’s mercy depends on) whether you remember that you were grafted in an extraordinary way to seek nourishment from Him, the source of all life. That You and I were those clay jars into which the treasure of God’s kindness was poured into; that It was not our effort or good works that made us tenable to obtain God’s blessings from above. Often than not, we tend to judge others, belittle those in pain and affliction, because we take credit for all that has been ours till date. We fail to see the invisible Hands of the Almighty upon us, who has been benevolently supplying our needs irrespective of whether we deserved them through our goodness or not. It is to this sense of entitlement, our generation has become so accustomed to, that Jesus once denounced saying that ‘if you do not repent, you will also perish like them’. Let us therefore compassionately look at the people around us, We who have been benefactors of God’s kindness in its abundance must be ready to replicate the same when it comes to those around us, especially the blind, the lame, the crippled, (those unworthy of our kindness and unable to pay back to us). This is the mind of Christ that we have been called to put on – of understanding, of empathy, of willingness to pray for and forgive those who have distanced us from their hearts. For the sake of the love of the Lord, let us put on His garb of love, tearing off our old self, the inclinations to pay back as received… Jesus, bless us with this grace of humility and prayerfulness. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

15/07/24, 09:29 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘the faith and love that spring from the hope stored up for you in Heaven and about which you have already heard in the true message of the Gospel that has come to you….is bearing fruit and growing…. since the day you heard it and truly understood God’s grace’ (Colossians 1:5,6)

The Holy Spirit teaches us today through St Paul, of what perpetuates growth in the theological virtues in our lives. The ‘Word of God’ and the understanding of ‘God’s power’ are what sets the stage for the seeds of theological counsels to sprout in our hearts and to bear fruit. The Disciples of Jesus (You and me included) are commissioned to spread the Gospel because of this immense power that would work in the hearts of men. Even with the fullness of human intellect, wit and wisdom, none of us would be able to trigger as small a change that would better the world, (not even our worlds). Time and again, we have accepted our frailties and flaws as human beings; And through all these situations in life, the Lord was teaching us to trust in Him, His unfailing love for us and what he could do for us. We have erred not because we were illiterate or irrational, but because we failed to understand the power of God and His Word in our lives. Our fruitfulness has been unsatisfactory at times, not because we weren’t ardent or sincere, but because we merely leaned on our own abilities and limitations. Here the Colossian believers set a beautiful example of faith, a faith inspired by love at work. The Spirit of Jesus challenges us to a standard as this today; to test whether we have been witnesses to the Gospel, by leading lives pleasing to the Lord. To bear fruits of love, joy, and peace, You have been called today; let us offer ourselves to Jesus, to be taught what pleases Him the most. Amen

16/07/24, 09:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For behold, from now on generations will call me blessed’ (Luke 1:48)

Mary, the mother of Jesus extols God for His graciousness and mercy and prophesies in the fullness of the Holy Spirit. What was promised to Abraham, God fulfills in due time to His descendants (God’s people) through sending His Son to the earth. That Mary would become ‘Theotokos,’ ‘Mother of God’ was in this well-defined plan of God. But the redemptive plan was not one unanimously executed by God; Mary’s consent and cooperation were equally decisive. Mary’s willingness was based on her faith and trust in God that He would fulfill all the promises He had given to her. This faith was the cornerstone for Mary to be hailed ‘Blessed’ by generations. Today She stands as a sure source of strength and refuge for her children, You and me, who were given her at the foot of the Cross of Jesus. She who has undergone the pain, suffering, rejections and agony of a lifetime, knows all and is willing to support, strengthen and direct us to Jesus. Where no consolation remains, Mary our mother steps in to support us, where all is lost, she intercedes to her Son for restoration and when all is in the dark, She leads us to the Light. Let us thank the Lord for this gift of His Mother to us. Mary, Mother most Immaculate and loving, teach us to magnify the Lord in all circumstances of our life to emerge blessed as you. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

17/07/24, 09:18 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For this is the will of God, Your Sanctification’ (1 Thessalonians 4:3)

Human living is a vicious circle, the sum and substance of challenges, trials, successes, and joys that frequent themselves all through life’s span on the earth. The former two are those that all human beings would love to evade, having given a chance, but the latter are those that are closely linked to and consequential to the former two. It's undeniable that every struggle in life is but an opportunity or encounter with God, an unusual occasion to learn new things that we would never have otherwise learned.  This occurs once we admit our inadequacies, and surrender ourselves to be controlled by God, seeking His intervention and His will to rule over the problems that disturb us. The ultimate will of God is our sanctification, our being made perfect like Him; our obtaining the likeness of Jesus. It is towards the fulfillment of this will of God that our lives were created and oriented towards. What may seem mysterious to us is that neither we nor any around us really know of God’s great purpose for our lives. We aren’t even bothered about this great purpose for us, especially when all is well and when things work per our plans. It is when things go off-guard, that we are perturbed. We then make every conscious effort to examine ourselves in the light of the Gospel. Therefore, let us pray for the grace to seek after God’s will always and at all times. We pray Lord, Let Your Kingdom come and Your will be done in our lives. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
18/07/24, 08:51 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And let us not grow weary of doing good, for in due season we will reap if we do not give up’ (Galatians 6:9)

There are times in life when whatever we do would seem fruitless. Our words, however, sincere may seem ignored or even misunderstood by others, our labours burdensome and bouncing with no returns, even our prayers seem to have struck deaf ears and been unanswered by God for years. Negativity (having pursued us for years) does have the power to outwit our morale. It could impact every aspect of our living and thinking; confidence in ourselves, self-esteem, and the broadness of our outlook. More than all, it could invade through the Spiritual boundaries of one, turning him reckless towards God too. The apostle Paul is believed to have experienced the worst kind of persecutions – rejection from fellow Jews, physical assaults and punishment from authorities (for no wrong), having had to fight nature’s calamities, sleepless nights and endless toil through he was highly educated, conflicts within – anxiety about the Believers in Christ. With the Lord at hand, He was victorious in getting through all these that posed a severe toll on his well-being. He invites You and me today, to follow his example – not to be swayed by the things of this world, but to put on Christ and live a life pleasing to Him. We are sure to reap a great harvest, this is God’s promise for you today. Be strengthened in Jesus and do what He asks you to do. Amen

19/07/24, 09:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘None of the rulers of this age understood it, for if they had, they would not have crucified the Lord of Glory’ (1 Corinthians 2:8)

It was Divine wisdom that Jesus brought to man’s revelation – the mystery that was hidden for ages, which God the Father had destined that all men should receive. And those who received were glorified, esteemed as Sons of the Kingdom. While Moses led the people of Israel as a servant of God, all who received the Son of Man were made sons of His Kingdom. But to be a recipient to this priceless wisdom, God demanded a ‘purity’ of heart, a heart that was purged from all egos mankind is likely to submit to. The lust of the eyes, the desires of the flesh, and the pride of life, which comes not from above, but from the world (1 John 2:16-17) – Jesus referred to these as those longings that one needed to refrain from, to be cautious of, that which could drag one away from God’s will. It is because of this that all could not perceive the worth of what God had freely given through His Son, Jesus. If they had, they would not have permitted the crucifixion, the humiliating death of Jesus on the Cross. It was the will of God that took precedence over human wisdom, and in the course of history, Jesus fulfilled this master plan for our Salvation, by which we were ransomed from the clutches of the world. Therefore, we who have been powerfully redeemed for Eternity must dwell on things above – on things that would please the Lord, on obeying His Word, on making every effort to enhance the Light He has lit within us. Wishing you a delightful Friday. God bless you. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

20/07/24, 09:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘So whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them, for this is the Law and the Prophets’ (Matthew 7:12)

By ‘The Law and the Prophets’, Jesus meant the fullness of the Gospel truth. Jesus came to this world not to dilute the Law (given by God to Moses to His people) or the Words of the prophets, but to bring them to completion. The sum and substance of This Law that Jesus explained to His disciples as well as to the crowds was the single commandment to love God and to love one another. Through our trust in God, through pouring out our heart to Him (in prayer) we express our love for God and from this stems the love for another too. Without being faithful to God, we would never be able to be responsible to the people around us. And this St Paul explains by stating that we are called to a faith expressed in loving one another and providing for those in need (material or spiritual). That God loves a cheerful giver, who is obliged not by compulsion, but by sincere devotion to the Lord is what the rich Believers in Corinth were exhorted to do. It doesn’t just apply to material wealth, but to every aspect that defines human living – Jesus exhorts You and me to share a smile, be patient with those weak, comfort the afflicted and bear one another’s burdens, thereby fulfilling His law. Let this be our motto today – Whatever we wish others would do to us, let us attempt to be first in doing to them. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

20/07/24, 18:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Here are some points from a research paper on using chest X-rays for TB detection ¹:
- YOLOv7 model was employed in this research for object detection problem, resulting in higher mAP and more generalized outcomes.
- Annotated images of chest X-rays were used to train YOLO to recognize patterns of diseases.
- Deep-learning models were used to extract the region of interest from CXR images for improved classification performance.
- YOLO architecture was incorporated to learn and recognize patterns of various diseases and conditions.
- A total of 11,200 chest X-ray images were used in this research, including 5000 healthy cases, 5000 cases with diseases other than TB, and 1200 cases with TB manifestations.

21/07/24, 08:45 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Having gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, let us use them …. prophesy, service, exhortation, contributing generously, leading and doing acts of mercy….’ (Romans 12:6-8)

Let us examine ourselves in the Light of the ‘gifts’ the Holy Spirit has endowed us with; each being different according to the grace and functions given to us. These specific gifts enumerated in Romans 12 have been conferred (to members of the Church) with the prime purpose of building up the Body of Christ – you and me.  Gifts of Prophesy, serving one another, gift of teaching/exhortation, of contributing towards growth (materially) of others, leading people towards worship and good works, and engaging in acts of mercy (Corporal and Spiritual) being some of these, St Paul has drawn our attention to as believers. The exercise of these gifts is founded on the three Evangelical Counsels, the Theological gifts of faith, hope and love. With this background, the apostle exhorts us to respect one another and to outwit all evils that may usually emerge in communities as inevitable fruits of the flesh. In proportion to one’s faith to promote the gift of prophesying, to accept those endowed with the gift of prophecy, encourage the ones who elicit interest in serving the community, promote the teachers among us, those gifted with abundance to give freely in a spirit of thankful generosity, to lead with zeal. In short to perpetrate the spirit of love, inclusiveness, forgiveness and humility – through this, the Body of Christ is nourished and nurtured. Let us pray for the grace to cherish God’s love poured into our hearts through the hands of the people He has surrounded us. Amen

22/07/24, 09:02 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘In everything, set them an example by doing what is good. In your teaching show integrity, seriousness, and soundness of speech…...’ (Titus 2:7)

Every Community of Believers in Christ is to function as a domain of excellence – an example of doing good. Because it is from here that to all may be unveiled the glory of God, the Light of Christ that has the power to illumine the darkest areas of life. Paul’s advice to Titus, his close associate and supporter in spreading the Gospel throughout the Mediterranean land, was one of universal intent, a protocol that would help administer the healthy functioning and growth of a community. A Faith that is built upon Christ, a faith that is centered on love and integrity in relationships will stand the tests of time and culture. Over the years, it is the Spirit of God that has been leading the Church, sustaining it, and preparing it to take on newer onslaughts, paradigm shifts and doctrines posing contradiction to faith and living. Why Christ died for us on the Cross, the purpose of his sacrifice is often forgotten by mankind. Christ redeemed us so that we may be delivered from all that is lawless, unjust and crooked, that we may be set apart for Him, His own possession, chosen to do His works on earth. Therefore, we are called not to be conformed to this world, its ways and beliefs, but to be transformed in the light of the Gospel message, to a life of peace, that this world cannot give. Jesus challenges us to offer ourselves to Him, to adorn innocence and prudence as hallmarks of our character. Amen

23/07/24, 09:14 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Having loved His own who were in the world, He loved them to the end’ (John 13:1)

Jesus loved His Disciples to the end – John, the Gospel writer while attempting to narrate the events that led to Jesus’s passion and death, begins with a special mention of a core trait/characteristic of Jesus’s personality. Every occurrence that took place (as we read through the rest of John’s Gospel) was exacerbated in the light of the love Jesus had for mankind. To You and me today, Jesus reiterates the truth of His unfailing love for us. That ‘nothing’ would have existed in Creation, had it been disliked by God, the author of the Book of Wisdom explains this plainly to us in Wisdom 11:24. But then why was it that the ‘one’ whom Jesus loved (Judas Iscariot), deceive Him?  The Bible gives us an answer so explicit – It was the devil who persuaded Judas to betray Jesus. Into his heart, the evil one had sown seeds of deceit, that which sprung up into a well-conceived plan, a pan-social one, that intended on the one hand to throttle the Roman Empire (by initiating riots and ruckus through Jesus’s arrest) and on the other hand, that meant financial gain to build up the Empire of Jesus (which Judas thought would be established in Palestine). Judas was exceedingly tactful and wise, in the way of the world, and was way ahead of his counterparts (the other disciples) in supporting Jesus, but mistook the real intent of Jesus and His message. At times You and I purport this image of Judas, as we turn successful in all that we undertake and all that we do, but do we disappoint the Lord and the Way He has called us to follow? Are we willing to take the narrow road of love that is inclusive, which hurts and irritates, or do we find the broader road of convenience and compatibility more worthwhile? The choice is left to You and me. Amen

24/07/24, 07:27 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Until what he had said came to pass the Word of the Lord tested him’ (Psalm 105:19)

It wasn’t as easy as it seemed for Joseph (of the Old Testament) to have acquired the fulfilment of God’s promise in his life. The Word of God made known to him his life’s purpose through dreams, which was different from the rest of his kinsfolk. The genuineness of his character led him to share these dreams with his family, but all the more he was put to the test and had to suffer grief. Until the time destined (by God) for his take over, the Word of God tested him - of how trustworthy and forthright he was through the miserable situations that he had to encounter one after the other in life. Joseph’s story in the Bible was not an outlier, an unusual one that had occurred to him alone. We see a similar pattern in the lives of Moses, David and many of God’s anointed ones, who had to bear tremendous humiliation, challenges and knock-backs before they were elevated to their esteemed positions. You and I are called to persevere, be persistent in our prayer and supplications before the Almighty, regardless of the kind of tribulations we may have to endure in life. The Spirit of God reminds us that when the Lord intends to entrust you with a higher task; He tests your trustworthiness and stewardship qualities, more than your skills. It is when you have passed your test that you are ordained for the greater tasks. May you be encouraged to pray and persevere through times of trial, to obtain what the Lord has kept in store for you. Amen

25/07/24, 08:17 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘As for the rich in this present age, charge them not to be haughty, not to set their hopes on the uncertainty of riches, but on God, who richly provides us with everything to enjoy. They are to do good, to be rich in good works, to be generous and ready to share thus storing up treasure for themselves as a good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of that which is truly life’ (1 Timothy 6:17-18)

Today’s Word from the Lord reminds us that God entrusts a higher responsibility to those who are ‘rich’ in this present age. To do good, to be generous, ready to share with those in need and thus to claim for themselves a treasure for Eternity. Being utterly generous, as God’s Word exhorts us may at times prove difficult - firstly because we live in an imperfect world and are often gripped by the human tendency to seek recognition or approval from men around us. Secondly, to be overtly generous may seem unfeasible also when for all the years of tireless toil and faithfulness in living, we felt we were taken for granted or became victims of circumstances (over which we had no control). Thirdly, some of us are reminded of the pain and sufferings we endured in the past (when we were in need) when there was no one around who cared to offer even a helping hand to us (though they were capable of it). Jesus tells us that these were special occasions when the Lord was teaching us to look only on Him and to trust Him alone. These were accounted for, recorded and stored up as a sure foundation for our future. Jesus assures us that none of these should hinder us from doing good or offering to those who are in need, especially those given to our care. When He provides there will be an abundance, And to those whose hearts resemble His (compassionate as His), He provides even more. Jesus calls you to re-order your tasks this day, keeping Him first and the rest He will cause to come to pass for you. Wishing you a grand day with the Lord as your provider. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

26/07/24, 10:39 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For whatever was written in former days was written for our instruction, that through endurance and through the encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope’ (Romans 15:4)

It is God’s spirit that fills one with ‘hope’; He enlightens the mind with the strength to overcome all limitations and barriers imposed upon it – be it by people or circumstances. Our Creator knew the kind of intricacies and complexities we would have to deal with in our earthly existence. He therefore gave Jacob (His inheritance) the Laws and precepts (the Decalogue). By following these instructions, His people would be blessed to enjoy peace and prosperity in the land. But as time passed, mistrust and corruption swept through the given laws, when these were interpreted for convenience to suit the interests of those in power and authority. It came to pass as though ‘Man’ was created for the ‘Sabbath’ and not the Sabbath for man. It is then that God sent His Son, Jesus, who became the Word who incarnated in flesh, to restore, interpret and fulfill the intent of God’s Law and commandments. The Holy Spirit reminds us that what was written in former days was for our instruction and will continue to be so till the end times. For, what God purposed for Eternity, could not be improvised to cater to human civilization or advancements. How could human wisdom ever be compared to the Wisdom of God? Today as ever, the Word of God is powerful to seep through human minds, understand the dwellings of the heart, to transform the minds and thought processes of every man, no matter how defiant, belligerent, or indifferent. Ask the Lord Jesus, the Word made flesh, to fill your hearts with hope and boldness to pray for the ‘impossible’ in life – people and situations. Amen

27/07/24, 09:38 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I want you to be wise as to what is good and innocent as to what is evil’ (Romans 16:19)

We have got into believing and accepting that acquiring life skills would mean imbibing all kinds of knowledge without a basic distinction between good and evil. This has had its influence upon our parenting techniques and tips to the extent that often we teach our children ‘survival skills’ by compromising the most needed virtues of truthfulness, faithfulness, and sincerity. Development of one’s personality would mean learning to be more humane, by building up on one’s strengths, identifying the opportunities that knock at our doors not so frequently, and working on mitigating the risks/threats associated with our weaknesses. This awareness of oneself helps us to accumulate what is good and let go of that which hurts and degrades our being. It is Divine wisdom that supports ‘personality development’ and thus God’s Word reminds us that the fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom. The fear of the Lord distances us from sin – all circumstances, objects, people and cognition that justifies what is wrong. In this perspective, what we and what our posterity needs to be groomed in - is an inquisitiveness that is not distorted, that which abides by the order of nature, the Divine laws, rest assured, this knowledge will guide our consciences to choose the good over bad and triumph in all situations of trial. Jesus desires our growth and transformation into His likeness, the Wisdom of the Universe. Lord, grace us to perceive as you did, to choose good over even the slightest of evil. To be doors of Your Word, to rejoice in excellence, than in modestly defending any vices, for there can be only one absolute truth. Amen

28/07/24, 07:37 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Son of man, I have made you a watchman for the house of Israel’ (Ezekiel 3:17)

The priestly prophet Ezekiel (one among the exiles to Babylon) was lifted in his spirit to the realm of the Divine, from where God spoke to him. God reveals to him the reason why His people lost their protection and had to submit to the Babylonian defeat as slaves. He commissions Ezekiel as a watch person over them, to announce to the people the way out for redemption (what God desired of them). The Commandments, the Law and precepts were given through Moses to the people, that they may live long in the land of their inheritance (the promised land), that they may live in righteousness before God and multiply in all dimensions, as God had promised them. As an overseer who would watch over the people, Ezekiel was entrusted with a greater responsibility – to build up and not tear down. He was to remind the people of repentance, of making amends in their lives so that they could provision themselves to receive God’s grace. It was the will of God, that they would undergo punishment for their transgressions (for some time) as exiles, but it also was God’s desire to reclaim them to their lost glory. For this God paves the way by anointing Ezekiel as their guardian and guide to lead them. You and I likewise have been appointed guardians of our homes, institutions, communities and societies. We would be rewarded based on how well we responded to God’s will and prepared the people given to our care to receive God’s grace. Every authority under the sun comes from above, and every supportive gesture from our end (to their just instructions and commands) avails us of a reward from the Lord. Let us examine ourselves in the light of God’s Word, whether we are trustworthy stewards over the flock of God, or whether we use our authority to pacify our interests/ambitions in life. ‘To the faithful, I show myself faithful; to those with integrity, you show integrity’ – the Word from the Lord prompts us to action. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
