28/07/24, 12:44 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Inclusiveness 
Concept that promotes the involvement acceptance and equal participation of all individuals regardless of their backgrounds, identities and differences, abilities or perspectives. It emphasises an environment that embraces diversity differences and ensures that everyone feels valued
28/07/24, 12:46 - Megha Sherino: Here's a concept for a poster on social inclusiveness:

*Title:* "Embracing Our Differences, Celebrating Our Shared Humanity"

*Background:* A warm, gradient background that blends different colors, representing diversity and inclusivity.

*Central Image:* A group of people from various ages, abilities, cultures, and backgrounds gathered together, forming a circle or a mosaic. This image conveys unity and togetherness.

*Key Messages:*

- "Everyone belongs"
- "Diversity is strength"
- "Inclusion is the key to a brighter future"
- "Break down barriers, build bridges"

*Visual Elements:*

- A puzzle piece or a mosaic tile with a missing piece, symbolizing the importance of inclusivity
- A tree or a garden with diverse flowers, representing growth and harmony
- A pair of hands holding a globe, signifying global unity

*Color Scheme:*

- Vibrant colors like orange, green, and blue to represent diversity and energy
- Pastel colors like pink, yellow, and purple to convey warmth and inclusivity

*Fonts:*

- A bold, modern font for the title and headings
- A clean, legible font for the body text

*Additional Ideas:*

- Include quotes or testimonials from people who have experienced the benefits of social inclusiveness
- Use icons or graphics to highlight specific aspects of inclusivity, such as accessibility, cultural diversity, or LGBTQ+ rights
- Create a sense of movement or progression, symbolizing growth and forward thinking

Remember, the goal is to create a visually appealing and thought-provoking poster that inspires viewers to embrace social inclusiveness. Feel free to modify or add to this concept to suit your vision!
28/07/24, 12:46 - Megha Sherino: Here's a concept for a poster on social inclusiveness for companies:

*Title:* "Inclusion Drives Innovation"

*Background:* A professional, neutral background with a subtle texture or pattern, conveying a sense of workplace culture.

*Central Image:* A diverse group of employees from various departments, gathered around a table or in a collaborative setting, working together and sharing ideas.

*Key Messages:*

- "Foster a culture of belonging"
- "Diverse perspectives drive innovation"
- "Inclusion is everyone's responsibility"
- "Empower every voice, every talent"

*Visual Elements:*

- A puzzle piece or a gear, symbolizing how individual contributions fit into the bigger picture
- A lightbulb or an idea bubble, representing creativity and innovation
- A diverse range of icons or graphics, highlighting different aspects of inclusivity (e.g., accessibility, cultural diversity, LGBTQ+ rights)

*Color Scheme:*

- Company colors or a palette that aligns with the company's brand
- Accent colors like orange, green, or blue to represent diversity and energy

*Fonts:*

- A clean, professional font for the title and headings
- A legible, sans-serif font for the body text

*Additional Ideas:*

- Include quotes or testimonials from employees or leaders on the importance of inclusivity
- Highlight company initiatives or programs promoting diversity and inclusion
- Use simple, clear language to make the message accessible to all employees

This poster aims to encourage companies to prioritize social inclusiveness, showcasing its benefits for innovation, collaboration, and employee engagement. Feel free to modify or add to this concept to suit your company's specific needs and brand!

29/07/24, 09:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Fear not Daniel, for from the first day that you set your heart to understand and humbled yourself before your God, your words have been heard, and I have come because of your words’ (Daniel 10:12)

However learned and intelligent we may be, or perceive ourselves of being, there is always space for ‘Divine understanding’ and ‘wisdom’ in our hearts. And when understanding from above possesses us, we are transformed. It is into a heart, that offers itself to be moulded that this Divine intervention occurs. You and I can claim to have every worldly possession but still wallow in discontent and distress merely because we are devoid of the loving countenance of the Lord. We distance ourselves from the loving-kindness of the Lord when we are self-sufficient in ourselves, proud of our successes/achievements, take credit for all the graces showered upon us, or even deny appreciating the good in others, those whom the Lord loves as well. Jesus knows our every weakness and thus forgives us whenever we make a sincere comeback. But the latter can be hard unless we are able to free ourselves from the strongholds that tend to keep us captive in our wrong notions. This is what Jesus does for you and me, who thirst to love Him – He delivers us from all that prevents us from growing unto Him, the epitome of Wisdom. Deliverance is yours, O Lord, the psalmist prayed with this realization in heart. Therefore, the Holy Spirit inspires us today to set our hearts on what is firm and solid, that which can deliver us, set us free – the Rock – Jesus Christ. From the very first day we set our hearts to understand and humble ourselves before the Lord, every unanswered prayer will find its answer and every desire and hope unfulfilled will see its fulfillment. This is God’s lovely promise for you in Jesus. May your minds learn to unwrap from its comfort zone to garb that which is more valuable for its well-being. Amen

30/07/24, 08:59 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Behold we consider those Blessed who remained steadfast. You have heard of the steadfastness of Job, and you have seen the purpose of the Lord, how the Lord is compassionate and merciful’ (James 5:11)

He who is steadfast in the face of hardships is ‘blessed’ because his Creator, the Lord accompanies him. It is the Divine presence with him, that has endowed his spirit with courage to prevail over difficulties in life. God’s Word reminds us that He never permits the indomitable in our life, and whatever has come to pass is all that was meant for our wisdom and strengthening. The Book of Job is first among Wisdom books because it portrays the life of an ordinary person ‘Job’, who extra-ordinarily surpassed life’s deterring situations to the end, emerging successful and being approved by God. What God tried to bring out through the person of Job was wisdom for a lifetime (meant for us too), the conviction of God’s goodness, and unending compassion for those who suffer for no fault of theirs. Job’s example serves an emulative model for mankind of all times. Whenever the going gets tough in life, we must be persuaded to pray, even harder, with faith that God who has permitted the untoward in our life does have a purpose to be fulfilled in us. He desires that we reach out to Him to know what his plan is all about, and what He intends that we must do. Prayer which would mean an open conversation with Jesus, would help strengthen us to keep moving on in life. Jesus who suffered every kind of affliction before His Sacrifice on the Cross, understands where we come from and what bold decisions/game-changers we need to adopt in life to make our mark. Let us summon the Lord when in need to support us, the One who delights in our well-being, to whom no other could compare. And our future would be awe-inspiring, just as was the case of Job who was blessed immensely after all his trials. Amen

31/07/24, 08:41 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For if a law had been given that could give life, then righteousness would indeed be by the law’ (Galatians 3:21)

Until the offspring (the Messiah for mankind) should come, God gave His people the Law, that they may experience ‘reprieve,’ that they may know what transgressions were and the lifestyle God expected from them. The Law given through ‘Moses’ was not foolproof, as it couldn’t guarantee ‘life’ in abundance, it couldn’t transform the sinful state of mankind into its preternatural state of righteousness. God who loved His own, did not foresee putting in place a mediocre plan for their Salvation. Similar to what we as parents would ensure for our children, God having seen the intricacies and anomalies in the law, decided to make it simple and sweet for His children. By faith in His Son, Jesus Christ, He assured us the perfection that would qualify us for Eternity. Not by works (the list of all good works we do or portray) but by our simple love for the Messiah, our urge to obey His Commandment, we are sanctified, set apart and bequeathed an inheritance as Sons and daughters of the Kingdom. No more to continue as slaves to sin, or death, but to live glorified lives as Witnesses to Jesus – this is why God created us and loved us so much. We are called to life – life in its abundance, this Jesus promises us. Wishing you a bliss-filled day with the Lord. Amen

01/08/24, 08:50 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Therefore let us be grateful for receiving a kingdom that cannot be shaken, and thus let us offer to God acceptable worship, with reverence and awe, for our God is consuming fire’ (Hebrews 12:28,29)

To partake in a Kingdom that cannot be shaken, You and I have been chosen in Jesus Christ; to uphold a position that cannot be destroyed by any on earth – thieves or moth or whatever that eat into the treasures of this world, our God has made ourselves worthy in Jesus. The New Covenant founded on the blood of Jesus, speaks in favour of us as a mediator before the Father. We often take light of the gathering, the assembly of innumerable angels, first born in Heaven, the righteous Spirits that dwell in High places of Heaven and of the Almighty before whom we come each day to make our sacrifice with Jesus’s sacrifice at the Altar. The author of the Letter to the Hebrews explains how Jesus simplified all the intricacies of the Old Covenant - the inaccessibility to the Holy of Holies, the sound of the trumpet, blazing fire, the tempest – all that frightened God’s people of Old to approach Him for a blessing. We can be proud of this privilege to approach so awesome a God, because of the blood of Jesus shed once and for all in Eternity for our sins and for the sins of all. Each time we approach the precincts of the Sanctuary of the Church, You and I are to remember the price paid by the Lord for our redemption. How could we repay God for this gift of grace? How much more obedient to His Commandments must our lives reflect each day? Amen

02/08/24, 08:59 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘When I called, You answered me; You greatly emboldened me’ (Psalm 138:3)

Let us pray this morning for strength, for the reinforcement of our being. To take up all responsibilities of the day, to take challenges in a stride, to persevere through hardships and disappointments even if the going has been tough for years, with the confidence that God gives us today. The psalmist (David) thanked the Lord for His graciousness towards him, for His unfailing presence that made him great. He called the Lord, the source of his strength. Where do you and I seek the comfort of our souls – in people around us, in wealth, in places of authority or influence that tower over our weak existences? Had the Lord not permitted, none of these would have remained with us today. With all the awareness within, we still tend to ridicule those who lack possessions on earth. This hypocrisy in us, Jesus condemns, He reminds us that one’s life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions (Luke 12:15) Therefore, let us look into ourselves, pay more attention to God’s Word – to discern if our souls have been tainted by greed and covetousness, if so it has lost its strength to thrive in the disruptions and uncertainties of this world. Let us turn to Jesus for a recharge and for the strength to remain resilient in all situations of life. The incidents of natural furies, calamities and setbacks from nature remind us to return to our stronghold – the Lord, rather than question Him. To garner strength from Him is our everyday call. Amen

03/08/24, 08:44 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Then he who is physically uncircumcised but keeps the Law will condemn you who have the written code and circumcision but break the law’ (Romans 2:27)

We who by way of Baptism have become sons and daughters of the Kingdom….to whom the Commandment of Love has been given, who know God’s will and approve what is excellent (that which builds up and encourages), refrain from keeping His commandment, how much more do we present ourselves counter-witnesses to the Lord who loved us and died for us? Thus, St Paul explains this fundamental truth to believers that it is not the things outwardly, the bylaws of custom, tradition and living standards that mark us as worthy disciples, but those inwardly. Do we harbour in us the Spirit of love, the Spirit of encouragement, of unity, of mercy and compassion towards the one in sorrow and affliction? If not we have neither fulfilled the commandment of the Lord nor have we stored up treasures that portray richness in God’s sight. If the non-circumcision of his sons was considered a transgression (of Moses) by the Lord (Exodus 4:20), how more accountable would each one of us be held, to whom the Son of God was sent for our redemption? Let us ask Jesus to fill our hearts with His love and compassion, thereby leaving no room for the taunts of the corrupted flesh. In Jesus, we are blessed with everything, all gifts needed to lead a life of holiness in accordance with God’s will. Amen

04/08/24, 08:08 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For the Lord will have compassion on Jacob and will again choose Israel, and will set them in their own land, and sojourners will join them and will attach themselves to the house of Jacob’ (Isaiah 14:1)

It is the Lord alone who will have compassion on us – Jacob of the Old Testament and the New Israel, which consists of all believers in Jesus Christ. The Divine promise for Jacob’s Salvation was given to Abraham, to Isaac and Jacob, the patriarchs. This promise was of the coming of a Messiah, who would wipe out the tears from every eye, declare the captives free from their bondages, healing to the sick and ailing… good news to the poor, and of the hope of the Lord’s favour to all. To the exiled Israelites, this remembrance of deliverance from God was a respite - amid torments of fear and hopelessness that had grappled them. The prophet Isaiah was sent to declare this impending act of God on behalf of His people. And so did it occur to them, that they were brought back to their own land with increased faith and sense of joy. The message to you and me is that however grim the kind of problems we encounter in our own world, we must believe that it is the Lord and He alone who will deliver us, who will reset and restore us to where we desire to be. Not from the East or the West, would our promotion come from; But from the Lord a mighty, righteous yet merciful judge. So let us offer ourselves wholly to Jesus Christ, the Messiah, who delivers and saves; prioritize Him as foremost in life. For it is always better to trust the Lord than repose our confidence in any mortal, the Word of God reminds us. Amen

05/08/24, 09:22 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Lord makes firm the steps of the One who delights in him; though he may stumble, he will not fall, for the Lord upholds him with His hand’ (Psalm 37:23,24)

That which has adorned human beings with glory by the Creator is their superior intelligence. Among the primates, with whom we share similarities, our distinctiveness lies in our capability to think, articulate and act. Even with all his faculties and skills intact, this creation, man is susceptible to making mistakes, to falling into errors and failing too. Man who is limited by the metrics of the physical world – space and time, would also need to submit himself to the metrics of the ‘Other world’ – the Spiritual. There is a world more expansive and powerful, beyond the perception of the physical, the existence of which human beings fail to perceive, understand or even comprehend. However, vehemently we may oppose the existence of this world, the elements of this world are powerful in transforming our thought processes, ideologies and personalities either positively or negatively. Even among siblings who shared a common childhood experience, there would emerge stark differences in how each would respond (to situations in life as they crop up later). Going by the standards of our culture, It is only a matter of time for human beings to succeed or fail in life’s long course. What matters the most is the ability to be resilient. And this resilience is not something one acquires through physical training of the intellect or by endurance, but the power of the soul which is strengthened by His Creator. It is this that today’s Word from the Lord promises us – that our steps would be made firm, even amid tumults, turmoils, slander, persecution and grave insults…. Because the Lord is at our side, no one can cause our defeat. We may stumble but He will never cause us to fall, but uphold us more mercifully than yesteryears. Amen

06/08/24, 08:46 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘After six days Jesus took with Him Peter, James and John the brother of James, and led them up a high mountain by themselves. There He was transfigured before them…….’ (Matthew 17:1-2)

The incident of Jesus’s transfiguration as narrated by Matthew, Mark and Luke in their respective Gospel passages was one of its kind. It was experienced by only three of His disciples. Jesus took them up a high mountain, apart from their usual circumstances, which was where they could witness this glorious revelation. Peter took cue from this mystic experience (2 Peter 1:16-18) he was personally blessed to partake in to emphasize the Gospel truth, of the second coming of the Messiah in pomp and glory unexplainable (to judge the living and the dead). As we meditate on the Lord’s great mystery of transfiguration before the disciples, we are called to seek after the Lord for a similar experience in our lives. Let us pray to Him to bring in a commemorative ‘breakthrough’ in our lives. To stir us from our present state of inertia, to a state of dynamism, thrill and enthusiasm to love and serve Him wholly, we are in dire need of this touch of the Holy Spirit. Jesus who read closely the thoughts of his disciples, saw the fire in three of them to love and serve Him. To these who ardently wished to be with the Lord and learn from Him, He granted them supernatural revelations that were then too hard for them to comprehend. Let us also thirst for this experience of delighting in the Lord and allowing Him to lead our ways today. Always remember, You and I are called for something different and better than yesterday. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
07/08/24, 10:34 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Woe to him who builds his house by unjust gain, setting his nest on high to escape the clutches of ruin’ (Habakkuk 2:9)

Babylon was at the heights of its prowess and glory, when it defeated the Judeans way back in the 6th century BCE. The Jerusalem temple (with all its treasures) was looted, people were robbed of their possessions and taken captives to Babylon. How these exiles struggled to make a living in the foreign land and the kind of challenges and humiliations they had to put up with, we get to know from the Prophetic books in the Bible and from other historic accounts. Habakkuk the prophet was distinct from the rest of God’s prophets. He spoke to God of the wretched situations prevailing in his times in the Judean society (before its downfall). The people lived erroneously violating every commandment of God, they were disregardful to the instructions of the prophets of God. Nowhere in the Book of Habakkuk, we read of his admonishing the people for their misdeeds and unholy ways. Habakkuk questioned God for his inaction – for delaying to heed to the supplications of the oppressed and those wronged. God’s response to this question of Habakkuk is answered through the entire chapter 2 of this Book of the Bible. God affirms to Habakkuk that ‘his righteous will live by his faithfulness. The people of Judah would soon pay a heavy toll for their unfaithfulness to God – the invasion by Babylon. Not that the Babylonians were any better. Under the pretext of securing their households from future ruin (attacks from the enemy), the Babylonians had fortified their dwelling places, set them on high, by unjust wealth accumulated through extortion, misappropriation and exploiting the Judeans. They dragged themselves to be judged by the Sovereign of all – God, who was righteous and would avenge for iniquity of men. God had declared their impending downfall that was to occur through the hands of Cyrus. To You and me who strive each day to make a better future for our homes, we are called to be faithful, to abandon deceit, conceit and pride, to be more human. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
08/08/24, 09:41 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Return O my soul to your rest; for the Lord has dealt bountifully with you’ (Psalm 116:7)

Our souls obtain peace only when we learn to surrender ourselves before the greatness of God. The works of the Lord are mighty, beyond human comprehension and expectation at times. It is when the eyes of our hearts are opened to grasp the hidden mysteries, the very many deeds the Lord has done for us, the most subtle of them often ignored in daily living that one acquires an inquisitiveness to know about God and to love Him more. The fear of the Lord is the beginning of all wisdom. The Wisdom that equips us to live joyfully, to count our blessings, to be thankful and to be supportive to another person who may be reeling with a similar kind of problem in life (that which we have successfully overcome with the Lord’s presence). David, the Psalmist and King of Israel, gratefully remembers the Lord’s deeds towards Him, how his soul was delivered from the brink of death, how God wiped the tears that filled his eyes, how his feet were kept from stumbling headlong. From all these experiences he went through in life, David concluded that the Lord is merciful, righteous and the one who preserves the innocent of heart. Therefore, He deserved to be loved, to be worshipped and thanked before all men. The same must be our resolve for this day – that I live to love, serve and worship the Lord. Let my life be a testament to all mankind of the marvelous deeds the Lord has done in my life, everything that I have gone through to get me to where I am today. Glory to Jesus. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
09/08/24, 09:22 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘There are six things that the Lord hates, seven that are an abomination to him; haughty eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood, a heart that devises wicked plans, feet that make haste to run to evil, a false witness that breathes out lies, one who sows discord among brothers..’(Proverbs 6:16-19)

You and I who claim to be followers of Christ are to walk in ‘the Way’ He has shown us. All things may be permissible to us, especially when at the helm of affairs (those in authority) may find people and situations at their beck and call. But for a believer in Christ, irrespective of who he or she is, all things may not be ideal to conform to. Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewal of the mind so as to accept only what purports in perfection to the will of God – this was how the Roman Christians were exhorted to live as St Paul advised them. To live by faith and not by sight. And those who live by sight are those prompted to live according to the dictates of the flesh – haughtiness of the eyes, hands that shed blood (that defame or destroy another), a heart which is deceitful and engages in brooding on plans to destroy, feet that follow after sin, a lying tongue and the initiator of divisions in a family. All these are an abomination to the Lord and when these are committed the doer distances himself from God’s grace. Calamity would come upon such a one and he will be broken beyond healing – as the author of Proverbs describes the predicament of his misdeeds. Let us therefore take on this day with a pure heart, one sanctified with the Blood of the Lamb, Jesus Christ, who suffered outside the gate for each of us. Let us approach Him and ask forgiveness, a reparation for all our sins and transgressions – the one who endured all reproach for our redemption. He will help us renew ourselves in His Spirit of truth. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
10/08/24, 08:26 - Megha Sherino: 095130 25255
10/08/24, 09:22 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘So then, those who suffer according to God’s will should commit themselves to their faithful Creator and continue to do good’ (1 Peter 4:19)

Every situation in life is one ordained by God. God willed our creation, so we were privileged to partake with all created on earth. In God’s plan everything has its destined space, time and cause for occurrence. You and I were there in God’s mind, as a masterpiece by whom, great things were to be established on earth. Even our sufferings in life, have a well-defined place in God’s ultimate plan. From ‘nothing’ God made everything in its present state, therefore faith teaches us that even from the most unpleasant experiences in life, God wills a greater good. All things work for the good of those who love God – as Paul reminded believers, he was asking them to submit themselves wholly to the Lord. The One who has authority over distance, over time, over uncertainties – Our Lord Jesus, will turn around every affliction into a disposition to greater blessings. Therefore, we who go through the trials in life are called not to sit and sulk in pain, but to surrender ourselves to the Almighty, seeking intervention from Him. Lord Jesus, as we enter a new day in Your name, we offer everything of ours to you, fill us with the peace that You alone can provide, lessen the burdens of our hearts as we plough through the tiring phases of life’s journey, replenish our strength and power to focus on you, do good to all who may need our support and accomplish your will for us this day. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

‘I want you to know, brothers, that what has happened to me has really served to advance the Gospel’ (Philippians 1:12)

In all things, God works for the good of those who love Him, this message Paul was reiterating while he was in prison in Rome. The confinement (he was presently undergoing) was what God was making use of in spreading the good news to all believers. Not just for Paul, but for each one of us, the message holds true, that every situation in life, is an opportunity for the intervention of the supernatural. In the weakest of his circumstances, St Paul states that he would boast, because of God’s power becoming manifest more evidently in Him. Whether we accept it or not, God works tirelessly in drawing His beloved ones to Him. He makes use of all the occurrences in his/her life to teach him the undeniable principles of His Kingdom. Even adversaries who contended tooth and nail with us were those who were arrayed by the Lord before us that we may learn that He is our source of strength and encouragement. The times of need were those that confirmed to us that He was our provider for all times. And as often as we do, we question the Lord of these impulses, of why these were permitted abruptly into our lives, driving away the peace in us. That we may not store up for ourselves wrath, on that day of God’s righteous judgment (Romans 2:5), that we may be saved in Jesus, by a repentant heart that reflects the mercy and love of God to all men. This is why He has allowed us through fire and water, through times of trial and tribulation, that we may be doers of His Word and fulfill the will of the Father. Amen

13/08/24, 08:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Daniel said, “You have remembered me, O God, and have not forsaken those who love you” (Daniel 14:38)

We are in the arms of a loving God, who longs to be gracious to us. At times in life, we fail to lose sight of the invisible Hands of the Lord at work for us. This is when we easily give in to anger, frustration, and hopelessness, looking at our worlds and at the imperfections at hand. It may take a lifetime to understand the goodness of God; of the reason why the untoward was permitted in our lives or even to be thankful to Him. Daniel was specially graced to walk with God, to proclaim Him through his words and deeds as he served the Babylonian King. His integrity, wisdom, and wit which were outstanding endeared him to the rulers in Babylon. Nonetheless, he had to put up with hatred and professional jealousy of the courtiers who worked with him for the King. One such instance of him becoming a victim of circumstances (people plotting against him) was when he had killed the dragon that the Babylonians worshipped to prove to the King that it was merely a creature of imagination. Here even the King (who was Daniel’s companion) could do nothing to protect him, fearing unrest from his people. So Daniel found himself thrown into a den of lions, with absolutely no hopes for a comeback. God remembers him, not merely protects him from the ferocious beats, but also provides him food and drink by sending his prophet to him from a faraway land. Like Daniel who acknowledged God’s kindness and concern for him, let us also thank the Lord this day for all the blessings given in our lives, big and small, for the people who have shown us kindness, for the great protection we have experienced to this day. Thank you Jesus, Amen

14/08/24, 09:06 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘No one can receive anything except what has been given from Heaven’ (John 3:27)

Through John the Baptist, the Holy Spirit made known to all mankind, what determined success in a person’s life. What is given to You and me from above is where we are called to focus our attention and direct our efforts. Each of us is given gifts – diverse and varied, congenial to our vocation or purpose in life. It is the Lord Jesus who has endowed us with ‘grace’ (His) that manifests in skillfulness and versatility in us. Quite often we limit ourselves, by looking into the other and brooding over their blessings, envying them and thereby creating a divide in our communities and societies. This is what Jesus tried to explain through the parable of the Talents. The three servants were entrusted with five, two, and one talents by their master, according to their ability. The master was assured of their stewardship and faithfulness when he chose these servants with whom he entrusted his property. That, they would make use of their capabilities to enhance and guard the wealth entrusted to them was the expectation he had of them. To him, the one entrusted with merely ‘one’ talent could be shouldering a greater responsibility, as he could only possibly safeguard and enhance the talent given to him with his specific skillsets. It was here that the latter miserably failed to realize his worth, the potential his master saw in him. If our hearts are opened to understand how much cherished and valued we are before our Creator, there would be no reason for us to prove our significance before men, but we would always be motivated to seek the fullfilment of God’s will and His Will alone through this life. Amen

15/08/24, 07:32 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘but why am I so favored, that the mother of my Lord should come to me’ (Luke 1:43)

The presence of the Blessed Mother at Zachariah’s home stirred up the Holy Spirit in his family. To Elizabeth, was revealed the mystery that the Mother of God had come to her. When the Blessed Mother comes to our homes too, we are born again in the Holy Spirit. The Spirit of Jesus that is enflamed in us teaches us all things – it trains us in Divine wisdom, understanding and counsel to deal with situations in life; fills us with the power of endurance and resilience to take on with courage and conviction, life’s challenges and deterrents. It discerns the desires and needs of our person, intercedes for us in our Spirit and transforms every occurrence to one of growth and strengthening. It enlightens our minds and intellect to perceive as Jesus did, to realize God’s love and concern for us, how to combat the infernal spirits that keep us at bay with approaching our Saviour. It finally tells us that our enemies are not flesh and blood, but the Spirits that thrive in the Heavens, the Dominions, angels of darkness that constantly work for our downfall. It was the Blessed Mother who stood by the Cross of her Son, supporting Him spiritually in His mission of redemption. Thus death was destroyed, sin and its consequences were washed away, and God’s works were restored for all mankind on the earth (who was subjected to the destruction of evil). Love triumphed over every snare of wickedness and cruelty. The Blessed Mother who supported Jesus in His mission of Salvation, is the one You and I would need to fall back to in life’s distress and agony. She, the favoured of God, the Father has been endowed with supernatural powers to secure our success too. Let us ask this Mother of Jesus for a special favour or intention that we desire for our lives today. Holy Mary, Mother of God, pray for us sinners, now and at the hour of our death. Amen

16/08/24, 10:04 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘… My only aim is to finish the race and complete the task the Lord Jesus has given me – the task of testifying to the good news of God’s grace’ (Acts 20:24)

Paul received a great mission from the Lord, though late. He was commissioned to go and proclaim the good news to the Gentiles. It was not what he used to be or what he intended to do then, that qualified him for the position of apostleship; but God’s love and invitation to His Kingdom that secured him this call. The Spirit of Jesus transforms him over the years, bringing him face to face with hardships, persecution, imprisonments and misunderstandings to equip him with the power to take on his responsibility. Neither his call, the revelations given to him nor his charisms made his path easier, it was the realization of his helplessness and magnanimity of God’s grace that helped him make strides in every missionary journey of his. His trust in Jesus, obedience to His promptings, and conviction that his reward was a sure thing, helped him stay the course to the fulfilment of his goal in life. It was not that he was unaware of the persecutions that awaited him in every further step he took. Paul’s life (his witnessing to the Gospel) is awe-inspiring, an emulative model to one and all who love the Lord and desire to live in His light. You and I likewise have been assigned a task, one royal and fulfilling, that which the Lord has called us to take up. Even the one called to his vineyard in the 11th hour was honoured with a due reward, and so will we who have attempted to complete our race with dedication and goodwill. For unlike the ways of men, God who looks into the hearts of every man knows their every thought and longing. So why waste time and effort in pleasing men, but strive for God’s kingdom and for His will to be honored on earth. Amen

21/08/24, 08:52 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘This is how we know what love is: Jesus Christ laid down his life for us. And we ought to lay down our lives for our brothers and sisters.’ (1 John 3:16)

Jesus gave us the most sublime illustration of what true love is – by laying down his life for our redemption. We were purchased from the slavery of sin and death by the price of His blood. If so, we owe a greater responsibility to reclaim the souls of those given to our care. Its so easy to love another who reciprocates our gesture, but quite hard when it comes to people whom we know take us for granted or make light of our sincerity. And the latter is the kind of love the Lord showed us. This is what laying down of one’s life for another would mean in its broadest sense. To be able to consider even the mean (the crippled, blind, lame and poor) who are unable to pay us back for what we do or say to them in love. It is the reward at the resurrection of the righteous that Jesus promises to those who have denied themselves to obey His commandment of love. After all, the toil and hardships of this world are not merely for burdening ourselves, but because in these we perceive for ourselves the hope of being with the Lord at His right side at the great day of His Second coming. To be privileged to banquet with Him in His kingdom with all saints and martyrs. The third - to receive from Him all that we ask for in prayer, because He delights in the welfare of His beloved. This is how we test ourselves whether we live by faith – If Jesus is in us, we will have all the reasons to love and be compassionate to one another, even if it defies every standard/limit/dictates of our modern culture/era we belong to. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

22/08/24, 10:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Salt is good, but if the salt has lost its saltiness, how will you make it salty again? Have salt in yourselves and be at peace with one another’ (Mark 9:50)

Saltiness has been interpreted as goodness, virtue, the positivity in relationships that keep people committed to their purpose in life. It can at times be interpreted to mean the sense of ‘pride’, ‘dignity of self’ that ultimately drives one ahead in his journey for contentment in life. Even for those living in abundance and extravagance (material possessions), Contentment may seem dubious or rather illogical; as they would never be able to reckon with (accept) an adverse situation of crisis and dearth. Each one of us is unique; our needs vary, and even the comfort zones we leave for ourselves may seem strange for another to understand. It is when our emotional boundaries get encroached upon by another that we find ourselves deprived of peace within. This saltiness (sense of contentment) can never be restored from things external; nor is the self ‘in itself’ efficacious to replenish its lost content. It is when the Hands of the Creator intervene in our lives, that saltiness in its right proportions is restored to our beings. How will you and I make for the lost saltiness in ourselves? By being recharged from above – Let us pray for the grace of contentment, of being the light for ourselves while also showing the light to another. Lead Kindly Light and let my inner being be kindled with your fire! Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

23/08/24, 09:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You will be enriched in every way so that you can be generous on every occasion, and through us your generosity will result in thanksgiving to God’ (2 Corinthians 9:11)

Today God’s Word draws our attention to the ‘spirit’ or ‘motive’ that should prompt one towards giving. St Paul explains this by stating that God loves a cheerful giver, one who shares from an inner sense of having received everything freely from above. Not because there wasn’t an effort on his/her part (of toil, perspiration), but because of his conviction, deep within that ‘had it not been for the mercy of the Lord’, his/her fate would have been the other extreme. Generosity has often been annotated to free giving/sharing of wealth and possessions. Gains and returns on investment may be decisive factors that, at times even determine the way money needs to be used for charity too. The generosity that the Gospel hints at is one that is radical, that which encompasses every aspect of human living even the most subtle ones. Apart from wealth as a resource for sharing, it covers ‘time’, ‘compassion’, ‘the ability’ to consider another, to reach out to another or even to support another going through stressful times in life……as insightful ways of expressing generosity. It is these dimensions of generosity that transcend the clouds and reach the Heavens as thanksgiving and praise to the Most High. When the Lord sent His disciples to share the message of the good news, he exhorted them to give freely to all whom they encountered, what they had received freely. Likewise, those whom He entrusts today with power, authority or wealth, are called to be His witnesses in every way – in faith, in speech, in knowledge of Christ, eagerness to love and share – into a lifestyle of generosity.  Jesus calls You and me personally to be His witnesses – to be like Him and share His Light to all in the dark and to be messengers of hope in a world so imperfect and infiltrated by hate. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

24/08/24, 11:02 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Let me hear joy and gladness, let the bones that you have broken rejoice’ (Psalm 51:8)

The roots of good, evil, life and death take origin in one’s mind, the consequences of which are experienced in the body. A wholesome mind is a medicine to the soul and the body. The power of the subconscious mind, a mystery to Science and Psychology has helped people retain their calm and composure and relapse to normal living, even when afflicted by untold sufferings. It is sin and transgression that has created so much unrest and bewilderment in our world, thereby characterizing it as an Imperfect one. Sin disrupted the harmony and rhythm of the human mind and soul, thereby interfering with the otherwise healthy functioning of the body. But God in all His wisdom, tried to convince mankind that where sin abounded, grace overflowed even more. Man was and is always incapable of restoring himself, or even protecting himself from the influences of the evil. The Son of God came that mankind may be re-established – Through His untold sufferings on the Cross, Jesus claimed for us a new beginning, a new heart, soul and Spirit – that we may rejoice and be grateful to God for His great deeds on our behalf. Without God knowing, nothing has transpired in our life, and without his tacit consent, there has been no untoward in our life. All that He has caused to occur in our life’s journey was in synch with His great plans for our welfare. This David, the Psalmist believed and proclaimed though he was overwhelmed by guilt, shame and the burden of his sinfulness. Let us therefore, admit our inadequacies, triviality and frivolousness before our Lord, who knows us and wills to redeem us. Let us pray to the Lord for greater joy and strength in our body and soul, to be a Blessing to those around us. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
25/08/24, 08:54 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I have told you these things, so that in me you may have peace. In this world you will have trouble. But take heart! I have overcome the world’ (John 16:33)

Jesus foreseeing His passion, death and resurrection, was making known to them the truth. The truth that it is in Him alone that they would find peace. This message was not meant merely for His twelve apostles whom He chose to be with Him in Palestine; but to each one of us who choose to follow Him today. To mankind who find themselves tossed and swayed by the twists and swirls of daily living, Jesus’s message comes as a pacifier. Not that there would be troubles no more in life, but what Jesus referred to was that His presence and the awareness of it in us would be our source of encouragement and strength. Our purpose in life can never change, nor would the roadblocks on the path be ever smoother. But without power from above, at times to move forward with the burdens of living may seem a herculean task. There are tasks that we are obliged by virtue of our vocation to fulfill and those that situations or societal influences may oblige us to do. A follower of Jesus is always called to deny himself, carry his cross to follow Him. He who has in all sincerity accomplished the will of His Father, going through the humiliation, insult, agony and pain, knows well in what way we would need to be strengthened to make our move. Jesus gives us His assurance that with Him, we would finally come up victorious. And this is the peace that would rule our hearts, in all the troubles of every day living. God bless you. Amen

26/08/24, 10:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Jerusalem, be confident! The One who named you will comfort you.’ (Baruch 4:30)

God reassured the Exiled Judeans that their time of redemption was at hand. It was not for their destruction that God permitted their suffering in a foreign land. It was their misdeeds that brought them the misfortune. To them was designated an interval for prayer, penance, and reparation – they were to patiently endure the trials under a foreign regime. This prophetic exhortation from Baruch, the assistant of Jeremiah, was a whirl of comfort to God’s people, who reeled under stress, guilt, hopelessness and distress. They believed in the words of the prophet, and received the deliverance that was promised to them. There was a glorious comeback for these people, because they accepted their shortcomings, mended their stubborn ways, and reposed confidence in the One who was powerful to recover them from the pit of desolation. At times when the roads we trod become rough, peace and joy may seem to have evaded us for long, God’s Word reminds us to keep patiently enduring the path with the Lord. We have a merciful God, who rejoices at our welfare, that He wouldn’t bear to see us suffer for long. In their desolateness, what the Israelites did, is what we are called to emulate – to Call upon the Lord for help. When only the Lord revives, are You and I strengthened, when He delivers there is perfect freedom in the mind and in Spirit. Let us therefore be confident in Jesus alone, letting go of the other strongholds that we have placed our trust to. He who has destined our purposes will never let us fail, however ‘bleak’ our chances for recovery may seem to the world at present. In the eyes of Jesus, You and I are honored and impeccably superb. Amen

09/09/24, 09:13 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Let us discern for ourselves what is right and let us learn together what is good’ (Job 34:4)

It is the Word of God who teaches us what is right and good. To discern and choose righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance, and gentleness, over that which is shallow - that leads to strife, envy, malicious talk, evil suspicions, and constant friction between men is our call. We have been called to inherit the eternal (what we often tend to forget). Jesus rightly said that ‘because’ we are ignorant of the ‘Power of God’ and of ‘His Word’, we end up doing the same things that are shallow and conflict with our call. The Word of God, Jesus dwells in us, and within us is the great power of God. Therefore, to prevail in this constant combat that involves our ‘self’ with the ‘Other world’, we are called to seek strength from within. Though we live as humans, our weapons of warfare are not of this world – St Paul explained to the Corinthian believers. And these weapons are ‘the full set of armour of God’ (Ephesians 6:11-17) – The belt of truth, the breastplate of righteousness, the footwear of peace that the Gospel reminds us, the shield of faith, wearing the helmet of Salvation (against the strongholds of the evil), and the Sword of the Holy Spirit which is the Word of God. Let us ask the Lord for the grace and wisdom to strengthen ourselves with these powerful instruments He has freely given us. Wishing you a productive and powerful week ahead with Jesus at your side. Amen

10/09/24, 09:53 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘When one of you has a grievance against another, does he dare go to law before the unrighteous instead of the saints’ (1 Corinthians 6:1)

Paul’s concern for the believers in Corinth was that they took up their disputes before Civil courts or before unbelievers for an appropriate decision to be made, rather than approaching those authorized by the Church to direct God’s people. This situation is no different even today among Christ’s followers, but even more grim, when at times, one finds himself lurking in the dark for a just outcome for some of life’s daunting questions or issues. What could have so easily been settled, resolved/finalized, and concluded with a compassionate forgiving ‘inclusive’ attitude, is that which is often being dragged for reasons so frivolous. The kind of excuses (formalities and procedural mandates) we put across the table when it comes to matters like new registration in parishes of faithful believers or being part of faith communities could be one of form over substance. Often Canon 518 is overlooked on flimsy grounds - due to the ignorance of the public or merely to create a situation of public approval/disapproval to some or all. The Lord who stood with the outcasts, the poor, the rejected, and the marginalised, would never have approved of such a situation of dissent and division among men. Jesus went after the sick, the sinner, the possessed and vulnerable, knowing that He had come to deliver, to restore, to replenish, and redeem for eternity. Do we have any better reason to exterminate, exclude or divide the body of Christ? Amen

11/09/24, 09:57 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Then the kingdom of Heaven will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went to meet the bridegroom’ (Matthew 25:1)

In the previous chapter of the Gospel of Matthew, Jesus taught his disciples the signs of the end times, how unexpected his second coming would be, and the need to be always prepared as believers. Thus, he continued with another parable – The parable of the Ten Virgins. These ten were equally invited to the grand wedding feast of the bridegroom. The event was sure to happen, but at what time, none could tell. This is why Jesus explained metaphorically in chapter 24 that ‘If’ the master of the house had known at what time the thief would be coming, he would have stayed awake and not let his house be broken into. Similar was the situation of Jesus’s second coming, for when it occurs, each would be judged according to his state of action. The readiness of a person would mean his tenacity to admit, accept, and correct the flaws of daily living. In His wisdom, God knew the frailty and insecurity in mankind, that they were so prone to temptation and sin. Even the little good that they could think of was but the Divine inspiration working in them. Therefore man needed checks and balances in life to direct him smoothly towards his destination. The lamps the virgins held were these signposts – ‘the Sacraments’ that help man to stay connected with God even in the dark. But these were to be polished, put to recurrent usage, imbibed with a heart of content and ‘preparedness’ so that even in the most unwitting of life’s circumstances these could be our saving light. The Sacraments are visible signs of God’s invisible grace overshadowing our lives. But when these become a ‘grooved in’ schedule in daily life, in the absence of ‘faith’(the fire that consumes our living offerings before the Lord), we fail to assimilate the grace that is poured through each encounter with the Lord. We become like those foolish virgins (five of them) who failed to carry flasks of oil needed to light the lamp of their lives, which proved fatal and depressing.  Today, Jesus tells you and me ‘Be dressed for action and keep your lamps lit always’ Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

12/09/24, 08:52 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘For everything God created is good, and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving, because it is consecrated by the Word of God and prayer’ (1 Timothy 4:4-5)

Everything has a place and purpose in the Universe that God created. Even those that we believe are undesirable have a destiny to fulfill in God’s master plan for mankind. It may at times seem incomprehensible to us (who attempt to make sense of things based on the present) to foresee a blessing in something that appeared to us a curse, agony or hurtful. But for God to whom all things are possible, it’s only a matter of His will to extract the hidden good from the evil. And when we seek His will, offer our prayers, our intentions, and dreams in compliance with His will, how can God prevent us from enjoying this treasure of good? Through today’s Word of exhortation that Paul made to his disciple Timothy, Paul was emphasizing the need to offer our prayers and supplications with a sense of thanksgiving always. For then, our prayers become anointed, sanctified by the Holy Spirit, and affirmed by the Word of God. Through the power of the Holy Spirit in work within us, and through the affirmation of Jesus, the Word, all our prayers become acceptable to the Father, who would ensure the fruition of all that we have asked for. Therefore, keep asking for things that You have forgotten (those that our intellect has sealed as impossible), seek for things that your heart cherished for years, and knock at the door of the ‘Throne of Mercy’ of Jesus, who waits to favor you and turn things for the greater good. Thank you Jesus! Amen

13/09/24, 09:18 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘I fed you with milk, not solid food, for you were not ready for it. And even now you are not yet ready (1 Corinthians 3:2)

Tests and turmoils are at times permitted in our lives; These help us evaluate our preparedness to take on the next phase in our lives. Often than not, You and I have failed these tests (not because we weren’t vigilant), or we somehow managed to gear down and keep moving ahead on our path because we knew we had to put up with life, however rough the road ahead was. But one thing we failed to do was to admit to the Lord that we knew not what to do; that our instincts were feeble, that what we needed then from Him was not just ‘support’, but wholesomeness. Jesus, who knows us so personally is fully aware of our need for wholesomeness. It is then better to approach Him with our troubled hearts and wait in silence. Being still and patient before the Almighty is perhaps the greatest gift a man could claim to possess, that would help him wade through the maze and find his way. For we are always prone to be led by the flesh, by this world and, by our convictions. To be able to outdo these persuasions is growth – It is God who gives us this growth - to withstand the towering demands of society, the pinning opinions of the crowds, to set our hearts and minds undivided in Christ alone, to be led by the inspirations of His Spirit, even at the cost of obliqueness and obscurity, to uphold one’s principles even when we have no shoulder to lean on, here is how He prepares us for the next. Let us pray for this grace of resilience, preparedness, and growth in life, to be established by the Lord and be able to fulfill all the purposes of our life. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

14/09/24, 09:46 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And now bless the God of All, who everywhere works great wonders, who exalts our days from birth and deals with us according to His mercy’ (Sirach 50:22)

After having portrayed the glory and acclaim of Simon, the High Priest – He was a man filled with wisdom from above, a man with a pure heart who knew what it meant to discern between the consecrated and the ordinary. His efforts in repairing the House of God, perfecting, sanctifying, and fortifying it for God’s people, laid the foundations for generations of Israel. By setting an emulative example to the people, He blessed God’s people thus, reminding them of the wondrous deeds that God did and was still doing for their behalf. Asaph the musician and worship leader at the temple during the times of King David, did express a similar kind of acclamation to God through his Psalm 73. Asaph was once wavering in his faith in God, impacted by a severe conflict from within, trying to make sense of all the afflictions that a believer had to undergo, while the wicked went scot-free. While in the presence of God, while in worship, the Spirit of God touched his heart to understand what was oppressive to him - that God is always good, to all who are true of heart. Guided by this counsel from God, Asaph raises this prayer – My flesh and my heart may fail, but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion forever. Yes, God is our strength and our portion forever, let us reiterate to ourselves. The gladness of heart is what He gives us, restoring our minds to comfort, even when the world says there is no hope and peace. His mercy and goodness shall surely follow us all the days of our life. His Cross - our salvation and deliverance throughout our days. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

15/09/24, 09:23 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But they soon forgot His works; they did not wait for His Counsel’ (Psalm 106:13)

‘They’ were just like us, ‘fallible’ human beings, not that they renounced their faith in the Lord. They were the chosen of God, His treasured possession, the people whom the Lord redeemed from Egyptian slavery, those who saw signs and miracles while traversing the Red Sea, those who were meant to inherit the Promised land – Canaan. But what befell them is what is interesting. Though these were witnesses to God’s mighty deeds and wonder works, at times of crisis (as it happens to You and me) they succumbed to the temptation of relying on themselves, on the ways of the world, on following the inclination of their intellect and thoughts, rather than asking God what would suit them the most. They believed the reason why they could not withstand their enemies was because they had no King to rule over them which all other nations had. The reason why they were not socially or culturally prominent, (they believed) was because they had no rituals and practices the other nations had. Therefore they yearned to adopt the ways of others, forgetting that they were a distinct people, chosen to be set apart, they failed to prioritize the counsel of the Lord to the opinion of the crowds - thereby setting themselves to unheeded hardships and difficulties in life. Had they listened to the Lord, awaited His advise, and guided themselves in obedience to it, how much more privileged and smooth their lives would have been? To us these pose a super example of what it means to trust in the Lord. Do we lead lives in obedience to the promptings of the Holy Spirit? or Do we merely satisfy the desires and wishes of our heart as and when they prop up? Let us meditate and pray. Amen

16/09/24, 09:23 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Lord is in His holy temple; the Lord is on His Heavenly throne; He observes everyone on earth; His eyes examine them’ (Psalm 11:4)

The Psalmist David described the kind of struggles (inner conflicts) he had to deal with as the King of Israel, (vouched by history as the greatest King of Israel). With all the authority and luxuries he enjoyed as the head of a nation, his life was not void of problems – troubles from enemies, strategies to defend his kingship and security of his people, insurrections within and without, negativities that could deprive him of needed peace…..There were decisive moments when the unrest within soared up so intensely that he felt like giving up or running away. ‘What can the righteous do, if his foundations are being destroyed?’ his drooping spirit kept reasoning to himself. It is then that from deep within the Spirit of God reminded him in prayer, that His refuge was none other than the Lord. And no matter how prepared his foes were, bending their bows and setting their arrows for offense; The Lord from His Holy Temple was watching over them all. He who is enthroned in the glorious heights of the Heavens, waits to avenge the wicked and bring in victory for the just. He examines the hearts of every man and deals with him according to his uprightness. No motive or intention of the human heart is hidden from God. This was David’s conviction when he began to pray, raising his praises and supplications before God. So would it be to us, who (when in the midst of life’s fiery trials), pause a while in silence seeking intervention and support from Jesus. He would come towards us, silencing the waves and turmoil, to enable us enjoy the fruits of righteousness. If the Lord Jesus is with us, who can then be against us? Amen

17/09/24, 08:56 - Megha Sherino: In robotics, infrared (IR) and ultrasonic sensors are used for different types of environmental sensing and navigation:

1. **Infrared Sensors**: These sensors use infrared light to detect objects and measure distances. They are commonly used for proximity detection, object avoidance, and line following. IR sensors can detect changes in the intensity of reflected infrared light to determine the presence or absence of objects and their distance from the sensor.

2. **Ultrasonic Sensors**: These sensors use sound waves at frequencies higher than human hearing to measure distances. They emit an ultrasonic pulse and measure the time it takes for the echo to return after bouncing off an object. This time measurement is then used to calculate the distance to the object. Ultrasonic sensors are often used for obstacle detection and ranging in robotics, as they can provide accurate distance measurements and work well in various lighting conditions.

Both types of sensors help robots understand their environment and make decisions based on that information, enhancing their ability to navigate and interact with the world around them.

18/09/24, 09:53 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Accept one another then, just as Christ accepted you, in order to bring praise to God’ (Romans 15:7)

Jesus accepts us as we are, He sees in us ‘the masterpiece’ that which was intended through us when we were created in the heart of God, even before the foundations of the world. This may be way different from what we are today, or how we have ever attempted to live to this day. But, so that the Father’s will for our salvation may be accomplished, though we were in sinfulness and shame, He chose us, instilled the light of His message (The Gospel) in us, and redeemed us. For all that HE has done for us, we have nothing in reciprocation to give; but our lives can transform into streams of mercy for those who are in dire need. It is not in material giving that we can express the mercy of Jesus through our lives, but in a Spirit of love, in prayer, in thanksgiving, in forgiving and bearing with the failing of the other. ‘Forgiving another’ would not imply condoning the wrong of the other or justifying sinful conduct, it points to a whole set of different actions - giving him/her a second chance to repent, rectify /remedy the wrongs committed wilfully or unknowingly. This was how the Lord corrected his own disciples, and is how he disciplines You and me to take on our destined path in life. You and I are called to carry His traits of love, compassion and mercy, to accept one another so that seeing the Light in us, the Heavenly Father may be acclaimed and honored. Lord, help us to imitate your way, follow after your truth and receive abundance of life, from here on. Amen

19/09/24, 09:44 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And He said to them. “What do you want me to do for you?” (Mark 10:35-36)

Jesus had been going about the towns and cities of His country teaching the crowds about the Kingdom of God, explaining to them that He was the door through which these would be able to enter God’s Kingdom. He elaborated to all the basic tenets of the Law, giving them fullness in interpretation, of what it meant to be righteous, of being His followers, of how they were to be like children. However much he reminded his disciples of His impending passion, death, and resurrection which was the wellspring of grace flowing to all mankind, they were never convinced of a spiritual or esoteric Kingdom whose existence was beyond human intelligibility. But these ordinary men (disciples) knew that when they were with the Lord, He would grant them almost everything they asked Him for. Through Jesus, all the desires and dreams of their beings would be fulfilled, as He was the One who held the keys of the Kingdom of God. This is why they approach the Lord barefaced, asking Him to grant them their wish, which they thought defined their purpose of living. Jesus the fullness of Wisdom, read their hearts, even before they approached him, but He did not discourage or express displeasure on their request. On the contrary, He conceded to their request (the urge in them to do good), opening their hearts to comprehend ‘greater’ truths of His Kingdom. No matter how illogical and unrealistic our requests, supplications, and prayers before Jesus may seem -  to Him, these are untapped potential or greater heights He calls us to explore in our lives with Him. Never be discouraged in opening your heart before the Lord Jesus. Amen

21/09/24, 08:51 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He will transform our weak bodies so that they be like His glorious body, by the power that enables Him to bring everything under His control’ (Philippians 3:21)

He who defeated death by the very power of His resurrection is in control of everything. Jesus assures us His unfailing companionship, though the going may be tough at times on this earth – through the storms and turbulences of this life, He shows us the way. We must say to ourselves that every occurrence in life is a distinct plan, a deliberate one, that which may be incomprehensible to us when perceived with the eye-piece of today, but which will be purposed by time and tide. There is no coincidence in fact in our lives. And even if something ‘untoward’ knocks at the door, the Lord knows how to deal with each in its appropriateness. Beyond this superficial understanding, He calls us to obtain, He invites us into a deeper understanding of the Gospel truths, provided we are fascinated to learn more. Our mortal bodies may be weak, but within it is His power that can turn the tides of time, one that can rewrite legacies and destinies. By the power of His Holy Spirit, He can induce the needed change in us to transform into glorious resurrected beings as Him. This transformation the Lord does is not merely after one’s death (sure thing, we believe in this resurrection of our bodies), but even before death, when with all that is sinful, unworthy, or stale (corruption in the flesh) in us, He evolves into vessels of grace to be bearers of His mercy. From this standpoint, You and I have nothing to claim about, but for His overflowing grace for which we hope and pray for each day. Amen

23/09/24, 08:34 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Our ancestors were all under the cloud and they all passed through the sea. They were all baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea. They all ate the same spiritual food, and drank the same spiritual drink; for they drank from the Spiritual rock that accompanied them, and that rock was Christ’ (1 Corinthians 10:1-4)

Paul narrates what occurred to God’s people while in the wilderness. He quotes from history as a warning to us believers, who like these claim to have been redeemed from slavery (slavery of sin and death), who have been guided through the desert (trials and turmoils of life), to whom God has extended great protection and companionship through Jesus, our rock and refuge. Here is the warning that the Holy Spirit voiced to us through the words of St. Paul – ‘If you think you are standing firm, be careful that you do not fall’. The Israelites fell, they were abandoned by God, they died in the wilderness, the entire generation could not meet with their hope – The promised land. And why? Because they were stubborn, they were hardened in their hearts, they succumbed to certain sins for which they had no remorse about and thus God was displeased with them. Those sins that led to the downfall of the Israelites may be worth mentioning, as knowingly or not, we tend to be swayed and persuaded to commit these in our lives too. The people turned ‘Idolaters’ – those who worshipped their glory, their successes, believed in their legacy, and failed to prioritize the ways and will of God before what they considered important. It is this that led to their dwindling faith, stubbornness, and self-reliance, leading them to commit grievous faults of adultery, sexual immorality, and so on….To them, honoring the ways of men, their customs, traditions, and human opinion was of higher import, to the commandments and precepts of the Lord – to His fundamental instruction of loving one another with the compassion and mercy of God. It is this that we have been exhorted by the Spirit of Jesus to protect ourselves from. Let us ask Jesus to help us to be more merciful, generous, and broadminded in our ways and to be able to practice the love the Lord fills our hearts with each day. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

24/09/24, 08:59 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I will take my stand at my watch-post and station myself on the tower, and look out to see what He will say to me, and what I will answer concerning’ (Habakkuk 2:1)

What Habakkuk (the prophet of God) did when distraught and unable to rationalize with life’s varied unanswered questions, would turn an eye-opener to us, believers in Jesus Christ. The prophet had been long struggling with the infidelities of the Israelites, trying to convince them of what would befall them as a consequence of their sinfulness. On the other hand, he was overwhelmed having been told by God of what would occur to Israel shortly due to their transgressions. This was dilirious to him and, having almost lost control of things, not knowing how to retaliate or make a come-back, he finally relents to God in silence. The prayer he makes at this ‘state of surrender’ to the Most High, gets him his answer of a lifetime, the confidence and assurance of peace - ‘ That the righteous will live by faith’. To You and me, who at times reach the crossroads of life’s journey, unable to practically sort out a way from all entanglements and strings that keep us tied, we would need to stand silently at the ramparts of our citadel – the rock of refuge we have been privileged to stand upon – Jesus. To look to Him, awaiting in patience for what He has to tell us. For He knows what befalls us, what is to occur, and what best we would need to do to manoeuvre ‘safe’ through today’s crisis. Not what holds strong in our minds and intellects (those determined by the past), but what the Lord has to tell us, we must obey. Then as the prophet thanked God, will we admit that the Sovereign Lord is my strength, the One who makes my feet like the feet of the deer, who causes me tread in the heights, though calamity surrounds me. Amen

25/09/24, 07:48 - Megha Sherino: 
25/09/24, 09:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘In the thirty-seventh year of the exile of King Jehoiachin of Judah, in the twelfth month, on the twenty-fifth day of the month, King Evil-merodach of Babylon, in the year he began to reign, showed favor to King Jehoiachin of Judah and brought him out of prison’ (Jeremiah 52:31)

King Jehoiachin was the last of the Kings of Judah, who succeeded the throne of his father (King Jehoiakim) and at a time most turbulent in Judean history. Hardly did he take over the reins of the kingdom after the death of his father, (for three months) he was deposed and taken captive to Babylon by the Babylonian King, Nebuchadnezzar II. His ill-fate was attributed to the transgressions, sinfulness, and atrocities his father committed as King, and against whom Jeremiah prophesied that there would come a retribution from God. This ‘prophesy’ became true when Jehoiakim had to succumb to a most humiliating death and his successor who was dethroned no sooner had to spend 37 long years as a prisoner of war. This young Jehoiachin, though of royal descent had nothing in his favor, but misfortunes and years of suffering, for no direct cause of his own. His silent suffering as a captive in the Babylonian prison, may have opened the eyes of many in that country and one among them could have been Merodach, Nebuchadnezzer’s son (a presumptive reason as to why the King was released from prison immediately after Evil-Merodach became king of Babylon). From then on Jehoiachin became a close friend to King Merodach, was given a high position in the kingdom, dined with the King regularly and was supported by him till the day of his death. Through this life story of King Jehoiachin, the Book of Jeremiah comes to an end, elucidating to us the truth – Even in the most grim situations of life’s journey, there is hope for a comeback, when the Lord is with us. The Lord who delivered Joseph from his captivity, who released Jehoiachin from his imprisonment, will deliver and rebuild the lives of all who trust in Him, those who are broken-hearted, distressed and have lost hope. Trust in the Lord Jesus and look forward to the greater days in store for you. Amen

26/09/24, 08:44 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘A wooden beam firmly bonded into a building is not loosened by an earthquake...’ (Sirach 22:16)

The power of the mind or one’s resolve is of course tremendous, but there are times when it is subject to weakness, when rejuvenation becomes all the more necessary. History is replete with incidents of men who finally gave up in life, who found themselves sinking in the miry bog of crisis and eventualities they could not conform to (those they were not responsible of, but made answerable to). Uncertainty and change ordered by the Divine can meet us at inflection points in life that they may turn out to be a ‘do or die’ situation for You and me. Here today’s Word assures us that be it a calamity of any sort, with the wooden beam of the Cross of Jesus bonded into our lives, we are invincible and nothing can pull us down. How often do we tend to forget the times in yester years when the Lord had been with us and delivered us through the reeling floods and calamities that were highly disastrous to us? And today let us be convinced that nothing but the Cross, is our way to salvation to revival, strength, empowerment and glory. Therefore, it is the power in us, the power of the Lord that drives us ahead through storms, onto a broad pathway. Thus the Psalmist prayed, ‘Though I walk through the darkest valleys in life, I fear no evil, for though art with me; Thy rod and thy staff are my comfort. Let the Rod and Staff of the loving Lord, His care and protection renew us in our minds our spirits this Thursday morning. Thank you Jesus for this realization that I am never alone in life. Amen

‘Give me understanding that I may obey your Law and follow it with all my heart’ (Psalm 119:34)

To be able to obey God’s law and follow it wholeheartedly, You and I need to be blessed with an understanding – the understanding of what would prove right and what would prove wrong through the eyes of the Lord. It is not through the lens of culture, ways of the world, human standards or intelligence or convenience of living that You and I are called to be obedient to God’s Word, but in the Spirit of the Lord. It is this Holy Spirit that helps us discern between the preferred choice and God’s pick for us. It is the latter that promotes growth in us and in the long run, no matter how unintelligible it may seem at the present. We sometimes get entangled in unlimited problems in life’s journey, and hardly know the way out; ‘What’ we then ‘need’ is the audacity and humility to sit back and ask the Lord of what He would want us to do. It is this understanding or wisdom that the psalmist prayed to be blessed with each day to guard and assure success in his ways. To those who ask the Lord, to those who seek clarity from Him, to those who are sincere in glorifying Him, our Lord opens the Heavens to bless and protect them. All through the day, it is the God of Jeshurun (You and me who petition the Lord in uprightness) who rides the Heavens to help us. Fear not, You are never alone, when the Lord has grasped your hand and is delighted in you. Amen

28/09/24, 09:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of the desire’ (Ecclesiastes 6:9)

In a commercial-oriented materialist society, what creates purpose for living is possessions, fascinations, dreams, and desires for novelty. Imaginations and desires never limit themselves unless intentionally curtailed or bridled by a thoughtful mind. It is always better to be content with what we have been blessed with, or what we ‘have’ than to be lurking around for things we do not have. Every individual is so unique and incredible a creation, that how he perceives and makes sense of occurrences in life may seem amazing. The human being has been given this power of co-creating with His Creator that: from the little, the oblivious, the subtle, and the negligible, does emerge something comprehensive, interesting, not just pleasing to the senses but also highly beneficial to mankind. It is in being conscious/aware of the little at hand that one acquires the skill of improvising and improving himself. Therefore, rather than impressing upon what others may think about us, what You and I are called to be worried about is how better we could grow by making use of the God given resources at hand. More than looking at others or into the world at large, let us find time for ourselves in shaping and honing ourselves, what could define us the better, and what could be done more in ourselves to outpace the real person in me. let us pray for insight and wisdom from above. Amen

29/09/24, 08:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘In everything David did, he gave thanks and praise to the Holy Lord, the Most High. He loved his Creator and sang praises to him with all his heart’ (Sirach 47:8)

What distinguished David from his predecessor and successors was this – He loved his Creator and sang praises to him wholeheartedly. Not that he was above board in his human nature, he was as feeble and vulnerable as You and me. But what He did for glorifying the Lord, was what endeared him to God. Thus we read in verse 11 that the Lord forgave his sins, and established his power for ever. It was with David that the Lord entered into a Covenant on behalf of his descendants, that his dynasty’s reign would endure through Israelite history. And so it was. Jesus, the Son of God took birth from this dynasty, as the Son of David. During his public ministry, the crowds who were benefitted through the works of Jesus, his miracles for them, addressed him as ‘The Son of David’. Such was the commendation King David received even after his death. As we honour the Lord this Sunday morning, let us pray for his intervention in our lives as the Son of David. So let us pray, Jesus, Son of David, Have mercy on us! Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
