30/09/24, 09:21 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Lord says to my Lord, ‘Sit at my right Hand until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet’ (Psalm 110:1)

You and I are called today, privileged to be with and banquet with the Lord at His right Hand. It is easy to thank Jesus, when all is bright and beautiful. But when under the canopy of a dark cloud that has been hovering over us for long, to thank the Lord and be cheerful at His presence may seem the most impossible task. Here Psalm 110 reminds us to be with Jesus, seeking His intervention and calling upon His mighty name, not merely because He saves, but because the Messiah was sent for all mankind, sent as a mediator between man and God and sent to make retribution for sins and transgressions of all. Wherever relationships between men have deteriorated and distorted beyond repair, this Messiah, the Lord Jesus holds the key to what next needs to be done for restoration. So therefore in the midst of sinfulness, guilt, despair, and dejection, our Souls must seek after the One who has power and whose saving power can never be limited. Are you in crossroads or a decisive moment in your life, the Holy Spirit inspires you to sit before the Lord, seek His presence and tangible support. He is the fullness of compassion and mercy; and nothing more would this soul need than the love of the Lord and His favor. My times are in Your Hands O Lord, my Saviour, Deliver me and cause me to ride smooth, lessen the distress within me and show me the way to victory as You have promised – the way that my enemy may be overcome and I may experience your glory. Amen

01/10/24, 08:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You may have strength to comprehend with all the saints what is the breadth and length and height and depth and to know the love of Christ that surpasses knowledge, that you may be filled with all the fullness of God’ (Ephesians 3:18)

Today God’s Word points to where exactly our issue in life lies in – Despite being recipients of so great a love, one which wiped all our sins, took our burdens, blessed us with all material and spiritual things we may never have asked for. We fail to realize the breadth, height, length, and depth of this love of the Lord for each one of us. Instead, we keep justifying ourselves, attributing the cause and reason (for our failures) not to our reckless living, but to the loving merciful Lord who has been gracious to us. And this explains the key difference between the Saints and us, who claim to be followers of Christ. So let us pray this morning for the strength – for the conviction that dominated the minds and hearts of saints, to know Jesus, the love He has for us, ‘the knowledge’ that takes us overboard to live our purpose with courage and determination, which would gradually lead us phase by phase towards the fullness of God, which is Christ in us. Let this knowledge of the love of Christ ignite us to initiate acts of charity, that which may be of no meaning to those around, but which will always be remembered and honored by the Lord, Most High. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

02/10/24, 10:19 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort, who comfort us in all our affliction, so that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God’ (2 Corinthians 1:3-5)

The truth is that unless ‘God’, the Father of mercies and all comfort, intervenes and enlightens our hearts and minds, ‘We’ will never be comforted and learn to keep moving ahead in life. When the world teaches us to search for happiness in our circumstances, the Spirit of the Lord comforts us by leading us to the source of all happiness. Even with all the comforts and luxuries of a worldly living, we might at times feel the vacuum deep within. This may be - not because we lack material things, but because it is the space our Creator desires to have within us to be with us. And daily in life, we come across disappointing situations, instances and disturbances to peace, because we fail to secure this space for the Lord of Peace in us. Paul was speaking out of his own experience and exhorted the believers in Corinth of connecting with God, through Jesus, the Prince of Peace – and as often as they encountered life’s uncertainties and daunting insecurities. This relationship in prayer, that You and I are called to imbibe through life, is where we start to grow in our inner beings. Every time we reach out to the Father of mercies, He fills our hearts with accomplishment, an appreciation of the self and by way of which we learn to be resourceful to many around us who know not how to keep going in life. The difficulties we take up each day, equip and prepare us to render ‘hearts’ open to receive grace and mercy from the Most High. This grace flows through our families, to those we come across in life and helps spread the message of Salvation to the world. That Not by might, power or intelligence will we endure, but by the Spirit of God. It is the ‘Joy’ of the Lord which is our strength, as the prophets of Old once proclaimed. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

03/10/24, 08:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You have given Him authority over all flesh, to give Eternal life to all whom You have given Him’ (John 17:2)

God sent His Son to this world in a human form, not to punish the world, but that through Him it may be saved. This is the authority of Jesus, conferred on Him by His Father – That in Heaven, on earth and under the earth, every knee shall bow and every tongue shall confess His Lordship for obtaining the glory of the Heavenly Father. This manifold, overarching authority of Jesus was made manifest during His public ministry when He healed many, casted off demons, calmed the soaring seas, when He forgave the sins of many, restored the hungry in Spirit and preached to all the Good News that the Kingdom of Heaven was at hand for all. Even today the risen Lord extends His power, which is His discretion to change destiny – The One who has authority over distance, over sin, diseases, and death; will surely come to our help whenever we believe in Him and seek Him with a thirsty Spirit. No matter ‘however’ gross and messy your past may have been, You still have a chance to be restored, to be rejuvenated, and established if You give yourself into the Hands of the Lord. He who has been given authority to work out Creation, the One who sustains all, calls you to experience Eternal life, the purpose of your life. Obey the Lord, keep faithfulness as your shield and truth as your armour, so that the Sun of Righteousness may shine over you, avenge your cause and exalt you among His favored ones. Amen

05/10/24, 09:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Who then are those who fear the Lord? He will instruct them in the ways they should choose’ (Psalm 25:12)

Who is this One who fears the Lord, the Blessed One, for there will be no want for him. He is the One who is taught by the Lord, instructed in His Way, and encouraged to endure hardships in life. It is the Lord who consoles, comforts and leads His path. This One will not experience want – not in the sense the world would understand of him. He may go through wilderness times, those that are dreadful to the worldly man, he may be led through loneliness, sorrows, and life-threatening torrents in the course of living, just to build in him the conviction - that One is established by His Creator and none. He would never retract to blame the Lord for the variety of ‘circumstances’, but would seek of Him the wisdom to comb cautiously through and discern what all these were meant for. When desperate, the Spirit of God restitutes him to the status quo. To be the favorite of the Lord is not merely a privilege, but a choice too. For with all the fascinations – the myriad colours of this world, how would One choose to be faithful always and in all ways to the Lord? Because he understands that the One who has called him is faithful, ardent and sincere, He will never fail or forsake. With such a Lord at our side, let us pray for the grace of loving the presence of the Lord, seeking strength for endurance and faithfulness, believing that He always rewards the One who is sincere. Amen

06/10/24, 07:52 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘…There is in my heart as it were a burning fire shut up in my bones, and I am weary with holding it in and I cannot’ (Jeremiah 20:9)

Jeremiah felt coerced – On the one hand, it was the sense of urge to ‘prophesy’ in God’s name His Word, while on the other he couldn’t face up to the people to tell them that doomsday was to come. The sins and transgressions of the people were at peak point that they hardly felt any remorse for their deeds. As it occurs in our lives too, little by little these people violated every commandment the Lord had provided for their well-being. And what beheld them was the repercussions for their deeds. It was not that God had forgotten His mercy towards His people, but the latter invited trouble for themselves because of their ruthlessness. Jeremiah who couldn’t bear with the gravity of this situation any longer, decided to abandon it into the Hands of the Lord. He surrenders all his emotions to God who is with Him, ready to support and encounter his foes to deliver him. He prays for Israel’s redemption from its foes – this ‘cause’/’intention’ he commits to the Almighty. Because he knew that God’s mercies transcended His wrath and none who came to the Lord found themselves disarrayed in life. Let us approach the Lord with a humble heart, pleading for his mercy and intervention, when things don’t seem viable, fair or equitable in life’s journey, when the going goes tough, when in twilight and unable to see the path ahead. His way is our way to success. Amen

07/10/24, 10:13 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘As you know we count as blessed those who have persevered. You have heard of Job’s perseverance and have seen what the Lord finally brought about. The Lord is full of compassion and mercy’ (James 5:11)

The Epistle of St James displays considerable similarity with the Wisdom Literature in the Bible and outlines some of the basic precepts of Christian morality. Fundamental virtues like restraint or control of speech, endurance or ‘patience’ in suffering are some of the overarching themes that bear resemblance to the ‘Sermon of the mount’ teachings of Jesus. While calling the one who places his trust in the Saving power of Jesus and thus combats difficulties in life – ‘Blessed’, this passage of the New Testament of the Bible points to those who have already shown us the way. The examples of the life of the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord and of Job who underwent countless sufferings though was blameless before God. One common point connecting these two types was that both were distanced and ridiculed by the rest of the crowds. These were subject to unfounded criticism, accusations were levelled against them which were unparalleled and had no firm grounding upon. What finally transpired to each is also mentioned in the Bible, that these were rewarded a double portion for all their hardships. Of more value was the conviction that became deep rooted in them, that the Lord is full of compassion and mercy. To those in the dark, those unable to see the light, those deeply wounded, those rejected, the Gospel of Jesus still rings out the same message today – The Lord is full of compassion and mercy. You and I become this image of the compassionate Lord when we are able to comfort the afflicted and help heal the one who is wounded in spirit. To be like Christ and to spread His Word is our mission; what more is our purpose on earth. Amen

08/10/24, 09:21 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘We groan while we are in this tent, burdened as we are, because we do not want to be unclothed but clothed, so that mortality may be swallowed up by life’ (2Corinthians 5:4)

If there is this vacuum in us, a burden we carry inexplainable to others, that we ourselves are unable to satiate, God’s Word encourages us to take heart. St Paul explains that this groaning is not merely in us, but one experienced by the entire Creation, who wait in eager expectation for liberation and freedom of the Children of God. And this has been fulfilled through the Incarnation of Jesus. That through His Sacrifice on the Cross, mortality and death have been destroyed. The Creation has been renewed and reclothed with life. What was set for decay and corruption has been reclaimed to its preternatural glory, as destined by the Father in the beginning. And so with us men, who believe in the mysteries of Christ’s death and resurrection, we partake unknowingly in this process of redemption. Though our outer beings may be weary and weak, yet, to fulfill this ultimate purpose, our inner-beings are in constant striving inspired by the Spirit of God. For our bodies were fashioned for this purpose, to be led by the power of the inner person. And unless we draw near to where our hearts moved by God’s Spirit leads us, we would never find the content or delight of living. You and I have been called to adorn life into our mortal spirits by putting on the mind of Jesus Christ. He was humble, content and submissive to the will of His Father and always empowered to action by the Spirit of God. Let us humbly seek God to know His will for us, submit ourselves to be tamed by His will and live the purpose we have been gloriously created for on earth. Thanks be to the Lord Jesus for this indescribable gift of understanding. Amen

09/10/24, 09:20 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Then you will be like a Son of the Most High, and He will love you more than does your mother’ (Sirach 4:10)

The Word of God advises us to endear ourselves to the poor and needy. Not just in the literal meaning of being poor and needy, but in its profundity, are we called to be like the Lord. One would make himself tenable to receive God’s graciousness and mercy, only when he is able to render respect and generosity to his fellow-beings. As human beings who live by human standards of an ever-evolving world, we tend to be led by ‘Utilitarianism’ – To us, those who have been able to make a mark, or those who have proven track records in terms of material possessions, influence and so on., are regarded high of and to be commended. But what about those who suffer, who have failed to ring the success bell, those who have been weak, have toiled relentlessly behind the curtains for the growth of many, those swept to the margins for no voice of their own, victimised or abandoned,……….Do they ever get recognized in an aggressive swift-paced society of ours? Are their dreams, beliefs or opinions ever regarded worth? These are the poor, the needy, the widowed, the distressed, the hungry and the oppressed. It is to these that Wisdom teaches her children to be merciful, to be tender, to be ‘a father to orphans’ and a ‘husband to their mother’. And this is what makes us Christ-like, the beloved of the Most High, to be blessed, loved, and tendered by Him. Not the so-called ‘deeds’ of charity, we proclaim as having done before the crowds, our generosity for which we have received recognition from people around. But to be the smile for the one who knew not how to smile. He will become the garden tended by the Most High. Amen

10/10/24, 09:33 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Not that I have already obtained this or am already perfect, but I press on to make it my own, because Christ Jesus has made me his own’ (Phil 3:12)

What is it that persuaded Paul to keep striving hard towards his goal? And what exactly was this goal? Here is where the Spirit of the Lord leads us to, initiate a reorientation in our lives. Paul was concerned only with pleasing Jesus. His eyes were focused on the righteousness that came from the Lord, that which would be conferred on all who believed in Jesus (in the outstanding merits that Jesus acquired for all through His Sacrifice of self). Thus he (Paul) was never occupied with the things of his past, nor overwhelmed by the grace that markedly distinguished him (setting him apart as an Apostle for the Gentiles) as of the present. But giving glory to the Lord, he strived hard to know more and more about the Lord, about his power of resurrection, of the privilege of sharing in his sufferings, that one day he too would qualify eligible to be crowned in the resurrection. Therefore despite all the surpassing revelations given to him of the Heavenly mysteries, he was unmarred, not distracted from his goal of Jesus Christ and Him alone. For this ‘reason’, all things were as ‘thrash’ to him – because the Lord Jesus has made him His own. You and I have been likewise possessed by the Lord through His Cross, through His Sacrifice- made coheirs to an unmatched grace and righteousness that comes from the merits of Jesus. But where are our eyes fixed upon – the things of beauty around or on the One who has the power to recreate/transform from nothingness. Let us ask the Lord for this grace to open our hearts to perceive what really matters to us. For Unless the Lord builds our house, our labours would all go in vain. Amen

‘According to your steadfast love remember me, for the sake of your goodness, O Lord!’ (Psalm 25:7)

The abundance of life, fullness of strength, overflowing joy and hope is the love of the Lord. Every morning has something new to teach us, to refine and restore in us, and it is given to those who ask of the Lord. So let us ask Jesus today, from the bottom of our hearts, to just ‘remember’ us - though we have been unworthy, so ungrateful, at times sinful and resentful. For who else knows us so well, as the Lord himself does? Who else can forgive our sins as He does? Who would help us carry our burdens as He readily waits for? For the sake of your goodness, O Lord, remember us, according to your unfailing mercy and graciousness, cleanse us from our transgressions, fill us with your Spirit that builds and generates growth in us, strengthen our minds to always reflect on your promises, to gain the courage and conviction to follow after you, no matter how hard things may seem to us. This we pray for this morning, Be with us Lord, as the day turns cloudy, as heavy clouds hover upon our skies, as the great King David prayed, Be my pillar, my fortress, my deliverer, my God, my rock, my refuge, my shield, my Salvation, my High tower O Lord Jesus, King of Kings – we worship you and surrender ourselves to you. Amen

15/10/24, 09:33 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For they persecute him whom you have struck down, and they recount the pain of those you have wounded’ (Psalm 69:26)

Quite strange is the kind of behavior, we human beings exhibit (distinct from the rest of creation) when it comes to specific instances when we are asked to be merciful. We, who have been created in the image and likeness of our Creator, at times, become so indignant and defiant to God’s mercy (that we have been privileged to experience in our lives). Some of us look down upon those in distress, those less privileged than us, judging these as deserving their plight – on account of their deeds or ways. Hardly do we realize that whether righteous or wicked, good or bad, clean or unclean, we all share a common destiny. And our destiny is in the hands of the Lord. None knows of what beholds his/her future, or the destiny that would overtake him. As it was for the psalmist David, who had to undergo unheeded persecutions (from his enemies), he was hopeless at times, but strengthened himself in the steadfast love of the Lord for him. And as Jesus reiterated – Not that the Galileans who were murdered at the hands of Pilate, ‘grievous sinners’; nor were the eighteen executed when the Tower of Shiloam collapsed utterly wicked, but for us it was a given as a warning for repentance. To learn to be more understanding, more merciful, gracious and less judgmental to others. For we all stand before the mighty presence of the Lord, the Judge of all. Just because He delays the execution of His sentence against evil, no man can prolong his evil ways and continue to sin. Though the Son of God had to experience much humiliation and suffering, his crucifixion dealt the final nail in the coffin of the devil, who had come to rob and destroy mankind. It was God’s will that Jesus was to suffer and that He was to be wounded, but for a greater purpose – for the redemption of you and me and all mankind. Likewise, none of the sufferings you and I have undergone in life, have been futile – the moment we realize this in Jesus’ name, there is healing, redemption and restoration – for us and for many. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

16/10/24, 09:47 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Answer me when I call, O God of my righteousness! You have given me relief when I was in distress. Be gracious to me and hear my prayer!’ (Psalm 4:1)

Have you ever felt choked because you were unable to vent out the indescribable emotions, thoughts and needs of your heart? The Holy spirit who knew that as human beings, you and I could occasionally get entangled in situations as this, where we would find none to lend a patient ear to us – gives us the consolance our hearts desired for all the while. It is the Lord who brings joy to our hearts, more than wine and grain in abundance can provide; From Him comes our deliverance from oppressors and plunderers; He holds close the righteous when they groan dispirited and distressed. He protects, upholds and blesses His righteous whenever they cry out for help. Surely His goodness and mercy will follow us all our days, as we acknowledge that the Lord is our shepherd! our fortress and rock! Therefore, whenever we feel abandoned, lonely and anxious in the wilderness of life, You and I are to pause, return and submit to the One who knows it all. For unlike men, who may rely on vain words and deceit, the Lord delights in the One who trusts in Him, who calls His intervention in their lives and He would fight for him to help him conquer the debacles that have disrupted his peace, and why? that he may realize and treasure his discipleship of being loved by the Lord and being with Him always; at all times. Amen

17/10/24, 10:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘If anyone serves me, he must follow me; and where I am, there will my servant be also. If anyone serves me, the Father will honor him’ (John 12:26)

Jesus knew that His time of glorification had come. The fact that some Greeks who had been at Jerusalem during the feast to worship wished to see Jesus – this was a sign to Jesus’ disciples that the ministry they were called to take up was worldwide. The good news of the Kingdom was an overarching message that was to be preached to all Creation and whose fruits were open to all mankind. These Greeks who expressed the desire to see Jesus were blessed with an overwhelming message - That unless the grain of wheat dies to itself it wouldn’t bear fruit. Jesus was setting up his expectations on how his followers were to be like. Anyone could take up this call of being Christ-like. But to be successful, he was to die to his own selfish desires, convictions and beliefs to secure a heart to follow Jesus and serve Him. Unlike the dictates or principles of worldly leaders, Jesus’ message was simple, yet hard, incomprehensible to the worldly human, but not impossible. To constantly be with the Lord, in whatever situation of life – be it in the good or the bad (Entering through the Narrow path), to obey His Word, however unrewarding or hard it may seem when weighed in comparison to public opinion and things around, To tirelessly fulfill the tasks of our vocation despite the trials and challenges that surface through – this one is whom the Lord addresses as His Servant. For this one, the Lord delights in, His heart yearns for and remembers always. Jesus, teach me your ways and train me to follow you always. I desire to see you; to learn from you, and to delight in you - as the Greeks who desired to see you, to whom you gave this extraordinary conviction of what it is to be a follower. Amen

18/10/24, 11:14 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Above all bless your Maker who fills you with His good gifts’ (Sirach 32:12)

To be able to share in the joy of others, to speak only when required and with few words, to train oneself in patience and humility, to entertain oneself but with needed constraint following the fear of the Lord – this is what the author of the Book of Sirach describes the etiquette of the wise. It is such ‘one’ who glorifies His Maker through his ways wherever he is – whether at home (in his privacy) or before the crowds. This one heaps up praises for himself from around. He is content, hopeful, and loved by all. His thoughtfulness for others makes him kind and generous, yet fully aware of his boundaries and limitations. He/ She has acquired rightful knowledge of oneself – strengths, weaknesses, opportunities that can be rewarding, as well as those that can threaten the foundations of one’s existence, while walking the smooth road. Our prayers each new day would be for a new revelation from the Most High, for a new gift - for wisdom that takes to conquer the day with its pride and challenges. For being able to forgive ourselves for past mistakes, for abrupt actions and decisions that may have created obstacles in our path. Even these were not hidden from the eyes of our Creator, for He knew our feeble frame. Lord, while thanking you for all the gifts that we have received from you to this day, we seek your presence and blessings for greater gifts and a Divinely inspired heart that delights in the light within than from without. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

21/10/24, 09:51 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But when I thought how to understand this, it seemed to me a wearisome task’ (Psalm 73:16)

It is joy and abundance of life that God gives to all who approach Him. During His public ministry, Jesus while inviting people to be part of God’s Kingdom by following the Beatitudes, promised them that they would be rewarded with joy and peace, the moment they embraced His kingdom. Paul said that the kingdom of God was much beyond food and drink, it was an ecstasy (of joy) in the Holy Spirit. You and I are called merely to rejoice. But when things don’t turn around as expected, how could we possibly learn to rejoice and keep praying? It was not until the psalmist approached God’s sanctuary, that he was able to discern and perceive what God willed through each of his trials. As a rationale human being, it wasn’t any easy for him to understand though he had been trying to make sense of his difficulties in the light of the lives of many around him. And by looking elsewhere other than the Lord, one would never succeed in finding the solution to his specific questions concerning life. Therefore, let us stop wasting time comparing ourselves with those around us, sighing, vying and competing with others before the Almighty. But power comes the moment we accept, oblige, and besiege the Lord, asking Him for mercy, intervention and help in the midst of upheavals in life. Lord, give me the tenacity and humility to keep asking You until all seems comprehensible and the unbelievable unravels in my ordinary life. Amen

22/10/24, 08:10 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He said it to me: ‘It is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End. To the thirsty I will give water without cost from the spring of the water of life’ (Revelation 21:6)

Jesus gives freely because He loves us. By taking reference to Alpha and Omega, He convinces us that ‘All’ that we would ever dream of being filled with in life – He gives. If it were for content, honor, respect, possessions and progress, nothing comes without a cost. This is what we have been learning, assimilating and striving hard for all the years of life. Since it’s hard for us to unlearn and accept the ‘truth’, that for the One who longs after His Kingdom, these and even greater gifts are for free. This ‘thirst’ for honoring God’s Kingdom – abiding in righteousness, peace and joy in the abundance of the Holy spirit, is what we (followers of Christ) are called to exhibit and express. But what deters us from going this way? The inclinations and thoughts of our minds, the admonitions of our experience, fear of insecurity: of sticking out as the sore thumb, all these have prevented us from growing into what we have been called ‘to be’; we are thus stunted, all the while trying to wade the same waters we have been tiringly battling with all our years, and finally ending up like everyone, striving and vying with everything and everyone to make it to our point. Let us ask the Lord for the grace of thirsting for His Kingdom, to be the change that our Lord expects and our era longingly waits for from mankind. Lord, I thirst for fullness and to be replenished from the Spring of Life – You. Amen

'Therefore a Covenant of friendship was established with him, that he should be leader of the Sanctuary and of God's people, he and his descendants should have the dignity of priesthood forever' (Sirach 45:24)
Phinehas is appreciated by God and the Holy Spirit ranks him third among the fathers of old for his glory. What exalted him, setting him apart from contemporaries of his time, was his enthusiasm in fearing the Lord. His courage and conviction to stand firm in the precepts of God when the entire nation of Israel disgraced God's name through their sinful ways and by following after the practices of their neighbours who did not know the Lord. Jesus rewards those who love Him and obey His Words - this is what we are assured of today. That even if the world stands against you, the Lord accompanies you all through your way to see you succeed. Let us therefore pray fervently for this Covenant of friendship with the Lord. The Lord who honoured Phinehas for his faithfulness, He who exalted the shepherd boy David to Kingship, He who honoured the Prophets of old who were meak and lowly, He will favour us, showering His goodness and mercy through life's journey. Trust in Jesus and live His Gospel that He may be glorified through all your good deeds. Amen

25/10/24, 08:53 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And why do you not judge for yourselves what is right’ (Luke 12:57)

When a lawyer once asked Jesus of the greatest commandment, the Lord said – It was to love the Lord your God above everything and love your neighbour too. From this commandment springs up all that is to be discerned as ‘right’ and what is to be considered ‘wrong’. At times we end up doing the wrong, because our understanding has failed to discern between the right and wrong. Our knowledge, experience and understanding are to be prioritised to God’s will, His Commandment of love and nothing else. The Bible explains the sin of Idolatry as putting ‘me’ first before God’s ways and will. It is then that our minds, thoughts, discernment get surrendered to the Prince of this world. The entanglement, the stronghold he creates in our minds and hearts is incomparable and at times seems irreversible. It is only Jesus who can set us free from these strings, make us free, happy and joyful people. The truth is Jesus and in Him would we be able to discern between right and wrong. The right is how the Lord perceives; and He perceives even the wrong doer with compassion. If we cannot forgive and let go the mistakes of the other for the sake of Christ, we fall into the category of those who have judged wrongly. Let us therefore ask the Lord to cleanse, transform and purge the prejudices of our thoughts, so as to be able to think and see as He does. Lord for this grace we pray today, Bless us Jesus! Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

26/10/24, 09:27 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He who descended is the one who also ascended far above all the Heavens, that He might fill all things’ (Ephesians 4:10)

He who descended to be with us was Jesus Christ, the only Son of the Father. He descended that we might know the truth, the love and mercy of God for mankind. We see a God who came down to redeem man, to save him from his sins, transgressions and the destructions he was subject to. There may be situations in life irreversible, some which others blame us as responsible for, those we have no clue for a comeback. In all these, He the Lord is ready to redeem and restore. We must always remember that as He descended, He also ascended gloriously to the Heavens, that every spiritual and material blessing lost to us may be restored. He gave gifts to men, as He ascended, the resurrected Christ. Therefore, whatever may be our vocation in life, whatever may be our trials and difficulties in life, He knows it all. Let us thus ask the One who holds the key to the Heavens and Earth to open for us greater gifts that we ask for today. With faith and conviction we pray, Lord, answer our prayers. Amen

27/10/24, 08:35 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Thus says the Lord, The people who survived the sword found grace in the wilderness; when Israel sought for rest, the Lord appeared to him from far away…’ (Jeremiah31:2,3)

These were the Chosen ones, who encountered great distress and somehow managed to save their lives. We may be reminded of specific instances in our lives too, when we managed somehow to evade great threats in life. To us it was a miraculous escape, but not at all a coincidence for the Lord who permitted it in our lives. Here the Israelites survived the great conflict and managed to escape to the wilderness. All through it was hardship after hardship, but now they could find the rest their hearts really needed. It was Jeremiah who sees the Lord appearing to them from far away – meaning incomprehensible to these people. And the rest was the Divine assurance given to them that the restoration (beyond imagination) was at hand. We are the chosen Ones of the Lord, those who were redeemed by the blood of Jesus on the Cross. We are not our own to fend for ourselves a way out. Even when in trials, the Lord is beside us, but as Jeremiah prophesied – far away. And the message He conveys from far away is that ‘ You have been loved with an everlasting love’; His faithfulness towards you still continues’. King David while falling into the trap of sin and greed, repented, and understanding this unfailing love of the Lord proclaimed aloud ‘ Lord! Your love has made me great’. Let us also convince ourselves of how much the Lord loves us, be thankful for his graces, and await the restoration that He has in store for us today. Amen

‘For thus says the Lord to Judah and Jerusalem, ‘ Break up your fallow ground, and sow not among thorns’ (Jeremiah 4:3)

With a new heart, one filled with gratitude, a mind that reckons itself blessed counting the many blessings that it has received, we begin the new day. Let not things of the yesterdays trouble us. There may have been pressures from within and without that have hampered our well-being, there may have been plots, tricks, challenges and foul games that we have endured through life’s journey – those from dear and near especially. These have particularly woken us up to the reality that we are so special, so distinct and a miracle that the world looks on to. From this perspective, we receive God’s love, His Word of life and healing. Into this good soil - ‘awareness’ that one’s origin and greatness is in the Lord, removing from us all that irks and irritates – the pride, jealousy, meanness, ………..we lift up our hearts to receive today’s promise of restoration and glory. Lord Jesus, with a new heart, mind and filled with Your Spirit of love, we welcome this new day, believing and longing for the fulfilment of all the promises this day is loaded for us. Help us to be guided by your Spirit, the Spirit of Truth, that counsels, admonishes, guides and reconciles according to the perfect will of the Father. Wishing you a blessed day. Amen

‘God being rich in mercy, because of the great love with which he loved us, even when we were dead in our trespasses, made us alive together with Christ – by grace you have been saved’ (Ephesians 2:4,5)

We have been raised and enlivened by God our Father, because He loved us so much. It was Jesus who gave up Himself for our elevation. How grateful we must be to the Lord for what He has secured for us, not that we deserved it, but because of the desire of His Father. In Jesus, we have become heirs to everything, beyond what this world can offer us. And the best is yet to come – His immeasurable riches of glory in Christ Jesus, to be unveiled to us as time unfolds. By grace, that is God’s power and will, we have been redeemed for Heaven, to live lives worthy of what we have been gifted. Hardly do we realize our gifts, rather do we care about them! Lord Jesus, open our eyes to see and understand what we have, why we have been given them, why we stand distinct from the rest and what we have been called to attain on this life’s journey. Make each one of us a blessing for those around us. However small or irrelevant we may at times feel, may our roles be played to its fullest for the greater glory of the Lord and for salvation of many. Amen

31/10/24, 09:27 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Finally, be strong in the Lord and the strength of His might’ (Ephesians 6:10)

You and I are asked to embody the Lord and His strength this day. To stand firm, to stand our ground, to be fearless when perplexities hit hard, we need power and inspiration. Every day is an opportunity to live the Word, to fearlessly make known the mystery of our Salvation and this as St Paul did we are called to do. To be ambassadors of Christ, but within the limits and limitations of our vocation. To be a votary for the Lord is not a simple task and those who respond to this call from the Lord, are strengthened by Him. A Christ ambassador is aware of his strengths and weaknesses. He is aware of the limitation of time and consults with the Holy Spirit of how he could best make use of his time. Wherever he finds himself, and with whomever he dwells, his refuge and strength is in his trust in the Lord. That even if the world or situations turn hostile, the One who is with him is always in full control and at his call when in need. It is into this kind of a trust You and I have been called to renew and grow ourselves into. Be strong in the Lord and in the strength of His might. Let this be our prayer for the day to renew ourselves as soldiers and ambassadors of Jesus. Amen

01/11/24, 09:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Dear friends, now we are children of God, and what we will be has not yet been made known. But we know that when Christ appears, we shall be like Him, for we shall see Him as He is. All who have this hope in Him purify themselves, just as He is pure’ (1 John 3:2-3)

You and I are a miracle – for the Bible says that what we will be has not yet been made known. Jesus sees us, as we are to be (not as we are now or what we were yesterday) and calls us to purify ourselves each day to resemble our ‘To be’ person. And St Paul rightly explained this mystery when he said, ‘Not that I have already attained my target, but I strive each day to approach it’. Are we aware of this great target fixed by our loving Father towards which we have been destined on earth to grow into? At times we tend to forget this purpose for which we have been created in Jesus Christ before the foundations of this world. Distractions of wealth, luxury, power, authority or many other things of this passing world weaken the desire in us to blossom towards the ultimate. There are times we have become disheartened understanding that somewhere we have halted and stunted in our growth. Here we have the array of thousands of saints who lived like us, before us and victoriously claimed their targets, to be rewarded by the Lord in their Eternal Homes. These kindly look over us, willingly interceding for us and encouraging us to keep moving up from where we have halted. Let us seek their intercession, that the incense of their pleasing lives and prayers may open blessings from the Heavens, and we may be empowered to live as ‘children’ of God. Always remember, what we will be is yet to be made known, but we strive hard to achieve what we have been created with love for by the Father of Lights. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
02/11/24, 09:30 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘One thing have I asked of the Lord, that will I seek after: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to gaze upon the beauty of the Lord and to inquire in his temple’ (Psalm 27:4)

The one desire of a soul that has experienced its solace, is to be in the house of the Lord all its days. Whatever it does is with this perspective of dwelling with God. The souls of the righteous are in the hands of God and no torment will ever touch them – the Bible teaches us so. While in this world, it is but a privilege to be disciplined by the Lord. To be tested and found worthy by Him is the one reward each man looks forward to at the end of this term of life. Thus, Jesus reminded us that it would be of no profit/gain for the one, who has achieved everything (that is passing) but for his life.  The Psalmist David wisely meditated and pleaded for ‘Eternal life’ with His Creator, accepting that nothing could suffice for the redemption of one’s soul. On this day, when the world remembers souls who have left their earthly homes for the Eternal, let us remember and pray for all souls, those from our homes, families – dear and near, that having obtained their eternal bliss, they may be a guidance, and help us to find our way home. Let our prayers be - I seek after the Lord this day, that which I have been asking for, to be with the Lord all the days of my life, to gaze upon the beatitude and pray for those who direly need His mercy. Amen

03/11/24, 07:06 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Is it not in your going with us, so that we are distinct, I and your people from every other people from the face of the earth?’ (Exodus 33:16)

The Book of Exodus tells us that the Lord spoke to Moses face to face as a man speaks to his friend. It was at the Tent of meeting, pitched far away from the residences of the people that Moses used to converse with the Lord and inquire of Him His will, interceding for God’s people. God’s presence and His glory would descend to the place, whenever this God man approached the Tent of meeting. Such was the powerful prayer Moses made to the Lord and how he was favoured by the ‘Most High’. He witnessed to the peoples’ that it was God’s presence and accompaniment with them that made them distinct and superior amongst all the earth. The Old Covenant permitted God’s own to a restricted understanding of who God was. It was Jesus who entered into the Holy of Holies, to break down all barriers that stood before God and man (through His sacrifice of self), that a new Covenant of friendship was opened to mankind before their God. In Jesus, through His precious blood, the foundation of the new Covenant, we have access to the Father. We have been privileged to be accompanied to the Holiest Heavens because of Jesus. And every time, we approach the Lord during the Eucharist, the Lord speaks to us face to face, as a man speaks to his friend. Lord Jesus, open the eyes of my heart to see you, worship and adore you and glorify you every moment in time by offering myself with Jesus in every Holy mass I attend. Amen

03/11/24, 17:57 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): 
04/11/24, 08:44 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For the love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this; that one has died for all…that those who live might no longer live for themselves but for Him who for their sake died and was raised’ (2 Corinthians 5:14,15)

The apostle Paul was inspired and motivated by the love of Christ that surmounted him. The mystery of the Gospel that he preached to all nations far and wide, was nothing but the breadth, length, height and depths of the love of Jesus that was rooted deep within him. All his sufferings, persecution, ailments and difficulties he encountered was assimilated by him in the light of faith – for the love of the Lord. He was convinced that even though his earthly tent (outer self) was being worn out day by day, he had a massive dwelling place in Heaven, eternal that God would reward him with. For our understanding and encouragement in faith, the Holy Spirit points to us the example of this fiery apostle, a tent maker by profession, who strived hard to build God’s kingdom. Let us take up every task of the day in the same spirit, for the love of Christ, who died for us to redeem us – that all may be done for His praise and glory. You and I have been appointed stewards for God’s wealth and people. The greater our love for the Lord and gratitude for all that He obtained for us, the greater will be our faithfulness and trustworthiness to our call. Let us thus no longer live for ourselves and merely for our selfish motives, but for helping, encouraging and edifying the ‘other’ whom Jesus has redeemed likewise according to the desire of His Father. Through His Holy Spirit, we are controlled, we are appointed for good works and encouraged to do our best. May the love of the Lord be your garb and strength this day. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
05/11/24, 08:45 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘See this alone I found, that God made human beings straightforward, but they have devised many schemes’ (Ecclesiastes 7:29)

Everything on earth was created by the Lord because He had a purpose for it. The sun, stars, Heavenly bodies were all predestined that they may shine at God’s command and provide the needed warmth and light for life. Every human being was created in God’s image and likeness – in preternatural holiness, with capability to love and co-create with God. It was the jealousy of evil that gave rise to death and defeat for mankind. Man was made the crown of all created by his Creator, the delight of His maker, to reflect the glory that was given to him by God. This is what today’s Word from the Lord interprets as ‘straightforward’ or ‘upright’, without deceit, conceit or fallacy. It was when men gave in to sin – though fully aware of God’s eternal and divine power, ignored to give Him glory or thank Him, they were enslaved to the darkness of the world around that enveloped their hearts, minds and thoughts. To them, survival on the earth was merely the ‘devising’ or ‘scheming’ against the other. What was created and purposed for love, thereby became redundant, futile and degraded. Often termed as ‘the fall of man’ by theologians, or ‘sinfulness of humanity’ as Paul described in his letter to the Romans, continues to this day (the modern era), so incomprehensible to the ‘Digitally intelligent man’ that we ‘still’ remain lingering in the dark for a resolution to our problems created of own accord. Unless we learn to unlearn the crookedness, the insincerity, the slyness and guile in us, we would never be worthy of God’s kingdom and His immense graces in Jesus Christ. For God created man for eternity, to love, to be merciful and to find peace in faithfulness. This is the truth of the Gospel, that we have been only asked to comply with. Amen

06/11/24, 08:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘…..Work out your own salvation with fear and trembling, for it is God who works in you, both to will and to work for his good pleasure’ (Philippians 2:12,13)

God’s saving Grace always envelops us, but it is our responsibility to be obedient and faithful to His Word. The salvation of mankind is in Jesus Christ, in acknowledging Him as Lord and Master of our lives. Everyone who seeks refuge in Jesus will be saved and gifted Eternity. For the Father has entrusted upon Him (Jesus) authority over all created. It is the Holy Spirit who teaches us to discern the will of God, the Father, to love Jesus His Son, our Saviour. To learn to surrender to Jesus, to be led by His unfailing love, though at times we may feel restricted, we may feel cowed down by the crowds, oblivion may have surmounted us for long. But even when none see us, the eyes of the Lord that is ten thousand times brighter than the sun, sees it all. This is why St Paul exhorted the Philippians to work out their salvation with fear and trembling, trusting in the Lord (not in the inclinations of their minds) that He would show the way ahead. It is the humility of the Lord that Paul first portrayed to the Philippians and what followed this self-denial of His was – Every knee shall bow before the name of Jesus and every tongue confess that He alone is Lord. Let this day be a new day when we offer ourselves, everything we possess before the Lord, to be blessed and multiplied. To do His will, to be His own and live our purpose to its fullest. God bless you. Amen

07/11/24, 09:37 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I rise before dawn and cry for your help; I have put my hope in your Word’ (Psalm 119:147)

The faithfulness and righteousness of God is what prompts the psalmist to rise early and offer his prayers. Throughout ‘Psalm’ 119, the steadfast love of the Lord, and His unfailing promise of salvation to the one who keeps His commandments, is proclaimed. All prophets of the Old Testament had this strong conviction of the blessings that would flow to God’s people, the moment they gave heed to God’s Word and trust in it. Even the one who was bewildered and disarrayed seeing massive atrocities committed by God’s people (prophet Habakkuk), to whom was revealed the price these people were to pay for the enormity of their sins – the love of the Lord filled him with Divine peace and hope. He thus proclaimed – In God was his strength, his joy and Salvation; though the fig trees had not blossomed, vines produced no fruit, the stalls were emptied of herds, in all these deprivations of God’s people, they would find perseverance, strength, courage and enlightenment from the Lord. The double-edged sword of God’s Word reminds us too, that we are protected, shielded, comforted and preserved for greater works, because of the unfailing love of Jesus for us. Let ‘hope’ govern us, the hope of the risen Christ, His promise of redemption and His peace be the beacon of our lives, as we keep journeying through the day. The Word lead you. Amen

08/11/24, 09:49 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘My soul waits for the Lord, more than those who watch for the morning’ (Psalm 130:6)

The morning is sure to come, no matter how dark and long the night may seem. The reality of the dawn breaking forth is the assurance for that ‘one’ who trusts in God. That his every prayer and supplication will be accepted, received, and processed ‘in favor’ and in line with God’s master plan for his behalf. You and I are called to thus patiently wait for God’s great works to be revealed. What could be more irksome than to wait for something, to see the fulfilment of one’s dreams – in the context of a fast-paced world, where our hopes may seem as mere mirages or fancies, and the oblivion/ the fear of being singled out from the swarming majority, an unpleasant reality. The Word of the Most High teaches us that what brings in a change is not one’s desire or relentless efforts alone, but grace and mercy from above. Without the latter, we would all be as though catching after the wind or chasing after shadows that hardly exist without light. The Light in us is Jesus and with Him all that we have dreamt of, of been created for and desire from the depths of our hearts will be fulfilled and find fruition. Wait for the Lord! Be patient, let your hearts garner courage; for the Lord is good to all those who wait for Him and take joy in His coming. I pray for you, that your faith be increased, your love and patient waiting for the Lord be motivated and you be blessed with all that you have been asking for in prayer. In Jesus name. Amen

09/11/24, 09:50 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You will succeed in whatever you choose to do, and light will shine on the road ahead of you’ (Job 22:28)

Because of the love of Jesus for you, you will succeed in whatever endeavour you choose to take up from now on. Your road ahead will be bright and lighted, because He goes before you, to level the mountains, the obstacles that may deter your journey. He will make your footsteps firm and in broad daylight will you walk. All these are the promises the Lord gives to the ‘One’ who honors Him, who reveres God before any other. The ‘one’ who has perceived the true meaning of what it is to love. You and I love those who love and consider us, those who are worthy to be loved (according to human standards), Our human hearts are incapable of loving another irrespective of his worth (power, money, influence and reciprocity). But every man is loved by His Creator, has been redeemed by the precious blood of His Creator, ‘All’ because he is loved by the Most-High. Do we have a claim before the Lord for disregarding or rejecting some? Let us ask Jesus to fill us with His love, His humility that made Him reach down to us on earth, to reclaim us to the Heavens with the Father. You and I are called to look at ‘Him’ and to ‘where’ He has called us to be, not to the trivial things around that keep us glued down, vying and battling with one another for nothing. May we be reminded of this special love of the Lord for us, the light that leads us through. Amen

11/11/24, 10:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse For Today

‘We have not ceased to pray for you, asking that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding’ (Colossians 1:9)

Today we thank God the Father, for what He accomplished for us through the sacrifice of Jesus, His Son – We have been delivered from the domain of darkness and given inheritance with the saints in the Kingdom of Light.  To learn how to walk in a way worthy of the Lord, to bear fruit in every good work, and to increase in the knowledge of God. Our driving force is the knowledge of the will of God. Every act and occurrence we relate to this ‘will’ of God, which is for our salvation, purification, and redemption. One tends to grow spiritually when he learns to relate everything to God’s will. His prayers and supplications thus spring out from the love of the Lord. By his deeds and words, he makes up for what lacks in Christ’s sufferings for the sake of His body the Church. This in other words would mean offering our unpleasant experiences for the Salvation of the souls (in Christ’s body) who are in dire need of our prayerful support. When You and I understand that we have been called by the love of the Lord, to continue the saving mission of Jesus, we then attain the knowledge and willingness to serve, to pray and petition for those around us, especially for the poor souls in purgatory who plead for our prayers. May the occurrences and deeds in your life today transform into worthy prayers for the salvation of poor souls in purgatory. This is the will of our Father and You have been predestined to serve in His kingdom. Amen

12/11/24, 10:24 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Of those whom You gave me, I have lost not one’ (John 18:9)

Jesus led his disciples to the garden that lay beyond the brook Kidron, there He prayed with his beloved disciples. He knew well of what was to take place soon. The pain and grief of being betrayed by the one whom he loved dearly, his concern for the lives of his disciples whom the Father had given Him – He knew that they (His sheep) would be scattered that night, in fulfillment of the Scriptures. So to the very last, He protected them. To the soldiers and the Pharisees who drew near to arrest Him, Jesus tells them that He was the ‘Jesus of Nazareth’ whom they had come in search of. The moment they heard the words of Jesus, they drew back and fell to the ground. The grace of God, ‘His power’ was experienced by these men (soldiers and those who had come to arrest Jesus), so that they may not harm His disciples. Then Jesus spoke to them, if You are looking for me, let these men go. We read thus, that Jesus was arrested and His disciples fled that night, unharmed by the authorities. Every time the name of Jesus of Nazareth is uttered in our midst – at our homes, institutions, places of worship, and so on, there is power, there is saving grace that flows to redeem all His disciples – You and me. Jesus made sure that none of those whom His Father had entrusted Him were harmed or lost. What about those whom the Lord has entrusted into our care and to us? Do we care for them selflessly? Have we received them thankfully from the hands of our Lord? Remember, every gift from the Lord, He gives from His heart, the moment we realize, we are capacitated to pray and support all whom the Lord has given to our care, setting aside their shortcomings and failures. The Grace of God has appeared to save all mankind and ‘We’ have been empowered by this Grace to love and serve as Jesus did. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
14/11/24, 08:48 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Let us fall into the Hands of the Lord, but not into the Hands of mortals; for equal to His majesty is His mercy, and equal to His name are His works’ (Sirach 2:18)

To persevere in our Trust in the Lord – this is what the Holy Spirit calls us to focus on. Because His mercy equals His majesty and the magnitude of His blessings testify His name, the author of the Book of Ecclesiasticus tells us. And how do we exhibit this unwavering faith in the Lord? By waiting patiently for His mercy, but convincing ourselves that because we have reposed our faith in the Lord, we will never fail, by accepting whatever befalls us (though hard) – and this is how the Lord grooms us in humble submission and obedience to His Word. What our frail minds may perceive or understand may be ‘nothing’, hardly the tip of the iceberg – or contrary to the reality concealed from our vision. It’s human nature to make mistakes, to judge erroneously, to be deceived by half-truths or lies. For the Prince of this world had from time immemorial been engaged in hiding the truth from us or exposing us to falsehood, because He feared what would become of us, going by the way of our Creator. But our Lord knew how feeble and vulnerable we were, how we thirsted for His saving grace; and ‘how’ to get us through all the mess we are in. Therefore, let us approach Jesus, for power, strength, and His love to triumph over all – that weakens, that threatens, that slows us down from moving to the ‘purpose’ for which we have been created by His love. Indeed, His love for me, (above all the created) has made me great. Thank you Jesus! Amen

15/11/24, 08:19 - Megha Sherino: 
15/11/24, 10:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Look to yourselves, that we lose not those things which we have worked for, but that we receive a full reward’ (2 John 4:8)

Watchfulness is what the Holy Spirit asks of us each day – to be mindful of the gifts we have been blessed with. What God has entrusted to us is the treasure in clay jars. It is our prerogative to protect, preserve, judiciously use, and multiply our talents and gifts. To guard and defend one’s possessions from being unlawfully encroached upon is one’s basic right. He may have to observe a safe distance, set up boundaries around his things, prevent unwanted intrusions, and do all in his care to ensure his possessions are safe. The same logic applies to matters of the ethereal world – faith. Faith must be guarded, nurtured, and encouraged through frequent listening to God’s Word. What we have obtained by way of baptism is the gift of faith, the privilege of being born again in God’s kingdom, to be built up by His Spirit, and to be nurtured to bring forth the harvest of a hundred-fold. To be filled with the weeds of this world is so natural – the weeds of jealousy, greed, self-righteousness, pride, etc. tend to intrude and interfere with our growth. Each day is a striving for growth, and there is only this ‘narrow way’ to be freed from all that obstructs and disrupts our progress. To look on to Jesus, to walk in His way and obey His commandments – that will lead us to the Light. His way and commandment is to love; even if we may be failing to keep on this track following the Lord, He turns back with a heart of love, willing to help us make it through. What He seeks in us is our determination to move on, to be watchful of what we have obtained from Him and be eager to persevere to receive our full reward. Never give up, there’s always a reason to be hopeful and for a comeback in life. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

16/11/24, 09:25 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Woe to them! They have taken the way of Cain; they have rushed for profit into Balaam’s error; they have been destroyed in Korah’s rebellion’ (Jude 1:11)

You and I know well the sin of Cain, that emerged from ‘jealousy’; the ‘lure for wealth and greater gains’ that coerced Balaam to speak against the Israelites as commanded to him by Balak the pagan King; the ‘arrogance and self-pride’ that prompted Korah to rebel against God- man Moses. It is so easy to slip into the sins that these had fallen prey to, because we too live in a world of imperfections, where slander, gossip, and fallacies reign the roost. God’s Word reminds us that even those whom He delivered from Egypt, as His own, could not all inherit the promised land, because of their unbelief. The Angels were not above board when some of these celestial beings abandoned their God-given positions and refuge in God’s kingdom, who had to face chains and darkness. Likewise, those who were led by the ways of the world towards lust and immorality of the flesh, had to undergo the punishment of eternal fire. Even today, the lust of the flesh, cravings of the eyes, and pride of life are the three baits that blind one from seeking his destiny, the Heavenly Kingdom. Let us pray that our hearts be filled with the mercy, peace, and love of Jesus, to prevent us from getting swayed by the tactics of the baited traps the world may attract us to. Lord Jesus let nothing dissuade us from following you, assimilating your truth, and thus obtaining the light of life. Comb us within the shadow of your wings. Amen

17/11/24, 07:34 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘We did this not because we do not have that right (the right to such help), but in order to offer ourselves as a model for you to imitate’ (2 Thessalonians 3:9)

When Paul was in Thessalonica preaching the Gospel there, he was determined to work hard to earn a living, rather than depend on the support given by the believers in Thessalonica or elsewhere. In his later epistles (especially the one to the Corinthians) he emphasized the command of God that those who preached the Gospel were to be supported by the communities who received their service. To financially help spread God’s Word is - the obligation of each one of us believers, as the Church exhorts us through its precepts. Even though this legitimate privilege existed for Paul the apostle, he chose to provide for himself through tent-making (his profession). This he did with the intent to set an example for believers that diligence and sincerity were the hallmarks of a Christ follower. And, this is the message the Holy Spirit holds for us today, whoever we may be, or whatever our tasks or roles in life be. To a Christian, his way is the way of the Lord – the true but ‘narrow way’, not for the sake of suffering or sacrifice, but to sow the seeds of diligence, faithfulness, and love to those around him, to posterity….and what more. Thus, the Lord said, If someone compels you to go a mile with him, to walk an extra mile too; not to withhold our tunic to the one, who takes away our cloak. To offer good for evil, to be kind even when it hurts, and thereby overcome the evil one. This is the model the Lord asks us to practice and demonstrate through our lives. To give away anger, haughtiness, slander, malice to one another and learn to respect the dignity of the one whom the Lord has redeemed by His blood. How difficult, but with Grace all things are possible for the one who yearns for it. Lord Jesus, direct our motives and actions in your truth. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
18/11/24, 08:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Love bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things’ (1 Corinthians 13:7)

Humanity was smitten so hard a blow by sin, that it not only left its imprints/scar on our preternatural glory, but also deprived us of our innate peace, foresight, and perfection. We were created in the image and likeness of God, to serve, keep, and preserve the fabric of creation in its harmony and fruitful relationship with God. But somewhere we lost the sense of ‘truth’ – in our perceptions, our beliefs, and in our reasoning. Thus though we persevered tirelessly to uphold our faith in the Lord, increase our understanding and knowledge of the Lord, and trust in His wonder works for us (through our works of faith, our religious rigour, and practices), we were able to see only in part, a reflection through a dim mirror. Today the Spirit of Jesus helps us to understand why we were not able to know in full, to understand in its entirety, and to endure through hardships to reap a hundred-fold – Because we were failing in our instinct to love. To love would mean to resemble God (love unconditionally), to fulfill our purpose of creation – to protect the other (always), to trust in God’s purpose for the other, to hope that the best is yet to follow. And it is here that our inner beings become strengthened to endure all things. Even when faith in us wavers, when the hope in us becomes as dim as a flickering lamp, this ‘love’ would prevail and help us conquer the darkest days of life. Yes, despite all things, we become ‘overwhelming conquerors’ because of Jesus who loved us and gave up His life for us. Amen

20/11/24, 10:04 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Wake up! Strengthen what remains and is about to die, for I have found your deeds unfinished in the sight of my God’ (Revelation 3:2)

Today the Spirit of the Lord emphasizes the need for growing in virtues – the seven Spirits as Chapter 3 of the Book of Revelation begins with. These seven Spirits are the ‘characteristics’ of God Himself, the sum of all three Theological virtues and the four Cardinal virtues. These are prudence, justice, temperance, fortitude, faith, hope and charity. To the Church of Sardis, an active fruitful Church founded in the late 1st century by St John or by his disciples (as tradition explains), the Lord chastises the believers of the Church, asking them to wake up from their slumber, to enliven all that is dead or extinct in them, so that their deeds may win them everlasting remembrance in the Book of life. It was not that these believers in Jesus Christ were unaware of their Christian responsibilities of charity, built upon faith in the Lord and hope in His reward during His second coming. They were well aware and were doers of the Word of God, they were fervent in their Spiritual practices, did show up for worship regularly. In the eyes of the world – they were the best community or people that anyone would love to be part of. And here comes the irony, though they possessed the best, anointing was dwindling among these people, thus many of the deeds (works of charity) they indulged in turned mediocre, lacking grace, and filled with distortions, guile, and vanity. These believers were asked to hold fast to the Word of God that was made known to them, renew themselves, repent, and seek Grace from the Lord to perfect all that they were doing. The Word of God is ‘Power’, like a double-edged sword, that pierces through, intervenes, and renews the hearts, flesh, and works of men. It is from here that one is strengthened to persevere his call, to invest his talents and traits, to discard the weeds (those sowed by the Evil one) to thereby produce fruit that would bring glory to God and support the lives of many. Jesus asks you to pause in life a bit, step back and garner strength from the Word and then renew our actions from the love of the Lord. You are called to be the wonder-worker for the Lord. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

21/11/24, 11:03 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘O Lord, you hear the desire of the afflicted;
    you will strengthen their heart; you will incline your ear’ (Psalm 10:17)

While explaining to us God’s tremendous mercy – His concern for the afflicted, those pushed to the margins, those ostracised, this prayer of the Psalmist directs our hearts to be merciful. It is the virtue of understanding that a man needs to obtain from ‘the Most High’ to discern between what pleases and displeases God. And this gift of understanding, the Lord gives to those who are like children – humble in heart and open to receive from Him. It is to these that the Kingdom of Heaven belongs to as Jesus once told His disciples. For us human beings to accept the fact that God is God and we are merely humans, a series of setbacks and trials in life’s journey may seem essential. How many have been those times when we disputed and vied with one another for the most unimportant things in life, only to realize later that they weren’t in any way essential to our growth. What flourishes, what expands one’s horizon of understanding, what helps men to make the right choices in life is not envisioned by human intellect, but ‘Grace’ – God’s favor upon one that guards him like a shield. Because of which, this one humbly offers his supplications to the Lord, he is blessed, is encouraged, his prayers answered, and his destiny set for success. WE have a God who never abandons the desolate in Spirit, the downcast, the suffering, the upright, but encourages each to begin all over again. You and I who have received this mercy from Him, are called to reciprocate the same compassion to those in need. Amen

22/11/24, 09:30 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you that you should go and bear fruit and that your fruit should abide, so that whatever you ask the Father in my name, He will give it to you’ (John 15:16)

We are what we are today, because Jesus loved us, chose us from among the swarming millions, and appointed us for the task of bearing fruits for His Kingdom. He gave us His ‘own’ example of how He loved the Heavenly Father, obeyed His will, and did all in His disposition to fulfill God’s will – the Salvation of mankind. We are likewise called to obey the will of Jesus and this is how we reciprocate His love for us. Not by our might, determination, and strength can we fulfill Jesus’s command to love one another. Jesus advises us to be filled with His love, to abide by the inspiration of His Holy Spirit in us. This is how we obtain well being, welfare, fortune, and prosperity that Jesus desires for us; His love is enduring, is patient, is not self-seeking, is always hopeful, protects and withstands all. To this end, we have been appointed by Jesus to reap a harvest. And to meet this objective, whatever we ask the Father in Jesus’ name we are assured to receive in bounty. With this understanding, let us look into our hearts, our orientations, desires and wishes. What more can be love than giving up one’s life for his dears. We are challenged to emulate Jesus in this, not loom large on the machinations and influences of this world, that may seem attractive but choke the life-giving Word of the Lord in us. Let us ask the Holy Spirit to guide us, how well we could channelize our talents for co-creating the good for the other and in cooperation with God who created all for the good. Amen

23/11/24, 09:50 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Preach the Word, be ready in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, and exhort, with complete patience and teaching’ (2 Timothy 4:2)

Our fundamental duty as a believer (in Christ) is to make Christ and His love known to the world. Paul exhorted Timothy, his beloved disciple to diligently pursue His Divine ministry. There would be those times, when we would feel discouraged, when it may seem that our prayers have been unanswered for long, that life’s debilitating crisis and difficulties may seem to be strangulating our enthusiasm, when our words may be as though falling into deaf ears, whatever be……Timothy was told to keep reproving wrongs, rebuking and rectifying faults, enduring suffering, and to continue in His task of teaching/guiding the flock of God entrusted to him in faithfulness and sober-mindedness. The truth may be evaded by men, but for how long? What is definite and perfect can never be watered or diluted for convenience however – Sound principles, Divine precepts, doctrines, morals and living in accordance to the Divine Law can never be refuted, checked or diminished by human laws or technical upgradations. This is what we mean when we speak about the ‘value’ system grounded in faith, which endures time, generation gap and space. Let us look into our flocks, those entrusted to our care – in our families, societies, communities, parishes and institutions – let nothing defined by the Lord – His laws, precepts and commandments be violated for the sake of human aggrandizement, human laws, technology or the era we live in. You and I are called to preach the Word – in season and out, to reprove, rebuke, exhort with complete patience, till the Lord and His Kingdom is established in our midst. Amen

24/11/24, 07:00 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You are a garden locked up, my sister, my bride; You are a spring enclosed, a sealed fountain’ (Song of Songs 4:12)

The King of Kings, Jesus today addresses You and me, the souls endeared to Him. Not in the way we see ourselves (sometimes), Jesus tells us that each of us is a garden, a spring enclosed and a foundation sealed for God’s works. Not a forest where every kind of fauna grows in a disarrayed manner, are we called to be; but to appear as that garden that emits beauty, charm, orderliness, and fruitfulness. In each of us is the Spring of life, the Holy Spirit, given us in Baptism, from which Divine grace flows to bear fruits of the Kingdom. A sealed fountain ‘one’ that is protected from all kinds of foreign interventions, and from being polluted. We must acknowledge God’s care for us, that we are within the wall of fire of His protection, that our skills, talents, personality would bring in long-lasting fruits of repentance, reconciliation and charisms to cater to the building up of others. All our works rooted in the love for the Lord, triggered by the inspirations of His Holy Spirit, aimed at encouraging and building up His Church (abandoning not even the most insignificant ones) is what the Lord expects to see in us fulfilled. It is the King of Kings, who has called and designated us for this task. Today, as we celebrate the Feast of this Great King of All – Jesus Christ, let us pray for the strength to honour His call, His Kingdom and do all for His greater glory. God bless you. Amen

25/11/24, 09:20 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For He himself is our peace, who has made us both one and has broken down in his flesh the dividing wall of hostility’ (Ephesians 2:14)

What a befitting way to begin the new week with the thought of ‘peace’ and that Jesus is our peace. HE who was God but imbibed our human form, so that we may be reconciled to the Father, that we may realize our true worth, that we are heirs to ‘the glory’ of the Heavenly kingdom. This is the comfort and calm, Jesus instills into the hearts of those who believe in Him and repose their trust in Him. That, however lacklustre or grim their worldly state of affairs may seem, however irrevocable, ineligible, or even repulsive their prospects for a revival may look, God loved us so immensely that Jesus would not give up for any of them for any reason. ‘Those’ whom His Father had entrusted to Him for His works, no one could snatch them away or derail them from their ultimate purpose in life. And since they belonged not to ‘this world’, it was sure thing that the world would despise and condemn them. How many have been those times when You and I felt that we were aliens to those around – our dear and near. How many times have we experienced being underrated and undervalued by men around! Because men who were in themselves imperfect (with planks in their eyes) attempted to judge us by the speck that they saw in our eyes. Whatever, the Lord reminds us to focus on Him, to open up to Him, asking Him for healing, forgiveness, and love…………..When the world stepped behind to support, always remember, the eyes of the Lord was fixed on you to redeem, reclaim, restore and build up. That is why Isaiah overwhelmed by the unfailing love of the Lord exclaimed to all the message You and I fail to hear from the Lord – You are precious and honoured in my sight, and because I love you, I will give people in exchange for you and nations in exchange for your life. Amen

26/11/24, 10:31 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Though you have not seen Him, you love Him: and even though you do not see Him now, you believe in Him and are filled with an inexpressible glorious joy, obtaining the outcome of your faith, the Salvation of your souls’ (1 Peter 1:8,9)

Faith is inevitable for You and I to obtain Salvation – ‘faith’ in Jesus Christ, which calls for (i)obedience to His Word, (ii)being sanctified by His precious blood, and (iii)being anointed by His Spirit each day. It was this Salvation that would render grace upon grace to all mankind, made known to the prophets of the Old Testament. To the prophets was revealed the ‘suffering servant’ of God, whose stripes, bruises, wounds, and afflictions brought for all mankind grace upon grace. Similar would be the grievances of a believer, one with genuine faith in the Lord, that his testing would be the reason for glory, honour, and praise before Lord Jesus at His second coming. Thus, we are invited to a life of hope, one that is always set upon an unfading, imperishable and undefiled inheritance – Eternal life, to be sober in mind, to be holy as our Father is, not to be conformed to our former ignorant ways, but to portray the risen Christ in word and Spirit. To put away from us all malice, hypocrisy, deceit and envy, and to long for being filled with pure love, that which would help us work for our Salvation. And anyone who pulls up his sleeves, and steps forward towards this outcome, will surely be rewarded by the Father, as He promised ‘ Whoever believes in Him will not be put to shame’. Amen
