27/11/24, 09:43 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Having the eyes of your hearts enlightened, you may know what is the hope to which He has called you, what are the riches of His glorious inheritance in the saints, and what is the immeasurable greatness of His power towards us who believe, according to the working of His great might that He worked in Christ…….’ (Ephesians 1:18-20)

Today the Good Lord desires that you and I may garner an augmented understanding of the gifts that we are being enriched with each day. To all who believe in Jesus Christ, in His saving power over sin, sickness, and the Evil one, to them is given an immeasurable great power. It is this same power that worked in the resurrection of Jesus from His grave, His elevation to be seated at the right hand of The Father in Heaven, and His overarching authority over all created in Heaven and Earth. By this, we have become privileged to partake in a glorious inheritance, with the community of the Saints, to live lives adorable to God and men. Because of this power, we have been gifted to do the supernatural, the things we were not sure about fulfilling with our meager strengths, to build and enrich the Body of Christ, to endure when resistances rule the roost in life, to conquer everything with the ‘love’ of Jesus Christ. When God’s Spirit opens the eyes of our hearts to perceive, to discern, and to cognize what in truth we have been called for, we imbibe the characteristics of Christ – faithfulness, peace, and compassion towards all. Towards Him and like Him, the Head of the Church, we grow, and this is our incessant call each day. Lord Jesus, help us to outgrow ourselves to become You. Amen

28/11/24, 09:03 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today 
'Let us then go to Him outside the camp and bear the abuse He endured' (Hebrews 13:13)

The camp is the flesh, 'our body' that we nourish and comfort daily. How we wish and pray for 'a pleasant weather', for better resources, favourable situations and people in our lives - how relieved to be with our dear and near, of being appreciated by the crowds, of enjoying success through life's journey, who would not desire for these? By giving us the gift of life, our Creator who loved us, intended all creation for our betterment, but when sin began to dominate human lives, we became enslaved to the Created and abandoned our authority over all to the evil one who claimed to be The Prince of this world. When Jesus, the Son of God incarnated on earth, He underwent tremendous humiliation, endured' pain in His sinless body and submitted to be crucified, to purge from us the curse of sin - the slavery of mankind. Do we understand and appreciate the price of our Salvation? how we regained our authority in Jesus. Therefore let not the flesh prevail, but let us be led by the promptings of the Holy Spirit. Let us move out of the camp (our comfort zone) to accept life's sufferings for saving souls, in thankfulness for what we received from Jesus. Amen

‘Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us’ (Galatians 3:13)

‘We’ whom the Lord knows are fallible, i.e., unable to obey the given ‘law’ or ‘commandment’, are given a privilege – a share in the numerous blessings accorded to the righteous Father Abraham. What Abraham expressed throughout his life, in word and deed was trust in the unfailing promise of God, that purported him righteous before God. That God is able and would bring into existence a perpetual place of habitation for His dear ones, that God would give him off-springs who would possess the promised land, that God is his wealth and abundance – all that he needed while living as a nomad in the promised land. And we become sons of this father Abraham, by faith in Jesus Christ. Because in Jesus, You and I receive ‘all’ the promises in Heaven and on earth, those material and spiritual– those given to Abraham. To God the Father, it didn’t matter whether we were Greek, Roman, Jew, or gentile, whether man or woman, whether we were outcasts, sinners or ridiculed by men, whatever be our curse, sinfulness, guilt or situation. Believe in Jesus today, call upon His ‘powerful’ name, and we would like Abraham become justified before God, all because of what Jesus paid for us on the Cross. On the Cross was taken our shame, guilt, sin, curse, sickness, and all that deterred us……He nailed them to the Cross as the Eternal sacrifice to the Father, so that mankind may be redeemed, set free, and live as free citizens of the Heavenly Kingdom. Not by might, influence, intellect, or good works, we have become sons of the Heavenly Father, but by Jesus, who loved us and gave us everything through the Cross and by the Holy Spirit poured into our hearts at the foot of His Cross. Amen

‘Since You have taken off your old self with its practices and have put on the new self which is being renewed in knowledge in the image of its Creator’ (Colossians 3:10)

We know that Jesus Christ has raised us up to be with Him in the Heavenly domain, setting our minds on things out there and not below. But the mere conviction of the death and burial of our old selves and its resurrection into a renewed one, would not suffice to transform us. We as normal human beings very often get annoyed, irritated, and deterred – these are so deeply imprinted in our human natures, life circumstances, and in the people around us; though we sincerely desire to get rid of anger, wrath, malice, slander………we fail and at times miserably. This means that the renewal of the self is a process ‘ongoing’ and continuing, takes off not by the flesh, but by God Himself. It is this power from above that brings us to the perfect knowledge of the image of our Creator – love. Every new day is a battle of sorts for the one who is with Christ – a struggle within (resolve of the mind to choose things above) and this is won over by the power of the Holy Spirit given to us. Jesus Christ is all what, You and I need to win this struggle, to get over temptations, to be more merciful, ardent, and understanding. His love has been poured into us by His Spirit, who induces us to keep seeking Him and asking from Him. Today, ask Jesus to occupy all the vacuum areas of your life, to imbibe the strength and power to love like Him, forgive like Him, and discern your true image. This transformation is our true purpose in life. I pray that you garner the courage to take one step forward towards this God-given purpose for you. Amen

‘For because He himself has suffered when tempted, He is able to help those who are being tempted’ (Hebrews 2:18)

Jesus Christ being in very nature, God, abandoned the glory and advantage of Godliness and assumed the form of man. This He did (taking the resemblance of man, and not of angels) to make propitiation for the sins of mankind. In all aspects (except for sinfulness), He was to be like You and me, to be that merciful and faithful High Priest before the Father on our behalf. He took on death on His body, to break the power of the evil one – who held the power of death till then. It is thus mankind was reclaimed and freed from the shackles of suffering, sickness, curse, and death. To all those who suffer, those who are tempted, tested, and rattled by the evil one, He (Jesus) is the way (out), the truth (for good innings ahead), and life (life in abundance). Because he suffered like us, tempted to the last till the will of the Father was executed, He is able to sympathize with all who are likewise put to the test. Be aware, that the One whom we have put our trust in, is not a mere human being who cannot understand what we are going through. He doesn’t judge by what is seen outside, but looks into every heart, to pick the sincere, the true-hearted, the downtrodden, and the broken in spirit. As He says – It is these who need a physician (He), rather than any other. Let nothing of this world weigh us down from our faith in Jesus Christ. The Messiah, great King, Counsellor, the root of Jesse will stand as a banner for us all. Today, let us pray for the strengthening of the Theological virtue of faith given to us. Amen

09/12/24, 09:22 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I will not leave you as orphans; I will come to you’ (John 14:18)

It is very often easy to get lost within the maze of uncertainties of today’s world. An era where rapidity, high scores, novelty, and innovative power have become decisive factors or determinants of success; where those in the fringes, the minorities, voices of the voiceless, or even the mediocre have hardly a chance for survival. And this frightening plight that has become rampant in every sphere of human activity has made us overtly anxious, timid, and self-oriented (individualistic) in our thoughts, faith, vision, and even influenced the way we bring up our generations. What the Lord has to tell us today, is that we need to slow down, to disengage ourselves with the culture of our times which is materialistic and cold towards the needs of others/the dignity of life, and challenges one’s spiritual progress. The question that possibly would haunt us is - how would we then deal with the consequent alienation, the odds and oddities of swimming against the current? The fact that history has proved since time immemorial, the Word of God has unequivocally declared through the Bible – that we would never be left orphans, that ‘Our Creator would be our defender and refuge at all times’. To the disciples who could not envision the picture of the suffering servant in Jesus, the precariousness (consequences) of losing their prestigious positions (having to live the rest of their lives without the Lord’s physical presence with them), Jesus ensures them that they would never be alone. He would accompany them in their Spirit, embody them with grace to do greater things, and this unfailing presence of Jesus was assured to them till end times. To You and me, who get upset so soon, having to fend for ourselves at times when there is none around who cares, Jesus tells us that He cares, He prepares and steers us on-route. Every ignominy, disgrace, and abandonment we have undergone in life was His sign/proof to us that He (Jesus) was with us during that time and calling the shots, all for the best. Amen

10/12/24, 09:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Do two walk together unless they have agreed to do so?’ (Amos 3:3)

To those of us who clamour for justice in our times, the Book of Amos serves a respite, as it affirms God’s impending punishment to all men for all kinds of iniquities and transgressions they have committed. Amos was a shepherd, chosen by God to prophesy to God’s people of the ill-fate that was to follow them, had they not repented and turned away from their sinfulness. His message was unequivocally made to all the tribes of Israel (in priority) and to their neighbouring nations who were warned of the consequences of their wicked ways. Speaking from the context of a marital relationship, God points out to the need for His people to reciprocate faithfulness and oneness in their lives. While the northern tribes were accused of their immorality, iniquities and ruthlessness towards the poor, the Southern kingdom (Judah) was accused of having rejected the Law of the Lord, His Statutes and pursuing after deceit, distortion, and falsehood (imitating the ways of their pagan neighbours). Reminding them of the Holy Covenant, that bound them to God - God questions (through Amos) their integrity and waywardness in obliging to God’s commandments. To us, today’s Word is a message to introspect into our relationships – with God, within our families, those places we have been designated to serve – Do our words and deeds match? Do we honour our relationships with faithfulness and integrity? Are we ardent to be peaceable in our lives? As Jesus teaches us – No man can serve both God and Mammon, at the same time. Let us pray that our lives may be fruitful and honourable to the Lord, who has always regarded us as His treasured possession. Amen

‘Since you have kept my command to endure patiently, I will also keep you from the hour of trial that is going to come on the whole world to test the inhabitants of the earth’ (Revelations 3:10)

Have there ever been those sorts of trials that have swamped your revival hopes in life? Swaying you from ‘faith’ - believing that You are still loved by the Lord? That you still stand a chance for outgrowing into the better version from the ashes of today? If life has taken you through the hardest realities ever, then you could compare yourself with the believers in the Church of Philadelphia. The Lord promises to them that whoever has endured patiently in their earthly living, holding fast to the Word of God and living by its tenets, will be declared a winner on the Day of the Lord’s judgment. This great hour of trial which is expected during the Second coming of our Lord awaits all human beings on earth. All will have to make an account of his lifetime (his words, deeds and thoughts) that very hour before the Throne of Justice and will receive his righteous reward. But here too, the merciful Lord exempts a category of men, stating that they would not have to face this hour of trial. And these are the ones who have been tested and refined in their earthly life through the fire, just like silver is refined and gold is tested. As the crucible for silver and furnace for gold, the hearts of these were tested by the Lord through untold sufferings, humiliations, and pains. And whoever overcomes these by holding on to the name of the Lord and seeking His mercy, will not just be sanctified and justified, but also set apart as contenders for the Heavenly reward. Therefore, whenever you encounter fiery trials in life, do not be discouraged or disheartened, embolden yourself in Jesus and in His unfailing love. He will cause you to triumph before all who looked down upon your life and then would be the count down on your next best innings ahead. Amen

13/12/24, 09:34 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘This is what the Lord says – Your redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: I am the Lord your God, who teaches you what is best for you, who directs you in the way you should go’ (Isaiah 48:17)

The ways of the Lord are incomprehensible at times - hard to accept, sometimes seemingly sinister, in contrast with our wishes and dreams of living. To Jonah, obeying God’s command of preaching repentance to the Assyrians, was antithetical, because of their animosity and contention with the Jews. When appointed by God to lead His people out of Egypt, Moses felt all the more delirious, as it was easier for him (Moses) to trust his instincts (his experiences and memories of the past when none of the Israelites believed him) than to take up the God-given task. For Abraham to leave his ancestral home and venture into the unknown as a nomad, it took courage to overcome all criticisms from dear and near unfazed. And when these obeyed, discarding all inhibitions of their mind, intellect, rationale, and the crowds, they were blessed, set apart, and exalted with impeccable capabilities and powers to succeed. Today the Lord comes to you and me, asking us to trust Him. He who holds the keys of the House of David, who knows exactly what has befallen us, and what is to come, and what is yet to be done – expects us to be calm, composed and obey Him, even if things may seem grim and unclear as of now. He is at work to augment things for our best. Amen

‘And to whom did God swear that they would never enter his rest if not to those who disobeyed? So we see that they were not able to enter, because of their unbelief (Hebrews 3:18,19)

God is our refuge, stronghold, shield, fortress, and horn of our Salvation. As long as we submit to God’s authority over everything, He is our provider – even the rest needed in our lives come from Him. The term rest can be interpreted to mean tranquil, calm, peace, confidence, and encouragement. It is this that we deprive ourselves, whenever we depart from faith, from trust in the Lord and rely on our self-sufficiency. The inclinations of our mind, our intellect, experiences, and counsel from others may cause us to unravel solutions to the many problems or difficulties in life. More so, because we fail to realize that none of these would have plagued our lives without the Lord’s consent. When the Israelites were delivered from Egypt to be given the promised land, they were called to a life of complete ‘trust’ in God. It was God who delivered them from the slavery of the Egyptians, who led them out into the promised land, which they were to possess soon. The earlier inhabitants of this land were displaced (because of their sinfulness) and their land handed over to God’s people, that they may glorify the Lord through their lives. But those who rebelled and challenged the many obstacles that came their way while in the wilderness, had to succumb to death even before seeing the promised land. Their unbelief proved them fatal. It is this message that the Spirit of God imbibes in us this day to return, and reclaim the glory that is ours – In Jesus is our refuge, shield, strength, and rest. In belief and waiting for His intervention will be our rest and reprieve for a lifetime. Amen

'Make me to know thy ways, O LORD; teach me thy paths. Lead me in thy truth, and teach me, for thou art the God of my salvation' (Psalm 25:4-5)

17/12/24, 05:59 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today 

'For we are what He has made us created in Christ Jesus for good works which God prepared beforehand to be our way of life' ( Ephesians 2:10)

Each one of us is that masterpiece of God created through Jesus Christ for a distinct way of life. Not to be configured to the ways of the world, or what people around usually do, but reclaimed gloriously through grace to be with Christ in Heaven. Our call is to know Jesus, and live a life in tandem with the Gospel light. To be the light in the dark, to be the hope for the disheartened, towards this we have been designated our specific roles in life. Whatever be our profession, vocation or identity, we resemble the Lord, being members of His body, the Church. Our Salvation is obtained for which we tirelessly Orient ourselves to. Let gratitude be the corner stone of our convictions and let each day bring glory to our Creator who compassionately gifted us Himself. May peace and joy be your gifts from the Lord today. Amen

20/12/24, 07:18 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today 

'For in Him all things in Heaven and on earth were created, things visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers of powers, all things have been created through Him and for Him' (Colossians 1:16)

Each new day is yet another opportunity to enrich ourselves with more spiritual wisdom and understanding, that would equip ourselves to be filled with the knowledge of God's will. Our motive of living this day is fulfilling God's plan for us in perfect surrender. Therefore what we are today and where we are, are not mere coincidences, but the unraveling of the definite purpose of the Lord for our specific lives. We were created for unique good works to be completed through the journey of life. As each runner in a race follows his distinct track, so do each one of us pace ahead towards our goal - Eternity. Irrespective of where we stand or what we do, the shortest and most compatible route for our race would be the track our Creator has destined and positioned us on. From our view point, other runners may seem suitably positioned, but that doesn't impact our course nor is our destiny assured if we take recourse to their tracks. So often we forget this fact and keep harping about the hardships on our track, failing to seek help from the One who has power to strengthen, sustain and establish us. Let us therefore be thankful to the Lord for what we are today, seeking strength, endurance and encouragement to move on, with an undivided focus on Him.....that we may complete our course in faithfulness and love, to meet our Creator. Fill us Jesus with the fuel of your Spirit this day as ever. Amen

20/12/24, 14:30 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Leveraging Union Territory Status for Development
Union Territory status in India offers numerous benefits, including efficient governance, central financial support, and special focus on development. The designated Union Territories in India, such as the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Chandigarh, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu, Lakshadweep, Ladakh, and Puducherry, enjoy these advantages. These territories showcase unique characteristics, ranging from scenic landscapes to cultural heritage, and leverage their Union Territory status to promote growth, preserve their identity, and enhance the quality of life for their residents. <This message was edited>
20/12/24, 14:32 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Puducherry: Formerly known as Pondicherry, Puducherry is a coastal town with a rich history of French colonial influence. The Union Territory status enables Puducherry to preserve its cultural heritage, attract tourists, and maintain its distinct identity.
21/12/24, 11:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today 

'The one who is righteous will live by faith' (Galatians 3:11)
To live by faith, this is the vocation you and I have received and been called to work on each day. This means that we go about engaging in all our tasks with sincerity and dedication, having our trust reposed in the Almighty. That He, our provider will ensure we lack nothing, we are protected from the snares of the evil, we will succeed because of the righteousness we have obtained in Jesus. Not that we have scrupulously worked to abide by God's laws and ways....there have been times we failed and that too miserably after many resolutions and efforts to change. But even when we were sinners, the mercy of the Lord hovered upon us to enlighten us towards repentance and a come back. The Holy Spirit reveals to us the enormous price for which we were reclaimed by Jesus, so that from now on, we offer our lives to Christ and He lives in us. Amid all intimidating situations of life, we are comforted by His Spirit of consolation, that after a while we would be strengthened, affirmed and established by the Lord Himself. Therefore to believe that all things will transform for the better, however grim, however disoriented or disheartening - is what it takes to gain righteousness before the Lord. This is simply what St Paul explained to live by faith and be considered righteous. The Lord has a special plan in place for you and me, so let's befriend Him, seeking support for every endeavour in life. Amen

22/12/24, 10:13 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today 

'For I will be a wall of fire all around it, says the Lord and I will be the glory within it's (Zechariah 2:4-5)

Even when Job's wealth grew immensely, and even when he suffered want, it was the Lord who protected and preserved him intact. Here the prophet Zechariah is given a vision where he sees two angels in conversation. One of the angels after measuring the width and length of Jerusalem (symbolic of the growth of Jerusalem beyond imagination) confirms to the other that the One who has caused Jerusalem to prosper, will be the wall of protection for her. Everyone who is in Christ Jesus is a new creation, destined for incredible possibilities, to be unwrapped over time. Only time can prove to us what we were created for. God asks you and me to erase the memories of the past that were distasteful, not to harbour the resentment and remorse but to progress with the confidence that comes from faith. His presence shall go with us and in all our challenges, we find comfort in Him who has loved us and has laid down his life for us. Today Jesus wants us to rid ourselves from unheeded fears, anxieties and worries that weight down on our trust in Him. He would be the secureness our hearts have longed for and the glory that awaits our days ahead. Amen

23/12/24, 09:42 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘When Christ came into the world, He said, ‘Sacrifices and offerings you have not desired, but a body have you prepared for me’ (Hebrews 10:5)

It is the will of the Father that we celebrate and honor every Christmas. The will that mankind must be cleansed once and for all from sin; sanctified, and engendered their position of glory – Sons of the Heavenly Kingdom. For this the Son of God became ‘man’, taking our resemblance, to offer to the Heavenly Father the price that was to be made for liberating all mankind. This is why Jesus made clear to us that not in sacrifices, burnt offerings and sin offerings is God pleased. These are what the Law prescribed to be offered to God to receive pardon for transgressions. The Law is but a shadow of the good things yet to come, that God wanted His people to reflect upon. No law, nor sacrifice of bulls or animals pay for the atonement of sins. The good that was yet to come is this - that God sent His only Son to the world, to save us, to redeem us from the clutches of sin, to wash away our wounds, the consequences of sin. Every Christmas brings to us this message of hope – that however dark and clouted our beings may seem before the world – Jesus has already obtained for us the grace of transformation, of unity with the Father, of fellowship and citizenship in His kingdom, and the power to overcome all these negativities, by that ‘one’ and only ‘one’ sacrifice of His on the Cross. Let us embolden ourselves with more faith and love in the Lord Jesus. Amen

26/12/24, 09:23 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And Stephen, full of grace and power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people’ (Acts 6:8)

What emboldened Stephen to witness Jesus Christ, to silence his adversaries, and to be appointed one among the first seven deacons of the Church, was the grace and power of the Lord that worked in him. He was able to perform extraordinary deeds of power – miracles, healing, and deliverance, in addition to serving the poor and needy, because of the Holy Spirit at fire in him. Despite being anointed with such unimaginable strength, what may have caused his martyrdom was the spirit of jealousy that reigned within the hearts of men who heard him. This was initially sparked by a controversy that arose between the Greeks and the Hebrews of the Early Church. The Greek believers alleged that their widows were being ignored, during the distribution of food at the community tables. This incident could have paved the way for the intrusion of evil into the otherwise harmonious community of believers, which culminated in the death of Stephen. Today as we remember this fiery ambassador of Christ, we are taught by the Holy Spirit to lean on grace and power that comes from above to manage life’s situations – whatever. At the same time, we are called to be cautious, not to permit the evil one to encroach into our life’s situations. It is through gaps created within ourselves – families, communities, and institutions that these perverse spirits intrude to create chaos in our otherwise harmonious relationships. The little Jesus born in humble circumstances on earth, looks into our hearts, knocks at our doors, so that we may be united with Him for the great banquet He has prepared for us. Lord, transform us (our hearts) to be worthy and acceptable before you and your kingdom, always. Amen!

Dr. Mini Sherino
27/12/24, 10:08 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?’ (Romans 8:35)

Because Jesus has turned us ‘more than’ conquerors through His love for us, therefore, with St Paul, we too can proclaim that none can separate us from the love of Christ. In this life’s journey, tribulations, distress, persecution, famine, deprivation, or peril may come, these can impact us upside down. All the joy of yesteryears may be forgotten by a moment of adversity that might have crept in ‘all of a sudden’ from nowhere. It is so easy to succumb to the pressures of distress and sorrow, that lead us to a realization of how weak and vulnerable we are. Without the Lord with us, we are weak, disabled, deprived, and defeated. It is His love for us that has sustained us all to this day. With Him, we have everything we need to be conquerors in this world. Today the Holy Spirit asks us to reflect on the love of the Lord, His accompaniment in every stage of our life, His timely provision for us, though many dear and near may have failed to understand our needs and His concern for our welfare every new day. Lord Jesus, I pray for the grace to be consumed by your love, to always walk under your mighty protection, to turn to you whenever I get disappointed and hopeless, and to do the things you desire of me each day. Amen

28/12/24, 11:23 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘By faith we understand that the Universe was created by the Word of God, so that what is seen was not made out of things that are visible’ (Hebrews 11:3)

All that is seen today was made out of the invisible, this is our faith. We are thereby led to believe that God who is only goodness will create the better and unseen things yet to come no shortly from now. Not by sight, but by faith, by expectation and hope we live, as believers in the Lord. This is the faith that the Lord expects to see in us, that no matter who we are and where we stand, God has a place, a plan, and still can move things for the best for us. This was the faith that made Abel righteous, that commended Enoch (who was assumed into the Heavens), the faith that persuaded Noah to revere God’s instruction and preserve humanity, that made Abraham, the Father of a multitude of nations, that made Isaac invoke the blessings for Jacob and Essau, for Jacob to bless the sons of Joseph too, for Joseph to envision the exodus, for Moses to be miraculously brought up by Pharoah’s daughter, and so on…….the Bible enumerates examples of courageous men and women who lived extraordinary lives because of their faith in the promises of God. All these were brought this hour to our remembrance by the Spirit of God because we have been called specially to display such kind of excellence in faith and works, to witness Jesus Christ, and to be His light in a world immersed in darkness and dismay. May you be inspired to live each day with love, hope and faith that builds, secures and revives that which comes from above. Amen

29/12/24, 07:50 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Put on the new self, which is being renewed in knowledge after the image of its Creator’ (Colossians 3:10)

A renewal of the heart is what the Holy Spirit asks of us today and emphasizes that we must earnestly pray for. To be renewed in knowledge each day to conform with the image of our Creator. God chose us, redeemed us through His Son Jesus Christ, as sons of the Heavenly kingdom, that we may be holy and blameless before Him. To resonate the spirit of compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience – all that we have received freely from the Most High, these we have been asked to give freely. To give freely to those in our homes, in our workplaces, in communities, and Churches, just as we were given them. Not that we earned the favour of God through our works or meritorious living, but because God loved us so much and intended that we must be saved. Through this Grace we have been saved, thus St Paul in his epistle to the Ephesians reminds us. Thus let us transform every moment of our day into a holy encounter with those around us. The feast of the Holy Family, we celebrate today, reminds us of the kind of attitude we need to uphold as sons of the kingdom. To put Christ Jesus first before all, then to do all things for the sake of His love, to build, encourage, support and love – to bring in harmony in our spaces, so that the fragrance of Christ, and His peace may radiate through us. Let us pray for this grace of transformation on this feast day – Not to be conformed to the ways of this world, but to be inspired to action every moment by the whispers of the Holy Spirit in us. Amen

30/12/24, 10:27 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And this is His commandment, that we believe in the name of His son Jesus Christ and love one another, just as He has commanded us’ (1 John 3:23)

When our lives are in harmony with God’s commandments, we have the confidence of God abiding in us and providing for us. The fundamental commandment given to all mankind, is to believe in the name of Jesus Christ the Son of God the Father, and obey His instruction to love, both inclusive. Being two sides of the same coin, this commandment stands unfulfilled if either of these conditions are not complied with. Belief in Jesus Christ as the Only Saviour of mankind is one and works of love are the other. The Holy Spirit asks us to examine ourselves in the light of this commandment of God – for ‘each and every’ prayer we have made that we claim has not been answered from above. Whether we have resorted to and trusted our skills and intellect alone to make decisions in daily life? Whether we have also considered the good and welfare of the other, before taking crucial decisions in life? God leaves it to us to think for ourselves. He respects our freedom and desires we make informed decisions in this regard so that we would not willingly transgress God’s command. Let this Christmas season and the Year end bring in a transformative change in our perceptions and attitudes, to be like Christ and put on Christ always. Amen

31/12/24, 09:30 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But the righteous live forever, and their reward is with the Lord; the Most High takes care of them’ (Wisdom 5:15)

The year 2024 with all its events, excitements, and memories (joyful and sorrowful) has traversed by and will give in to another. All things have vanished like a shadow, and like a rumour that passes by, as King Solomon quotes of the days that have gone by for the worldly one. Therefore, irrespective of the tracks of the past we journeyed through, we are called to make a start once again. Trusting in the Lord, knowing His ways and will, thanking Him for all the graces that were given us despite our unfaithful ways and seeking for extraordinary grace to make a change for the better. Because we are convinced that He rewards the ‘righteous one’; let us take account of what are those specific blessings that await the one who follows after God’s ways. They will receive a glorious crown and a beautiful diadem from the hands of the Lord. His right arm will shield and protect them. The Lord will zealously work for this one, equipping all Creation to repel the evil one, with righteousness (as a breastplate), justice (as a helmet) and holiness (as an invincible shield). While thanking the Lord for His goodness and mercy we have been benefitted in the past year, let us earnestly pray for being armed with the virtues of righteousness, justice, and holiness in the new year to come. Let God arise and let His enemies be scattered; let those who hate Him flee before Him! And let us be filled with joy, exultation, and jubilance in this Jubilee Year 2025. Amen

01/01/25, 11:38 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls’ (Matthew 11:29)

Some questions in life may still remain before us as though unanswered or even unheard by the Lord. However much we attempt to seek from Him an answer, He may just leave a smile without words. Because He knows our every being, our needs, our future, and beyond, which hardly anyone else does. At our level/stage of understanding, He knows we would never be able to comprehend the ‘why’ of His ways. With utmost love and concern for our well-being, the Lord encourages us to learn from Him the inevitable tip for life’s success – To put on the mantle of Him, His gentleness, His lowliness of heart, and He will lead us to the rest our souls have always longed for. The Year 2025 is a new beginning to learn to be like the Lord, to groom ourselves in His traits of humility, gentleness and silence. As it was proclaimed through the prophet, years before the Messiah’s coming, that in quietness and calmness of Spirit will be your rest. To us too, this year, the Spirit of Jesus enlightens our mind to enter the thresholds of the new year with more joy, peace, optimism and hope, because the Lord is at work for us, on our behalf, and He will never abandon us half way through, but carry us through that we may see the best yet to unfold in life’s journey. Lord, help us in our resolve to imitate You and only You to welcome the Jubilee Year with blessings and power. Happy new year

02/01/25, 09:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘That times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord, and that He may send the Christ appointed for you, Jesus’ (Acts 3:20)

This we pray repeatedly to the Lord, that we may experience the time of refreshing that Jesus, our Lord has obtained for us. But first we must acknowledge that we are all sinners and have fallen short of Divine glory. This realization would point us to the immediate need for God’s mercy and His forgiveness. Jesus Christ has become the expiation for the sins of the world. In Him we have become recipients of the forgiveness and mercy of God. To possess this which is already in place, our hearts must be prepared with humility and love for the Lord. Here is where we must repent, and surrender ourselves to His will and ways. St John, the evangelist explains this in simple terms thus - what we would become, has not yet been made known to us, but we are assured that when Jesus comes, we would be like Him. Therefore, every-one filled with this hope will purify himself from all the stains of the world and wait for the fulfilment of His Word. Trust in the Lord, that all your times are in His hands and he will work for the good for all those who love Him. Thank you Jesus for the hope, love, and contentment you have stirred into our hearts today, by the working of your Holy Spirit in us. Amen

04/01/25, 08:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘What we have received is not the Spirit of the world, but the Spirit who is from God, so that we may understand what God has freely given us’ (1 Corinthians 2:12)

By way of our baptism, we have been gifted the Spirit of God. This distinguishes us from the world, not that we are no more meant to live in the world, but that we have been appointed and set apart by the Spirit of God to live a life of excellence – with a designated full-fledged purpose and design. In our human nature, it is so usual that we are prone to sinfulness. And the Lord understands this well, that through His Spirit in us, He inspires us to follow His way, which leads us to the truth and life. Now to be able to obey Him (for the human spirit to yield to the promptings of the Spirit), will need a lot of courage and will-power. This is poured into our hearts through God’s love. Every moment we experience or realize the power given to us, we are anointed, which opens our hearts to surrender, and oblige to God as a reciprocation for His love, which surpasses every other understanding in us. This St John explained by saying that, we love, because He first loved us and gave up His life for us. Let us thus pray today for the humility to love Jesus, for our minds to be opened and anointed to obey His will, with courage and determination all through the day. Amen

06/01/25, 08:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He gave Himself for us to redeem us from all wickedness and to purify for Himself a people that are His very own, eager to do what is good’ (Titus 2:14)

Jesus redeemed us, reclaimed us from all iniquities and sins, and transformed us into the likeness of righteousness, which was His image. And this He did, to oblige to the will of God the Father, who desired that all mankind must be saved by the sacrifice of His Son. How privileged a race, we are to have a God who longed after us, to save and render us purified as Himself, so that we may equally reciprocate to the world what it means to do good. Jesus has put into our beings this ‘passion’ to be zealous to do good. Sometimes the trials and difficulties in life were given us, only to remind us that we are frail beings, so dependent on support from the other and on God for mercy. There is in us, in this clay jar of ours, nothing to boast of, or to uphold as better than another, all because of the gift of Grace from above. So let us humbly ask the Lord, what He intends for us this day, what we are called or destined to fulfill for His glory and promise Him our commitment and dedication to all that He has opened before us to do. In all that I am Lord, and with all that You have given me, I pledge to do my best today, trusting that You will support me, equipping me with the needed skills to overcome the obstacles that clog my path and be the better ‘me’ You see within. Thank you Lord! Amen

07/01/25, 07:49 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Who is He that overcomes the world? Only the One who believes that Jesus is the Son of God’ (1 John 5:5)

To You and me who believe in Jesus, the Messiah of the world, the One sent by God, the Father, manifold blessings await us. St John the Evangelist lists the privileges that behold believers in his first letter. Firstly, only those who believe in Jesus transform into the children of God. It is the Spirit of God that induces in them this urge to believe and proclaim Jesus as Lord. Thus all who acclaim Jesus are from God and by this is made known to all the Spirit that dwells in one – either the Spirit of Truth or the Spirit of Error. Secondly, if You and I are of God, we will obey His commandment. His commandment to us is to love – Because He loved us and became an expiation for our sins, we are pressed to follow Him and love one another. In love a multitude of sins are forgiven, so here comes the third blessing we inherit – of forgiveness of sins and trespasses. Since all have fallen short of grace, as the Bible teaches us, we need this assurance that our sins are forgiven and this is obtained in Jesus Christ, who became a sacrifice once and for all. Fourthly, the One in Jesus would turn into a conqueror, even with all his frailties and shortcomings as a believer. In every odd and oddity of life, the love of Jesus emboldens us with the hope, poured into our hearts by His Spirit in us. In Jesus, we have everything – every promise, every desire deep within accomplished, because we walk with a God who cares for us. Ask the Lord today, with faith and love of those things you have long forgotten and buried as impossible, He will manifest His glory for you, He will make all these possible, to make you a staunch witness and powerhouse to this world. Amen

08/01/25, 09:51 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And He saw that they were making headway painfully, for the wind was against them. And about the fourth watch of the night He came to them walking on the sea. He meant to pass by them’ (Mark 6:48)

Into the deepest moments of turmoil and helplessness, when His disciples gave up their hopes for a comeback, Jesus stepped into their lives. He was aware that they were struggling to wade through the waters, the weather was hostile and even the best of fishermen would run the chance to make it to the shores. Jesus gets into their boat (takes control of their lives) and the winds subsided. The Lord wanted to teach His disciples a tip or basic instinct for survival, what we call the fundamental life-skills (in its modern connotation). With all the advancements in Science, logic, and technology today, we believe we are sufficiently equipped to take on life. Even with all life’s comforts, technical know-how, and intellectual pride, we fail, that too miserably, before some of life’s daunting realities. Many of these occurrences in life, barged in, just like that, unexpected, though only of a momentary nature, was painful, leaving us agitated and victimised. This tip that the Lord caused the disciples to experientially learn was to call upon His name in the early hours of the morning (in the fourth watch) to make a breakthrough, and this is what the Spirit of Jesus reminds us to inculcate and follow in our daily living. Great miracles took place in the lives of many (as we read through the Bible) during these wee hours of the morning (before 6AM), and so will it be for us. Lord Jesus, grace our hearts to open up to you in prayer every morning to see the wonders you desire to unravel for us. Amen

09/01/25, 09:43 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Now the house of David was told. ‘Aram has allied itself with Ephraim’ so the hearts of Ahaz and his people were shaken, as the trees of the forest are shaken by the wind’ (Isaiah 7:2)

Chapter 7 of the Book of Isaiah brings forth the Messianic prophesy (of protection and hope) in an altogether contradicting circumstance in the life of the Judean people. Hardly had these managed to escape the slaughter, captivity, and loss of wealth during the war with Israel that broke out and brought them great dishonor, no sooner did they come to know of the Northern kingdom (Israel) allying with Syria for another major attack. It was only human for them to be shattered, be disgruntled by fear, and lose all hopes. It was not because the Kingdom of Judah was in any way inferior in strength that they became a scourge before their enemies, but because of their infidelity and wickedness, they were disciplined by God. Now as always, the memories of failure and humiliation leave an imprint in the human mind that doesn’t fade away so soon, and often interfere with the power of reasoning at times. Here is what transpired for the people of Judah too. To awaken to the realization that God is in control and trust His words of comfort for them was an onerous task for the people. Thus through the words of Isaiah, the prophet, God assures King Ahaz and his people not once or twice, but three times that they were to trust in Him and Him only, finally giving them a sign of the Messiah who was to be born (in due time) as a descendant of the Davidic dynasty. In all life’s situations, the Lord asks us to trust Him, for He holds the keys of the House of David, He holds the solutions to all those vexed problems of our life. Lean not on the inclinations of your mind, but on the unfailing love of the Lord for you. Amen

10/01/25, 08:35 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

'For he strengthens the bars of your gates; he blesses your sons within you. He makes peace in your borders; he fills you with the finest of the wheat' (Psalm 147:13,14)
For the warmth of His protection, for all the blessings He opens to the people given to us, for the prosperity and material sufficiency He provides us, many are the reasons for which we are obliged to glorify the Lord. Along with all creation, for the redemption and forgiveness He has caused us to inherit, for Jesus, His Son by whom we have been made Sons and daughters of the Heavenly kingdom, we must give praise and thank His Holy name. That through the mystery of Christ revealed to us, we (who were Gentiles) have become heirs to the promises given to the Patriarchs, we believe and  accept these from God, the Father. No more to live as aliens or strangers in the Kingdom, but as partners with the saints, to be merciful and receive mercy. This is our resolve, we make with thanksgiving to the Lord for all that He has graced us( adorned our human nature) with. Glory be to the Father, to the Son and to the Holy Spirit, for ever. Amen

11/01/25, 07:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But the people of God who pursued the law as the way of righteousness, have not attained their goal. Why not? Because they pursued it not by faith but as if it were by works. They stumbled over the stumbling stone’ (Romans 9:31,32)

The people of Israel, God’s Chosen ones, could not attain their goal, as the Holy Spirit points to us. The goal they were called for was to be witnesses to the One and only One God, to secure righteousness that emanates from God and to draw all mankind to the Salvation that God promised for all. And why were they unable to succeed? -  though the Law was given to them, the promises of blessings given to them (through the Patriarchs, Prophets and Kings), the Messiah incarnating as one among them, and God’s favour resting upon them. Only because their knowledge of the Law, their interpretation of God’s precepts and the wisdom they possessed did not lead them to a belief in Jesus Christ, the sum and substance of the Law. They pursued the Law so meticulously, so scrupulously, giving heed to every literal aspect it contained – they observed the Sabbath, tithed even a share of the mint, rue, and herbs (mentioned in the Law) but failed to express love, mercy, and compassion, which was the underlying principle of the entire Law. They failed to accept Christ Jesus, follow and obey His Words, trust in Him, despite being witnesses to all the wonder works and miracles He did for the people. This was the stumbling stone, the rock (Jesus) on which they faltered and tripped, that evaded them from the righteousness of God. This rock Jesus was at the same time the cause for the Salvation of the Gentiles, who knew nothing of the Law. While the latter offered themselves to the perfection of all Laws (Jesus), reposing faith in Him, the former leaned on their understanding, experiences, their learning, and perceptions, which were human and imperfect. Let this be our lesson today, to always trust in the Lord, to obey His Word, to be led by His grace, even though things seem irrational for our minds and Spirit. Amen

12/01/25, 11:24 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Thus says the Lord, the Holy One of Israel, and the One who formed him: Ask me of things to come; Will you command me concerning my children and the work of my hands’ (Isaiah 45:11)

If You and I are children of the Lord God, then what should stop us from reaching out to Him? What can hinder us from pursuing after Him, day and night for our unfulfilled wishes? What could possibly be a barrier in asking the Lord for what next? Will you command me, is what the Lord says to His beloved children. Regarding His plans for us, on the purpose of our lives, of the paths in life we need to take on, the digressions, corrections, and amends in life we are hard-pressed to make, and of course, our state of mind, dreams, and wishes. The Lord yearns to listen to us, to heed to our voice (from among the crowds), to hear us brainstorm on perceptions and perspectives in life. Who else could we lean on, who else could understand and accept us as we are? Therefore, let us ask forgiveness to Jesus, for the times we have accused Him for the uncertainties in life, for our lack of trust in Him and His ways (that He is in charge always) and for purporting to ourselves the position of pride, taking glory for all life’s ascents and successes. Had it not been for His mercy and unfailing love for us, nothing could be better in this life, as we see it today. All praise and honour to the Lord, the Saviour of all mankind, who was, who is and who is yet come again. Lead us this day through the green pastures for your name's sake.Amen

13/01/25, 10:57 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Then He rejected the tents of Joseph, He did not choose the tribe of Ephraim; but He chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which He loved’ (Psalm 78:67,68)

The descendants of Joseph of the Old Testament are referred to as the tents of Joseph. These were rejected and their place of pride given over to Judah, as today’s Word from God reminds us. What could have caused such a change in God’s preference? is what we are concerned with today. The Israelite history ever since Exodus, accorded a place of eminence to Joseph (owing to his faithfulness and uprightness before God). His two sons were inheritors to a share in the promised land, who were later referred to as the half tribes of Jacob viz., Manasseh and Ephraim. Of Ephraim, God called him as His dear one, in whom He delighted and desired to show compassion. (Jeremiah 31:29) Joshua was a descendant of this tribe, so were some of the great warriors of Israel; even God’s Ark, the dwelling place of God’s presence for several years was housed in Shiloh, within the territory of Ephraim. But later there came descendants in this tribe who were unholy, wicked, and corrupt – An example was the sons of Eli, who ministered in this Holy place of God’s presence with utmost defilement. The sins committed against God’s commandments, blasphemy, irreverence and pride did not just provoke God’s wrath upon them, but deprived them of the glory of God. Devoid of God’s glory in them, they fell prey (easily) to the conquest of the Assyrians. God looks into our hearts, He looks for a heart that is devout, humble, contrite and submissive to learn from Him. What benefitted David and the tribe of Judah was this heart of David, that longed after God, attracting Him. You and I are chosen to be with Jesus, to garner strength and encouragement from Him. Whenever our hearts get distracted with things around us, we lose focus. All that is required of us is faithfulness, caution, and perseverance to obey Him each day - one day at a time, as tomorrow will carry its own burdens. Wishing you a blessed Monday. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

14/01/25, 08:27 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘God left nothing that is not subject to them. Yet at present, we do not see everything subject to them’ (Hebrews 2:8)

The world at large was subjected to mankind and not even the angels. The beauty in creation was that man was crowned with glory and honour by his Creator, given authority for the upkeep of the entire creation. But as sin overwhelmed the hearts of men, many aspects of the authority they received from God, were divested from them – authority over suffering, pain, and death. Thus God who loved the pinnacle of his creation – man, came to its rescue by sending His Son in the form of man, (making Him lower than the angels for a while). Jesus, His Son took on suffering and death and triumphed over them, that through Him (all who honored Him) mankind would overcome both death (Eternal death) and suffering (with an eye on our Eternal reward). In fact Jesus was raised by God, the Father, as the pioneer of our faith, a role model to adopt, and a faithful High priest who would always stand by our side, advocating our cause to the Father, for our redemption. The Bible asks us - How could You and I let go of such a Salvation – a perfect role model, the prefiguration of our glory yet to be seen, and the source of all riches in this life – material and spiritual? If Jesus is at our side, who can then stand against us? In Jesus, God has now left nothing that is not subject to us. Believe and receive your share of blessings today. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
15/01/25, 09:46 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Rain in abundance, O God, you shed abroad; You restored your inheritance as it languished’ (Psalm 68:9)

The people of Israel were led through the wilderness by the Lord (we read in the Book of Exodus), onto the promised land. Through the parched lands, the Lord was their shield and provider. During the scorching heat of the day, He walked with them as a Pillar of Cloud and as a Pillar of fire during the night. And they were a great nation thus, because of the accompaniment of the Lord with them. Not that they were not wearied by the extremities of nature, the envies of men, or the torments of their own sin, but every time, they called upon God, He was at their beck and call to deliver, heal, and restore to their fullness. The Inheritance of the Lord today is ‘You’ and ‘me’. From His bounty, the Lord provides for us each day. Knowing our needs, our scarcities, and our weaknesses, the Lord replenishes us with His glory. The power of the Holy Spirit in us is the glory we behold, what distinguishes us from the world and our worth incomparable. Let us ask for more grace and power from above, the strengthening by the Holy Spirit, which recharges and equips us for greater tasks and challenges of a new day. All that was incomplete, imperfect, and inadequate of yesterdays, needs to be rebuilt and augmented today. He, the Spirit of Jesus, will empower us to bring in this change, innovative and appropriate in all walks of our life. With God, all things are possible. Amen

‘I will make you majestic forever, a joy from age to age’ (Isaiah 60:15)

If trouble and grief have been (for quite some time) your companions and compatriots in life’s journey, you have every reason for hope. Your hope comes from the Lord Jesus, in whom you were chosen for Eternal glory and after this ordeal of suffering is completed, you will be perfected and established into the new paradigm ‘You’ that He had envisioned before the foundations of creation. This is the promise that the Lord wishes to enlighten you with today. No matter how grim your situations have been for years, ‘lacklustre’, disenchanting, and despised by men, He has taken control to intervene, supplant, restore, and revive you more than you would ever have expected. He would make you majestic forever, the cause of joy for generations, ‘You’ who were subject to gross injustice, and made the laughing stock in town, Jesus uplifts you to enjoy His unfailing grace. Thus He says, to all those who patiently endure through Him, that they would never perish, they would be given eternal life, and none could ever snatch them away from His mighty Hands. It is no man who has assured us this blessing, but God, our Lord Jesus, who wields authority over everything under Heaven and Earth through His majestic conquering of death. Give praise to the Lord and thank Him for His powerful message, Let hope germinate in every mind and heart that trusts the Lord. Amen

17/01/25, 10:18 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Walk about Zion, go around her, count her towers, consider well her ramparts, view her citadels, that you may tell of them to the next generation. For God is our God forever and ever; He will be our guide even to the end’ (Psalm 48:12-14)

The best way to start a day anew is by giving glory and praises to God our Creator, and Saviour. For in His great mercy, He has led us to another beautiful dawn, to gaze at the wonders we have been called to be partakers to, to greater heights we have been designated to explore and conquer, within the bounds of His protection. And thus, We are the Zion, the Holy mountain of God, the dwelling place of God, which He has made secure. The numerous blessings He has showered on us, are the endless towers of Zion, her ramparts, and her citadels, and for these, we must sing praises to His Holy name, meditate on His Word each day, on His unfailing love for us that never ends. The Lord is pleased to dwell in Zion forever, to make it His abode and place of reign, which is the cause of why the many peaked mountains envied it – the greater rugged mountains of Bashan, as the psalmist exclaimed in Psalm 68:15. We have a God who loves to be with us, lead our way, remove all obstacles that may harken our progress and be the power within. There is no need to fear the ones around you, those with the powers of this world. For with a God who made the Heavens and Earth, you are terrific, impeccable, and inconquerable, (even with all the weaknesses that pull you down), a force to reckon with. Count your many blessings and give glory and thanks to the One who dwells in you – Jesus. Amen

18/01/25, 10:09 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Then King David was told, ‘The Lord has blessed Obed-edom’s household and everything He has because of the Ark of God. So David went there and brought the Ark of God from the House of Obed-edom to the City of David with a great celebration’ (2 Samuel 6:12)

Having known of the favour that Obed-edom and his household received from the Lord, because of the presence of the Ark of God with them, David reverently responds by bringing the Ark into Jerusalem, the City of David. The transfer of the Ark, (symbolic of God’s presence with His people) from Shiloh to Jerusalem, was in accordance with God’s will, providence, and master plan for Salvation of all men. When the House of Joseph (Ephraim and Manasseh – warriors among the Israelites) failed to respond gratuitously to God’s favor towards them, they lost their glory. It is written that the Ark never returned to Shiloh, after the war with the Philistines. For some time it was at Beth-Shemesh, and then taken to Kiriath Jearim and from there King David ordered it to be taken to Jerusalem. While in Beth-Shemesh and from Kiriath Jearim, when people inappropriately welcomed the presence of the Ark, they set themselves in great trouble (being punished by God for their sacrilege). David was perplexed and afraid, of what would be the plight if the Ark was brought to His palace, so it was left at Obed-edom’s place. Here the favour demonstrated by the Lord to Obed-edom (by making his household prosperous), was a sign to David to move forward with his plan of bringing the Ark to the City of David. The King therefore goes to Obed-edom, with the Levites and all people, in due reverence in compliance with the tenets of God’s law, the Ark is brought to Jerusalem with much celebration. How do we respond to grace, when given freely from above? Do we accept it wholeheartedly with mind, body, and spirit, or do we leave it to the realm of the spirit alone, with our bodies into the world? The Lord blesses the contrite of heart, the humble and yielding in spirit to His Word. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

20/01/25, 08:47 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And they shall live, and to him shall be given of the gold of Sheba: And men shall pray for him continually; They shall bless him all the day long’ (Psalm 72:15)

Psalm 72 is a hymn of Coronation for the King – a prayer offered on behalf of the King, that the kingdom may prosper by his efforts, and that welfare and justice to all may be secured. We pray this Psalm in its spirit for all those who have been entrusted authority on earth – on rulers, bureaucrats, leaders of institutions and families, that the Holy Spirit may wrap them with power to do their best for those subject to them and may fill their hearts with love and compassion. Again, this prayer is meant for You and me, all mankind, that in Jesus, we shall live, be enriched spiritually and materially by His provision for us, and be the cause of blessing to many around us. You and I are a royal race, saved and redeemed by the precious blood of the Son of God, Jesus. We are a priestly people, who are obliged to offer our prayers and supplication for all those whom the Lord brings to us. We are God’s children, co-heirs with Jesus, and entitled to every blessing that our Heavenly Father has promised to those who obey Him and believe in Jesus. We are empowered to thwart from us all that has not been sowed by the Father, all that the world has caused us to germinate and produce. Let us pray for the grace to renounce the weeds planted by the world – weeds of disbelief, despair, pain, sorrow, sickness, impulsiveness, and all depravities, through calling upon the Holy name of Jesus. Let us bank on the promise of Psalm 72:15 for life in abundance, for richness, possessions, proximity with the Lord, and love for all men. Lord, possess my heart, perspectives, and beliefs to honor You always, and to transform into your image to be a blessing to many. Amen

‘See I have engraved you on the palms of my hands; Your walls are ever before me’ (Isaiah 49:16)

But Zion said; ‘The Lord has forsaken me, the Lord has forgotten me’. This One was called by the Lord, to be a light to the world, to comfort those distressed, tormented by the trials and ordeals of life on earth. And how was this ‘one’ called by the Lord? Before the foundations of the world, while in the womb of his/her mother, this one was called by a name, emboldened with a heart and tongue that longed to reprove evil and injustice, concealed from the peoples, as a polished arrow in the quiver. But along the way, the kind of experiences the Spirit of God permitted into the life of this one was tremendous, sometimes difficult to bear and reckon with. He went through suffering, rejection, humiliation, abandonment, failures, and the sort for no fault of his. When the going became even more tough, this one complained that for no account he had been toiling, spending his strength in vain, for no reward and the Lord had forsaken him. In this juncture, the Lord in all His splendour, appears before this one, reminding him of the great commission that was given to him. Knowing ‘him’ in all dimensions – his strengths, weaknesses, attitudes, perceptions, beliefs, destiny, and identity as a whole, the very reason why he was picked up from among the billions to co-create a distinct mode, a plan for restoration for all. Nothing comes to you, dear brother and sister as a coincidence, everything pre-destined by the Lord and given you, because He knew you had the power to triumph over them most successfully with the light in you. Seek of the Lord the grace to discern and work for the fulfilment of His plan for you (a definite unique plan just for you), today. Amen

21/01/25, 17:08 - Megha Sherino: 
22/01/25, 10:03 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For seven days they celebrated with joy by changing the Festival of Unleavened Bread, because the Lord had filled them with joy by changing the attitude of the King of Assyria so that he assisted them in the work on the house of God, the God of Israel’ (Ezra 6:22)

Chapter 6 of the Book of Ezra gives an account of how miraculously the Great King Darius issued a decree to the Jews encouraging them to complete the rebuilding of the temple at Jerusalem and how this temple was successfully dedicated to the Lord. The reconstruction of this temple was decreed way back during the first year of the reign of King Cyrus. Though work began in full swing by the Jews, they had to face stiff opposition from their adversaries, the neighbouring subsidiary nations, who (on account of envy) raised serious obstacles (erroneous claims) to delay the reconstruction works, which were finally halted until the second year of King Darius. During this time, the people persevered in their efforts towards rebuilding the temple (despite the opposition), inspired by the revelations of the prophets Haggai and Zechariah. It so occurred in history that this King ‘Darius’, too became instrumental in completing God’s will in erecting the temple at Jerusalem. It was clear to the people thus, that what God had proposed, He would cause to fulfill in its time, despite the obstacles that may arise in their path. A lesson to us too - that God is ‘in control at all times’, i.e., what He has destined would occur in its time. But to avail of some specific gifts or blessings, we who are unworthy, would need to ask of it, be perseverant in our prayers and efforts to receive them, to remove all delays in its reaching our path, or evading any possibility of losing it because of our lacklustre attitude. The Lord wills to do that miracle in your life, to fill your life with joy, by changing the minds and hearts of men and removing all hindrances that may evade your blessing. Trust in the Lord and delight in Him. Amen

23/01/25, 09:53 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?’ (Psalm 108:10)

With its mountainous terrain and strategic location for trade, Edom was indeed a powerful nation. Being the descendants of Esau, the elder son of Issac, who is remembered in the Bible as the one who compassionately forgave his brother Jacob (the one who took away his blessings), the relationship between the Edomites and Israel (descendants of Jacob) had their ups and downs (was never smooth). With God’s favor, King David was able to subdue Edom (the fortified city), which wasn’t as easy as for the rest of his conquests. But when David repeats the account of this victory of his against the Edomites in two Psalms (60 and 108), it does have an overarching effect, an emphasis on how God helped him conquer all his foes and emerge triumphant. It was through the conquest of these nations surrounding Israel, that David emerged as an emperor at his times, having consolidated a large portion of territory and having amassed the wealth of these subdued nations in the form of taxes and spoils of war. It was this treasure of wealth that was passed on to his successor, Solomon, by which he was able to construct (without much hazzles) the historic and incredible temple of Jerusalem, the prayer house of the nations. To us, who meditate on the victory of King David, let us ask the Lord to be our guide and protector to conquer all challenges that may pop up our path this day. Even in the most submerging difficulties, we would be able to rebound successful, with the power of the Spirit of God in us. Lead us Lord Jesus this day, however rigid be the problems that await our path today, show us the way, be the truth to guide and counsel us and give us life in abundance to be conquerors with you. Amen

'By the grace of God I am what I am, and His grace towards me has not been in vain. On the contrary, I worked harder than any of them, though it was not I, but the grace of God that is with me' (1 Corinthians 15:10)

How beautiful and in the fullness of the Holy Spirit did Paul give his testimony - on his urge to spread the Gospel to the corners of the world. Better late than never, they say. And Paul's life and his words were just that. Having realized the richness of glory in Him, the presence of Jesus and of the richest he inherited in Jesus, nothing could hinder St Paul from proclaiming the Word, that which was revealed to him by the Lord. All that was valuable to him till then ( his noble birth, learnedness)no more did seem of worth when compared to Christ. The power of Christ in him pressed him to make 3 missionary journeys to reach out to the Gentiles, to suffer hunger, poverty, discouragements, and persecution.....all for the sake of Christ. And he worked much more than others, but with the conviction that it was the grace in him that kept doing the work. Thus bearing the marks of Christ in his body, he remained the fiery apostle and ambassador of Jesus Christ. To us, here is the example of die hard commitment, dedication and trust in grace that we are called to embody in our nature, to ensure no stone remaining unturned in our paths to make a break. Lord Jesus, I am what I am because of your works in me. I wish to keep doing, to the best all that you entrust to me with a firm resolve and trust that you will fulfil towards success the works of my hands. Amen

‘Therefore, He is able to save completely those who come to God through Him, because He always lives to intercede for them’ (Hebrews 7:25)

Jesus saves all those who come to Him drawing them to the Father. His sacrifice on the Cross fulfilled this reconciliation of mankind with God the Father. Created according to His (God’s) image and likeness and destined to glorify God in themselves on earth, was mankind’s purpose in creation. But when sin overtook their hearts, their Creator knew that they were enslaved, and by themselves, deliverance was an impossibility. Therefore God devised a plan for redeeming mankind and this was by sending His Son, Jesus in the form of man, so that man could relate so closely with Him and experience deliverance. In the due course of time, Jesus incarnated as a man, born to the Virgin Mary, for the sake of completing God’s immense plan for all men. By His life and death, He made the perfect atonement for the sins of mankind, because He was the High priest Eternal, who was sinless and so made His offering (the perfect sacrifice) of self to the Father for all mankind. Corruption and death had thus no right over Him, glorified through resurrection, He sits with the Father in Heaven, pleading our cause to the Father. He is the One who came down from Heaven to ensure each one of us secure our place with the Father in Heaven. Having revealed such a profound mystery of our Salvation, how can one not praise and thank the Father, the Son and the Holy Spirit for giving us all that we need to make life a blessing. Glory be to the Holy Trinity, come and dwell in us and in our homes today, Lord God. Amen

27/01/25, 09:10 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘If a Kingdom is divided against itself, that Kingdom cannot last. And If a household is divided against itself, that household can never stand’ (Mark 3:24)

Jesus’s opponents once accused Him of casting out demons by the power of the Head of demons – Beel-zebul, to which the Lord replied thus, Any kingdom divided within would only perish. This Word of God is brought today for our reflection and meditation by the Holy Spirit, being one of utmost criticality in a world that goes with the principle of ‘Divide and rule’. The world values opportunism, or fishing in troubled waters as an inevitable leadership trait at times. But the kind of chaos, unrest, and hatred, these bring into human relationships and the hollowness of trust these perpetrate can pose a serious threat not only to those manipulated by these tricks but also to those who knowingly manipulate. How many families have been shattered because of the division induced by someone wielding authority? How many relationships have been broken between spouses who have given way to opportunism and division for the sake of trivial gains? How many of our Christian communities have lost ground and turned counter-witnesses to Christ, just because the differences within themselves were not allowed to be tackled with prayer, love, understanding, and commitment towards growth. Jesus was giving His disciples a valued tip of what would be the nature of trials and tribulations they were to encounter in the world they lived. Without humble submission to the will of God, without offering ourselves to be combed by the Spirit of God, none of us would be able to make a change in a world wrought by division and separation. Let us offer ourselves to the Almighty, who knows our every care, who has the solution to every problem we face in life, who wields the wisdom superior to any, to guide and counsel us today in each step we take, that our words and deeds may bring glory to Him and encourage building up of mankind. Amen

30/01/25, 09:29 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Very truly I tell you, whoever believes in me will do the works I have been doing, and they will do even greater things than these because I am going to the Father’ (John 14:12)

Through a very short yet purposeful lifespan of His on earth, Jesus did set for us a benchmark to follow. Numerous were the miracles, signs, and wonders that He brought into fulfilment during His public ministry of 3 years. The blind saw, the lame began to walk, those oppressed found hope and liberty in His message and His life, even were there those risen from the dead, whom the Lord had favorably looked upon. Apart from these individual blessings many were beneficiaries to, through the sacrifice of Himself, Jesus gave mankind many ‘wholistic blessings’ – salvation from sin and Satan, the power of redemptive suffering, the urge for peace and reconciliation for all men, etc., and above all Eternal life. The re-establishment of order (peace) was one of the greatest gifts, Jesus offered mankind. Every Christian who believes in Jesus and obeys His command is therefore called to be a peacemaker on earth. And Jesus reiterated this in His Beatitudes approving peace-makers as children of God. All of Jesus’ works towards the Father’s plan for saving mankind were oriented towards bringing in peace, reconciling differences, and impressing upon all that they were no more strangers, but part of God’s household and family. Let our works, efforts, and gestures also support peace building in our spaces – our homes, society, community, and the world at large. Because, it is the authority of this world (Satan), who creates differences, divisions, and turbulences among men. With Jesus, who has authority over all, let us reclaim the peace and tranquil that should surmount our thoughts, minds, and activities on earth. In Jesus name we pray. Amen

Therefore, our message today is to lean on to Him, start the day with thanksgiving, offering our fears, anxieties and discouragements to Him as petitions of support. The peace of God would then blanket our understanding to withstand and face all things with the power of Christ in us. Amen
