02/02/25, 08:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You must be blameless before the Lord your God’ (Deuteronomy 18:13)

Abraham, the Father of Believers, was known for his righteousness and integrity. But it was through years of continuous walking with the Lord, obeying His Word, and learning to surrender before God’s plans and will, that Abraham learned what it meant to be blameless. This stature of high repute that God gave him, was not God’s work alone, but inevitably due to his desire and efforts to obey God and make needed changes in life, even to the extent of permitting his instincts and intellect to be smothered by Grace (when he agreed to sacrifice his only son Issac according to God’s test). God proved him righteous, beyond his human failures and mistakes. And thus, the Holy Spirit today engenders in us this message of being blameless before our God. Not in pleasing men around, by practicing charity for name and fame (to satiate the deities - pride and vainglory in us), but by being just and understanding to those whom God has brought before us, those within our own families, our communities, and places. It is to these that we are called to be merciful, compassionate, and if possible to walk that extra mile to be a follower of Christ. God who ponders on the cravings of the human heart, knows well our motives and intent. It was thus that David, the neglected son among his brothers, was chosen by God, as the one to rule God’s people. It was thus, that the woman who anointed Jesus before His death with the costly ointment was commended by Him, though she was sneered at by those who were present at that dinner. Let us ask the Lord this Sunday, for each one of us, of what would constitute our lives to be blameless before our God? Let us plead with the Holy Spirit to enlighten the eyes of our hearts to see and change those corners that are dark and need Divine light. Jesus be with you today. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
03/02/25, 09:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But while everyone was sleeping, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and went away’ (Matthew 13:25)

In the parable (Parable of the Sower) just preceding this parable of the weeds, Jesus taught his disciples and the crowd about the need for a ‘prepared heart’ to receive God’s Word and do His will. Every disciple is called to participate in building up this Kingdom of the Lord on earth, by listening to God’s Word, and modifying our life – its priorities and responsibilities to suit the Divine law. Now even when we have a prepared and humble heart, to receive every Word of God and abide by it, there always stands a chance of us missing the mark. And this is when we become less cautious, or resigned/content with the present situations, or when complacency offsets the most needed diligence and prudence, giving way to an equally precarious situation in life. It is then that the enemy comes and sows the tares that weigh down on our productivity and adversely impact the fruits yet to be ripened or produced. The enemy came when everyone was sleeping, the Holy Spirit cautions us. What was untoward was sowed in confidence and when we were ignorant/unaware, lest we would have prevented it by resisting it tooth and nail. This is exactly how the best of circumstances turned out for the worse, the Sacraments meant for our sanctification led us to condemnation (because we failed to receive them worthily – a repentant heart, or gave importance to the form over the substance), even the Sacrament of matrimony seemed a customary social get-together for pleasure and revelry (with wine, bachelorette/bachelor’s parties, bridal shower, or any other pagan practice for that matter) without the imprint of the Trinity (the source of Grace). This parable – the parable of the weeds within the wheat (best quality), was all the more meant for believers, than others, for the Lord knew our hypocrisy, vain glory, deification of the self, and the lack of His love in us, that spurred the need to outsmart others than promote harmony. Lord, help me to see as you see and love as you did, to bear fruit a thirtyfold, sixtyfold or a hundredfold, as you would expect of me. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

04/02/25, 10:09 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘If our hearts condemn us, we know that God is greater than our hearts and he knows everything’ (1 John 3:20)

For the one who is in Christ, this is their encouragement – that there would be no condemnation, as long as he abides in Jesus; and that though he is so susceptible to sin, he has obtained the righteousness of Jesus Christ (the favor of God), because of his clinging on to Jesus with trust. None on earth can claim to be devoid of sin, for all have fallen short of Grace as the Bible teaches us. But to one who without works, trusts Him who justifies the ungodly, his faith is reckoned as righteousness. And this is the one whom King David referred to as ‘Blessed’ in Psalm 32 – the one whose transgressions are forgiven, and sins are covered. Does this mean that these are exempt from obeying the Divine law? By no means, On the contrary, these are protected from the snare of sin and filled with Grace and truth to triumph over sin. So what matters is not one’s worth and good works, but being filled every moment in life by the truth and grace that comes from above. To all those who carry their crosses with humble submission to God, the poor in Spirit, these are privileged to be considered by the Lord. So let us ask of the Lord today, to be filled with His Spirit, His Grace, and Truth to be regarded graciously, to grow into our true identity, and to exhibit the same generosity to those around us. It is thus we fulfil the entire Law and the Commandments, to be with Christ Jesus. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

05/02/25, 10:00 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘My goal is that they may be encouraged in heart and united in love, so that they may have the full riches of complete understanding, so that they may know the mystery of God, namely, Christ’ (Colossians 2:2)

The Early Christian believers were united in one goal and this we read from the Letter of St Paul to the Colossians – to be filled with God’s love and His wisdom. It is from this that we obtain the strength to overcome tribulations, the deceptions from culture, human traditions, and elemental forces of this world, that seed in us doubtfulness and deviate our understanding from the Spirit of truth. In Christ is perfection, He being the head over every power and authority. And we who through Baptism have been partakers to a death similar to His, have every reason to share in His resurrection – the fruits of His Salvation. This is the great mystery of God, that He desires we must remember wholeheartedly to be thankful to Him in word and deed. We have received deliverance, healing, and ‘all’ of the Messianic gifts in Christ Jesus, so that going forward, we live a life of light before the world. So each day is a new opportunity to be filled with the unfailing love of God, His mercies, the wisdom that comes from Jesus, that renews our lives, teaching us to dwell in the realm of the Divine, to let go base compulsions of the world. Let us surrender our hearts and Spirits to be governed by the love of the Lord and His wisdom, today and ever. Amen

06/02/25, 09:54 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Therefore put on the full armor of God, so that when the day of evil comes, you may be able to stand your ground, and after you have done everything to stand’ (Ephesians 6:13)

The Holy Spirit instills in us encouragement and boldness to stand firm, as warriors of the Kingdom of God. Not to trust in those feats of the past (we maneuvered) that have brought us through ‘victorious’ and unfazed; but in the full armor that God has given us. We are called to trust in the Lord, in His strength, and in His deeds of power that have been fulfilled for our redemption. And the reasons why it is futile to lean on our intelligence and tactfulness is ‘firstly’ because our combat is not against flesh and blood (though may be used by the evil) and because there are times destined as ‘days of the evil’ in one’s life, when one’s mode of operation would be highly restricted to that of defense alone. During the latter times, all that we are asked to do is to quietly resume those responsibilities of our calling (vocation) standing firm in the Lord and draped in His armaments. It is this that gives us confidence before the Throne of Mercy, that we have done everything He has asked us to do. To be truthful, upright, peace-makers, with increased faith, hope in God’s fulfilled works for our Salvation and in the promises He will fulfill till the end times.  Be still, when times seem tough, knowing that the Lord is in full control of all that overwhelms us in the present, His plans for us are unchangeable, and so are His promises that would come to pass in due course and all for good. And everything will turn out for the greater good in us, yes as we love Him, but more because He loved us first and died for us. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

07/02/25, 10:00 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘This is my Gospel – Jesus Christ a descendant of King David was raised from the dead’ (2 Timothy 2:8)

St Paul while exhorting his disciple Timothy to courageously spread the Word of God, pinpointed to what was the crux of our faith. That Jesus Christ, a descendant from the lineage of King David was the Messiah whom God (The Father) sent to save mankind from their victimization to sin and death. The Resurrection from death, Jesus attained was the proof of His triumph over death and the devil, the glory He poured out to all those who received Him in faith. It is from this glory, Grace from above, that we are called to strengthen ourselves, to live the life of a Christian. Not that our lives will be devoid of troubles, and turmoil of sorts, but in every difficulty, we hold firm to our trust in the Lord, that He will not forsake us to wilt and wither – This is His promise, the promise of the Messiah, the High Priest forever, our pioneer of faith, who is ever faithful (even though we may at times be faithless to Him). God is faithful and He rewards all those who are faithful. Here comes the significance of King David, and the reference of Jesus Christ as His descendant. David, whom God anointed in place of King Saul to be the King of Israel, was a man of impeccable trustworthiness and love for God. It took him years and years of waiting to obtain the promise of Kingship over Israel (at the age of forty). From the moment he was anointed to the time he was made King, he had to go through the toughest terrains in life – fleeing from home, from the palace of King Saul, to the wilderness, with his family to Moab, then into caves, mountains and through the lands of Judah, for saving his life, the kind of enemies he had to reckon with were enormous, the threats to his life were many, but all through these years of severity, he persevered in his integrity and trust in the Lord, in every step he took ahead. Thus his life was preserved from all dangers (by God), and he was all through equipped to rule the nation, expand its boundaries, enhance its wealth and prowess and much more to handover a strong prospering nation to his posterity. What You and I do today in faithfulness and trustworthiness, would make a mark not just for us for Eternity, but also remain through posterity as a blessing that nothing can replace. Let us walk with the Lord, one step forward today, Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

08/02/25, 09:50 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘According to the grace of God given to me, like a skilled master builder I laid a foundation, and someone else is building on it. Each builder must choose with care how to build on it’ (1 Corinthians 3:10)

St Paul was referring to God’s grace by which he wholeheartedly proclaimed the Gospel of truth. Like a skilled master builder, he worked day and night, undergoing all kinds of trials, persecutions and difficulties to make way for God’s Word among the Gentiles (as this was what he was anointed for). For us to whom the Lord has entrusted specific and unique responsibilities (according to our vocation), let us examine how we go about fulfilling these tasks. Each one of us are co-creators with the Lord, to bring about development and sustenance to our Universe. But, are we truly aware of this, our purposes in living, or do we take a mere lackluster approach by ensuring we are politically right always? Do I build up with the grace and strength given me by inviting the Holy Spirit to explore and unravel the best, or do I lean on my understanding, my years of experience, the intelligence of this world, to take on my God-given tasks, faithfully? Do I build on the foundation of Jesus Christ, my master or go about doing what pleases me and what is of much convenience to me? What emerges as the masterpiece, the end product of my effort will that day be put to test, a testimony of how carefully I worked, the artist in me that created such an incredible feat. A man will be known by his children and by the city he has built/established, the Bible teaches us (Sirach 40:19). Unless the Lord builds, all things I do go in vain. And here is the wisdom that the Lord desires of us to imbibe today. Let us engage with the Lord, on the foundation He has created and carefully build on, to create the best possible for His greater glory. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

10/02/25, 09:31 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today
'In all their distress, it was no messenger or angel, but His presence that saved them. In His love and pity He redeemed them. He lifted them up and carried them all the days of old's (Isaiah 63:9)

As to Israel, God's own people of the Old Covenant, so it is to us, the New Covenantal people in Jesus Christ. God deals with us not in tandem with our ways and eligibility. Because all we have in us is the fallen nature, the tendency to think or do evil. But in His love and compassion for us in our helpless state, the Lord is moved to pity. Not through a messenger or angel did He redeem us, but by giving up Himself on the Cross, we were bailed out from our guilt and it's repercussions. Jesus paid the price for setting us free, that we may live not as we did, but with the realisation and thankfulness for His gift of Himself for us. All the promises of God for our goodness and prosperity have been fulfilled in the resurrected Jesus, we need to merely draw and secure them for us and for posterity by petition and prayers. But only with an upright heart can we approach and seek grace, letting go of all notions within, the ways of the flesh, pride and greed of the eyes. Lord Jesus, listen to our prayers and show us your unceasing mercy, that we may be moulded in strength and integrity, always to radiate your effervescence, your fragrance to our worlds. In Jesus name we pray. Amen

' Your gates shall always be open; day and night they shall not be shut, so that nations shall bring you their wealth, with their kings led in procession' ( Isaiah 69:11)

To receive from the bounty provided to you from the Heavens, God asks of us this one thing. Let your hearts be prepared in humility, charity, and gratefulness to the Lord. For He, our Father knows and wills to enrich us in wealth both material and spiritual, that we may exhibit His traits and do His works on earth. All those who receive Jesus as Lord and Master of their lives, will be empowered to function like Him - and even to do greater works for he will be loved by the Holy Trinity who would make dwelling in him. That You and I are called to be stewards to His Grace, and to work for His Kingdom. This Divine Kingdom has already been established in our midst by Jesus. By His crucifixion and death, He has nailed the powers of the other world( infernal powers) and rendered them armour less. That with hope, joy and peace of the Holy Spirit in us, we may relentlessly toil for the propagation and spread of the Heavenly Kingdom for all mankind. Anyone who spares time,  and his resources for this Divine Kingdom, Jesus has promised will be rewarded a hundredfold while on earth and much more in Eternity. So let us imitate the zeal of St Paul, who spared nothing in life that impeded the flow of grace in him. Let us also open the gates of our hearts to the infilling of this grace, that our human nature needs without fail and permit this grace to work on our behalf. Let the immeasurable riches of grace be the saving power of our lives and families. O Holy Spirit, let all that is not from above be plucked and weeded by your fire in me. Amen

12/02/25, 08:45 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

'Your Father knows what you need before you ask Him' (Matthew 6:8)

Our Heavenly Father knows it all, the cares of our life, our needs - physical, spiritual and emotional, our strengths, the very things that disturb us, and even the fact that our beings are dependant on Him for wellbeing. But to cause us to be aware of this fact is an effort in itself. In our love for ourselves, we tend to be introverts, withdrawing into the hollowness of our security, safety, comfort and so on, that hardly do we spare time and space in our minds for God and His Kingdom. That we were created to know Him and love Him, is often an ignored fact. God initiates this realisation in us that without Him we would be helpless, just to prevent us from straying away from Him( source of our strength)and being lured into the world and its enticements. What could be easier than the dangers of getting lost and wounded in the cares of this world - this our Lord understands. With compassion He awaits for us to call Him, seek support, ask Him to intervene in our daily life.  Not that He doesn't know what we need, but that we do not know about what would satisfy us, the risks that lie on our path, the trials that await our fall...We are called to reach out to our Father in Heaven for His Holy Spirit to take control of our lives, to follow after God's will and purpose about us and to be successful. Thankyou Lord for your goodness and mercies, all that you have given me to this day. Lead kindly Light. 
Amen

13/02/25, 07:50 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Now He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath, And behold there was a woman who had a disabling spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and could not fully straighten herself’ (Luke 13:10,11)

This woman who had a disablement knew that there was none other than God who could help her. And this was the reason why she was present at the Synagogue on the Sabbath to in the presence of God. One could imagine the extent of pain and difficulty she might have taken to reach the Synagogue, the place of worship, most accessible and proximate to her. Probably it was to specifically meet this person, and to grant her healing, that our Lord arrived at that particular synagogue that very day (for Jesus was on His way to Jerusalem, as we understand, and He could have dropped in somewhere else on the Sabbath). God acknowledges every single effort a man makes to give honour to His name. Even the most subtle gestures of love, and devotion we have expressed before the Lord at times of grief, sorrow, pain, and indignation, will never be forgotten by Him. Because He is the Messiah, the Servant Leader, who ensures no harm to the reed that is bruised or the ‘smouldering wick’ (every human being in pain and suffering), until justice triumphs in their lives. To the crowds, (the world around us), these may seem the most insignificant, those to be ignored, or sidelined, but never for Jesus, who is our compassionate Saviour. Jesus taught us to be like Him, humble, and gentle in heart, to be able to consider the weak, the disabled, the downtrodden, those sidelined by society, those within our families whom we never took seriously, to be a light to them, that we may be honoured as messengers of His mercy and love, this world deeply needs. Wishing you a blessed morning. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
14/02/25, 09:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And Elisha said to him, Take a bow and arrows. So He took a bow and arrows. Then he said to the King of Israel, Draw the bow and he drew it. Elisha laid his hands on the King’s hands…..then Elisha said: Shoot, and he shot. The Lord’s arrow of victory, the arrow of victory over Syria! For you shall fight the Syrians in Aphek until you have made an end of them’ (2 Kings 13:15-17)

For quite a long Israel had to counter the attacks of Syria, its neighbouring Kingdom, and it was during King Ben-Hadad’s reign, the Syrians conquered Israel, destroying almost the whole of their military force – their armed men, horsemen, chariots, etc, barring a few, thus turning them into a vulnerable lot. Despite the presence of ‘the two’ most powerful God-men (renowned prophets in history) in Israel – Elijah and his successor Elisha, the kind of influence they could create in the mindsets of the Kings of Israel was not much. Thus when everything was falling off for these kings, they still believed in themselves – in securing dominion through might and military competence, rather than in reposing their trust in the Lord God, who had control over all. The Syrians conquered Israel when it was ruled by King Jehoahaz. The reason why Israel fell prey to its neighbour was its sinfulness. But when King Jehoahaz sought the name of God for help, the nation was saved from the hands of Syria and Israelites remained in their land. However, what was most essential, a change never occurred. The people continued in Idolatry, the sin of Jeroboam – who initiated places of worship (to the Baals and Astartes) in Samaria (Israel) so that none would go down to Jerusalem the City of God in Judah (solely to satiate his interest in securing political sovereignty of Israel). Faith, trust, and love for God are invaluable gifts that can neither be compared nor be substituted with another. What was lacking in Israel was this – for which they had to pay a great price. Even when at his death-bed, Elisha supported King Joash, by asking him to shoot the arrow of God’s victory over Syria – What dominated Joash’s mindset and lorded him was his defeat and the impoverishment of his forces before the enemy. Rather than believing in God’s might, and imposing trust in God, he failed miserably at the most defining moment of his reign, which could have otherwise reversed the fortunes of Israel to rewrite a different history. Israel could thus withstand Syria only three times, (as Joash struck only three arrows) and was finally overpowered by the enemy. Remember, Opportunities knock at our door only once, unless you and I prudently make use of every little situation to our favor, trusting the presence of the Lord with us, success may seem to evade us. But even with failures, we have confidence that God Almighty is with us, to carry us through to that promised shore. May today’s Word from the Lord fill you with strength and encouragement. In Jesus name. Amen!

Dr. Mini Sherino

15/02/25, 09:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that in the Lord your labour is not in vain’ (1 Corinthians 15:58)

All that has been sown in this perishable body, sown in pain, dishonor, and weakness, will be raised in glory that one day. When the trumpet sounds, in a moment, a twinkling of the eyes, along with the dead, we will be raised to accompany the Lord to Eternity with our imperishable bodies. Therefore even the subtle of things we have done with a loving gesture, will count as treasure compiled for in our Heavenly abode. We are assured this because of Jesus Christ, who proved to us through His resurrection, through the graces bestowed on us through His resurrected body, and through the Holy Spirit poured into our hearts that tells us that we are not our own, but purchased for a price. So let us ensure that all we do, we say, and even the words of prayer that we utter, become thanksgiving to the Father for the victory, Jesus has given us. Let us not indulge in worthless speech, arguments, and judgments that abet sinfulness and hurt in another, but as followers of Christ, let us bear each other’s burdens in the spirit of love and compassion. With all that we have acquired through the gift of faith, our beliefs, convictions, principled living, handed down from generations of old, need to be steadfast and preserved, to keep us from swaying from the truth to the relative which is of this world and subject to change – of both space and time. In the Lord and with Him, every effort of ours is intact, and would be recompensed by Him in its time. Let us pray for caution and prudence – that the words of our mouth and the meditation of our hearts be convincing and commendable to the Lord. Amen

‘Keep me from deceitful ways; Be gracious to me and teach me your law’ (Psalm 119:29)

Every new day broadens the scope of the canvas of our minds to grasp newer elements in the precepts and Laws of the Lord. This understanding deters us from being persuaded by the status quo of yesterday, to imbibe a growth mindset to improve ourselves each day. While on this earth, none can claim to have reached the zenith of his/her potential, by merely having completed the standard set of tasks that is usual to all humans. This would not only be deceitful but also underestimating God’s plan for us. Wisdom from the Lord is overarching, sufficient, and superior to any other kind of human learning or intelligence. I may be wrong at times, but with you and in obedience to your ways, in your sight, I will always be upright and fervent to do your will. Amen

19/02/25, 09:38 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For the appeal we make does not spring from error or impure motives, nor are we trying to trick you’ (1 Thessalonians 2:3)

The Thessalonian believers laid an example before all the Churches as imitators of the Gospel. They were persecuted in all ways, enmity from Jewish counterparts in Thessalonica, the mob who were stirred against Paul and the believers in Christ…. Despite all, they stood firm to what the Gospel taught them, of living a life worthy of God who had called them to be citizens of His Kingdom and to partake of His glory. Paul while remembering the difficulties he had to undergo while attempting to preach the Word of God at Thessalonica, is thankful to God for the grace that filled the hearts of the people who listened to him. They received His words, not as that from human beings, but as the Word of God, which was in itself powerful to work wonders in their midst. And so did it resound in the hearts of these believers, that helped them persevere through the trials that awaited them soon after. To us, this opens a warning, that in all that we do or say let us be convinced that – It does not merely depend on our individual exertion (hard work) or ambition, or way of words that brings in the required success, but through grace and the mercy of God, that helps create everything from the non-existent. However shrewd, calcitrant or powerful be our words, it is doubtful if hearts and minds can be touched by them to bring about even a subtle change, but God can make all the change that is expected in the lives of men, even in the most unbelievable manner, if we trust in Him and surrender our littleness to His mighty presence. Be the power in me, Lord Jesus. Amen

‘Yet it was the Lord’s will to crush him and cause him to suffer, and though the Lord makes his life an offering for sin, he will see his offspring and prolong his days, and the will of the Lord will prosper in his hand’ (Isaiah 53:10)

His life was made an offering for sin and the will of the Lord prospered in his hand – Isaiah’s prophesy of the Suffering servant, the Messiah, Jesus for mankind – this we always recall and claim for the sake of atonement for our sins and for receiving grace, that fulfills all God’s promises for us. But even when we believe in Christ’s redeeming suffering and his glorious resurrection, we fail to acknowledge the reenactment of this suffering in the lives of many around us. As the world is, we are prone to be close to those who are affluent, those who proved remarkably successful in life, those who are in positions of power and authority – not just that; we even tend to trivialize the many faults/mistakes these may perpetrate in daily life. All because we have gotten to believe that prosperity alone is a blessing from God. It is true and undoubtful that prosperity comes from God, but even sufferings may be permitted or imposed upon persons (not all) for the sake of God’s marvels being unravelled through their lives. When the compassionate Lord, permits such kind of unwelcome circumstances in one’s life, He ensures to empower him/her with a double portion of grace to work out a ten fold blessing from the awkward. The uniqueness of this person who suffers is that he is the beloved of the Lord, the one with whom the Lord walks with. And because of His (God’s) great trust in this faithful one, the Lord takes him through the trials, the failures, the untoward situations, rough patches in life, not for a lifetime, but for a while so that with the wisdom he has acquired, he would be able to outdo/overcome the faults the evil has imposed upon his life. Therefore, if You are in great suffering, if people have rejected and condemned you on account of your burdensome life, you have more to hope about - the Lord loves you, upholds you, and will carry you through onto the safe shores. This He desires to let you know. You are the apple of His eye. Amen

22/02/25, 19:31 - Megha Sherino: The **Metro on Cable** (also known as **Cable Metro** or **Cable Car Transit**) operates on the principle of **aerial cable transportation**, using cabins or gondolas suspended from a continuously moving cable. It combines elements of **ropeway technology** with **public transit systems**, providing efficient urban mobility, especially in hilly or congested areas.  

### **Principle of Operation:**
1. **Support and Propulsion:**  
   - A system of **towers (pylons)** supports the cables, which carry the cabins.  
   - The **main cable (haul rope)** is driven by a powerful motor at the terminal station.  
   - A **looped cable system** allows continuous movement of cabins, ensuring smooth passenger flow.  

2. **Stations and Loading:**  
   - Stations have **detachable grip mechanisms**, allowing cabins to slow down for safe boarding and alighting.  
   - Some systems use **fixed-grip technology**, where the cabins move at a constant speed.  

3. **Safety Mechanisms:**  
   - Emergency brakes engage in case of failures.  
   - Backup power ensures operation during power cuts.  
   - Wind and seismic monitoring systems enhance stability.  

### **Advantages:**
- **Efficient in Dense Cities:** Overcomes traffic congestion by utilizing airspace.  
- **Cost-Effective:** Lower infrastructure costs compared to traditional metro rail.  
- **Eco-Friendly:** Electric-powered with minimal land impact.  
- **Connectivity:** Ideal for linking metro stations to difficult terrains (e.g., hills, rivers).  

### **Examples:**
- **Medellín Metrocable (Colombia)** – First urban cable car for mass transit.  
- **Mi Teleférico (Bolivia)** – World’s longest cable car network.  
- **Roosevelt Island Tramway (USA)** – Connects Manhattan with Roosevelt Island.  

Would you like details on design, implementation, or specific case studies?
22/02/25, 19:31 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): https://youtu.be/i2gxfB1aW48?si=q9KkRHGi8u5pUur1
22/02/25, 19:32 - Megha Sherino: No, **Metro on Cable** is not a new technology, but its application in urban public transit is relatively recent. **Cable cars and ropeways** have been used for over a century, primarily for transporting goods and people in mountainous regions, ski resorts, and mining areas.  

### **Evolution of Cable Metro Technology:**
1. **Early Uses (19th-20th Century):**  
   - Used in mining and industrial transport.  
   - First passenger ropeways built in the early 1900s (e.g., ski lifts).  

2. **Urban Transport Integration (Late 20th Century):**  
   - Cities like Medellín (Colombia) pioneered cable metro systems for public transit in 2004.  
   - Inspired by gondola lifts, these systems were adapted for urban mobility.  

3. **Modern Expansion (21st Century):**  
   - Many cities, such as La Paz (Bolivia), Rio de Janeiro (Brazil), and Mexico City, have implemented large-scale cable car networks as part of public transit.  
   - Technological advancements have improved **speed, capacity, safety, and efficiency**.  

### **Why It’s Growing Now:**  
- **Traffic congestion and urbanization** demand alternative transport solutions.  
- **Lower construction costs** compared to metro rail or elevated highways.  
- **Eco-friendly benefits** align with global sustainability goals.  
- **Improved technology** allows for larger, faster, and safer systems.  

Would you like to explore its feasibility in a specific region or compare it with traditional metro systems?

24/02/25, 11:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel’ (Matthew 9:33)

Through three or more incidents of healing in Chapter 9, Matthew the Evangelist throws light on the peculiar nature of our Lord – His compassion; that which he continues to this day. The Lord’s compassion is very personal, specific, and unique to each of those who prayed for His intervention in their lives. The first healing was the raising from death, the daughter of the leader of the Synagogue. Jesus respected this high official, honoured his faith in God’s power, and therefore made his way (with his disciples) to this pious man’s house. On his way, there appeared among the swarming crowds, a woman, afflicted by bleeding for 12 long years. Probably she could have been the most oblivious person in a society, known for its disregard and contempt for women. What she desired in her heart was to touch the fringe of his clothes and believed that it was enough for her healing. Jesus reached out to this woman, granting her wish. And more than that He consoles her, commends her for her great faith, that which was beyond her expectation. Then is narrated the miracle of healing two blind men who followed after Jesus praying ‘Son of David, have mercy on us’. Their wishes were granted. Jesus reached out to each of these giving them not just physical healing, but a healing of their mind and soul as well. Thus the crowds who witnessed these signs and wonders acclaimed that ‘nothing like this has ever occurred to them through history’. Even now the Lord works in ways unbelievable and even incomprehensible to us. What we receive from Him is what is most appropriate for our holistic growth. He cuts through the divides of polity, economy, and societal strata to confer us what best fits us because He knows us so personally. Whether it is peace and tranquil you are on the look out for, or for a healing in your body, or for deliverance from the dark areas of life that seem to have overpowered you……Ask Jesus to bless you. He will intervene in your life in the most ‘phenomenal’ way, that none has ever seen before. Amen

25/02/25, 09:59 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘So Abraham called the name of that place. ‘The Lord will provide’; as it is said to this day. ‘On the mount of the Lord it shall be provided’ (Genesis 22:14)

Abraham was in deep distress, that which could not be shared with any other – neither Sarah, his wife, none of the others either. Who could ever attribute rationality to his decision - to sacrifice his son, Issac as per God’s instruction? After all that he had to persevere through in life, Abraham was given in to realize that power was God’s and it was his to create, to command obedience, and to fulfill the most impossible things in life. This realization dawned in him the tenacity to follow after God, to ask Him for a way out, and to be convinced of the faithfulness of God; thus what occurred to his life was a great miracle. Of God intervening at the right time, with His solution, His healing, and something more with a blessing for generations to cherish. Abraham thus spoke to all generations, to all believers the profound truth about God – that we have a God who provides for us always, especially at those moments of desperation and blackout, when nothing seems to work out humanely, when we reach out to Him at His mountain with faith (in prayer and humble surrender to His will). To all who reach out to the Lord, calling upon His name, in Jesus name, will be provided to them all that they had been petitioning the Heavens. This is our hope not just for the day, but for all times – My God will provide me from the richness of His Glory, everything I need, through Jesus Christ. Amen

26/02/25, 07:33 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I sent you to reap what you have not worked for. Others have done the hard work and you have reaped the benefits of their labour’ (John 4:38)

It is Jesus, the Lord who has sent us to where we are today – to bear fruits of the Gospel, to share the good news to many around, irrespective of whether people stand by us or not. As mortals, we fail to acknowledge God’s greatness and endless mercies towards us sometimes. There have been times we have gathered the fruits for which we have never worked for, but it was God who did permit us to reap the benefits, acclaim, name, and fame, though we did nothing to prepare the ground for the harvest. What Jesus expects of us is a transformation into His likeness, a broad mindset to accept, and encourage the building up of others, to emanate virtues of prudence and patience to correct, exhort and, reproach the evil, not to stand with the majority, or support the ways of the world. If the way of this world is to appropriate name, fame, and position, from the toils of another; Jesus asks us to be faithful in every task He has assigned to us, even in the most ordinary tasks we do. For all that is reaped in gratitude and humble submission to the Lord awaits a reward in Eternity, apart from the joy and satisfaction one receives on this earth. To us, this means, that we are to do all in our care to ensure being witnesses to the Lord in our little worlds – homes, parishes, and communities. Not to judge people from the half-truths that are revealed to our fragile senses, but to align to the ways of God’s Spirit and to engage in our tasks and obligations. Let us be thankful to the Lord for all that He has brought us to this day, for all that He has been working for us to bring success in our lives, and for the grace to be merciful to those who seem less fortunate than us. Remember We are what the Lord has taken us through and we still bank on His graciousness today as ever. Lord cause me to do your will always. Amen

27/02/25, 09:21 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘O afflicted one, storm-tossed and not comforted, behold I will set your stones in antimony, and lay your foundations with sapphires’ (Isaiah 54:11)

Today’s message from the Lord is one of assurance, encouragement, and power. Even when nothing seems right as per how things have evolved and transformed to date, for the Lord, these have been as expected (as per His plan), thus He is never surprised. Through these debilitating circumstances that have tossed life up to the unknown (uncertainty) and dismay, Jesus is assuring You and me that He is setting up a strong foundation for your life, He is recreating with enhanced beauty, and charm, the lost things in your life. Though certain things we have long presumed as ‘inevitable’ and unavoidable in life have been taken away, we are taught as to what ‘really’ adds meaning to our lives. And when it is the Lord who teaches us, it is the most effective pedagogy that He deploys, to enhance the scope of our understanding and discernment. Jesus merely tells you that no matter how distraught and unbelievably broken our lives may seem, He is the potter recreating from these ruins, a vessel, that is most honoured and sacred, that which is capable of storing only the precious, (which is anathema to the world), that which will share a place among His arsenal of equipped weapons to fight against the enemy. Be comforted, that it is the Lord who establishes, works for you, and lays your foundations, unlike any other – Honour Him with your time, your capability and your resources, as He is the King of Kings and Lord of all the Universe. Amen

28/02/25, 10:17 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Lord apportioned to them great glory, His majesty from the beginning’ (Sirach 44:2)

It was His glory and His majesty that manifested in these ancestors of old, the Patriarchs as meritorious for mankind to behold. Conspicuous of them was their ability to make a mark in whatever they did – there were rulers of high accord and fame, people renowned for their wit and wisdom, capable of giving counsel and prophesying God’s will to His people, those who were musicians, rendering glory to God through the verses they wrote, many of them were materially rich and those who enjoyed a pride of place among their contemporaries, the name they left behind for generations to cherish. But there was one string that connected them all – their compassion and meekness that made them adorable human beings. It is this that our Lord brings to our meditation today, to reflect on how these could prove successful in captivating the hearts of both God and man alike. Jesus asks You and me to train ourselves in this essential life-skill of being humble and meek, to be compassionate and understanding towards the faults and shortcomings of those around us. Easily said, but what else could be more challenging than getting to deal with real-life situations and indifferent persons. What first comes to our mind is our sense of justice, of why men fail to adequately reciprocate the good offered to them, or why a sense of entitlement rather than responsibility rules the human mind. Here, Jesus tells us to take up each situation with Him, with the sense of Him having ‘full control’, and ‘complete knowledge’ of the way out in each crisis, not relying on our perspective and bend of mind, which can do nothing when the tides rise high. Remembering the great men and women of old, our ancestors, those who triumphed and succeeded to wade through rough waters and reaching their destiny, this is the lesson each one of them has to tell us. It is His Glory and His majesty that has to dominate our lives each moment in time, good and bad. Let us pray for His glory to overwhelm our lives, our thoughts, ways, and words, to be able to inscribe our distinctness into the annals of human history. Jesus be with you. Amen

01/03/25, 11:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He who sat there (on the throne) had the appearance of jasper and carnelian, and around the throne was a rainbow that had the appearance of an emerald’ (Revelation 4:3)

The One enthroned is God, the Almighty – St John saw this vision of The Throne in Heaven, where was seated The Heavenly Father. The apostle was guided by the Holy Spirit to envision what would occur every time, a soul united itself with its Creator in prayer and humble submission. Then to him was revealed the majestic presence of God, who loved to be with His children. The appearance of jasper and carnelian, two precious stones signify some of the distinct characteristics of God, and how we mortals must prepare ourselves to beseech His mercy – Holiness, Purity, Righteousness, and Power. We are reminded that every time we approach this Throne of Mercy, it is with praise and admiration that we must acknowledge God’s presence. And only a heart that strives for purity, righteousness in Jesus Christ, and is willing to submit to God’s plan, will be eligible to seek and see God. ‘The Blessed’ as Jesus calls these people, whose hearts are pure, who see God in majesty and Glory, they are in turn filled with His abundance of love – ‘The rainbow’ that is seen around God’s throne. Symbolic of the fact that God wills to renew life in His people, those who seek Him, even though they may be in the midst of unending trials in life. In short, God expressed Himself to us mortals in the most simplest ways so as to fill us with hope, love, and faith. Let this sink deep into our hearts that, whenever we utter the name of God worthily, whenever we make the prayer of ‘Our Father’ with trust and love for God, this Throne of Majesty and Mercy appears before us to renew us, to strengthen and console us, protect us from all that the enemy has erected for our destruction. Whenever we glorify our Lord in the Sacrament of the Holy Eucharist, this throne of Mercy emerges to transcend us from where we were to where God wills to elevate us to. We have such a loving Father who always seeks our goodness. Amen

02/03/25, 07:29 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the Devil’ (Matthew 4:1)

The Holy Spirit led Jesus into the wilderness as Matthew the Evangelist narrates this incident in the life of Jesus. It was there that Jesus fasted for forty days – a period of intense prayer and fellowship with His Heavenly Father. But immediately the devil springs in with his intent to tempt and derail Jesus from His convictions. The Gospel does not tell us how many times this kind of a situation may have occurred in the life of our Lord while He lived on earth as a human being, it merely states that after His failed attempts of temptation, the devil left Jesus. In the context of this incident, what the Holy Spirit intents to teach us is this – It is the will of God that at times we must be led to wilderness experiences in life. Not due to our sins, or transgressions alone, but because God has a definite plan for our lives, that we must never be shrouded by the thoughts of self-sufficiency, that we must learn to seek Him always, by expressing the essential trait of being poor in our Spirit. When God leads us through such kind of adversities in life, what Jesus did must also be our way ahead – to be with the Lord in prayer, fasting, and acts of reparation for sins. This is because when things seem well-aligned and comfortable, it would be so easy to abide by God’s ways and precepts, but not so, during rough times in life. There is a need of fresh anointing and support from above during times of trials and difficulties. But with the Lord, we are promised an eventuality that is triumphant. As we prepare for the Holy season of Lent, let this be our bend of mind too – that Jesus never leaves us abandoned during the times of our hopelessness, He comes with us, to guide, supplement and encourage our moving ahead. Though the world may have rallied to keep us aloof in their own ways, the Lord stands by us, emotionally strengthening, guiding and taking us through our next phase of spiritual journey with Him. He never fails, He (though was God) who experienced rejection, abandonment, and contempt while on earth, will mercifully carry us through to overcome all temptations that the evil one may induce to disturb our peace. Lord, be our Light, Life, and Encouragement. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
03/03/25, 08:47 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Although they know God’s righteous decree that those who do such things deserve death, they not only continue to do these very things but also approve of those who practice them’ (Romans 1:32)

The righteous decree of God is His mercy – that though we are all undeserving of His favor, He confers to us righteousness, to those (only those) whose lifestyles have been demonstrative of their obedience to faith in Jesus Christ. And those who discard this knowledge of God’s righteous decree fall prey to the consequence of their disobedience – a depraved mind. This is why many in the world do what they ought not to do, why there is strife, envy, murder, deceit and malice among men, why insolence, arrogance and innovative ways of doing evil are so rampant in our present culture. Because though we all know that God exists, about His infinite power, and the truth of God revealed to us through the Created world, which was reiterated to us by God reaching out to us in our human nature (Incarnation), many among us failed to glorify God, giving priority to the things of this world – the Created, finding excuses and reasons for elevating ourselves. Thus, what transpired was not only practicing of the evil, but also the glorification of that evil, justifying it as a change (essential) in line with trends and times, a broader outlook or appreciation of individual freedom. What becomes even more dangerous is that the remnant of good that we see around often is slammed/ highly overlooked as cringe, or abnormal. It is to such a culture that looms large today, the Gospel proclaims – those who do such things deserve death. Let us ask the Lord Jesus, for pardon, for forgiveness, for ignoring, belittling the ‘truth’, for condemning the right and justifying the wrong, for whatever reasons…., for all such situations where we have failed, let God’s mercy prevail, to restore, ignite and empower the truth. Amen

04/03/25, 08:51 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘This will take place on the day when God judges people’s secrets through Jesus Christ, as my Gospel declares’ (Romans 2:16)

Before the Throne of Judgement of our Lord, there will be no disparity – between those who follow the tenets of the Law knowingly as believers or unbelievers. The Unbelievers are those who are ignorant of the Gospel, but by nature obey these as if the precepts of the Law were written in their hearts. Glory, Honor, and peace with Eternal life are promised to these doers of the Word. So, it does not consist in whether we proclaim or depict ourselves as people of the Lord (by outwardly devotion or practice of tradition), but when we discern what God’s will for us is, and yearn to live according to this will, when we learn to appreciate what is excellent before God’s eyes and strive to obtain the same, we become the doers of the Gospel. It is this motive of us that will be made public on that Day of God’s judgment; when all secret intents, and intentions that were masked in conscientious observation of Law and its practices, would be unveiled, judged and condemned by the Lord. Pointing to this dimension of our infidelity and faithlessness before God, St Paul quotes from the Old Testament Scriptures, how God’s heart wrenched with hurt and pain – because the name of God is blasphemed before the Gentiles because of you and me. So let us pause for a while today, to look into ourselves, our motives, those aspirations that are driving forces for us each day – whether we follow after the Lord and His precepts because of love for Him or because of those individual interests of ours tagged to self-aggrandizement? What would prove for our Salvation on that day would be none other than the marks of Christ in us, we have been marred with, those of pain, suffering, of rejection, …..our Crosses that by which we were united to His Gospel. Amen

‘All the works of the Lord are very good, and whatever He commands will be done at the appointed time’ (Sirach 39:16)

At His appointed time, in line with His majestic plan for the Salvation of all mankind, God commands all the events of this Universe. If ever you have been led to think that certain works of God are delayed or appear so, some of our prayers seem unanswered for long, the Holy Spirit reminds us that God’s kindness, forbearance, and the patience with which He deals with sinners is all intended to lead all to repentance. The Lord knows all, He holds with Him, the apt solution for every problem that our world faces today, and had foreseen them even before they ever existed. Let us be thankful for the wonder that each of us are, the innovativeness we have been called to embrace each day in our Spirit with humility and gentleness. Let us ask the Lord to bring into fruition all that He had planned to be fulfilled through us this day. Lord Jesus, make use of us as the channel of your peace, love, creativity, and hope. Give us a heart that yields to your will and is ready to work wonders with the power of your Spirit in us. Thankyou Jesus. Amen

06/03/25, 10:14 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But let our might be our law of right, for what is weak proves itself to be useless‘

(Wisdom 2:11)

The norm that rules the roost in our present culture is that ‘might’ is our right. Cultures and societal norms don’t just occur like that, in the blink of an eye, for, these have been honed, nourished, and rationalized by men over generations and thus take the form of legitimate norms for societal living. If generations have taught us that growth would mean the expansion of one’s horizons, conquering people and things, successfully competing with compatriots, and taking on an elevated position; If strict diktats of do’s and don’ts have been the prerogative of men who have succeeded (in the context and meaning of what the world has defined success to be), then where does ‘humaneness’ find its significance in a Super-human powered world? Even with all the pride, greed, chaos, lust, and deceit in humanity, we continue to thrive because of the imprints of God’s hands manifest in our midst. The meek, the patient, those differently abled/physically challenged who have outmanoeuvred impediments to survive, the kind, the merciful, those who have transformed their failures as stepping stones for others to succeed……...we are called to tag on to and build up this endless list of successful people to rewrite the definition of what it means to grow. It’s better late than ever, to make the most insignificant of initiations (of this nature) in our homes, our families, and societies. We would need to teach the coming generations that it is ok to fail at times, to be discouraged at times, to stand up against unhealthy competition, to face rejection and disapproval boldly, to say ‘no’ when values seem comprised, and to live with the power of the Spirit of God, to make that extra-ordinary mark in a disarrayed world, which thirsts for good somewhere. Let the Holy Spirit be our inspiration each day. Amen

07/03/25, 09:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For You granted Him authority over all people that He might give eternal life to all those You have given Him’ (John 17:2)

Jesus was praying for Himself, for all those whom God had granted to Him for redemption. And we know that His prayers were heard – He was glorified by the Father after His suffering, passion, and death, through a resurrection unheard of by humanity till date. This is our assurance that we are eligible to Eternal life, provided we accept Jesus, as Lord and Master of our lives – To Him has been given authority over all people. With this in mind, let us approach His Throne of Mercy with the confidence that He will never let down anyone who approaches Him. He who has authority over all and to judge all, will hear our prayers, honour them in His due time. Let us thus pray for ourselves, to be washed, sanctified, renewed and reclaimed for His glory (through these days of penance and reparation), let us pray for those given to our lives – our dear and near – within our families, extended families, those far from us but close to our hearts, those whom the Lord brings to us this day, those memories and moments of the past that have often been offending to us, those whom we owe forgiveness and those to whom we need to forgive wholeheartedly, those around us who have supported us and support us to this day, those in administration, our leaders – socio-political, and others whom we remember, that they may also receive the abundance of life, Eternity, by being truthful to their call, and worthy by way of their deeds, that all may know and acclaim Jesus, as Lord and master for a new and better ecosystem for all humanity – Thy Kingdom come, O Lord, on earth as it is in the Heavens. Amen

08/03/25, 10:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Now we are released from the Law, having died to that which held us captive, so that we serve in the new way of the Spirit and not in the old way of the written code’ (Romans 7:6)

What held us captive was ‘sin’, that which found a way to manifest itself through loopholes in the Law. Therefore, God in His mercy, came down to simplify the Law for mankind; In place of the existing laws, sub-sections, and provisions, of moral conduct, He condensed the sum and substance of these Laws, encapsulating a single commandment(instead) to be followed by all men – the commandment to love. Through this ‘one and only’ commandment authorized by Jesus through His sacrifice on the Cross, mankind has been delivered, healed, and set free from all bonds/shackles of confinement due to sin. We have been freed from fear, anxiety, worries, and all strongholds that plague our minds, because of the love of Jesus and the price He paid for our liberation. The Holy Spirit enlightens in us this realization of the truth, that we have been called/ordained now to serve, in love, like Jesus Christ, in the way of His Spirit. To grow in ‘love’ is our call, which neutralizes a multitude of sins in us. To imitate Christ, to make light and carry the burdens of one another, to be tolerant to those weak, and of less understanding, to be the fragrance of Jesus Christ in a world of avarice, mistrust, intolerance, unforgiveness, and hurts, to bring healing and upbuilding of the other. To rewrite those codes of old, those redundant, those that deny inclusiveness and create more have-nots – towards this we have been redeemed by the Lord and entrusted greater responsibilities on earth. Let us therefore not deprive, but be co-creators of a new world order with the Lord, who had a place in His Kingdom for outcasts, women, have-nots, underprivileged, and marginalized. Jesus challenges us to expand our horizons to serve like Him. Happy Women’s Day!

10/03/25, 11:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For the Lord Himself is his portion and inheritance’ (Sirach 45:22)

The Lord Himself would be all that Aaron and his descendants would need. This is exactly what today's Word of God reminds us of what made Aaron so distinct from the rest of the crowd. He was indeed one of the heroes of great esteem chosen by the Lord in the line of the Patriarchs. To him was conferred the position and prestige of priesthood, clothed with glory, adorned with a crown of Gold inscribed with ‘holiness’ on it, unknown to people before him – all to remind him of his responsibility towards God, to offer sacrifices on behalf of himself, the people, to bless the people, to teach and enlighten God’s people about the Divine Law, precepts and to pronounce judgment in wisdom. There were in those times a few who criticized him, questioned his authority, scoffed at him, and conspired against him, similar to what we see today. But God’s favor and delight followed him all the days of his life. The Levites were not allotted any specific land among their fellow Israelites as owners, only given certain cities, as we read in the Book of Joshua, Leviticus, and Deuteronomy. Why God proposed so, could be because He considered them special, He wanted them to rely on Him always, He wanted them to be remembered as a living symbol/presence of God’s providence, His Holiness, and distinctness before the world. Now the relevance of this mention of the Levites and God’s will about them has a lot to do for us too. You and I are called to be living symbols/witnesses (to the world) of the love, mercy, providence, and companionship of the Lord who walks with us. Faith demands that we rely on God alone, attribute worship, glory, and honor to people, places, and things that are Divine as against things of this world, and strive to be filled with the favor and delight of the Lord all the days of our life on earth (whatever be our calling). As for the Levites, the Lord Jesus Himself is all that we would need in our lives. Amen

11/03/25, 09:47 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory that is to be revealed to us’ (Romans 8:18)

In this Jubilee Year of ‘hope’, it is this Word of God that is to reverberate in our ears, hearts, minds, and spirits. In fact, the entire creation eagerly awaits to experience this glory that is to be revealed through the redemption of humans. St Paul explains that Creation was subjected to this futility in the light of the great hope to be unveiled; all to remind us (humans) that the present suffering is just a matter of time. To prepare us to be worthy to behold the glory that our Heavenly Father has placed in store for us, to reckon of it as the mighty work of the Lord, to stand before the Lord in gratitude and awe, we are now permitted through the tunnel of agony, of pain, of hardships, or even neglect. No amount of words may be sufficient to explain the kind of difficulty one has to deal with when one undergoes trials. The Son of God had to undergo pain, suffering, and a humiliating death, before He emerged triumphant and majestic in resurrection. He set an example for us to accept and imitate in life that just as His earthly life was wrenched in pain, despite the good He did for mankind, so would be the course of life of His followers. Not to be absorbed lifelong in the suffering, but through each pain, to imbibe the lesson the Lord intended to teach us, to humble ourselves from our pride and vanity, and more than all to be a lifesaver to another who knows not why this suffering. We have a God who is tremendously powerful to create from nothing, to empower the weak, to enlighten the unwise, and this is the hope that takes us through the periods of suffering, that with the Lord, who fights with us, we are safe, our battle is won, and victory is assured, no matter how feeble and unworthy we may be. The Holy Spirit invites us today to be proponents of ‘hope’ that the world thirsts for, to encourage the weak and stumbling to befriend the Lord and make every step ahead with the conviction that Jesus would turn it all to the best. Remember, our present sufferings are nothing when compared to the glory yet to be revealed to us, so let us wait patiently and courageously for our joy to be complete. Amen

12/03/25, 10:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body, to make you obey its passions’ (Romans 6:12)

Sin awaits to take over every human being. This was what God reminded Cain about (before he thought of committing fratricide) as a warning, to be careful about, to do all in his means to master it. How sin approaches one, or tempts one, is to be mindful of always. It lurks and knocks at our doors (the doors of our hearts) in the most unexpected ways - It sneaks in and takes a grip over the human heart so stealthily, as something desirable, inevitable to possess or own, and something that causes self-aggrandizement in an ever-evolving world. The Holy Spirit teaches us to thwart every opportunity, or occasion that can lead one to sinfulness and take a precautionary measure in our spiritual journey. Each day, to offer our body, mind, and soul to God, to be sanctified by the blood of Jesus (who died for our sins). It is then that our mortal bodies will become instruments of righteousness, and filled with grace to be reigned not by the passions of the world, but by the power of God. It is not by our fortitude, power, effort, might, or prayerful exercises that we obtain the power to conquer sin, but God’s mercy and love for us, that helps us to triumph and obey God. Let us therefore, master the skill of pleasing God, of heeding His Word, of prioritising our lives to comply with God’s precepts, by offering ourselves (with all our limitations) as holy sacrifices of praise and surrender before Jesus. Lord Jesus, teach us to honor your Laws always, to depart from sin, and to live under Your Kingdom with hope and joy. Amen

‘Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled’ (Matthew 5:6)

14/03/25, 08:51 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Lord saw how great the wickedness of the human race had become on the earth, and that every inclination of the thoughts of the human heart was only evil all the time. The Lord regretted that he had made human beings on the earth, and his heart was deeply troubled’ (Genesis 6:5,6)

God created man in his ‘own’ image and likeness, the Bible tells us the purpose of human creation that stirred the heart of God, when He breathed life into man. That man would be different from the rest of creation, would keep and till the land, have dominion over all the created, and would dwell in the bliss of oneness with God to Eternity. But it was no longer so for mankind, as they fell into sinfulness and deprived themselves of the source of their glory. The history of mankind is the story of how man declined each step from His ‘greatness’, when he saw himself great in his own eyes and ignored the presence of the Lord. The knowledge of this world that led him, deceived him from discerning the truth. Thus every inclination of his mind, his thought processes, his beliefs, and convictions, were darkened by the tenets of the evil one. If man was created to love, to forgive, to support, to let go, and to uphold the weak, he was coerced to create hate, to take revenge, to avenge for self-pride, to oppress the weak and disabled, thus his choices were destructive, more than creative. This was why God regretted and was grieved to the core for having given the gift of life to His masterpiece creation – humankind. Not a thing of the past, but even today, this continues to be the recurring story of mankind, and of how inhumane cultures, traditions, and norms get accolades and recognition in our modern civilized world. What we ought to do is to seek the Lord, ask to be cleansed and sanctified by His Holy Spirit - every inclination, thought process, imagination, determination, will-power, and intellect that makes up our personality; that we may reflect Jesus Christ, who portrayed to us what it means to be a real human being. Lord, grace us with a new heart, mind, spirit and inclination to be another Christ. Amen

‘For we know that the Law is spiritual: but I am carnal, sold under sin’ (Romans 7:14)

It is none other than St Paul who declared himself a sinner – the one who was taken into the realm of the ‘third Heaven’ and to whom was revealed great mysteries of the Divine. Inspired by the Holy Spirit he spoke on behalf of all mankind – that we are carnal by nature, attuned to satiating the desires of the flesh. By desires of the flesh, one would mean a mind that seeks pleasure, to be at its comfort and delight, instant gratification, worldly aspirations of growth, pride in oneself and in one’s successes, and the one who prioritizes people and things around him over and above placating God. Laws and tenets are framed so that men may be informed of what is permissible and what not. Therefore, merely by following the Laws (Code of Ethics) ‘alone’ one would not find himself justified by God, but by submitting himself/herself to God’s sovereignty and might. With the incarnation, death and resurrection of our Lord Jesus, we have entered as sons and daughters of the Kingdom into an era of grace. Grace would entitle us to understand/accept our vulnerability while at the same time, the fact that God is graciousness and Almighty. Let us therefore look into ourselves, into where we would need to humble ourselves before the Mighty Throne of God, that His Grace transcends the frailties of our human nature. This is our vocation – to put on Christ Jesus, to live like Him, to be the Light like Him to a world clouded in ignorance, vanity, and hopelessness – to be the living hope to this world in this Jubilee year. 17/03/25, 09:04 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘To love Him with all your heart, with all your understanding and with all your strength, and to love your neighbor as yourself is more important than all burnt offerings and sacrifices’ (Mark 12:33)

It could have been with the intent of testing Jesus that the Scribe approached Him with the question of the greatest of all commandments. To him was reiterated the Jewish foundational Law of Shema – to love God with all your heart, understanding and strength and to love one’s neighbor as oneself. Throughout the Bible, we encounter this truth of what pleases God the most – a loving and sincere heart that seeks God, and that which expresses the same sober-mindedness to fellow beings. This God approves more than burnt offerings, sacrifices or ritualistic practices. To God’s people in the Old Testament, offerings and sacrifices were of phenomenal importance and there were thorough and detailed laws outlined in the Torah of how offerings were to be made before God. The motive behind every offering was to honor God with one’s wealth and resources, and thereby to be blessed (obtain absolution for sins, transgressions and iniquities). From the very beginning of creation, (even before detailed laws were compiled) men offered sacrifices and gifts to God – as we read of the offerings of Cain and Able. What concerned God was the heart of the giver, whether it was gratitude and humble worship that constituted the substance of the offering, or was it an exercise from an empty, envious, and unjust giver. Thus, Able’s offerings pleased God, but not Cain’s. When Jesse (the father of King David) brought his sons before the Lord (prophet of the Lord) for being anointed, it was into their hearts that the Lord looked – thus David, the least of them was chosen to be ‘the anointed’ of the Lord. Let us therefore, look into ourselves, our inner beings – if we are rooted in true love - for God and our fellow beings – whether it is the dictates or norms of this world that decide our course of action and deeds? Whom do we please by our deeds? The people who see our magnanimity or the Lord who perceives every motive of our deeds. Lord Jesus create in me a pure heart, a new Spirit that longs after you, so that I may hurt none by word or deed – You who measure the depths of each soul, grace me with increased love and strength. Amen

‘But all of you who were faithful to the Lord your God are still alive today – every one of you’ (Deuteronomy 4:4)

Moses reminds the Israelites of their past – the wilderness experience of 40 years that had come to pass, of the deprivations they had to put up with, of the humiliations and rejections they had to encounter all through those hard times. That, if it was not for God’s works in them, they would have perished halfway, in the wilderness (the many perils and hindrances they faced were hazardously endangering), unable to see the promised land – the land of abundance. It was to prepare them for something supernatural that they were led through suffering and pain none other could think of. Nothing for them to be boastful about, but the fact that the mighty Hand of the Lord held them, protected and preserved them to this day. This message rings out loud and clear to You and me, who live in the modern era – the epoch of technology – of swift changes and uncertainties that can make or break us. But God’s Word assures us that all the needed skills for thriving on would be provided by Him, who has the world in His Hands. Just as it were to the faithful among God’s people who braved all odds to make it to the Promised Land, given for them to inherit, so would be the case of all who believe in Jesus Christ and obey His Word. And for those who deny Him and His Word, would have tp pay the price of what occurred to the unfaithful among God’s people – they questioned, doubted, criticized and disbelieved in God, only to find themselves swamped halfway in the desert. We have a merciful God, who desires our progress. Let us hold firm to the Lord, despite the things, people, and circumstances, we are placed into. Amen

19/03/25, 10:23 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘We demolish arguments and every pretension that sets itself up against the knowledge of God, and we take captive every thought to make it obedient to Christ’ (2 Corinthians 10:5)

St Paul was explaining the secret of his spirituality – keeping his heart, mind, and thoughts submissive to Christ. There would arise arguments, pretensions, beliefs, philosophies, and so-called ‘truths’ that contradict God’s Word and His promises to His people, each day on earth. The Gospel spread and believers increased in numbers all over the world, not when things were favourable and conducive to believe, but during a time of staunch opposition, persecution, and outright annihilation of believers in Christ (the first 100 years after Jesus’s resurrection). In Roman, Greek, and Jewish writings (scholarly/historical), Jesus was referred to as the one crucified for treason, blasphemy, and rebellion. But as time passed, His followers expanded, His mission was taken over to faraway lands, healing, and deliverance were blessings testified by thousands of men (who believed in Christ) all over the world. God’s works can never be curtailed by human action or laws of the land. What He has planned to occur, can never be thwarted by any human being or thinking. This is today’s message for us, who at times are pruned to follow and align with the ways of the world, for convenience, power and purse. And as we remember St Joseph, Jesus’s foster father, who tended, led, and assured the Holy family a safe and secure living, let us learn from him, the virtues that attributed to his greatness. No human law, dictates of religion, or opinion of men could deter St Joseph from disobeying God. He was one who demolished arguments, pretensions against the knowledge of God – Jesus and wholeheartedly gave himself up for fulfilling God’s plan of Salvation through Jesus. St Joseph, pray for us to be dedicated to the Word of God always. Amen

20/03/25, 09:53 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘They devour widow’s houses and for a show make lengthy prayers. These men will be punished more severely’ (Mark 12:40)

Jesus’ condemnation of the Scribes for their pretense/guile, for being hypocritical of obeying God’s Laws outwardly but at the same time depriving those vulnerable of their legitimate rights to a peaceful living. In the days of the prophets, there were a few classes of people who were stranded, swept to the fringes of society, and had absolutely no dignity of living. One among them were the widows who had nobody to plead their cause. God was their refuge, and the people of God were instructed to be supportive of them. The ‘widows’ are those characterized for being tender, being naïve – those poor in Spirit, relying on God’s mercy for a secure living. Perhaps they would have had to blindly rely on people who represented God or the temple itself, by offering their tithes, or a portion of their wages as entitlement to the temple servers. There were those in society who could afford this - highly rich or influential, but who never were obliged by these laws. The term ‘widow’ has wider ramifications in today’s modern era, where in society we can identify several groups equally oppressed and marginalized. Looking into the most basic unit as a family, have we not discriminated between the vulnerable and the strong? When God Himself came down to us, in search of the weak, the sick, the disabled, demon-possessed, and sinful so that they could also be ensured a place of pride through redemption, do we not fare even worse than the Scribes of old? Let this season of Lent be a realization of where we are called to make a change – in our hearts, in our perception of self and of others. Grace us Lord to set away all that is hypocritical and to adorn ourselves with You – of humble and gentle Spirit. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

21/03/25, 10:46 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘If by the Spirit you put to death the misdeeds of the body, you will live’ (Romans 8:13)

So long as You and I are in the flesh, we would exhibit knowingly or unknowingly the traits of the flesh – hate, envy, fornication, debauchery, deceit….. the long list of vices. But ever since we have known Christ Jesus, of the marvelous sacrifice He accomplished to redeem us through His sufferings, death, and resurrection, there is this hope in us who believe in Him that we would be partakers to the miraculous resurrection and Eternity, through His Spirit in us. We have an obligation more than ever – this obligation to live as sons and daughters of His Kingdom, to die to the flesh and live for Christ. This we do not by strength, or words, or by our innate tactfulness, but by the Spirit of Jesus in us. This Spirit – the Holy Spirit urges and purposes us to put to death the misdeeds of the flesh, each time we are tempted. Temptations are bound to recur till this bodily form departs from us, but we have been privileged to live as children of God, with immeasurable power and determination. This is not without a reward, there is certainly a reward a great one for all who live in the Spirit and not according to the dictates of the flesh. There is a compensation – Eternal that awaits us who live in peacefulness and holiness. Therefore, let us meditate this Word today – that we live by His Spirit in us, who puts to death the vices of the flesh, Thanks be to God for His indescribable gift to us in Jesus Christ. Amen
