22/03/25, 10:33 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Shepherd your people with your staff, the flock of your inheritance, which lives by itself in a forest, in fertile pasture lands. Let them feed in Bashan and Gilead as in days long ago’ (Micah 7:14)

The prayer we make (from our hearts) today to the Almighty is asking for His providence, protection, and restoration/reinstatement for us His people. The prophet Micah knew that unless the Lord builds the house (the house of Israel – which would also mean each one of us), the builders labor in vain. Thus at a time of deep distress, deprivation, and sinking glory of the Israelites (God’s people), the prophet prays to God for His intervention and rectification. There are moments in life when we are also led ‘to live by ourselves in a forest’ as God’s people had to put up with – deplorable because they felt like those who gathered summer fruit at the gleaning of the vineyard (when barrenness takes over the place of plentitude), none around them who were faithful or upright (a situation of loneliness and gloom) and when across-board there is oppression, conspiracy, and iniquity of a kind till then unknown to them. There were many great men (in the Bible) who had encountered grievous situations as this at one point in life. Joseph of the Old Testament (until he was reinstated as the second in command of Egypt by the Lord), King David (though anointed but fret with threats throughout), Moses (the Leader of God’s people, who was never obeyed by the people)… the list continues. What all these did, and what prophet Micah advised God’s people to do, was to inquire of the Lord, seek His intervention, ask for His restoration, and constant help to overcome difficulties posed by the evil. Let us therefore take heed from the prophets and patriarchs of Old, as to what we must also follow when things seem confusing, disarrayed, and disenchanting in life – to pray. Lord Jesus, shepherd us with your staff, lead us to those fertile green pastures that would support our well-being, as we remember our glorious days of old, Be with us and even more to conquer all these that threaten our comfort and peace. May the peace of Jesus rule our minds always. Amen

23/03/25, 07:49 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘They said, ‘Come let us build ourselves a city, with a tower that reaches to the Heavens, so that we may make a name for ourselves; otherwise we will be scattered over the face of the whole earth.’ But the Lord came down to see the city and the tower the people were building’ (Genesis 11:4,5)

When given two options at hand, how would we go about choosing for ourselves? The people whom God had caused to survive after the great floods, those who had seen the glory, majesty, and greatness of God, were given to choose between two options. One was to acclaim pomp and prowess for themselves by exhibiting and working on their talent and capability. The second was accepting God’s might, His prowess and glory above anything to come up in life’s situations. As we would all be prone to choosing - to rely on our sufficiency (hard work, determination, oratory skills, strategy and tact…) rather than reaching out to the Divine for superior strength and guidance – these men chose to invest on their superior skills, technological prowess, and work a name for themselves. They had decided to build on their strength without inquiring with God, or seeking His will. Thus, we see that the Lord came down to see the city and the works of their hands, uninvited. What occurred soon was the origin of differences – languages, custom, traditions, cults, and nations in the world. While highlighting this positive turnaround in the history of mankind, one must not forget that the tower of Babel was never built. To us the indication is so plain and clear that we are called to invite the Lord in every endeavor of life – mankind was created to journey with the Lord, hand in hand, seeking guidance, support, at every step – because we know not what will come to pass soon after, but He knows it all. Let us learn to humble ourselves by submitting all that we are to God and upgrading the next best version of us, which is needed to keep going in life’s journey. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
24/03/25, 09:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Abram traveled through the land as far as the site of the great tree of Moreh at Shechem. At that time the Canaanites were in the land. The Lord appeared to Abram and said:’ To your offspring I will give this land. So he built an Altar there to the Lord who had appeared to him’ (Genesis 12:6,7)

Abram (Abraham) set out from his native place – Harran, with his wife, his nephew, and belongings. The Lord had appeared to him and asked him to leave his ancestral home and move on to a land which, He would show him. It was clear that not until Abram had reached the tree of Moreh at Shechem (which is in the land of Canaan), the mention of ‘Canaan’, as the promised land (as the land Abram and his descendants were to possess) was not made by God. It is when one obeys God’s will, trusting in His sovereignty, and power to fulfil what He has promised, he makes himself worthy and approaches close to God’s promise. The context of Abram’s call narrative gives the reader a deeper insight into the nature of God as One who discerns the inner selves of men before making His choice of the one to fulfill His plan. God could instrumentalize the salvation of mankind in any possible way. But He chose to pick a man (Abraham) to partake in His plan, so that through His elect (chosen one) men may know the power and magnanimity of God and would be reconciled to their true purpose in creation (harmonious living with God). Abram/Abraham did just that, he obeyed God, set out as per His instruction, traveled as far as he was led to reach the promised land not knowing that it was here that his descendants would possess the land as a nation. God confirms His promise to him there; In response, Abraham honors God by building an altar to offer worship and praise to God. This union in Spirit and heart, God expects to see in each one of us, His followers, as we journey through this season of Lent. By reconciling with God and our fellow beings, we affirm the consummation of all God’s promises made to us in Jesus Christ as ‘Amen’

25/03/25, 10:00 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I have surely heard Ephraim’s moaning: You disciplined me like an unruly calf, and I have been disciplined. Restore me and I will return, because you are the Lord my God’ (Jeremiah 31:18)

The people of God repented, admitted their waywardness, and prayed to God. Jeremiah, the prophet portrays the imagery of how the people prayed during their days of distress – They accepted their period of ‘exile’ as a needed chastisement from God who (though loved His people) was determined to correct them as it would be for ‘the master’ of an untamed calf. These people obtained the kind of disposition of their hearts to please God and be favored by Him. Their prayers were offered with a contrite heart aligning with the truth, that presumed a two-fold dimension that God was sovereign and they were of little or no knowledge and were weak. From this bent of mind, they sought to be restored by God. When God is given an upper hand in one’s life, He takes over, rectifies, restores, and reinstates one to the purpose for which He was called. And God answers such a soul with much compassion and readiness of heart (Jer 31:20) At times we fail to understand the vulnerability in our lives to bring in a state of status quo, and we go about finding solutions for the problems others face, unaware of what the next moment holds in store for us. Let us learn to look into ourselves first, to accept our faults, seek correction from the Lord, accepting that we are like the ‘wild bull’ that needs its master’s guidance to stay on track. Restore us, O Lord, and we will return, just as you would want to see us through, for that would be all that you expect from us to be restored to glory. Amen

26/03/25, 09:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The land could not support them while they stayed together, for their possessions were so great that they were not able to stay together…And quarreling arose between Abram’s herders and Lot’s’ (Genesis 13:6,7)

Abram had come back from Egypt to the place between Ai and Bethel where he had originally tented and worshipped the Lord. Now that he had expanded in terms of wealth, herders, and servants, the land that was previously inhabited by both him and Lot, became inadequate for them to live together. This has an indirect reference to the circumstances in our families today – not in the number of members, but in their specific choices, interests, needs to cater to, children, and of course financial stability, that it may seem inappropriate to live together harmoniously. It is here that prudence demands respecting each other’s boundaries; maintaining a healthy distance; foreseeing the possibility of quarrels due to intrusion of space/privacy and preventing their occurrence proactively. At the end of the day what matters is being bound by a true spirit of love, rather than being led by unnecessary pretension of unity before men. Abraham and Lot perceived the many complexities that would arise if they stayed together; taking (into account) that they lived among the Canaanites and Perizzites who could make things even worse between them. Let us ask the Lord Jesus, to teach us to be grounded in love, while making space for others as well as ourselves, to prevent unnecessary conflicts/quarrels among ourselves, to be Your witnesses to the people around us (our commission and vocation). Amen

27/03/25, 09:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘If you do these things, show yourself to the world’ (John 7:4)

One possible reason (as we usually are prone to thinking) why the brothers of Jesus spoke to Him this way was to provoke Him. They had off-late been hearing of His wonder works, miracles, and extraordinary deeds, and it did no good to them in terms of earning a name and fame for themselves. That, even ‘they’ had no idea of Him being the Messiah, and of the Divine purpose of His incarnation is so explicitly been made clear by this statement. Thus, they urged Him to accompany them to Jerusalem for the feast of the Tabernacle, (maybe) unaware of the threats posed to Him by the Jews there. Jesus stayed back at Galilee, but after His brothers went, He made His journey for the feast in secret. The only explanation He gave them (His brothers) was that His time had not yet come. His time was well in alignment with God’s definite plan for human salvation. This Jesus knew and wants us to also realize today that ‘nothing’ in life occurs without a purpose. There is no coincidence of situations or things in life, only the fulfillment of God’s plan in its predestined time. For nothing is covered that will not be revealed to us, Jesus says. Our loving Father has and is preparing us through our journey for the best – Eternity. All that has transpired were to bring us closer to the truth and to train us to be better versions of ourselves. And the final version would always be revealed by the Lord to the world, without human intervention or explanation. The Bible is replete with numerous personalities who are remembered to this day, not because they built a name for themselves, but because the Lord did it for them. Amen

28/03/25, 10:33 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I do not want you to be ignorant of this mystery, brothers and sisters, so that you may not be conceited: Israel has experienced a hardening in part until the full number of the Gentiles has come in’ (Romans 11:25)

The ways of the Lord are indeed mysterious to man. It is inappropriate, therefore, to try to understand His ways merely by our bend of mind or intellectual faculties. Pointing to the Jews who failed to recognize and believe in Jesus the Messiah, Paul affirms that God is still able to save them. He will turn His attention to these (His Chosen ones), soon after His master plan for redemption of the Gentiles (the world) is completed. This is so, that men may not fall prey to pride, and that what has been obtained was the work of grace and not their doing. Human beings the way they are, so easily get prone to aggrandizing themselves or staking claims on success – both material and spiritual. We often fail to glorify the invisible Hands of God behind every achievement, or situation in life. There are some among us, who magnify people, or relationships/networking skills as key factors to success in life, some hold fast to perseverance, hard work, and determination as attributable to progress…But the truth remains that each of these was inspired by grace. Had it not been for the mercy and works of the Lord for us, none of these would have transpired to our advantage. What God’s Word teaches us today is to be thankful to the Lord, to be able to honor Him by our every word and deed, and to pray for His unfailing presence in our lives. We have a God who does everything to make us delightful so that we may call Him ‘Father’ and proclaim His greatness to the world around us. Amen

29/03/25, 10:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Pay to all what is owed to them: taxes to whom taxes are owed, revenue to whom revenue is owed. Respect to whom respect is owed, honor to whom honor is owed’ (Romans 13:7)

Christian Spirituality is grounded on respect for the dignity of the human individual. The reason why God took the form of man to redeem mankind was to teach man the significance of his creation, the purpose attached to his existence, and to demonstrate the unending compassion of God towards a sinful human race. Not because man merited praise, but because he was the pinnacle of God’s creation entitled him to claim the unalienable rights of equality, liberty, fraternity, and peaceful co-existence. We sometimes fail to understand this basic principle; thus, we go about questioning the need for giving respect to others. To some showing respect would be proportionate to one’s stature in society, merits, charisma, and general opinion of the crowds. Off-late people have defied authority on the pretext of some of their unpleasant characteristic traits, thus undermining the authority/sovereignty of God who has elevated them as part of His plan. To comprehend God’s plan and His purpose at each moment in time may not be possible for us human beings. What we have been called to do is to accept and obey God’s will, acknowledging God’s superior wisdom over ours. Paying respect to another would just fall in line with obeying God’s plan and purpose of creation. Let this season of Lent be a homecoming for us to oblige ourselves to follow certain virtues of outstanding import. To be tolerant, a bit more patient, fervent in prayer, and obliging to one another, are things more potent than any other. Lord Jesus, give us the grace to practice these virtues even though they may seem challenging in life. Amen

31/03/25, 12:43 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Therefore, as you received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk in Him, rooted and built up in Him and established in the faith, just as you were taught, abounding in thanksgiving’ (Colossians 2:6,7)

The Gospel, the truth, is yet to be spread to the corners of the world, but we have been the privileged few to know Jesus as Christ, the Saviour of mankind, and of all that He has accomplished for our behalf. Our obligation rests not just in this knowledge of Jesus Christ, but by being faithful to Him – His Word. Despite being inheritors to a humungous promise in Jesus Christ, we still fail to live up to His standards, the benchmark set for us believers. St Paul enlightens us to our ongoing duty of living with Jesus, walking with Him, being influenced by His attitudes, behavioural peculiarities (mercy and compassion), and rooting ourselves in faith that is expressed through acts of love. One begins this walking with the Lord, the moment his being is able to appreciate the goodness of God (being thankful for everything in life), and willingly surrenders to His plan. His master plan for You and me is to be rooted and built up in Jesus and bear fruits of His Spirit that can change the world around us. Love, joy, peace, patience, goodness, gentleness are some of these tangible fruits the Lord expects to see in us as we make each step on His path. Let us take stock of how much we have grown in patience, gentleness, and forgiving others as we await the Easter season which is just round the corner in two weeks. Lord Jesus, help us this day as we endeavour to do things with more love and forgiveness. Make us worthy of the gift that we have received in you, to be thankful for it and to live with more conviction in faith. Amen

01/04/25, 10:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Now He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will also supply and increase your store of seed and will enlarge the harvest of your righteousness’ (2 Corinthians 9:10)

The ‘seed’ is the manifold resources that our Creator has blessed us in life. Not just the material resource of money, but our unique abilities – intellect, knowledge, wisdom, understanding, time, faith, God’s Word, management/ life skills………the list is unending. To the sower, God gives in abundance, so that he may reap a plentitude of harvest. In addition to this harvest, he is given a multiplication of the store of resources (seed), so that much more can be sown and harvested for posterity. Who is a sower? The one who gives freely, willingly, and delightfully, the one who is ready to spend, invest, or even waste his own - to see a smile, satisfaction, and prosperity for the other. In this regard, the sower could be anyone not necessarily an agriculturist or planter, he can be a person as ordinary as a parent, a guardian, a teacher, a professional, an administrator, a leader… With all that you have, if you are willing to invest time, waste your leisure, dedicate yourself, or engross yourself in innovative thinking, what you can do with the Lord at your side is marvelous. If only every individual knew the resourcefulness with which God had created him, he would do all at hand to disproportionately multiply and support those given to his care with it – knowing that all that is spent is not lost but stored for a righteous harvest. Look into your life, be thankful for all the Lord has done for you to this day, Ask Him of how you could most wisely make use of what He has kept hidden in you. You have been called to develop, multiply, and progress in life, not to wilt, stagnate and die. May the Spirit of Jesus inspire us to this motive. Amen

02/04/25, 09:59 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You, dear friends, by building yourselves up in your most holy faith and praying in the Holy Spirit, keep yourselves in God’s love as you wait for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ to bring you to eternal life’ (Jude 20,21)

You and I are obliged to strive for progression in faith. One strengthens himself/herself in faith in Jesus Christ by frequently listening to God’s Word – being keen in learning God’s Word, listening to spiritual messages, interpretations on Scripture and the teachings of the Magisterium. These resources have become even more accessible to us with the boom and expansion of the Virtual reality space. It is the Holy Spirit who guides, inspires and takes us through these open resources for elevating our understanding and horizons of knowledge of what constitutes God’s will for us. We were created to know God, His plan for our well-being, and to love Him wholeheartedly. This love for God keeps us sensitive to those around us too. We are called to keep ourselves vibrant in this love for God, a step more each day. The Lord is powerful to guard us from falling off, this we must know. And this encourages us to seek His mercy each day, not relying on past successes or failures, but on God’s works to unfold for us in newer ways. There may be things, factors, behavioural traits that the Lord may point to us as inhibiting His works in us and here is where we ask His support to overcome. Let us learn to live as ‘ourselves’, by worshipping the Lord, trusting in His mercy and grace, and preparing ourselves for that great banquet to which we have been invited in Christ when we see Him face to face in Eternity. Amen

03/04/25, 10:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘It is not by strength that one prevails’ (1 Samuel 2:9)

With all the strength and passion in him, Moses, (the one born to deliver the Israelites from slavery) thought he could prevail over the Egyptians with his might and tactfulness. But he had to flee from the palace to the wilderness and lead years of oblivion and hardship before being called and anointed by God at the right time. Joseph, his father’s most preferred son had to toil and struggle for years as a slave among the Ishmaelites and to the Egyptians to preserve his life until God’s appointed time for his redemption and upheaval was at hand. His father Jacob had to prevail against extreme oddities in life, which none could have survived through, without the invisible Hands of the Lord who carried him through years of struggle and pain. These and the life histories of many great men dissect before us the truth that we sometimes fail to understand. Mere human capabilities have taken none anywhere in life. Even the strongest or the most equipped may find themselves limited by someone even stronger. It is God’s will and power that proves decisive in attaining success, exaltation, and growth. Man has been created to co-create, cooperate, and collaborate with the Lord for the development and upkeep of the earth. Not by rebellion, self-aggrandizement, or merely by his wholehearted efforts do men do wonders, but by the finger of the Lord who creates from nothing, and arrays from chaos. Let us therefore offer all our strengths to the Lord, for with the blessings of He, the provider, our strengths just multiply in leaps and bounds. Lord, teach us to be faithful to honor You for what you have provided us. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

04/04/25, 06:59 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You have not given me into the hands of the enemy, but have set my feet in a spacious place’ (Psalm 31:8)

All is not over. For the Lord has never given up on the one who has made Him his refuge. Longer years of distress and pain may have had a toll on your health – body, mind, and soul. You and I may be the subject of mockery or contempt before the others, an object of dread before our near and dear, a person lost or forgotten, in other words, a seemingly broken vessel…. but you still are beloved to the Lord. Your times are in His hands. And if you were to imagine of ‘the good’ that He has kept in store for you, (because you fear Him), you would have no bounds to joy. To be wrapped under the protection of the love of the Lord is more valuable than any other. David, the greatest of all Kings had this experience of His soul longing for the Lord than any other. Thus he wrote “Deep calls to Deep” and in the still, in the silence, when all alone, he found ‘the solace’ to quench the ‘thirsts’ of his life. Always believe, that you and I are in the Hands of a mighty God, who saves, protects, shields, until we are on firm ground. He can never abandon, though the world may - when situations change, the unexpected confronts you as a city under siege, the Lord Jesus has something else to tell you. Hold firm to His Hands, and make every step with Him, He was a pioneer and so would you be too. Take heart in the Lord, the message for you today. Amen

05/04/25, 10:34 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘With bow and arrows a man will come there, for all the land will be briers and thorns’ (Isaiah 7:24)

A land once known for its ‘bounty’ would become a ground of thorns and briers. The consequence of disbelief, of diminishing trust in God, can be alarming, as Isaiah had been foretold of what would become of the Judaic kingdom a few years from now. It is not that they would produce no fruit (a vineyard, renowned for its rich produce worth a thousand Shekels of Silver), but would turn into a rough and dangerous landscape where even human habitation was near impossible. Evolution and progression can lead human beings to venture into extremes, unaware of the fact that it is here that danger lurks and intrudes into their lives. It is not from one instance or occurrence that a man loses his faith, but years of numbness; disobedience to God’s ways, tenets, warnings, and preventing grace from overpowering his nature, that one fully aligns with the world. Thus, God’s Word reminds us time and again that it is better to trust in the Lord than any human being. By placing this as the median verse (middle) in the Bible, the Holy Spirit intends to communicate this message to us loud and clear. In the context of Isaiah’s prophesy it was King Ahaz (King of Judah) who was warned for his over-reliance on the Assyrian support (political alliance for defeating Syria and Israel), rather than turning to the Most High for strength and counsel. He was forewarned of what would be the consequence of his act in years not far from now, that his kingdom would be destroyed, made desolate, and people be made exiles and refugees of war. So would this verse apply to our personal lives, when we override God’s sovereignty, and take control of matters by way of tact, strategy, or influence, might…..Lord Jesus, teach us to hunt down the pride in us. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
07/04/25, 09:29 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Then hear from Heaven your dwelling place and forgive and render to each whose heart you know, according to all his ways, for you, You only, know the hearts of the children of mankind’ (2 Chronicles 6:30)

‘Heaven’ is God’s dwelling place – we must proclaim, so that our hearts may be filled with reverential love for God, who is above all. That we are mere mortals is what proceeds immediately when we give God His place and men theirs. Solomon, the second king of Israel built the great temple of Jerusalem and dedicated it as ‘the prayer house for all mankind.’ During the ceremony of consecration of the temple, Solomon made a long prayer on behalf of himself and for the people, before the Most High. He began this invocation by asking if God would dwell with men on earth, and if so this temple was symbolic of God’s dwelling among mankind. Indirectly to You and me, it would mean that in us, in the temple of our bodies, is the dwelling place of the Most High. A God who is omniscient and whose glory overwhelms that even the Highest Heavens cannot contain Him, willingly desires to live and work within You and me. He forgives our iniquities, trespasses, and faults, every time we kneel before His majesty and accept our sinfulness. To all He opens up His store of blessings, and each will be rewarded according to His deeds, as it is the Lord alone who knows the hearts of all flesh. But we must plead with Him that His mercy may overrun His justice, for if according to our deeds, we were to be recompensed, it may be likely that we would hardly earn anything. Thus to the Lord who reigns most High, who knows everything, who destines our goodness, let us humble ourselves, ask for the grace of forgiving ourselves and others, to be able to do justice to His call for us. May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart always be acceptable to You Jesus, my Lord and Master. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

08/04/25, 08:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘When I am afraid, I put my trust in you’ (Psalm 56:3)

It's easy to get terrified or fall in despair, it comes as a natural consequence of unexpected turnarounds in life. Especially when resources (time, persons, wealth ……...,) at hand (with all that you possess or have earned) are insufficient to defend oneself and stay intact on life’s journey, it is usual that any normal mortal would succumb to fear and anxiety. And what if the human mind becomes incapable of arresting the impact of this fear or bear with it; It could have deeper and severe consequences on the survival and healthy existence of a human being. Men have succumbed to fear, not because they were inept, incapable, or foolish, but because there wasn’t for them at that critical moment ‘a stronghold’ that could provide them a refuge or hope for moving on in life. An overtly consumerist society, community, inadequate familial support, or inept spiritual ecosystem could all be attributable to this fall of man. But does this mean that there is no panacea for this psychological problem? The Holy Spirit hints to us what essentially every human being is called to turn to, when life seems blank and bleak – to put our trust in the Lord; not in systems, people, institutions, or authorities. As these may mislead one, and at times even bring the way of truth to disrepute. The panacea to all fears is to hold firm to the Lord’s Hands, to hopefully wait for His works for us, Without Him, we are merely dust and dirt, the salt that has lost its saltiness, no longer good for anything but to be thrown and trampled underfoot. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
09/04/25, 08:32 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today 
'For your steadfast love is as high as the Heavens; your faithfulness extends to the clouds' (Psalm 57:10)
When all that constitutes our skies fail to yield the expected fruits, we humans tend to acknowledge that our skies are inadequate. David, the psalmist once prayed ' that the power of the Most High is not manifest in me is the cause of my grief'. And thus he asks God to envelope his skies with glory. His conviction was that God would soon send from the Heavens, His saving power. To David this expressed itself in the steadfast love and faithfulness of God. To You and me, the Lord Jesus is the same yesterday, today and tomorrow. His saving power, steadfast love and faithfulness would reach us the moment we call upon His name.His name is strength in weakness, abundance of peace when our hearts are disturbed and inevitable power in times of need. May your steadfast love O Lord reach up to the Heavens and faithfulness touch the clouds. Thankyou Lord
Amen

10/04/25, 08:05 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You have done foolishly. You have not kept the command of the Lord your God, with which He commanded you. For then the Lord would have established your kingdom over Israel forever’ (1 Samuel 13:13)

Every promise of God is meant to be for Eternity. But it always comes with a condition. And the condition on which the consummation of God’s promise depends is complete obedience on our part. Every vocation or calling of God is unique and special, as it was meant for the fulfillment of a great purpose in God’s plan. But men accord importance to the privilege associated with this call, and ignore to pay heed to obeying God’s will and command, there is a disruption in God’s plan. In all His greatness, God would never need you and me as inevitable for the successful completion of His plan, He can reach out to another who is wholeheartedly obliging to His call. This was what occurred to King Saul as he disobeyed God’s will to appease the people whom he led. We see this beautifully enacted in the series ‘House of David’, where the character of Saul is depicted as one who was initially humble, but lost the sense of His calling, because of his arrogance and pride. There is grace and power that builds up a man, when he is subservient to God’s command and desires. This blessing evades the one who fails to obey, however brilliant and capable a man may be. This was why the seer Samuel rebuked Saul, the King as having done foolishly, for which he would be deprived of a permanent legacy, (that which was to flow to his descendants too). Let us learn to give glory to God always by obeying Him, His command of loving one another and thus fulfilling our purpose in life. Amen

11/04/25, 06:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘From His fullness, we have all received grace upon grace’ (John 1:16)

The One for whom the prophets longed to see, to whom every soul would need to reorient to when in the dark, the One whose judgments teach the inhabitants of the world what it means to be righteous – He is Jesus Christ, the Lord and Master of all. He was sent to His own, they failed to recognize Him, nor did the world who was created and established by Him. In Him was the Light for all mankind, the Light that no darkness could overcome. Through what He accomplished for men, flowed into this world grace upon grace, to them - who believed and trusted Him. It was the Law, (which God the Father Himself knew that men could never be true to), which was still given to men by God’s servant Moses. But from time immemorial, the history of mankind has been one of absolute disobedience to a Divine Law they could not comprehend. Thus to this failing race, God opened the floodgates of Mercy, through the heart of His beloved Son, Jesus, who was pierced. From it flowed the precious blood, that which erased the sins of all mankind, and rewrote their destiny. What has come to us today is the truth – To All who believe in Jesus, who accept Him as Lord, they are transformed as ‘the children of God’, born not of the flesh, but in the Spirit and truth, to worship God in fullness and perfection (as God is Spirit). Let us thus, render our hearts, minds, and spirit to the Lord Jesus, surrender all that we carry within, to be emptied of vanity and to be filled by perfection – Grace, to love as He did, and to testify to the world that He is the only Light. Amen

12/04/25, 07:53 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You know all things and that you do not even need to have anyone ask you questions. This makes us believe that you came from God’ (John 16:30)

The disciples expressed their faith in Jesus, that He was the Son of God the Father. This faith was however from their experience, from what they heard, saw, and learned all through their three years of living with the Lord, while He was on earth. But even so, they failed to hold on to this faith in Jesus, when crises erupted in life. (Jesus was abandoned by these disciples, when He was arrested, taken for trial, and sentenced to death.) If ‘these’ who walked, talked, listened, and lived with Jesus slipped from faith at one point in life (until the Spirit of the Lord came upon them in anointing), so would be for each one of us, who proclaim our love for the Lord, our commitment towards His call, and desire to be with Him always. Jesus knows it all – what we are, what we have been designed for, and where we stand today. So let us ask Him to pray for us that we do not slip from faith, that we become anointed by the power of His Spirit, and live to fulfill the will of God for us. It is this that we submit in prayer before Him, for ourselves and for all whom He has given to our care. Jesus be with us. Amen

13/04/25, 07:05 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You were cut from what is by nature a wild olive tree, and grafted, contrary to nature, into a cultivated olive tree…….’ (Romans 11:24)

It was and is God’s doing, for which man can have no claims of greatness. That we who were just like the ‘wild olive’ to wilt and fall off, were tended to (in an extra-ordinary way) and grafted to the cultivated plant (a high-yielding variety), to be nourished from the sap of the latter to grow into a fruit yielding tree. Whether we are bearing fruit or not? is the question God asks us. There is no superiority from the cut-down branches, We who were grafted and cared for, can claim. Because of the kindness of the Lord, because of what He accomplished for our progress, because of the faith He instilled in our hearts by His Spirit in us, we have become one among the olives growing in the house of God. The Old Testament points to us, Joseph, the one whose branches were hindered by walls, the one whom archers bitterly shot at and wounded, but whose arms/feet were firm, which were made agile, by the hands of the Almighty. We are called to therefore return to our ‘Stronghold’ the Lord Jesus, who ensures us restoration in its fullness, not to fall back on the weapons/instruments of this world. He grafts the wild, propels growth in the weak, and prepares us for a yield in full measure for His Kingdom. As we commemorate the coming of the righteous King, our Saviour Jesus, humble on a donkey on to Jerusalem, let us ask Him to restore our foregone glory, the devastations of old, and make way for a victory that a thousand generations may know of. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
14/04/25, 09:14 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Six days before the Passover, Jesus therefore came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, whom Jesus had raised from the dead. So they gave a dinner for Him there. Martha served, and Lazarus was one of those reclining with Him at the table…’(John 12:1-2)

To Bethany, a rather insignificant town till then, Jesus halts for a stay on His way towards Jerusalem. Jesus knew that His time had come, the time to return to His Father in Heaven, after having fulfilled His purpose of Incarnation. This was the purpose for which the Son of God came to this world, as John the Evangelist explained (1 John 3:8) – to destroy the works of the Evil one. Thus from Bethany, we see the unraveling of all the events that were prophesied (of the Messiah) by the prophets – The dinner at Lazarus’s home (symbolic of the impending Passover dinner which was the inauguration of the New Covenant), Lazarus who reclined at the table with Jesus (the cause of which many longed to see Jesus and believe in Him), Martha who served (symbolic of what the New Covenant demanded of the disciples of Jesus – to love and serve God and fellow-beings), Mary’s anointing of Jesus’ feet (which depicted the disposition of heart of a disciple would need – worship and reverence to Jesus), the later events that follow were Judas’s reaction to Mary’s devotion (the hypocrisy that leads to the downfall of any believer’s soul) and Jesus’s triumphal entry into Jerusalem which occurred the next morning. It was from Bethany that the Salvation events in the life of Jesus began, where he appeared to His disciples after His resurrection to give them the great commission, from where He ascended to the Heavens, and into where He will come a second time to carry us into our promised Land. What He would expect from us then would be to be like the family of Lazarus – a witness to the world, a heart filled with gratitude that serves, and a heart that always gives glory to God, no matter what. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

15/04/25, 10:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He trains my hands for war so that my arms can bend a bow of bronze’ (Psalm 18:34)

It was God who trained His people to withstand their enemies is reminiscent from the numbers that constituted the armies of Israel. The people of God never had an organized army or militia to fight for them until the coming of the Kings. It was after King David’s reign that a more sophisticated army evolved with horsemen, and chariots being added to the existing infrastructure of foot soldiers. Despite not having the numbers as against their enemies (Philistines, Ammonites, Canaanites) God’s people won due to their obedience to God’s directions and the strategic overtures they took up. David had to defend himself from his enemies way before he ascended the throne. His initial struggles for self-defense emerged from the animosity of King Saul who was hell-bent to murder him. There were many others from the twelve tribes who fought against him, there were times he had to hide himself taking refuge in the wilderness, and as a refugee under King Achish of Gath (who was one of Israel’s greatest enemies). All through these years, it was the strength, support, and encouragement from the Lord that equipped him for battle, protected his life, and preserved him to the time appointed for his coronation as King of Israel. None of us would enjoy being at battle- ground, or encountering daily challenges and struggles that threaten us to the very core. Each one’s struggles in life can be very different, and none can claim to have understood another (like him) of the kind of tribulations he is going through. The fact remains that it is God who sustains one and empowers him with needed strength (spiritual), strategy, tact, and bend of mind to be decisive at life’s most indecisive times. Let us pray for this grace to be led by the Lord always, to be equipped by him, who enables one to bend even the now of bronze (that which is ordinarily impossible). Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

You and I have been called to be partakers of the New Covenant, because of Jesus Christ. He is the reason why we stand worthy (freed from our sins and transgressions), not in any Holy place, but in the Heavens before the presence of God our Father. 16/04/25, 09:28 - Megha Sherino: Selective rewriting of history often fuels polarization, not clarity.

When history becomes a battleground for ideology rather than a source of reflection, it can fracture society and foster hatred instead of understanding.

The discipline of history demands a careful and nuanced discernment of the past. It is not merely a recollection of events but a study of causes, consequences and context. However, when history is weaponised in the form of revisionism, especially with an intent to restore a perceived lost glory or correct historical wrongs by reverting to a "status quo ante", it ceases to be a guide for the present and becomes a tool for division.

The grundnorm of this discussion is that while historical wrongs must be remembered to avoid their reputation, they must not be interpreted as mandates to reclaim past statutes or boundaries.
16/04/25, 09:34 - Megha Sherino: It is important to distinguish revisionist history from historical reinterpretation. Reinterpretation is a legitimate academic exercise, where new evidence or perspective reshapes our understanding of the past. The revisionist history however, especially in the political sense, seeks to reinterpret the past to justify present day political agendas, often tied to nationalism, identity politics, or territorial claims.

' But I have called you friends, because I have made known to you everything that I have heard from my Father' (John 15:15)

Our lives are bound to Jesus as the branches to the vine. And the reason we have been designed in this manner is that God the Father desired that with Jesus, His Son, we would also bear fruits, that we would be like Him and follow Him. God our Creator, knew our feeble frame, that which Jesus reiterated to us that without Him we can do nothing. To be a Christ follower, to witness to the world about Jesus is the Father's will about our lives. And to this one, is promised everything he asks for, because the Holy Trinity is pleased with him/her. Before He died, Jesus who loved all those His Father had given to Him on earth ( including you and me) took one step forward to express the magnitude of God's love and concern for us. That we are not merely His disciples, but 'His friends',  to whom He intends to reveal even greater mysteries of God's kingdom. That to us He will send another helper, The Holy Spirit that comes from the Father. This Holy Spirit will teach and remind us all that we would need to uphold to make life pleasing to the Father. It is this pleasure of God that we are bound to secure while on earth. At times this calls for moments of silence, of being lonely but with Jesus in spirit, and of letting go of all those thoughts and opinions the world may have defined us with. 
Lord Jesus, teach us to spare time with you in prayer so as to overcome the trials and tribulations of the noisy and busy world around us with strong determination and conviction of the impending good yet to transpire . Amen

18/04/25, 07:30 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Simon Peter followed Jesus, and so did another disciple. Since that disciple was known to the High priest, he entered with Jesus into the courtyard of the High Priest, but Peter stood outside at the door’ (John 18:15)

Who could have been this other disciple? Bible scholars have different opinions, some state that this disciple could have been John or some other influential disciple of Jesus. Now since the name is not revealed, it is left to us to infer who this disciple was, about whom nothing more was mentioned. To me, he was Judas Iscariot, who was brilliant and known to the High priest, for the conspiracy hatched with them a week ago at the temple for thirty pieces of silver. In every possibility, Judas had just the right disposition of the heart towards Jesus, one would say – he believed that with the funds he raised, he could help Jesus overthrow the current politico-religious institution and establish His kingdom. But we are told (the Bible tells us) that his deed triggered the outbreak of Jesus’s arrest, unjust trial, and verdict of crucifixion, which we remember today. Jesus’s death on the Cross is a reminder to us that all the pain, suffering, agony, and burden of sin that Jesus carried on our behalf upto Calvary where He was crucified, was a new beginning for mankind. All our curses, treachery, iniquities, transgressions, diseases, he bore as a sacrifice of atonement before the Father for our redemption and reclaim into His Kingdom. You and I have become what we are today because of Jesus. Judas Iscariot, whose role was instrumental in Jesus’s arrest and death, stands an eye opener to us, Any one of us can at ‘such a weak’ moment in life(as he was in) turn out to do what he did, irrespective of who we are or what we proclaim. Let us therefore ask the Lord for forgiveness, grace and mercy to be faithful to Him always and throughout life. Amen

20/04/25, 06:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He said to them - Thus it is written, that the Christ should suffer and on the third day rise from the dead, and that repentance for the forgiveness of sins should be proclaimed in His name to all nations beginning from Jerusalem’ (Luke 24:46,47)

The Good news today brings to all nations is this – Christ has risen after suffering and death, and through Him all mankind has received forgiveness of sins. Now as any other Covenantal promise of God, this New Covenant is not without a stipulation put before mankind. The precondition for forgiveness of sins is that one must repent for his sins, must accept that every day in time there have been numerous sins that he has committed, for which he is unable to make ‘repair for’ by himself. As it is said, the word that has left the mouth is just like the stone that has been pelted which is beyond control, once released, so are our transgressions, iniquities, deeds and words. Knowingly and unknowingly through life, we have committed sins of omission and commission. And so it is in our imperative to make the reparation for these to set things back to their original orderliness. Jesus Christ has done this for us – He the unblemished lamb of God, bore the sins of the world and took upon Himself the punishment that was due to You and me. Thus we are here today, in joyful acclamation of the reason of our hope. To us this is His great commission – as He gave His disciples, who were asked to go forth to the ends of the earth to let all know the crux of human salvation that was already completed. As we commemorate this supreme feast of our redemption, let us make known to all - what Jesus has done for us, let us approach His Throne of Grace and mercy with a penitent heart, to be blessed to be called ‘The Holy People’, ‘The Redeemed of the Lord’, ‘The City sought after and not Forsaken’ as was foreseen by the prophets of Old. Thank you Jesus! Happy Easter!!!

21/04/25, 10:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You have made known to me the path of life; You will make me full of gladness with your presence’ (Acts 2:28)

The path to Life was opened to Adam and Eve in the Garden of Eden. To them was entrusted the upkeep, sustenance, and maintenance of the living world. God in His foreknowledge accompanied them with a limited list of don’ts (too) that were to be conscientiously adhered by them. Man was unable to keep to the limits set by God, somewhere sometime, sin crept in life through deceit of the Evil one, who manipulated the gift of curiosity (of man) to the untoward, thus inviting its unforeseen consequences of slavery and death. God in His mercy decides to restore the lost glory of man by making use of the sin to bring forth increased grace through His Son Jesus Christ. He who is the resurrection and Life came down to uplift the plight of man. He was the Way, the truth, and the Life, over whom no darkness could prevail. He (Jesus) made known to mankind the love of the Father, His saving grace, and the abundance of gladness He filled the hearts of those who believed in Jesus and accepted Him as their master. David the patriarch was one distinct for his steadfast faithfulness to God, for which he was rewarded with victory, greatness, joy, and a great descendant in line. Because of this promised descendant, Jesus, we await the same gladness, victory, and triumph as David. Let the words of King David, the psalmist reverberate in our mouths and hearts – we pray for unending life, life in its abundance today from the resurrected Lord Jesus. Amen

21/04/25, 10:35 - Megha Sherino: Article 26 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the freedom to manage religious affairs, ensuring that every religious denomination or section thereof has the right to establish and maintain institutions for religious and charitable purposes, manage its own affairs in matters of religion, own and acquire property, and administer it in accordance with law. This right is subject to public order, morality, and health. 
Key provisions of Article 26:
Establish and maintain institutions:
Religious denominations can establish and maintain institutions for religious and charitable purposes, including educational and welfare institutions. 
Manage own affairs:
Religious denominations have the freedom to manage their own affairs in matters of religion, including determining their organizational structure, internal governance, and religious practices. 
Own and acquire property:
Religious denominations can own and acquire movable and immovable property, including land, buildings, and other assets used for religious purposes. 
Administer property:
Religious denominations have the right to administer their property in accordance with law, which means that state laws can regulate the administration of religious property, but not infringe upon the right to manage religious affairs. 
Limitations to the right:
Public order, morality, and health:
The right to manage religious affairs is not absolute and is subject to restrictions imposed for the sake of public order, morality, and health. For example, religious practices that incite violence or harm public health can be regulated by the state.
Legitimate state regulation:
The state can enact laws to regulate the administration of religious property, but these laws must be validly enacted and cannot infringe upon the right to manage religious affairs. 
In essence, Article 26 protects the collective freedom of religion by ensuring that religious denominations have the autonomy to manage their affairs, establish and maintain institutions, and own and administer property, all while remaining subject to reasonable restrictions for the sake of public order, morality, and health

22/04/25, 08:20 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘While they were talking and discussing together, Jesus himself drew near and went with them. But their eyes were kept from recognizing Him’ (Luke 24:15,16)

The Risen Lord is with us; He himself draws near and walks with us always. But do we realize His presence with us in the very ordinary moments of life? It was not that these disciples (who were on their way to Emmaus) were unaware of the extraordinary things that were happening in their lives, in Jerusalem. They had heard the testimony of Mary Magdalene who claimed to have seen the Risen Jesus, that of Peter who spoke to them all about the empty tomb. But all these seemed to them (as Luke rightly interprets the situation) as idle tales that they did not believe. It was despair and lack of clarity of ‘what next’ that ruled their hearts. After all, the wonders they had seen while with Jesus for three years, His words and prophesies, were just forgotten by them when crisis struck (headlong) and there was nothing much they felt they could now think of to move ahead. This story of Emmaus which is only read in the Gospel of Luke could have been real or merely one of imagination, but with a profound intent of what ‘faith’ would mean to every disciple of the Lord. Even in the most ordinary and clumsy moments in life, faith teaches us to believe that the Lord works in us and through us. What makes the difference is in our acknowledging and asking Him of what He needs us to do in cooperation with Him. Emotions of despair, of anger, of pride, of jealousy, are all bound to erupt in our daily lives, He knows and accepts it all, but if we were to manage them constructively, it would be the work of Grace and nothing else. Let us pray for being overwhelmed and led by Jesus in all the encounters of this day, one day at a time, not to be taken over by the anxiety of tomorrow. (tomorrow will take care of itself). Amen

29/04/25, 10:16 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘How can you believe, when you receive glory from one another and do not seek the glory that comes from the only God’ (John 5:44)

Jesus was doing the works of His Father, the healing at the pool of Bethsaida was one such incident. Jesus came in search of this invalid man who lay by the pool of healing expecting to be healed for over long thirty-eight years; and the story tells us that this man was healed by God’s Word (in an almost extraordinary way). Now what mattered to the Jews who were witnesses to this miraculous healing of the man, was the Sabbath and laws connected to it. The pain and agony that this man carried within all these years was of least concern to them. Thus they argued with Jesus on the pretext of the Sabbath and were furious, trying to find a way of getting rid of Him because He equated Himself to God the Father. In this context, Jesus unanimously speaks about Himself, making it clear that He dwelt on the will of His Father and the glory that comes from Him, never on human acceptance. If You and I are to persevere in faith, we must bear in mind that there would occur in life several circumstances when we would be abandoned, misunderstood, left all alone to bank on the plentitude of glory that comes from above. And the truth is that it is this glory that we would need in our lives for sustenance and growth, the Grace from above, over any other human acceptance or acclamation. Let us thus be seekers of the Glory of God, His Son Jesus who was given to us for our redemption and resurrection to life. Lord Jesus, let your glory surround us every moment in life, Amen

05/05/25, 09:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Who is wise and understanding among you? By his good conduct let him show his works in the meekness of wisdom’ (James 3:13)

What true wisdom and understanding should lead us to is explained throughout Chapter 3 of the Letter of St James. Wisdom would exhibit itself in goodness and meekness of the spirit. Contrary to the ways of the world, where little knowledge would speak for itself and express itself ruthlessly in judging another. The fact is that we hardly know much about ourselves, often forgetting what we have been called to become or grow into, but we pretend to be ‘knowing it all’, unable to bridle or restrain our tongues to speak ill of anything and anybody. The Word of God reminds us (James 4:12) ‘There is only one lawgiver and judge, he who is able to save and to destroy. But who are you to judge your neighbor? The Lawgiver and judge is God; and what He expects of us is to be holy and blameless before Him, to live lives worthy of the love He has given us in His pleasure and will through Jesus Christ. You and I are accountable before this Almighty God, not for the many deeds and works of power and pomp we did accomplish through our lives. These may have been driven by the jealousy and self-ambition within us, the reason for all disorder and boastfulness. We will be accountable for our failure to be filled with wisdom from above, which is pure, peaceable, gentle, open to reason, full of mercy and good fruits, impartial and sincere. To be meek, humble, and compassionate as Jesus – this is why we have been redeemed, reinstated as sons and daughters of the Heavenly Father, and predestined in Jesus. Let us discern what it takes to be wise and understanding before the Lord and not just before mortals. Amen

06/05/25, 09:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You who received the Law as delivered by Angels did not keep it...’ (Acts 7:53)

To the Scribes, the Elders, Priests, the High Priest and the entire Council of the Senhedrin to whom he was brought for trial, St Stephen boldly spoke about Jesus. Taking references from the Scriptures - the Books of the Law and the Prophets, he explained to them the mystery of God’s salvation that was fulfilled in Jesus. It was this Salvation accomplished in Jesus, which God tried in His wisdom to make known to His beloved people (the Chosen race). They failed to keep to the Laws and submit themselves to this Divine Law, which would have been a cause of their salvation. As the Bible says, they stumbled and fell. This message of the Cross which was ‘the real power’ (the world could not understand in all its wisdom) was made comprehensible and manifest to those who believed in the Word as it is preached by ordinary men. After giving an account of what transpired to those who rejected this Salvation, the very question the Holy Spirit asks You and me is this ‘Do you still intend to persist in the stubbornness of disbelief (stiff-neckedness)? Is it not better to submit ourselves to the wisdom and magnanimity of God’s mercy which longs for our come back? Let us humble ourselves before the Mighty Lord, to be exalted and glorified by Him. Amen

07/05/25, 09:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘All those the Father gives me will come to me, and whoever comes to me I will never drive away’ (John 6:37)

The Bible says that the Father has given Jesus, His Son all things, which also signifies Jesus’s ultimate authority over all men on earth. To befriend Jesus is indeed our greatest privilege; for we have a God who whenever we call out to Him, comes near to comfort, support, strengthen, and deliver us. When at good times in life, or when we come across success after success, we may be led to believe and think that it was the works of our Hands that gave us all that we own today. But the fact remains, that it was the Lord who worked for us, on our behalf to operationalize our plans and bring them to fruition. Even among those who walk with the Lord, there are occasions when they tend to drift into the human way or reason by way of their intellect. It is then that subjectiveness/prejudice takes the upper hand, even though we know that we have been called not to judge or counsel but to love unconditionally. Jesus knows this frailty of the human nature. Therefore, He promises us that when You and I are with Him, we would outgrow our limitations, no matter how weak, inept, and underprivileged we may be as per worldly standards. And this applies to all men, whoever comes to Jesus – to him is given unending possibilities while on earth and abundance in Eternity. So let us ground ourselves in the basics of our faith – to trust in Jesus always, to be compassionate to those around us (our roads to Eternity are unique)and seek His counsel of what next needs to be accomplished in daily living. Whenever in hurt, pain, and woundedness, this promise of Jesus should be our lifeline and hope for a come back. Jesus be with you today. Amen

10/05/25, 08:33 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

'Someone will say, You have faith and I have works. Show me your faith apart from your works and I by my works will show you my faith' ( James 2:18)

The Bible teaches us that faith and works go together in God's kingdom. Among the two, faith predominates - it being a theological virtue along with hope and charity, gifts given from above. It is thus that without faith, one cannot please the Lord even with all the good works of our hands. Faith implies being expressed in good works. It is faith in our loving Father that persuades one to act in charity - to help the needy, to support the less privileged, to spend time and resources with another, or to walk and extra mile for another. By faith we believe, that the measure with which we have given will be given back to us, pressed down, shaken together and poured into our hands by our Father.  But since You and I have not been called to do every good deed, it is in what we do that our faith is proven. What I must do to demonstrate my unfailing trust in the Lord, will be to obey Him.  So when I begin to start doing things that the Lord asks me to do, by virtue of my faith I please the Lord. Therefore doing good which I have not been assigned to do is not my call. It is thus today's World from the Holy Spirit tells us that one cannot perceive of faith apart from works and works that do not emerge from faith. Let us humbly ask the Lord Jesus of what He expects of us today and work towards its fulfilment. In Jesus name, we pray. Amen

'I will turn all my mountains into a road and my highways shall be raised up' (Isaiah 49:11)

The mountains are the blocks that prevent you and me from connecting with God. 14/05/25, 09:35 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The tenth is collected by people who die, but in the other case by Him who is declared to be living. One might even say that Levi who collects the tenth, paid the tenth through Abraham, because when Melchizedek met Abraham, Levi was still in the body of his ancestor’ (Hebrews 7: 8-10)

Abraham believed and this made him righteous before the Most High. Abraham’s belief/conviction of God’s power over everything was expressed by him in several modes throughout his lifetime. To be able to stand firm for what you believe, be it in the Almighty or even in worldly aspects calls for much grit and wisdom. So You and I must accept the personality of this Patriarch, Abraham, who not only stood firm for what he believed, but also was ready to go out of the way to prove his beliefs. At a time when the Levi priests were inexistent, Abraham went above board to pay a tenth of the spoils of war (material wealth) to Melchizedek, the Priest of God Most High. His wisdom gave him the discernment to understand that Melchizedek was no ordinary one, a priest forever, the King of Peace and righteousness, to be honored and the One through whom the Blessings of God were to bear fruit in him (Abraham) and generations. Jesus who is this Melchizedek, is our High Priest forever, the Prince of Peace and righteousness (it is He who would judge the nations on that Final day). Does this knowledge about the Lord engender in us the works of faith - love, hope, that would bring into the world mercy, compassion, and care for one another? If not, we must ask the Lord to pour into our hearts His Spirit, the Spirit of discernment, wisdom, and understanding to distinguish the right from the wrong, to voice out for those marginalized, those in need and whom the Lord brings before us to be generous. By listening to Him, we become righteous, by adhering to His inspirations, we become wise, as Abraham our Father of believers. Amen

15/05/25, 09:45 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Truly, truly I say to you, a servant is not greater than his Master, nor is a messenger greater than the One who sent him. If you know these things, blessed are you if you do them’ (John 13:16,17)

We know that a servant is not greater than his master and that a messenger can never outdo the One who has sent him, but we often behave like the unfaithful servant and messenger. It is God our Master who has sent us as stewards of his goodness and graces to this world, and how do we in turn vouch for his faithfulness to us? To some wealth, possessions, name, fame, and all seem a blessing we owned through our meritorious deeds and ways. This is why we end up accusing God when suffering and pain make unexpected entries into our lives. We forget that it is Jesus who has chosen us as his prophets and witnesses in this world, to be His light, and be a shadow of His presence in a dark and disheartened world. There are ‘many’ who go around deepening the wounds of others, increasing the burden of their sufferings, and judging others. The shortcomings and set-backs of others may seem so obvious and consequential to us who (in fact) know nothing of their call or destiny, and the irony is that we neither know how to tackle even the most insignificant issues in our personal lives many a times. Thus the Lord tells each one of us – If you know these things – that you have been entrusted from above, that you are called to fulfil God’s intended purpose about you; then you are to engage in them, to be blessed. The blessing aligned to every promise is open to us when the promise is consummated, the Bible teaches us. Let us ponder upon what constitutes those promises analogous to our state of living – our Divine vocation and pray for the grace to live our lives worthy to secure these promises. Amen

16/05/25, 09:20 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Ask of me, and I will make the nations your heritage, and the ends of the earth your possession’ (Psalm 2:8)

There are times of helplessness in everyone’s life, when all that one does would only fall out of place, even the most extricate decisions (the lifesavers, those taken with utmost care and tact) would appear futile and of no avail. It is then one learns to calm oneself and surrender himself to the One authority Most High. The soul is tamed to trust in God, to be relentful in words, ways, and deeds, to accept how jeopardous a life would be outside the framework of God’s protection. And here is where the Lord calls us to be sensitive about as we go about life’s calling each day. Ask of me, Jesus tells us, assuring that even the nations would be made our heritage, when we ask Him with faith and with a heart ready to acknowledge His greatness. We have a God who is above all, above board, and in control of the Cosmos. It is not merely this knowledge that must dawn in one’s mind, but also the conviction to approach Him more than any other, that would lend credence/traction to our supplications made before Him. Not that God is unaware of our needs, our inadequacies, or fallacies, but He intends to uplift us to a higher level in our spiritual journey that He leads us through ‘valleys of death’, that we acknowledge, it was because of Him and His unfailing love for us that we overcame, emerged victorious, and triumphed every time though we (leave apart the people around us) never expected to make a comeback. Therefore, let this prayer be ours from the bottom of our hearts, with gratitude – Let us ask the Lord for all that we wish to be blessed with, material and spiritual progress – for a life of dignity and well-being. Amen

17/05/25, 11:44 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But the one who prophesies speaks to people for their strengthening, encouraging and comfort’ (1 Corinthians 14:3)

To bring to all men the message of Christ – Paul undertook different modes of preaching the Word. He spoke to them based on the revelations received from above, to the Greeks who were considered highly civilized and wise, he spoke to them the knowledge of Christ and the Cross, he prophesied at times and at several times gave the Christian communities a code of living through compilation of instructions. It was the Word of God – Jesus, that Paul introduced to many, proclaimed, emphasised, and pointed to through all his missionary journeys, his persecutions, trials, sufferings and life. Love for the Lord was what persuaded him to get going and to continue with his mission, across stormy seas, in the midst of persecution, temptations, and trials. The underlying theme that motivated him was love, into which he was growing more and more – into the likeness of Jesus Christ. And this prompted him to take to preaching the Gospel for strengthening, encouraging, and comforting believers, to increase their faith and align their lives in the light of love. This is the message for us today, to ground ourselves in love, by learning to be like Jesus, speak like Him, forgive like Him, and live like Him through this new day, He has gifted us. We may fail and even fall, but to the Lord what matters is whether we give it a try. Let our every action today be ignited by consideration for the other. Amen

23/05/25, 09:24 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘…...so that your faith might not rest in the wisdom of men but in the power of God’ (1 Corinthians 2:5)

We might at times feel that certain occurrences in life are unexpected. But today the Holy Spirit tells us that there are no coincidences or unexpected turnarounds in life. Every situation in life comes with its unique lessons and realizations. Abraham would not have turned into the Father of all believers, had he not left his native, his kin, and the certainties of life while at Ur. He wouldn’t have learned the meaning of perfect sacrifice to God, had he not been asked to sacrifice his only son to God (though God prevented the untoward). Isaac who learned from his father (Abraham), was calm and composed with no bickering towards the Canaanites who confiscated the very wells he dug. Jacob would never have understood the greatness and mercy of God; had he been confined to the comfort of his home with his mother. Nor would he have ever learned to develop his life skills (God-given) had he been given a better and understanding father-in-law (who never provided for his children and grandchildren – the Twelve patriarchs). The Bible tells us that no one could be as crooked as Labaan (Jacob’s father-in-law), who would go to any extent to misappropriate Jacob’s hard-earned wealth. All through the Bible enumerates tales of the unfortunate whom God chose for His great plans. Now, if in life, you are going through the unusual, the unexpected, highly distressing moments in life (blameless), it is that your faith might grow, not to rest in the wisdom of men, but in God’s might, God’s power, and God’s marvels. The Lord loves you so much, that He wishes to open the doors of Heaven at your call, so that by knowing Him, You might become His witness to the world around you. Taste and see how great the Lord is! Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

24/05/25, 08:32 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today 

' for the law made nothing perfect, there is on the other hand the introduction of a better hope through which we approach God (Heb 7:19)

Even the most righteous among men may find themselves grappling with faith when things and people around seem to adversely surmount them. Moses, the Law giver found it hard to contain the rebellious people whom God entrusted to him to be led to the promised land. He failed to obey God (though only once) when tormented from all sides by criticism, allegations and insults. This was to prove to us that none could attain perfection before God (by himself) or by stringently observing the Laws. The Laws were a shadow of better things yet to be revealed to us. The hope that everyone could be saved and perfected to worship God by the offering (of self) of Jesus, the High Priest, was what these precedents pointed to. We are enabled to approach the Throne of Mercy, through Jesus to be cleansed, renewed and reinstated in glory. And we have obtained this by His power and might. Not for just a few, but to everyone who humbles himself before the Lord 'accepting' his vulnerability to sin, this grace is open to. This is the hope the New Covenant provides us, that in Jesus we have become inheritors to all God's promises, provided we trust in Him. Thus St Paul spoke, we have become all the more conquerors because of Him who loved us. Thankyou Jesus. Amen

25/05/25, 08:05 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The voice of your watchmen – they lift up their voice; together they sing for joy; for eye to eye they see the return of the Lord to Zion’ (Isaiah 52:8)

It took me some time to comprehend the meaning of watchmen in this verse from the Lord. The Spirit of God tells us that You and I are called to take up the role of watchmen or sentinels. To be the proclaimers of ‘good news’ to those around us, those looming in the dark, those who need support to be revived/ to be filled with God’s mercy. Isaiah took up this role; he kept reminding the forlorn people that it was no more doomsday for them, but hope, restoration, revival, and glory once again that awaited them. He continued this prophesy of ‘hope’ and ‘joy’ when he reminded them that ‘behold, Jerusalem, I have set sentinels around you, who never sleep or slumber, who lift up their voices of prayer for your behalf, before the Mighty One. We sometimes sympathise with ourselves saying that we have no one to pray for us, to listen or understand us. This is not true; the Lord God reminds us today – Around you is a host of sentinels who keep communicating with the Most High all day long for you and me, to remind the Lord of His great promises for His beloved ones – you and me. This is the silver lining for you and me, that no matter how much we feel lost, abandoned or disheartened, we have this multitude of hosts who intercede for us, the saints, the martyrs, and even the Queen of all Angels – Mary. The reality is even more promising, it is the Lord Jesus, Himself who intercedes for our mercy before His Father. Thus, we must affirm ourselves in faith, by thanking the Lord for His salvation, and also because of this revelation of His compassion, love, and unfailing presence before us. Amen

हाल ही में किस राज्य सरकार ने भारत की पहली विस्टाडोम जंगल सफारी ट्रेन लॉन्च की है?
*Answer:— Uttar Pradesh*

*4. How many lakh new members have been registered in the Employees Provident Fund Organization till March 2025?*
कर्मचारी भविष्य निधि संगठन में मार्च 2025 तक कितने लाख नए सदस्य पंजीकृत हुए हैं?
*Answer:— 14.58 lakh*

*5. Recently, President Murmu has given how many bravery awards for bravery and valor?*
हाल ही में राष्ट्रपति मुर्मू ने बहादुरी और पराक्रम के लिए कितने वीरता पुरस्कार प्रदान किए हैं?
*Answer:— 39*

*6. On which date was 'World Metrology Day' celebrated recently?*
हाल ही में किस तारीख को 'विश्व मापविज्ञान दिवस' मनाया गया है?
*Answer:— 20 May*

*7. In which country has the World Hydrogen Summit 2025 been organized recently?*
हाल ही में किस देश में विश्व हाइड्रोजन शिखर सम्मेलन 2025 आयोजित किया गया है?

*Answer:— 253rd*

*9. Who has recently announced to launch a nationwide public movement campaign against plastic pollution?*
हाल ही में किसने प्लास्टिक प्रदूषण के खिलाफ राष्ट्रव्यापी जन आंदोलन अभियान शुरू करने की घोषणा की है?

26/05/25, 11:06 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For to this You have been called, because Christ also suffered for you, leaving you an example, so that you might follow in His steps’ (1 Peter 2:21)

The passage goes on to enumerate the things that Jesus did and what we have been called to imitate through our lives, to conduct ourselves honorable before men and before the Lord. Jesus committed no sin, He had no deceit in His mouth; when reviled He did not give back in return; when put to undue suffering He did not threaten anyone; ‘instead’ He entrusted Himself to Him who judges justly. What could be more onerous for us than to imitate the ways of the Lord? Thus, the Spirit of Jesus urges us not to be led by the passions of the flesh, not to be stumbling stones to those whom the Lord dearly loved, those whom He intended to transform. Because of You the name of God is profaned before the nations, the Word of God reminds us. To us is given this message from above, that now is the time to repent for all the unkind words, gestures, and actions we have knowingly or unknowingly committed, by which many have been distanced from the ‘truth and light’ of the world – Jesus. As we celebrate the mysteries of His passion, suffering, death, and resurrection each day through the Sacrament of the Eucharist, we are called to mind the excruciating pain and agony the Lord underwent (blameless) just for the sake of fulfilling the will of His Father for all mankind. Let us ask Him for grace and mercy to transform into Him this new day, to be His light in the world, and to follow closely His footsteps each day. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
27/05/25, 10:48 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For though the Lord is high, He regards the lowly, but the haughty He knows from afar’ (Psalm 138:6)

Even in a society that was far from egalitarian, referring to the times in the Old Testament, when the Laws were in force for God’s people, there was a Divine law that provided for the needs of orphans and widows. We have a God, who in all His greatness, shows special regard to the poor, the downtrodden, the orphaned, the destitute, and those renounced to the fringes. It is thus that Jesus warned the religious leaders of His time (Pharisees, Scribes) - what was acceptable before God was not the mint, rue, and herbs they tithed so conscientiously at the temple, but of a lifestyle that welcomed inclusiveness, of mercy, justice, and faithfulness. It was the latter that was to be given prominence over ritualistic tithing. One’s predisposition of the heart is what makes one acceptable before the Most High. It is this one (the humble one), who when raises his arms in prayer, is most powerful to penetrate the clouds and bring an answer from the Lord. To be just, merciful, ‘faithful’ in word and deed is what the Beatitudes revolved around, and where the real transition of our lives is intent upon. Jesus taught us the very fact that none could make a change in the heart of another, without the touch of God’s Spirit. And with God in us, nothing is impossible – the Blessed Mother reminds us. Let us pray for growing near to the Lord this day, by assimilating His humble Spirit that brings us closer to Him. We pray for grace to overshadow the works of the flesh in us, to be led by Your Spirit all through this day. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
