28/05/25, 09:45 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you that you should go and bear fruit and that your fruit should abide, so that whatever you ask the Father in my name, he may give it to you’ (John 15:16)

It was in God’s master plan that we were created, each unique with definite characteristic traits, put into different life situations/contexts, and called to bloom in a land that became our homes. When God decided everything for us, we are assured that He meant it for our good; for who else could have known the most minute details of our lives as He did. Those concerns, burdens, and struggles we go about each day in bringing to fruition our daily chores, He sees and appreciates so greatly, that which none other may discern. Standing firm to our call, He expects us to be blameless, holy, and fruitful in all that we engage in. When the ways of the world are to gratify the delight of the eyes, the pride of riches, and luxury, Jesus asks us to be blameless, holy, and fruitful – to be like Him. Every task Jesus did while on earth was with the connivance and consent of His Father. None in history could have claimed victory in fulfilling His purpose of life, as Jesus did. This is the fruitfulness that the Spirit of Jesus prepares us for each day - to please the Father, to seek His will about us, and to work for His purpose on earth. Let us pray for this Spirit of God to hover over us, our tasks, our beliefs, attitudes, words, and actions; that filled with Him, we may be yet another Christ. May all that you wish to accomplish this day turn fruitful because of the love of Jesus that works and intercedes for you. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

29/05/25, 11:09 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For Christ has entered not into Holy places made with hands, which are copies of the true things, but into Heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God on our behalf’ (Hebrews 9:24)

The significance of this day – The Ascension of our Lord into the Heavens, is – that we have in the Highest places before God, a mediator who would intercede for us always in prayer. He who became like us in every way apart from sin, took our human form was subject to temptation, to disease in the body, to suffering, and an excruciating death on the Cross, knows our every need. There have been times when we have longed for someone on earth, to whom we could reach out for support and prayers – one who understands, who never accuses but chides us for our shortcomings with a view that we outgrow them. The Spirit of God tells us today, that ‘this one’ whom we can trust wholeheartedly, who would never let us down is Jesus. God loved the world so much that He gave up His only Son for us that through Him we may be transformed and saved. Jesus is the Prince of Peace, the one who fights for us when the enemy is oppressive, who carries us on His shoulders when life’s burdens and cares weighs us down, who advocates for us before the Father despite our sinfulness/unworthiness, so that we may receive grace and mercy that uplifts. Before the Lord, all other delights, worldly pleasures, and human assurances would turn as shadows, as He is the Light, that enlightens the depths of our person. Now that He has entered into the Holy of Holies beside His Father, we have more to rejoice. Because of His promise of another helper, the Paraclete who proceeds from the Father and Son, who will be sent to us to empower us. Let us seek anointing of this Spirit of Power whom Jesus has assured us of, ask His intervention into the trials and challenges of life’s journey, and seek His inspiration before we make a word or deed today. Come Holy Spirit come!

Dr. Mini Sherino
30/05/25, 09:29 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For the One whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for He gives the Spirit without measure’ (John 3:34)          

The Oneness of the Holy Trinity where the Father, Son, and the Holy Spirit work in unison to fulfill the master plan of Salvation for mankind is brought to our meditation today. The saints prayed for this oneness with the Lord. Paul in all humility exclaimed that even with all the revelations, the stigmata, the various signs and wonders, the Spirit of Jesus filled Him with, he was never perfected as Jesus was, while on earth. Thus, the apostle admitted that he pressed on to take hold of what Jesus had endowed in Him, not that he had already obtained his goal. But every day was an attempt for him to persevere ahead forgetting the failures of the past. The love of Jesus so fervently possessed him that he couldn’t imagine of anything greater than the Lord. You and I are likewise called to live up to what we have already attained – the gift of Sonship because of Jesus. And anyone who enters into unison with Jesus, to him is promised the power of the Holy Spirit without limits. It is unto Jesus that all authority of the Heavens and the earth has been given, that His Spirit of truth may also transform our lowly bodies to the glory that emanates from His resurrection. In Jesus’ ‘name’ we pray today for healing, for deliverance from all that is earthly and undermining our Spirit, and for the gift of anointing we earnestly need today to the One who will provide without measure. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

31/05/25, 08:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘When a righteous man fled from his brother’s wrath, she guided him on the straight paths; she showed him the Kingdom of God, and gave him knowledge of Angels. She prospered him in his labors and increased the fruit of his toil’ (Wisdom 10:10)

The righteous man referred to here is Jacob. It’s hard for us to accept Jacob as ‘righteous’ considering his past ways (deception and taking over the birthright cum blessings of his brother, Essau). Even in him, God perceived righteousness – the audacity to seek God, to learn His will, to follow the path of righteousness even when confronted with hardships and challenges in life. Jacob’s determination to brave all odds in life – through the many struggles he endured both against men and God, proved him righteous, and therefore God honored him with the title – Israel. God’s faithfulness to Israel was above board (Covenantal), even when the Israelites proved unfaithful. All He expected of them was their willingness to make a comeback. You and I are the New Israel, who have covenanted with God, through the meritorious works of Jesus, His only Son. Jesus is the personification of the wisdom that led Jacob. Through Him(Jesus) we are taught the mysteries of God’s kingdom, the paths of righteousness, and given all under Heaven and Earth as a blessing. Whoever seeks Jesus, the Way, Truth, and Life that He showed us are freed of their sins, their unworthiness, made sons of the Kingdom and co-heirs with Jesus. Know this and take heart that, in all our struggles, pain, and suffering while on earth, we are redeemed, justified, delivered, and honored by Jesus, the ‘Cornerstone’, the rock and shield of our Salvation and because of whom we live. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

01/06/25, 06:59 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And I said, ‘Woe to me! For I am lost; for I am a man of unclean lips, and I dwelt in the midst of a people of unclean lips; for my eyes have seen the King, the Lord of Hosts’ (Isaiah 6:5)

As he stood before the Lord in prayer at the temple - to Isaiah, was revealed the glory and greatness of the Creator. All that he could describe was what he spelt out in human words as the Lord seated upon a throne, high and lifted up, the train of His robe filling the entire temple. Above Him in awe and reverence stood the Seraphim, who kept continuously praising the Lord reciting – Holy, holy is the Lord of hosts, His glory fills the whole earth. This vision which was given to the prophet for a flash of a moment reminded him of his unworthiness, his sinful nature, the nothingness that tried to overpower him from deep within his being, not just that - of the vanity of everything around him. Thus, his immediate response to this realization was contriteness, a humble submission of himself to the Holy One, an urge to be silent/to restrict himself from sinning by word. The Lord was pleased to sanctify the prophet owing to this mindset of his. And here we are asked to examine ourselves, of how we appear before the Most High in the Sacrament of the Holy Eucharist. Do we express the same reverence and awe before the Lord, or do we take things and people around us for granted? (as a matter of repetitive monotonous ritual). Do we acknowledge that God is High above, way above our comprehension, prayers, and ways; or do we desecrate Our Creator by equating ourselves to his realm through our arrogance, idle talk, and hard-heartedness? Let our participation in the Lord’s Banquet be an occasion for worshipping Him, giving Him thanks, and thus channelizing grace into our humble lives. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
02/06/25, 09:33 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘We ought always to give thanks to God for you, brothers beloved by the Lord, because God chose you as the first fruits to be saved, through sanctification by the Spirit and belief in the truth’ (2 Thessalonians 2:13)

We are indeed privileged to be the beloved of the Lord because God chose us as the first fruits of Salvation. But all is not done, there is much more to be transformed, harvested, and redeemed till the end times, God’s Word reminds us. The will of God is that all men shall come to know Jesus and the Salvation that God meant to be perfected through Him. As were we sanctified by the Holy Spirit and grounded in faith (faith in Jesus), with nothing meritorious in us to claim Salvation, so would this process of redemption be fulfilled by God in all mankind far and near. When Abraham was called into this Salvation, he was perfected in faith through many trials, tribulations, and life experiences – the same fate awaits everyone(likewise) called by the Father to Salvation. An ongoing process of purification lies in waiting for you and me in life. It is the Holy Spirit that purifies and sets one apart to be used for God’s greater purposes. Unless one humbles himself by submitting before the Almighty to be purified, he remains the salt that has lost its saltiness only to be trampled on the ground. Thus Peter was told by the Lord (during the Last Supper) that unless I wash you, you will have no share with me. To Paul, the Lord showed him (well in advance) the very many sufferings he had to endure for the sake of the Gospel. We too carry our burdens of suffering, pain, and difficulties of living each day; we must ask the Lord, of what He intends of us, of the way ahead, and to take us through these trials to be triumphant beneficiaries of His Kingdom. Guide and strengthen us through this day, Lord. Amen

03/06/25, 09:02 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘…. The land of a rich man produced plentifully, and he thought to himself, what shall I do, for I have nowhere to store my crops’ (Luke 12:16,17)

To be careful, to be on guard against all kinds of covetousness of this world, was what Jesus was trying to impose upon the people who came to listen to Him. Thus, he spoke this parable to them which began thus…. The land of a rich man produced plentifully. When abundance flows in (be it in the form of material blessings, success, accomplishments…...), it is so usual that people tend to oversee its source and those obligations attached to these riches. Instead, pride takes a grip on their intellect and faculties of thinking, thereby leading them to possess and hoard them. Over time, they become prisoners to these, rather than the Holy Spirit in them, thus falling prey to misappropriating these goods for hypocrisy, vainglory, and deification of the self. The rich man in the Parable narrated by Jesus, was just an ordinary person like you and me, who was fortunate to enjoy bounty in his produce. The sin of pride that unknowingly sprouted in him, caused him to think and reason out with himself of what he would need to do to store up the riches. Rather than taking recourse to the Lord (who gifted him with plentitude) he chose to decide for himself, what would best suit him. The Prince of this world, blinded him (the rich man) from realizing where he belonged, the purpose of his calling, and the right use of the resources given to his care. Thus, he failed in fulfilling or finishing the purpose of his life, His Creator had envisioned of him. A reminder to us, that to fall or to err is human, but to make a comeback by seeking after the Lord is our Divine purpose, the reason why we have been called to this earth. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

03/06/25, 20:07 - Megha Sherino: The **face lock feature** is a biometric authentication system that uses **facial recognition** to unlock a device or grant access to a system. It relies on a combination of **hardware components**, **software algorithms**, and **digital system concepts** to function accurately and securely.

---

## 🔧 **Digital System Concepts Involved in Face Lock**

### 1. **Data Acquisition**

* Involves capturing an image or video of the user’s face using a **camera sensor**.
* The image is converted from **analog light signals** to **digital data** using an **ADC (Analog-to-Digital Converter)**.

### 2. **Image Preprocessing**

* The captured image is processed to improve quality using filters (like histogram equalization, noise reduction).
* It may involve **cropping**, **scaling**, or **normalizing** the face region.

### 3. **Feature Extraction**

* Important features like distance between eyes, jawline shape, and nose contour are extracted.
* Algorithms like **Eigenfaces**, **Fisherfaces**, or **deep learning models (CNNs)** help in feature encoding.

### 4. **Face Detection and Recognition**

* **Face detection** locates the face in the image using algorithms like **Haar Cascades** or **DNNs (Deep Neural Networks)**.
* **Recognition** matches the extracted features against stored templates in the system.

### 5. **Template Matching / Classification**

* Uses **distance metrics** (e.g., Euclidean distance, cosine similarity) or **neural network classifiers** to compare the real-time face with stored data.
* If the match score exceeds a threshold, access is granted.

### 6. **Decision Making**

* Based on the comparison score, the system decides whether to unlock or deny access.
* This step uses **digital logic systems** for binary decision making (unlock = 1, deny = 0).

### 7. **Storage and Database Management**

* The face data is securely stored in encrypted format using **digital memory** (like flash storage) and managed using **database systems**.

---

## 🧩 **Main Components of a Face Lock System**

| Component                       | Description                                                                                                     |
| ------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Camera Module**               | Captures the user's face image. Often infrared cameras are used in advanced systems for night vision and depth. |
| **Microcontroller / Processor** | Processes image data and runs recognition algorithms. May use dedicated AI chips (like Apple’s Neural Engine).  |
| **Memory / Storage**            | Stores the face templates and system software. Can include RAM and flash storage.                               |
| **Software Algorithms**         | Handle face detection, feature extraction, and matching.                                                        |
| **Display/Interface**           | Gives user feedback (e.g., “Face Not Recognized” or “Unlocked”).                                                |
| **Security System**             | Encrypts the data and protects against spoofing (e.g., liveness detection to avoid using photos).               |

---

## 🧠 Technologies Used

* **Computer Vision**
* **Machine Learning / Deep Learning (CNNs, FaceNet, etc.)**
* **Cryptography** (for data protection)
* **Embedded Systems** (microcontrollers, SoCs)
* **Digital Signal Processing (DSP)**

---

## 🛡️ Security Features

* **Liveness Detection** – Detects if it’s a real human face or a photo/video.
* **Anti-spoofing Techniques** – IR sensing, 3D mapping, blinking detection.
* **Secure Enclave** – Stores biometric data in an isolated, encrypted area.

---

If you're building a prototype or need help with the circuit or code behind a face lock system, I can guide you step-by-step.
03/06/25, 20:08 - Megha Sherino: Here's a detailed explanation of the **digital system concepts and components** behind the **auto screen lock feature** used in smartphones:

---

## 📱 What is Auto Screen Lock?

The **auto screen lock** feature automatically locks the smartphone screen after a period of **inactivity** to save battery and enhance security. It uses sensors, timers, and logic systems to detect when the user is no longer actively using the device.

---

## 🔧 **Digital System Concepts Involved in Auto Screen Lock**

### 1. **Timer and Inactivity Monitoring**

* The system starts a **software timer** when no touch or activity is detected.
* This timer is implemented using digital counters in the OS.

### 2. **Input Signal Detection**

* The touchscreen or other sensors (like accelerometer or gyroscope) send **digital signals** indicating user interaction.
* No signal = user inactivity.

### 3. **Interrupt Handling**

* User actions like taps or swipes generate **hardware or software interrupts** to reset the timer.
* If no interrupt is received for a set time, the system triggers screen lock.

### 4. **Logic Control**

* Uses **digital logic** (like IF-THEN conditions) to decide when to lock the screen.
* Example logic:

  ```
  IF (no input AND timer ≥ set limit) THEN lock screen
  ```

### 5. **Power Management**

* The auto-lock mechanism is part of the **power management system**, reducing screen brightness and turning off display hardware after inactivity.

### 6. **Security System Integration**

* When the screen locks, the system may activate **biometric authentication** (face lock, fingerprint) or **PIN/password** on next use.
* This ensures the phone is protected after inactivity.

---

## 🧩 **Main Components of Auto Screen Lock System**

| Component                                         | Description                                                                |
| ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Touchscreen Controller**                        | Detects touch input and sends digital signals to the processor.            |
| **Sensors (Accelerometer, Gyroscope, Proximity)** | Detect motion or position changes; help determine user activity.           |
| **Processor / SoC**                               | Runs the timer, logic, and controls the lock event.                        |
| **Timer (Software)**                              | Tracks time since last user interaction. Implemented using internal clock. |
| **Display Driver Circuit**                        | Turns off or dims the screen based on commands.                            |
| **Operating System**                              | Manages the timer settings, logic, and triggers the lock screen UI.        |

---

## 🛠️ Technologies Used

* **Embedded Systems**: Microprocessor/microcontroller in the phone controls logic and timers.
* **Digital Electronics**: Logic gates, counters, interrupts.
* **Software Algorithms**: Implement timer, activity detection, and locking mechanism.
* **Sensor Fusion**: Combines data from multiple sensors to improve accuracy.

---

## ⚙️ User Customization Options

* **Time Duration** (e.g., 15 sec, 30 sec, 1 min)
* **Smart Lock Features** (e.g., don’t lock when in pocket, connected to a trusted device, or face is visible)
* **Power Saving Mode Integration** – Reduces lock time to save battery.

---

## 🔐 Security Features

* Prevents unauthorized access after period of inactivity.
* Often triggers **biometric re-authentication**.
* Integrated with **secure OS layers** to prevent bypassing.

---

If you're interested in how to **program** an auto-lock system for a device using Arduino or Android development, I can show you a code or circuit example too.

May the Spirit of Jesus, guide, counsel, protect and preserve us as we make each step in work for God’s Kingdom. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
06/06/25, 13:44 - Megha Sherino: Reached Kengeri bus stop
06/06/25, 13:47 - Megha Sherino: Boarded 378
06/06/25, 14:34 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): https://www.facebook.com/share/v/16MjmeCVJQ/
07/06/25, 09:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For through the Law, I died to the law, so that I might live to God’ (Galatians 2:19)

St Paul was the apostle of Jesus, to whom the Divine mysteries were made known through revelation. He was highly learned in his religion, one who had attained extensive knowledge of the laws from Gamaliel, a doctor in Jewish laws, and above all he was a Pharisee by birth. In God’s plan for his Salvation, it was through the understanding of the laws, through the myriad experiences he had to encounter in life, was he brought to faith in the Lord Jesus. After having received enormous revelations while in prayer in the wilderness of Arabia for three years, Paul was fully convinced that all that he was fortunate enough to learn from his childhood (the Mosaic Laws) found their consummation in Jesus Christ. Before the Most High name of Jesus, every other thing seemed meaningless, worthless, and futile. He was ready to abandon everything for the sake of the Lord and to be considered righteous before Him. To a world that is so consumed in itself, which is on the lookout for novelty, that derides the weak, the fallen, and the ostracized; what could be more daring than Paul’s perception and lifestyle as a witness to Christ? You and I are called to die to some of our die-hard convictions, experiences, learnings, and bend of mind, to embrace Jesus our Lord, to enhance our trust in His unfailing love for us. May God’s Spirit induce in us the fuel of conviction to live by faith and not by sight. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
08/06/25, 07:54 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I saw the glory of the God of Israel coming from the east. His voice was like the roar of rushing waters, and the land was radiant with His glory.’ (Ezekiel 43:2)

The prophet Ezekiel was shown God’s Glory that came down into the land, a land which was till then desolate (Babylonian exile and destruction of the Temple). It was not without signs that God’s glory filled the land – with the sound of many waters and a light that radiated throughout the land. To the prophet was revealed the restoration of the lost glory of the people – God in His amazing mercy revived His people, gathered them from all the places to which they were strewn away, and promised them a greater future. It was this Glory of God, His Spirit that changed the plight of these grief-stricken, distraught people, who had ‘absolutely’ no hope for a comeback. The abundance of God’s grace emerged from the reconstructed temple, the place of worship of God. Similar instances of God’s glory replenishing His people were revealed to prophets Isaiah and Jeremiah, who urged the people to pray for this upcoming event to take place in their lives. In the New Testament of the Bible, we come across (a similar situation in the life of) the disciples who were plunged into darkness, unable to see the light on the other side. These were encouraged to pray in unison for power from above, the promise of the Father (His glory) to overshadow their lives and future to give rise to the next big event in history – And this was the Pentecost. As we celebrate this day of God’s glory overpowering the apostles, of overturning the course of history inaugurating the Church, and the miraculous lives of the early witnesses of Jesus, it’s our turn today, to call upon the Lord, to shower upon us the power of His Spirit, the promise of His Father, that all mankind shall be filled with glory and power, to see the great change we envision for ourselves, our families, societies and the world at large. Come Holy Spirit come, Fill the hearts of your people, Ignite in us the flame of Christ’s love. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

*2. Recently, former Foreign Minister of which country Annalena Baerbock has been elected as the next President of the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA)?*

08/06/25, 20:05 - Megha Sherino: https://youtu.be/IkHgxKemCRk?si=bOeWVVmzwTsXr9Nk
09/06/25, 09:08 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And the Lord has declared today that you are a people for His treasured possession, as He has promised you, that you are to keep all His commandments, and He will set you in praise and in fame and in honor high above all nations that He has made, and that you shall be a people holy to the Lord your God, as He has promised’ (Deuteronomy 26:18,19)

To partake in His glory, the Lord calls you and me today. We were chosen before the foundations of the earth were established (in Jesus), that we may inherit this privilege of Divine sonship and be raised in fame, honor, and praise above all the nations that were created. Now if You and I were to exercise this inheritance of ours, conditions apply – we must be submissive and willing to obey every Word that comes from God. Jesus asks us to come to Him, we who carry our burdens, and are weary; that we may find the console and comfort to get going in our journey. His promises are assured, but there are periods of trials, dark situations, and chaos that we may have to encounter as we walk with Him. He permits these in our lives so that the most needed cleansing, purification, and sanctity of our souls are accomplished. Moses was led through 40 years of wilderness experience (rearing the sheep of his father-in-law) to sanctify him, to be transformed as the most capable leader for the people of God (trustworthy and meek before men and God). Joseph (son of Jacob, of the Old Testament) was deprived of the comfort and secureness of his home and family, and forced to slavery for years, while the rest of his brothers (even those who ditched him) enjoyed the pleasures of a secure life with their father. To participate in the glory that comes from above is costly. And whoever loses his life to obtain this will surely be rewarded many times more here and with a hundred-fold in Heaven. Let us plead to the Holy Spirit to help us in today’s journey to obey God’s will, His Word, and to walk before Him blameless. We admit Lord, that not by power or might will this be attainable, but by the Grace and love that flows into us from your Sacred heart. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
09/06/25, 10:56 - Megha Sherino: Amen
09/06/25, 19:05 - Megha Sherino: Are you free amma?

10/06/25, 05:15 - You pinned a message
10/06/25, 09:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘To this end we always pray for you, that our God may make you worthy of his calling and may fulfill every resolve for good and every work of faith by his power’ (2 Thessalonians 1:11)

You and I have been called to toil in His Kingdom, to expand the boundaries of God’s kingdom through our maturing in righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit. Jesus thus said that this Kingdom is in our midst, not something that is superficial and will be experienced in the other world. The Bible tells us that because God is with us, none can stand against us. All decisions of ours taken with good intent, the works of our hands done according to His Word, will surely see their fruit in due course of time. It is the Lord who works for you and me to will and do according to His good purpose. This power of His that works invisibly for us, behind our efforts, to ensure that all that we do get counted, is His grace. Even those efforts of ours that seemed to us like chasing the wind, the difficulties and obstacles we undertook to get things moving ahead for us and others, were His works on our behalf, that we might know and give glory to His holy name. The sum and substance of all these would constitute our works for His kingdom – our prayers in silence, prayers for others, counsel for the good of others, relentless toil for securing the lives of many, our soothing words that encouraged some who were in the dark, the smiles we exhibited (that lighted up minds) even with all the burdens weighing us down within…… it was for these that His mighty Hands worked for us. And for these alone, we would be acclaimed worthy before the Most High, may not be before men. God who reads the hearts of men will give each one of us our due reward, and even more, because of His love and faithfulness for us. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

11/06/25, 08:48 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘…………..in order to make sure that I was not running or had not run in vain’ (Galatians 2:2)

Paul emphasized all through his missionary journeys (in all the places he proclaimed the Gospel) the mystery of Salvation in Jesus Christ open to all men by the grace of God. Not from the adherence to the Laws would one receive this redemption and forgiveness of sins, but from faith in Jesus Christ and in acknowledging what He obtained for all mankind through His sacrifice. This acceptance of Jesus would lead one to repentance (awareness of his unworthiness), the prerequisite for ‘righteousness’ before God. Therefore, it is not in ‘what’ one does, or ‘aspires’ to do which he considers is good that makes one justifiable before the Most High, to embody oneself with grace upon grace. It is the favor of God, His delight in one, that makes one ‘blessed’. Jesus had taught His disciples of what was most pleasing before the Father – to believe in the one whom He has sent. Paul, who was called to be the Apostle for the Gentiles, was moved by this Spirit of God which motivated him to do all things for God’s greater glory. Not that he needed the endorsement of Peter, James and John who were considered ‘the pillars’ of authority in the Church, but he made sure that nowhere he would fail the delight of the Lord. It was the delight of the Lord that would fulfill all his plans, that would make his missionary journeys a success, that would bring people to the knowledge of the truth of the Gospel. Therefore, like Paul, let us also ensure that we in no means hurt the Holy Spirit, the Spirit of Jesus, the Spirit of truth, love, and harmony in our everyday chores and responsibilities in life, to come out fruitful a hundredfold. Amen

12/06/25, 09:49 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Yet to this day, whenever Moses is read a veil lies over their hearts. But when one turns to the Lord, the veil is removed. Now the Lord is the Spirit and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom’ (2 Corinthians 3:15-17)

It is through Christ Jesus that we have been assured a permanent glory – a life in the Spirit of Jesus. A life of freedom, driven by a hope that gives us the boldness to approach God and seek support from Him, whenever strangled by life’s inadequacies and insufficiencies. It is the Spirit of Jesus that fills us with His abundance when we call upon the name of Jesus. Paul exclaimed that He was called to minister to this works of the Spirit and not to the Mosaic Law that was fading in its relevance and impact with the advent of the Lord. Therefore, we are exhorted by the Lord to a higher state of living, to be led by the Spirit to walk that extra mile. And that if our virtues go no deeper than that of the Scribes and the Pharisees, we would never get to where the Lord has called us to be with Him. Not by our human sufficiencies, do we equip ourselves to do excellent works of charity, but by the inspiration, urge, and promptings that the Spirit of the Lord gives us each moment in time. Now none can claim to have already met this expectation of the Lord for us, but we will with the power of the Spirit of God in us, endeavor to do all that we are told to this day, for the sake of the love of the Lord and for all that He has completed for our redemption. Lord help us to dwell in freedom, we who behold your glory, to be enabled to be transformed into your image one step ahead each day. We admit that this is your will and that we belong to you alone, Lord. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
13/06/25, 10:06 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘If I have prophetic powers and understand all mysteries and all knowledge and if I have all faith, so as to move mountains, but have not love, I am nothing’ (1 Corinthians 13:2)

Paul knew that the circumstances he dwelt in made him at times arrogant, rude, to insist on his own way, he was irritable at times, and even more resentful. Considering the kind of suffering, the persecution he had to put up with, the apostle accepted that he had to first condone (himself) before the Lord, wholeheartedly accept that he was the first among sinners. And whenever he did so, the Spirit of the Lord - the Spirit of freedom, would enlighten him to experience the immeasurable greatness of God’s power at work in him, that coerced him to oblige to the Commandment of love. For us too, to oblige to our Divine call – to love is never going to be easy. (No doubt) to those who love us, who are our well-wishers, even to those who may be strangers to us, it may be quite easy to exhibit love and kindness; But what about the ones who knowingly torment us, those who are our dear and near, those who speak ill about us despite our innocence (the envious), those who are deceit filled, those who connive with the injustice we have to bear with……the list can be even longer, what we may in our human nature do is the least to ignore them. The Lord challenges us by asking us not to approach them for reconciliation, but to make a first attempt towards loving them – by praying for them. No human act or initiative can change the heart of another, without the touch of the Holy Spirit. Let us therefore seek the intervention of this Spirit of truth in us, to be able to ardently pray for those who oppose us, who misunderstand us, who disturb our sense of peace because of their erroneous perception and guile. The will of the Heavenly Father is that we all need to be saved, and so You and I are called to just plead for this grace to love and pray for all. WE ask this grace for all whom His Spirit reminds us now, that they may also be filled with power, strength, and wisdom to discern the true way – the way of Jesus – to love to the end. Amen

15/06/25, 07:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: ‘I am the Lord your God, who teaches you to profit, who leads you in the way you should go’ (Isaiah 48:17)

It is the Lord who whispers to our ears, imprints in our hearts this message of truth – that You and I have been all these days carved in the Mighty Hands of the Lord. He who knows our boundaries, our weaknesses, our limits, set us on the broad path that we may smoothly tread our journey in our predestined paths. There might have been decisive junctions, those confusing mazes in everyone’s life, but it was the Lord who brought us to these crossroads, so that we might relent and seek His advise of where and how. It was He who trained us, equipped us, and facilitated the changes we direly needed in life to transform into the one we were called to be. Even those times when we believed all was lost, He opened new paths for our revival, and because of His great compassion for us, we moved on through turbulent seas and fires unaffected. Moses the Leader of the Old Testament perceived God’s power and unfailing love in the burning bush on Mount Horeb. Joshua heard God’s words that strengthened Him to win every battle for the people and lead them to the promised land. And today the Lord speaks to you and me, wishing to console, comfort, and elevate us from the strongholds of the enemy that has confined us, to look on Him alone, the pioneer of our faith, and to make a bold decision ahead in life. The assurance He gives is our past successes, and that we are called to triumph over all that poses an obstacle in our present by the power of His Holy name. May the Spirit of Jesus prompt us to action through faith. Amen

17/06/25, 09:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Do not even the tax collectors do the same?’ (Matthew 5:46)

If it were in our times that the Lord had spoken this principle, He would have substituted ‘the unjust’ or ‘oppressors’ for ‘tax collectors. To the people of Israel (God’s people) they were subject to unjust deprivation/impoverishment because of these officials called the Tax collectors; Ironically, many of these were from among the Jewish community but clung to their jobs owing to the lucrative salary and perks they were offered by the Romans. We saw around us many – in our families, in our communities, parishes, Churches – those who knowingly afflict us, oppress us, demoralize us, who deal with us in gross injustice, who live to satiate their interests, convictions, and beliefs with the slightest of regard for the existence of others…….The Lord today points to each one of these kind around us, whom we at times avoid owing to it being hard for us to deal them, and again to be able to speak to them considerately would never be possible (with all our frailties, suppressed/denied memories of the past, the burdens we ourselves carry in life). Jesus calls us not to justify their errs, or mistakes, but to pray for them with a heart that He gives us today, that the eyes of their hearts may be moved by His Spirit, that brings repentance in men. Lord, with all that we are, and what we do, we are in-equipped to take on this task, you have entrusted us; but trusting in your support, your empowerment, and the pouring of your love into our feeble selves, we wish to make a try to be like you, perfect like our Heavenly Father. Be with us today, Jesus. Amen

18/06/25, 09:31 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And this is the will of Him who sent me, that I should lose nothing of all that He has given me, but raise it up on the last day’ (John 6:39)

Jesus knew the will of His Father – that none who was given to Him would perish, but each of these would see the Light of Life and would be raised for Eternity. He continues this saving mission on earth even today through You and me. The Lord has given to each of us those whom He wills to be saved, and redeemed for Eternity. He has chosen us before the foundations of this world, to be blameless in love before Him, and to fulfill His mission through our lives. Who are those the Lord has entrusted to us to be saved? To those who have been called to a familial vocation – each of their family members; To those whom He has called to share in His Priesthood – all those who have been territorially entrusted to their care and nourishment; To those who have been called to share in religious life – all those who have been given to them (those in their immediate surroundings and even those who may be at a distance whom the Lord reminds to be consecrated for His glory). Now the question that will be a sure shot to you and me on that Great day of judgment would be – I was hungry, did you feed me? I was thirsty, did you help quench my thirst? I was naked, did you help clothe me? I was deserted, a prisoner, abandoned, and set to the margins in society, did you consider me? I was homeless, did you perceive me as needing support?... With all the graces He has gifted us, in all that we have been materially and spiritually filled with, have we ever thought it primordial to turn to the Lord, asking Him of what He needed of us (at the moment) to be faithful to our call? Lord Jesus, I may not have been successful in catering to my vocation with the determination and zeal, You may have expected of me, but I ask you to help me walk with you today. I wish to ensure that none whom you have decided to bless, may fail to receive grace (You) because of my shortcomings and frail human nature. Jesus, Be my strength, I trust in You! Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
20/06/25, 09:04 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I have much more to tell you, but you cannot bear it now’ (John 16:12)

Jesus’ disciples were with Him for almost 3 years – they were those who lived with Him, saw and heard Him do the deeds of power and miracles, those who learned to pray from Him, and above all who experienced the love of God more than any other. Yet when it was time for Jesus to bid farewell to them, (knowing that He was to complete His vocation and return to the Father), what pained Him the most was that His disciples were not equipped to fulfill their call. They were chosen by Him (as the Father chose them first and gave them to Him), but as far as the principles of God’s kingdom were concerned, they were like infants/ toddlers, trying to make sense of what was told to them. Thus Jesus opened up stating that there was much more He wanted them to be aware of, which at present they would not be able to comprehend, even if He spoke to them. If these disciples who saw, heard, and lived with the Lord found it hard to assimilate the truths of His kingdom, how hard would it be for us, who live by faith? This we must accept that God is God and we are mere mortals (our dimensions are totally incompatible), if so what we know today is just the tip of the iceberg; what has been imbibed in our hearts by the Father, Son, and the Holy Spirit, as a gift by faith. There is much more the Lord wishes that we must know. But, for this, we need a heart in perfect disposition to receive what He has in store for us. And this is made ready each day when we read His Word, frequent the gifts of grace – the Sacraments, and resolve to attempt to do whatever He asks us to do. None can claim to have mastered the secrets of Heaven, however brilliant, tactful, and competent (as approved by this world’s benchmarks), but we can ask the Lord to open up these secrets to us through our efforts of prayer, worship, and listening to His Word by the power of His Spirit in us. Remember, the Lord loves to teach us more.Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

22/06/25, 08:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Why will you still be struck down? Why will you continue to rebel? The whole head is sick, and the whole heart faint…….’ (Isaiah 1:5)

The reason why these were struck down and ‘were’ subject to oppression and distress was this, as Isaiah told the people – their sins and trespasses. They refused to accept the Word of God, to modify themselves according to the tenets of God’s Law, instead, they persisted in their ways. They were unjust, oppressors of the ill-fated – the widows and fatherless, rebelled against God’s ways by doing what was evil in His sight. And thus, the consequence was them being tormented by the evil they did. The head (which devices plans for a better future), was rendered invalid to reason out for the good. The whole heart which was to reflect God’s love and care, turned cold and incapable. This was not a prophesying made out (merely) to the People of God who lived as contemporaries to Isaiah, but to each one of us today. When we fail to understand what God wills of us, what it is to be humane? What our purpose of living on earth would mean? What are our roles and responsibilities as per our vocation? When we miserably default in love while executing our tasks ( or take things and people just for granted)……Let us look into ourselves this Sunday, asking the Lord to help us perceive the areas of life where we need healing, where a transformation of heart is impending, the people whom we have failed to love, and for all the missed opportunities in life to live the call we have been chosen by the Lord with much expectation. For it is not the approval of men that could change our lives, but the countenance of the Lord that could work wonders in this world. Wishing you a blessed Sunday with Jesus. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
24/06/25, 08:21 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Now the Word of the Lord came to me, saying,

Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, and before you were born I consecrated you; I appointed you a prophet to the nations’ (Jeremiah 1:4,5)

You and I are inevitable to this world, and more so to the Lord, since we have been devised for a unique purpose, that ‘which’ none other can bring to completion. Our paths may never be the same, but we eventually converge when it is time to leave our earthly abode. Even the challenges we may encounter in this journey called ‘life’, may seem strange to another. To get caught in the delirium of anxiety or ‘worries’, is just so natural – in a world where uncertainties rule the roost, and when faced with the reality of a society that has forgotten to love. One may at times be forced to ‘evaluate’ one’s strengths and threats when it comes to taking on anything new. Thus, more than ever, to make wrong decisions, or tread through harder realities in life becomes possible even to the wisest among men. And when we realize that there have been flaws in our thinking, reasoning out, or in our decisions in life, we get hurt, discouraged, and easily reminded of our vulnerabilities. Here, the Lord speaks to us the same words He told Jeremiah, the Word that strengthened Jeremiah’s resolve to vehemently undertake His mission, with hardly any support from around. Yes, it is the Lord who has consecrated you and me to take on all the tasks given to our care, this is our courage that whatever we embark upon today, He will cause to completion, betterment, and progress. As we stand before Him in prayer, Jesus tells us that He knows us, ‘every little bit’ of us, our strengths, our failures, and that we would not make it without His encouragement and strength. Be our strength today, Lord. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

25/06/25, 09:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Therefore, my Beloved, as you have always obeyed, so now, not only as in my presence but much more in my absence, work out your own Salvation with fear and trembling’ (Philippians 2:12)

There are times in life when we wished we had someone to turn back to, just for an ‘assurance’, one who could give us a pat on the back, who could counsel and guide us, understanding the path we are called to take on, with its specifics – the usual ups and downs in the journey. Paul who had deep love towards the Philippian believers in Christ, was trying to do just this to them. It was a time when he was behind bars in Rome (probably), but his concern for these Churches who went through untold persecution and difficulties to put up with their faith, kept him agile and available to them through the letters he wrote to them. All he did was to try to enkindle in them the growth in the fruits of the Holy Spirit – strengthening faith, hope, and love in them. The apostle knew that it was not his efforts alone that brought them to the Lord, but the sincerity and dedication these did towards earning their Salvation. The love for Jesus, sprung from their obedience to God’s Word, to the traditions of the Early Church acquainted to them by St Paul and by the constant work of God’s Spirit in them. Today’s context and verse from the Bible enlightens us to build our faith through obedience to God’s Word and continuous efforts to sharpen ourselves to be tuned to God’s will. There is one other direction, or stipulation that the Spirit of the Lord intends to see fulfilment in our lives – to be the cause of assurance of another in faith, love, and hope in this world. In a world that counts on technology, money, and words of influencers, we need to be the Influencers for the Lord to all those who may find it hard to move ahead in life. Paul worked hard and prayed for these disciples of Philippi, likewise we are called to pray and be the way(His Way), the light, and cause of hope for many around us. God bless you. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
26/06/25, 09:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But I ………could not address you as spiritual people, but as people of the flesh, as infants in Christ’ (1 Corinthians 3:1)

This was what St Paul spoke of the Corinthians believers in Christ, those who were renowned for their growth in the Holy Spirit, those who exercised the many charisms and gifts of the Spirit. Despite the fact they were an anointed community, there were blemishes and sins they struggled with, which delayed their growth towards Christ Jesus. The works of the flesh, as Paul called them; these sins were jealousy, strife, disunity, sectarianism, and hypocrisy, apart from graver sins of licentiousness, adultery, and sexual disorientation. When one grows into a deeper love for the Lord, His grace supports a genuine conversion in this one, giving him the strength to do the impossible, to outwit the works of the flesh, through the power of God’s Spirit. It is this what Paul advised that these would need - to be led by the Holy Spirit, to be filled with this Spirit, by grounding himself in the Word of God, to earnestly desire for more superior gifts of God’s Spirit, which is love. Unless they were inspired, driven, and overtaken by this love, they were still infants (unable to ingest higher nutrients), and in spiritual terms called the people of the flesh. Now to you and me, who desire to be with the Lord, do we strive to attain that higher stage of spirituality by reading God’s Word, or are we like any other, the people of the flesh (who live by what pleases, and aggrandizes the flesh)? Jesus asks us to look into ourselves, into who we ‘really’ are – our identity today; whether we have taken a step ahead to walk towards our true identity (whom we have been called in Christ to be) – to love? Let us pray for this grace today; to be adorned by superior gifts of the Holy Spirit. For this, we need to admit our inability, and that we have failed miserably many times. Jesus will work on our behalf towards this, because He loved us so much. Amen

‘But God shows His love for us in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us. Since therefore we have now been justified by His blood, much more shall we be saved by Him from the wrath of God’ (Romans 5:8,9)
The love of God was made known to us, that though we were unworthy, Christ died for us. His blood has rendered us righteous, saved from the wrath of God that is to fall on the ungodly. It is this love of God that instigates in us the hope each day, that God’s glory will shroud over all that is dark in our lives today. We have been called to imitate the Lord, following His footsteps, taking on the way He has shown us; for He is our pioneer of faith, the one alone whom we are to trust and focus on. He has promised us that He will never fail or forsake us, though circumstances may take a twist for the adverse, though those dear and near to our hearts may relinquish, or give up on us, though the shadows around may seem intimidating day by day…….His heart still throbs and burns for us. The unfailing Light of His presence in us would sustain us through, It is He who has permitted these twists and turns in life, some of which (we) have no clue of how to make or break. All that have been egregiously alarming to us, all that have rattled us to the very core, those that have turned us outrageous and discouraged were like those thorns that His heart withstood and prevailed over. Every skirmish, bruise, and wound He bore on His body ‘was’ for us, and for our Salvation, so that we would gain the courage of conviction to win over every wound inflicted by the Evil, by perseverance like Him. Today is the day when His love is venerated and glorified, above all, the love that changed the course of this world, the same love which will transform ours too. Let us ask the Lord Jesus to grace us with some of the virtues that overflowed from His Sacred Heart – faithfulness, compassion, forgiving love, and silence. Wishing you a blessed day!

‘Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved, a worker who has no need to be ashamed, rightly handling the Word of truth’ (2 Timothy 2:15)

Let us thus thank the Heavenly Father for considering us worthy to be with Him in the Heavenly places because of His Son, Jesus Christ. Amen

30/06/25, 09:46 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘When she heard about Jesus, she came up behind Him in the crowd and touched His cloak, because she thought If I just touch His clothes I will be healed’ (Mark 5:27,28)

This sick woman was healed - the one who believed that if she had just touched the fringes of His clothes, she would be healed. Her inner resolve to brave all odds, to pass through the unruly crowds, and to seek the remedy for her illness (which had chained her for twelve long years), was never deterred by the norms of society – societal stigma, the reproach of people, shame and guilt that were derivatives of her wounded personality, helplessness of being a woman or any other. She knew that there wasn’t a way ahead for her, as she was impoverished (having spent all her earnings on the treatment of her ailment), and even though she thrived within a society that was too cold to understand/consider her, she was not numbed, because of the light of faith in God that kept burning within her, giving her the hope for a revival. And to prove her right beyond any other, this story of the healing of the woman who suffered from haemorrhage is repeatedly narrated in all three Synoptic Gospels. To open our eyes to the fact that even in the most unchangeable situations of life, it is Jesus ‘alone’ who can make a breakthrough, ‘a twist’ for the better, one may never have thought about. Therefore, however distraught and dismaying things/people around us may appear, we who trust in the Lord, have the hope of amazing grace over-shadowing us all through life. The faith as slight as a mustard seed is all that we need to express and proclaim for the Lord to take control. Amen

01/07/25, 10:48 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Angels urged Lot……..but he hesitated, so the men seized him and his wife and his two daughters by the hand, the Lord being merciful to him, and they brought him out and set him outside the city’ (Genesis 19:15-16)

The Lord was merciful to them and thus the two angels led Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand safely out of the city of punishment. They were hesitant and lingered for, to their eyes the massive wealth, property, and security of a home (at Sodom, the city that God was about to punish) was what meant everything to them. We know the context of Abraham interceding for his nephew Lot, and for the land before God, when God made known to him, His plan to destroy the city on account of the sinfulness of the people. For us to meditate upon this morning is that we too like Lot have credited the mercy of God, not because we were worthy of it, but because of Jesus and for the ransom He paid for us, to set us free from the wrath of God that was to fall upon all mankind. Therefore, having understood of God’s magnanimity, and graciousness in Jesus Christ, we are obliged like Lot (who was asked to flee from the city to another land) to live lives worthy of God’s will and to seek what pleases Him the most. Let us ask the Lord Jesus, to grace us with the gifts of His Spirit – wisdom, counsel, knowledge, understanding, fear of God, fortitude, and piety, that we may do the little we can today for God’s kingdom, to share the joy, peace, and forgiveness of the Lord to those given to us, and to be humble to reap the fruits of His favour for us. Amen

02/07/25, 08:22 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, laughing. So she said ‘Cast out this slave woman with her son, for the son of the slave woman shall not be heir with my son, Isaac’ (Genesis 21:9-10)

To this, St Paul once explained, ‘What fellowship has light with darkness?’ What he meant was exactly what Sarah expressed through her protest against ‘the demeanor’ of the son of the slave woman. What the Spirit of the Lord tells us today is this – there is a difference between the ways of the world and the ways of the Lord; what the Divine Law has imposed on us can never be overridden by any law – of the land, of society, of any other human institution. This applies to every aspect of our life, be it the family, be it the society we are placed to be part of, or any community. What errs with the law of love can never be compromised to serve any interest – be it individual or common or however highly significant. Taking the case of the family, what God has designed needs to be preserved – in a marital relationship, it is the good of the other spouse, that would take precedence to any other societal norm. Now if we were to turn to society – it is the integrity of each individual and family that needs to rescind any other societal obligation. If we were to take a country, it is the rule of liberty, equality, and fraternity – the realm of Divine law, that needs to counter any other revolutionary idea or ideal. In the Book of Genesis, Sarah was convinced that the child of the Promise would not prevail if he were to dwell with the child of the flesh. What diverges from the natural law, what counters with morals, can never be compromised to serve human survival and existence. This is what we need to enrich our upcoming generations with that - in whatever we ‘say’, or do, it is God who needs to be magnified, more than any other. Amen

03/07/25, 10:06 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘So then you are no longer strangers and aliens, but you are fellow citizens with the saints and members of the household of God’ (Ephesians 2:19)

This was the message St Thomas, the apostle of Jesus Christ spread as he journeyed from Jerusalem throughout Asia – India, China, and Malacca to proclaim the Gospel. That you and I have become one in Christ Jesus, one into the family of God, with the saints and members of God’s household. Having been adopted into God’s family, we are expected to exhibit the ‘etiquette and elegance’ that characterizes God and His kingdom. Kingdoms of this world have a set of codified rules and traditions (passed on to them from generations) that are obligatory on all members of the royal heritage and which distinguish them from the rest of the crowd. The ways they speak, the words they are not supposed to use, their attire, and even ‘public display of affection’ at times are strictly banned for these members. If members of royal lineages (in this world) are bound to oblige these codes, how much more should we who are members of the Heavenly Kingdom adhere to the commandment of love. We love one another because He (Jesus) first loved us and gave up His life for us. He the Lord has shown the way, and this is our way – to follow Him, to be blameless and holy in love before the Father. St Thomas, our father of faith, has proven His way, and walked the talk, when he once said; ‘Let us also go and die with Him’. Let us imbibe this same courage and strength, that the Spirit of Jesus builds us with to be the beloved dwelling place of God, to co-create with the spirit of love, leaving aside all silly interests unbecoming of the Lord and His kingdom. Amen

04/07/25, 11:04 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice’. For I came not to call the righteous, but sinners’ (Matthew 9:13)

You and I learn to become humane, only when we ‘are able to’ call to mind the magnitude of mercy we have received from above (ever since our birth). It is not that we were destined for fortune or deserved it, but it was the Lord’s doing in giving us the fortunes of a good living. Thus, had it been not for the mercy of the Lord, we would never have been the true selves we are today. God in His mercy, created each one of us, distinct, with unique traits and roles to take on in life. Knowing our frailties and weaknesses, He appointed to our care the Angels – to guard, protect, and guide us through this journey. He sent His only Son, Jesus like one of us, so that we may learn from Him the meaning of what it is to be merciful. To be considerate of the needs of people around us, to be thoughtful of those around us is the kind of change the Lord tried to bring into this world. His mission – the fulfilment of The Law (Divine Law) could never be accomplished (merely) through the strict adherence to the many laws that were already in vogue, or by countering these laws. Jesus went to the downtrodden, the least, the minorities, the destitutes, the stigmatized, the women folk – all those in the fringes who were ignored (till then by society), because He knew that the Divine Law of love encompassed all lives. We see similar progressions in society today through - The Economics of Happiness – Local futures – These state that future well-being leans upon bringing the economy back to human scale by creating self sufficiencies at the local, regional, or neighbourhood level. Nothing can suffice the love, care, and consideration of the local (neighbourhood), even if we were to go by the pretext of equating resources to money. The world is in dire need of this transition – from consumerism to mercifulness – consideration for the other, which would be the panacea to all the psychological, physiological, and economic issues, we are currently grappling with as human beings. The Spirit of the Lord coerces us to go and learn what it means to be merciful. God bless you. Amen

05/07/25, 09:49 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

' And to know the love of Christ that surpasses knowledge, so that you may be filled with the fullness of God's ( Ephesians 3:19)

That love, the highest of all gifts of the Holy Spirit has immense and uncountable powers - to heal, to motivate, to inspire, to ignite, to transform...this was what St Paul meant when he exhorted Christians to strive for excellence, to earn superior charisms. When one is filled with the love of Christ Jesus, he is filled with fullness that comes from above. The perfection of God is where we are called to reach out to and aspire to grow into. In fact growing in faith is each step we make in knowing Christ's love. In this love, every other desire becomes secondary, losing its charm, and He gives us the drive to stand firm in the wake of adversities. No matter how knowledgeable we are, however prepared, and decisive in our moves, we attain the eligibility to learn the love of the Lord only when we trip, we fall, bruise ourselves - when we lose our confidence, when bright hopes seem mere dreams, and when we accept there is nothing we can do for an upheaval. In the vacuum of our inabilities, in the dark solitude of hard reality, the Lord manifests to us the marvel of who He is to us. Because He knows in all that we know, with all the vanity we possess, we would never harbour the most needed humility that takes to receive Him, the Source of all Creation. Our realm of understanding, our horizons, are 'debilitatingly' small to grasp the unending dimensions/the realm of the Divine. Therefore believe that when things don't seem right, when those arrayed by us just seem to fall, there is a Light, seemingly luminous for us waiting right at the end of the tunnel. All we are asked is to pray for strength and perseverance to make it to this destination. And whatever we do towards this end, will be acknowledged by the Lord in His Book of life, for which we will be rewarded. This is the truth. May the knowledge of the love of Christ be your beacon of hope today and ever. Amen

06/07/25, 12:32 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until He comes’ (1 Corinthians 11:26)

Until His second coming, You and I are exhorted by the Lord to frequent the Holy Eucharist. To partake in the Bread and drink from the cup of the Lord, which gets transubstantiated to His Body and blood in every Mass. And when we participate in this Heavenly banquet, we are reminded of the Lord’s death – the passion and death He endured to elevate us worthy for the Kingdom. This Eternal Sacrifice, when reenacted in time and space in our midst, opens the Heavens – abundance of graces to us. As He ascended to the Heights, people were filled with gifts, as St Paul explains to us in his Epistle to the Ephesians. The question posed to us now is ‘Are we prepared to behold the gifts, the graces the Lord showers on us’ each time we partake in the Banquet of Christ? Are we dressed up in the Heavenly attire He expects to see us in? (washed by His blood and sanctified to be presented to the Father). Do our prayers rise as Incense before the Heavenly Father? Let us ask the Blessed Mother to prepare us each time for the commemoration of Her Son’s sacrifice. Let us seek intercession of the saints to be able to worthily receive the Lord in bread and wine, to accept the immense graces He has bestowed on us. Amen

07/07/25, 08:10 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For we do not want you to be unaware, brothers, of the affliction we experienced in Asia. For we were so utterly burdened beyond our strength that we despaired of life itself……But that was to make us rely not on ourselves but on God who raises the dead’ (2 Corinthians 1:8-9)

The truth is that afflictions, struggles, and pain will occur in everyone’s life. These can play havoc with our otherwise smooth living, curbing the peace and calm within; and can leave us desperately gasping for ‘breath,’ ‘for survival.’ And one day, ‘Out of the blue’, it may seem to us that nothing would ever turn out as organised as it used to be before. You and I may recall several situations in life like this, when we thought all was lost. But we are back here again, even without us knowing that we successfully did make a comeback. Because it was the Lord’s doing for us. It was to teach us that we are not to rely on ourselves, but every time on God who is powerful, who has permitted these to our lives, that we may always ask Him the way out. To be able to stand before Him, when He comes again in glory the second time, to be righteous in His presence then, that He has allowed us pass through these turmoil in life, (we were not responsible for), that we may be refined like silver, like gold in His furnace of sanctification. Also that we may prove the way to many around, who may be reeling in the darkest hours of their night; that all is not lost, because we have a Lord who is master of All. Today’s reminder of the Spirit of Jesus to each one of us is that He is the way, the truth, and the Light for an abundant living here on earth and for Eternity. Amen

08/07/25, 10:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And Jacob was left alone. A man wrestled with him until the breaking of the day. When the man saw that he did not prevail against Jacob, he touched his hip socket, and Jacob’s hip was put out of joint as he wrestled with him. Then he said ‘Let me go, for the day has broken. But Jacob said, I will not let you go unless you bless me’ (Genesis 32:24-26)

Everyone on earth will have to encounter a day like the one Jacob had, when he was left all alone. Having obtained peace with his father-in-law, ‘Laban’ and each take on their way of life; Jacob touches upon the most crucial phase of his life – where he meets with the reality of having to face his brother, Esau, whom he had defied not once but thrice and from whom Jacob had snatched the birth right and blessings. But Jacob was never the ‘old’ Jacob who had run away from his home 20 years ago; he had grown in wealth, in prosperity, in number, and in his trust in God who had been with him all the way. Even then to face the worst was never going to be easy for him, he knew, and more than him, His Heavenly Father knew – so he is comforted by the Angels of the Lord who meet him on the way at Mahanaim. After having sent his family and belongings across the stream of Jabbok, Jacob stays back (thinking) that with the peace and solitude at that place he would be able to fix and set things right before his encounter with Esau. Here, God volunteers to prepare Jacob for his next combat, by bringing before him a wrestler, an invincible one (possibly an angel), with whom Jacob is said to have wrestled all through till the day broke. The wrestler attempts to prevail over Jacob by striking and dislocating his hip joint; and then pretends to take leave from him. Jacob holds on to him, asking for a blessing before he left. This blessing Jacob sought from the Lord, equipped him to seek favour in the sight of his brother, ‘Esau’ and to be the blessing (God intended of him) for all generations. Nothing in life comes without a price. All the pain, sacrifices, and efforts one has invested in life are counted by the Most High. Even when unrecognized by the people around us, in the most insignificant moments in life, however obscure, believe that with the Lord at our side, we are being prepared for the incredible. Let us learn to grow in trusting the Lord Jesus, always. Amen

6. _Virtual Reality Therapy_: Design VR therapy platforms for mental health treatment, rehabilitation, or stress relief, providing immersive and effective experiences.

7. _Edge AI_: Develop AI-powered edge computing solutions for real-time data processing, analysis, and decision-making in industries like healthcare, finance, or manufacturing.

*Recent Breakthroughs:*

11/07/25, 10:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Do not repay evil for evil or reviling for reviling, but on the contrary, bless, for to this you were called, that you may obtain a blessing’ (1 Peter 3:9)

You and I have been called to obtain the blessing. Not just this, but to pose that blessing to those who knowingly or unknowingly may also obtain it because of us. The ones who unknowingly obtain this blessing are those who are remembered before the Throne of Mercy because of our cries before God. What must have caused us to open up to the Lord could be their misdeeds, provocative behaviour, vices, injustice suffered from them, whatever…But when we lift our voices, seeking justice from the Most High, we must believe, and the Lord has promised us this: that He would intervene for their repentance, the reason for their blessing. And of the one who has brought back the sinner, will not just save his soul (the offender’s), but will also have made a restitution for a multitude of his sins too. It’s quite hard to bring into practice, especially when the offence seems to have occurred from those close to our hearts. The pain/the agony of having suffered deception, or a kind of setback, or of being snubbed by those whom we sincerely loved and cared for, could be the deepest wound in one’s heart. And no amount of soothing talk, human counselling, or medicine can ever bring about a healing so deep as this. Here, the Lord tells us to count each one of these, one by one, offering them to the Immaculate Heart of the Blessed Mother, the one whose heart was pierced and wounded by the sword of condemnation. She will teach us how to let go of the pain, the deep wounds of yester-years, the torments and disdains of a lifetime, that we were never responsible for, for she knew what it meant to suffer for no cause of one’s own. She who bore the Son of God, nourished and grew Him up, only to be led to crucifixion, had the courage and conviction to accept God’s will above any human explanation. Let us Offer ourselves and this day to the Sacred Heart of Jesus and The Immaculate Heart of Mary, may we be renewed, restored and comforted through them. Amen

12/07/25, 08:47 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For I delight in the Law of God, in my inner being, but I see in my members another Law waging war against the Law of my mind and making me captive to the Law of sin that dwells in my members’ (Romans 7:22-23)

It would be irrational if you and I believe that everything on earth can be achieved merely by self-confidence. And this is what today’s Word from the Lord tries to teach us. In every person, there is this constant war between the law in his/her flesh and the law in his/her heart. The former is the law of sin, the ways of this world, which many a times may contradict the commandments and precepts of God. One obtains the courage to comply with the Divine law (however difficult it may seem) only when coerced and led by God’s Spirit. Therefore, we are called to enkindle this power of the Spirit in us each day, by our spiritual rites, customs and practices. The latter help us stay connected with the Lord, to be able to discern His will in life. We are called to envelop ourselves (shield ourselves) in God’s Word, by reading, meditating, and memorizing His Word. It is this Word that works on our behalf in our most desperate times. It is this Word that rejuvenates and reminds us of what we must do, when things just fall apart, when distraught, when all alone in the valley of tears. It is this Word that protects (works for the good of) our children, who hear the Word and may not be able to comprehend it in its fullness. This Word reminds us that perfection may not be attainable at times, but the little we do can be wrapped in love and concern for others. Let us therefore, ground ourselves purposefully in God’s Word, in the Sacraments that pour into our hearts grace, and unite ourselves to the Law of God that dwells in our hearts. This we need to practice, to garner strength to defeat the Law of sin that is working (constantly) to bring our downfall. Lord Jesus, teach me and lead me in your laws and ways that I may (proudly) toil for the building up of your kingdom on earth. Amen

14/07/25, 09:09 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For God has consigned all to disobedience, that He may have mercy on all of us’ (Romans 11:32)

How often have we come across those times! when all was well, and then suddenly, ‘as a bolt from the blue,’ came the untoward. We have tried to reason out on this, either by finding fault with ‘circumstances’, the irrational decisions taken by people around us, or unwise calculations or choices made by us, which had finally brought us up to the hapless situations in life. Whatever be the cause, or even if we know not the cause, Jesus tells us to surrender these to Him, to His Sacred Heart. For even before we knew these would occur in life, God had foreseen them; He knew pretty well how these were to be dealt with, and how to deliver us from their entanglements, provided we reach out to Him. The disobedience of the Jews (decision of their uninformed choice) was what God used to show mercy to us, that we may be saved in Jesus Christ. And considering the ‘wonder ways’ of the Lord, so incomprehensible to the human understanding, every sinfulness, weakness, vulnerability in humans, He transforms - as opportunities for enhanced Grace and mercy. That none can afford to claim glory for his good works; and that none of us can judge another whom we hardly can ever understand. All You and I can do (or are expected to do as mortals) is to trust in the Lord Jesus, offer to Him all the agonizing moments, people, and situations, and ask Him to intervene (on our behalf) with the most optimal solution. His ways are greater than ours, and so are His thoughts. When we see the tip of the ‘iceberg’; He sees the block of ice/the glacier that stands to interrupt our forward movement, let us therefore fall into the hands of the Lord, rather than rely on the comfort zones of this world. Let us learn to trust in His grip than our grasping on to Him. Lord, have mercy on us. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
15/07/25, 08:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘One day, after Moses had grown up, he went out to where his own people were and watched them at their hard labour….’ (Exodus 2:11)

Moses was the greatest among God’s prophets, and superior among all leaders who led the Jews throughout their history, as we understand from the Bible. Tradition speaks that it was this great leader who authored the ‘Pentateuch’, first five Books of the Bible. God gave His commandments to His people, through this man, who trained and taught them how to walk according to them. It was Moses who led the exodus (liberating the enslaved people of God) into the Promised Land, which became their inheritance from the Lord. What perfected this man of God, was of course, his noble birth (a Levite), his upbringing as the son of Pharoah’s daughter, his obedience to the Spirit of God, who led him/opened his mind to understand and sympathize with his own people, who lived in utter hardship. It is this revelation of human suffering, hardships and pain, that opened the eyes of Moses’ heart. Only when our inner eyes are unveiled to see the many challenges, difficulties, and obstacles faced by people around us - either to make a living, to ensure both ends meet, to ensure they are not submerged by the indifference that surrounds them in today’s cold world; then we begin to grow in our spirituality. What faith means is to align with God: in our perception, understanding, our reasoning, which transforms us, makes us more flexible, empathetic to others, and non-judgmental. Here is where leadership (in every domain of society) is called to align towards. Without grass-roots humility, the audacity to learn and unlearn from the present, the growth mindset /experience, none of us can claim to be a good leader, an efficient guide, a counsellor, a good parent, or even an ardent person. Let us ask the Holy Spirit to teach us, reorient us to where we have been called to engage with more love, to give us a humble heart to admit that we make mistakes, and to make restitution without second thoughts, whenever we realize that many souls were (indeed) wounded because of our carelessness. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
