16/07/25, 08:35 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Say to those who have an anxious heart, ‘Be strong; fear not! Behold your God will come with vengeance, with the recompense of God. He will come and save you’ (Isaiah 35:4)

Be strong, fear not! Today’s Word from the Lord reverberates to you. And You and I are called to speak this Word loud and clear before the hearing of all. It is God who saves, God who recompenses us for all that we have gone through to this day. Even if to ‘Others’, our travails, sorrows, and anxieties may seem foundless, to Jesus, it has a deep and profound eventuality. It is not before a mythical perception or concept that we have attached ourselves to, but the ‘person’ Jesus, who is the sum and substance of life for the world. When in doubt, let us ask the Lord to intervene; when our souls are disturbed, let us ask the Lord to send to us His peace; when in the dark, let us ask Jesus to surround us with His Light; when in anxiety, let us ask the Lord to confront us with His recompense and hope. You may be going through endless trials and hindrances for years, but take heart, it was the will of the Lord that you were to be tested and sealed with His mark of acceptance; not because you lacked something that the world attributed to (the reason for your plight), but because you were special in the Hands of the Lord, who wanted you to be brought up to ‘trust’ Him alone. Therefore, tell yourself that the coming of the Lord is so certain, as is the coming of the dawn after the night. The night may have been long, but not to let you give up there, but to teach you to endure, to make you a testimony of endurance for posterity – that all may believe it is good indeed to trust in the Lord always. Amen

17/07/25, 07:13 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘God has not rejected His people whom He foreknew. Do you not know what the Scripture says of Elijah, how he appeals to God against Israel?’ (Romans 11:2)

Paul, who was once a staunch adversary (a persecutor) to the followers of Christ, was transformed into an ardent believer, an ambassador of Christ to the Gentiles. The Apostle quotes his example as ‘first among sinners’ who was called to embrace Salvation, because of God’s mercy and compassion towards him. God is at work in our midst; He never rejects His own; and the reason for this, the apostle gives in his longest Epistle (to the Romans) is that God foreknew each one of us. You and I were created as His masterpieces, to take on our predestined good works through our faith in Jesus Christ. It is this knowing of Christ that is the foundation of our personalities. And we are justified for Salvation only through this faith we uphold (in Jesus). It was faith in God’s works that set Joshua and Caleb apart, to be the only survivors of their generation to inherit the Promised land. It was faith in God’s faithfulness that inspired the Judges to fight the enemies of God’s people and liberate them from their tyranny. It was the faith of King David (love and obedience to God) that established him as the greatest of Kings of Israel – the establisher of a dynasty, and more than anything else - for his descendants to be the remnant of God’s people who were saved by grace. The Holy Spirit teaches us today that God’s works are marvellous, ever since the time of man’s creation, and continues to this day. The prophet Elijah was lost and distraught, having seen his pitiable plight amongst an unfaithful generation. God consoles him that even among these, He has chosen and set apart a few remnants by grace. This is what the Lord comforts you with today – Even if all seems dire and cloudy, God is at work, and He is sure to restore, establish, and perfect you, after a while of suffering (now that you are undergoing) as you continue your trust in Him and plead for mercy. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

18/07/25, 09:52 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Pay to all what is owed to them: taxes to whom taxes are owed, revenue to whom revenue is owed, respect to whom respect is owed, honour to whom honour is owed’ (Romans 13:7)

The sum of Jesus’s teachings – the Gospel outlines the essentials that contribute towards peaceful living on earth. There will be trials and tribulations in this life on earth, but the Lord has assured us His peace, because He has overcome them all. This peace stems from the practice of virtues of justice, mercy, and love of God, all founded on the underlying commandment ‘to love one another’. Jesus was once questioned by the Pharisees. On the legality of paying taxes to the Roman authorities, whether He supported this or not? His response was not one of disrespect/contempt for the authorities (political, religious, and social), but one of obedience, submission, and affirmation to authority (by which He clearly spelt out to His spectators that all authority was from above). Paul in his letter to the Romans, expands the horizons of this teaching of the Lord by encompassing several dimensions to it – of honour, respect and love. The voice of the Holy Spirit as is dictated to us by Paul, is to ‘pay’ to all what is owed to them. Let us first examine whether we are aware of all those to whom we owe something, whom we are supposed to pay to, and what they deserve from us? For this let us ask the Holy Spirit to enlighten our minds and hearts to see all those who deserve our honour, our respect, our consideration, our love…….It is then that we are informed of the good deeds that Jesus expects of us to do to them. Not what our intellect or reasoning may incline us to think, but what the Lord has to say to us, we are called to obey. Lord, guide and inspire us to follow your precepts and teachings (to be worthy) to receive all the promises attached to them. In Jesus name, we pray. Amen

19/07/25, 09:46 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The one who speaks on his own authority seeks his own glory; but the one who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and in him there is no falsehood’ (John 7:18)

The Jews who were astounded at Jesus’s teaching (content and profundity) continued to doubt and question Jesus. To them, He was merely the son of a carpenter; and how could it be that such great knowledge came from Him, that was incomparable to the words of their Rabbis? This was what left them perplexed. Rather than approaching Him (leaving away their prejudices) and trying to learn from Him, they criticized Him, made false allegations against Him, thus increasing their burden of ‘sinning against the Spirit of God’ who worked through Jesus. So Jesus made his point clear that whatever He spoke was in alignment with the will of His father. This applies to us too, who claim to be followers of the Lord, but are fervent in imposing our will ‘always and at all times’ upon others. Do we ever test if our thoughts, perceptions, and deeds were those approved by the Heavenly Father, or would these be in tandem with the ways Jesus would have responded had He been in our stead? Jesus explained that when one speaks on his own authority, he would only be seeking his glory, which would lead to pride and error (knowingly or unknowingly). But when one ensures that God’s will is secured through his words and deeds, he can never go wrong, as it is the Glory of God who protects, perfects and establishes him. Let us ask the Lord to guard our minds, intellect, and thoughts, to ‘think, reason out, and execute’ imitating Christ Jesus, and thus to be the light to many who are lost in the maze of this world’s clout. Jesus, be our light today. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
21/07/25, 09:34 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Until now you have asked nothing in my name. Ask, and you will receive, that your joy may be full’ (John 16:24)

At first, what struck me was that after all the asking, pleading, and supplications made by you and me, for years (over and over again), and we offered each to our Heavenly Father in ‘Jesus’ name, but some yet seem to us unanswered. It is Jesus who spoke this to His beloved disciples - at a time when He knew it was time for the Father’s plan (for all mankind) to be fulfilled through Him. He had been with His disciples for the past three years, grooming, training, and orienting them to trust God the Father, who sent Him to the earth. Even then, He knew how feeble the faith of the disciples were; how trivial were their arguments and the convictions that they stood for; how easily these could be manipulated and influenced by the ways of the world. Thus came this conclusion to them – until now you have asked nothing in my name. That these poor disciples were totally unaware of God’s plan about them, that they were to proclaim Jesus to the ends of the earth, and what they needed was the courage, strength, and drive to fulfill their purpose – Jesus knew this and tried to progressively inform the disciples about this, but their hearts were not prepared as their times had not come to understand. This St Paul, explained ‘When I was a child, I spoke like a child, I thought and reasoned like a child, but I no longer do now.’ And what was he (exactly) doing now? He was praying for being filled with the completeness that comes from the Lord – His power of love, that surpasses all understanding. It is for this that we too are to ask in Jesus’s name – for being filled with the completeness of our personalities, through the perfection of Jesus’s love in us. Ask and you will receive, the Lord promises us; and that will make your hearts overflow with joy. Let us wholeheartedly ask the Lord to fill us with His love. Amen

22/07/25, 09:50 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘O God, you are my God; earnestly I seek you; my soul thirsts for you; my flesh faints for you, as in a dry and weary land where there is no water’ (Psalm 63:1)

King David prayed from the depths of his heart, acknowledging how much he needed to be with the Lord; how much his soul thirsted for God. Every occurrence in the life of David was an awakening of his soul to this awareness, that he needed nothing but the Lord for restoration. This instinct in him drew him close to the heart of God, made him distinguished before God. David was certified by God Himself as a man with a heart that clung to Him. None of his descendants could claim this merit, ‘that which made David distinctively above board,’ the cause of a perpetual dynasty for Israel, the one who prepared the way for his son to rule in peace and majesty. The Holy Spirit points to ‘David’ as a lesson, for all generations (here to this day) to imitate. To seek the Lord, to thirst for Him alone, whatever be the trials and twists in life, It is the Lord alone You and I need for today’s revival, a new start, progress, and tryst with destiny. All who longed for the Lord were honoured during their life on earth and much more in Eternity. St Mary Magdalene was one of them, the first among those who witnessed the Glory of the Lord, and who was sent to proclaim it to the world. Let us pray for His steadfast love and mercy to overshadow us today and every day. Amen

‘They remembered that God was their Rock, that God Most High was their Redeemer’ (Psalm 78:35)

Yes, they remembered that God was their ‘rock’, who provided for them, all through the wilderness, who delivered them from the hands of their masters, the Egyptians. But as it occurs to us at times, they too (Israelites) got to remember the great deeds of the Lord, only after the strongest among them - their young warriors had succumbed to death half-way through the desert. Though they were given ‘Bread’ from Heaven and plenty of water to drink from the rock, though as a pillar of cloud and a wall of fire the Lord God accompanied them with His protective loving care; they failed to give thanks to His Holy name. They failed to recognize His presence with them, His concern for them as they traversed through the wilderness, as they were led through the midst of Red Sea over to cross into the Promised land. They were His treasured possession, those chosen from among the earth to worship and praise His name. So are we, believers in Jesus. We have been set apart from the world to proclaim ‘Jesus’ to those around us. For all that we are today, for the blessings we have been bestowed upon to this moment, for the gift of life, for the privilege to know Jesus and of His greatness accomplished on our behalf, we owe a lot to God. Therefore, in all that we do and say, let us be thankful to God, through Jesus Christ; let us offer everything today for God’s glory and for the sake of our love for Him. In whatever phase of life, we may be the truth is that Jesus alone is our ‘rock’, our ‘redeemer’, and the one whom we can trust even though things seem disrupted and disarrayed to us. For when with Him, there’s nothing we would want. Amen

28/07/25, 09:02 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I will speak to them in parables to explain things hidden since the creation of the world (Matthew 13:35)

Jesus spoke to his disciples as well as the crowds through parables; these were short stories with greater content and intent (expressing things beyond their literal meaning). Through these short stories, the disciples were exposed to the mysteries of the Kingdom of God. Jesus opened the faculties of the disciples, that they may be privileged to grasp what was not revealed yet to the world. Even after spending three long years with them, teaching, preaching, and training them in the principles of faith, He knew that they needed grace to wholly comprehend and live these ways. Therefore, He sent to them the promise of His Father, the Holy Spirit, the Paraclete, who would advise, remind, teach, and inspire them to tread this path towards Eternity. What was hitherto veiled before men (ever since the creation of this world) was unveiled and written into the hearts of men through Jesus Christ. This powerful dynamic that works within one – the inspiration of our hearts, fuelling our passions, desires, and motives, framing in the mind a sense of ‘determination’ - the power of the will, and forcing our words and deeds to requisite action. It is this dynamic that creates a transformation in one, the transition that can do marvels in this world. All who are led by God’s Spirit thus acquire the status and décor of God’s children. So was the Saint of this day, the first woman saint of India – St Alphonsa, whose inspiration through the love for the Lord, transformed itself into concrete action of love – love for other fellow beings. She was an exposition of the greater truths of the Heavenly Kingdom revealed to her by God; and thus she remains in the heart of every believer an example of lived faith, glorified by the Father, ready to help each one of us to live our faith to the fullest. Happy Feast of St Alphonsa!

‘In the Law it is written: “With other tongues and through the lips of foreigners I will speak to this people, but even then they will not listen to” (1 Corinthians 14:21)

In the past, God spoke to our fathers through the prophets, but in these days He has spoken to us through His Son, Jesus, whom He has appointed as heir to all things. The privilege given to you and me is that the mysteries of the Kingdom have been proclaimed in our hearing, our lives, through the Word of God, through the Gospel message. Unlike it was to those before our times, who lived in the fear of approaching God, to whom the face of God ‘mercy’ was not made known, they struggled to make sense with faith. But we have received the comfort, consolation, and promise of God, the needed repose and rest for our minds and bodies through Jesus. We have been sanctified, washed, justified, and brought to the Holy of Holies, with the hopeful confidence of being able to reach out to His Mercy, because of what Jesus accomplished on our behalf. But as were the Israelites, whom God loved as His own, who failed to heed to the teachings, disciplining, and precepts of God, so do we (and very often), when swayed by the fantasies of this world. These (the Israelites) had the Law, the Judges, the Prophets, the seers, the priests, through whom God made known of His will for them. But when pride in riches, influence, self-sufficiency, and confidence overwhelmed their hearts, their minds were blinded from seeing the true riches that came from above. They had to succumb to harsh realities, to be taught of what mattered to them the most, only after years of deportation to a foreign land and among foreign people. It was the latter who revealed to them who they were, where they truly belonged to, and what their purpose in life was. Let us look into ourselves, be thankful for all that we have been blessed with, and ask the Lord to open our hearts to live worthily in accordance to His call. Amen

‘But as for you, O man of God, flee these things. Pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, steadfastness, gentleness. Fight the good fight of the faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called’ (1 Timothy 6:11-12)

Temptations to be led by the standards of this world are sure to come. But as people of God, we are called to take hold of Eternal life by endurance in our trust in the Lord’s providence for us. It has led many to stray from faith, to plunge themselves into harmful desires that ultimately bring destruction and ruin. People tend to be highly self-centred, individualists, ready to compromise on sinfulness even at the cost of the Divine order. Families get impacted, societies all the more, and what emerges is a generation that believes in abandoning the path of life to secure prosperity and wealth. Let us sensitise ourselves and posterity to what makes for a meaningful life on earth. We are called to live as ardent warriors of ‘truth’, ‘life’, and ‘the way’ – which is Jesus Himself. Lord, give us the grace to pursue your precepts, rules, and laws, to be warriors of Your Kingdom; over any other priority of this world. Amen

02/08/25, 08:39 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘What agreement is there between the temple of God and idols? For we are the temple of the Living God…...’ (2 Corinthians 6:16)

What differentiates the generations of old and ours is in the aspect of ‘permissibility.’ While there were limits, constraints, and restrictions – the do’s and don’ts prescribed by the elderly, cultural taboos framed which were conscientiously followed by families and societies a generation back, these seem to have lost their relevance in the lives of people today. The ways of our culture (today) have stoked a genuine fear of the endless consequences that could come about upon humanity that is devoid of principle and etiquette. Could value systems, morality, and lifestyles detach themselves from what is naturally ordained on the pretext of liberty and unbridled individual freedom? What agreement can there exist between righteousness and wickedness? Or the Holy and the Ordinary? The Holy Spirit reminds us that we are the temple of the Living God, created to fulfill those good deeds destined for us in Christ Jesus. We are those to dictate the precepts of God to those in ignorance, error, and sin. Not to wallow in relationships with everyone and anyone at the cost of our faith. Let us not throw away our pearls to swine, to those who would never appreciate the worth of what we have been entrusted through the generations. We are a privileged race, a royal priesthood, and holy ones of God, to live in perfection and pride of the image and likeness of our Heavenly Father. Amen

03/08/25, 18:24 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): QUIZ 2017-18.pdf https://share.google/5NHwLH9YeHcC9eTDe
03/08/25, 18:29 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Top 50 GK Questions with Answers for Students

General Knowledge (GK) is an essential part of a student’s education. It not only helps in academic growth but also enhances critical thinking and awareness about the world. Whether you’re preparing for a quiz, an exam, or just curious, this compilation of gk questions with answers will serve as a valuable resource. Additionally, understanding the importance of reading and being aware of global educational standards, such as those in international schools in Bangalore, can further enrich a student’s learning journey.

1. Geography
What is the capital of France?
Answer: Paris
Which is the largest continent by area?
Answer: Asia
Which river is known as the longest in the world?
Answer: Nile
Mount Everest is located in which mountain range?
Answer: Himalayas
Which country is known as the Land of the Rising Sun?
Answer: Japan
What is the smallest country in the world?
Answer: Vatican City
Which desert is the largest in the world?
Answer: Sahara Desert
Which ocean is the deepest?
Answer: Pacific Ocean
Which country has the most number of time zones?
Answer: France
Which is the only continent without a desert?
Answer: Europe
2. History
Who was the first President of the United States?
Answer: George Washington
In which year did India gain independence?
Answer: 1947
Who was known as the Iron Man of India?
Answer: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel
Who discovered America?
Answer: Christopher Columbus
Who was the first woman Prime Minister of India?
Answer: Indira Gandhi
Which war ended with the Treaty of Versailles?
Answer: World War I
Who was the first Emperor of the Maurya Dynasty?
Answer: Chandragupta Maurya
Who was the founder of the Mughal Empire in India?
Answer: Babur
Which ancient civilization is known for its pyramids?
Answer: Egyptian Civilization
Who wrote the Indian national anthem?
Answer: Rabindranath Tagore
3. Science
What is the chemical symbol for water?
Answer: H₂O
Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
Answer: Mars
What gas do plants absorb from the atmosphere?
Answer: Carbon Dioxide
What is the hardest natural substance on Earth?
Answer: Diamond
Which organ is responsible for pumping blood in the human body?
Answer: Heart
What is the boiling point of water?
Answer: 100°C or 212°F
Which part of the plant conducts photosynthesis?
Answer: Leaves
What is the center of an atom called?
Answer: Nucleus
Which vitamin is produced when the skin is exposed to sunlight?
Answer: Vitamin D
What is the primary gas found in the Earth’s atmosphere?
Answer: Nitrogen
4. Literature
Who wrote ‘Romeo and Juliet’?
Answer: William Shakespeare
Who is the author of the ‘Harry Potter’ series?
Answer: J.K. Rowling
Which novel begins with the line ‘Call me Ishmael’?
Answer: Moby-Dick
Who wrote ‘Pride and Prejudice’?
Answer: Jane Austen
Which Indian author wrote ‘The God of Small Things’?
Answer: Arundhati Roy
Who is known as the Bard of Avon?
Answer: William Shakespeare
Which book features the character ‘Big Brother’?
Answer: 1984 by George Orwell
Who wrote ‘The Adventures of Tom Sawyer’?
Answer: Mark Twain
Which epic poem is attributed to Homer?
Answer: The Iliad
Who wrote ‘The Jungle Book’?
Answer: Rudyard Kipling
5. Current Affairs
Who is the current Secretary-General of the United Nations?
Answer: António Guterres
Which country hosted the 2024 Summer Olympics?
Answer: France (Paris)
Who is the current President of the United States?
Answer: Donald J. Trump
Donald J. Trump was inaugurated as the 47th President of the United States on January 20, 2025.
Which country recently became the newest member of the European Union?
Answer: As of now, no new country has joined the European Union in 2025. However, Serbia and Montenegro are the most advanced candidates in their negotiation processes with the EU and may join sometime between 2025 and 2030.
What is the name of the mission that aims to return humans to the Moon?
Answer: Artemis Program
Which country recently launched the Mars mission named ‘Tianwen-1’?
Answer: China
Who is the current Prime Minister of the United Kingdom?
Answer: Sir Keir Starmer
Sir Keir Starmer became Prime Minister on July 5, 2024.
Which country is known for the ‘One Belt, One Road’ initiative?
Answer: China
What is the name of the vaccine developed by Pfizer for COVID-19?
Answer: BNT162b2
Which country recently legalized same-sex marriage nationwide?
Answer: Thailand
Thailand legalized same-sex marriage on January 23, 2025, becoming the first Southeast Asian country to do so.
Conclusion
04/08/25, 08:14 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires 

I will show the holiness of my great name, which has been profaned among the nations, the name you have profaned among them. Then the nations will know that I am the Lord, declares the Sovereign Lord, when I am proved holy through you before their eyes. (Ezekiel 36: 23)

This verse is a part of a bigger promise that God makes in Ezekiel chapter 36. Through this verse God assures the exiled Israelites (who are living as captives in Babylon), that He will deliver and restore them on account of His Holy name.
The Israelites, through their unholy ways of living, had defiled the holy name of God due to which they were exiled and kept as captives during the Babylonian reign. During this period of trouble and sorrow, God promises them through prophet Ezekiel that He will deliver His people (the Israelites) on account of His Holy name. 
This verse reminds us that no matter how low we fall in life, and no matter what trials and tribulations we face, we have a wonderful God who is ready to forgive us and deliver us, no matter what pit we have put ourselves into. Let us pray: O God, on account of your Holy name, guide me and protect me today.
Amen
Megha Sherino

04/08/25, 08:55 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Simon, Simon, behold, Satan demanded to have you, that he may sift you like wheat. But I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith may not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers’ (Luke 22:31,32)

Simon was the ‘rock’ (according to Jesus), whom Jesus saw as the foundation to build His Church, and lead it. At one critical juncture in life, Jesus saw that Simon was demanded – to be tempted, to be sifted by Satan. Tossed about in confusion, delusion, and helplessness, by the Evil, so that he who was chosen to guide, protect, and console, may speak and do things contrary to trust in the Lord. As we read further (in the Gospel) about the course of events that took place, we see how this determined man was swayed and intimidated by what we would regard as some of the least significant people and circumstances. The matter of fact is that he was impacted, for a few moments abandoned to doubt, darkness, and fear, but he repented and cried his heart out. I believe, that it was the powerful prayer of the Lord (for Simon) that saved Simon and brought him to where He was chosen and set apart to be. Satan still keeps demanding to see our fall. What could then save us? Not our works, thoughts, strategies, or even our prayers at times (because we know not how to pray). It is the intercession of Jesus for us that would help us survive and emerge triumphant in all these situations. Let us therefore ask the Lord to pray for us, to save us from stumbling and falling prey to the traps of the enemy. For we are merely flesh and blood, but in constant combat with the rulers, authorities, and spirits of darkness, that hail even in the Heavenly realms. Jesus, My Lord and Master, be my Saviour; I may be angry, unforgiving at times, intolerant, and unrelenting; but You be my ‘rock’ and staff of comfort to move on. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

Submit yourselves, then, to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. (James 4: 7)

As human beings, we are weak beings, often falling for the vanities offered to us by the devil. But in today's verse, God reminds us who we are, He reminds us of the authority and power vested on to us, as we read in Luke 10: 19- I have given you authority to trample on snakes and scorpions and to overcome all the power of the enemy; nothing will harm you.

We God's children are called to prioritise His will in our daily life and put a stiff resistance against the evil one; then God assures us that he (devil) will flee away from us.

05/08/25, 10:24 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Submit yourselves, then, to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you’ (James 4:7)

The Holy Spirit advises us to ‘submit’ to the Lord, no matter how difficult, how weak, incapable, or distraught – because we belong to God. It is the Lord who awaits to see us bloom, to spread the effervescence of love, hope, and faith; and thus to see us bear fruit in every season of life. Whenever in the dark, when in doubt, when disturbed, remember that the enemy is at work, under cover, and he intends only to steal, to rob, and destroy all that the Lord has offered to us. But when in ‘dire’ state, we are often tempted to abandon the Lord, to break our bonds with Him in prayer, to put the blame on Him for all the untoward. Let us humbly submit ourselves before Jesus, the ‘One’ who ransomed us by a sacrifice of self to the Father. Let us draw near to Him in our ‘own ways’ - You and I are unique, and so is the way we converse in prayer. Let praise, thanksgiving, and supplications be poured from our hearts to the Lord, who delights to hear from us. This is how we learn to resist the evil one, who will be forced to flee from us. Wishing you a blessed morning! Amen

06/08/25, 08:10 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires 

He was given authority, glory and sovereign power; all nations and peoples of every language worshiped him. His dominion is an everlasting dominion that will not pass away, and his kingdom is one that will never be destroyed. (Daniel 7: 14)

Today as we commemorate the Transfiguration of Jesus Christ (where He momentarily manifests the glory and authority vested on Him), this verse reaffirms it. 
Prophet Daniel in chapter 7 is given a terrifying vision of four deadly beasts, in his vision he sees how these deadly beasts persecute the people of God, but at the same time he is also shown the power, authority and glory bestowed on a human being (Jesus Christ). The glory given to Him is everlasting and his kingship will never be destroyed. This was a prophecy given to the people of the Old Testament, who were expecting a powerful king. 

Today, God asks us not to fret or be afraid of the difficulties and sufferings we face, because all authority, power and glory on earth is bestowed on Jesus Christ, our Lord and Savior. His kingdom will never end and His reign will be filled with peace and everlasting joy. We , the children of God are no less than princes and princesses. Keeping this fact in our mind, let us live a life giving glory to God.
God bless you
Megha Sherino
06/08/25, 10:05 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But You have come to Mount Zion, to the city of the Living God, the Heavenly Jerusalem. You have come to thousands upon thousands of angels in joyful assembly’ (Hebrews 12:22)

The Wall or mountain of protection, Mount Zion – it is here we draw near every time we visit the Tabernacle of the Lord. I will be a wall of fire around you, says the Lord and the glory in you – what more can we comprehend for protection from God. Even with all turmoil around us, distress and disappointments within, You and I can claim to be at peace, because of the love of Jesus for us. He has promised us that in Him is our peace; that there will be trouble around and within while on earth, but He is the one who has conquered the world (John 16:33). It was this peace and serenity that surmounted the disciples Peter, John, and James, who were fortunate to be present at the event of the Transfiguration of our Lord. They were spell-bound, and dumb founded, that there wasn’t anything much they could explain by word of mouth of what they saw and felt while up at the Mount Tabor. So it is with us whenever we believe that this immeasurable power touches us as we approach Jesus in the Holy Eucharist. What was only the privilege of the Heavenly Hosts (to enjoy the presence of the Living Lord) is open to each one of us who believe in the Eucharist. Indeed, we have come to where thousands of the Heavenly hosts have assembled, to where praises rise incessantly, and to where we experience the real transformation of issues that entangle us. May the transfiguration feast prove a life changing experience for you this day, for this we pray. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

“But what about you?” he asked. “Who do you say I am?” Simon Peter answered, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
(Matthew 16: 15- 16)

In Matthew chapter 16, Jesus asks his chosen disciples this question, giving them a chance to proudly acknowledge their faith. Probably this question might have also been a test of faith. Likewise in our lives, many times we are given the chance to openly witness Christ. Let us retrospect our lives, were we able to pass this test of faith? 
Let us pray for the grace to live a life as a true witness to Christ.

Now, Israel, hear the decrees and laws I am about to teach you. Follow them so that you may live and may go in and take possession of the land the Lord, the God of your ancestors, is giving you.

Today's verse reminds us of the blessings in store for the one who faithfully follows the commandments of God. In the Old Testament, the Israelites' lives are very examples for all of us. 08/08/25, 09:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘So shall my Word be that goes out from my mouth; it shall not return to me empty, but it shall accomplish that which I purpose, and shall succeed in the thing for which I sent it’ (Isaiah 55:11)

Every Word that God has spoken is ‘life’ and ‘power.’ As I read, recite, and proclaim it (me unknowing), I am filled with immeasurable power which is His Grace. What no eye has seen, no ear has heard, no human mind has ever perceived, are the things God prepares for me because of Grace. And to wrap it on, we are told that what we are now, is nothing but Grace at work. You and I in whom this Grace is implanted, is called to work a hundred-fold in dedication, in strength, and in courage, to walk that extra mile for the sake of what we have been gifted. But even when we work as hard as we can, we must admit that it was not I who worked, but His grace that did it exemplary for me. This is the quintessential source of blessing that comes from God’s Word spoken and read by us. It shall never return empty, but will work through discerning the deepest aspirations within me, and seek to accomplish each one of these, because we were loved and redeemed by the Lord. Therefore, let us assure ourselves that we who believe in Jesus, who trust in His promises, will see the fulfilment of each one of these in their most appropriate time. Let us hold firm to what we have been taught, believed, and entrusted down to by generations of old, by what our forefathers have told of the great works of might and power they were witnesses to during their lifetime. So will be our heritage, as we go about trusting the Lord. That ‘things’ now ‘not existing’ will come to pass soon, and all our hopes will become true. Amen

But I tell you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you (Matthew 5: 44)

In today's media and entertainment industry, through all the movies and series- What is being promoted? Revenge, violence, lust, hatred, and many more, unfortunately all anti Christian virtues are being glorified. 

What is the foundation of our faith? Love. In Matthew 22: 37- 40, Jesus summarises the ten commandments into 2: love God with all your heart, soul and mind and love your neighbour as yourself. This is the core of our religion and faith- love of  God and man. But today's media is making a mockery of our God and his words.

What we can do as christians is to hold firmly onto our religious principles and teachings. Let us boycott these movies and series that are made to mock our religion and faith. Let us not see these shows and promote our friends and family to do so.
God's words are eternal, in Matthew 23: 35, Jesus says, "Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away".
Wishing you a blessed Saturday.

09/08/25, 09:31 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today 
 
'For judgment will be without mercy to anyone who has shown no mercy; mercy triumphs over judgment' (James 2:13)

Going by the standards of this world, it may seem ridiculously strange to be merciful, especially when by all means of the law (we are subject to), the provisions or possibilities of remission  has exhausted. Even in the realm of religion, adhering to rules and bylaws get predominance over the needed concern for the other. Thus we have failed to honour Christ and for the principles He stood for. Jesus reminds us that every moment in time in creation, there is this groaning for redemption. The opportunities where we have failed our egos were those where we resembled the work of Christ - His redemption. Such is the significance of mercy that purports to it a genre and authority over any other. Prompted by love does spring mercy and to all who have triumphed to love have gained the perfection that cleanses all imperfections and sin. Therefore let us pray for a heart that is softened by compassion and love, that promotes unity, harmony, and the good in another. This is when we attain the likeness of Christ our Master. Amen

‘For Zion’s sake I will not keep silent, for Jerusalem’s sake I will not remain quiet, till her vindication shines out like the dawn, her Salvation like a blazing torch’ (Isaiah 62:1)

What is sure to come for the ones who are the beloved of Jesus is this reward that He brings when He comes. Though it may seem quite some time, believe that Our Lord is faithful, compassionate, and determined to fulfill His promises and reward those who bear His name. So teach us to yield to what Your Spirit in us inspires us to do. For we are your masterpiece, your wonder work, created in Christ Jesus to do those specific deeds of power that has been destined before the foundations of Creation. Thank you Lord for holding us precious to Your heart, and honing us to walk with You, always and at all times. Amen

12/08/25, 08:14 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires 

Be strong and very courageous. Be careful to obey all the law my servant Moses gave you; do not turn from it to the right or to the left, that you may be successful wherever you go.
(Joshua 1: 7)

In today's verse, God reassures us to be brave and bold and to holdfast to all his commands and statutes, so that we can be victorious in all our undertakings. 
When we look into the Old Testament, Joshua son of Nun, was chosen by God to lead his people into the promised land. In his conquest of Canaan, he had to engage in numerous wars against various canaanite groups, like the battle of Jericho, the battle of Ai, the battle against the kings of Jerusalem, Hebron, Jarmuth, Lachish and Eglon. Throughout his life, God gave him victory. The Israelites were victorious in all the battles they fought under his leadership.

His life is an example for us. In the day to day battles of our lives, God assures us that he will give us victory, provided we follow his words and lead a life faithful to his commandments.
God bless you

12/08/25, 08:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But the wisdom that comes from Heaven is first of all pure, then peaceable, considerate, submissive, full of mercy and good fruits, impartial and sincere’ (James 3:17)

We are called to yearn for the wisdom that comes from above. And this wisdom clearly distinguishes itself from the wisdom that we are often accustomed to. What we have learned from childhood has been to compare, compete, vie with one another, and then demonstrate our distinctness. But this is not what the Spirit of Jesus expects of us in our daily life. The so-called features that make for the sublime wisdom from Heaven is first of all pure, it is peaceable and accommodative, it is inclusive and considerate towards all, it is submissive before the Almighty and His precepts, it encompasses mercy and all good fruits that emerge out of a compassionate heart, it is impartial exhibiting no double standards between the haves’ and the have not’s or those marginalised, and finally it is sincere because it is the offshoot of God’s love. The love of the Lord prevails over all human perspectives, inclinations of the mind and heart, protects against sin, and finally rewards the sower with fruits of justice. Let us therefore examine ourselves in this light of God’s Word, whether the kind of wisdom we have been yielding to was that inspired by His Spirit or the promptings of our flesh. Wherever there is bitter envy, selfish ambitions, and boastfulness, there is disorder, disorientation, and the denial of truth, God’s Word teaches us. Lord Jesus, incline my heart to love you above all Creation, that I may be filled with the Divine wisdom that brings growth, healing, and deliverance in my life and in many around me. Amen

13/08/25, 09:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘From now on, therefore, we regard no one from a worldly point of view. Though we once regarded Christ in this way, we do so no longer’ (2 Corinthians 5:16)

What we know is what our physical senses have permitted us to know, nothing beyond; unless the Spirit of God enlightens our hearts and minds to a deeper revelation. We thus have no right to judge another, who is like us because what we know and perceive of him/her is only in the periphery. St Paul remarked (in his First letter to the Corinthians) on the deeper dimensions of spiritual charisms, stating that the first and foremost of them all was ‘the ability to love.’ It was to this apostle that the Divine mysteries were made known through revelations by the Holy Spirit. And thus he spoke, when I was a child, I spoke and responded like a child, but not now. The question he poses to each one of us today is – Where do we see ourselves in the realm of Spiritual growth? Are we still children who are to be fed on milk and nothing else, or have we grown enough to digest greater realities of the revelations of God’s Spirit? If the way we speak, respond, perceive, and judge is from a worldly point of view, we are no more than toddlers and infants, hardly capable of being entrusted ‘any’ task of worth to be fulfilled for the Eternal kingdom. Let us humbly submit to our Lord, asking Him for forgiveness for the times we failed to perceive, understand, and empathize from His point of view. Let us learn to silently listen to others without being defensive and respond to others without being offensive. WE have been called to be merciful, understanding, and supportive, just as God the Father, has forgiven us in the Lord Jesus. Amen

14/08/25, 08:05 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires 

And the Lord said to Joshua, “Today I will begin to exalt you in the eyes of all Israel, so they may know that I am with you as I was with Moses.
(Joshua 3: 7)

Today's verse is a reminder of the authority vested on God's chosen people. 
Who are God's chosen people? Are they just the priests and religious? No, the leaders of our country, leaders of our state, and even the higher officials in the organisations and institutions we work or study in. It is not merely talent or hard work that elevated these individuals to their positions, but the grace of God that entrusted them with the responsibility of leading His people. 

Many times we criticize the bad governance in our country or we complain about the injustices within these organisations and institutions. Today, we are reminded of the vital call to pray for those in positions of power, and a reminder that true success comes not merely from talent or tactics, but from the grace of God.
Wishing you a wonderful Thursday.

14/08/25, 09:02 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Is anyone among you suffering? Let him pray. Is anyone cheerful? Let him sing praise’ (James 5:13)

The Holy Spirit today wills to teach us this fundamental lesson that God is our refuge and strength; a very present help in trouble, as echoed through the words of King David. This great King was convinced of how near the Lord was to him, when in trials, in sickness, in tribulations, in suffering, in success, and when joyful. Therefore, he prayed, ‘Do not take your Spirit away (your presence) from me, O Lord, but instead grant me a willing spirit, and sustain me.’ More than any kind of human intervention or empathy, we all need the ever-abiding presence of the Lord in life. So, to the one in distress, the Spirit of the Lord calls him/her to seek the will of God for him through Jesus. To those in suffering and pain, God’s Spirit compels them to reunite with God in prayer, rather than turn to any fragile human support. If all the negative emotions (one can ever describe) seek to strangle your mind and deprive you of needed strength, seek help and comfort from the Lord, who will break these strongholds that rattle you. When God is for you, who can defeat you? Believe and say to yourself, All that was done were the doings of the Lord for me; for He is the source of my encouragement and strength. He will work for me today, as He did (for me) yesterday and in the past. Even in the middle of suffering, I am content because He is with me, and His goodness shall follow me all the days of my life. How privileged I am with the Lord of Hosts at my side. Amen

15/08/25, 07:17 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘This, the first of His signs, Jesus did at Cana in Galilee, and manifested His glory…’ (John 2:11)

The first of the Signs or wonders Jesus performed on the recommendation of His Blessed Mother was the miracle at Cana in Galilee (water being transformed into wine for the banquet). But first there was something to be unquestionably fulfilled – to fill the jars with water. This - the servants obeyed by filling the six jars with water to the brim. Now ‘filling water’ (that too in jars kept for purification) would not solve the issue of ‘scarcity of wine’ in a Jewish wedding banquet where many dear and near were a part of, and which according to their custom, tradition, and beliefs was a bad omen for the newly wedded couple. But this family was distinct: they had a special love and concern for the Blessed Mother ‘who’ was present with them, and they had love for the Lord, who was also invited to partake in the celebrations. Therefore, when Mother Mary, the mother of the Lord advised them to do whatever He tells them to do, they yielded to it without doubt or hesitation. This paved the way for the first sign of Jesus. They were instruments to God’s greater works in Jesus, only because they had a believing and pleasant heart. Let us ask our Lady whose Assumption feast we celebrate today, to intercede for us, to be more ‘humble and yielding’ to God’s ways, so that His will would manifest great glory in our lives, especially in the most pressing needs of ours. Remember, God’s ways are love and compassion. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus be with you. (Romans 16: 20)

Today's verse is an encouragement and assurance of God's protection and power. In St. Paul's letter to the Romans, he advises the church to be prudent in choosing what is good and guileless. But at the same time he also assures the believers that united with God, they will experience victory over the powers of darkness.

This verse is also a reminder of the spiritual warfare we face in our daily life. As St. Paul says in his letter to the Ephesians 6: 12- "For we are not fighting against flesh-and-blood enemies, but against evil rulers and authorities of the unseen world, against mighty powers in this dark world, and against evil spirits in the heavenly places." 
But do not fret as St. Paul again reminds us in Romans 8: 31- If God is for us, who can be against us?
Let us be united with God and crush the powers of darkness working against us.

16/08/25, 09:04 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Keep me safe, my God, for in you I take refuge. I say to the Lord, You are my Lord; Apart from you I have no good thing’ (Psalm 16:1,2)

Let this new day garner for me the courage to wait for the Lord. In the Lord is our refuge, our source of empowerment, and above all, every goodness we would need for this life is from Him.  What could be more annoying in life than waiting for the Lord? We who live in confinement of physical time and space are at times overwhelmed by having to set our eyes on a hope that traverses hope. The Bible not only assures us that God’s intervention is just at the door, but gives us instances of the many who were restored, reignited, and therefore rejoiced because their trust was in the Lord. For all the security I have been fortunate to enjoy at each stage in life, for all the helpful people around me who accompanied me to this day, for all the situations that taught me the lessons I would never have otherwise learned, I am indebted to the Lord. Even when I thought my feet were slipping, it were His gentle arms that held me firm. When in doubt, anger, or remorse, it was He who healed the sinking realms of my mind, reinstating me with a reason to rejoice. If ever I smarted at the little works of my hands, He let me so knowingly, because He knew my feeble frame and that I needed the joy in little things too. Into the arms of such a magnanimous Lord, I give myself today, because He teaches me His ways, the truth, and replenishes my life each day with some more of goodness, that is He. Amen

17/08/25, 07:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Turn again, O God of Hosts! Look down from Heaven and see; have regard for this vine, the stock that your right hand planted’ (Psalm 80:14,15)

You and I are the stock, the vine that God planted to be where we are now. He made from one man every nation of mankind to live on the face of the earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands and territories, the Holy Spirit reminds us today. Where you have been planted is your home, destined by the Lord, from where you have been called to bloom. Because His plans for you were distinct, special, and unique – different from others, He caused you to be within your circumstances. When the people of Judah were defeated by the Babylonians, exiled for 70 long years as had been foretold by the prophet, they were asked to follow suit (not to revolt against the Babylonians) but to build houses, engage in their livelihoods in their place of deportation, in a foreign land. For God had promised that even in a foreign land, His mighty Hands would work for them and bring in healing and restoration. Those who believed and complied with this diktat of the Lord were the ‘remnants of Judah’ whom we read about; who were fortunate to reclaim their territory (Jerusalem) and rebuild their lost glory. Today, whatever be your situation in life, believe that the Lord has called you for greater glory, that You may become His witness on the earth. Let us pray as the remnants of Judah prayed, ‘Restore us O Lord, let your face shine upon us, that we may be blessed. Amen

18/08/25, 11:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Behold, we consider those blessed who remained steadfast. You have heard of the steadfastness of Job, and you have seen the purpose of the Lord, how the Lord is compassionate and merciful’ (James 5:11)

Being resolute and steadfast could be the hardest and most precious life skill one could imbibe in life. Living in a culture that adores ‘novelty’, and is easily drawn to the peripheral more than the core, it is so usual to be swayed by the currents of the day. Even if the so-called cold culture has had its influence over almost every aspect of society, there is still room for hope. And this is what the Holy Spirit inspires us through today’s Word - bringing to us the illustration of this wonder-man, Job, who was tested to the core. Not that his righteousness was unknown to God, but it was the Lord who permitted this man to be stripped off all that was glorious in him. To this innocent man, what could be more painful than the fact that even to his wife and his close associates, he was regarded but a black sheep. We read in the Book of Job, that this man vented out his complaints to the Most High. The waiting was quite a while, but sure as the dawn. Not that God ignored Job’s suffering, but all the while, Job was being prepared for a greater wisdom (a wisdom unknown to the world), which was only revealed to all mankind in the ‘course of time’ (through the glorification of Jesus Christ). Job was also restored a double fold for all that was taken away from him. In life’s journey, how many have been those ‘times’ when we struggled irrationally through situations, difficulties, and misunderstandings. We often felt all alone, away from the crowd, disowned, and deprived of even what we rightly deserved. But in all these, ‘believe’ that we were chosen by the Lord, who trusted in our potential, and who intended to reap a harvest by the fulfilment of His great purpose through us, not just while here on earth, but even for Eternity. You are precious, though little in your eyes, because He has regard for you as the apple of His eye. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

19/08/25, 09:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Pardon me, my Lord, Gideon replied. but if the Lord is with us, why has all this happened to us? (Judges 6:13)

At times, we must ask our loving Father through Jesus Christ (but with an obliging heart) why it seemed as though we were abandoned, when trials and tribulations surge past in life, when shaken by the least expected, and when in distress. This young man, chosen by God as ‘Judge’ for the Israelites, Gideon, was once in deep trouble. On the one hand, there was this ‘Midianite oppression’ that tormented him and the Israelites at large; and on the other, they had to secure food, oil, and water (necessities of living) without being plundered by their dictators. For all that they had produced in the land was confiscated by the latter, leaving them impoverished, offended, and disappointed. Having understood the travail of His people, God intervenes in their lives by anointing one among them as Judge by whom they were to be delivered from these ruthless oppressors. Thus an Angel is sent to the house of Joash, where Gideon (his son) was threshing wheat in a winepress to hide himself from being noticed by the Midianites. The Angel appeared to Gideon with God’s exhortation of encouragement, addressing him as a Mighty Warrior. Gideon would have laughed his heart out loud, having heard of the Angel’s greeting (to him it was yet another mockery, for which he had no time to waste) because ‘After all the struggle against Midianite abuse, here comes another to ridicule’. Therefore, his response was sharp, to the point, and very reasonable. It’s ok at times to ask the Lord and argue with him for all the events, occurrences, and overtures that have disturbed the otherwise smooth flow of our lives. God expected a similar reaction from Gideon, who was agitated and almost devastated. He blesses Him, empowering him to go ahead with the same vigour, to outdo the Midianites and deliver Israel from their undue subjection to the evil. Strengthen us Lord Jesus, as we make every effort today to do your will, even in the most mundane of tasks we may have to undertake today, be our provider and the reason for our vigour. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

Today's verse is a foundation of our faith and ethics. Loving God is not merely an emotional feeling, rather genuine love for Christ is demonstrate through our adherence to His word.

21/08/25, 07:46 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But He gives us more grace. That is why Scripture says, God opposes the proud but shows favour to the humble’ (James 4:6)

God our Father longs to see us walk with Him. He knows and accepts that it is so easy to be swayed by the currents and tides of our times. Therefore, He promises us His power–Grace; And when we are filled with the strength from above, we are extraordinarily equipped to set aside those things that label us as people of the flesh. The Salvation of our souls is what God desires and has initiated through the sacrifice of His Son, Jesus. He expects us to be grateful and reciprocate our love for Him by living lives faithful to His precepts. What God insists of me and you may vary according to our living and circumstances in life. But one thing is sure, what He asserts of us will surely lead us to our destiny. Not that we are not aware of the omniscience of God, but many are the factors that deter us from complying with God’s commands. Firstly, going the ‘narrow way’ would seem the toughest, making us the laughing stock in town. The risks of being ostracized, because of being different from the crowd, of swimming against the tide may have delirious consequences. But when you are swept to the fringes, You still have this promise from the Lord who assures you, that He would never abandon and forsake you (Hebrews 13:5). Therefore, as the Apostle James exhorts us through his letter, Let us submit ourselves to God, who gives Grace, who opposes the proud and shows favour to those who have been subdued by the torrents of this world. Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth. Amen

Lord Jesus, grace us with the strength to long after you always, to be protected from the strongholds of all that is lawless and wicked. Amen

24/08/25, 10:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘As a mother comforts her child, so will I comfort you; and you will be comforted over Jerusalem’ (Isaiah 66:13)

During the days of the restoration (after the Babylonian deportation), ‘Jerusalem’ to the remnants was indeed the land of Jerusalem, from where they once lived in glory. The prophet thus spoke to revive hope in these people, who had been lost in despondency and anguish. But to us, Jerusalem is not a specific place, but the heart of our Lord Jesus Christ – the heart that was pierced for us, from which flowed the water and blood to restore, revive, and reinstate all that is lost. A mother’s love and comfort to her child are irreplaceable; even when a mother forgets, the Lord remembers. And unlike a mother, He steps in with an overwhelming heart that throbs for us. It’s not just ‘love’ or emotional support the Lord gives us, but something more – healing, encouragement, and redemption. Every misery unspoken of, resting deep within the corners of our heart, he detects, every dream lost in the back-burners, He revives and gives wings to. It is ‘He’ our Creator and Saviour, who can re-establish and reaffirm us to where we have been destined to be, when all directions lead to nowhere. Today, Jesus promises to be our comfort, the way we are, to be our guide, and direction in life. Trust Him, and move ahead with boldness, as the prophets and Judges of Old did with a firm resolve. He would never let us fail and in Him we have and will receive grace upon grace till we reach the shores of destiny. Amen

25/08/25, 09:30 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You, however know all about my teaching, my way of life, my purpose, faith, patience, love, endurance, persecutions, sufferings …. I endured’ (2 Timothy 3:10-11)

Whatever the type of training, mentorship, parenting, or refinement, oriented towards upbringing, what brings in its fruits is a life lived with integrity. No matter how much the investment in terms of resources, money, time, or motivational support, none of these could transform into lifebuoys or gamechangers for society, unless when intricately woven into the fabric of life’s realities. That is what St Paul tried to impress upon his favoured disciple Timothy, also a Bishop of the early Church. It is not merely in technological prowess, a change mindset, or in the practice of traditions, rites, or customaries that a society’s future is correlated upon. It is on values, principles, purpose, sanity, and love that institutions, organisations, dynasties, families, and of course individuals have picked up, thrived, and emerged stronger. So devoid of these fundamentals, despite the kind of incentives we provide, we would find ourselves back to square-one again, trying to mend, argue, and justify our efforts and actions. What generations of old have entrusted to us is so precious, not in terms of quantity (the consumer content in them), but because of the great wisdom they possess. Let us introspect into our lives, whether our words, actions, beliefs, and contemplations were based on the plank of love, sincerity, and good for the other, or were they mere bubble-like ideologies that left no meaningful takeaway for humanity? Let us weigh our actions in the light of God’s Word today. Amen

‘If I say ‘Surely the darkness shall cover me, and the light about me be night, even the darkness is not dark to you; the night is bright as day, for darkness is as light with you’ (Psalm 139:11-12)

As broad as the day, as sure as is the sun, the Lord hems me from behind, from before, and all around. I may fail to recognize the wonder the Lord is to me, His light that shines within me, His wings that are spread to protect me all day long, especially when combated by doubt, fear, anxiety, and worries; when faces around me turn wry. He calls you too, today - to be the light, the city on the hill, that cannot be hidden, because the Light of life (Jesus) walks with you. You need not worry about the darkness that may be covering you at present; For to Him even the darkness is as bright as the Light’, He was the Light that had incarnated to conquer the night. Amen

‘but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the Gospel, so we speak not to please man, but to please God who tests our hearts’ (1 Thessalonians 2:4)

That God cannot be mocked is what the Holy Spirit, through the words of St Paul conveys to us today. For all that we have spoken, done, or accepted in Jesus’ name and for His greater glory, there is a reward. It doesn’t matter if before the world we have become an anathema, if like a bird perched on top of the roof all alone, we have been secluded. As a Christ follower, we have been called to preach, teach, and honour Jesus Christ alone and prove faithful to His Kingdom. You and I may not be able to preach or teach each day, but to live a life pleasing to the Lord is our obligation and choice. The core of our faith is to do all that draws the favour of God. To receive (to believe) the one whom God the Father has sent is that work that pleases God above any other. The message of Jesus was that of love and hope, fixed on the bedrock of righteousness, from which emerges peace. All that severs itself from the principles of love, of hope against hope, of righteousness and serenity, are never the works of God, however politically correct they may seem. We have a God who ponders not on the periphery but the inner recesses of one’s heart, who discerns his motives and intent. Lord Jesus teach us to reorient our words, actions, and beliefs in the Light of Your Word - the Truth, Life, and the Way to Eternity. Amen

28/08/25, 07:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘May God grant that I speak with judgment and have thoughts worthy of what I have received, for He is the guide even of wisdom and the corrector of the wise’ (Wisdom 7:15)
Solomon prayed for wisdom, that He might speak, judge, and act in compatibility with the heart of God. God is the provider and source of wisdom; Jesus Christ is the wisdom of God, and this personification of wisdom made known to us by the Holy Spirit, enlightens us - how to claim not just ‘wisdom’, but every other gift and promise for mankind’s well-being. In ‘Jesus’ You and I have been made eligible for every gift that comes from above. But what occurred in the life of this great King Solomon (despite his knowledge and understanding) can take control of our lives too if not cautious. Solomon was overwhelmed by the gift of wisdom, his distinctive characteristic trait. The moment he reveled in himself and failed to recognize His Creator, he was misled to rely on the inclinations of his mind, intellect, and emotions, becoming no better than any ordinary mortal. His life is a great lesson, for us, who believe we have conquered speed, time, and the Universe at large because of the so-called progress in Science, technology, Managerial skills, and foresight…..But the fact remains, that to speak with judgment and express thoughts worthy of what we are, we still need to be guided by the personification of all wisdom – Jesus Christ, who knows what best suits us and befits each one of us. Amen

28/08/25, 07:32 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires

We are therefore Christ’s ambassadors, as though God were making his appeal through us. We implore you on Christ’s behalf: Be reconciled to God. (2 Corinthians 5: 20)

In today's verse, St. Paul reminds us of who we are. As believers, we are 'ambassadors of Christ' on earth. 
What is an ambassador? An ambassador is the highest-ranking diplomat representing their home country's government to another country.

We are God's ambassadors, representing His kingdom on earth. Our thoughts, actions and deeds must be for the glory of our God and His kingdom. We are tasked with reconciliation with both God and man. 
What is the role of an ambassador? To uphold nad protect the interests of its nation and promote diplomatic ties between the two nations. 
Today, let us start our day by asking God's grace to fulfill our role as Ambassadors of Christ.

Wishing you a wonderful Thursday.
God bless you.

29/08/25, 09:49 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Therefore from one man and him as good as dead were born descendants as many as the stars of Heaven and as many as the innumerable grains of sand by the seashore’ (Hebrews 11:12)

From one man, Abraham, who was as good as dead (at an alarmingly feeble old age), were born descendants who were God’s Chosen. As do the grains (countless) that constitute the seashore serve multifaceted purposes of ecological protection, conservation, and balancing the ecosystem with its varied lifeforms that thrive near the shores, so did these descendants (of Abraham) work as instruments in the Hands of God fulfilling His ultimate purpose of the Salvation of mankind. Undoubtedly, the ‘glory and fame’ these people upheld was incomparable. The letter to the Hebrews further tells us that God is not ashamed to be called their God, and for them He prepared a great city. They had been seeking a ‘homeland’ that was far from better from what constituted their land (where they were aliens, slaves, and sojourners). This trust in God they exhibited throughout their journey transformed them as inheritors of the promised land, a prefiguration of the Eternal Kingdom. The Lord today enlightens us with the ways and manner of these people, not to leave us amazed or spell-bound, but to compel us to likewise demonstrate fortitude and a firm resolve when fret with life’s uncertainties. Be bold and courageous, the Lord tells us, because what we are about to assume would be far beyond our comprehension or imagination, incomparable to the struggles of today, and worth a lifetime.  Even when You and I believe that there is no point any further, the Lord still has a destiny for us, and He is sure to lead us there. In quietness and trust will be our strength. Amen

30/08/25, 10:30 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You will seek me and find me, when you seek me with all your heart. I will be found by you, declares the Lord, and I will restore your fortunes and gather you from all the nations and all the places where I have driven you, declares the Lord. I will bring you back to the place from which I sent you into exile’ (Jeremiah 29:13,14)

King Jeconiah’s rule, one highly eventful will always be remembered in Jewish history as a pivotal juncture of change of destiny for the People of God. It was a brief (three-month) period in 597 BC, marred by revolts, siege, captivity of the royal family, the skilled craftsmen, and their deportation to Babylon. Only the poorest of the poor (among the Jews) remained in the city of Jerusalem at the time of the Babylonian subjugation, to fall prey to the sword and famines later. To this young ruler, soon after he was taken captive to a distant land of the Gentiles, was the message of God conveyed through Jeremiah to him. It was a message of hope, of a greater connect with God, and of their fortunes to be restored in the future. God asks the King, the royal family at large, and the nobles to pray for the welfare of the land to which they were enslaved, for their future was dependent on the prosperity of the alien land they were made to inhabit. The King with his nobles repented, asked God for forgiveness, and patiently endured this period of agony, trusting in God’s intervention and waiting for the same. History tells us that 37 years later, this King was released from prison, honoured by a descendant of the Babylonian King, and given his due. God fulfilled his promise of restoration, bringing the remnants of His people back to Jerusalem in the course of time, where they rebuilt the temple and city. It is God who reinstates, recreates, and re-establishes us to our state of glory. Now imagine, if we were to receive a message from God like the one King Jeconiah received, at a time when all hopes and dreams for a future are utterly shattered? Would we ever pay heed to what God wants to tell us to make a comeback? Or would we rely on our sinking beliefs, failing hopes, and the opinions of those around us? Let us ask ourselves. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

31/08/25, 08:18 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Therefore says the Lord who redeemed Abraham, concerning the House of Jacob: Jacob shall no more be ashamed, no more shall his face grow pale’ (Isaiah 29:22)

The greatest promise made to the Chosen race was the one given to Jacob: that he shall no more be ashamed, his face shall no more grow pale (be disappointed). And this has been reiterated several times to God’s people through the prophets – an exhaustive bounty (material, spiritual, and eternal). What brought Jacob this blessing was not his tactfulness – In fact, Jacob was deprived of enjoying the fruits of the promise for as long as 21 years because of his deceptiveness. The will of God, Jacob’s humility to receive from God, his seeking after God for a decisive intervention in life – were those factors that gave him predominance in the heart of God (to be worthy of the promise given to Abraham). God’s promises are eternal and never revocable. For You and me, He opens His heart, and desires that we cherish these blessings. But one’s disposition of the mind (heart) will qualify him/her eligible to inherit the promises. Truly, Jacob shall no more be ashamed and no more shall his countenance fail, so long as he lives in the true Light – Jesus. For us this Sunday, the Holy Spirit opens the floodgates of Heaven to choose and claim as sons of the Kingdom. Help us Lord to be worthy of all that You have given to us. Amen

01/09/25, 09:57 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘In this the love of God was made manifest among us, that God sent his only son into the world, so that we might live through him’ (1 John 4:9)

Through His only son, Jesus Christ, by sending Him down to our midst, as one among us, God tried to let us know this, that He cares for us. He knew that our wounded hearts, the afflictions in our minds, would fail us from reaching out to Him, the real source of deliverance and healing. From among His ‘created’, He chose ‘Mary’ (in whom He found completeness of humility and obedience) as the pathway for the incarnation of His Son as a human being. Down to the foot of the Cross, Mary proved herself trustworthy in fulfilling her purpose of Co-redemptrix with her son, Jesus. Even to this day, she is honored by the Heavens for what she made possible - that men will live because of what Jesus accomplished for them. Mary is not merely one among creation, but the best of them all because of the fullness of Grace that constituted her being. She, the beloved of the Heavenly Father, will intercede and open new doors for each one of us from the Heavens when we call out to her for help. None other would dare to shut the doors that our Mother has opened for us. Into our hands is given each day, the Rosary, the key to open these doors – some of us wear it as a necklace, while others carry it as an ornament, but how awesomely powerful a weapon it is, is sometimes forgotten. It’s the first of September again, a seven-day ‘preparatory’ to celebrate the birth of this great Creation - Mary, the manifestation of God’s love, mercy, and grace. Let us pray the Rosary with added fervour, asking our Lady to reign over all that constitutes our being – our homes, minds, thoughts, relationships, and our future. What more do we human beings need? than a mother who constantly remembers, pleads for us, and cares for us. We are never alone. Day 1 - we pray to the Lord for serenity and peace in our worlds. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
02/09/25, 10:13 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But since we belong to the day, let us be sober, putting on faith and love as a breastplate, and the hope of Salvation as a helmet’ (1 Thessalonians 5:8)

To be swayed by the winds of the trends of times, dissuaded by words that are only half-truths, to be tossed by the swirls of trials and testing times, it is so natural for mortals to misconceive who they really are, or what they have been called to do. Our true- identity has been imprinted deep within us, much before we were even born, that we are children of the Light, we belong to God. Therefore St Paul, while exhorting the Thessalonian believers to wait for the Lord’s coming, did not intend to scare them. He stated that the day of the Lord’s coming was sure; not that all on earth would be prepared and rehearsed for it, but we are to rehearse ourselves in faith, love, and hope that are gifts from the resurrected Lord. Mary, the mother of Jesus, was one who meticulously prepared herself to be worthy of the Lord; this brought her the status of ‘the favoured one’ of God for all generations to know. What added spotlight to the miracle at Cana (the wedding at Cana) was not merely the water changing into wine, but the fact of God’s ‘change of heart’ to obey His beloved’s request. Such was the tremendous power this humble virgin - Our Lady of the Rosary, wielded during her lifetime (on earth), and does possess so even more; Every time, when You and I reach out to her with our supplications, she is sure to plead for us to the Father, who will be glad to fulfill them so that in course of time we may also learn to imitate these qualities of light that characterised her. You belong to the day, the Blessed Mother reminds you. For Day 2 - Put on faith, love, and hope that You have been gifted with, to live a life that gives God glory always. Amen

03/09/25, 08:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘..the faith and love that springs from hope stored up for you in Heaven and about which you have already heard in the true message of the Gospel that has come to you’ (Colossians 1:5,6)

Hope wields a tremendous influence on a person’s emotional, mental, and physical well-being, causing him to keep going on through life’s challenging paths. It is God’s Word alone that can trigger the impulses to ‘sustained hope’ in mankind. Every time it is heard, God’s power begins to manifest in the lives of the listener, encouraging him to wait for the best which is yet to come. It is this hope that produces the fruits of faith and love, transforming one to a serene disposition of mind to accept reality. Society or culture may have urged us to think that it is in one’s doing, tactfulness, and intelligence that relationships are sustained. But the Spirit of God tells us that it is Grace that sustains, nurtures, and develops healthy relationships and mindsets in mankind. This Grace has the power to circumvent all human errors, forgive, forget, and transform the human mind from all that negates and destroys. It is Jesus, His message, the Gospel that does this for us. When weak, when all alone, when disappointed, when fret with impossibilities, God’s power works within us through His Word, silently, but with precision and perfection. Let us therefore permit the working of God’s power in us, by humbly opening the doors of our heart, by listening to God’s Word and improvising our lives in tune with its promptings. Our world needs this message of hope, and none can predict the kind of change the Lord can bring to a disillusioned world that thirsts for hope. Day 3 – Let us pray with the Blessed Virgin to be able to spread the Gospel – the message of hope to the world. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

05/09/25, 07:00 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But this is to fulfill what is written in their Law: They hated me without reason’ (John 15:25)

At times, we begin our prayers in the form of a protest before the Lord, stating ‘Despite…this is what I get’ and no doubt, our feelings and the thoughts of our mind are perfectly true. And to this, Jesus explains to us that if the world hates you, keep in mind it hated me first. But our logic and reasoning would tell us that Jesus is God, and I am just an ordinary person. All whom God chose to be His, He insisted in them the traits that constituted the Divine, extraordinary, beyond human comprehension, and often contradictory to the ways of the world. Take the case of ‘The Chosen race’ of the Old Testament – they were considered holy and set apart, for God’s works; they were in no way bound to practice the traditions and cults of those among whom they lived. The greatest of all kings of Israel, David, had to prevail over numerous struggles (from his enemies, His subjects, even His own family) during his kingship; It was his very words penned down (in Psalm 69:4 and 35:19), taken for our meditation today, that Jesus quoted while speaking to His disciples. The Lord delighted in calling Himself ‘The Son of David’, not because of the pride in David’s royalty, but because of his faithfulness to God. This Patriarch of Old speaks to us (who have our own reasons for anguish) – God sees our humble state, The Mighty intends to do great things for You and through you. Trust in Him, as the Mother of Jesus did in all the trials and misunderstandings of her life. She proved a faithful servant of God that His will may be fulfilled for the Salvation of all mankind. Believe that God, who has a purpose for ‘you’ will initiate and fulfill His purpose for you. Day 5 – let us pray for increased faith and learning to trust in the Lord. Amen

'whoever says I abide in Him, ought to walk just as He walked' (1John 2:6)

He walked in the path of Light, His steps were firm even when the ground beneath appeared tardy and narrow. We who boldly claim to be His followers are called to tread the same path. But the fact remains, that we are so feeble, unable to move forward when the journey becomes long, tiring, and exhausting. Our hearts may fail us when fret by obstacles on the way. How enthusiastic we were when we began, trusting in the gifts that He bestowed to us? But when taunted by trials all the way, we were distraught, deprived of strength. Know this, that He was aware of us, of the desire in us, so He never let go of us. Somewhere down the lane, we lost our grip, but we were still under His radar. Jesus knows and cares for you - will never let you down. Trust and ask Him for the empowerment you would need to make this day one memorable and enriched. Let us ask the Blessed Mother to intercede for us in this regard. Amen
