08/09/25, 10:42 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘But why am I so favoured that the mother of my Lord should come to me?’ (Luke 1:43)

Mary’s visit to Elizabeth (the Visitation), about which we meditate in the second joyful mystery of the Rosary was an extraordinary event mentioned by Luke in his Gospel soon after the Anunciation made to Mary. When the mother of the Lord journeyed all the way into the hill country of Judeah to the house of Elizabeth and Zachariah, she brought with her God’s power and mercy into that little home. Our homes can be just like this one (though prayerful and upright), wearied and exhausted (at times) with the overwhelming nature of the crosses we carry - sickness, distress, unfulfilled dreams, and so on. In addition to these come a full load of societal norms, perceptions, beliefs, and misconceptions, the fears and insecurities of a lifetime. This old couple (Zachariah and Elizabeth) had together waded through troughs and peaks in life; the events in life had seemed so incomprehensible to them that there was none with whom they could share their pent-up emotions. It was these subdued feelings that found expression when the Mother of Jesus came to their home. God’s concern for them, love, and amazing ways of intervention in their life left them spell-bound and filled with gratitude. We are indeed so favored, a people of God, whom God hovers around, to protect, preserve, and replenish. God knows our every care, when intimidated by trials and torments, let us take courage to invite the Mother of the Lord to stand by us, intercede for us, and strengthen us. And when ‘She’ comes she brings in the Heavens – abundance of graces to our side. Today is the birthday of Mother Mary! May she be the enlightenment of our homes, to look on to the Lord at all times, to be filled with wisdom that comes from above, and to win over the enemy who only intends to throttle us. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

09/09/25, 09:23 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For the Lord will be your confidence and will keep your foot from being caught’ (Proverbs 3:26)

God’s promise for you today is that wherever you go and whatever you engage in, He will be your confidence. Not that You and I are equipped for perfection, but it is God himself who would work to make things intact. How very usual that we get tossed by waves of uncertainty, waves of contempt, failures, and even forgetting that He has called us for a purpose, so distinct and special. The Lord stands by us, knowing this feeble frame of ours, that we can be sceptical; we need encouragement and assurance at times. The support systems we have architected around us may be easily infiltrated and never foolproof. Not that everything is set to doom, but though hard-nuts to crack, at times situations and circumstances convince our minds to a state of utter insecurity. It could be the experiences of our past, or things we have knowingly or unknowingly imbibed through our specific ecosystems (different from those of others), or what we have been told by people around – whatever….the ‘vulnerability’ of our minds and thought processes is undeniable. Here, the Lord comes to our refuge, explicitly telling us that He is there to calm the storms, the turbulence within, nothing surpasses His Holy name. But we must tell ourselves repeatedly then, that we are well under His providence and control. It is in none other but Jesus that we would find ourselves confident and bold to keep moving on, irrespective of the errors of the past, or our wrong decisions, or many who tried to pull us down. Into His loving arms, we were preserved, protected, and cared for to reach our destined shores. Amen

10/09/25, 09:22 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Put on the new self, which is being renewed in knowledge in the image of its Creator’ (Colossians 3:10)

The new self is one adorned with compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience. Above all, it is subdued by the virtue of love. The knowledge of Christ Jesus, of what God obtained for us through the sacrifice of His Son, is what inspires us to ground ourselves in love. It is this knowledge that brings us each day closer to God and helps in discovering our call. The knowledge that ‘God’s Spirit’ who was at work on the day of Creation, is still at work in us, would prompt us to co-create with God, not to be complacent with the views and perceptions that have defined our thinking from of old. To be able to adapt and live ‘the convictions’ proven to us by the Lord Himself is where breakthroughs emerge in life. There may be times when people around may seem to misread and misinterpret our ways. It’s never going to be a pleasant experience for one to be distanced from the rest of the crowd. Nor can things ‘turn the table’ overnight. It is a constant and a slow exercise (to be practiced day in and day out) when life’s follies, trials, and setbacks teach us what it means to be with Jesus Christ. What may be meaningful to me may never seem the way for another – this is the paradox, because we are so fearfully and distinctly made. But to those who ask Him for the way ahead, He shows the Light, the path ahead, and the road to their destiny. Let this new day fill you with greater insight of what the Lord wants of you, and has in store for you, who have been so wonderfully made by Him. Amen

11/09/25, 09:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I have told you these things, so that in me you may have peace. In this world, you will have trouble. But take heart! I have overcome the world’ (John 16:33)

Jesus knows that in the world He has sent us to inhabit, there will be tribulations, troubles; there will be situations that cause you to break away from Him and from the truth. In everyone’s life (whoever), there will be a period when he/she will find no peace, nowhere to reach out to, and a time when he/she desired (earnestly), if there had been someone who would be of solace or a source of comfort. These are really tough times, and to endure would mean to garner strength from somewhere beyond. It is here that the Lord reminds us that the strength we need is all in us, deep in our hearts. We need not go anywhere. In fact, Jesus also warns us that when you weep and lament, the world around will rejoice, because it is never adept to understand the Divine purpose (to be accomplished) behind your baffling situation. All you need is to take courage to seek the Lord who dwells in you. It is His calling to you, whether you permit Him to sign on is up to you. But this intervention is what He demands to make in your life at that very moment. In Him You will find your peace – the peace that the world can never provide. Jesus promises you encouragement, the strength to endure, and to make a mark in your life – as He did at the cross in Calvary, where He triumphantly fulfilled the will of His Father for all. He who has overcome the world, will never let you down. Though men, situations, and times may keep changing, Jesus will be the same today, as He was yesterday and even more tomorrow. Take heart and be your best. Amen

‘Therefore every scribe who has been trained for the kingdom of Heaven is like a master of a house, who brings out of his treasure what is new and what is old’ (Matthew 13:52)

15/09/25, 09:08 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I know how to be brought low, and I know how to abound. In any and every circumstance, I have learned the secret of facing plenty and hunger, abundance and need. I can do all things through Him who strengthens me’ (Philippians 4:12,13)

Psychologists may attribute ‘life-skills’ as key, and primordial, even to ‘emotional intelligence’ for a human being’s holistic well-being. But from where do we imbibe these essential skills? These skills equip a person with ‘flexibility’ needed to navigate through life’s twists and turns, lows and highs, plentitude and hunger, and most of all braces one up to take on life’s challenges, though all alone and incompatible with the crowds. Paul, the apostle of Jesus Christ (for Gentiles), leaves before us a mind-blowing testimony, one pragmatic, and uttered from the depths of sincerity. Unlike other disciples and apostles of the Lord, Paul was blessed with several traits (qualities) that distinguished him from others, and set him at a higher pedestal/ranking than them. Firstly, he was a man of noble origin, a Pharisee by birth; He was a Jew, from the tribe of Benjamin, something to be proud of. Secondly, None could detest his authority/knowledge of the Laws (Mosaic law), something incomparable with the rest of Jesus’ disciples. He was a man of firm determination, one who was hellbent to pursue his cause (what he thought was the truth), something that many of us today relent from in the face of difficulties. Though many of His Epistles, Paul described the kind of trials, sufferings, persecution, and challenges, he had to put up with for the sake of His preaching God’s Word (having known the ultimate truth – Jesus). What empowered him with the needed perseverance (then) was not his characteristic traits/virtues, efficiency or tactfulness, but from the Lord Jesus. His love for Paul, the understanding of the magnitude of this love for him, was what strengthened the apostle to move way beyond His possibilities, to fear nothing before Him, and to boldly continue His mission. His life-skills were the promptings of the Lord’s Spirit in Him, who protected, guided, encouraged, and vitalized him to work harder by the Grace given to him. So shall be the way the Lord teaches us when we trust in Him. And this is how posterity needs to learn. Amen

‘Turn to me and be gracious to me, as is your way with those who love your name. Keep steady my steps according to your promise…...’ (Psalm 119:132,133)

Today, as in the days of Old, Jesus promises to you His faithfulness, His unfailing presence all through the ways that He has opened for you. To all those who love Him, this is His affirmation – that the roads ahead may be long, winding, uncertain, and even strange; none may seem to give you company, yet you are not alone, His favour will always overshadow you. His Wings will hover over you as a protective shield against the snares of the evil one. So do we pray today, lifting our petitions, supplications and above all our hearts with praise and gratitude, that Your will may be done today in my life. Show me your paths Lord Jesus, and be my Light, that your purpose for me is what I work to accomplish today. Amen

‘He appeared in the flesh, was vindicated by the Spirit, was seen by Angels, was preached among the nations, was believed on in the world, was taken up in glory’ (1 Timothy 3:16)

The mystery that underpins our faith is this one-liner that Paul made to his favoured disciple, Timothy. That God took the form of man, that the Spirit of God testified to all men that Jesus was a complete man while being God Himself. The Angels conveyed this message of Jesus’ birth to even the most ordinary men of the time (the shepherds). All the deeds of power, Jesus did during His lifetime and the fact that He was condemned to a humiliating death on the Cross. Things did not end up there; It was made known to all men that He resurrected from death, and more than all He ascended in glory to the Heavens and is seated there making intercession for you and me (to the Father), on the pretext of His sacrifice of self. At His name, all dominions, powers, authorities - in the Heavenly places and on earth bow down in adoration, praise, and surrender. This is the crux of our faith, which we need to live each day – In Jesus, before His Holy name, none prevails; all the problems, vexations, and tribulations of a lifetime just surrender before Him. He who dwells in you is greater than all that is in the world. Therefore, whatever be your task for this day, He will provide you with all that you need to make things work for the better. He is Emmanuel. Amen

18/09/25, 09:52 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘……(God) who wants all people to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth. For there is one God and one mediator between God and mankind, the man Christ Jesus’ (1 Timothy 2:4,5)

The truth is that Salvation is open to all mankind in Jesus Christ. This is the truth that the Heavenly Father wants each of us to be reminded of; the message He desires must spread far and wide. For all men (irrespective of time and space), Jesus gave Himself up on the Cross as a ransom. He is the one and only mediator for the sins of mankind, through the expression of mercy and grace that was given by the Father. Thus, while on earth, Jesus taught the crowds that ‘If the Son of Man has set you free, you are indeed free’. Now ‘we’ who believe in Jesus’ name have been set free from (i) sin, (ii) transgressions of the law, (iii) our iniquities of the past, provided we yearn for this righteousness in Jesus. His Doctrine was the law of love, that which fulfilled the purpose of all existing laws.  God looks into the hearts of men, to grace those who are contrite, accept themselves as sinners – those poor in the Spirit. King David proved himself a man with a heart that ‘followed’ God, and therefore all his sins were forgiven, his House established (by Covenant) to reign over Israel as against the House of Saul, whom God condemned (though first anointed). Paul called himself the worst of sinners (one who blasphemed and persecuted Jesus Christ); but through God’s great power and might, His mercy that surpassed justice, Paul was reborn a witness for Jesus Christ, an apostle to the Gentiles. You and I are likewise called to be an example of God’s mercy to those who are ignorant of Christ Jesus. May our words, ways, and deeds prove the way. Amen

19/09/25, 10:15 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘…...for His word possessed authority’ (Luke 4:32)

All through Jesus’s public ministry years, the crowds swarmed around Him to listen to Him; this continued till the moment of His final arrest and detention for trial by the Sanhedrin.  What drew these people towards Him was the authority that was manifest in the words and ways of Jesus. His teachings were distinct, of a different genre and type that was more humane and ‘for’ the people, in contrast to the Mosaic Law that was too voluminous and penalizing. His words were life for many, healing for the sick; His Words discerned through the minds, hearts, and spirits of people to uphold them and bring them closer to the mercy of God. Jesus was the true image of God, His mercy and unfailing love for all mankind. In the Synagogue, there was a man who possessed an unclean Spirit (who lived with/accommodated with the demon), probably unaware of his plight and the influence of the evil one on every aspect of His life. What brought him to the Synagogue, the place of prayer for the Jews, was the inadequacies of his life, the adversities he had to put up with (due to the demonic influence). Where else could he expect to be consoled than before God and His mercy? Jesus depicted the power and authority of His Word to heal this man, to drive away the evil Spirit from him, without causing him any harm - he was indeed restored. Even today, though we live in the era of Digital activism and automation, there are things we all struggle with. Those inadequacies of life, hardships, obstacles, and inhibitions that slow us down, or even detract us from our goals. To some of these, however discreet we may be, we never have answers or explanations for. Let us ask Jesus our Lord, to speak His Word of authority over all these; that tend to intimidate and deactivate us. His Word still possesses authority and will work marvels to get us through the most dire straits in life’s journey. Amen

God’s Spirit emboldens us to approach Jesus in prayer, with all our petitions, concerns, worries, and supplications. This is God’s will for us and we have this confidence (like St John) that whatever we ask according to His will, He will surely grant us. Amen

24/09/25, 10:38 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Does the ax raise itself above the person who swings it, or the saw boast against the one who uses it? As if a rod were to wield the person who lifts it up, or a club brandish the one who is not wood!’ (Isaiah 10:15)

Every other sin is perpetrated by the sin of pride, and God’s Word reiterates to us that Pride goes before a fall. Today’s Word throws before us the imagery of inanimate substances that are used as instruments or effort magnifiers to achieve certain tasks. Do these instruments wield themselves against their Creator? God asks His people. And this is how we at times behave and portray ourselves before God and men. For success and achievements in life, we go about boasting, exhibiting vain glory, and exalting ourselves, forgetting the fact that we were graced or fortunate to be blessed. When the contrary occurs, we attribute it solely to God (who is responsible for our ill-fate). Humility and spiritual dependency are the keys to worship. And these insist that we surrender ourselves (with all that we believe is ours) to the Almighty, our Creator, who has enabled us this far, and will continue to do so, for greater tasks yet to unfold. This is why Jesus reminded His disciples, ‘Apart from me, you can do nothing’. Giving us a more pleasant imagery of the vine and the branches, Jesus asks us to view ourselves as the branches of the ‘vine’, which is He; to draw sap and supplement from Him to emerge stronger and bear fruit. To befriend the Lord, asking His intervention and support in our lives, is how we make things work each day. As the disciples Peter and John asked the Lord how He would wish ‘of’ them to prepare the Passover meal (the Last Supper) for them all, so should we too do (each day), asking Jesus of what He desires from us this day. Ground us to be closer to you always Lord Jesus. Amen

26/09/25, 09:51 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Be strong, all you people of the land, declares the Lord, and work. For I am with you, declares the Lord Almighty. This is what I covenanted with you when you came out of Egypt. And my Spirit remains among you. Do not fear’ (Haggai 2:4-5)

To build up the ruins from ‘of old’, to raise up the temple of Jerusalem (God’s dwelling place) and the pride of the people of God was what God inspired His people to do through the words of Haggai, the prophet. Every purpose of God, He fulfils by His Word. Thus, this exhortation was made addressing not just the Jewish exiles, but also their Governor, Zerubbabel, and the High Priest Joshua. At His time, God appointed Zerubbabel, Governor for Judah, entrusting Him the task of rebuilding the Jerusalem temple, with the added promise that what would come out would surpass the former glory the temple once possessed. You and I are this temple that God desires to dwell in, to work wonders through, and be a sign of His authority, His unfailing presence in the world. We are the Covenantal people of the New Testament. Whatever you and I have undertaken to do today, the Holy Spirit commissions us to get going, to work with the conviction that He (the Spirit of Jesus) is working beside us. Not to look behind at the numerous errors of the past (knowingly or unknowingly we/others have been responsible for), but to boldly make every stride in its perfection. God is with you, so you need not fear. Jesus will equip you and me with all that we would need (resources) to set things functional and make headway. And as was the promise to Zerubbabel, that the future glory would be greater than the former, so does He bless you. Amen

27/09/25, 10:25 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘No one after lighting a lamp covers it with a jar or puts it under a bed, but puts it on a stand, so that those who enter may see the light’ (Luke 8:16)

We at times behave like fire-fighters, ready to extinguish when there is a flame. And many a times, though we know that there could be outbreaks or it would be better to take precautionary measures than wait for the worst to take over, simply because we are lethargic and refuse to own things up. Not trying to sound cynical, but speaking from the perspective of today’s reality - we have lost on several accounts (in any dimension of human living – material, familial, spiritual), we have proved our ignorance over and over again, because we have failed to understand who we are and what we have been called to represent. We have been called by Jesus, the Light of the world, to bring light to the world, to uphold true light, and to defy everything that counters or interferes with human existence with all its dignity. But are we true to ourselves? Do we portray the true light, or do we try to silhouette this light? Do we encourage virtues in human living or discount good examples for trivial gains of influence, power, and money? How often have we overlooked holiness and truthfulness for vain glory and the opinion of the crowds? Jesus reminds us that nothing is hidden that will not be made manifest, nor is anything secret that will not be known and come to the Light. So let us take care of how we see, hear, speak, and portray before the world – because ultimately every word, gesture, and deed will be tested by fire – the fire that refines the soul to demarcate between the good and the evil before the One who has authority to judge. Wishing you a blessed day! Amen

29/09/25, 10:49 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And to Him was given dominion and glory and a kingdom, that all peoples, nations, and languages should serve Him; His dominion is an everlasting dominion, which shall not pass away, and His Kingdom one that shall not be destroyed’ (Daniel 7:14)

This dominion of the Son of Man, which Daniel once saw in a vision is a prophecy made for all times. To him (Daniel) was given the interpretation that the Godly Kingdom would rule sublime, above all kingdoms established by men. This has become even more relevant to us who live in times of unlimited possibilities, the sky is our limit (as they say). God’s Kingdom that dwells in our hearts is even beyond, dwells in the realm of the ‘impossible’, gains ground and reaps a harvest when all hopes are lost. The Spirit of God reminds us that when you are within God’s Kingdom, your tent is peaceful, and when you visit your dwelling, nothing will be amiss. To this kingdom are we all invited as members/citizens by the Ancient of Days (God) to partake, to participate and expand. The key to this Kingdom is befriending the Son of Man, Jesus, who has been given dominion and power over all Creation. Therefore (as St Paul explains), it doesn’t matter whether Greek, or Roman, whether Gentile, or Jew, or whether captive or free, man or woman, what matters is knowing Jesus Christ, and becoming like Him. With Him, we are raised to sit in the realms of the Heavens, because of Him who has authority over all and conquered all. Amen

30/09/25, 10:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘All my springs are in you’ (Psalm 87:7)

Springs are symbolic of inner joy, peace, beauty, and sound health. In the course of time, seasons do change; summers, autumns, and winters give way again to the advent of spring. The delight, hope, beauty, and energy of Spring revitalize our spirits, but what makes it explicitly known to us is its distinctness from other seasons. Likewise, the adversities in life, pain, and suffering are a reality, but the hope of an upcoming reward or greater glory can nullify all these troubles, equipping one with the needed strength to move on. This hope is what we find in Christ Jesus, and when the Lord comes He brings to us inexpressible springs of joy and peace. The Spirit of God teaches us where to reach out for happiness, for contentment, for encouragement, and courage whenever life goes down the lane, may seem merely black and white, and even disoriented and all alone. The soul that trusts in the Lord will always revive in strength, will capture the best in memories, and radiate it as light to those around. May you be like this one. Amen

01/10/25, 09:03 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He found him in a desert land, and in the howling waste of the wilderness; he encircled him, he cared for him, he kept him as the apple of his eye’ (Deuteronomy 32:10)

It was when the people of God were enslaved in Egypt, when they were stripped of their privileges (as descendants of Joseph, the once Prime Minister of Egypt), when they were subjected to undue hardships that they cried out to God for redemption. Till then, the dream of the Promised land was almost forgotten by these people. They were accommodating to the inconveniences of their difficult living, not knowing that God envisioned a better plan for them. It is then that they realized the providence and love of God (for them) – while in the wilderness. The Song of Moses was a reminder God made to them who were just a few miles away from the promised land, to reminisce on how marvelously God cared for them as the apple of His eye all these years. A reminder to us (too) that all that has occurred in our life was predestined by the Lord and meant to be a means to a greater end. In itself, an event may seem meaningless (just a piece in the jigsaw) but when seen in its totality, everything counts. Jesus sees through every situation in life, He equips us for greener pastures, the paths ahead may seem rough, but take heart, it is all within His well-defined plan. Let each day teach us to trust the Lord even more. Amen

' I love those who love me........'(Proverbs 8:17)

The Wisdom that created the Universe and sustains it is Jesus. He speaks to you and me this morning, giving us the assurance that He cares for us, and provides for us each day. A list of graces He opens to all mankind who honour Him in word and deed -  (I)Riches, honour, enduring wealth, prosperity
(II) Fruit and yield that are more valuable than fine gold and silver 
(III) He prospers them and heaps up their treasures
(IV) Transforms knowledge to profound understanding in those who love Him
(V) To the one bowed by anxieties and worries of life, comfort and courage of conviction is the strength given
From one end of the earth to the other, the Lord seeks for the one who loves Him, to help, honour, and uplift Him....whatever be the anguish in his heart, the losses or unfulfilled dreams of his heart, the Lord will uphold and elevate Him in His due time. 04/10/25, 10:30 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today 

'May I never boast of anything except the Cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world' (Galatians 6:14)

When in Christ Jesus, all other priorities of earthly living orient themselves, meaning they get aligned  orderly to serve the purpose of life - the glorification of God. St Paul, an authority in the Laws of Moses, was once set to prove himself (docile and fervent to fulfill the Laws), but when Christ came to his life, he was never concerned of proving himself but in working relentlessly for the Lord and His kingdom. He was focused and true to his purpose, never again had to waste time and effort towards his objective, as the Lord worked with signs to confirm his authority. This gave Paul the conviction that every other thing was irrelevant before the message of His Saviour. This trust in the loving providence of Jesus, is what he exhorts us to upgrade to - a living faith. In the Cross of Jesus Christ is our cause for boasting, as he has accomplished for us all that we ask in prayer and by which we are made eligible to receive. Praise the Lord. Amen

05/10/25, 07:03 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Behold, the Lord God comes with might, and His arm rules for Him; behold His reward is with Him, and His recompense before Him’ (Isaiah 40:10)

The Lord comes to those who call His mighty name; to surround them with His love – that which is new every morning. He comes to fill each heart with graces that perpetuate growth. Even though it may at times seem to us that ‘the world’ around has given us a cold shoulder, the Lord God, ‘the Almighty’ does care for every word and deed of ours. And when comes, He comes to us not barehanded, but with His recompense, His reward - for all that we have gone through in life, all the tears of a lifetime, our contriteness expressed through overflowing mercy to others. Let us therefore learn to befriend the Lord Jesus, call to mind what pleases Him and His Father, let us yearn to be filled with the Spirit of God – that builds, unites, heals, and transforms. For the Lord rules; all the Created (however supreme) must pay homage to His Majesty. So let those hard of heart, learn to be tender, for when Jesus comes – all valleys will be raised up, every mountain shall be made low, rough ground will be levelled, and rugged places made plain. As we begin this Sunday, let us be thankful for all that the Lord has prepared for the day, let us praise, worship and above all, love Him for He deserves to be praised, honored, and revered. Amen

06/10/25, 09:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘So they picked up Jonah and hurled him into the sea, and the sea ceased from its raging. Then the men feared the Lord exceedingly, and they offered a sacrifice to the Lord and made vows’ (Jonah 2:15,16)

The one chosen by the Lord to preach to the Ninevites was Jonah. Even though he defied God’s command, running away from the Lord to the region of Tarshish (which was away from Nineveh), God’s plan did not fail. Jonah was appointed prophet/witness to the Gentiles (ones who did not know God) to convey the message from God that if they did not repent, destruction was impending. When God calls one to spread His Word, He accords him freedom to choose if he really desired to be a messenger or not. God never imposes upon the free will of any human being; He merely knocks at our door, asking for our readiness to take up our call (also) assuring that the one who is ‘willing’ will not just be rewarded, but will be heirs to greater/impeccable gifts or insights that are yet to be revealed to mankind. He will be made a witness to God’s glory and majesty to the world. This was what transpired to God’s prophet – Jonah. He who accepted God’s calling wholeheartedly, but failed to obey His ways (by trying to execute a plan that seemed just to him) was forced to surrender to God’s ways. So it would be to you and me, for whom the Lord has a predestined plan – an incredible one; whenever we feel superior to our Creator by disobeying Him and following the inclinations of our heart, He draws us back – at times it may be painful, unpleasant, and hard for us. But when we give up, He picks up from where we have fallen, draws the path ahead for us. What we would need to do is to submit to Him, oblige humbly before Him and pray – Your will be done, O Lord. Amen

‘He has come to the help of his servant Israel for He has remembered His promise of mercy, the promise He made to our Fathers, to Abraham and His children forever’ (Luke 1:54,55)

God came to the rescue of His people – Israel, to whom was promised a land of rest. This promise is open to everyone who believes in God, in what He has done for our sake. The Bible asks us - He who did not spare His own son but gave Him up for us all, how will He not also with Him graciously give us all things? His works (of redemption) were finished from the foundation of the world, but for mankind who dwell within constraints of time and space, it was enacted in history once and for all in Calvary. The promise made to Abraham and His children was fulfilled for all times to all those who believe in Jesus and His saving power. It was the Mother of Jesus, who stood by Him all through the pain and suffering He underwent to offer the sacrifice of self as a ransom into the hands of the Father. Therefore as we commemorate the feast of Our Lady of the Rosary today, may we seek the intercession of Mother Mary, for being faithful to serve and honor the Lord to the end. May we prepare ourselves to receive His mercy, to reciprocate this mercy to those around us, and to be bearers of His light to the time we meet Him in Eternity. Amen

08/10/25, 10:05 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And the Lord said, You pity the plant for which you did not labor, nor did you make it grow, which came into being in a night and perished in a night’ (Jonah 4:10)

At times, we too adopt this attitude of Jonah - exhibiting overt caution for things of least relevance while ignoring things of greater purport. We go about pleasing and convincing every person around, except ourselves and those entrusted to our care, believing that we could always have a second bite at the cherry. Jonah was headstrong in demonstrating his role as God’s prophet to the Chosen people, but when it came to the Ninevites (Israel’s greatest oppressors), it was detestation that overtook his prophetic role. This explained his intolerance and anger when they were spared by God (because in his opinion, they deserved to be punished). God revealed to Jonah his heart, that all must be saved, must be brought to the right understanding of good and evil. His message to each one of us is that God wills the transformation in every unrepentant person, that every heart be made contrite and faithful to God and his fellow beings. The more we are filled with the love of Jesus, the more we learn to be sensitive to others, to be tender and selfless. To be the aroma of Christ Jesus to this world, You and I have been called to. Let us pray for grace to overwhelm all the intolerance and hatred in us, to be peacemakers in a war-torn world. Amen

09/10/25, 09:43 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Your words have been hard against me, says the Lord….You have said it is vain to serve God. What is the profit of our keeping His charge …?’ (Malachi 3:13-14)

God censures His people on their harsh words and comments made about Him. Mankind was saved from the wrath of punishment, not because of their good deeds, but because of the mercy of God. We often tend to overlook the fact that we were redeemed by faith in Jesus. This faith, trust in Jesus, opens our hearts to be humane, to be thankful even for the little things of life. When things don’t transpire in accordance with our plans and schedules, at times, we fail to submit before God, asking of His will in our lives. Instead, we go about seeking help or advice from mortals who hardly understand our path ahead or know the upcoming events in our life’s journey. Often we end up taking wrong decisions not because we were too hasty, but because we were at crossroads (critical junctures in life) and there wasn’t any to consult with or whom we could rely on. These were circumstances created for us to seek the Lord with our whole heart. It was the Lord who led us to the brink; an opportunity He gave us to learn what it would be to be led by grace. But did we make good use of it? Or miss the opportune moment by accusing Him (implicating Him as the cause) for all the tribulations in life? Let us introspect, repent, and correct ourselves to be within the Book of remembrance of the Lord. They shall be mine, a treasured possession, I will spare them just as a father has compassion and spares his son, the Lord of hosts speaks of such a one. Amen

‘Let your adorning be the hidden person of the heart with the imperishable beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit, which in God’s sight is very precious’ (1Peter 3:4)

For a gentle and quiet spirit, we are called to pray for today (both men and women) while living in a world of noise, where all is ‘hat and no cattle’. Often, it becomes hard for us to identify those who really mean what they say. Thus, the Gospel warns us from exalting human beings or giving them glory that essentially belongs to God. You and I have been called to resemble or put on Christ and none other. Every human being, however unique in his abilities and purpose of life, can resemble Christ Jesus – the innate traits of love, forgiveness, compassion, trustworthiness…and this is what brings him closer to the heart of God and fellow beings. But this grooming into Christ Jesus can occur only with the works of God’s Spirit in us. Because though our Spirits may be willing, our flesh is still weak, bound to the things of this world. Jesus understands our design and helplessness, and therefore, He tells His disciples to pray for strength and endurance for change. Let this be a hallmark or resolution we take of where we see ourselves growing to from where we stand today. May our inner person grow and outdo its existing elegance through prayer and the working of the Holy Spirit, that it may find itself precious and delightful in the eyes of the Lord. Amen

‘For the desires of the flesh are against the Spirit, and the desires of the Spirit are against the flesh, for these are opposed to each other, to keep you from doing the things you want to do’ (Galatians 5:17)

Jesus knew the vulnerability of the human mind, which always leaned towards being independent, but not in its complete sense. True independence and freedom of the mind would only spring from its sense of doing its purpose. Whatever else one does will never give him/her a sense of content. Therefore, to his disciples, Jesus exhorted them to pray and unceasingly pray to ensure they do not miss their purpose by doing anything and everything. You and I are called to do those specific good deeds that were predestined for us when we were created by His Word, Jesus Christ. The desires of the flesh are always opposed to the desires of the Spirit of God in us. ‘Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering, for He who promised is faithful’ (Hebrews 10:23)

To sway, to doubt, to be churned in the whirlwind of uncertainties, is so usual for us ‘prisoners of hope’ as we call ourselves. The hope is that by His (Jesus’s) blood, we have confidence to approach the Holy of Holies; and for us through the curtain of His body (ripped apart in Calvary), a new and living pathway has been opened. But we are still prisoners, bound and unable to make a move because of the shackles that have chained us. The key to liberty is trusting in the Lord, holding fast to and declaring the promises He has commissioned by His sacrifice on the Cross. Not to look at those that surround you, the voices from the crowds that serve only to disappoint, do not fret over situations that only take the wind out of your sails. Our lives are firm in the Hands of none other than our Creator, who firmly knows what’s next. May all the impediments of a lifetime be the very reason for joy, unwavering faith, and lead the way to our destiny. In Jesus' name, Amen

14/10/25, 09:41 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For I long to see you, that I may impart to you some spiritual gift to strengthen you – that is, that we may be mutually encouraged by each other’s faith, both yours and mine’ (Romans 1:11,12)

Everyone longs for ‘encouragement’ - this extends not just to the material realms alone, but also to the spiritual arena. A healthy support system is what perpetuates progression and growth. It is one where one’s strengths are highlighted and appreciated, while at the same time, weaknesses are pinpointed for rectification. Therefore, every unit of society - beginning with ‘the family’ must mould itself into a wholesome support system for human dignity and values to thrive and be sustained. Each one of us has a responsibility to share the Gospel, the evangelical virtues of love, faith, and hope within our families. This doesn’t take place by default, but when each one of us (especially elders and parents) consciously devotes time, effort, and space for God’s Word and His kingdom every day. For God’s Word works in manifold marvellous ways, to teach, reprimand, advise, and train in righteousness. It is then that we obtain grace to mutually encourage ourselves in our faith, offer a shoulder to the one who is crying, strengthen the doubtful, and admit that we are all vulnerable human beings who are interdependent and need help. Paul longed to meet the believers in Christ in Rome, that they may benefit from his spiritual gifts and he be consoled in Christ with the encouragement of their progressing faith. This example he offers is valid for us too, in our homes, institutions, communities, and Churches, that enrichment in Christ’s love may help us overcome all imperfections (as existing today). God bless you. Amen

15/10/25, 10:19 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Therefore you have no excuse, O man, every one of you who judges. For in passing judgment on another, you condemn yourself, because you, the ‘judge’ practices the very same things’ (Romans 2:1)

St. Paul, while vehemently speaking about the sin of condemning others, adds that it is most despicable in the eyes of God. The more we have overlooked this sin, the more it has become part of our culture, and commonplace in our society. But can mere popularity or public approval neutralize the gravity of a sin? Firstly, as it opposes the principle of love (disregards differences in people and circumstances), secondly, as it is hypocritical, encouraging in the one who judges, a puffed-up unrealistic assumption of his uprightness; and thirdly, it hardens one’s heart (lack of the virtue of compassion), thereby incapacitating him from being receptive to grace that builds goodness in him. Finally, the one who indulges in judging another would end up doing the very same things he condemned in another. Therefore, as the generations of old have taught - let us be concerned about our ways, handle them to the best, and ask the Lord to shadow us with His power from above, to do every task assigned to us in solid perfection. Jesus, help us to be mindful of our ways and deeds, while also being considerate to those who seek help from us. Amen

‘God’s righteousness, in His ‘Divine forbearance’ He had passed over former sins. It was to show His righteousness at the ‘present time’, so that He might be just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus’ (Romans 3:25,26)

This justification is added grace that inhibits us from falling into sin, avoiding all situations that cause us to sin (sin of omission and commission), being more grateful to Jesus who has accomplished for us this privilege of reconciliation with the Father. Let us be obliged to God, for His goodness, His protection, His longing to see with Him through Jesus. Let us praise and glorify God, making every effort to offer all that we do for His greater glory, seeking to be blessed more by forgiving others who have impeded our good, for this is what the Lord expects from us – To be merciful as the Heavenly Father is. Amen

17/10/25, 09:42 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘However, to the one who does not work but trusts God who justifies the ungodly, their faith is counted as righteousness’ (Romans 4:5)

To believe in God would mean to trust Him, that His plans for us are always for our goodness. You and I may at times be wrong – we may make hasty decisions, may misunderstand, fail to champion a just cause, or even utter discouraging words. We are mere mortals, susceptible to falling off, even when we consider ourselves ‘firm’ and secure on the heights, we must admit. Therefore, when things seem like falling ‘way’ apart in life, let us learn to surrender, to let go of our plans, and humble ourselves to accept that He has a way. This one is justified by God, whom God certifies as ‘the faithful one’, who leans not merely on the inclinations of his intellect, but accepts his frailty before his Creator. To the world, he may seem ‘foolish’, but before the Lord, he becomes the wise man who built his house on the ‘rock.’ The rains lashed, the floods overwhelmed, but his house was on a firm footing, because it was built on the rock Jesus Christ. Thus was Abraham, the father of all believers. He left the security of his home, hometown, his people, and land on to a land he till then never knew about, because he trusted in the ‘one’ who called him, believing that he had designed for him a city most habitable. And it was granted to him as he wished, not just to him, but to numerous generations from then on, to all who exhibited this trait of trusting in the Lord and obeying his will. May you be one among them, the blessed of the Lord. Amen

21/10/25, 07:55 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Blessed are those servants whom the master finds awake when he comes; truly I say to you, He will gird himself and have them sit at the table, and he will come and serve them’ (Luke 12:37)

To keep awake is what our master expects of us, having entrusted us with so great tasks to be fulfilled in this life’s journey. At times we fail to understand the trust reposed in us by the Lord Jesus as He has called us, anointed, and sanctified each for his specific tasks. To all those He called, He sanctified, justified and equipped them with gifts to meet their tasks. We tend to forget and ignore the fact that it is God who has called us and set us forth to our respective domains on earth. To give Him glory by performing our responsibilities with due diligence, in accordance with His law of love, and to ask Him for needed gifts to endure when things go wrong, is how we prove ourselves faithful before our Master. Not to be swayed by what the world tells us, because of our purpose, and design, it knows not and hardly cares for. One who accepts God’s diktats in life is also one who is humble, who has no time to waste or to ponder on finding faults with people and things around. He asks for grace to be renewed and rebuilt to move on in life, amidst the difficulties and obscurities that he may encounter on the way. Most important he is thankful to the Lord for His unimaginable love for him, and this encourages him to keep awake through the watches of the day, to be found faithful and blessed. The reward for his works is incredibly high but also sure thing – when he comes, He will gird himself, have us sit at His table of delights and He (Jesus, our Lord) will come and serve us. Lord, transform me into your most loved servant. Amen

22/10/25, 08:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Do not present your members to sin as instruments for unrighteousness, but present yourselves to God as those who have been brought from death to life and your members to God as instruments of righteousness’ (Romans 6:13)

God leaves us a choice to discern and understand that we who were once dead were brought into life through Jesus Christ. This realization must prompt us to submit to Grace, than to lean on our strengths. Because where sin reigned, where there was no relenting, Jesus redeemed us from its clutches by pouring out grace upon grace, that our human nature may be overwhelmed and taken forward by obedience to the promptings of God’s Spirit in us. This anointing in grace didn’t take place by mere words, but by His example of submission to the will of His Father, by the sacrifice of self on the Cross. Difficulties and sorrows have power to rule over the human mind, leaving us prisoners of fate, but not so any more, as Jesus has shown us the way, by enduring on the Cross, till the goal was attained – the Salvation of mankind, liberation from the strongholds of death to that of life. There is much more we are called to do, as believers, as disciples of the Lord – to work out our Salvation with endurance, with determination to do all that God has asked of us. It is through obedience to what Jesus has asked of us that we elevate ourselves to receive grace, not by waiting upon the words and ways of people around us. So let us pray to the Lord for more strength, power to belong to Him, no matter how tiring the journey has been along the way, no matter how disappointing things may at times seem to the human eye. He knows well what needs to be done and what can transform us to the image He created us, into the perfection He has called you for. As it is for slaves who look at their master, so do we do, Lord, wait upon you, until you come and take control. Amen

23/10/25, 09:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Nothing in all creation is hidden from God’s sight. Everything is uncovered and laid bare before the eyes of Him to whom we must give account’ (Hebrews 4:13)

Unlike what we may be prone to thinking, no one can manoeuvre to change the plans and promises of God his own way. When David, the King of Israel, tried to cover up for his sins (of adultery and murder), God sent Nathan the prophet to confront him. The king had no escape but to admit and bear the consequences of his misdeeds. When Jacob the Patriarch deceived his brother and eloped with the blessings of his father, God pursued him all the way; for 21 ‘long years’ he had no rest and reprieve until he repented and asked forgiveness from his brother. When Abraham sent Ismail (his son from the servant girl Hagar) away with his mother barehanded, he was subject to a similar situation of having to abandon his son (Isaac, the son of promise) to death to prove his faithfulness before God. Everything is laid bare before the eyes of God; it is to the Lord that we will all have to give an account – for our words, deeds, motives, and thoughts. He judges by the yardstick of ‘love’, the measure we have invested in justice, equity, fairness, trustworthiness in every event and eventuality will be His concern. Therefore, whenever things don’t seem fair to us, let us also discern those circumstances in life when we were partial, unjust and insincere to those given to our care. How many times have we demonstrated wit over wisdom, over love, over selflessness and pride. Let us ask the Lord forgiveness, and openness to receive wisdom from Him that teaches us to be obedient to His ways and be more humane. Amen

24/10/25, 08:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?’ (Luke 12:57)

‘Do you not know that you and I have died to the Law through the Body of Christ, so that we may belong to Him, who was raised from the dead, that we may bear fruit’, St Paul reminds us of why Jesus asked His disciples the question of right judgment. For discernment of right from wrong, we must ask of the Lord, that our choices may align with the tenets of His kingdom. King Solomon was regarded wise because he asked for this capability – for the gift of discernment and right judgment. Not only did God bless Him abundantly with wisdom, but also with peace in his kingdom that followed righteousness. At times, we yearn for peace in our walks of life – homes, institutions, communities, societies, and the world at large. It is God who gives peace and the Bible reminds us that Jesus is our peace, the One who broke all barriers of division and reconciled entire mankind to the Heavenly Father through His sacrifice of self. Now than ever more, the world dreads and mourns conflicts, wars, and their after effects on human development. The Lord comes and asks each one of us – why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right? If only we had the audacity to submit to His will, to humbly ask to be led by His Spirit, how much would we be contributing to righteousness, to peace, and to God’s dream for humanity. Towards respect, dignity of self, uprightness of the mind and spirit, towards humaneness, we have been called to co-create, not add to the hate, rebellion, and turmoil, that poses hazards to our every existence. Lord Jesus, open the eyes of our hearts to see what you envision about us, to hear the voice of Your Spirit in us, and to be your messengers of hope in the world. Amen

27/10/25, 09:52 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Lord says, I will bring them from Bashan; I will bring them from the depths of the sea’ (Psalm 68:22)

One of the significant victories that Israelites made under the leadership of Moses, was the defeat of King Og of Bashan. Known for the Rephaim’s the giant warriors of those days, the land of Bashan was indeed a formidable place to be won over. But the Lord gave this land with its fortunes over to the Israelites, because they trusted in Him and moved according to His will. They were a people enslaved by the Egyptians, but when God liberated His people and led them over to a promised land, there were great miracles that occurred through the hands of these people. Those nations that oppressed them were defeated utterly, their spoils taken, the land they trod became theirs as was promised to them by the Lord. Not because of the strength of the warriors among them, in fact these people had the slightest of training in wars and weapons. It was God’s Spirit that empowered these people to move on beyond the wilderness and enter the promised land without fail. Even the Red sea was not a deterrent to these people who were able to cross over as if on dry land and march ahead. History repeats itself to this day, when you and I believe that it is the Lord who is with us, leading us through, making firm our footsteps, and ensuring that we reach our destination, falling short of nothing. Let us thank the Lord for His protection, inspiration, and love for us. Wishing you a blessed Monday. Amen

30/10/25, 07:25 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘If God is for us, who can be against us?’ (Romans 8:31)

One with a load of optimism, of hope to a believer in Jesus Christ, chapter 8 of St. Paul’s Letter to the Romans begins with the most appealing assurance to the one in Christ. It affirms that to the one who is in Christ Jesus, there is no more condemnation, because he (the one with Christ) is led by the Spirit of Jesus. The profundity of this spiritual truth was known to King David, who prayed that he be not cast away from God’s presence and God’s dwelling in him remain, even though he was corrupted by sin. When weighed down by sinfulness, David still hoped in God’s greatness and His magnanimity to save and redeem. It is this that the Lord asks of you and me today, who proclaim our faith in Jesus Christ. Even though there have been omissions and sins of commission in our life, we hold on to the Lord with a heart of repentance, asking Him for forgiveness and for the grace to conquer everything that pulls us down. It is thus that the Holy Spirit comforts us that for this one whose heart delights in the Lord, there will no longer be a condemnation. For Jesus, who now sits at the right hand of the Father in Heaven, our High Priest, intercedes for our righteousness and rebuilds us from all our ruins. Befriend the Lord Jesus, plead to be filled with His Spirit, the power you need to make a mark in this world, being yourself. Amen

This is to inform you that all students must complete a compulsory internship after the 2nd Year during the year holidays.

🎓 After your 4th Even Semester End Exams, you are required to do a Summer Offline Internship for 30 days.
This internship aims to provide industrial exposure and practical learning experience.

🔍 Important Tip:
Start searching for a reputed company for your internship by the beginning of December.
During that time, students from all over India begin reaching out to companies for internship opportunities and registrations.
So, starting early will help you secure a good company and avoid last-minute rush. 💼✨

📄 Internship Letter Application:
A notification will be sent on your KP Portal around January or February regarding Internship Letter Registration.




You will have two options to choose:

1️⃣ Whomsoever Letter – For internships in companies/industries of your friends, relatives, or family connections.

2️⃣ Company-Specific Letter – For internships in a particular company where you have applied or wish to apply.

📝 Details to be filled in KP Portal for Company-Specific Letter
Contact Person
Designation
Contact Email
Contact Mobile Number
Company Name
Company Address

⏰ Note: There will be a deadline to fill in the internship details.
✅ All students must complete the process before the deadline.

📬 After Submission:

The HOD will verify and approve your application in the system.

Once approved, collect your Internship Letter from the Placement Office:
📍 Block 3, 1st Floor (Opposite to Bio Lab, Room No. 317)

Then, get it signed by the International Affairs Department (📍 Block 1, inside the Bank).


Safely keep the Internship Letter from the University 
Take a photo of the Letter as your backup.



After completing your 30 Days Internship coming back to college in 3rd Year 

📝 You need to prepare your Internship Report and Presentation. Your University Mentor may ask you to submit the report duly signed by the HOD.

🎓 You must now prepare your Internship Report in the proper format — you can get the report format from your assigned University Mentor.

31/10/25, 11:48 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘They are Israelites, to them belong the adoption, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises. (Romans 9:4)

Now, if we were to take this Word of God in the context of our oneness with Christ Jesus, then what more do we need to live a life of faithfulness to God? Many a times, the crux of the message the Holy Spirit intends to convey to us is impaired because of its reference to the Israel of the Old Testament and not us. To each one of us who has received Jesus as our Lord and Master in baptism, or by faith – a great entitlement has been bequeathed to us – Sonship in God’s family, Glory of Divinity, Covenantal relationship with God, the Ten Commandments all boiling down to the Law of love, worship of the One and only God, the promises both of this world and of eternity. We hardly realize, and even if we do, we fail to exercise or make use of them, because of our sinfulness that distances us. It is our habituation to certain sins – pride that builds in us hardness of heart, preventing us from acknowledging the truth and standing by it. Instead, we iconise ourselves as righteous, more worthy, and indulge in judging others, thereby assuming God’s place. Virtues of mercy, compassion, and understanding become severely deficient in our lives. God asks us to look into ourselves, our innermost selves, to pray for the grace of knowing who we are; to acknowledge our mistakes, make amends wherever it may be needed of us, and stay humble to be worthy of the blessings that He has laid in store for us. In Jesus name, we pray. Amen

‘For this imperishable body must put on the imperishable, and this mortal body must put on immortality’ (1 Corinthians 15:53)

To adorn the imperishable and the immortal body is what we are purposed towards in this life’s journey. The month of November, specially dedicated to remind us of this obligation we have to raise our thoughts to the ethereal. For flesh and blood can not inherit the Kingdom of God. If children of God, we have a great inheritance, but what we will become is yet to be unveiled. It is through the Heavenly fire (the Holy Spirit) that we will all be tested, and bound to give an account for. None can claim to have already achieved the criteria of perfection to be in the Heavenly Kingdom, but each day we work towards obtaining perfection inch by inch. Therefore, while on earth, we must seek to be purified by God’s Spirit. In all that we go through as of today, the sufferings of the past, the pain and afflictions of a lifetime, these can be offered to the Lord as reparation for the many imperfections in life. Let us also pray for the souls who seek our prayers for this process of refinement. Day 1 – Let us offer and make reparation for all sins of the tongue that we have committed and for those souls in purgatory. Cleanse us O Holy Spirit! fill us with repentance, accept our offerings and prayers, open the doors of Heaven to us. Have mercy on us. Amen

02/11/25, 07:20 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘In the year that King Uzziah died I saw the Lord sitting upon a throne, high and lifted up, and the train of His robe filled the temple’ (Isaiah 6:1)

Specific events or occurrences in life (good and bad) have a lasting impact upon our hearts and minds, for these trigger in us a sense of sudden realization of certain realities of life, which though were existent were in deep slumber in our hearts. You and I have encountered similar situations of the sort in life that proved to be turning points later. These were revealed to us by the Lord Himself and were meant for a greater purpose. But the shock these may have created may evade us of the calmness and serenity that till then ruled our hearts. The prophet Isaiah came across a similar kind of realization when he was in the temple, at a time after the death of the glorious King Uzziah. A deep anguish took over his heart, mind, and spirit – creating a chasm of gloom, of anxiety, and fear of what would occur then. This vision of the Lord sitting upon the throne, lifted high, and the train of His robe filling the entire temple was a healing to the prophet, from the depths of anxiety, worries, depression, and overwhelming fear. His fears were not unreal, because He knew what was to fall upon God’s people (nearing of their exile), but God’s suzerainty over everything – events, circumstances, and people was a daring reality that wiped away all negativity from his heart. He thus offers himself to God, as a witness to be His voice for the people, to comfort, console, build up and redeem them from a possible state of hopelessness. You and I are called to take the place of Isaiah, to pray for God’s people, for those given to our care, for those departed from our midst. Let our prayers and conviction be the cause of their upliftment. In Jesus name, wishing you a great Sunday. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

03/11/25, 09:47 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For the gifts and the calling of God are irrevocable’ (Romans 11:29)

The twelve apostles were chosen by Jesus, not because they were extraordinary, but because Jesus loved them (the way they were). All who were called were justified by Him, were sanctified by His Word, entrusted with the task of proclaiming the Gospel message, and were glorified. God (The Father) foreknew them and predestined them to conform to the image of His son, Jesus. As Jesus ascended into Heaven after His resurrection, He took with Him captives of men and bestowed upon them gifts. The gifts and the calling of God are irrevocable, the Bible teaches us. This means that each one will be accountable for the gifts received from the Lord. The gifts would include health, wealth, knowledge, influence, and the greatest of them all - The Holy Spirit. Have we built upon these gifts for the glory of God? - the test of fire we would all be forced to refine ourselves through? None can exclude himself/herself from being tested in this way. It is not in the intensity or magnitude of success that would be our worthiness criteria, but in all that we did, exerted ourselves into, the courage and conviction we showed in coming back even after failing time and again. St Paul warns us that let none of us be conceited by words, but let grace overshadow us. For, as it is to the Israelites, who were ‘the Chosen’ of God, they are still loved on account of the faithfulness of the Patriarchs of Old. There will be a time when these will also be wholly saved through God’s ways. Let us not judge and condemn, but pray for those who are lacking in faith; let us offer the hardships of today for a greater cause – for saving souls of lesser faith, that in Jesus our light may never fail. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

04/11/25, 10:16 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For as in one body we have many members, and the members do not all have the same function, so we, though many, are one body in Christ, and individually members one of another’ (Romans 12:4,5)

The Holy Spirit exhorts us to put on Christ, exhibit the characteristics of a true follower of Jesus Christ by expressing genuine love. It was the Lord Jesus, His sacrifice on the Cross, and His mercy that bestowed us with gifts of varied purpose, intent, and magnitude. Not all have the same gifts: some of us are blessed with the charism of prophesying, some to serve, some to teach and exhort, some to inspire and encourage, some to give generously, and again few more with compassion. We are members of Christ’s body; we exist for one another, and this is how we perpetuate ‘abundance of life’ - the crux of the Gospel message. If one member is in pain, it impacts the entire body in some way or the other. The way of the world may be to be oblivious to the have-nots, the poor, the minorities, the marginalized, the afflicted – to have one’s way by hook or crook, to impose one’s superiority over the other. This cannot be our way, because we are one in Christ. You and I have been called to honour one another, to be devoted to those in need, to cling to the good, to be zealous for the Lord, joyful in hope, patient in suffering, faithful in prayer, hospitable always. Let us ask forgiveness from the Lord for the times we have not been faithful in word and deed, for the sins of commission and omission through which we failed to honour one another. Let us offer our prayers and reparations this day, for the souls in purgatory who may have also committed sins of the kind. Holy Spirit, enlighten our minds, hearts and Spirits to love Jesus and honour this Word from Him. Amen

05/11/25, 10:02 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Owe no one anything, except to love each other, for the one who loves another has fulfilled the Law’ (Romans 13:8)

The Commandment Jesus gave us is to love one another. He sent us into the world to ensure that we may demonstrate to a cold, rigid world the meaning of love. To love would mean to do no wrong to another (Romans 13:10); to love would mean to limit our words or to maintain silence when our actions and intent go beyond what words can clarify (Matthew 5:37); to accept the garb of an unworthy servant after having done everything (Luke 17:10). Love would lessen one’s tendency to be arrogant, to be irritable and resentful when things don’t work our way (1Cor 13:4-5). Love would inevitably encompass qualities of inclusiveness, forbearance, hope, and faith (1 Cor 13:7). When compared to all the virtues a human could practice in his lifetime, if devoid of love, it would mean nothing. Do we really own this sort of trait to love? This is what the Holy Spirit asks of us today. Let all our words, motives, and deeds be judged within the framework of this kind of love. Do I walk the way of love? Of course, since I have been designed in the image of God, my Father, I have been created to love, but have I expressed my resolve to love as the Lord has loved me? Lord, give me the grace to be compassionate, to love, and serve as you have been to me all the while. Amen

06/11/25, 09:38 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘One thing have I asked of the Lord, that will I seek after: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life…’ (Psalm 27:4)

To dwell in the house of the Lord – to live with the Lord, not merely when we are at a place of worship, but at every moment of life. To be able to converse with Him as with a friend, to open up to the Lord those hidden within the secret chambers of our heart, to ask Him for counsel, for advice, to be able to allay our fears when before Him, this is what King David meant to dwell in the house of the Lord. This experience of closeness/proximity with the Lord was what made the King distinct and counted as the first among the greatest of all Kings in history. David worshipped the Lord at the place of the tabernacle (the then living presence of God in the Old Testament), but even more honoured Him in his palace, while making significant decisions for the people, while fighting wars against enemies, and even when publicly acclaiming his folly and unworthiness in mistrusting God at times in life. He knew that it was far ‘much’ better to surrender himself to the wrath of God (seeking mercy from Him) than striving to please human beings. Thus, his strategies and decisions (political, economic, societal, welfare measures) were made putting God first, and for giving glory to God’s Holiness. Theoretically, we, ‘the Israel’ of the New Testament, know that ‘apart from Jesus’ we can do nothing, but have we been able to demonstrate and prove this conviction of ours in the perspectives of daily life? Let us ask ourselves, as to what drives the hope in us – positivity of things around, or the trust in the Lord that, at the time of need, He would pour upon us grace and mercy to prevail over all adversities in life. May we seek after you Jesus, all the days of our life. Amen

07/11/25, 09:35 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I will not venture to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me to bring the Gentiles to obedience – by word and deed’ (Romans 15:18)

All that he was able to do for God’s Kingdom was what distinguished Paul from the rest of the apostles. His birth as a Pharisee, from an elite background (unlike other disciples of Jesus), and being fortunate enough to be educated in the Laws from Gamaliel, the Elder Rabi were indeed vivid and colourful feathers to his cap, but not till he met and experienced Jesus, his Saviour. There came about a paradigm shift in his perspective towards life soon, and his immediate next steps were to secure his position as a fervent minister of the Lord. His efforts in introducing Christ Jesus to the Gentiles, in establishing communities of believers through his missionary journeys through Asia Minor, Europe (cities like Philippi, Thessalonica, Athens, etc.), and Rome were undoubtedly great successes. Paul acknowledges that everything he did from then on – whatever he spoke or argued for this cause was perfected by signs and wonders, and accomplished by the Lord Himself. A lesson to us, who keep boasting on the many blessings in life, as though they were not graces bestowed on us by the Lord’s mercy. And because of this, we fail to show compassion towards another, to understand another, or to think from his/her perspective rather than judging. Lord Jesus, help me to appreciate and understand things of real value in life (from your point of view) and work towards fulfilling them, so that I also attain the distinction and confirmation that you bestow on your favoured ones. Amen

08/11/25, 10:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Lord is gracious and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love’ (Psalm 145:8)

The marvels of the Lord (signs and wonders) emerge from this nature of His towards us. Though we are unworthy, He looks at us with mercy and compassion, like a father to his child, He pours upon us the graces that we need to sustain ourselves. Like an eagle who stirs its nest to prepare its little ones for flight, the Lord prepares us for our daily battles, equipping us with strength, making our ways blameless, and giving us a broad footing that we may not fall. Generation after another may commend your works and declare your greatness, David prayed, meditating upon the magnanimity of God. Even when he was failing and falling, the King knew the source of His revival and what could make a drastic difference in life. So he counted on the graciousness of God, His steadfast love towards Him, and pleaded for His mercy to overwhelm Him. So must we too do, we who have seen and experienced the wonders of the Lord to this day. It is the Lord who has preserved us to this day, protected us from all possible harms, follies, and misfortunes, and led us through His path for His name’s sake. Let us therefore pray, Jesus, I praise you, adore you, and thank you for this day, for the gift of life, and for all that you intend to teach me this day. Broaden my intellect, understanding, and my horizons for growth. Amen

We who wait for the Lord; we who receive Him in the Eucharist daily; to us, the Holy Spirit asks for an introspection – Do we approach the Lord with a lowly heart? Do we admit the various sins we have knowingly or unknowingly committed? Do we justify ourselves as the ‘Pharisee’ in the parable of ‘the Pharisee and tax collector’ who prayed in the temple? Do we still nurture in our minds ill-will and hate feelings towards people around us? 12/11/25, 09:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For the Ruler of all shows no partiality, nor does He fear greatness, because He himself made the great as well as the small, and provides for all alike’ (Wisdom 6:7)

He provides for all alike, and on this the Spirit of God draws our attention to, as we begin a new day. If the Ruler of all - Lord Jesus has provided for all alike, how then do I still see the disparities of all sorts around? We often ask ourselves this question, failing to understand that as ministers of His Kingdom, You and I have been entrusted with the task of bridging the gaps. The divide between the haves and the have-nots, the divide between the skilled and unskilled, intelligent and ignorant, the affluent and the marginalized……..the list goes on and on. It’s not everywhere that we have been assigned authority and power, but each has his/her own realm or sphere of authority, where he has been entrusted to rule and empower. How faithful have we been in those little areas? Have we been cowed by the fear of the great and belittled the small? Have we shown the guts to open up and speak the truth in the face of opposition or disagreement? Have we watered down the segmentation between right and wrong merely to please the egos of the superior or influential? If so, we will have to encounter a severe judgment from the ruler of all. The mighty (those who lorded over many because of their position) shall be put to the test ‘mightily’, but the lowly will receive pardon because they were merciful. Let us ask the Lord to fill us with wisdom to discern what He wills of us, not what we have learned from the ways of the world, or from public opinion, but to concur with what we have been created to do and be. For we have been created for Eternity and not for this world. Amen

13/11/25, 08:13 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘May God grant me to speak with judgment and to have thoughts worthy of what I have received, for He is guide even of wisdom and the corrector of the wise’ (Wisdom 7:15)

At times, our moves and ways are governed not by the wisdom from above, but by earthly wisdom – the events, occurrences, and beliefs that have summed up who we are. To some to be able to reconcile with the self is the most herculean task (if not impossible) than any other. The mind keeps seeking answers to questions that are way beyond the interpretation of the intellect or senses. It is only this one who has mastered the skills of looking beyond who will succeed to swim against the tides in troubled waters. Unlike what men may think, this essential life skill doesn’t get triggered merely by human effort or the power of the mind (determination). It is wisdom from above, His power, that hones our Spirit to look beyond, to find meaning in turbulent seas, to speak words of comfort, to harbour thoughts of peace, and gain the conviction to unlearn what one has learned. This is the work of grace, and to love, to live, to forgive, to undo, and redo correctly, we are all in need of grace, more than anything else. Just as light will have to give way to darkness, so will every other strength fail in the face of crisis. It is trust in the overarching accompaniment of the Lord, in His wisdom that created and sustains everything, that will drive us to our destiny unharmed. Therefore, let us pray, Lord grant me your wisdom, that I may speak with judgment, and have thoughts worthy and thankful for all that You are doing for me. Amen

nor will they say, ‘Look, here it is!’ or ‘There it is!’ For, in fact, the kingdom of God is among you.
(Luke 17: 21)

As christians our ultimate goal in life is to attain 'eternal life' and to dwell with the Father in His kingdom. In today's verse, Jesus unfurls this divine mystery.

When the Pharisees asked Jesus when the 'Kingdom of  God' would come, they expected a political leader who would gather all the 12 tribes of Israel and overthrow the Roman rulers, to restore Israel's lost glory. Their minds were so confined to the world that they failed to realise what 'God's kingdom' truly meant. Jesus clarifies this, by stating that the Kingdom of God is not something external, but it is within us- it is in the purity of our souls. 
In today's context, this verse holds significant importance. Like the Pharisees, we too forget the importance of our 'inner man: our soul'. Today Jesus reminds us that the Kingdom of God is in our hearts, the moment we truly change from within, we have achieved His kingdom and will be worthy to be called as His children.

Wishing you a fruitful Friday.
God bless you.

14/11/25, 10:04 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Let them know how far more excellent is the Lord than these; for the ‘original’ source of beauty fashioned them. If they were struck by their might and energy, let them realize from these things how much more powerful is the one who made them’ (Wisdom 13:3,4)

The Author of Beauty (used in Revised Standard Version Biblical text) is the name given to God, the One who fashioned this world and all created. I happened to read about the new ‘Tesla Cybertruck’, an Electric utility truck, with far more features and with its ‘elegant’/ ‘sharp’ looks – for the Automobile industry, this has opened doors for more innovation, creativity, with a futuristic utilitarian outlook. Likewise, the coming up of several humanoids – Optimus Gen2, Vyommitra (India), Generative AI agents, Quantum drug discoveries ……. have left us dumbstruck contemplating on these wonders of sorts, world over, on how the human brain has been discovering and helping renew and reshape lives of human beings on earth. If so, how much more excellent and powerful would be the One who created the human mind? This is what the Holy Spirit intends to bring to our understanding: no matter how much does, each of these revelations in Science and Technology innovates for us, we will be stalled/stuck somewhere until the Author of Beauty, our Lord decides to decipher to us.  He created everything that men may benefit from them and glorify His name. You and I have been called to co-create with the Lord, to make the world a better place to live in (with respect, integrity, and human dignity intact) rather than sitting idly and denouncing the deeds of another. Even the most ordinary or mediocre task, will be reckoned part of this co-creation, provided it is done with a sincere and faithful heart. Lord increase our skills and our trust in You. Amen
