‘For a blameless man was quick to act as their champion; he brought forward the shield of his ministry, prayer, and propitiation by incense; he withstood the anger and put an end to the disaster, showing that he was your servant’ (Wisdom 18:21)

Because of what the Israelites spoke, because of their rebellion and hardheartedness, they blasphemed, questioned Moses, the one appointed by God to lead them. Thus, what happened to them explains the context of today’s Word from the Lord. A plague came upon the multitudes while in the desert, and many of them succumbed to death. It may be due to their struggles while in the desert, the odds and oddities they had to put up with for a very long time that provoked such a kind of anguish, an emotional outburst in them. But even at this moment, the mercy of God was poured upon them, by the intercessory prayers and propitiations made by Aaron, the High Priest. As a priest, Aaron exercised his ministry and obligation to pray for the salvation of God’s people, to see them subdue the great crisis they encountered in their journey, and walk with the strength of God, on to the promised land. The Bible upholds him as ‘righteous’, ‘a champion’, because he stood between God and His people (as was his obligation), as a mediator to speak for them, to champion their cause and to gain their favour before the Most High. This he did not by demonstrating human intelligence, or flexing himself before God and man, but by appealing words before the Throne of Mercy, to shower mercy upon the people of Oath/the Covenantal people. You and I are called to offer ourselves as priests, for interceding for the people given to our care. We are called to be champions for those near and dear to the Lord – for those who are ignorant of God’s mercy, for those who are drenched in despondency, unable to turn to God, for those who are overwhelmed by burdens and afflictions……Do we sincerely exercise this ministry of ours before God? Do we yearn to lessen their sorrows? Or are we merely protagonists of self-righteousness, displeasing God, and condemning our neighbours? Let us ponder and repent. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

Why do you call me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ and do not do what I say?
(Luke 6: 46)

In today's verse, the message is simple and clear. God asks us this simple question implying that it is not in the eloquence of our words rather in our actions and deeds that He is pleased. He calls out on the futility of verbal profession.

In John 14: 15, Jesus says, 'If you love me, you will keep my commandments'. It's a fact that loving Jesus means to obey all of His words and commandments. God looks into our hearts, a heart driven by the love of God will be able to obey and understand the sanctity of His words.

Let us all pray for the grace to lead a life in accordance with His words, thus proclaiming our love for Him.

17/11/25, 10:24 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘In those days certain renegades came out from Israel and misled many, saying, ‘Let us go and make a Covenant with the nations around us, for since we separated from them many disasters have come upon us’ (1 Maccabees 1:11)

There were renegades even in those times – the Hellenistic period, when Greek culture and civilization began to spread worldwide because of the Alexandrian conquest. But historians consider this era as the end phase of the Greek dominion of the world: fragmentation of the Alexandrian empire, evil descendants to the throne, their assumption of sovereignty (till then they were mere Generals of Alexander) led to the erosion of humanitarian culture, politics, philosophy, and achievements so far made to the world by the Greeks. In no time, animosity, rivalries, and tyranny was the rule - innocents were put to death, greed spread like wild-fire, and many other cultures were impacted. It was in this chaotic scenario there arose a group of apostates – renegades, who believed in compromising with the culture of the land, to imitate the ways of nations around, to evade the disasters that accompanied the political disruptions. None of these were able to make an impact either in their own lives, or in the lives of those who were influenced and lured to their thought process. However history underlines the beginning of a revolt that started to take place soon – which had far-fetching consequences – The Maccabean revolt. A people who were true to their identity, sincere to the Divine Law, obedient to the will of God, which finally secured for them their temple, its rededication, revival of worship, and a peaceful living. Our takeaway from this Scriptural passage is to be hold firm to what we have been trained, taught, and testified over the years by the Lord. No matter how difficult things turn out for us, the kind of noises that surround, the notion of being all alone in a dreaded sea of turmoil, so long as the Lord is with you, You hold the chance not just for survival but to be the one who caused it for many around you. To be the ‘Grape-picker who though late filled the winepress’ is our call. Amen

This God- his way is perfect; the promise of the Lord proves true; he is a shield for all who take refuge in him.
(2 Samuel 22: 31)

This is a verse from king David’s song of Thanksgiving in 2 Samuel chapter 22, in this passage David expresses his incomprehensible joy and thanksgiving to God who had delivered him from all his enemies and from the hands of king Saul. Here David describes three characteristics of God:
1.	Absolutely perfect,
2.	Trustworthy, and
3.	Powerful protector.
Today’s verse is a reminder that just as our almighty, perfect, trustworthy God lifted David a mere shepherd boy to the greatest king of Israel, so will He lift each one of us to a greater glory. Just as how God saw David’s heart and chose him, guided him and protected him, so will he also guide us and protect us. Let us keep loving Him and lead a life that pleases His holy sight.

18/11/25, 10:29 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘……. Him, who is able to keep you from stumbling and to present you before His glorious presence without fault and with great joy -’ (Jude 1:24)

It is He alone (Jesus) who can keep you and me from stumbling, not any other person, institution, or presence whatever. Therefore, it is to Him that we owe our faithfulness, our obedience, and trust; to Him must we submit our will, intellect, skills, our whole being. What He urges us must be our call and purpose in life. It is so easy to feel disarrayed, disoriented, and cold in life – people, circumstances, and everything around may seem to be pointing fingers on us; our convictions, and at times our very words may be misread and questioned. In fact, the very reason positions, leadership, and power were created was to rescue men from sinking in the mires of dejection, dismay, or discontent, and to empower them towards a life of dignity. Contrary to the ground reality, what we see around – many stumble, fail, and even fall, not because they were inept to live, but because the very hands designated to protect brought down their very fall. Let this message ring loud and clear that each one of us has a responsibility, whether we accept it or not; we have been gifted life to support, build, and protect not just our boundaries, but also of others. This is what the Lord meant, when He asked us to be merciful – to the poor, the needy, the widow, the orphan – not merely in its literary sense, but in its profundity. May my gestures, the words of my mouth, and the meditation of my heart always be pleasing to you Lord God, my refuge and strength. Amen

19/11/25, 09:40 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘A nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom and then return. Calling ten of his servants, he gave them ten minas, and said to them, ‘Engage in business until I come’ (Luke 19:12,13)

Jesus, as he said this parable to the people gathered at and around the house of Zacchaeus, was counting his days on earth. He was nearing Jerusalem, and the people believed that the kingdom of God was soon to be established by Him. This was how he began the parable – A nobleman was about to journey to a far country to receive for himself the Kingship of the land. His nobility, resoluteness, and diligence are proven in him entrusting his 10 minas (coins/ treasures) to ten servants of his choice. The purpose he gave them these minas was (i) to ensure their safety; (ii) the acceleration of their value; and (ii) the return on investments made use for the welfare of His future kingdom. He expected those who were entrusted with this wealth would be aware of their stewardship roles and responsibilities and support Him with the same bend of mind as His. When he came back, the same servants who had the minas were called back to give an account of the money. The first of them proved himself worthy by giving back ten times what he was given. The second one gave back five which he managed to enhance in place of the one given to him. Now the third servant, not that he was not aware of his duty as a steward to safeguard, but because he was probably ignorant, or puffed with pride, or a wounded/deceived person, could never think beyond his convictions, held firm to his ego and way of living, thus fearing the possibility of losing the treasure, he held fast to it, rather than exploring possibilities to magnify it. He was condemned by his master, the passage continues. For us, who live in a totally imperfect world, the Lord understands what it means to make a mark, He knew perfectly well what it meant to be betrayed, deceived, abandoned, and lost in a swift world. Therefore, He demands nothing much of us, except that we trust in Him, and take every stride this day with confidence, with commitment, and with unwavering trust in Him, to obtain the result. However routine, mundane, and uninteresting may be the demands of your day, do it to glorify the One who has entrusted this to you. Amen

"Shadow Identity Protector" is one of the MOST unique, modern, and impressive cybersecurity projects you can build — and it is perfect for copyright because the idea itself is original, the logic is custom, and the software has real practical impact.

Below is a detailed, clear, technical + simple explanation of the project.


---

🔐 SHADOW IDENTITY PROTECTOR

Detect Apps That Secretly Access Your Sensitive Data


---

🌟 What This Software Does (Simple Explanation)

Whenever an app on a phone or computer tries to access:

🎤 Microphone

📷 Camera

📍 Location/GPS

📱 Contacts

🗂️ Files

📋 Clipboard


Your software will instantly detect it and show a warning like:

> ⚠️ Alert: Instagram accessed your Camera at 10:42 AM



It then logs it in a privacy report dashboard.

This project protects users from spy apps, background trackers, and sneaky permissions.


---

🎯 Why This Project is So Unique

✔ Almost no student chooses this idea
✔ Cybersecurity + privacy + device monitoring (teachers love this)
✔ Real-world use
✔ Extremely original → High copyright value
✔ You can implement it even with intermediate skills


---

🧠 Core Concept (How it works)

Your system runs a background monitoring service that continuously checks:

1️⃣ System Permission Access Logs

Detect when apps trigger:

Camera API

Microphone API

GPS API

File read/write


2️⃣ Process Activity Monitoring

Identify when apps launch hidden services like:

com.app.microphone.use

com.app.location.request

com.app.camera.capture


3️⃣ Trigger Alerts

When suspicious access happens:

Popup notification

Sound alert

Log entry


4️⃣ Privacy Report Generator

Shows daily/weekly logs like:

App Name	Accessed	Type	Time

WhatsApp	Microphone	High risk	11:23 AM
Facebook	Location	Medium	01:55 PM
Chrome	Clipboard	High	09:10 PM



---

🧩 Project Architecture (Easy to Present in College)

📌 1. Monitoring Module

Checks all permission and resource access.

📌 2. Event Detector

Detects trigger events like:

MICROPHONE_STARTED

CAMERA_OPENED

LOCATION_REQUESTED


📌 3. Alert System

Shows instant notifications.

📌 4. Logger Module

Stores records in:

SQLite

JSON

CSV


📌 5. Dashboard UI

Displays privacy activity:

Graphs

Tables

Risk score



---

🛡️ Features You Can Add (Choose Any 6–8 for your project)

🔥 Main Features

Real-time access detection

Pop-up alerts

Permission history

App risk rating

Data access timeline


🔒 Advanced Add-ons

Live Camera Access Indicator (green light)

Microphone Access Waveform

"Privacy Score" for each app

Auto-block unknown apps

Dark mode UI



---

🚀 Technology Stack Options

If you want to build for Android

Java or Kotlin

Android API hooks

Accessibility Services

Notification listener


If you want to build for Windows / PC

Use Python + Win32 APIs:

psutil

pywin32

watchdog


If you want a web-based dashboard

Python backend

HTML/CSS/JS frontend


I can help you choose the easiest version.


---

📚 What Makes This Project Copyrightable

✔ Your detection logic
✔ Your risk calculation formula
✔ Your UI design
✔ Your code
✔ The conceptual model
✔ Your documentation

This makes your software legally identifiable as your own creation.

For many are called, but few are chosen. (Matthew 22: 14)

In today's verse, Jesus reveals that salvation is for all, but it is our decision whether or not to choose it and live a life worthy of it. 

In the parable of the wedding banquet we come across 3 category of people, the invited who were carried away in their 'worldly afflictions', people in the main streets (both good and bad), and the one without a wedding robe- the one who made himself unworthy to attend His banquet. Today let us retrospect into our lives, and analyse which category we belong in. God's salvation is for all, but He also gives each individual the freedom to accept His call or decline it. 
In our context, accepting His call signifies our will to receive His words into our hearts and act in accordance to His words. Just as in the parable of the Sower, God sows the seeds everywhere (God's word (salvation) is given to all), but only those that fell on the good soil produced a hundredfold.

‘Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God shines forth’ (Psalm 50:2)

The Lord calls you to be this perfection of beauty, in every sense, to be Zion, from where He will shine forth to the world. This perfection is attained by being thankful for everything that the Lord has ordained for you and me this day. After all, our days may at times seem so long, struggles and afflictions may have heaped up at every nook and cranny, and there may have been those memories of old that have been forgotten under the weight of our present trials. Whatever, let us choose to clothe ourselves with this tinge of thankfulness, to admit that there have been so many blessings, hidden, unearthed, and unimagined, that we were holding on to all the time. Even for those unfulfilled wishes and desires of our hearts, these were stored up in us by our Creator, and will bear fruit in His destined time, which is our perfect timing. It is gratitude that wraps one in grace, in the glory that comes from above. Let us pray for this transformation of mind for the power/ability to appreciate all that we have been bestowed with, many of which we haven’t asked for, and for those yet to be opened up for us – those on the way. We are children of a loving God, who gave up even His only Son (Jesus) for our reclamation - Will He ‘not’ along with Jesus give us every other thing that would bring the fulfillment of our dreams. So we trust in the Lord, the One with the Father, who intercedes for us and grants us better gifts each day. Amen

He is clothed in a robe dipped in blood, and his name is called The Word of God. (Revelation 19: 13)

As Christians, we look forward to the second coming of Jesus. We believe that Jesus will come in glory to judge all nations and establish His holy rule in heaven and earth by eradicating all that is evil. 

Today's verse reaffirms this. The core of our Christian faith is established on the foundation of Jesus- His resurrection and His second coming. In this verse, God shows John a warrior (Jesus Christ) riding on the white horse, who is called faithful and True. He is clothed in a robe dipped in blood, indicating God's final judgement (The day of God's Wrath) where he will judge the whole world (both living and dead) according to their deeds. He is called The Word of God, symbolising the authority of Jesus as the Son of God. 

Today let us ask ourselves whether we are prepared to face God's judgement and receive Jesus in a worthy manner.

21/11/25, 09:47 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Say therefore to the people of Israel, ‘I am the Lord, and I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, and I will deliver you from slavery to them, and I will redeem you with an outstretched arm and with great acts of judgment’ (Exodus 6:6)

Through Moses, God tells His people that He is there for them - to bring them out of their burdens, to deliver them from slavery to the Egyptians, and to redeem them so that, freed from the vicious circle of captivity (of deprivations), they would joyfully enter the promised land. Under the Egyptians, the Israelites lost all hope for a comeback. What was until then impossible to them, even by a figment of imagination, was what the Lord spoke to them. Therefore, they ignored the words of Moses and went their way, heartbroken and angry. We sometimes behave this way – when fret by trials and challenges one after another in life, when unable to find meaning in our efforts, after all the hard work and toil, nothing tangible appears to us, when people around, though half-hearted, reap heavier returns, we get annoyed with everybody and everything around, even to the extent of stating that God can do nothing. When the Israelites felt so, the Lord intervened, asking Moses to go and ask Pharaoh to liberate the Israelites as per His command. Perplexed and dumbfounded, Moses was in a fix, unable to make sense of what He was to do when even God’s people did not give heed to what he said to them. The Bible tells us that the Lord speaks to Moses and Aaron and gives them charge over His people and the Pharaoh, that His plan for their liberation may be fulfilled. God’s plans are irreversible, no matter how hard situations, people, technology, or politics may seem. God is in full control; He was and continues to be the author of our story. Let us trust in Him. Amen

22/11/25, 08:53 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘To what distress I have come! And into what a great flood I am now plunged! For I was kind and beloved in my power’ (1 Maccabees 6:11)

These were the words of Antiochus Epiphanes at his death bed, the King of the Seleucid Empire, who once was a symbol of terror and tyranny. His initiatives and reforms marked the beginning of the Maccabean revolt, after the Jews found themselves subjected to severe persecution and distress. What became a realization to this King at the time of his death was his once glorious reign over the divided territories of Alexander the Great; and what prompted his fall from the heights - his pride and hunger for power drove the beast in him that led him to the extreme. He defied the Temple of Jerusalem, took away all treasures within, the sacred vessels, lampstand, and table of presence and left it desecrated. As if this was not enough, he continued his evil ways by torturing Jews, persuading them to give up their faith and worship the Greek idols. His miserable death poses a lesson to us – to be cautious of our attitude and ways during our ‘spring times’, when all things fall in place; for we never know what life has in store for us. Let us pray for this grace of doing the will of God at all times. What more assurance do we need than to be enveloped in His love, mercy, and forgiveness, so that no regrets or remorse take a grip over us. Wishing you a blessed Saturday. Amen

24/11/25, 10:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And in every matter of wisdom and understanding about which the King inquired of them, he found them ten times better than all the magicians and enchanters that were in all his kingdom’ (Daniel 1:20)

These four youth, who were faithful to the tenets of the Law, and who were careful not to be defiled by anything (the culture and ways) that pertained to a foreign cult, found favour in the eyes of the Lord. As exiles under a foreign ruler (miles away from their own country) in Babylon, there was nothing much for them to be excited about. Had they been in the comfort and security of their own land (Judah), their story would have been totally different. But they chose to honour the Lord and be faithful to him, even when swamped by the crisis and the uncertainties that followed the political turnover of Judah and in its aftermath. Because of God’s presence with them, they found compassion in the sight of the officer in charge of serving them food, thus asking him to relieve them from the kind of food and drink that was anathema to their nobility. Even the slightest of virtues that one may practise that which is hidden from others, is noticed, applauded, and accepted by our God. He takes ownership of us then, fills us with His power to sojourn beyond the natural, to be supernatural. This power filled the lives of the four youth, who honoured God in the land of exile. The King was amazed at their intelligence, prowess, and capabilities, that was ten times ahead of all the extraordinary people who were known to the king till then. It is from God alone, that one learns the finesse of skills, attains brainpower, logical reasoning, and all of them put together ‘life skills’. No other authority, human power, or influence can get you to where you ought to rise. Trust in the Lord. Amen

25/11/25, 10:29 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘A stone was cut from a mountain by no human hand, and it broke in pieces the iron, the bronze, the clay, the silver, and the gold….’ (Daniel 2:45)

To King Nebuchadnezzar was once given a dream, so impactful that it left him troubled and sleepless for days. The King immediately summoned all the men of knowledge, magicians, sorcerers, enchanters, in the land of the Chaldeans, asking them for an interpretation of this dream. The dream was thus that from nowhere there came a stone hurled at an image, a gorgeous one. This gigantic image was extraordinary, its head was made of gold, it had arms and chest of silver, thighs of bronze, and legs made of a mix of iron and clay; and in no time it was thrashed and broken into pieces. The stone that struck this image then grew into a great mountain and filled the whole earth. None of these wise men could decipher the dream or give a true interpretation to the king. Daniel, filled with God’s power, was able to intervene in this situation to give the king a correct interpretation of his dream. The message God revealed to Daniel was not just for the king to ponder on, but also for you and me. The gold, silver, and bronze have been used as metaphors to refer to significant improvements in Science & Technology in our world - the world of AI, with connected devices empowering communication, real-time predictions, swift delivery of products and services, robotics, and so on. Everything created (their glory) will surrender and be subject to the ultimate plan of God. He is the author of Creation, and His plans can never be subdued by any human creation, however substantial. Human dignity, empowerment, and the welfare of mankind are God’s purpose in creation, overriding every human instinct, intent, and design. Whenever the order of nature is challenged by self-centric motives, there is sure to follow a terminator (an action or creation of God) that will downplay or dismiss the human innovation. That men may acknowledge the glory of God and that they are mere mortals. Amen

‘Then King David went in and sat before the Lord, and he said: Who am I, Lord God, and what is my family, that you have brought me this far’ (1 Chronicles 17:16)

The greatness of King David was not his kingship, or his victories in battles one after another, or the glory of having had a continuing dynasty in Judah, but of his holistic understanding of himself. David gave credit to the Almighty for all the works of wonder in his life. He called the Lord, ‘the source of His strength and prayed for a heart of purity, penitence, and proximity to God. Therefore, he ruled over Israel for forty long years, enjoyed wealth, honor, and died at a good old age, as the Chronicle accounts of David’s death references. Stories apart, let us look within, of how much do we know ourselves? With and without the people, situations, and challenges we face each day. In one way or the other, each one of us has a niche, our area of strengths and capabilities, our domain of weaknesses, we may be right at times and equally wrong at several other times. We need each other, and this emphasises the fact of how much more we need the work of grace in our lives. It is this supernatural power that builds us, helps us learn and unlearn from past mistakes; to accept we are unworthy of favour from God, considering our ways and responses to His expectations of us. All that has transpired has been His mercy and love for us, and from here emerges our ability to forgive, let go, and forget the wrongs of others. To be merciful as my Heavenly Father is – let this be the benchmark /measure of my progress level as a person. Amen

28/11/25, 11:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You are the Light of the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hidden’ (Matthew 5:14)

You and I cannot, but live as the true light – the light that chases out all darkness. At the time of Creation, God spoke and willed that ‘there must be light’. He sent His only Son, Jesus, to demonstrate to mankind on what it is to be a source of luminance to all. Jesus prayed, comforted, consoled, strengthened, and empowered those who felt they had no ground – the marginalized, widowed, orphaned, the sick, the trivialized. He taught the world the essence of being poor in the Spirit, of being merciful, of waiting to be satisfied from above when agonized with injustice and iniquities from men, of learning to be resilient and reticent in the midst of adversities in life, theories that were till then unknown to the world. And then He built us up as a ‘City’, the dwelling place of His Spirit, that we may live these values extraordinarily, that men may see and learn that ‘what is impossible for men, is always possible with God’. Without His Spirit in us, we are just cities – unfortified, so vulnerable to attacks, disasters, and hazards at any time. Great men and women of old, the prophets, great Kings, and seers longed and lived as cities of the Most High. They were tested, tried, put to suffering, but their endurance made the mark, and their names indelibly monumented in the pages of history. You are also called to be illustrious, the light of the Lord, the beauty in His garden, the stream that flows in the desert, to be approved not just by men, but by Hands that carved your frame. You are so ‘phenomenal’ a person – What you do, none can, and what you fail to, none can set right. Believe and be the light! Amen

29/11/25, 09:30 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I am with you and will watch over you wherever you go, and I will bring you back to this land. I will not leave you until I have done what I have promised you’ (Genesis 28:15)

To those who regret that their dreams are all chased out, and there remains nothing hopeful, Jacob is the motif that the Bible upholds to give you strength today. A man who was chased out of his home (though in obedience to his mother and father), a man who was thrown out to diffidence and uncertainties in life all of a sudden, one who had to constantly battle with loneliness, one who had none to counsel/advise or suggest an alternative to life, to this one – the Lord offers His unconditional love, His Covenant of ownership, that wherever he went, He was the dwelling presence of God, and that everything was a new beginning in life, yet to be worked on. God’s promises are always unconditional, though we tend to attribute life’s adversities to the intervention of God. God is faithful and promises His unfailing love every moment in life, but it takes a lot of effort (faith) to trust and obey Him. This is exactly where you and I stumble and fail. This is where the people of God (of the Old Testament) failed – to reckon with the fact that God is merciful, ever faithful, and forgiving even to the greatest of sinners, He gives another chance for a comeback through repentance. Jacob was taken aback, having seen and experienced God’s saving presence with him. In him was filled a fearful reverence for the Lord, which made him make a vow before the Lord. Like Jacob, we too make conditional promises before the Lord, when distraught by illness, setbacks, and distress in life. Even then, the Lord promises that He will never let go of us, until He has fulfilled all that He has promised – Such is the beauty and charm of our Lord. Lord, help me love you and patiently wait for the fulfilment of all your promises in my life. Amen

It is the Law of the Divine that honours both individuality and communal interests, to ensure liberty, equality, and fraternity are enjoyed by all His creation. To trust God is indeed a choice, ‘our choice’, but to everyone who trusts He ensures them a life filled with the righteousness that comes from above. The righteousness that is not self-centred, one that can make sacrifices to move on with courage, one that God and men alike will approve. On this Day 2 of Advent, let us pray for the virtue of top-notch faithfulness in life, to demonstrate faithfulness to the end before God and before people whom God has given us. Amen

02/12/25, 13:58 - Megha Sherino: In today’s world, our phones know more about us than even our closest friends do.
They hold our photos, messages, locations, passwords — almost our entire identity.
But the scary part is… many apps access this information silently in the background, without ever asking us or telling us.

Most users don’t realize when their microphone turns on for a second…
When their location is tracked repeatedly…
Or when their contacts and files are quietly scanned.

This hidden access is what inspired us.

We wanted to build something that gives people back their right to privacy.
Something that tells you,
“Hey, this app is accessing your identity… do you want this?”

And that’s how Shadow Identity Protector was born —
a simple but powerful tool that watches what happens behind the screen and protects your personal identity from silent digital threats.

Our aim is to make privacy easy, transparent, and in the hands of the user again.
Because your identity is yours — not something apps can take without permission.

02/12/25, 19:59 - Megha Sherino: oh amma, ill send you the previous abstract too, just so that you can include the important technical concepts
02/12/25, 20:00 - Megha Sherino: Shield Mate is a privacy-focused cybersecurity system that detects and alerts users when applications access sensitive device resources without explicit consent. With the rise of digital surveillance and hidden data collection, users often do not know when their microphone, camera, location, contacts, files, or clipboard are accessed in the background. This project tackles this important privacy issue by developing a real-time monitoring and intrusion-detection tool that continuously tracks and detects unauthorised and unusual access to identity sensitive resource. The system embeds a ‘Behavioral Analytics Engine’ BAE that analyses patterns of permission usage, background activity, API calls, and network behavior through rule-based checks and unsupervised anomaly detection techniques. From these analyses, an SIPI is derived that presents users with transparent and interpretable risk scores for each installed application. Additional features include a sandbox mode that supplies obfuscated identity data to high-risk applications, further enhancing user protection. This project shows a practical and innovative way to boost user digital privacy in everyday life.
02/12/25, 20:02 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Shadow Identity Protector , it is a privacy-focused system designed  to alert users whenever an app secretly accesses  the device's features such as the microphone, camera, location, files, or contacts. It continuously keeps tracing how various applications use permissions in the background, making it easier to find  unauthorized actions. The tool studies the patterns of resource usage and quickly finds anything that is out of place. When a  suspicious activity is being  detected, the user immediately  receives a warning so that they can take action. It being a lightweight tool, ensures that the monitoring runs smoothly  and quietly without slowing down the device. The  main goal is to provide the  users more transparency about what is happening behind the scenes on their own devices. This system allows people   and helps them to stay aware of the hidden data collection and protects them from illegal  tracking. It also provides an additional benefit of safety against malicious apps and privacy leaks. By bringing  real-time awareness to permission misuse of apps , Shadow Identity Protector facilitates individuals to have  control of their personal information. Overall, it presents a practical and modern approach to strengthening digital privacy in everyday usage.

‘You will keep in perfect peace those whose minds are steadfast, because they trust in you’ (Isaiah 26:3)

To be like that strong fortified city, the Lord God has called us this day. The condition of the mind of the ‘one’ who is upright, faithful, and trusting in the Lord will be likewise. 05/12/25, 09:41 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘In a very short time, will not Lebanon be turned into a fertile field and the fertile field seem like a forest?’ (Isaiah 29:17)

Lebanon was one of the boundaries that constituted the Northern Kingdom of Israel, renowned for its Cedars that were once materials gifted to King David by his vassal kings. But Lebanon can also refer to those situations and people in our lives branded as ‘nominal’, ‘aggressive’, and ‘untamed’ – those situations we often term as a consequence of ‘fate’, beyond our control and unchangeable. Today, Jesus asks you and me, “Do you believe that these situations and people would forever remain unformidable in your life?” “Don’t I, the Lord (who permitted these into your life) have the power to reverse and modify them to your benefit?” In a short while from now, you will see the change you expected, that you prayed for most fervently. But the price it costs you is to wait in patient hope for it, to trust in my goodwill for you. All your times are in my hands, and whatever I give, it comes in abundance, and from my heart. Will I ever forsake you? No matter whoever does. And further, the Holy Spirit reminds of the future glory of this city (you and me) – I will rebuild you with stones of turquoise, your foundations with lapis lazuli, your battlements of rubies, gates of sparkling jewels, and your walls of precious stones. You will be established in ‘righteousness’. This is God’s promise for you and me today. Believe and claim for yourself with a meek and yielding spirit. Amen

06/12/25, 08:50 - Megha Sherino: Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign: The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and will call him Immanuel.
Isaiah 7: 14

Dear friends, this is a verse we often hear during the advent season. But in today's verse God has a message in store for the youth. When you go through this verse in the Bible, you'll come across the footnote which says that some Hebrew translation refers to young woman instead of virgin. 
For fulfilling His salvific plan, God called a young woman Mother Mary, a young girl who is 15 or 16 years in age. And Joseph was a young man who was not more than 20 years in age. These two young people had to sacrifice their youth years to fulfill the salvific plan of God. These young couples received Jesus into their lives, sacrificed so much in their life to grow up baby Jesus. In turn Jesus also sacrificed his life at the tender age of 33. 

Today God is calling us youngsters. The world tells us to enjoy our youth, and today many of the youth and youngsters have infact even left their faith for the joys of this world. Today Jesus calls us. Let us sacrifice our lives for God and fulfill the purpose he has for us

06/12/25, 10:07 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Lord longs to be gracious to you; therefore, He will rise up to show you compassion’ (Isaiah 30:18)

The ‘forgiveness of sins’ and ‘redemption’ are the two gifts the Lord wills to shower upon you without measure. Not that we have earned His goodwill, but because He cares and loves us. The Bible tells us, ‘This is God’s love for us,’ not that we loved God, but that He loved us and thus sent His only Son as an atoning sacrifice for our sins. And Jesus commissioned His disciples by telling them to give freely what they freely received from Him. If God has forgiven your sins and shown you compassion, then reciprocate this goodwill to those around you – your dear and near – forgive and be merciful to them. Then your light will break forth like the dawn, and your healing will quickly appear; your righteousness shall go before you, and the glory of the Lord will be your rear guard, this is His promise for you today. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over, will be poured into your lap (Luke 6:38). Believe and retrieve this blessing in all spheres of your life, from the mighty arms of the Lord. Amen

07/12/25, 08:30 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For whatever was written in former days was written for our instruction, that through endurance and through the encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope’ (Romans 15:4)

The fundamental virtues of faith, hope, and charity emanate from God, and indeed from Him springs the quintessential values of endurance and encouragement. God, who spoke through the prophets in older days, now speaks through His Son Jesus. Anyone who reads through the Scriptures discerns God’s will and His way – Jesus Christ. The Bible teaches us that this Jesus was ‘the Word’ that was with the Father from the time of Creation, who sustains the Universe, and reclaimed all humanity from their sinfulness by His incarnation in the flesh. Jesus Christ is the same today, yesterday, and forever. Therefore, whatever was written in former days was meant for our instruction to this day (as well) – their context and depiction may at times seem different. But their intent can never change – by endurance and encouragement we progress in hope – the hope of a resurrection like that of Christ from all that is dead, from the culture of death, and to a likeness and imitation of Jesus himself. And when Jesus breathed His last on the Cross, He forgave His persecutors, to fulfill what was once told by the prophets – The reproaches of those who reproached you, fell on me. We who are strong, are likewise called to bear with those weak, and not merely please ourselves. Let this be the exercise for us this Sunday. Fill me with power, Holy Spirit. Amen

08/12/25, 06:46 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires

Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign: The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and will call him Immanuel. (Isaiah 7: 14)

During the advent season, we have heard and meditated on this verse multiple times. When I read this verse in the Bible, I came across a footnote which said that some Hebrew translation refers to young woman instead of virgin. 
For fulfilling God’s salvific plan, a young girl Mary was chosen. She was not more than 15 or 16 years in age when she received God’s call. Likewise, Joseph, a young man, not more than 20 years, was called to accept Mary and baby Jesus. These two young people had to make a lot of mortifications and take a lot of risks and uncertainties in their life for the salvation of mankind. In turn, Jesus also fulfilled God’s plan by sacrificing His life for us at the prime age of 33.
Today God calls us to make little mortifications for the fulfillment of His purpose in our lives. We are to become people that channelises its vigour not just for the world and pleasures, but for the coming of God's kingdom in our midst. Let us live a life of integrity, resoluteness, and purity, empowered by the Spirit of God. It is these lives that become instruments in the hands of God's Spirit to work greater things and to bring change. We are called to live this phase in humble submission to God's ways for us, because ultimately it is God who guides the destiny of a man.

Wishing you a wonderful week
God bless you.
Megha Sherino
08/12/25, 10:13 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘You may have the strength to comprehend with all the saints what is the breadth and length and height and depth of the love of Christ’ (Ephesians 3:18)

The love by which the Universe, you and me, and all the created were architected, arrayed, and set in place, the love that has helped us thrive and grow to this day, the love that strengthened us to walk through troubled seas, the fire, and the mountains that were enormous and way beyond our capabilities, the love that persuaded us to give our best even when we were at our lowest ebbs, the love that taught us to flip our scripts and thoughts from those cultural patterns, daily realities, and pulse of this world…..the love of Jesus Christ. It is towards this love that the Spirit of the Lord attracts you today, to rewire yourself, reorient your likes, priorities, and preferences, to be ‘yourself’, the person you were created to be. The fact is that you are most precious, chosen, and have been adopted into the Heavenly Kingdom, in accordance with the desire of the Father, that in Jesus Christ, by being a new Creation through Him (moulded in love), you would inherit all the Heavenly riches in the Higher places. This is the breadth, length, height, and depth of God’s love for you……that He awaited to see you through, by comprehending how much He loved you. Let us entreat the Blessed Mary, our mother, to help us grow up this ladder of love of Jesus. Amen

09/12/25, 11:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And if He finds it, truly I tell you, He is happier about that one sheep than about the ninety-nine that did not wander off’ (Matthew 18:13)

Every crisis, challenge, and adversity in life is a new opportunity for growth. Of course, none can compare to their impact on our otherwise serene (peaceful) mindset. And it is these situations that help us understand how much Jesus means to each one of us. When life sails smoothly, when things are all in array and order, our horizons tend to descend to where we are. Because we attain confidence, the visibility of the future, we tend to lean on ourselves, believing that nothing much needs to be done, and it is these instincts that guide our minds, convictions, and words, whenever we meet another. But this can never be so, when things seem adrift, or circumstances contend with our convictions and beliefs, when uncertainty sets in, and we get into a fix trying to find peace in the midst of the chaos. It is then that one goes beyond what he/she would normally attempt to do (usually), the aggressiveness to find a solution would bring a great transformation in our ways, words, and even mode of living. Our horizons expand much more than what we perceived them to be. And rather than calling for help from around, we are taught to reach out to the Lord. Here comes the turning point in life, when results start to show up, at times very slow, but sure. It is this one sheep that has strayed off, in search of solutions in life, for growth, that the Lord God is so pleased with. Because he has overcome the tides, the barriers of approval, of being lauded by the crowds, the insecurity of even being regarded ‘the black sheep’. Be yourself and partner with Jesus. He is happier about you trying hard to discover yourself, rather than fitting with the crowds, as a square peg in a round hole. You are loved by Him because of your uniqueness. God is with you. Amen

10/12/25, 08:33 - Megha Sherino: Here you go — clear, clean, and exam-ready steps for installing **Visual Studio Code (VS Code)** properly on your system.

---

# ✅ **Steps to Install VS Code (Visual Studio Code)**

### **1. Download the Installer**

* Open your browser and go to the official website:
  **[https://code.visualstudio.com/](https://code.visualstudio.com/)**
* Click on **Download for Windows** (or choose Linux/macOS if needed).

---

### **2. Run the Installer**

* Locate the downloaded file (usually in **Downloads** folder).
* Double-click **VSCodeUserSetup-x64.exe** to start installation.

---

### **3. Accept License Agreement**

* The setup window opens.
* Read and click **I accept the agreement** → **Next**.

---

### **4. Choose Installation Location**

* By default, it installs in:
  `C:\Users\<YourName>\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code`
* You can keep the default → Click **Next**.

---

### **5. Select Additional Tasks**

Enable these options for best experience:

* ✔ **Create a desktop icon**
* ✔ **Add “Open with Code” to Windows Explorer file context menu**
* ✔ **Add “Open with Code” to folder context menu**
* ✔ **Register Code as the default editor for supported file types**

Click **Next** → **Install**.

---

### **6. Let the Installation Complete**

* Wait until the setup installs all required files.
* Click **Finish**.
* VS Code will launch automatically if **Launch Visual Studio Code** is checked.

---

# 🎯 **After Installation (Recommended Setup)**

### **1. Install Extensions**

Open VS Code → Left sidebar → Extensions icon

Useful extensions:

* Python
* C/C++
* Code Runner
* Prettier
* Live Server
* GitHub Copilot (optional)
* Material Icon Theme

---

### **2. Configure Your First Project**

* Create a folder.
* Open it in VS Code: **File → Open Folder**.
* Start coding!

---

### **3. Install Compiler/Runtime (if needed)**

Depending on your language:

* **C/C++** → Install MinGW or GCC.
* **Python** → Install Python from python.org.
* **Java** → Install JDK.
* **HTML/CSS/JS** → No compiler needed.

---

If you want, I can also give you:
✅ Steps to configure VS Code for C
✅ Steps to configure VS Code for Python
✅ Steps to configure VS Code for Java
Just tell me!

10/12/25, 08:41 - Megha Sherino: // Smooth scrolling for internal links
document.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]').forEach(link => {
    link.addEventListener("click", function (e) {
        const targetId = this.getAttribute("href");
        if (targetId.length > 1) {
            e.preventDefault();
            document.querySelector(targetId).scrollIntoView({
                behavior: "smooth"
            });
        }
    });
});

// Mobile navigation toggle
const navToggle = document.querySelector(".nav-toggle");
const navLinks = document.querySelector(".nav-links");

if (navToggle && navLinks) {
    navToggle.addEventListener("click", () => {
        navLinks.classList.toggle("show");
    });
}

10/12/25, 11:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Come to me, all you who labour and are overburdened, and I will give you rest’ (Matthew 11:28)

At times, you and I only need a ‘reboot’ or ‘restart’ from the Lord, our Maker, not a ‘shut down’. This is easily understood by us, when we talk about the tools, instruments, and devices we use in daily life. A mere plug-off and re-plugging in would be all that is needed to get things running back to their normalcy. Our lives are no different. Spring, summer, autumn, and winter constitute the seasons in life, and often it is challenges, adversities, disruptions, and issues that reconstruct the person in us. Some may say that had we been a bit more patient, cautious, slow, or even still, many of these situations in life would never have occurred. But the truth is far from this. In every life situation, we have a stress buster, a niche to unwind, stretch ourselves, and recharge, carved for us alone, and it is with the Lord. However exhausted, demotivated, desperate, or detached, one finds himself/herself refreshed when with Jesus. Thus, the Lord asks through His prophet Isaiah, ‘To whom will you compare me? Who is my equal? This very question He asks you today, this 10th day of Advent Season; To whom/To what would you match me? Whenever you are burdened, weary, tired, and disturbed, turn to me, find in me the peace, joy, and hope that will revive your spirit. Every Word of mine is strength to the weary and power to the weak. There is nothing in this world that can outshine the glory of Light, that is me. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

11/12/25, 11:55 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I write these things to you who believe in the name of the Son of God so that you may know that you have eternal life’ (1 John 5:13)

Eternal life is not on account of uncountable deeds or merits of a human being, but because of his faith in Jesus Christ. Not in any way, to be indifferent to the needs of another, but accepting that all that is done in charity is but a reflection of the love the Father showed us through His Son. Every day brings with it opportunities to be fruitful, but it is the willingness from within a man that determines his productivity. This willingness in us needs to be stimulated by the desire, conviction, and emotion in us; and there can be times when the three seem to be at their lowest ebb, especially when physically exhausted. It is God’s Spirit who induces purpose, meaning, and drive in a man to make his next move. It fuels and ignites us by reminding us of a bigger purview, a larger canvas, and yet something greater to unveil for us – Eternity. It’s so usual to get stuck in the vicious circle of daily living and worries, to be lost in the attractions of this world, that one may find it exceptionally hard to think beyond. The incarnation of Jesus was meant to enliven us to this truth of Eternity. Our temporal life on earth must take us through with Jesus to our destiny. You and I have been called to inherit a great fortune and even better things, not merely wallow in the valley of tears and inhibitions. God’s Spirit awakens you to this message today. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

12/12/25, 09:42 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Sing, rejoice, daughter of Zion, for I am coming to dwell in the midde of you – it is the Lord who speaks’ (Zechariah 2:14)

The Lord who has laid the foundations of the earth calls us to rejoice in Him today. He has come to dwell with us, in the form of bread, every time we partake in the Holy Eucharist. He who knows what is best for us, wills to teach us this, and direct our path in the ways we must go. The more we know ourselves, we understand that it is difficult for us to tread new paths, to change our ways, to come out of our comfort zone, to sacrifice a little by which many may benefit from our lives…… to such like these, Jesus, the King of all Kings, the Lord or All, God and Master of all creation, came down to the level of creation, to redeem us from our fallen state. He knows our inhibitions, our weaknesses, and shortcomings, yet loves us. In us, He sees not what we see, or men may have declared to have seen, but the fullness of His Creation. Thus, we have more reasons to rejoice when the Lord comes to us, though we may be poor, unworthy, blemished and sinful, inappropriate in our word, deed, and ways, the One who has power and authority over all, can change, modify, elevate, and rebuild. Thus says the Lord to you and me in the way we are today – You will surely rebuild the ancient ruins and will raise up the age-old foundations; You will be called Repairer of broken walls, Restorer of streets with dwellings, because I the Lord will guide you, will strengthen, and satisfy all your needs. Amen

Dr.Mini Sherino

13/12/25, 07:45 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires

Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: I am the Lord your God, who teaches you for your own good, who leads you in the way you should go. 
(Isaiah 48: 17)

Who are we? 
Our earthly life is filled with confusion and uncertainty, we have ambitions, aspirations, dreams and desires. None of us know who we are or what we are to do, finding this is the core reason why we live on earth. No one but God knows of our destiny. 
Our ultimate goal or purpose in life is to attain salvation, to live with God eternally in His Kingdom. But, apart from that, we also have an earthly destiny, which is unique for each of us. We are created for a purpose, no one but God knows of this purpose. Today’s verse reminds us that, all we have to do is follow God’s ways and teachings in order to understand our destiny or mission on Earth. In our earthly lives filled with confusion and uncertainty, God asks us to quieten ourselves and follow Him, He will take control of our lives by leading us to the right path. No one but God knows who we are and what we are to achieve. 
Let us offer our lives wholly to God and lead a life that pleases His Holy Sight. God assures us that he will lead us to the way we are to go. Because no one but God knows of our destiny.

Wishing you a wonderful weekend.
God bless you
Megha Sherino

13/12/25, 10:10 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘so that you may live a life worthy of the Lord and please Him in every way: bearing fruit in every good work, growing in the knowledge of God’ (Colossians 1:10)

Knowing God is the key to living a life worthy of the Lord and pleasing Him. For when this happens, good works emerge from us as fruits, just as fruits are borne in a tree. And Jesus reminds us that no bad tree would bear good fruit, and it is from the fruit that the tree is known. Knowing God would lead us to revering Him, as His power is beyond all that is created. It is this power that encourages us towards forbearance, to hold firm to the right, even when things around seem doubtful. To live a life worthy of God’s pleasure need not always be acceptable to society, customary regulations, or established norms of this world. You and I may prove an anathema to many around us. It takes courage, conviction, and inspiration from the spirit of God to walk in the ways of the Lord. When the Apostle Paul spoke about a life pleasing to God, he did not try to enforce upon us something hard, but to live in obedience to the promptings of God’s Spirit in us. This strengthens us to outlive our human instincts, tendencies of the flesh, inclinations of the mind, and man-made practices. Character building is that which gives one the drive to outlive the flesh and move towards his purpose as he increases in years. May you be inspired to live a life centred in Christ, focused on your purpose. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

14/12/25, 10:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘They will see the glory of the Lord, the majesty of our God’ (Isaiah 35:2)

This was Heaven’s standard – the lowly things of this world were chosen, the despised, the things that were not, to bring to nothing the things that were…. But to us humans, who focus on things that matter, God sent His only Son in human form, that we may learn what it is to be human-like. Thus, the prophets spoke of the times when the Messiah comes, that the wilderness and dry land will be made glad, deserts would rejoice and be made fruitful, and the whole creation would be mesmerized at God’s great works of wonder. It is in this context that we are told to rejoice, no matter how things may seem indifferent to us, to garner up strength, as when the Lord comes, He will come with His recompense. The Lord comes to establish His reign over mankind, His holiness and righteousness. He comes to ransom all that was confiscated, deprived, and lost. Beyond the confines of time and space, this restoration of Jesus still occurs in our midst, in our hearts, minds, and homes, whenever we joyfully exalt His greatness. So, along with St Paul, let us also preach the message of the Cross to the world that let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord. WE humbly await your glorious coming into hearts, O Lord. Amen

15/12/25, 10:44 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘A star shall come forth out of Jacob, and a scepter shall arise out of Israel’ [Numbers 24:17]

An icon of Light and power shall emerge out of Jacob, and not over Him, was the prophecy Balaam was given, and of which he spoke before the King Balak. When it arises, the people of God will see the light of day. Those dwelling in the shadows of ‘hopelessness’ will be guided by this light that shall be decisive in directing them to their destiny. Mankind shall be taught to obey the Statutes and precepts of an Almighty God, by which peace shall overrule all chaos. His precepts shall be rooted in love, which respects, builds up, sustains, and empowers towards progress. No human law can overpower this law, as Jesus had come not to negate the laws but to fulfill them. Let us pray that our hearts and minds may be opened to receive this Light in its totality. However much ever we try, we may fail to illumine this light in all humility, but the Lord is faithful; He will never fail or forsake us, He knows our humble make-up, and He knows what it takes to lead us to where He sees us. Be my Light, my Way, that I may live my life in the truth. Chastise me in your mercy, when I go wrong, that I may serve and honour you in the Spirit and truth. Amen

16/12/25, 11:06 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘My soul shall make her boast in the Lord: let the afflicted hear and be glad’ (Psalm 34:2)

I need not be self-sufficient in the sense of this world; there are still those patches in me that need to be set right. I may be angry for no reason at times, may be hurt, may express resentment, disbelief, or doubtfulness at other times, yet my Lord knows me, cares for me, and attends to my needs when I call out to Him. The four essential facets of a true disciple are (i) audacity to listen; (ii) genuineness to accept one’s wrongs; (iii) to trust in the Lord; and (iv) to reach out to Him. And these have been outlined by the prophet Zephaniah as he describes a rebellious city (one oppressed and defiled), because it lacked these characteristics that cater to growth. And as You and I live our lives in its integrity (accepting God’s greatness), we make manifold a contribution to those afflicted, those in the dark, those in anguish and despair – that there is hope. God’s glory manifests most fruitfully through broken lives, writhed hearts, and contrite souls. The apostle Paul underlined this spiritual truth by testifying that it was the Lord who led Him through sufferings, afflictions, sickness, persecution, imprisonment, and severities to preach the Good news with enthusiasm. Jesus invites you and me today to imbibe and assimilate the truth that will capsize our perception and future – He says ‘My Grace is sufficient for you, for in your weakness my strength is perfected’. Amen

17/12/25, 07:48 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires

Therefore wait for me, says the Lord, for the day when I arise as a witness. For my decision is to gather nations, to assemble kingdoms, to pour out upon them my indignation, all the heat of my anger; for in the fire of my passion all the earth shall be consumed. (Zephaniah 3: 8)

Advent season has two dimensions; one is the preparation of receiving Jesus into our lives and the other is the preparation for the final day of Judgement. It is a time filled with hope and at the same time a reminder, towards repentance and reparation, as God is infinitely merciful, but at the same time He honours Justice and His day of judgement is not far. 

The one who wears the crown, bears it. In Christmas, we are crowned with mercy and hope by our merciful father, but at the same time we also have the responsibility to change our lives and live for His Glory. This is the message God gives us during the advent season. So, Christmas is a symbol of God’s infinite mercy, His invitation to repentance and His delaying Judgement. Do not misinterpret this as God’s rage, rather it is His Holy wrath against injustice, oppression and spiritual arrogance.
Let us lead a life worthy of God’s mercy and fulfil His purpose in our lives.

Wishing you a wonderful Day!
God Bless You

17/12/25, 11:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Judah is a lion’s whelp; from the prey, my son, you have gone up. He stooped down, couched as a lion, and as a lioness; who dares rouse him up?’ [Genesis 49:9]

The most spectacular characteristic of the lion is its courage to rise, and at the same time stoop and rest in peace. No matter how silent this animal behaves, it rules the jungle not because of its aggressiveness, but merely because of its presence. Jacob’s blessing of Judah was summarized thus that he had been bequeathed the primogeniture authority that usually was to flow to the firstborn. Why was Judah elevated, though he was the most envious of his brothers (towards Joseph)? He was so wicked as to plot to do away with Joseph, though providence saved him. The Bible tells us that as time passed, he felt a great remorse within, of the consequence of his act, of having sold away his brother. Years later, when he came across a similar crisis, he was ready to give himself up as a prisoner in Egypt rather than lose Benjamin, his youngest brother. This willingness to pay for the mistakes of his past was a turning point in his destiny. Thus, from nowhere he emerged heir to the throne that was to give way for the birth of the Messiah. To pause, to couch at times, admit our mistakes, be willing to change, could prove significant in the life of any person, for him to emerge strong, to rewrite his history, make a sensation, and achieve blockbuster success. Always remember, Judah never knew of his rewritten destiny, nor did he exist to see the Messiah, but his one act had a lasting influence on the mind of God who worked out everything to fulfill his blessing. Amen

18/12/25, 10:27 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and they will call him Immanuel (which means ‘God with us’)’ [Matthew 1:23]

The prophecy Isaiah made (700 years before the coming of Jesus) in Isaiah 7:14 was quoted as having been fulfilled in Matthew’s narration about the birth of Jesus. This sign, of a virgin giving birth to a son, was of great significance when given to Ahaz, the King of Judah. God sent his prophet to the king (wavering in faith) to reassure him of His presence, concern, and protection for Judah (against attacks from its powerful neighbors). The symbolism of ‘the virgin’ would refer to an extraordinary intervention from above, not one that was of human design or innovativeness. Even today, God intervenes in our lives in the most unexpected ways and through the most unimagined of persons. But one thing is essential for us to see this occur in our lives – the audacity to accept and testify to all that God is with us – Immanuel. When our hearts affirm with love, and our tongues speak the name of Jesus, that He is with us, the prophecy of Isaiah becomes reinforced in our lives as well. Jesus is incarnated in the flesh (in our midst) again with His Divine and human nature. Every Eucharist replays these mysteries of Incarnation, nativity, passion, death, and resurrection of Jesus, and as we partake with love and devotion, we walk with Jesus, become transformed into Him, and it is He who lives with us. What occurred to the people of Judah, would recur in our lives then – the extraordinary intervention of God into the darkest recesses of our hearts, minds, the purification of our souls. The power we obtain is ‘unimaginable’, as what rescued King Ahaz; though he was sinking in faith, a worldly man, one who upheld human strength and tact over Godly supremacy. May Immanuel strengthen you as well, make us worthy of receiving the Word made flesh in our lives. Amen

19/12/25, 10:26 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Be thou to me a rock of refuge, a strong fortress, to save me, for thou art my rock and my fortress’ [Psalm 71:3]

There are memories in life that die hard. The circumstances of a sort, characteristics, or events that remind us that we are frail human beings, so vulnerable, and insecure. Though we know it’s high time, and time out that we change, mend, let go, rather than zoom into the burdens of the past, we hardly can. Because it may not be humanly possible to ignore what one has learned and re-postured from learning. But the one who has caused these to appear in our lives permitted them not for us to fall headlong. He knows specifically the intent and portent of these in our destiny. Therefore, it is to Him that we need to seek an answer for the strength to move on in life. Jesus is that rock, the strong fortress, in whom we can seek refuge, given by Heaven as a gift for all the problems of mankind. You and I need to sit in His presence in the Eucharist to ask Him how and why. His intervention can revive, restore, and reposition one for a restart in life. When all other posts/pillars evade and fail us, when life throws up unexpected tantrums hard to deal with, we need to hold on to that ‘rock’, that ‘fortress’ which would not only give us a firm footing but also help advance further on. Here with the Lord, we make bang on. Whenever he was out of sight, Moses went into the tent of meeting to consult with his rock; Joshua marched into the promised land every step with the Lord; so do we each day, inch closer to Jesus to celebrate the great day of His coming. Amen

20/12/25, 10:39 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘He must increase, but I must decrease’ [John 3:30]

In every aspect of life, Jesus calls our attention today to bring to expediency this Word of God. Jesus must grow in us more and more to render less important our instincts, perceptions, and self-centred desires. This message has become even more relevant in our era - where the ‘I’ in individuals has expanded beyond ‘leaps and bounds’ to restrict chances of survival of newer generations. Whenever people vie with one another, threats to peaceful coexistence emerge. To live and let live is one of the basic principles of life, which is forgotten by modern human beings. Violence, selfish motives, high-handed demands have thus crept into the fundamental unit of society the family, the consequences are what we see today. When Jesus comes, He focuses on ‘what we are to become’, and not, on what we are. A vision, purpose, and mission characterise what it means to develop. This applies to us human being too, without which we would be no better than any other creature. These have the power to transform, change priorities, encourage, build and support growth. The Bible reminds us that a man is remembered through his offspring and through the city he has made. You and I have been called to be the cause and foundation for generations to come, to be remembered as the repairer of breaches in walls, and restorer of streets to dwell in (Is 58:12). For this, He must increase in me, and I diminish. Lord Jesus, grace us today and grant us our prayers. Amen

21/12/25, 08:33 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Behold the dwelling place of God is with man. He will dwell with them, and they will be His’ [Revelation 21:3]

When God restores, there is a holistic renewal - an enhancement that goes far beyond the former state of existence. It is then that brokenness turns to beauty, joy, and purpose. The glory of the present house will be greater than its former, the prophet explains in Haggai 2:9. At the time of creation in the Garden of Eden, God used to stroll with man in the evenings (the Book of Genesis describes this), but after the restoration, when the new Heaven and earth are created, God comes to dwell with man. We sometimes fail to understand how precious we are in God’s sight. God longs for each soul, but for men to recognize this, it takes effort, the inspiration of the Spirit of God that opens the eyes of our hearts. Jesus’s coming to the earth in human form was one of the many initiations of God for us to know Him in fullness, in truth, and in the Spirit. Can you enumerate a few of God’s such initiations, he undertook to befriend mankind?  When He comes to dwell with us, everything changes - situations, perspectives, priorities, and even our very purpose. Let this Advent season be a conscious effort on our part to befriend the Lord, and to urge Him to be with us. We start this by spending time in prayer, making every supplication of our hearts known to Him, and asking Him to have His way in us. Wishing you a blessed Sunday with the Lord. Amen

22/12/25, 10:35 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Whoever believes in me, as the Scripture has said, ‘Out of His heart will flow rivers of Living water’ [John 7:38]

Out of your heart will flow rivers of life-giving water. Even then, you will never seem to own up, because it was given to you, that too freely. You have been made His (Jesus’s) channel, by which many may be sustained, because you trusted in Him, who is the Lord of All. This truth, St James explains further by saying – Every gift, ‘good and perfect’ comes from above - the Father of Lights, with whom there is no variation (James 1:17). When He ascended to the Heavens, glorified by the Father, He took with him captives of men, and to many were given gifts, the Bible teaches us. The gift of intellect, understanding, consoling, comforting, and encouraging, teaching, of praying for one another… all these were ordained to us by the Glorified Lord. He did so, that we may not contain or possess them or accumulate them for ourselves, but these be poured out to be the cause of growth for many. It is from the heart that good and evil come. When with the Lord, His Spirit fills us with love from the Father, urging our conscience to filter all thoughts, actions, and words through the goggles of love. We are then taught to appreciate the good in another, to value other lives, to perceive as Christ and with Him, to co-create abundance of life in our world. Like any other exercise, being with Jesus needs to be continuous, not just for one Christmas, but for every day. We slowly get remoulded, fitted into our purpose, turn out to be the ‘masterpiece’ He created us to be. And day in day out, unknowingly, His will and way be ours. God bless you

24/12/25, 09:44 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘The Lord says to my Lord: Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies your footstool’ [Psalm 110:1]

As we read and pray this Messianic Psalm, the Lord tells you and me to calm ourselves. To move away from the noise, clamour, and distractions of light and sound, to sit beside Him in worship. To surrender to Him our faculties, our capabilities, intellect, and heart, and wait in the stillness for His move. All the troubles of our hearts, He sees, hears, and knows, the cares and struggles of our times, the wishes and dreams yet unfulfilled, the obstacles and concerns that pose as enemies. These will be made ‘footstool’ for us to emerge stronger when He comes into our lives as a baby in the manger. At times, we wait and are impatient, seeing nothing tangible and transparent appear for long. Into the vulnerability of our lives, God comes, and He comes with graces when it is a willing and abiding heart that calls. Those who once sat in darkness, who dwelt in the shadows of death, then would see the light of Life. King David, who penned down this ‘royal psalm’ was convinced of what would occur in life when the Lord rode Him through victorious heights. He struck down his enemies - the Philistines, Moabites, and the Arameans; made many submissive, who paid tributes to the king. For his love for the Lord, he was promised an everlasting dynasty to rule over Israel. In all that he did, he prospered. The Lord, King and Master of all, urges you too to stay by His side, to let go and give Him the chance. He will cause you to ride upon the heights of the earth. For this is the promise of the Messiah. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

26/12/25, 10:20 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Oh, how abundant is your goodness, which you have stored up for those who fear you and worked for those who take refuge in you, in the sight of the children of mankind!’ [Psalm 31:19]

God, who knows and cares for us so personally, has stored up for us His abundance of goodness.  What would mean abundance (for me) may not be the abundance that applies to you, as each one of us is unique. And He gives these gifts to us not behind closed doors, but in the sight of all mankind. The Christmas season is an occasion for introspection and thanking the Lord for all His graces and blessings in our lives. It is also an occasion to assess the level of goodness we have attained or grown over the years. Psalm 31 is a prayer of trust, of confidence that in all situations of life, the Lord stands close to us, to uplift, console, elevate, and protect. At the outset, everything may not seem soothing, but each has its specific purpose in time. Many of these have helped us through the years to walk by faith, to be more generous, not to think as the world does, to dare to be different, to endure through the thick and thin, and more than all to wait in hope of the Lord’s coming and His works. And Jesus has promised us a reward for this. Be faithful, even to the point of death, I will give you the victorious crown of life, were the words of Jesus to the people (Church of Smyrna) who went through deep suffering and persecution for the sake of the Gospel. Let us pray for this courage to be worthy of the Crown of Glory of the Lord. Amen

27/12/25, 09:35 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘the life was made manifest, and we saw it, and testify to it, and proclaim to you the eternal life which was with the father and made manifest to us’ [1 John 1:2]
Along with all the other apostles, John witnessed the Life, which was the Light of the world, that came to dwell with mankind, because of His love for them. He was with the father in creation, and the Word that co-created with the Father and the Holy Spirit, It was Him who was to sustain all, and relieve them from the corruption of the evil one. St Paul, understanding the essence and magnitude of this mystery,  and of how much mankind was in dire need of this Saving grace, described that it was the whole universe that groaned in pain for the redemption of mankind from its captivity to sin. Jesus was the Eternal life, who was with God, the Father; made manifest to our eyes in human form. That, we who have entrusted our hopes in Him would be sealed by His blood (of the New Covenant) and made worthy to be God’s own people. This was what prompted St John to speak of nothing else than love. And with the love of God overflowing in Him, he was given the revelation of end times and of Eternity. You and I too need to be filled with His love, the love that heals, sanctifies, restores, and saves. Let us pray for this grace to be with Jesus, to open our eyes, ears, and hearts to the revelation He gives us as we go forth on our journey towards Eternity. Amen

29/12/25, 10:16 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘He persevered because he saw him who is invisible’ [Hebrews 11:27]

The foremost among life skills is the virtue of perseverance. The life of Moses, quoted as today’s Word from the Lord, teaches us what it means to forbear and persevere. Perseverance is triggered by faith, trust in God’s goodness and faithfulness; that His plan is always way and beyond our thoughts, expectations, and what we can imagine. The birth of Moses occurred at a time of deep crisis when ‘the Israelites’ faced threats of annihilation in Egypt. The Pharaoh had ordered the killing of all baby boys born to Israelite people. Moses’s mother, owing to her faith in God, hid the baby in a reed basket, setting him afloat on the river Nile. The baby was taken up (from the river) by chance by Pharaoh’s sister, who raised him as her adopted son, and thus Moses lived as Prince in the Egyptian palace till he was forty years of age. All the skills Moses acquired by way of training at the palace was part of God’s definite plan for the liberation and uplift of His people. Moses’ distinct experiences in life taught him of what it meant to be faithful to God, to be led by His call, and to live according to His commands. It is this that instilled in him the virtue of perseverance that helped him to move on in life, to accept life’s realities, and ask God for its meaning. In every state of his living, Moses saw the hands of the Almighty, he saw Him who was invisible, and thus persevered although he never continued as Prince in Egypt, lived for years in the wilderness, was reduced to none other than a shepherd, and finally had to put up with a rebellious and faithless people whom he was called and chosen to liberate. He did not even enter the promised land, a sad truth, but was always favoured in the eyes of the Lord. God has high expectations about you and me. Believe and ask him today. Amen
