30/12/25, 12:28 - Megha Sherino: Switching from **LPG cylinders** to a **piped natural gas (PNG)** system at home comes with some very real, everyday advantages. Here’s a clear, practical breakdown—teacher mode with a friend’s tone 😊

![Image](https://blog.etundra.com/wp-content/Media/2017/03/dormont_flexible_gas_hose_IMG_6558.jpg)

![Image](https://i.pinimg.com/736x/5e/f7/12/5ef712ac44350048c8f838a231fd8e4a.jpg)

![Image](https://www.think-gas.com/img/WhatsAppImage2025-09-22at11.40.15PM-1758565118.jpeg)

![Image](https://www.jaxenergy.com/images/uploads/content_images/JEA_ProperFlame_LCA.jpg)

---

## 🌱 1. Cleaner & Healthier

* PNG burns **cleaner than LPG**, producing fewer pollutants.
* Less soot on vessels and walls → a **cleaner kitchen**.
* Better indoor air quality, especially important for daily cooking.

---

## 💸 2. More Economical in the Long Run

* **Pay only for what you use** (metered billing).
* No cylinder refilling charges or delivery fees.
* Prices are usually **more stable** compared to LPG cylinder rates.

---

## 🔥 3. Uninterrupted Gas Supply

* No fear of “gas finished in the middle of cooking”.
* Continuous supply—no booking, no waiting.
* Ideal for homes with **regular or heavy cooking needs**.

---

## 🧯 4. Safer When Used Properly

* PNG is **lighter than air**, so it disperses quickly if leaked.
* Modern systems include **leak detectors, pressure regulators, and safety valves**.
* Eliminates risks linked to storing heavy cylinders indoors.

---

## 🏠 5. Space-Saving & Convenient

* No bulky LPG cylinders in the kitchen or balcony.
* Cleaner layout and better use of kitchen space.
* No physical effort in changing cylinders—especially helpful for elderly users.

---

## 🌍 6. Environment-Friendly

* Lower carbon emissions compared to LPG.
* Supports cleaner urban energy infrastructure.
* A small but meaningful step toward sustainable living.

---

## 🛠️ 7. Easy Maintenance

* Centralized supply means **less handling and fewer mechanical issues**.
* Annual inspections are usually handled by the gas provider.

---

## ⚖️ A Fair Note (Limitations)

* Initial installation cost (pipeline + meter).
* Availability depends on your city/local infrastructure.
* Minor inconvenience during installation phase.

---

### 🧠 Bottom line

If PNG is available in your area, it’s a **cleaner, safer, more convenient, and often cheaper** upgrade from LPG—especially for long-term residential use.

If you want, I can also:

* Compare **LPG vs PNG in a table**
* Explain **how billing works**
* Help you decide based on **apartment vs independent house**

Just tell me where you’d like to zoom in 👍
30/12/25, 12:32 - Megha Sherino: Switching to a **PNG (Piped Natural Gas)** system is mostly a one-time setup followed by smooth sailing. Here’s a **clear, step-by-step guide**, practical and India-specific 👇

![Image](https://www.plumbwellservices.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/KeyBenefits-ConvertingNaturalGas.jpg)

![Image](https://v-gazolution.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/IMG-20210825-WA0002.jpg)

![Image](https://www.tirupatigasservices.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Technician-Installing-Copper-Gas-Pipeline-768x512-1.webp)

![Image](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/I/71EmOdpyD0L.jpg)

---

## 🧭 Step 1: Check PNG Availability in Your Area

* PNG is available **only where city gas pipelines exist**.
* Check:

  * Your local gas distributor’s website
  * Customer care helpline
  * Apartment association / neighbors (quickest way)

👉 If your apartment already has PNG, you’re almost done.

---

## 📝 Step 2: Apply for a PNG Connection

You can apply:

* **Online** (most providers have portals)
* **Offline** at the gas company office

You’ll usually need:

* ID proof (Aadhaar / PAN)
* Address proof
* Latest electricity bill
* Passport-size photo
* Property ownership proof / rental agreement
* NOC from landlord or apartment association (if required)

---

## 💰 Step 3: Pay Installation Charges

Charges typically include:

* Security deposit
* Meter installation cost
* Internal pipeline charges (kitchen line)

💡 Some providers allow **EMI options**, so you don’t have to pay everything upfront.

---

## 🔧 Step 4: Installation & Safety Check

Technicians will:

* Install the **gas meter**
* Lay pipeline from meter → kitchen
* Connect your stove (or advise nozzle change)
* Conduct **leak and pressure tests**
* Explain emergency shut-off procedures

⏱️ Usually completed in **1 day**, sometimes 2.

---

## 🔥 Step 5: Stove Compatibility Check

* Most LPG stoves **can be used for PNG** with a **small nozzle change**
* The technician usually handles this
* Or they’ll guide you to an authorized service center

---

## 🧾 Step 6: Start Using PNG & Monthly Billing

* Gas usage is **metered**
* Monthly bill (like electricity or water)
* Payment via app, UPI, net banking, etc.

---

## ⚠️ Important Safety Tips

* Never tamper with pipelines
* Keep the main valve accessible
* Report even minor gas smell immediately
* Do not enclose the gas meter fully

---

## ✅ Quick Checklist

✔ PNG available in your area
✔ Documents ready
✔ Installation fee paid
✔ Stove nozzle adjusted
✔ Safety demo attended

---

### 🧠 Final Thought (Friend Advice)

If PNG is available where you live, **switching is absolutely worth it**—especially for daily cooking. Once installed, you’ll wonder how you ever managed cylinder bookings 😄

If you want, tell me:

* **Apartment or independent house?**
* **Which city you’re in?**

I’ll tailor the exact process, cost range, and timeline for your situation.

‘The righteous live forever, and their reward is with the Lord; the Most High takes care of them. Therefore, they will receive a glorious crown and a beautiful diadem from the hand of the Lord, because with his right hand he will cover them, and with his arm he will shield them’ [Wisdom 5:15-16]

God’s Spirit enumerates the list of rewards for those who are righteous. The righteous live by faith and not by sight. It is their trust in the Lord that justifies them, though they may be weak and vulnerable as any other human being. If we were to understand what it means to be righteous from the perspective of the Lord, it would contradict the dictates and rationale of this world. At his hour of death on the Cross, there was a man justified, sanctified, and declared righteous by Jesus to inherit the Kingdom. He was none other than the repentant thief, the one who humbly submitted to Jesus that he was a sinner, and finally made his supplication to be remembered by Him in his kingdom. The woman (a public sinner) stoned to death as per the Law of Moses was another whom Jesus justified and uplifted to work for His kingdom, to redeem many souls. What Jesus expects of us is the bent and disposition of our hearts, whether we are willing to begin again from scratch, whether we ardently choose to love, to serve, to rectify, and to come back. Heaven and the hosts of angels rejoice at this. This last day of the year is a time for introspection -  of what were our ways, our motives, the changes Jesus has required of us, of whether we stay worthy of being remembered by Him in His kingdom. Remember the rewards are numerous – Divine protection, deliverance, glory, and empowerment (just a few of them), but the most significant of them all – His unfailing love and presence with us in each step we take forward in life. Amen

03/01/26, 08:18 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘So then, those who suffer according to God’s will should commit themselves to their faithful Creator and continue to do good’ [1 Peter 4:19]

The suffering that bears fruit – to this the Spirit of the Lord draws our attention and understanding today. Of being subject to difficulties, pain, ailments, insult, or the fiery ordeal sent (permitted by the Lord) to test your faith, as St Peter names this type of suffering, this is what it means to suffer as a Christian, a prototype of Christ. Now, suffering is never acceptable to anyone, or pleasant to the human senses. And when pain strikes one, the one who is afflicted only knows what he or she is going through in life. It is God, and no other who can provide him the console or comfort he deserves at that time. Therefore, as Christians, we are called to be merciful to those undergoing suffering, to refrain from judging another in pain (from our perspective). The one who goes through rough times in life must believe that he is treading the very path that Christ did. The Spirit of Christ Jesus, who raised him up to glory will as well abide in this one, who walks with Christ. So let us offer the hardships and sufferings of our lives to our faithful God, and continue to do good, for this is what is expected of us, for Christ to be glorified through us. You and I are witnesses to the Lord, and called to be the Light to this world, Let none of us bear sufferings on account of our misdeeds, but let us bear the sufferings of Christ in us that builds, supports, plants, and triumphs for greater glory to be manifested in the lives of many. Jesus be with you. Amen

07/01/26, 09:57 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘And He saw that they were making headway painfully, for the wind was against them…’ [Mark 6:48]

You and I are special in the eyes of Jesus, for the heart of the Lord never fails, forsakes, or gives up on anyone. Jesus knew that His disciples understood nothing much from the multiplication of loaves and fish for the crowds. They were made participants (to serve food to the gathering of 5000 men) and not mere spectators in this miracle. They had their fill – the twelve baskets full of bread and of fish. Jesus, perceiving the state of their hearts, desired to teach them the most difficult lesson in life, of trusting in Him. So, soon after, even before dispersing the crowds, the Lord sent His disciples to go before Him to the other side of the shore. As disciples of Jesus, the Lord permits certain unavoidable circumstances/uncertainties in our lives too (those we never expected, or were never prepared for). Being His disciples, we have been faithful to Him, with committed hearts and determination, no doubt.  But the twists He allowed for us were meant to train us to emerge better out (stronger and firm in faith), never to perish. We must call out to Him, ask Him counsel, to make wise decisions, to do the right things (that which the disciples in this narrative failed to do). As was his response to them, He would surely accompany us to show the way, that we make the best of the situation. All the painful headways you are making in life today are but opportunities for you to make breakthroughs in life, with Jesus accompanying you. Increase our faith, Lord, we pray. Amen

09/01/26, 10:32 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life’ [1 John 5:12]

The Old Testament was a time of preparation, when God prepared His people, Israel, to receive and proclaim His Salvation (that was yet to be unveiled in time). God gave His people the Ten Commandments then. The Chosen people were required to memorise these rules, obey them conscientiously, and teach them to their posterity. All prosperity and blessings were to flow to them when they obeyed these commands. God’s command, meant to be simple and definite, was over time subject to many intrusions, erosions, and denudations owing to human and cultural interference. Thus, in place of the Ten fundamental tenets, there arose 600 or more precepts, subclauses and principles that were to be followed closely, regardless of whether these fitted and benefited human living or the common good. Jesus came into our midst to set things right for us, to point to us the law that was of utmost priority in God’s kingdom, which was to be fulfilled above board. Honouring and loving God first, and then to love and respect others, created in the image of God. This law was not something new, but an affirmation of the Ten Commandments previously given by God to His people. Today’s Word from God assures us of this testimony given by God the Father – that in Jesus Christ we have life for Eternity. Life comes from obeying Jesus and His commandment to love. Let us pray for this grace to love, like the Lord, to obey like Him, and to partake in Eternal life that He gives us. Amen

10/01/26, 09:55 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘And they came to John and said to him, ‘Rabbi, he who was with you across the Jordan, to whom you bore witness- look, he is baptizing, and all are going to him’ [John 3:26]

What the world and our senses may lead us to think is often the half-truth, one that is relative. It would seem to us a kind of sugar-coated pill that in itself is awkwardly bitter. The Jew, mentioned in Chapter 3 of John’s Gospel, in the passage of John the Baptist exalting Jesus, was doing just this. He approached the holy prophet under the pretext of allegiance and respect for him (John) to alert him to the need for greater caution in permitting others to take up his mission of baptizing the people. What he had foreseen was true, but not completely. Neither he nor John knew the absolute truth, though John had testified to Jesus as the Lamb of God. Nevertheless, John knew that his destiny was to prepare the way for the Messiah. Therefore, he proved faithful to his calling. Not for his glory, but for God’s glory did he work. Not his mission, but the mission conferred on him, even before he was born, was what he was determined to live for. He had no time to entertain any discussion, gossip, or agenda of another, thus leaving no iota of doubt, he boldly responded to the allegation made by the Jew against Jesus. And what he said rings loud and clear to us, who at times waste energy and time criticizing, back-biting, and even condemning others, about whom we hardly know a bit. Unless given from the Heavens, none would receive anything. Let this be our inspiration to encourage, respect, and uphold the dignity of another, whom God created. Amen

11/01/26, 09:50 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘In faithfulness, he will bring forth justice; he will not falter or be discouraged till he establishes justice on earth. In his teachings, the islands will put their hope’ [Isaiah 42:3-4]

When iniquity piled up and distorted the face of the earth, God regretted his plan of having created mankind. Though moulded in the image and likeness of God to live as His reflection on the earth, man succumbed to his concupiscence, and all what was in his heart was vile and wickedness. Man found disfavour in God’s sight because of his lack of faithfulness and integrity, his inability to admit his mistakes, to repent or set right his wrong ways or to make reparation for sins. Thus, He sent His son, in the image of man, to teach men what was most endearing to God. The Messiah was an extraordinary man because he was faithful to the core. What was denied to mankind because of their sinfulness, ‘justice’ was brought forth by the Messiah. It was the mission of the Messiah to restore mankind to their righteousness before God and man. He was born (Servant of the Lord) to uplift the poor, downtrodden, the discouraged, the outcasts and marginalised, and more than all to open a sky of hope to the Islands, the lands in the dark. He would not refrain from completing His works till justice was established and affirmed on the earth. If you and I are disappointed or disturbed owing to life’s twists and turns, let not disbelief take over us, but let us with increased faith, call the Lord Jesus, the Messiah, asking Him for restoration, peace and justice. He knows the way for He is the way. Wishing you a blessed Sunday. Amen

12/01/26, 10:57 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Her rival used to provoke her grievously to irritate her, because the Lord had closed her womb. So it went on year by year. As often as she went up to the House of the Lord, she used to provoke her…...’ [1 Samuel 1:6-7]

Hannah was constantly provoked (insulted) by her rival, Peninnah. This went on year by year, as often as the family went to the House of the Lord at Shiloh, to offer sacrifices and prayers. Because Peninnah received the ‘blessed portion’ after the sacrifice in greater quantities, owing to her many sons and daughters in contrast to Hannah, who had no offspring. To her (Peninnah), the favour of God (children and the distinction she received because of this) was perceived as a distinction, a matter of pride, and one of the major reasons to mock and belittle Hannah, who underwent deep suffering and ridicule (of course, not due to any particular fault of hers). Never did Hannah imagine nor, did she ever realize that, ‘this phase’ of humiliation in her life was (in fact) elevating her up to a great door of blessings. The pain and agony, she bore was unmatched and unthinkable of in a society that was highly hypocritical and opposed to women. What Peninnah failed to understand through her blessing was made known to Hannah, through her humiliation, suffering, and endurance. As was in her days, so it is in ours. Grace flows when there is a vent or vacuum in the human mind/heart through suffering, pain, or when it opens its heart in humility. While the latter may be hard for an ordinary human being due to his/her love for the self, the former is what the Lord allows in one’s life, which predisposes the heart to receive greater graces and mercy. Hannah is remembered not only because of Samuel (the greatest prophet), and her other sons and daughters, but also because of the fervent prayer she made, inviting God’s intervention in her life. Her canticle of praise is renowned and being recited even to this day. Let us pray for this power of prayer, and for a heart like Hannah that glorified the Lord for all. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
13/01/26, 10:22 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘There was in their Synagogue a man with an unclean spirit……Jesus of Nazareth, what have you to do with us. Jesus rebuked the Spirit……and the Unclean Spirit came out of him’ [Mark 1:23-26]

God willed the deliverance of this man, who had an unclean Spirit. While he had been in the Synagogue on the Sabbath for prayers and worship, when Jesus came in to teach, the Spirit in him was incited. It (the unclean Spirit) knew that in the presence of Jesus, it had no business and no existence. To escape being noticed, judged, and being evicted by Jesus, it put on the garb of praise, which none in the community would ever recognise. Jesus immediately rebuked this Spirit and cast him out of the man who had been bearing the brunt of its atrocities all the while in life. This narrative in the beginning of Mark’s Gospel was meant to introduce Jesus as the Holy One of God, the Son of God, who was sent to earth with the mission of saving mankind. This plan of God and His mission was also known to the evil one. Therefore, he lost no opportunity to thwart the good works of Jesus during his public ministry. Nevertheless, whoever wished to receive healing, deliverance, and support from Jesus, whoever approached him with their needs, were never sent empty-handed. Our Lord continues His mission of deliverance, healing, and restoration to this day in our midst, and irrespective of race, ethnicity, caste, religion, or societal status, whoever approaches Him with faith and love, will surely receive the blessing due to him, and much more than they ever desired. This is who Jesus is; Let us pray for increased faith and for a heart that follows Him always. Amen

Dear Student,

This is to inform you that your Fee Demand Slip has been updated to include the Industry Training Course Fee of Rs. 20,000/-, in addition to the approved fee structure. This component was not reflected earlier in the already published Demand slips. After the publication of the Academic Year 2025-26 fee structure, the University introduced Industry Training Courses for all Second and Third Year B.Tech students. As the finalisation of these courses took place after the fee structure was released, the corresponding fee could not be included in the previous year’s Fee Demand Slip. Accordingly, for the Academic Year 2026-27, an additional amount of Rs. 20,000/- towards the Industry Training Course has been added to your existing fee structure. Please note that this revision is applicable only to Second and Third Year Fee only. The fee for Fourth Year fee structure will remain as per the approved fee structure, unless any new programme is introduced for the Fourth Year in the future.

The revised Fee Demand Slip will be made available on your KP portal shortly. You are requested to review the details carefully once it is published.

Thank you for your cooperation.

14/01/26, 10:34 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Let us go on to the next towns, that I may preach there also, for that is why I came out’ [Mark 1:38]

Jesus healed many and cast out demons from many who came to Him in Capernaum to be healed. He then moved out of the crowds to a desolate place to pray (even His disciples were not aware of where he had gone). Through this, Jesus was demonstrating to His disciples what a mission grounded in the Kingdom of God would look like. It may at times fail human interests, instincts, and ways of thinking, because it is aligned and tuned to fulfilling God’s will. You and I are called to discern the will of God for us and work to its completion. This does not happen by itself, nor does it occur when counselled by another, or when in the midst of the noises and confusion around us. It occurs deep within one’s heart, where God dwells, and where one can make an interaction only if he distances himself from the world around. If only one seeks the Lord, will He be found, and to the one who sincerely seeks Him. Sometimes, the so-called vexatious problems that we incur in life are but occasions to seek the Lord, and a personal invite He gives us to be with Him whenever we knock at His door for help. Next time you get into something distressing, remember it is God calling you to spend some time with Him. All you need to do then, is not go to every other pillar and post searching for a solution to your problem, or waste yourself in agony and stress because of something beyond your making and control (as we usually tend to), but reach out to Him with a humble and submissive heart. You and I are called to engage ourselves in the works of His kingdom, not to loaf around seeking popularity and approval from those around us. His friendship with you is all the approval you would need in this life. Amen

‘If you will, you can make me clean. Moved with pity, He stretched out his hand and touched him and said to him, I will; be clean’ [Mark 1:40-41]

 We have a God who is compassion and mercy, whose tenderness overflows, seeping through the cracks and crevices of our being. You and I can be ‘fallen’, the weaknesses in our physique that keep interrupting with a quality life, or wounded by sorrows of a lifetime, or those wishes and dreams unfulfilled, the sinfulness of our flesh, the negativity of our thoughts, ….  If ever you felt you were most incompatible with circumstances, situations and people around you, it is time for you to rise and ask for help. Some of us believe that there is no turnaround; all that we could do would be, to linger in there, adjusting, and trying to fit in, like a musician fine-tuning a guitar with broken strings. The Holy Spirit teaches us that things are never sorted out that way, but by taking it to the Lord of All, who knows how best to rectify and revive. What the poor leper (who had absolutely no hope) did was exactly this. He gave up on his ways, thoughts, family, and society to which he was an outcast, and reached out to the Lord Jesus, who could help him out of his situation. He was convinced that if the Lord desired to heal, he would be made clean. Thus, with a heart of humility, he approached Jesus, expressing his trust in him, and asked Him for a healing. It was not just his body that was healed, but also his heart, mind, and spirit. What he never imagined or asked for was given to him, the touch of God, which was healing for a lifetime. What you and I need in our lives too - is a mind and heart touched by God, by seeking Him always especially, when in need. Amen

16/01/26, 10:19 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘But the people refused to listen to Samuel. No! they said. We want a King over us. Then we will be like all the other nations, with a King to lead us and go out before us and fight our battles’ [1 Samuel 8;19-20]

We might think of the People of God (the Israelites) as imprudent and foolish. These were those who (themselves) witnessed God’s power working for them, and God’s favour towards them, ever since they were slaves in Egypt to their inheriting the promised land. Even when all was well for them, they were worried; anxious about what would become of them in the future. What would occur to them after the death of Samuel? Who would lead and deliver them from the attacks of their enemies (the Philistines)? These were the ones who were far more advanced (than the Israelites) in artillery, warfare, and weapons. Like what has gripped many of us today, the fear of uncertainty, and the possibility of ‘adversities in future’ was what tormented these people who were, those days guided by Samuel. Now, Samuel, the trustworthy prophet of God was getting old, and his sons were no better a replacement (to take up his legacy) of supporting God’s people (as they were wicked and lacked integrity). Looking at nations around them, what they saw was greener pastures, the ability of pagan nations to thrive and sustain themselves solely because they had kings to lead them. This was what provoked the people to the need for a king. What God desired of them was their incessant reliance on Him, their provider. So does the Lord desire of us today. To trust Him, to believe and acknowledge in our hearts Him as King of our lives. Let us pray and plead for this kind of faith to stand firm in the face of uncertainties and fear, by looking at Jesus, our Master and King, and being able to obey what He tells us to do. Amen

‘Samuel took a flask of olive oil and poured it on Saul’s head and kissed him, saying, “Has not the Lord anointed you ruler over his heritage?”’ [1 Samule 10:1]

Over the heritage of the Lord, ‘Israel’, Saul was chosen King to protect, serve, and cause to flourish God’s people. God’s faithfulness is in this that, though the people clambered for a king, all for the wrong reasons (to express their prowess and pride as other nations did), God chose for them the ‘one’ from them, who had the determination, grit, outlook (both perspective and appearance) to steward His people. That God chose and anointed him to rule over Israel was a matter of shock to Saul, who never considered himself worthy to adorn a position as glorious as this. In his perspective, ‘his’ was the least among tribes and among all clans of the tribe of Benjamin. Therefore, though he did as Samuel had told him to do, he feared of telling anyone what the prophet had said about him. God’s decision of Saul’s Kingship was proclaimed and publicly revealed to all Israel by Samuel, the seer. Saul’s initiation to kingship was thus glorious, marvellous, and exceptional owing to his humility, which drew people to him immediately. Similarly (as Saul was chosen and exalted by the Lord) you and I have been chosen by God, set apart before the foundations of the world, in Jesus Christ for those specific works of Goodness that were destined for our doing alone. By way of our Baptism, we were publicly recognised, loved, and exalted by the Lord to be the stewards of His Kingdom. Have we reciprocated His love, concern, and faithfulness for us with sincerity and resolve to serve? Let us pray for His grace to take over our human weakness, to be able to love and serve Him better in His kingdom. Amen

‘…………...For I am honoured in the eyes of the Lord, and my God has become my strength’ [Isaiah 49:5]

The choice of God is what matters the most because of which You and I are honoured. We were chosen by Him (The one who formed us in our mother’s womb) as servants, His messengers, for a specific cause. There have been times when you and I have questioned God of His purpose for us. There have been times we tried giving up, saying that nothing ever worked in life. But even in those bitter moments of life, the Lord cared for us. His regard for us was utmost, and He willed to see us get back to ourselves, and set an example to those who knew not the Light. These were those instances when we were honoured in the eyes of our Lord, not that we were successful in all that we did. But that we cared for His calling, we did our best (all that we knew) for the sake of His kingdom, and we encouraged peace when our hearts were turbulent and disappointed. In all these circumstances, it was the Lord who became our strength, who counselled us in the way we must take, and calmed our Spirits with His overwhelming presence. Jesus cheers you up to keep pacing ahead until you reach your destiny, even if the world has nothing to tell you. He adds on, what you feel so insignificant all the while, were taken up by Him to make you the Light for the nations and for His Gospel to reach the ends of the world. This Sunday message for you is that you are precious and honoured in the sight of Jesus. Amen

19/01/26, 09:38 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Has the Lord as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of the Lord? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to listen than the fat of the rams’ [1 Samuel 15:22]

To know what God desires of us is the first step in understanding our true selves. What King Saul failed to realize and what David always remembered was what proved them distinct in the eyes of the Lord. David called himself ‘a humble shepherd’ whom God elevated to the position of kingship of all Israel. All his deeds, words, and the rationale behind all decisions he took were founded on this realization or formation of his conscience. Thus, though he failed at times, severely succumbing to sin, he was able to make a comeback and reclaim the Spirit of God given to him. Saul, on the other hand, forgot all those circumstances of the past that, though he was little and unworthy, God chose him to be the leader of all the tribes of Israel. Therefore, after each victory he won over his enemies, he tried all the more to appease his subjects - the people of Israel, convinced that this would secure his House for kingship through generations. Let not what proved destructive to Saul and his house transpire to us who believe in the Lord. What the Lord asks of us (not what we feel we must do) is what brings success to our lives, families, and generations. It is in obeying Him that we grow more into ourselves. He has called us to be more gentle, humble, merciful, and yielding to Him. This is what pleases the Lord above all rituals, sacrifices, religious obligations, and the like. No human discontent can put you down, unless decided so by the Lord. Take courage and strengthen yourself in the Lord. Amen

20/01/26, 10:25 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Therefore, Saul sent messengers to Jesse and said, “Send me, David your son, who is with the sheep”’ [1 Samuel 16:19]

Israel’s King Saul was in deep trouble. He was being tormented by an evil Spirit, which would bother him even to the extent of disrupting his kingly responsibilities. This had become public to all those in the royal service, and so they came by suggesting practical measures to help the king overcome his present crisis. Had there been anyone in the King’s court/his family to persuade him to reach out to the Lord for a remedy, his destiny would have been different. To compare this to what occurs in our daily lives is what God’s Spirit inspires us to do. When a crisis hits hard, and all of a sudden, what are we prone to doing? Of course, by hook and crook, we would explore all the measures at hand to set things right, and that too at once, without anyone knowing of it. Are not we too supposed to turn to the Lord at first and ask Him of the immediate steps to be taken then? Going by what Saul did, he was indirectly (unknowingly) working towards God’s plan (about David) being progressively fulfilled. David was accorded entry into the royal service of the King by this very incident. From then on, the house of Saul proved to be the formation house of David, Israel’s impending King and the favoured one of God. To David, this would have meant a paradigm shift, a total transformation from that of a humble shepherd to a warrior whom God chose him to be. God’s ways are high and can never be thwarted or averted by human wisdom or intellect. Those obstacles that you and I see in life, those that were the work of others, our weaknesses, physical incapacities, or the like (those that we have always regretted about) could be stepping stones for us to climb higher, to unimaginable heights. For this is God’s doing for us and for Him ‘Nothing’ is impossible. Amen

21/01/26, 10:17 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And all this assembly may know that the Lord saves not with sword and spear. For the Battle is the Lord’s…….’ [1 Samuel 17:47]

In those days, war had broken out between the forces of Israel and the Philistines. The Philistines, who were determined to destroy their neighbours (Israelites), approached them this time with a new strategy to outwit them. They camped on a hill opposite to where the Israelite armies camped, which gave them a superior view of all the surrounding places.  Their target was the Judean highlands that they intended to conquer. Spotlighting a giant warrior, Goliath (who was far greater in might, also because of the weapons he used that were made of iron) in the forefront, they challenged the armies of Israel to agree to a single combat with this champion, that they may finally surrender to the Philistines. When the hearts of God’s people were failing in the face of the crisis, God chose David to strengthen them. To be a witness to them all of God’s might, valour, and his faithfulness to protect His people when they cried out to Him. Neither was David a warrior, nor did he know (or ever was trained) how to use a sword, shield, or spear in battle. He went forth courageously against the giant warrior with his sling and stones and with a heart that believed that this battle was ultimately the Lord’s. He would fight for His people to save them. Of David’s conviction and trust, we read in the Book of Psalms thus, ‘He trains my hands for war, and my fingers for battle………… He is my stronghold, my shield and refuge, who subdues people under me (Ps 144:1,2). It is God who trains us to face our daily troubles, hardships and adventures. God wills that we must understand that He saves not in the ways that men do (by sword and spear), but with Him we emerge every time stronger, firmer, resilient, and adept to call the shots in life, not just for us to be triumphant, but to show the way to many to fight their battles with courage and conviction. Amen

22/01/26, 09:48 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘As soon as he had finished speaking to Saul, the soul of Jonathan was knit to the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as his own soul’ [1 Samuel 18:1]

It is the Spirit of the Lord that bound the souls of David and Jonathan in friendship. Jonathan, an unfailing warrior and the Crown Prince of Israel, was not disappointed at David’s triumph over Goliath, the champion of the Philistines. He was ‘all the more’ glad to see the people of Israel saved and delivered from the hands of the enemy, through the instrument of David, whom God anointed as their upcoming King. The Israelites ascribed glory to David, and he was portrayed in the Books of Samuel, Kings, and Chronicles, as one with iconoclastic majesty – all because the Lord loved Him and so rewarded him for his faithfulness. But for God’s greater plan of Salvation to unfold in history, there were many chosen to pave the way to make things work for the better. And one of them (often not greatly remembered) is Jonathan, the elder son of King Saul. He was the real King-maker to David, who was merely a shepherd by rank. What he chose to disregard (though ignored by men and history) - the rights of primogeniture to King Saul (his father), combat and fighting skills, his superiority over any other citizen in Israel – was colossal in the eyes of the Lord. God sees and understands, even when no other does. What Jonathan set as a precedent to us, in a world that cares for nothing and has grown cold, will be remembered loud and clear in the hearts of believers. Instead of vying with David for the throne and curbing his possibilities of survival in a hostile Kingdom, Jonathan chose to protect and support David, to be a doer of what God desired of him, even at the cost of his personal losses. The Spirit of God today enlightens us to ponder into our own lives, the people whom God has given us, trusting that we would ensure their growth, protection, and nurturing to their destiny. Have we been faithful to our call? Or have we responded according to our human instincts, aspirations, and grudges to pacify our interests alone? Obedience to His will is our call. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
23/01/26, 09:51 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For in you my soul takes refuge; in the shadow of your wings I will take refuge, till the storms of destruction pass by. I cry out to God Most High, to God who fulfils His purpose for me’ [Psalms 57:1,2]

When people and circumstances around me rule out a possibility for a comeback, in God Most High will I take comfort. The soul may be wearied when one after another ‘adversity’ meets a man face to face, and to him it may seem that the dead end is near. More than any other, this soul needs upliftment, and encouragement can never come from the world. It comes from the Lord, who not only knows the beginning and end of everything, but also is so close to the one who struggles. David poured out his heart and the pangs of a wearied soul (in prayer), that were distraught by affliction and insecurity for quite a while. If he were to look at the people who supported him, or the context in which he was at the moment, he would never have had the courage to pray like this. The Spirit of God enlightens us through the example of David to stand firm in the face of adversity by first strengthening the soul – by seeking strength and protection from above. Once this is achieved, the mind (and heart) of the person is prepared to think, reason out, and define what the possible turnaround on the matter could be. Therefore, like David, we must cry out to the Lord till the turbulence has passed, till the affliction has ceased, and till the phase of testing has been complete, so that God’s purpose for us may be fulfilled and in his time. David got the strength to discern (rightly) that paying tit for tat (evil with evil) to Saul (God’s anointed and king) was not desired by God. Instead, he made use of the opportunity to arouse repentance and conversion in this King. He patiently waited for his vindication from God through His intervention in life. Let us pray for the grace of discernment and fortitude that builds up a man with extraordinary power. Amen

24/01/26, 09:48 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For as his share is who goes down into the battle, so shall his share be who stays by the baggage. They shall share alike’ [1 Samuel 30:24]

The ones (soldiers) who accompanied him to the battle front against the ‘Amalekites’ were those who disputed sharing the spoils of war with those who stayed back guarding their supplies. David puts an end to this contention by declaring that the spoils were to be equally divided among all men. What prompted David’s decision was a thankful heart, not anchored on pride, greed, or aggression. It was those men who valiantly fought with him against the Amalekites that won him an overrunning victory (he was able to reclaim his wives, children, valuables, and even the wealth that came from the enemy camp who raided his people), but David remembered that it was the hands of God who gained him and these men the power to overtake the enemies. When one’s heart is filled with gratitude, to him nothing else would seem substantial than the reciprocation of this love for God by being merciful to those around. A true and responsible leader would always ensure the welfare of his/her subordinates. In one way or another, You and I have been appointed leaders in our specific niches, to lead, teach, proclaim, and deliver from evil and iniquities. Have we been equally compassionate to those who contributed their parts to a cause (goal/objective) but in a silent, discreet, and low-profile style, or have we been overtly careful in appeasing those who were loud, demanding, the extroverts, and rebels among us?  A difficult question, but the Spirit of the Lord asks us to ponder deep. Everyone has his/her unique role to play in our lives - the more we broaden ourselves towards inclusiveness, the more we serve and honor the Lord. Amen

25/01/26, 08:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Therefore, let us leave the elementary doctrine of Christ and go on to maturity……..’ [Hebrews 6:1]

The Holy Spirit asks us to elevate ourselves from the stage of being an Elementary Christian and grow higher. And how are we envisaged to do this? By the constant practice of seeking discernment with the Spirit of God of what is good and what is evil. This consistency is attained when one is firm on the foundations set by Jesus in the Gospel through repentance from all dead works, and emboldening our faith. For this, we must begin each day by asking the Lord (as the apostles asked him) to increase our faith. Let this Sunday morning be a reminder to us of the love of the Lord for us, of the royalty He bequeathed to us through His passion, death, resurrection, and ascension. Rooted in this love for the Lord we earn the ability to move ahead from merely practicing the elementary rites of faith – the rituals, ceremonies, and rites. Of course they are significant, but when one grows in Christ, to love and reach out beyond these becomes our priority in life. Let our minds be renewed in Christ. Wishing you a blessed Sunday. Amen

26/01/26, 12:17 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I remind you to fan into flame the gift of God, which is in you through the laying on of my hands’ [2 Timothy 1:6]

One thing that is common to all children of God is the gift of God’s Spirit in us. By virtue of our belief in Jesus (the Son of God), we are made children of God, and co-heirs with Jesus Christ. It has already been instilled in us, this gift of God’s power in us. And we are called to live in this power by magnifying it, fanning into flame the fire of God’s power through us. Hardly do we realize God’s power in us, that we are extraordinary, not merely mortals; our lives have been ordained to accomplish a specific purpose, and that we live not by sight but by faith. Therefore, Paul as he exhorted his beloved disciple Timothy, tells us to exercise the deposit of faith in us with accelerated power and conviction. None can claim to have already attained this momentum, but as King Solomon prayed, we must seek in prayer, accepting that we are weak, short-lived, with little understanding and judgment. The commemoration of the feast day of Saints Timothy and Titus rings out this message that God’s Spirit has been given us - not for stagnation and redundancy in faith, but towards generosity, love, self-discipline, and fearlessness. You and I are to hone, shape, and sharpen this Spirit-led conscience of ours to give glory to God and honour others created in the likeness of God. Amen

27/01/26, 10:24 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘And David knew that the Lord had established him king over Israel, and that he had exalted his kingdom for the sake of his people Israel’ [2 Samuel 5:12]

It is this realization that David had, which made him stronger and more eager to pursue God’s will. David’s coronation as King over Israel was not something that happened soon after his anointing. Even after the death of King Saul and his sons at the Battle of Gilboa, warring continued between the Houses of Saul and of David for many years (Abner, the commander in chief of Saul’s army, continued the fight after reinstating Esh-bosheth, the surviving son of Saul as King over Israel). David was merely crowned the King of Judah by the elders at that time, and so most of his initial years of reigning over Hebron were marred by conflict, opposition, and deceit from the Israelite territory. However, none of these deterred his spirit of righteousness before God, before the men he served, and even before men of significance in the House of Saul (David’s respect for Abner, a good example). This was probably what pushed through (fast-tracked) David’s coronation. For us, this serves well set a precedent - when at times, we struggle for years fighting challenges, difficulties, and adversities one after another – to be ourselves steadfast and headstrong in integrity and righteousness, with the hope that one day the Lord will establish us to where we belong to. Amen

28/01/26, 10:23 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will solve my riddle to the music of the lyre’ [Psalm 49:4]

The truth is always like a proverb, ‘a riddle’ that is to be solved by effort. What the intellect may persuade us to accept may not always be the absolute truth. A blend of wisdom, intuition, a meditation on thoughts and experiences of life, and an open heart (the ear that inclines to listen) - all these have their proportioned contributions to help decipher the riddle of life. I would then calm my spirit, not to fret and fear because of the things that surround me – the wickedness and iniquity of men, the pride of wealth, pomp and splendour devoid of understanding. None of these has permanence; none of these would suffice as a ransom to God for the gift of life. Both low and high, rich and poor, wise and foolish, all have the same fate – tombs will remain their houses forever, though many of them have named lands after themselves. Not to say that building a future or a name for oneself is guilt and sin. How could God who created man to rule over the world with justice, cause him to perish like any other creation of his? But in his fallen understanding, man gave glory to what he was blessed with more than to the One who blessed him. Therefore, his ambition became unruly, unhealthy, sufficing only to dominate, than to support the weak and the least. On this point, the Spirit of God calls us towards repentance – that deprived of love, compassion, mercy, and gentleness, any man in his pomp will perish like any other beast of the earth. With the psalmist, let us also praise and thank God for his revelation (to us) of this riddle of life. Amen

29/01/26, 10:28 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching a light, and the reproofs of discipline are the way of life’ [Proverbs 6:23]

God spoke to His people (The Israelites) through the prophets, the judges, seers, and even through their life experiences. He gave them the Decalogue (The Ten Commandments) that were to be observed conscientiously by them. In His Eternal plan for Salvation, at every stage, He visited His people through the prophets and judges who advised them, taught them the way of life, and reprimanded them when they strayed away from the path of life. God continues His Salvation master plan throughout history and even to our days through the same modus operandi in Jesus Christ. By familiarising us with His Commandments, teaching us His Word, and offering us every opportunity for a comeback through mercy in Jesus’ name, we are led by the Lord in His path to reach our destiny. But what if we fail to learn from what He teaches us, advises us, or mercifully reprimands us? The mercy of God would subdue us into wanton sufferings, to teach, warn, and correct us to take on the path of Light. We must remember that through His suffering and resurrection, Jesus has enabled us to approach the Throne of Grace to seek mercy and strength whenever we are weak or sinful, but with a contrite heart. A Universe of Grace the Lord has created and submerged us into, that through those around us, He teaches, advises, reprimands, and corrects us. Are we willing to humbly yield to the promptings of the Lord is where we are asked to examine ourselves today. All the commandments (rules), the counsel/suggestions of those who wield authority, and even their admonitions are Divinely inspired and given us, let us prudently discern how to make constructive sense of these to attain our goals in life. Amen

30/01/26, 09:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘The Kingdom of God is as if a man should scatter seed on the ground. He sleeps and rises night and day, and the seed sprouts and grows; he knows not how’ [Mark 4:26-27]

Jesus was speaking to his disciples, inviting them to partake in the Kingdom of God. And for this, (to be members of the household of God) the Word must first be sown. As are seeds scattered by the farmer in the field, so must God’s Word and His will be propagated, adhered to, obeyed, and accepted by our hearts. Both the proclaimer of the Word and the listener are alike, unaware of what may occur as the Word sinks deep within them. It is thus said that the sower then takes his leave, sleeps and awaits night and day, till the grain is ripe, fully grown and ready for the harvest. It is God who works behind the scenes to ensure the seed sprouts and grows into a plentiful harvest. Though in the outset of things, the farmer who has sown the seeds is unable to see anything happen, things have been set into ‘moulding’ to fit into God’s plan for our greater goodness. The farmer doesn’t need to check beneath the ground, to ensure himself if anything is really happening. Because he knows that what he could do has been done, and beyond this, there can be nothing else done from his side. So must we believe, that when God’s Word is spoken, read, meditated, recited, and proclaimed, what we ought to do is done. What would transpire after is something that God promises to do. Let us therefore, humbly receive God’s Word, Sure, there is a great harvest that is coming for us. When God gives he gives abundantly and therefore, You and I could reap possible benefits of the work of many others too. Believe and receive in Jesus name. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

31/01/26, 05:56 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires

The world and its desires pass away, but whoever does the will of God lives forever. (1 John 2: 17)

Every human being on earth has a purpose in life, a divine calling, a great destiny set by God. And we can choose between following God’s plan and accepting our call or leading a pompous but vain life fueled by the world and its desires. But due to our innate concupiscent nature, we are by default pulled towards the world, and we tend to seek pleasure over purpose. 

Today, we are reminded and encouraged to pursue something eternal, something beyond our comprehension, something true and meaningful, rather than wasting our time and energy on following the futile ‘trends’ of the world. The road that leads to salvation is indeed rocky and narrow (Matthew 7:14), but it is rewarding and eternal. God is true and eternal, so are His words and plans. 

Let us all lead lives that pleases His holy sight and fulfil the purpose He has for us in our lives. May God bless us with the grace to break free from our inclination towards the temporal world and to choose the eternal over the temporal.

Wishing you a wonderful weekend!
God bless you.

31/01/26, 08:50 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘he was unwilling to take one of his own flock or herd to prepare for the guest who had come to him, but he took the poor man’s lamb and prepared it for the man who had come to him’ [2 Samuel 12:4]

God sent Prophet Nathan to convince King David about the gravity of his sin and prompt/urge him towards repentance. The parable shook David as a bolt from the blue, not that he was awakened to realize his mistake, but what infuriated him was the attitude of the rich man. To take on the only lamb that was of the poor man and make a feast of it for his guests was outrageous and highly to be condemned. He gives orders for this rich man to be executed for his crime, and also that a four-fold restitution be made by him for the injury caused to the other. Nathan then pointed out to David that it was he who was the rich man who had confiscated the lamb (the solemn and most valued possession) of the poor for the sake of satisfying his momentary desires. David was brought to repentance, to realize the intensity of the series of sins he had committed (the rest of the passage describes of how he attempted to make reparation for his sins in the public). Nathan’s parable has outreaching significance in our times too. We who are part of the Digital era, who claim to be swifter, more efficient, and successful than our previous generations, still fail to succeed in being human. Has respect for human dignity and sincere love suffered a setback in our lives today? we would need to ponder on. As Pope Leo the XIV stated, as he addressed the gathering of the faithful – Have we as parents (successful) curbed the dreams of our children who are yet budding? Have we as leaders subverted the growth prospects of our subordinates for our petty interests? Have we as Christians failed to understand the needs of those less privileged before us? The Spirit of the Lord calls us to repentance. Amen

01/02/26, 08:29 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

I Daniel, understood from the Scriptures, according to the Word of the Lord given to Jeremiah the prophet, that the desolation of Jerusalem would last seventy years. So, I turned to the Lord God and pleaded with him in prayer and petition, in fasting, and in sackcloth and ashes’ [Daniel 9:2-3]

It was the Spirit of the Lord who revealed to Daniel, through the Scriptures that the desolation would last for seventy years. The moment he realized that God had permitted this desolation over His people for the set number of years as a reparation for their sins and transgressions, Daniel sprang up in action. He started to repent, fast, and pray for God’s mercy and so that his sins and transgressions, along with those of entire Israel’s would be atoned for before the Lord. Daniel was chosen by God, set apart for the task of restoring the people after their exile, to strengthen them in God’s Word and ways. Not towards despair did his heart turn, when he understood that the destruction that had come by was for a cause, but towards reconciling his heart towards God. You and I have been chosen in our walks of life, by way of Baptism to be the ambassadors of Christ Jesus on earth. Whenever we encounter hindrances and struggles in life’s journey, let us not fall into despair, let us not look for human support, but plead before the Lord for mercy, and reparation for our sins. Who else can console and strengthen us than the Lord and His Spirit. Let this Sunday be an eye opener for each one of us to plead before Jesus for mercy for reparation of sins (words, deeds) and omissions to do the good we were called for. Amen

02/02/26, 09:57 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Behold this child is appointed for the fall and rising of many in Israel, and for a sign that is opposed (and a sword will pierce through your own soul also), so that thoughts from many hearts may be revealed’ [Luke 2:34-35]

The truth (always) will be hard, daring, incomprehensible at first, unacceptable to many, but nothing triumphs like absolute truth. Jesus took the shape of/ similarity with us (flesh and blood) to convince us of the truth. The truth is that what we fear (worried or anxious about) is instilled by the power of death, the devil. Therefore, by taking on death, Jesus proved to us its destruction and that we are now liberated from its stronghold. To believe is a choice given to you and me because the Lord loves our individuality. And when we believe - those things that were till then disturbing, disgruntling, and suffocating to us turn idle tales, they become means to our Salvation, our reconciliation to God and His people. Many may hate, scorn, ridicule, and badmouth the truth. This was what Simeon prophesied about Jesus, ‘A sign that is opposed’ and would divulge the thoughts of many around. It doesn’t just end there, but will pierce the soul that is so intimate to the Lord, His mother. This prophecy of Simeon was not intended for the parents of Jesus (who offered Him to God at the temple for fulfilling the Law of Moses), but for us to reflect upon. It is God who causes one to fall and rise. Who are we to judge when one falls? His trust in God is enough to lift him, elevate him from the ground, and establish him firm. Let us be concerned not about him (who has fallen), but about each one of us who are susceptible to fall at any time. Remember, it is not their hearts alone that we wound by our condemnation, but the heart of Jesus and His mother who willed the Salvation of all men. Amen

‘Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light’ [Matthew 11:29,30]

If Christ is your King and Lord, You and I are expected to live like Him. ‘My yoke’ here refers to a lifestyle that is in alignment with Jesus and His teachings. We, who have known Jesus Christ and experienced His saving grace are those set apart, those called to show the way, to dare to be different from the ways of this world. Unless and until we humble ourselves to imitate Jesus Christ and unlearn our worldly ways, we will never find rest. And anyone would agree to the fact that all those who decide to live a Godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted (2 Tim 3:12). Persecution, opposition, criticisms… all these are sure things for a believer in Christ, but for a while. Therefore, many are hesitant to take risks, tend to live the middle way, appeasing both sides (the world and the Lord), claiming to be peaceful and reasonable people. Here, Jesus offers to slow down, to reach out to us to teach us, so that we can walk with Him, hand in hand, gaining His support in times of need. His way for us will always be the balanced way, the best way, and the easiest to reach our destiny. For once we start trusting Him, nothing would seem a burden in life. In trust and in quietness, we will find our rest, no human intervention can compare. Help me Lord Jesus, to take up your yoke and teach me your ways that I may not deviate from the path of Light. 04/02/26, 10:21 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘If anyone serves me, the Father will honour him’ [John 12:26]

Jesus never said that all those who were faithful to ‘Him’ would be rewarded by the world. But He stated unanimously that the one who served Him would be honoured by His Father. This inevitably means that there is a loss, a dishonour, a disgrace, or even humiliation, one may find himself in when he begins to realign himself according to the Gospel. The hearts of many may be revealed - the opinion of our dear and near, many who hardly recognise the purpose of one’s life, uncertainties of tomorrow, a lot of comparisons to lives around, or forgone fortunes……….But for this one who is convinced of what he does, the Spirit of the Lord, the source of courage, power and love, would embolden and glorify him. He doesn’t (in fact) do anything on his own (intellect, thought process, or rationale), but yields himself submissively for God’s Spirit to make use of him. Thus, the one who is docile to the promptings of the Spirit of Jesus is the one who serves Jesus. Contrary to what we may think or reason out, in the eyes of the world, nothing much can be measured that would qualify for performance, productivity, or a narrative about this one. But his presence would outwit any other because of the grace working (transforming, transcending, and intervening through) every aspect of his life. Joseph of the Old Testament was one like this, Moses, the judges, and the prophets were likewise, so was Joseph (the foster father of Jesus). You and I are next in line to continue this legacy, only if we choose to be faithful. Lord Jesus, teach us the virtue of fortitude to be daring and faithful to you always. Amen

‘But a Jew is one inwardly, and circumcision is a matter of the heart, by the Spirit, not by the letter. His praise is not from man but from God’ [Romans 2:29]

God’s Word speaks to us thus …... ‘But a Believer in Christ is one inwardly, and circumcision is a matter of the heart, by the Spirit, not by the letter.’ Every disciple of Jesus Christ is to look into himself, not to what he does, but into what lies within his heart. For out of the heart of a man comes all evil – murder, adultery, sexual immorality, theft, false testimony, slander…. Jesus reminded his disciples. So, what occurs outwardly is immaterial, the customs, the so-called traditional rituals, our diligence to religious practices, our social commemorations, because without love, gentleness, diligence to one’s personal obligations/responsibilities, all the others would seem meaningless. Nevertheless, so-called foundational and critical concepts of individuality and inalienability of sacred institutions - ‘the family’ have been forgotten or rather back-burned. Here is where God’s Spirit leads us to reflection and a comeback. The home is (of course) where the heart lives. How faithful have we been in standing up for our families for reclaiming virtues of love, forgiveness, mercy, and compassion? Have we been truly diligent in reinstating holiness within? Have we been fervent in prayer for those given to us than for things we possess? We must always remember that our commendation will finally come not from man but from God who reads our hearts. Amen

06/02/26, 10:29 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘And he said, ‘Open the window eastward,’ and he opened it. Then Elisha said, ‘Shoot’, and he shot. And he said, ‘The Lord’s arrow of victory, the arrow of victory over Syria!’ [2 Kings 13:17]

Jehoash the then King of Israel was in deep distress, as he had to struggle hard to secure his boundaries from the threats of the Syrians. Due to sinfulness the entire territory and military forces of his kingdom were utterly devastated by the enemy. Not more than fifty horsemen, ten chariots, and ten thousand footmen were left with the King. Understanding that Elisha, the prophet was nearing his death, the King was all the more disturbed. Hardly did he have the strength, nor the courage to work out a strategy to defeat the Syrians. In all dismay and discouragement, this King approaches Elisha, seeking his prayer and the will of God. Elisha tells him to take his bow and arrows, which he obeys; he then asks him to draw the bow. Elisha then blesses the hands of the King. What he is told to do next is what we are called to reflect upon. To open the window eastward and shoot. If you and I were told to just go and face the army when in dire straits and all lost, we would have first hesitated and even opined that it was imprudent to do so. King Jehoash was asked to stand firm against his enemy, the Syrian King who reigned towards the east and shoot an arrow at that direction. He did exactly what he was told to do, not trying to reason out the possible consequence of his act. Thus followed the declaration from above (God) spoken through Elisha – The Lord’s arrow of victory, the arrow of victory over Syria! The story continues explaining that during his lifetime (King Jehoash), the kingdom of Israel was safe and could outwit every attack that came from Syria. For you and me to ponder - Does faith encourage us to take the harder step forward or do we retreat when things ahead seem dampening and depressing to us? Believe in Jesus and take that step forward today. None will be able to defeat you in this lifetime of yours. Amen

07/02/26, 09:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to gratify its desires’ [Romans 13:14]

For you have been reclaimed by Jesus Christ, through His blood, and your Spirit has become one with Him. Therefore, you and I have no room to leave for the flesh and its gratification. Not to annihilate our bodies, which are the dwelling place of God, but to elevate our bodies to a realm higher which cannot be subdued by the recklessness of this world. Jesus warned His disciples about this when He foretold them of his death and resurrection and of the end of times. He counselled them to be on guard that their hearts may always throb for Him and the precepts they were taught. Three things He specifically pointed out to them in this regard were: (1) to be careful not to seek and yield to pleasure, (2) to beware drunkenness or inebriation of any kind (alcohol, social media), (3) not to be swayed and wasted by the anxieties of daily living. So much relevant today! The foresight of the Lord, of how a believer’s heart may be weakened (unknowingly and over a period of time) to drift away from His presence and love. Great prophets and Godly people lost their purpose in life and became no better than the dust of the earth, (merely because of this) though they were once instruments of God’s great revelations to His people. The story of Samson (God’s chosen and Israel’s mighty judge) is one of sorts and an eye-opener to us, of a God man who failed to realize that his sinfulness and continued non-repentance had finally drained him of God’s Spirit and its works in him. We, the children of God, therefore have just two options laid before us – either to put on Jesus Christ and conform to Him, or to put on the world and live by its precepts. May God’s Spirit in us enlighten us to adorn the former, always. Amen

08/02/26, 07:05 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘And I will give them one heart, and a new Spirit I will put within them. I will remove the heart of stone from their flesh and give them a heart of flesh’ [Ezekiel 11:19]

Peace, oneness, and contentment are gifts of the Lord today for you and me. Not that we were worthy by our works to receive them from above, but because the Lord is merciful and gracious to us. Our eyes and our hearts reveal our state of being, the heart being the storehouse, while the eyes are the ‘outlets’ to the world. As are their roles, so do they function for the well-being of our body – eyes open up and reveal, while the heart secures, defends, and restricts its outpouring to the world. When God promises one heart and a new Spirit (His Spirit) in us, He pours into us His likeness by which we are taught to protect and guard not just our integrity, but also the needs, deprivations, and dignity of those given to our care – our family, our communities, our societal gatherings, those who work with us and those we connect to. The heart of the worldly man would merely defend himself, seclude from being attacked or challenged, and therefore is self-centric by default. It is God who restores relationships by installing a heart that includes, improves, and builds up one another, because Jesus is our peace. And in Him, we have been called not merely to survive our daily storms, but also to be rejuvenated by His Spirit to a higher realm of Christian life. Our hearts are essentially like stones that have passed the tests and trials of time and again, but we need to be like Jesus, who heals, restores, and sacrifices for the greater glory of the Father.  Lord Jesus, give us your heart of love, teach us to forgive, and help us to be peaceable in a world that cares for none. Amen

09/02/26, 10:41 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Therefore, let no one pass judgment on you in questions of food and drink, or with regard to a festival or a new moon or a Sabbath’ [Colossians 2:16]

There must have been something spectacularly noticeable (in conduct, word, or ways) that had provoked the crowds to pick on and respond to the Christians in Colossae. They were undoubtedly strong believers, rooted in Gospel teachings, and firm in love. But, they were also influenced by prophets and preachers who forced them towards asceticism and the observance of customary traditions/ritualistic practices of a devotional nature. St Paul, while in prison, wrote to these believers to encourage and strengthen them in their faith and love for Jesus Christ, which was all that was needed to sail past the storms of erroneous teachings. To the one who is in Christ, no other rules or precepts mattered, however alluring, or highly devotional in context and content. Mere obedience to the Gospel rule of love is what he is called to adhere to. And this obedience to the Law of love would need to be nurtured daily, beginning with loving God, oneself, and those given to our care. When this love for God and man begins to decline, all other observances (be it) - our gatherings, congregations, festivals, and entertainments would turn provocative and only for people to stumble and fall. Temptations, false teachings, and all kinds of vulnerabilities are sure to come – this was what Jesus told his disciples, while exhorting them to be cautious about themselves. God wills that the seed of faith in us must grow day by day, not be stalked by things and people around. Lord Jesus, Increase our faith, we pray. Amen

10/02/26, 09:45 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘But will God indeed dwell on the earth? Behold, heaven and highest heaven cannot contain you; how much less this house that I have built?’ [1 Kings 8:27]

King Solomon built and dedicated the grand temple of Jerusalem as a place of worship and as a place set apart for man’s reconciliation with God. Engulfed in the glory that filled the temple (the clouds overshadowing the temple), Solomon’s heart was opened to recognize that God’s greatness could never be contained in the structure he built or in its grandeur, but was to be made manifest in the daily lives of the people. By walking before the Lord in uprightness and honouring His Covenantal faithfulness only, would the people begin to experience the favourable intervention of God in their lives. A city built in grandiosity and earmarked as the Dwelling house of God would not assure peace, security, and prosperity for the people. To the contrary, when evil and wickedness rule the roost in the hearts of people (inhabiting such a blessed territory), their sin maligns or corrupts the sanctity of the place, thus degrading and disqualifying any eligibility to assume grace through worship. Jesus once stated that true worshippers would worship God in truth and Spirit – in the truth that was revealed in Jesus, and by being led by the ingenuity of God’s Spirit who constructs and creates. You and I have been consecrated by virtue of our Baptism as dwelling places for the Lord. Would my heart befit the holiness of God, that would make me eligible to receive grace, or do I merely worship by lip service? The Holy Spirit asks us to realign ourselves, reorient our priorities, and then approach the Lord with a pure heart. God desires that we become living sacrifices of His presence to the world, that He who has humbled Himself to be with us (His creation) would delight in our good deeds and be glorified through us. Amen

11/02/26, 10:25 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘For from within, out of the heart of man, come evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder, adultery, coveting, wickedness, deceit, sensuality, envy, slander, pride, foolishness. All these evil things come from within, and they defile a person’ [Mark 7:21-23]

Most of our actions and reactions are default responses that emerge from ‘attitudes’ that are formed in us. Howsoever religious, disciplined, and responsible we may think we are, we still have those attitudes that are specific to us – those that have developed deep within over time from the experiences and circumstances we have encountered in life. Some of them may be really welcoming – warmth, inclusiveness, broad-mindedness, endurance, generosity, forgiveness, patience… etc. These have been pruned in us by the Spirit of God through our life situations. But we must admit that we also hold (in us) certain negativities too, those that our flesh has captured, and assimilated as the truth, through the influences from the world around us. You and I have been empowered to neutralize these influences that the world tries to draw you to by befriending Jesus and asking Him for support. This is why Jesus explained to His disciples that nothing from outside a person can defile him, unless permitted by the conscience within him. Our consciences need to be washed, purified, and transformed each day to simulate that of the Lord’s. Looking into the kind of common negativities that men harbour within – evil thoughts, deceit, envy, pride, sexual immorality, iniquity, slander, foolishness, and the like…. These are never those that dictated the conscience of the Lord Jesus Christ. Thus, He humbled Himself (not considering His Godly stature as something that would be impacted when He took the form of a human being), into a lower being, similar to you and me, merely to win our hearts for God. He reminds us that we came from God, that where our source is, there we belong, and that He called us to live like Him, with a heart that burnt for Him, and to do His will is our mission. Then will we find our rest. May you be inspired for a change. Amen

12/02/26, 09:19 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘Remember me, O Lord, when you show favor to your people; help me when you save them, that I may look upon the prosperity of your chosen ones…that I may glory with your inheritance’ [Psalm 106:4,5]

The prayers and efforts of this passionate leader, ardent yet fervently dedicated to the cause of God’s people (their well-being) are echoed through this Psalm. You and I, have been anointed by the Lord to lead those given to our care, in our families, communities, parishes, institutions, and groups. What is, and what God desires of us, is the attitude of a ‘father’ who protects and assures his children. He ensures they are firm in their tasks, nothing deters their progress – be it the situations and circumstances they encounter, the people they meet with, that fear does not suppress their purpose and conviction in life, also that a sure strong foundation is set up for them to build their lives. The question we need to ask ourselves is just this – Do any of my words, actions, or beliefs counter or disrupt the pace of progress of those given to my care? Do my interests (individual, social, or any other) pose obstacles to their zeal in carrying on with their goals in life? Do I unduly judge my people (given to my care) without understanding them or stepping into their shoes (their lifestyle, struggles, pain, and challenges)? Do I sincerely pray that they may see the success they wish for themselves? And when I ensure my ways confer with their success, it is then that my works become favorable to the Lord. I can with pride glory in the inheritance that the Lord has specially granted to me. Grant me this bent of mind to be remembered by you. Amen

13/02/26, 09:59 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Therefore, the Lord said to Solomon, ‘Since this has been your practice and you have not kept my Covenant and my statutes that I have commanded you, I will surely tear the kingdom from you and will give it to your servant’ [1 Kings 29;11]

Despite being warned by God not once (but twice) that He was to keep the commandments and precepts of the Lord intact and observe them flawlessly, Solomon continued in his ways, relying on the inclinations of his thoughts and experience. Contrary to the conviction of his father (King David always persisted in God’s ways and honored God’s will above any other), Solomon was swayed by the wisdom he possessed, the victories he gained for himself, the secure kingdom he reigned for many years, and the glory that was given to him. When God warned him not to take wives who belonged to foreign customs, religious traditions, and ideologies, revealing to him that the consequences would be far-fetched and irreversible, Solomon took lightly of these warnings. He had before him the precedent of King Saul, Israel’s first king, who bore the brunt of his disobedience (to God) through a tragic and humiliating death. His father, King David was of sober mind to the last, and even when at his deathbed, he reminded his son to be faithful to God always (giving him instructions). Nevertheless, none of these seemed to have cautioned Solomon from disobeying God and having his way. Slowly, without him knowing, Solomon began to have adversaries who sprang up against him and instigated revolts in many parts of his till then secure kingdom. The protection of the Most High that enveloped him till then slowly started to fade away. The last years of Solomon’s reign were filled with chaos and turmoil, as he had to fight one adversary after another to arrest rebellion. To us, the lives of these kings serve as precedents for diligence in Christian living to adhere to God’s Word to enjoy peace and security. When we dilute these precepts of the Lord to satiate our whims and fancies, we deprive ourselves of the privilege of God’s ownership and His protection over us. Obedience brings blessings that are inherited by generations, while disobedience invites adversity and struggles through generations. Lord, teach me to obey your will. Amen
