14/02/26, 09:20 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Then, this thing became a sin, for the people went as far as Dan to be before one. He made temples on high places and appointed priests from among all the people, who were not of the Levites’ [1 Kings 12:30-31]

Jeroboam became King of all Israel (except for Judah and Benjamin) after Solomon’s death. And this had come to pass to fulfill what was spoken by God through His prophet Ahijah. While Solomon’s intelligence and glory caused him (his descendant, Rehoboam) to disobey God and dilute His commands, Jeroboam (who took over the ten tribes of ‘Israel’ called the Kingdom of Israel henceforth) was no better. It never crossed his mind that God was the author of a man’s destiny. Instead, Jeroboam further ventured to secure his authority through strategy and tact. His first move was to set up the Idols of Golden calves at two ends of his kingdom – at Bethel and as far as Dan. What he intended through this step was to prevent his subjects from travelling all the way to Jerusalem (the House of God) for worship, which he thought would decrease their loyalty towards him and bring them closer to the King of Judah. His second move was even more daunting, as he took upon him the authority of appointing ‘priests’ (whomever he liked) to offer sacrifices at the temples he built for his people. The Levites (who were chosen by God) to minister to God were disregarded, and so they had to travel and settle in Jerusalem due to the king’s attitude and ways. Jeroboam the capable man, chosen by God to rule over the ten tribes, thus ensnared himself, by doing evil in the eyes of the Lord. He not only lowered the glory of God to that of Idols and instigated people to Idolatry, but also caused insult and degradation to God’s chosen ministers by the manipulation of his God given authority. What caused him and his dynasty doom was this overt inclination to his tactics and human ways of thinking. An eye opener to us, who live in the abundance and swiftness of the digital era. No matter what the world may prove to us, God’s ways and His will are supreme and would ultimately reign over all created. You and I are called to humbly accept that we are instruments in the hands of God and not executors of our will. Amen

15/02/26, 06:59 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘…. He himself partook of the same things, that through death He might destroy the one who has the power of death, that is the devil, and deliver all those who through fear of death were subject to lifelong slavery’ [Hebrews 2:14-15]

He (Jesus) partook of the same things, which means those specific traits that constitute or define a human being. He incarnated in the flesh, became like you and me, being subject to the physical, emotional, and psychological forces that influence us. And this was merely to give us the confidence that by His death, all that curbed life (for us) was made subject to and destroyed by Him. Now, it would be imperative for us to look into ourselves, on what really curbs or hinders life in abundance for us – fear (of any kind), sorrows, situations and circumstances that are way beyond our control, oppression from people around, helplessness because of rejection and insult, even the kind of habitual sins that may have subdued our consciences that we find ourselves enslaved by them (addiction to social media or gadgets, whatever) – In all these we have Jesus who triumphed for us, who took all struggles (uncountable) to Himself, bore our pain, fears, sorrows, the shame and contempt, that demobilizes us, upon His Cross, just to see us delivered, redeemed, and triumphant. He humbled Himself just to see us through the burdens we have been carrying all the long. This victory, St Paul (in his epistle to the Romans) described so effectively thus, that We have become conquerors in all these because of Him who loved us. Remember, You and I are not forsaken, not rejected, or desolate, for we have Jesus, our Saviour, who lives, moves, and dwells with us. Amen

18/02/26, 03:55 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today 
' As the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell upon Abram, and a deep and terrifying darkness descended upon him' [Genesis 15:12]

Abram had asked for reassurance from God. Before his eyes there was nothing that could prove fruition of God's promises in his life. God had spoken to him of a great reward upcoming. But the poor old man didn't have a descendant either and this was quite depressing to him. And here to confirm the surety of his reward, God initiates a Covenant of faithfulness to Abram who believed His word. The day was almost over and sun was setting - a hint on Abram's life, his human possibilities ahead. God puts Abram to sleep, which mean't God asked him to do nothing but wait in hopeful trust. It was not that easy, a step into the dark - with no idea of what next, no answers to any logical questions any would ask. And in this situation God makes His Covenant. Sometimes, we too are permitted to walk through, wade through the uncertain, with absolutely no picture of a feasible next. The Lord merely asks us to be serene and trust in Him. He is in the meantime working for us and when He does, it can never fail or be futile. He not only initiates, but also strengthens us in every upcoming phase of life to hold on to Him, knowing that we are feeble. Irrespective of how terrible the darkness around seemed to Abram, he was renamed Abraham, the Father of all believers, so shall we who fervently trust in His love. Our God is merciful, slow to anger, and ever waiting for our comeback. Repent and reconcile with Him. Amen

19/02/26, 10:43 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for Today

‘I have set before you life and death, blessing and curse. Therefore, choose life, that you and your offspring may live, loving the Lord your God, obeying His voice and holding fast to Him, for He is your life and length of days, that you may dwell in the land that the Lord swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Issac, and to Jacob, to give him.’[Deuteronomy 30:19,20]

Today, our Lord Jesus invites us not merely to choose life that has repercussions for generations, but also explains to us what ‘life’ signifies and what we must do to earn it. The choice of life is the junction or prerequisite for obtaining God’s promises. All God’s promises are yes in Jesus Christ. Therefore, ‘the land promised to our fathers’ would imply abundance and prosperity in the material parlance; and if we were to (spiritually) connotate for it, it would mean ‘Eternal life’. Now let us closely examine what it takes to choose life – (1) to love the Lord; (2) to obey His voice (His Word); and (3) to cleave on to Him (no matter how difficult and humiliating our situations can be).  He is our life, and the length of our days would in turn refer to the foundation of our faith in Jesus, who is our way, our truth, and our life. There can be no life, no sustenance, no hope, no joy, or any consolation beyond Him and without Him. Whoever you are, whatever you are called to do, your blessing and life is dependent upon Jesus. Whomever the Lord recognizes as His Beloved, He forgives their sins and transgressions, redeems them to embrace Salvation, and invites them to a life of contentment by partaking in His Kingdom. So now, the choice of repentance, turning to the Lord, and cleaving to Him in trust is up to you. If you choose Him, your choice is above board, and He who never forsakes or abandons His beloved ones, will never leave you a chance of looking back or getting distracted by the things and ways of this world. Amen

20/02/26, 10:02 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘…...declare to my people their transgression, to the house of Jacob their sins. Yet they seek me daily and delight to know my ways, as if they were a nation that did righteousness and did not forsake the judgment of their God’ [Isaiah 58:1,2]

The prophet has been asked to declare to God’s people – to us it is a declaration to ourselves and to those God has given to our care. The declaration Isaiah made was to the people of Judah, who witnessed to the other nations, that they were God’s people, the true worshippers of God (unlike those of the Northern Kingdom who were Idolaters for decades) and doers of His will. That is what the prophet mentioned in verse 2 of the chapter - they seek God daily and delight in knowing His ways. This was rather painful to the Lord, who saw their hearts. This is explained satirically by the prophet who states – ‘As if’ they were a nation that did righteousness…. So does the Lord look into our hearts and ask us to desire for sincerity, for faithfulness, and truthfulness. Not that Jesus doesn’t understand our fragile framework as human beings placed in a corrupt world. But He explained thus that ‘Unless’ your righteousness surpasses that of the Pharisees and the Scribes, you will not inherit His Kingdom. The onus is therefore on us to seek grace and strength to be meaningfully righteous and to remember what the judgment of the Lord would mean if we continue in our ways. However our Lord is compassionate, so merciful and gracious, that the moment he sees us striving to attain this standard of uprightness in our daily struggles, He shadows our ways. His accompaniment and protection are all that You and I need to fulfil our obligations to concur with His will. So whatever you do and say, do it in a way that would accord praise and honor to God who watches over you. By ensuring we observe verses 4-8 of Chapter 58, Jesus shows us His path, the path that assures us great promises for generations. May you be inspired to read and meditate what the Spirit of the Lord wishes of you this day – Read Isaiah 58:1-14 and prepare to receive what God delights to fill you with. Amen

21/02/26, 08:54 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘I have not come to call the righteous but sinners to repentance’ [Luke 5:32]

Levi’s call to ‘discipleship’ was an event Jesus used to clarify His stand/motive to all who wished to be his disciples. Jesus explained that discipleship was not a default setting, meaning it would not befit one unless he accepted his need for the Lord. Only the man who had a heart that was yielding or willing to submit to His Lordship could become a disciple. Discipleship has a cost, and only the one who was ready to pay for it could attain it. The foremost cost of discipleship of Jesus is the denial of the ‘self’. How easily said, but the most difficult to strive for, because with all the training and experience of this world, all our efforts for years have been towards improvement and improvising the person we are. Our looks, our attire, the way we portray ourselves, our behavioural traits, our genetics and heredity, all these aspects have been undergoing radical changes ever since we were born. And in a world where the law of ‘survival of the fittest’ still looms large, it becomes hard to open up one’s weaknesses, flaws, and shortcomings, for fear of being lost in the rat race. Moreover, some of us even tend to put others down or step on the other in our effort to prove smarter and successful. Pointing to these characteristic traits of ours, Jesus lovingly corrects us stating that He has come for the weak, the repentant, the sinner, the unsuccessful, and the one who is the have-not. Unless we go to Him and speak up for our needs (to be supported, for a change, for our sinful ways, our eagerness to prove ourselves, to own success at any cost……whatever) we would never find Him. Our destiny in life is not to compare, contrast and be successful, but to be the ‘real’ true version of ourselves. And this is what the Lord can help us with, because what He needs for His kingdom is not the false selves we portray or carry before the crowds, but our very nature. He called Simon to be ‘the rock’ Peter; He called James and John to be ‘the Sons of Thunder’, knowing well how their strengths and weaknesses could build His kingdom on earth. So does He look into us, see us, and dream about us. Hardly do we realize! Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

22/02/26, 08:17 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘For the Lord is righteous; he loves righteous deeds; the upright shall behold his face’ [Psalm 11:7]

All righteousness comes from God. He who has created the Universe ensured its order, its perfection, and usefulness that man (the glory of creation) would not just thrive but also maintain it, grow to excel, and imitate His creator. When the first man and woman were in absolute harmony with God, they were righteous, could see God, and walk with Him. But when sin entered man’s domain (word, deed, and motives), this natural character of being with God was lost to them. The curse of struggling with nature for survival, henceforth (that became man’s way of living) was directly attributed to this decline in their mindset/conviction to achieve without the friendship of God. Thus, in all that man began to do, there arose this sense of animosity in him – towards his neighbour, towards His Creator, and even towards the environment he lived in. God knew this state of human helplessness. In His righteousness, He decided to redeem mankind by sending His Son incarnated in the flesh. Jesus taught and showed us how to reconcile with our Father in Heaven, how to trust in His will, and how to live a life of perfect harmony even in a world that was imperfect as it is. He calls us to befriend Him, and to accept that through Him and with Him we would be successful in conquering all the imperfections of this world that force us towards iniquity and wickedness. He told us that ‘Unless’ we are born again, we would never be able to inherit the Heavenly Kingdom. He gave Himself up for us as a ransom for all our sins, and to help us more, He has given us a helper – the Holy Spirit to guide us in His way. This Sunday of Lent is a call to honor Jesus, to heed His call to repent of our crooked ways, to adorn the spirit of truth and righteousness, and be able to see Him. For HE has promised that those who are pure in Spirit will definitely be able to see Him. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

23/02/26, 09:34 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘…Moreover, by them is your servant warned; in keeping them there is great reward’ [Psalm 19:11]

To understand the depth of the Lord’s Word to us, we must begin reading and meditating from verse 7 onwards, where the Law of the Lord is mentioned. Moving on, it points to the testimony of the Lord, His precepts, the commandments, the fear of the Lord, and ‘the rules’ of the Lord. Each of these enlightens our minds to a specific characteristic of God’s Word to us. The Law of the Lord opens to us a holistic overview of God’s desire and will. What God intends that we must hold on to within, in every thought, action, word, and deed. The love for God, His glory, and love for our neighbour is what God wishes us to keep as motive as a believer. The reward is certain, as when obliges to God’s Law, He is revived, restored, and a new creation. Now, Testimony of God is all that He has promised He would do for His treasured possession – you and me, if we adhere to His ways. You and I become inheritors of all God’s promises – this is for sure. Our only way, the truth, and means of life is Jesus Christ, through Him we live as righteous children of God. Precepts are the guidelines, the do’s and don’ts, we are cautioned by His Church of what may lead us to do the right and what would bring real joy in life. The commandment of the Lord is what He has authoritatively insisted that we must observe flawlessly, and there can be no dilution to God’s command, by any humanly instituted legislation. (He is King of all Kings and Lord of all). And when you and I give foremost priority to His ways and Word, the fear of the Lord encompasses our souls. It is this that demarcates us eligible to receive mercy from above than to be judged based on our true worth. Let us thus desire for God’s Word each day, humble ourselves to observe these to the core, be aware of what these may warn us to be careful of, and be reverent to the Lord to bequeath the mercy He showers upon us each day. Amen

24/02/26, 10:09 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘…and those who hate the righteous will be condemned. The Lord redeems the life of his servants; none of those who take refuge in Him will be condemned’ [Psalm 34:21,22]

Every person’s life on earth will be marred by ‘afflictions’ one way or the other. The righteous person is no exception, as he would also have to come across disappointments, discouragements, dejections, and failures through his journey on earth. But the Bible makes a ‘distinction’ between affliction that falls on the righteous and the wicked /the wrongdoer. While many are the afflictions and cares of the righteous, the Lord will deliver him from all of them. He keeps all his bones, not one of them is broken – referring to the bones of Jesus’s legs on the Cross. The sufferings of the righteous are likened to Jesus’s suffering, who underwent such humiliation and pain on the Cross, but all this was meant for a glorious resurrection to come soon. This would not be the case for the afflictions borne by the wrongdoer. It is his alienation from the righteous, or erroneous conduct or omission to do good, that would backfire as suffering on his behalf. The ways of this world are to subdue another, to impose dominance, or to succeed by means of hegemony (power of muscle, of men, or wealth). Jesus warns us about this – that those of us who hate the righteous (the one empowered by grace), or the one whom the world has labelled as ‘weak’, would be condemned by Him, and would succumb to the afflictions that have been the creation of his own actions and motives. If ever we have been unjust, uncourteous, judgmental, or deliberately the cause of another’s suffering in the body or mind, we must repent, ask the Lord for forgiveness, and decide to make reparation for the same. Let us also be explicitly careful and ask for the grace of sanctification of our hearts, motives, and intent, that when on that final day, we might not be found wanting before our Lord, who would be our Judge and King. Amen

25/02/26, 08:59 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘He began to say, “This generation is an evil generation. It seeks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of Jonah’ [Luke 11:29]

Despite the signs, miracles, and deeds of mercy, Jesus did for the people, He understood their hollowness. Jesus looked deep into their hearts and knew them. They were a people who kept clamouring for signs, not to scale up their faith, but to expect to find something to pick on from the signs Jesus may give to them. In other words, it was their urge to criticize, to find fault, to find excuses for disbelief that prompted them to ask for a sign. Rather than giving glory to God who was worthy of it, they attempted to hold glory for themselves and give glory to those like them, in their futile attempts to please others. This displeased the Lord, who sharply rebuked them and warned them of the consequences of their hard heartedness. The Lord tells us the same today – we who claim to be ardent believers and those who uphold His Word. Anything that emerges out of faith (not concurring to faith) is a sin, and will likely hinder our prospects to receive grace from above. Be it the desires of our body, the lust of the flesh, the lust of our eyes, pride of wealth, or any other hidden sin, this would cost us heavily, our possibilities for growth, our relationships God has given us, and even the intensity to grow in material benefits (wealth). It is not that everyone around does things, that we must also follow suit, but in ensuring that all that we do and say are within the precincts of God’s purview, His Kingdom and its rules. The only sign those who disbelieved then were given was the sign of Jonah, so it is to us, the sign of the death and consequent resurrection in 3 days of Jesus. For all that we would need to be blessed in the material and spiritual realm have been given in Jesus Christ to us. Let us humble ourselves to receive the Lord wholeheartedly. Amen

26/02/26, 06:48 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘So in everything, do to others what you would have them do to you, for this sums up the Law and the Prophets’ [Matthew 7:12]

When Jesus taught the crowds, He spoke to every ‘individual’ heart. His messages were never generalistic or democratic ( to a majority), but meant to be observed by each person in their daily living. His messages were radical, no doubt, and at the very outset, one would find them a hard pill to swallow. They were never about compromise, or ‘nice’ to listen to or to do. But they were the truth and the way ahead. This remains explicitly so when Jesus speaks to us today, reminding us of the Golden rule of a Christian - Do to others what you would have them do to you. Now, the problem in an imperfect world (we live in today) is this – that, having done all this for another, we get discouraged at times because we never get to see the same being reciprocated in our lives. And then we angrily ask the Lord ‘Is this what we get when we do good to another?’ To this,  Jesus gives us only a silent smile because He was perfectly convinced of this rule and its ramifications when applied to by people. During His days of public ministry, He went about doing good, healing the sick, delivering the possessed, consoling those in tears, all not to win these for something in return. He did this solely to explain ‘what it meant to love’ and expected nothing in return from them. He knew the hearts of men, that in men there would be nothing but the element of sin and selfishness. The love of Jesus is and was patient, kind, not envious, not boastful, not proud, it never dishonored any, never delighted in evil, and was always protective. Unless we learn to put the garb of love that Jesus showed us, we will never be able to proclaim our faith. Let us thus begin in our very homes – practice the lesson of love to those given to our care, not expecting to receive from them – hard but with the Lord nothing is impossible. Amen

27/02/26, 07:23 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Beware lest you say in your heart, ‘My power and the might of my hand have gotten me this wealth’ [Deuteronomy 8:17]

Both years of abundance and of scarcity are testing periods in one’s life. The believer is exposed to a risk in both these situations. While it is the assumption of ownership over all things that brings one’s downfall in the years of ‘plentitude’, years of Acute scarcity can provoke a sense of anger and defiance in one’s mind towards God and ingratitude for what we have received from Him. These attitudes are evidently erroneous, and not to fall for these, a temptation that this world puts before us. However, the former has more severe and dire consequences that may delay or even prevent a comeback (repentance) of a person, because of the people around him, the pomp, influence, elegance, and luxury, and an attitude of self-sufficiency that condones the many transgressions one may commit because of the pride that accompanies this person. Pride would essentially bring about his fall in the eventuality. While the latter also distances a person from God, it could inevitably force him to draw back, to take counsel and to turn to God for support. And when he does so, the mercy of God overwhelms him. You and I are called to reach out to God, take counsel from Him, and seek His will, irrespective of our situations at the moment. It is His mercy and solely His mercy that has permitted us into situations of either surplus or deficiency. And why? that we may call to mind and always remember God’s covenantal faithfulness to us, the descendants of Abraham (by faith); that we may be good stewards of His Kingdom; Also that we may be merciful to those around who may be have-nots or less privileged. This is God’s will for us. Amen

‘And the Lord has declared today that you are a people for his treasured possession, as he has promised you, and that you are to keep all His commandments, and He will set you in praise and in fame, and in honor high above all nations’ [Deuteronomy 26:18,19]

It is not to anyone, or to anywhere that we must look - to fix ourselves while on earth; only to God, our Father. Whenever our focus shifts or gets disarrayed to ‘any’ of the Created, we tend to fall and fail. History has given us numerous instances of ‘nations’, ‘people’, who failed to be grounded or bound to their cultural heritage, and of the colossal losses they had to bear through generations who were totally unaware of their true identity, their distinctive strengths, upheavals, those great opportunities their forefathers made use of to step up to where they are today. Likewise, let us be reminded of who we are, how distinctly we compare with others (those who don’t know Jesus), how well we made use of and how much more we still can (use our capabilities with the empowerment of God’s Spirit) to co-create a better world. It is then that God will set you and me in praise, in fame, and honor above any other, because we honored Him, His Word, and our lives were made testimonies for Him. It all starts today, the Lord has declared so, and today we make the change to align to our true selves. God bless you. Amen

01/03/26, 07:52 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘And after six days Jesus took with him Peter and James, and John his brother and led them up a high mountain by themselves’ [Matthew 17:1]

Was Jesus partial when He chose the three disciples? It may seem so, but was not. Jesus had seen the hearts of these three men. From among the rest of the twelve, whom He chose, whom He loved specially, He saw in these three something distinct that persuaded them to be taken separately. Those who are set apart must remember that they were never meant to gel-along with everyone, to engross themselves in everything that others did or even to remain silent when the situation demanded a response. Looking at the characteristic traits of these three disciple – Peter, James, and John – Jesus saw in them a fire, that was to be kindled (Peter was called Cephas, James and John were called – Boanerges); they were eager to be with the Lord at all times, they went in search of the Lord when He was away from them, they accepted correction and discipline from the Lord, they were quick with their questions and responses whenever they were in doubt; and more than all they had a heart that was after the Lord even in the midst of trials. These were some of the traits that endeared them before Jesus. Our Lord is compassionate and merciful. Unlike men, He never kills zeal, never suppresses our instincts and urges to know and love Him. On the contrary He gives us more and newer opportunities to understand, comprehend, and accept His Lordship over our lives. Now the question to you and me – Do we hold a zeal, a special love, and urge in us to know the Lord, atleast during this season of Lent? The Lord invites you and me with a heart of love and promise to kindle the fire in us. Amen

02/03/26, 08:56 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘But we your people, the sheep of your pasture, will give thanks to you forever; from generation to generation, we will recount your praise’ [Psalm 79:13]

Yes Lord! ‘Your people’ will give thanks to you forever. From generations of the past, who have passed on to us faith, we will continue to be grateful for all that we have heard from them, and will make known to generations your great and mighty works that have brought us to where we are today. From the rising of the sun to its setting, the psalmist reminds us that God’s name is to be praised and honoured by men (Psalm 113:3). And God’s mercy shines forth in our lives the more we give glory and praise to Him. Even when one is in despair, praise uplifts him and equips him for a better and renewed battle henceforth. The one who loves Jesus more is the one whose trespasses and sins are forgiven, and to whom special graces will flow - Jesus reminded the Pharisees who condemned the sinful woman who anointed his feet with oil. Not to say that this attitude of gratitude has fully vanished from the hearts of mankind today, but how we express or make known our faithfulness to God becomes a spectacular witness to many around us who may be confused in a cluttered and self-centred world that knows not the Lord. What distinguished yesterday’s ICC Men’s T20 World Cup match from the other matches (India played) was the humble gesture of Sanju Samson in giving glory to God for the victory he obtained for the team at its last breath-taking moment of the final ball. The question for us to meditate deeply today is: ‘If we are comfortable in acknowledging God’s greatness in our daily lives? Or do we just express a personal gesture of thanks in our hearts, fearing disapproval or detest from people around? Your people O Lord will give thanks to you forever. Let the words of the Psalmist inspired by the Holy Spirit resound in our hearts and lips today. Amen

03/03/26, 08:48 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘But you are not to be called Rabbi, for you have one teacher, and you are all brothers. And call no man your father on earth, for you have one father, who is in Heaven. Neither be called instructors, for you have one instructor, the Christ’ [Matthew 23:8-10]

The conviction or mindset of a sincere disciple of Christ was what Jesus was referring to when He spoke to the crowds. He took the examples of three titles – Rabbi, Father, and Instructor to bring more clarity to his audience. A Rabbi was the authority in the Torah (the Law of Moses), and was intellectually the focal point of reference in times of doubt or clarity in the Laws. Jesus was not trying to denounce the need to learn or gain expertise in the Laws (that emerged from the Decalogue), but he condemned the hypocrisy (puffed-up spirit) in the one who claimed to be a know-all in the Laws. Knowledge puffs up, but love builds – St. Paul added this explanation to the mindset of a person who was an authority in Scriptures. It is when love and mercy take priority, thereby strengthening the inner being of a person, he is said to have clarity in the Laws. Each day is an opportunity given to you and me to learn the new dimensions of God’s Law and His love. In this context, Jesus states that none of us is to be called Rabbi, but we would all need to, strive towards knowing the Divine by a willing and humble heart. Of ‘Father,’ would mean a mindset of ownership, or Spiritual authority over another human like him (of course, not related to the biological relationship); To imitate the ways/absolute lifestyle of another in our spiritual journey may never be our way ahead, as we have been created (before the foundations of the world) in Jesus Christ for those specific good deeds meant for us alone. You and I have a defined purpose of living, that which our Father in Heaven has send us for, so all we are to do is to obey Him. An instructor or ‘Master’ could be a role model, one takes in life whom he believes can delineate and clarify the path for him too. And here Jesus says, He is the way, the truth, and the life! And none of us can do anything apart from Him. He is the Christ, the one sent from Heaven to instruct us in our distinct paths in life. So let us yearn to be fervent disciples of Jesus, by adoring his path, the humble, the narrow, and fruitful path, that would lead us to resurrect along with Him. Amen

04/03/26, 08:19 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘But Jesus called them to him and said, You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them. It shall not be so among you’ [Matthew 20:25,26]

Today, Jesus does the same to us. He calls us to Him and asks us to look within, not around, not into the world, but into our hearts. What are those emotions that still hold hegemony within? the urge in us to assert ourselves, the false identity formed based on the things of this world (power, money, influence), the fear that lingers about losing control or dominance, the egos that suppress love, respect, and honour for another……. whatever. And whenever there is this spirit of competing with, of vying with one another to impose upon, men have played their cards so well, yet, there has always been confusion and chaos in societies, and cracks or crevices in relationships that brought a final downfall to many. From the start of human history, it has been this love for dominance, power, and significance that has guided mankind. And against ‘this’ the Lord asks His disciples to be the change, and set the change. If so, we need to bring clarity (in our minds) on certain fundamental questions in life (1) Who are we? (2) Who is God for us? (3) our purpose of living. All these tend to be answered in the fundamental question we have learnt in Catechism -that we were created by God in his image and likeness, to know, love, and serve God, and to be happy with Him forever. We resemble none but God. We owe everything to none but to God, and our vocation (purpose) is to know Him, love Him, and to be happy eternally. If ever we could relate ourselves to this ‘wholistic’ view of the Catechism, we would never attempt to assert ourselves, to be the final word in the crowd, or be the cause of ignominy for another created like us, or even judge another. May God’s Spirit inspire us to be ourselves, by knowing our call, and asking the Lord to strengthen us towards this path. Amen

05/03/26, 06:49 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘There was a rich man who was clothed in purple and fine linen and who feasted sumptuously every day’ [Luke 16:19]

Now let’s take this rich man as ‘You’ and ‘me’ in particular (because God’s Word teaches and encourages individual correction and learning). How elegantly have we been blessed to dress ourselves up? with the best clothes, most trendy, comfortable, and suiting our convenience. How ‘even more’ blessed we have been in enjoying God’s provision in terms of food, the roofs above us (shelter), those people given to us, opportunities for education, growth, ambition……...and let’s stop right here and take a peek into our hearts. To where are our hearts inclined or predisposed towards? Further to ourselves, or filled with gratitude to the Lord, opening a compassionate outlook towards those around us – both the haves and the have-nots? Some among us keep on building upon ambition over ambition, for love of the self. We are never satisfied with the prowess and grandeur the Lord has adorned us. Again, some of us keep vying or envying with another who seemed (to us) to enjoy a better version of material bliss (in one dimension or another in life). Thus, we are never content; this sense of discontent that builds up in our hearts leaves us unattached or inconsiderate to those around us - the less fortunate. And here is where we too fall into the sins of the rich man. Today’s Word from the Lord calls us to remember that we have been adorned with a purple garment – the greatness of Christ, not to be disregarded for trivial reasons of things around us; so are we to put ourselves up before the Lord and before man. Lord Jesus, give us a grateful heart and a content spirit that is motivated to love and serve. Amen

06/03/26, 07:11 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Until what he has said came to pass, the Word of the Lord tested him’ [Psalm 105:19]

Our experiences, the ways of the world, have programmed us to believe that to ‘earn recognition’, ‘you’ and ‘I’ would essentially need to demonstrate our skills, our potential, or validate our worth. The logic being that nothing comes for free, therefore we must invest ourselves to prove to be ‘the best fit’ for a system that thrives on efficiency, speed, and flexibility. Applying the same principle to the domain of spiritual revival, there is this kind of effort required of us (a constant one); but not to assert ourselves as we are, but to yield to the promptings of the Holy Spirit, by way of obedience. It is the Spirit of the Lord that created purpose, order, and charm to a world that was a mere void when created. This Spirit of God constantly works in us to align us to our purpose, orient our priorities, and enhance our charm to the world. Unlike worldly education, the Spirit nurtures in us a sense of obedience to God, to deny ourselves (the desires of the flesh) and fully comply with the instructions God has for us. There is not much we are called to do, or to prove ourselves, or even to justify our motives, in life’s circumstances, but to silently accept, humble ourselves, yield to His promptings, and cooperate for initiation of the change in us. This is what ‘testing’ of the Word of the Lord would mean. Everyone who is called for ‘a purpose’ by the Lord would need to undergo this testing phase of the Word of God in his/her life. There is no exception. Thus, today, if you feel things don’t go your way, all that you have been working on has been just like drawing in the air, if people seem to keep distancing from you for no specific reason of yours, believe – that the Word of God is at work in you, reconstructing, reorienting, and rebuilding you into the true version ‘you’ have been called by your Creator. All you need is to humbly accept your situation, yield to the pain and humiliation, and ask the Lord what He wills to initiate through you. For, until what is said of you comes to occurrence, you would be tested by His Word and remoulded by Him. Such is the love of the Lord for you. Amen

06/03/26, 21:25 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires

And do not keep seeking what you are to eat and what you are to drink, and do not keep worrying. For it is the nations of the world that seek all these things, and your Father knows that you need them. Instead, seek his kingdom, and these things will be given to you as well.
(Luke 12: 20-31)

During the course of our lives, we are often striving our level best to be at par with others, to do the best in our endeavours, to meet up all expectations set by the ‘world’, to establish a firm career, and many more. In today’s verse, God reminds us of a very fundamental fact that ‘He is aware of it’. He is aware of our anxieties, our hard work, our failures, our tensions. He is aware of our aspirations, our ambitions, our dreams and desires. He knows it all better than us. He knows us so much more than what we know about ourselves. He is aware of every single thing we do. 

That’s why today Jeus tells us to ‘seek His Kingdome first and all these things will be given to you’. He asks us to put God in the summit of our lives, rather than our worldly pursuits. 
God is in control of everything.

Wishing you a wonderful weekend.
God bless you

07/03/26, 09:15 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘But the father said to his servants, “Bring quickly! the best robe, and put it on him, and put on a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet. And bring the fattened calf and kill it, and let us eat and celebrate’” [Luke 15:22,23]

 It was deprivation (need) and rejection of men that led this prodigal son to seek his father and to return to his home. The guilt of sinning against his father created such a sense of remorse in him that he was willing to get up from where he was lying, reach out to his father, and tell him that he was sorry for all that he had done. This was more than what his father expected of him. Thus, even before he could make his confession, his father, who understood this realization in him, seeing him from far, received him, embraced and welcomed him. He clothed him with the ‘best garment’, put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet, to remind him of who he was and what it meant to be at home. We sometimes forget that we are a people of a royal lineage, a priestly class, and God’s own people. If so, we are called to live worthy of our state with God (our father), our provisions are bountiful because they come from Him who knows our needs. Whenever weak and unable to put our best foot forward, we have a God who appreciates and encourages even the slightest of gestures of faithfulness from our side. It takes humility to accept that we are nothing without the Lord, and every humble heart, he wraps with His glory and royalty. Let us ask forgiveness from the Lord for not having lived lives worthy of His call and His will. Lord, we seek grace and mercy from you. Amen

08/03/26, 07:32 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘The woman said to him, “Sir, give me this water, so that I will not be thirsty or have to come here to draw water”’[John 4:15]

The Lord Jesus came to the city of Samaria, sat near a well (the well of Jacob), waiting for the coming of a woman whom He knew would be there at this hour of the day (noon). I have always wondered why John never mentioned the name of this woman, who became the first missionary of Jesus to the Samaritans. Possibly, to enlighten our minds of the darkness of a culture that ignored more than 50% of its creativity and potential to grow, or to impress upon us ‘the impartiality’ of God’s Kingdom that, unlike our world (today), does not make distinctions between a man and a woman, or the rich and the poor, the influential or have nots when it speaks of redemption and Salvation. To a world that has grown repetitive, structural, impassionate, and thus finds itself on the brink of an existential crisis, the Lord opens up through the symbolism of the ordinary woman of the well. The woman is depicted as one who asks for a basic, primordial, and existential need for her life – water that would sustain her for life. Jesus invites us to ask for the same, to be practical in life (to dare to be different from the set structures of society, religious practices, and culture) and reach out to the Lord, to receive from Him what matters the most in our individual lives. Although our needs may differ (based on our lifestyles, our bent of mind, age or whatever), we all fundamentally need the empowerment, counsel, and guidance of God’s Spirit. The water that ‘satisfies’ our thirst for more! for love, for wealth, for power, for influence, for acceptance, for recognition……..is this Spirit of God given us through baptism, whom we have at times silenced, whom we have at times disobeyed, or ignored, whom we have never fanned to fire. Let us humbly ask the Lord for forgiveness, for not heeding to His Spirit in us, for having disregarded His promptings and advice, for having lived a life being part of the world as the salt that has lost its saltiness. Lord, give us this water, that we will not thirst to draw from the waters around us, but would make us instruments of charity to those in need around us – the Light and the Salt of this world. Amen! Wishing all women folk a memorable Woman’s day!

Dr. Mini Sherino
08/03/26, 17:36 - Megha Sherino: <Media omitted>
09/03/26, 09:49 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘But in truth, I tell you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the Heavens were shut up three years and six months, and a great famine came over all the land, and Elijah was sent to none of them but only to Zarephath, in the land of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow. And there were many lepers in Israel in the time of the prophet Elisha, and none of them was cleansed, but only Namaan the Syrian’ [Luke 4:25-27]

The pattern (of God’s interference) remained the same, though generations passed by (Elijah followed by his successor Elisha). In both instances, the Gentiles claimed God’s victory, while many of the so-called Chosen people remained unhealed and rejected. The widow of Zarephath in Sidon received her fill and sustained her family during the famine, because she was ready to accept and obey God’s words spoken to her through the prophet. To Elijah the prophet, this was the fulfilment of God’s promise to him that he would be fed by a widow in Zarephath (the land of Jezebel) throughout the three years of the drought. Elijah was being prepared for greater deeds through this experience (of trusting God), even when his life was under constant threat from Ahab the King (who decreed to put to death the prophets of God). Likewise, during the time of his successor, Elisha, it was the commander of the armies of the Syrian King, Namaan, who was healed of his leprosy by the pray and mediation of the prophet. There is this common thread that runs through the two narratives mentioned above. Namaan was healed not because he was a great commander of the armies of the Syrian King, but because he humbled himself to travel all the way to Israel to seek a remedy from God through his prophet. And the widow of Sidon was a woman of faith who believed that only God could help her recover from the pit of helplessness and poverty she had been plunged into. Jesus points to us the basic tenets that a believer’s heart must possess when we express our faith and call upon the Lord for support – (1) the willingness to accept the Lordship of Jesus in our lives and (2) the humility to obey what He tells us to do whenever we pray. There were many staunch religious followers in Israel during the time of Jesus (especially at his hometown), but none of them had the heart and foresight to see the Messiah, to obey Him, and to learn what it meant to love God. Therefore, none in his hometown received any of the miracles or wonder deeds that God desired to do for them; more than all, these in their hardheartedness, attempted to murder the Lord (in their fury) because He pointed to their mistakes and heaviness of heart. Lord, create in me a heart of love and obedience to you. Amen

 Dr. Mini Sherino
10/03/26, 09:43 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Therefore, the kingdom of Heaven may be compared to a king who wished to settle accounts with his servants’ [Matthew 18:23]

Jesus was again trying to explain (to his disciples) a profound truth, one of a higher order. He was exhorting Peter and the rest of his disciples to forgive unconditionally, not because it justified the existing human rules or laws, but because it was quintessential in reinventing the human order that had descended into chaos. God took the initiative to restore humankind to its preter-natural state by sending His Son in the form of a man who settled all claims against mankind’s slavery (to sin and death) into their true citizenship as sons of God’s Kingdom. This King (as mentioned in the Parable) was a prudent and upright statesman, one who desired to re-establish order and welfare in his territory. Before him was brought a servant, who owed a huge amount from the treasury of the Kingdom, but was unable to repay it. Considering his dues (the debt inclusive of years of interest, which could have been invested in other development projects of the country, where Return on investment (ROI) or growth rate (IRR) was far higher), the king mandated that it was to be repaid to the treasury to contain any further losses at any cost. The debtor begged the King to permit him time to continue in the kingdom (without selling him and his family/being deported), stating that he would find a way to pay back in full, and his plea was granted. In other words, this is what we have received from God, our Father, to whom we owe greatly for all the graces, mercy, and blessings in Jesus Christ. Could we ever claim to be able to repay for what we have been bestowed? We can plead, however, that while on this earth, we would be more compassionate, loving, and grateful. All that we have stacked within – our resentments, remorse, unforgiveness, ungratefulness, bitterness, anger,……..we can pack them up to be offered as a sacrifice in every Eucharistic sacrifice to our Lord. He is willing to help us settle accounts and to equip us for greater tasks, as efficient stewards in His kingdom. God sent His Son, not to condemn the world, but to save each one of us. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
11/03/26, 07:22 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Only take care and keep your soul diligently, lest you forget the things that your eyes have seen, and lest they depart from your heart all the days of your life. Make them known to your children and your children’s children’ [Deuteronomy 4:9]

The reason why you are waiting for this message from the Lord and ardently meditating on His Word and commands is because you are the Chosen remnant – a believer in Jesus Christ, who enjoys a place of favour in the heart of God our Father. And to be demarcated, a remnant is no easy feat or a plain title, as we may be prone to understand the term. What distinguishes this remnant from the rest of his compatriots is that he is constantly reminded of the wonder-works of God that built up his life to this day. It is so easy to fall into complacency and take lightly/ or even ignore the blessings of a lifetime. And when we tend to forget all the goodness of the Lord in our lives, we become no better than people who are totally unaware of God. The Israelites were the most fortunate people, as they were the people who held God’s promises, but what transpired to them when complacency gripped their hearts and distanced them from their former intimacy with God? They were made exiles, deported to a foreign land, deprived of all their privileges and fortunes of being the people of God, disappointed to the core, those who lost their legacy – the tradition, culture, glory, and pride of yesteryears. Seventy long years they had to make reparation for their sins and transgressions and to reclaim their lost glory. Thus, as Moses commanded them to heed to God’s laws and precepts, and make them known to their children, so are we commanded to cherish the memories of God’s great works for us, His mercy, and keep telling our generations of them. We are the treasured possession of God, and let nothing of this world deceive us, for all of these were His creation. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

12/03/26, 06:41 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): <Media omitted>
12/03/26, 10:18 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘……Then I will make this house like Shiloh, and I will make this city a curse for all the nations of the earth’ [Jeremiah 26:6]

The ongoing conflicts with the Philistines proved detrimental to the Israelites, and this is often attributed as the principal reason for Shiloh’s destruction. Shiloh was a place of high regard for the Israelites, as it was where ‘the Tabernacle’ with ‘the Ark of God’s Covenant’ was housed, and therefore was the place where all religious, cultural, ritualistic, and political decisions were taken for the people. But as time passed, people began to forget the essence of the Covenant, their relationship with God, their need to obey God’s commandments and laws, to imitate God’s justice and faithfulness in their lives, which would build them wiser, stronger, and capable of prevailing against any onslaught or attacks from their vying neighbours and enemies. There was a significant decline in the spiritual and ethical lives of these Chosen people. They believed that by merely giving honour to the tabernacle and the Ark of the Covenant (outward rituals), they were fulfilling God’s will about them. Even within this sacred place, there arose severe desecrations of the offerings to God by the priests who ministered irresponsibly and carelessly. It was God who protected Israel from their enemies, gave them peace and prosperity, put before them the Laws and Divine practices, they needed to abide by. But, they were disobedient, resisted the words of the prophets (sent by God) and gave up on all possibilities for a comeback. Thus, the edifice of their glory, the renowned ‘City of Shiloh’ was dismantled by a great fire that broke out in the 11th century BC, as historians quote this event. This phenomenon continues to our times – whenever we place our heart and soul into systems, structures, edifices, relationships, cultural grandiose, and prowess, without giving glory to the Lord and honouring human dignity, we bring destruction to them. Let the Lord’s passion, His cause - the Salvation of souls, and His glorious resurrection be foremost in our hearts, minds, and spirits as we journey through the Lenten Season. Lord Jesus, grace us with wisdom to know what pleases and matters to you the most. Amen

13/03/26, 09:22 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘O Ephraim, what have I to do with idols? It is ‘I’ who answer and look after you. I am like an evergreen cypress; from me comes your fruit’ [Hosea 14:8]

The compassionate Lord knew that Ephraim had long forgotten his faithful ways. Therefore, God reaches out to him to awaken him to the truth that he was to repent, and to mend some of his ways that he thought is the way. Ever after the first disobedience in the Garden of Eden by Adam and Eve, ever since they ate the forbidden fruit, their eyes were open to decide what was good for them, and what was evil. All generations have come forth with this congenital defect – they were convinced that their intellect and calibre was all that was needed to decide for themselves the way of life. They sought for themselves their goal, the path they had to tread, their purpose of living, their anchor of security, peace, and joy. This is what God referred to as ‘Idols’. What may be an idol to you? may not be for me, and what I may consider as inevitable (the idol) may not be so for you. Where have we dug our sense of satisfaction, content, and comfort? Is it in some person (dear or near), some substance (money, power, influence, control, strategy), or is it some exceptional quality (talent, intelligence, power of the tongue) you and I possess? Whatever, so long as the Lord is put second to any of these, we would see ourselves losing out: losing ground, confidence, strength, simply because these were never meant to be our protectors. God reminds us today, It is He who answers and looks after us. It is in His greatness and magnanimity that we become capable of bearing fruit in all walks of life. What may not appear so apparent to us (since we may be going through difficult times, indescribable), would be the door towards our innings. Jesus knows what it takes to lead us to our destiny, let us learn to only trust in Him. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino

14/03/26, 10:13 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice, the knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings’ [Hosea 6:4]

What God desires of us is a heart of love, that which is steadfast, that which is not swayed by circumstances, tribulations, people, and the ways of the world. The latter is always subject to change, and in all these volatilities/uncertainties of life, it is the love for the Lord that sustains us and creates in us hope and resilience. St Paul spoke about this when he said that we are more than conquerors in Him who loved us, and who/what can separate us from the love of the Lord? It is this knowledge of ‘what’ God wishes of us that we obtain during this period of Lent and abstinence. About this, God who understands us so well states – Your love is like a morning cloud, like the dew that goes early away. Let us examine ourselves – of those situations, factors that distance us from acknowledging and loving God? It could be our areas of inner wounds, the habits we carry on with that prevent us from reflecting, attachments to things or the created, our pride, overt sense of independence, fear of loss…..All these may seem minor imperfections, but over time, these have dealt a great toll to our convictions, upset our attitudes, turned us more mechanical or less tangible human beings who have lost their ability to love and live. What matters most to God and to us is a comeback to our real selves, to understand where we have failed, missed the mark, and of-course those areas where only we can call the shots. To life and life in abundance we have been called by the Lord, to let our lights shine and transform the darkness around us, more than a lifestyle of religious austerity. Let Jesus shine in us and through us. Amen

16/03/26, 10:36 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘For behold, I create new Heavens and a new earth, and the former things shall not be remembered or come into mind’ [Isaiah 65:17]

To the ‘Chosen’, who lived in a phase of combat, struggling to make sense of God’s promises to them in their abased state of losses. As they were known, they were the remnants, those who had been exiles for years, devoid of political, economic, and even their cultural affinity, but well aware of their former state of grandiose. To them, the term restoration could only seem bizarre and ridiculous, though not impossible for God. Here, the Lord does not merely console them but initiates a healing of their minds. At times, it may seem impossible for us to condone ourselves, due to our past actions, inactions, their consequences and failures that have dealt us severely. But Jesus tells us that the restoration and healing He provides us is above board, it is beyond human imagination or works. He initiates a change in our cosmos, the very things, situations, or attitudes we live with (a new Heaven and a new earth). Therefore, no more shall we ponder on the things of the past – be it the mistakes of others, or our own, it is He who rewrites our destiny. What the Lord promises us today is this – a new Heaven and earth, that we rejoice and abound in life. It is for us to choose – either to be strangled by the past, or to rejoice in the fulfilment of His promises today and to look forward to an even better tomorrow. Let us pray seeking this grace from the Lord to rejoice with Him in the new Heaven and earth He creates for us, let us accept the mistakes of the past as they were (all within the plan of the Lord for our Salvation), and let us optimistically wait in hope for the wonders of this day to be explored with Him and through Him. God counts on your trust and love for Him. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
17/03/26, 08:53 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘God is in the midst of her, she shall not be moved; God will help her when morning dawns’ [Psalm 46:5]

It was a slight spring of water that trickled out from the temple threshold as Ezekiel had seen (in his vision). This was the source that enlivened not just the temple, but all the way down to the Arabah, the sea, and on its way life bloomed – trees bringing forth its fruits and leaves that were fitting to heal. This temple is not any physical structure, but the dwelling presence of God – You and me. Owing to our baptism, we have God’s dwelling within us, in the midst of us, that none can ever touch or destroy our very being. The spring is little so insignificant to be seen or manifested to the human eye, but as we acknowledge its presence, its power in us is unimaginable. It is our faith in the Lord Jesus and of the awareness of what we are in Jesus that begins to flow as a river from within. And as you and I thank the Lord, enjoy His presence, beseech His companionship everywhere we go and in whatever we do, we transform into a river that is overflowing. This living water enlightens our minds with joy, with hope, not that there are no hindrances or challenges in life. As the psalmist explains, the earth (the world around us) may give way (may abandon us), the mountains may be moved (solitude), and the waters may roar and foam (volatility and distress that may circumvent our hearts), even then there is this serenity that the city is blessed with, because He lives within, and controls from within us. The dawn is sure to break forth, and the darkness cannot linger for long, for He works for us all through the night to secure our minds and hearts, for His Holy name’s sake. Glory be to the Holy Trinity who dwells in us. Amen

18/03/26, 10:53 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘I will keep you and give you as a Covenant to the people, to establish the land and apportion the desolate heritages’ [Isaiah 49:8]

By giving us Jesus Christ, His Son, God the Father once and for all established His ultimate Covenant with mankind. God so loved the world that He gave up His only Son, that by Him (Jesus Christ) we would obtain Eternal life. The presence of God with us, His providence, and protection every hour are the boons we experience because of being part of the new and final covenant. The condition of partaking (in the New Covenant) is believing in Jesus Christ, obeying His words, and surrendering ourselves to be led by Him. When one does so, irrespective of what he is/was, he is not merely protected and preserved by the Lord, but also honed/pruned for greater works of the Lord. To the Israelites, their land was God-given, one that differentiated them from others, gave them a sense of cultural identity, prowess, and glory; it was the fulfillment of the promises made to their forefathers, more than all – it was a big headway in the master plan (God’s) of mankind’s Salvation to be executed through these chosen people. To us, this Covenant places us into an unimaginable position/authority where grace is manifested through our words, deeds, and works. What was considered gone, destroyed, and irretrievable (the desolate) becomes existential and possible to us through this Covenantal relationship mediated by Jesus for us. Recollect all those areas of your life, where you believe no one can help, and no amends can be made….today, because Jesus is in you and beside you, even these will transform to the better, because God loved you so much, that He desired to keep you and project you as ‘His Covenant’ for many around who need to be convinced there is hope in the waiting. Glory to the name of Jesus Christ, our Saviour. Amen

19/03/26, 07:19 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘But as he considered these things, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, “Joseph, son of David, do not fear to take Mary’” [Matthew 1:20]

By the righteousness which Joseph upheld within, which was never in alignment with the tenets of the Law, Joseph was blessed and set apart. God addressed Him thenceforth (by means of His Angel), as ‘The Son of David’ – the one through whom the Covenant of David was to be fulfilled. King David dreamt of building a dwelling place for the Lord; God accepted his love and his heart that longed for Him, thus elevating him as the protagonist in the Masterplan (God’s) for human Salvation, the one who really built an everlasting dynasty. The Angel’s address to Joseph was not just a reminder of this plan to be unveiled, but also a call towards empowerment – an exhortation to Joseph to take up his vocation - a task that was ‘exceptional’, till then given to none other – The Protector and provider for Jesus. And Joseph put his heart and soul into it, because he trusted in the One who called Him, ‘the omnipotent one’, who could bring into existence that which was not. Thus, though he was a poor carpenter, with none of the signs of his royal ancestry, he did what he could, and to the best to take care of those entrusted to him - Mary and Jesus, his foster son. In our world, when everyone believes in smart work, in passing the buck, in wrong pretension and exhibition, St Joseph stands out (to us) as an example of love, of faith, and of humility. All these are highly desired and appreciated by the Heavens, and this is what we are reminded today, his feast day. Let us ask him to intercede for our families, that they become cradles of fellowship, love, and faith. May our fathers and spouses be blessed to emulate these qualities (to take charge of their families) that distinguished St Joseph, and walk that extra mile even when times are tough and highly demanding. St Joseph, pray for us. Amen

19/03/26, 11:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): വചനത്തിലൂടെ എന്റെ ജീവിതം

 

‘‘അവൻ ഇതേ കുറിച്ച് ആലോചിച്ചുകൊണ്ടിരിക്കെ, കർത്താവിന്റെ ദൂതൻ സ്വപ്നത്തിൽ പ്രത്യക്ഷപെട്ടു പറഞ്ഞു: “ദാവീദിന്റെ പുത്രനായ ജോസഫ്, മറിയത്തെ ഭാര്യയായി സ്വീകരിക്കാൻ ഭയപ്പെടേണ്ടതില്ല”’ [മത്തായി 1:20]

 

ജോസഫ് ഉള്ളിൽ ഉയർത്തിപ്പിടിച്ച നീതിയാൽ - വെറും നിയമപരമായ ന്യായപ്രമാണ വായനയ്ക്ക് അപ്പുറത്തേക്ക് ഉയർന്നുവന്ന നീതിയാൽ - അവൻ അനുഗ്രഹിക്കപ്പെടുകയും വേർതിരിക്കപ്പെടുകയും ചെയ്തു. ദൈവം തന്റെ ദൂതൻ വഴി അവനെ “ദാവീദിന്റെ പുത്രൻ” എന്ന് അഭിസംബോധന ചെയ്തു: ദാവീദുമായി ഉണ്ടാക്കിയ ഉടമ്പടി നിവൃത്തിയേറേണ്ടിയിരുന്നവൻ.

 

ദാവീദ് ഒരിക്കൽ കർത്താവിനായി ഒരു വാസസ്ഥലം പണിയാൻ ആഗ്രഹിച്ചു. ദൈവം ആ ഹൃദയവാഞ്ഛ സ്വീകരിച്ചു, അതിനെ അതിലും വലിയ ഒന്നാക്കി മാറ്റി: അവൻ ദാവീദിനെ മനുഷ്യരക്ഷയ്ക്കുള്ള തന്റെ മാസ്റ്റർ പ്ലാനിൽ ഒരു നായകനാക്കി, അവനിലൂടെ ഒരു നിത്യ രാജവംശം സ്ഥാപിച്ചു.

 

അങ്ങനെ, യോസേഫിനോടുള്ള ദൂതന്റെ അഭിസംബോധന കേവലം പൂർവ്വിക മഹത്വത്തിന്റെ ഓർമ്മപ്പെടുത്തൽ ആയിരുന്നില്ല; അത് ഒരു ദിവ്യ ശാക്തീകരണമായിരുന്നു - രക്ഷാചരിത്രത്തിൽ അസാധാരണമായ ഒരു വിളിയെ സ്വീകരിക്കാനുള്ള ഒരു ആഹ്വാനമായിരുന്നു: യേശുവിന്റെ സംരക്ഷകനും ദാതാവുമായി മാറുക.

 

ജോസഫ് പൂർണ്ണ വിശ്വാസത്തോടെ പ്രതികരിച്ചു. ഇതുവരെ പ്രത്യക്ഷപ്പെടാത്തതിനെ സൃഷ്ടിക്കുന്നവനിൽ വിശ്വസിച്ചതുകൊണ്ടാണ് അവൻ ആ വിളിയിൽ ഹൃദയവും ആത്മാവും അർപ്പിച്ചത്. രാജകീയ വംശപരമ്പരയുടെ ബാഹ്യ അടയാളങ്ങളൊന്നും വഹിക്കാത്ത ഒരു ദരിദ്രനായ മരപ്പണിക്കാരൻ മാത്രമായിരുന്നിട്ടും, തന്നെ ഏൽപ്പിച്ചവരെ - മറിയയെയും തന്റെ വളർത്തു പുത്രനായ യേശുവിനെയും - പരിപാലിക്കാൻ അവൻ വിശ്വസ്തതയോടെ എല്ലാം ചെയ്തു - അത് നന്നായി ചെയ്തു.

 

പലപ്പോഴും ബുദ്ധിപരമായ കുറുക്കുവഴികൾ, ഉത്തരവാദിത്ത കൈമാറ്റം, നടിക്കൽ, പ്രകടനം എന്നിവ വിലമതിക്കുന്ന ഒരു ലോകത്ത്, സ്നേഹത്തിന്റെയും വിശ്വാസത്തിന്റെയും വിനയത്തിന്റെയും ശാന്തമായ ഉത്തരവാദിത്തത്തിന്റെയും ഒരു തിളക്കമാർന്ന മാതൃകയായി വിശുദ്ധ യോസേഫ് നമ്മുടെ മുന്നിൽ നിൽക്കുന്നു.

 

സ്വർഗത്തിൽ ആഴത്തിൽ വിലമതിക്കുന്ന സദ്‌ഗുണങ്ങളാണിവ, അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ തിരുനാൾ ഇന്ന് നമ്മെ ഓർമ്മിപ്പിക്കുന്നത് ഇതാണ്.

 

നമ്മുടെ കുടുംബങ്ങൾക്കായി നമുക്ക് അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ മധ്യസ്ഥത ചോദിക്കാം: അവർ കൂട്ടായ്മയുടെയും സ്നേഹത്തിന്റെയും വിശ്വാസത്തിന്റെയും കളിത്തൊട്ടിലുകളായി മാറട്ടെ. പ്രത്യേകിച്ച് ഭർത്താക്കന്മാർ, പിതാക്കന്മാർ എന്നിവർ അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ ധൈര്യം, ആർദ്രത, സ്ഥിരത എന്നിവ അനുകരിക്കാൻ അനുഗ്രഹിക്കപ്പെടട്ടെ - തങ്ങളെ ഏൽപ്പിച്ചിരിക്കുന്നവരുടെ ഉത്തരവാദിത്തം ഏറ്റെടുക്കാനും, പ്രതിസന്ധികളിൽ തളരാതെ, തങ്ങളോട് ആവശ്യപ്പെടുമ്പോൾ അധിക ദൂരം നടക്കാനും സാധിക്കട്ടെ.

 

വിശുദ്ധ യൗസേപ്പ്, ഞങ്ങൾക്കുവേണ്ടി പ്രാർത്ഥിക്കുക. ആമേൻ.

 

— ഡോ. മിനി ഷെറിനോ
19/03/26, 11:58 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): ಇಂದಿನ ವಚನ

 

‘ಆದರೆ ಅವನು ಈ ವಿಷಯಗಳನ್ನು ಯೋಚಿಸುತ್ತಿರುವಾಗ, ಇಗೋ, ಕರ್ತನ ದೂತನು ಅವನಿಗೆ ಕನಸಿನಲ್ಲಿ ಕಾಣಿಸಿಕೊಂಡು, “ದಾವೀದನ ಮಗನಾದ ಯೋಸೇಫನೇ, ಮರಿಯಳನ್ನು ಸೇರಿಸಿಕೊಳ್ಳಲು ಭಯಪಡಬೇಡ...” ಎಂದು ಹೇಳಿದನು. (ಮತ್ತಾಯ 1:20 ರ ಸುವಾರ್ತೆ)

 

ಯೋಸೇಫನು ತನ್ನೊಳಗೆ ಎತ್ತಿಹಿಡಿದ ನೀತಿಯಿಂದ - ಕೇವಲ ಕಾನೂನುಬದ್ಧವಾದ ಧರ್ಮಶಾಸ್ತ್ರದ ಓದುವಿಕೆಯನ್ನು ಮೀರಿದ ನೀತಿಯಿಂದ - ಅವನು ಆಶೀರ್ವದಿಸಲ್ಪಟ್ಟನು ಮತ್ತು ಪ್ರತ್ಯೇಕಿಸಲ್ಪಟ್ಟನು. ದೇವರು ತನ್ನ ದೇವದೂತನ ಮೂಲಕ ಅವನನ್ನು “ದಾವೀದನ ಮಗ” ಎಂದು ಸಂಬೋಧಿಸಿದನು: ದಾವೀದನೊಂದಿಗೆ ಮಾಡಿದ ಒಡಂಬಡಿಕೆಯು ನೆರವೇರಬೇಕಾದವನು.

 

ದಾವೀದನು ಒಮ್ಮೆ ಕರ್ತನಿಗಾಗಿ ಒಂದು ವಾಸಸ್ಥಾನವನ್ನು ನಿರ್ಮಿಸಲು ಹಾತೊರೆಯುತ್ತಿದ್ದನು. ದೇವರು ಆ ಹೃದಯದ ಹಂಬಲವನ್ನು ಸ್ವೀಕರಿಸಿದನು ಮತ್ತು ಅದನ್ನು ಇನ್ನೂ ಹೆಚ್ಚಿನದನ್ನಾಗಿ ಪರಿವರ್ತಿಸಿದನು: ಅವನು ದಾವೀದನನ್ನು ಮಾನವ ಮೋಕ್ಷಕ್ಕಾಗಿ ತನ್ನ ಮಾಸ್ಟರ್ ಪ್ಲಾನ್‌ನಲ್ಲಿ ನಾಯಕನನ್ನಾಗಿ ಮಾಡಿದನು, ಅವನ ಮೂಲಕ ಶಾಶ್ವತ ರಾಜವಂಶವನ್ನು ಸ್ಥಾಪಿಸಿದನು.

 

ಹೀಗೆ, ದೇವದೂತನು ಯೋಸೇಫನಿಗೆ ಮಾಡಿದ ಭಾಷಣವು ಕೇವಲ ಪೂರ್ವಜರ ಘನತೆಯ ಜ್ಞಾಪನೆಯಾಗಿರಲಿಲ್ಲ; ಅದು ದೈವಿಕ ಸಬಲೀಕರಣವಾಗಿತ್ತು - ಮೋಕ್ಷ ಇತಿಹಾಸದಲ್ಲಿ ಅಸಾಧಾರಣವಾದ ವೃತ್ತಿಯನ್ನು ಸ್ವೀಕರಿಸಲು ಕರೆ: ಯೇಸುವಿನ ರಕ್ಷಕ ಮತ್ತು ಪೂರೈಕೆದಾರನಾಗಲು.

 

ಜೋಸೇಫನು ಸಂಪೂರ್ಣ ನಂಬಿಕೆಯಿಂದ ಪ್ರತಿಕ್ರಿಯಿಸಿದನು. ಆ ಕರೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಅವನು ಹೃದಯ ಮತ್ತು ಆತ್ಮವನ್ನು ತೊಡಗಿಸಿಕೊಂಡನು ಏಕೆಂದರೆ ಅವನು ಇನ್ನೂ ಕಾಣಿಸದಿರುವದನ್ನು ಅಸ್ತಿತ್ವಕ್ಕೆ ತರುವವನಲ್ಲಿ ನಂಬಿಕೆ ಇಟ್ಟನು. ಕೇವಲ ಬಡ ಬಡಗಿಯಾಗಿದ್ದರೂ, ರಾಜಮನೆತನದ ಯಾವುದೇ ಬಾಹ್ಯ ಚಿಹ್ನೆಗಳನ್ನು ಹೊಂದಿರದಿದ್ದರೂ, ಅವನಿಗೆ ವಹಿಸಿಕೊಟ್ಟವರನ್ನು ನೋಡಿಕೊಳ್ಳಲು ಅವನು ತನ್ನಿಂದ ಸಾಧ್ಯವಾದ ಎಲ್ಲವನ್ನೂ ನಿಷ್ಠೆಯಿಂದ ಮಾಡಿದನು - ಮತ್ತು ಅದನ್ನು ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿ ಮಾಡಿದನು: ಮೇರಿ ಮತ್ತು ಅವನ ದತ್ತುಪುತ್ರನಾದ ಯೇಸು.

 

ಬುದ್ಧಿವಂತ ಶಾರ್ಟ್‌ಕಟ್‌ಗಳು, ಜವಾಬ್ದಾರಿಯ ವರ್ಗಾವಣೆ, ನಟನೆ ಮತ್ತು ಪ್ರದರ್ಶನವನ್ನು ಹೆಚ್ಚಾಗಿ ಬಹುಮಾನಿಸುವ ಜಗತ್ತಿನಲ್ಲಿ, ಸಂತ ಜೋಸೆಫ್ ಪ್ರೀತಿ, ನಂಬಿಕೆ, ನಮ್ರತೆ ಮತ್ತು ಶಾಂತ ಜವಾಬ್ದಾರಿಯ ಪ್ರಕಾಶಮಾನವಾದ ಉದಾಹರಣೆಯಾಗಿ ನಮ್ಮ ಮುಂದೆ ನಿಲ್ಲುತ್ತಾನೆ.

 

ಇವು ಸ್ವರ್ಗದಲ್ಲಿ ಆಳವಾಗಿ ಪಾಲಿಸಲ್ಪಡುವ ಸದ್ಗುಣಗಳಾಗಿವೆ ಮತ್ತು ಅವರ ಹಬ್ಬವು ಇಂದು ನಮಗೆ ನೆನಪಿಸುತ್ತದೆ.

 

ನಮ್ಮ ಕುಟುಂಬಗಳಿಗಾಗಿ ನಾವು ಅವರ ಮಧ್ಯಸ್ಥಿಕೆಯನ್ನು ಕೇಳೋಣ: ಅವರು ಸಹಭಾಗಿತ್ವ, ಪ್ರೀತಿ ಮತ್ತು ನಂಬಿಕೆಯ ತೊಟ್ಟಿಲುಗಳಾಗಲಿ. ವಿಶೇಷವಾಗಿ ತಂದೆ ಮತ್ತು ಸಂಗಾತಿಗಳು ಅವರ ಧೈರ್ಯ, ಮೃದುತ್ವ ಮತ್ತು ದೃಢತೆಯನ್ನು ಅನುಕರಿಸಲು ಆಶೀರ್ವದಿಸಲ್ಪಡಲಿ - ಅವರಿಗೆ ವಹಿಸಿಕೊಟ್ಟವರಿಗೆ ಜವಾಬ್ದಾರಿಯನ್ನು ತೆಗೆದುಕೊಳ್ಳಲು ಮತ್ತು ಸಮಯಗಳು ಕಷ್ಟಕರ ಮತ್ತು ಬೇಡಿಕೆಯಿರುವಾಗಲೂ ಹೆಚ್ಚುವರಿ ಮೈಲಿ ನಡೆಯಲು.

 

ಸಂತ ಜೋಸೆಫ್, ನಮಗಾಗಿ ಪ್ರಾರ್ಥಿಸಿ. ಆಮೆನ್.

 

— ಡಾ. ಮಿನಿ ಶೆರಿನೊ
20/03/26, 08:27 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘They didn’t know the secret purposes of God, nor hoped for the wages of holiness, nor discerned the prize for blameless souls; for God created humans to live forever. He made them as a perfect representation of his own identity’ [Wisdom 2:22,23]

When one distances oneself from His Creator, everything around him will seem to be transient and short-lived – people to be subjects of his dominance, things merely meant to be grabbed disproportionately, money and possessions to be hoarded, might to be the law of right, and (even) life meant only to be revelled / for indulgence in pleasures. Sin blinds us to look beyond, and to foresee things from the perspective of Eternity, which is the reality. God’s Word reminds us that we were created in the image and identity of God, to be like Him on earth and to remain so for eternity. If death is the wage of sin (Romans 6:23), life is the reward for the blameless. It is Jesus Christ who redeemed us from our verdict/judgment of death to that of life. Jesus gifted us eternal life by His sacrifice of self on the Cross, through which all that was written against us as the consequences of our words and deeds (sinful) were washed. We were made a new creation before God, the Father, to inherit in all that was of Jesus – royalty, priestly holiness, and a glorious distinction. This is our true identity, which we at times forget for flimsy reasons, excuses, and ungodly pleasures. Salvation is given to us, but whether we accept or not, whether we desire it or not, determines our destiny. This is the mystery (the fortune bequeathed) that was fulfilled for us by our Heavenly Father though Jesus. Let us therefore remind ourselves that there is no excuse for us anymore, for every word and deed we would be accountable before the Throne of Heaven on that day of Judgement. But when with the Lord and Him in us, we become heirs to all promises of Heaven, just as Jacob was (mistaken and blessed as if he were Esau) in sheep skin. Let us yearn to be like our Father, let us put on Christ Jesus in every attitude, word, and deed of us, to give up our filthy ways and hold firm to what is our true identity. Amen

22/03/26, 07:08 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘And I will put my spirit within you, and you shall live, and I will place you in your own land. Then you shall know that I am the Lord; I have spoken and I will do, declares the Lord’ [Ezekiel 37:14]

The Lord spoke and affirmed His Word by means of a metaphor – being placed in one’s own land. By all means, we could remain strangers or refugees or estranged in the places we live, as what constitutes a human (his being) is not just the physical body, but also his mind, heart, and spirit. There are inevitably those times in everyone’s life, when even when all seems well, our hearts and minds would disagree with the fact that we are content. Not in the sense of greed or covetousness, as some may try to interpret, but in the sense of actualization or self-realization. And our Lord understands this scant in us so well that He assures us that all the lost glory (pride) and satisfaction, we look forward to, He will provide. This was what he revealed to the prophet Ezekiel, through ‘the valley of bones’, that these were merely heaped up remains of the ruins (as it may seem to the world around), but He is not done working in them, and with His Spirit in them, He will create in them something breathtaking and marvelous to all creation. Jesus reminds us this Sunday, that we are not of the flesh, His Spirit dwells in us, and is working towards a grand renewal. What we will be is not yet known to us, but because of His love for us, even though we may be dead in several aspects of life, we will be optimized, elevated to proclaim His glory to all mankind. The Bible attests this by bringing forth several promises made to Jacob/Israel (through the prophets); and the instance of Lazarus (whom the Lord loved so much) being raised from his death was another defining moment through which Jesus tried to let us know what it means to trust in Him. His disciples and many who witnessed this miracle of Lazarus’ restoration believed in Him. So must we, as we prepare ourselves for the Heavenly banquet today. Amen

23/03/26, 09:08 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies; you anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows’ [Psalm 23:5]

It is for this table, the sign of respect and equality, that people, nations, and alliances have been fighting with one another, all through history. When God prepares a table, it is His faithfulness, His security, and assurance of plentitude, in the midst of all adversities and humilities of life. You and I have been bestowed with dignity and value from above, that which we believe is the essence of why God loves us. It is thus that we must love ourselves, uphold Divine virtues of faith, hope, and charity, and grow into the likeness of God through the practice of these values in daily living. In the absence of God, these virtues are repressed and human rights violated. Our beings (ourselves) can turn out to be our foremost enemies, and this occurs when apart from God, human dignity is preserved or honoured. When we dictate what suits us the most without consulting God, we can meet with peril, no doubt. This can wreak havoc in every aspect of life – relationships, money, health, and overall well-being. The other two enemies are (familiar to us) the devil and the world around. God assures us today that if we are with Him, standing firm on our rock (Jesus Christ), He will prepare the banquet of abundance for us – His table of grace that protects, envelopes, hides, secures, and empowers with confidence the mind and heart to proclaim His glory even when things around may seem lacklustre. He anoints us to overcome all obstacles with a calm spirit and a determined bent of mind. Surely His goodness and mercy shall follow us through this life. Amen
