24/03/26, 09:47 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘But you, O Lord, are enthroned forever; You are remembered throughout all generations’ [Psalm 102:12]

It may not be the psalmist King David, who wrote this Psalm (102 – we are not sure), but the context in which this prayer of a soul is placed is understandable -  of distress, sorrow, pain, endurance, and anguish, a soul that wails in the depths of darkness and solitude. And therefore, the prayer is structured thus – O Lord, do not hide your face from me, let your ears be inclined to me to give heed to me when I cry in pain. The reasons why I persist in my supplication before you are many - the state of my body (sick), my heart (weakened by afflictions), the world around that believes in oppressing me, my soul withered down by a sense of despair, the loneliness around (owl of the wilderness and sparrow on the rooftop), lack-lustreness that surmounts my days (like an evening shadow) – verses 5-11. Placed in the midst of all this, the soul pleads for strength and support, knowing that there is no other refuge but the Lord Almighty. Here, the prayer turns (twists) into a hymn of praise to God – remembering the days of old, when things were glorious, that God had been merciful and will be so through the generations. Everything being the work of your hands will have to yield to change, and this change would come from you, enthroned forever, who looks from the heights to reign in your justice. The Lord will rise, He will build Zion, in all His glory will He appear, to answer the prayers of the destitute, and to assure security to the oppressed. Your intervention will not end just there, but will extend to ensure that their offspring are established to stand before your mighty presence, that the name of the Lord may be honoured by all nations. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
25/03/26, 08:45 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Mary said, ‘Behold I am the servant of the Lord; let it be to me according to your word’’ [Luke 1:38]

The Feast of the Anunciation of the Lord is a decisive event in the history of human salvation. Through this defining moment, Jesus came down (from the Heavens) to dwell with us in our confines of time and space. He was the Word that was with God, and through Him all things were brought into existence (created). He chose to enter the human realm not just to redeem mankind, but also to set a precedent (an example) for what it means to be human. Decisions have immense power to either make or break. The Anunciation was a decision God made, but there too He chose to instrumentalize His glory through a human being – Mary. Mary’s decision to obey is our highlight, and will ever be so for generations after, of what it meant to stand firm on one’s feet to glorify God, to prioritise Him above any other. For Mary, it was never an easy ‘cosy’ choice that she had taken. She knew and had foreseen some of the possible consequences of a choice of ‘this kind’. She knew that things would never be the same for her, henceforth. Before the world, there was nothing to prove her case – the Laws were conspicuous to censure her and even put her to a humiliating death. And where could she have or who could have possibly emboldened her with courage and conviction? The Lord Himself, by empowering her with faith, a faith that dared to hope against all hopes. To us who remember this fiat of Mary, hers is a precedent that Heaven asks us to implore and emulate in our daily lives. When you and I take one step to honour God, and to fulfill His will, Heaven rejoices and empowers us with honour, glory, and grace to make superior/supernatural things work through our humble hands. Mary, Mother of Jesus, our Mother of Grace, intercede for us that we may be inspired to live like you. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
26/03/26, 09:13 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Your father Abraham rejoiced that he would see my day. He saw it and was glad…..Truly, truly I say to you, before Abraham was, I am’ [John 8:56-58]

Abraham rejoiced, having received the Covenant from God that He would be the father of a multitude. How one receives God’s Word makes a stark distinction in one’s life. Abraham, who was till then known as ‘Abram’, was no more so: he was renamed (by God) as ‘Abraham’, the Father of many. The trust he reposed in God was what proved decisive for Abraham. This made him to reckon the promise (of a son), not just to end up in Isaac, but one that was to descend through generations, through Kings, and even bring in ‘the Messiah’. And to the unbelieving, hard-hearted Jews who claimed to be of a superior descent (the sons of Abraham), Jesus divulged of them that they were nothing in comparison with the staunch patriarch, Abraham. Abraham humbly believed what he could not see, what was to come forth thousands of years later, and this reckoned for him as righteousness, which the Jews failed to recognize or understand, though Jesus was visible to them, close to them, and spoke to them. Jesus thus makes a final declaration to them that He is the ‘I Am’, who was there even before Abraham. Jesus continues to echo to each one of us this message, that He is the ‘I Am’, the One who wrote the commandments, the One who gave bread from Heaven to the Israelites, the One who finally gave Himself up on the Cross as the one and only one sacrifice to save all mankind. And the One before whom all men shall be brought to give account for. Let us pray for the grace to rejoice through faith in Him, in His Word and promises, that like Abraham we would also receive our true identity, one given to us by Jesus. Amen

27/03/26, 11:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me’ [John 10:27]

The sheep follow ‘after’ the shepherd because of their instincts for survival – for food, water, direction, and security. These animals are timid, well-aware of the possibility of losing track or going astray. It is the voice of the shepherd that they long for, to be led, to know what or where ‘next’, to be assured when in doubt, or sickness, or when injured. No words may be required, but merely the voice of that person (dear or near) on the other side (to be listened to) would all be what we at times needed to be comforted and consoled when in distress. That is why Jesus tells us that He is not just our shepherd, but our ‘Good Shepherd’. He is the Good Samaritan who compassionately took care of the dying Jew, who was abandoned by all. And Jesus continues that He knew His sheep, which would mean that He recognized them (voices, needs, uniqueness, identity, inner fears, and inhibitions). He will lead them to green pastures to refresh them. It is the love, acceptance, and mercy of Jesus that leads us all through our life’s journey to Eternity. When distress comes our way, we must learn to pray like David, that the Lord is my shepherd, my rock, and my stronghold. Because He is with me, leading and guiding my path, even though I walk through paths of darkness, I will not fail. I follow Him because He first loved me and gave Himself up for me. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
28/03/26, 09:43 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation’ [John 11:48]

The history of mankind reveals to us the truth that whenever men tried to protect, nurture, or establish themselves (their interests, position, influence over others, control…...), they fell headlong, succumbing to the very fears that moved them. It was always so, beginning with the fall of man in the Garden of Eden, where Adam and Eve tried to secure for themselves what they considered important, evading God. They lost their Paradise and their state of bliss (they died that very moment). The Tower of Babel was another major milestone, when men attempted to establish a memorial for themselves using baked brick and mortar (symbolic of human civilizational progress). ‘These’ were a generation that survived the great floods, so they were expected to honour God more than any form of self-aggrandisement (exhibiting their expertise and abilities), and we read that they were not successful. The Jewish leaders (Pharisees, Scribes and Sanhedrin who were contemporaries of Jesus) were no different in their thinking. More than the fear of God, their fears were fixed on losing their position, influence, and control of the Temple. Thus, those who were supposed to reveal Jesus to the people ended up conspiring to eradicate Him forever. They weren’t able to save the temple, or save the people, nor were they able to defeat the Romans, and judged themselves unworthy of Eternal life (Acts 14:46). What transpired to these learned men of God, happens to us too, as we make our choices of daily living. Let us ask the Lord to help us discern what matters most to God, what honours Him, what suits our purpose in life, and to surrender to Him those areas that seem blurred to us as of today. Remember, God is the author of our destiny. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
29/03/26, 07:10 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Who is this who comes from Edom, in crimsoned garments from Bozrah, He who is splendid in his apparel, marching in the greatness of his strength?’ [Isaiah 63:1]

In all His majesty, Lord Jesus chose to be one with us. He took the form of mankind, thus penetrating human history to address all its realities – the frailties, weaknesses, hypocrisies, inadequacies, iniquities, and to reign in justice. He was holy (sinless) - God and so could have dealt with us in other ways from the heights of Heaven where He ruled in glory. Yet, His mode of execution was not royal, nor political or strategic, but through the very setbacks that characterised human nature. In the most unexpected way, in unexpected places, and His own time, He set forth to reclaim the human race. He came to His own, but they did not know Him, nor did they receive Him, as the apostle John explains this to us. And today is the day, when the King of Kings and Lord of All, Jesus Christ made His entry into Jerusalem. People around expected this entry to be decisive, as they believed He was on His way to rebel and crush the Roman authorities. But creating a twist, he enters the temple at Jerusalem, as the Lamb chosen for the Passover to be sacrificed for human redemption. 4 days hence, He makes this known to His disciples through the establishing of the Eucharist, through which He knew He could reach out to you and me and generations to come. And all who believed in Him, His works and marvellous ways, were those who washed themselves in the blood of this Passover lamb. Let us join the grand procession to the temple, to learn from Jesus and receive the redemption He prepared for us. Wishing you blessings on this Palm Sunday, May you be (also) worthy to sing praises to the King of Kings for Eternity. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
30/03/26, 10:01 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘So the chief priests made plans to put Lazarus to death as well, because on account of him many of the Jews were going away and believing in Jesus’ [John 12:10,11]

The background for this conspiracy by the authorities of religion was that of Lazarus being raised from his grave after death and burial (by Jesus). This news (of the miracle performed by the Lord) spread like wildfire; Lazarus, who was until then an ordinary Jew, became a kind of celebrity to the people – a witness to Jesus the Son of God. Though Jesus spoke, preached, and taught in the temple, day and night; healed the sick, gave sight to the blind, and cast out demons (works of wonders), His authority was questioned, He was alleged a blasphemer for calling God, His Father, and none of the authorities were convinced of what they saw and heard. It was here that the miracle of Lazarus being brought back to life from death creates a twist among the Jews. Seeing this, many of the Jews, the Pharisees and Scribes, were convinced of Jesus, that He was the Messiah, the one who would deliver them. Now contrary to what we may suppose would be the turnaround, the Chief Priests (who were the higher authorities) fail to acknowledge the Divinity of Jesus. What bothered them was that many of the Jews were moving away from the so-called systems and established structures of power, knowledge, and influence. As Jesus had prophesied to the Samaritan woman, that true worshippers would worship God in truth and Spirit, the people began to realize the true meaning of honouring God. They slowly began to accept the precepts, the Beatitudes, the teachings of Jesus, as the way to honour God. To the people at the helm of affairs, this was debilitating, to find themselves (one fine morning) being ignored, given less heed to, or  the least being kept at a distance. As Jesus earlier had stated, they longed for human respect more than honour from God. Thus, rather than acknowledging Jesus as the Son of God, they make plans to destroy Him, not just Him, but also Lazarus whom God raised from death (a living presence of Jesus). Whenever we too as humans, believe in outwitting others, or winning over others by our humanly thinking, we behave the same way as these authorities in Jerusalem did. They became crucifiers of their Lord of Salvation and endured the wrath of God. Apart from me, You can do nothing, Jesus Christ reminds us today. So let us seek after Him for the way out in our struggles and difficulties each day. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
31/03/26, 11:12 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘For I have given you an example that you also should do just as I have done to you’ [John 13:15]

We were those who were weak, hypocritical, self-glorious, and unworthy, but despite all this, Jesus never disowned us. He continued reminding us that we were most precious, loved, and admired by Him, to the end, to the extent of giving Himself up on the Cross. Our sins, transgressions, humiliations, curses, pain, defiance, and all that we consider loathsome, He took on Himself, nailing them to destruction on His Cross, and all that were the consequences of these He rewrote as blessings for generations to come. But even before this great sacrifice of love, He loved His disciples dearly, and to let them understand this, He took the illustration of washing their feet (dusty and dirty), asking them to do the same henceforth. To wrap one devoid of glory, of dignity, of respect, with the garment of love and compassion, thus helping them regain their lost glory. This, the Lord exhorted His disciples to practice and to do as they witnessed to Him in the world. This Holy command has been given to all, not just to priests, religious people, and leaders, but to each one of us in our relevant spheres of living. When I find it difficult to imitate the Lord (by demonstrating mercy), it is not the excuses (easy to point to) that I would need to search for, but to sit before the Lord in humble submission, seeking from Him the strength and power to be like Him. He will surely groom us in the way most appropriate for a change. For what the Lord expects of us is not sacrifice and mortifications, but a heart that beats for Him and to do His command. Give me a heart, O Lord, we pray, one that longs for, believes in, and trusts in your way, your truth, and your life, above all. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
01/04/26, 10:54 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘Judas, who betrayed him, said, ‘Surely not I, Rabbi?’ He replied, ‘You have said so’’ [Matthew 26:25]

This ‘one more chance’ God gives to all of us. Even to the disciple whom He knew would betray Him soon, Judas, He opened up an avenue for a last chance. While Jesus was dining the ‘Passover meal’ with His disciples, He revealed to them that one of them would betray Him. The disciples were greatly distressed, having heard this from Jesus, that they began to ask Him who it would be. Each of these disciples (one after the other) kept asking Him, Lord surely not I. And this was from the heart, a sense of sorrow - of deceiving the one who loved them the most. It was their Lord who chose them, took them with Him everywhere He went, taught them some of the mysteries of the Kingdom, which was never told to the crowds. To them, Jesus was everything, so even in their faint conscience, how could they ever imagine to attempt to deceive him? Just then, Judas also followed suit, asking, Rabbi, surely not I. His asking was however, not with the intent of love or sincerity to the Lord. He was merely imitating the others, knowing well what he had to do, and when to do, and of the money he would receive for doing so. To him, Jesus was merely a teacher of the Laws, a Rabbi. Thus, even when Jesus replied in the affirmative (pushing hard to correct him), it was hardly of any concern to him, because what controlled his thinking and logic was merely the money and the magic of influence with money. Whenever we stand by our thinking and logic, leaving no space for the Lord in our lives, we too become like Judas; we reduce Jesus to merely our teacher (good to listen to), and not our Master and Lord, whose power extends beyond any other. Let us learn this Wednesday what it means to accept Jesus as ‘Lord’ and ‘Master’ of our lives, with the help of His Spirit in us. Lord, even though I may seem correct, create in me a simple heart to yield to you always. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
01/04/26, 12:06 - Megha Sherino: Boarded nice amma
01/04/26, 19:31 - Megha Sherino: <Media omitted>
02/04/26, 08:21 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another: just as I loved you, you also are to love one another. By this, all people will know that you are my disciples if you have love for one another’ [John 13:34,35]

The Commandment Jesus gave to His disciples was not one that was not until then given or written in the Laws, but it was one with a higher intensity and gravity. It is easy to love when things are all well, when people around love us, respect, and accept us, but not so when nothing around seems bright. When Jesus put forth a higher standard of love, it was not merely by word He did this, but by being an example, an evident example to them. It was just after Judas left the room (after the Passover meal) into the darkness, to execute his plan, that Jesus gave this new commandment. Not that he (Judas) wasn’t given a chance to come back, but he was not ready to rethink. The New Commandment was a call to all disciples to desire to emulate Jesus. Jesus was practical and told them that in their present situation, they would never be able to make it, but once they would be successful. Simon Peter went a step ahead to argue that he would be by the Lord even if it meant to the extent of being imprisoned, but Jesus who knew him, explained that he wouldn’t be able to immediately, but would be prepared for it. What Jesus taught the disciples then, was meant for each one of us. It was only when we understood His sacrifice of love, you and I would be able to express the same in some form or another. Jesus never asked for perfection in one go, but demanded that we obey Him and desire to fulfill His will for us. Whatever is impossible in our lives, let us ask the Lord to intervene, to overshadow our shallow hearts with His love, so that we would also be able to express love in its true quality, colour and density. It is by this, that all people would know that we are His disciples – by our humble surrender and silence when wounded. Let us ask the Lord for this grace. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
02/04/26, 17:33 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires

If you then, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask him! (Matthew 7:11)

In this verse, Jesus uses the methodology of human imperfect love to compare the greater, incomprehensible, and unconditional love of God. Jesus highlights the fact that if human love that is imperfect and affect by sin can still produce goodness, then how much more can God’s perfect love produce? 
Human love is not independent, it comes from God, what I mean to say is that we can love only because He taught us to. If our imperfect love blemished with sin and selfishness produces good things it is only because our love is a reflection of God’s love. Then how much more the perfect love of God can transform our lives. Another reflection I would like to share is that, it is God who sent his only begotten son into the world to die for our sins, so that we may be elevated to the position of His children. It is this incomprehensible love of God that gave us the right to call him ‘Father’, here Jesus emphasises that by merely believing in Him, we receive
02/04/26, 17:46 - Megha Sherino: This grace and priviledge of asking our Father good things.
02/04/26, 17:51 - Megha Sherino: God Inspires

If you then, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask him! (Matthew 7:11)

In this verse, Jesus uses the methodology of human imperfect love to compare the greater, incomprehensible, and unconditional love of God. Jesus highlights the fact that if human love that is imperfect and affected by sin can still produce goodness, then how much more can God’s perfect love produce? 

Human love is not independent, it comes from God, what I mean to say is that we can love only because He taught us to. If our imperfect love blemished with sin and selfishness produces good things it is only because our love is a reflection of God’s love. Then how much more the perfect love of God can transform our lives.

Another reflection I would like to share is that, it is God who sent his only begotten son into the world to die for our sins, so that we may be elevated to the position of His children. It is this incomprehensible love of God that gave us the right to call him ‘Father’, here Jesus emphasises that by merely believing in Him (accepting Jesus as our Lord and savior and living our life in accordance to His words), we receive this grace and privilege of asking our Father good things.

Let us contemplate on His love this good Friday.

God bless you
Megha Sherino <This message was edited>
03/04/26, 07:20 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘So shall He startle many nations. Kings shall shut their mouths because of him, for that which has not been told them they see, and that which they have not heard they understand’ [Isaiah 52:15]

For what has not been told them or heard of, is what they understand. Isaiah was totally taken aback, having seen the vision of the Messiah who was to come. There was nothing of power, of superiority, of dominion, of violence that was expressed in what He (The Servant/The Messiah) said and demonstrated. In His silence, pain, suffering, and surrender, He startled the nations; Kings found themselves unable to argue with Him; His was a strategy, a way, that was unknown to the world till then and even to this day. All the authorities had to stand barefaced before Him, all their wisdom was incomparable to the King of Kings, Jesus of Nazareth. He is the source of all knowledge, wisdom, prophesy, and revelation. Thus, before His Words, His sacrifice on the Cross (for the whole mankind), nature, the Universe, and even the Heavens had to relent. No longer could the sun shine, nor was there light in mid-day (the time in history when Jesus died), the Centurian who witnessed all these things exclaimed with his heart and soul, that ‘Surely this is the Son of God, the righteous one’. Today, as we remember the sacrifice of our redemption, we learn that there is great meaning in silence, in suffering, in solitude, in disgrace, in humiliation, because when He took all these and nailed them to the Cross, nothing else could stand before Him as equivalent in the Universe. Lord Jesus, as we remember all that you underwent to take us to where we are now, and further our travail to Eternity. We love you, thank you, honor your name, and proclaim to the world, that In you and in nowhere else is Salvation for all men. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
04/04/26, 06:51 - Mini Sherino ( Mother): Verse for today

‘For the death He died he died to sin, once for all, but the life He lives he lives to God. So, you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus’ [Romans 6:10,11]

Sin had once and for all times lost dominion in the lives of mankind because of the sacrifice of Jesus Christ. Not that men would not sin anymore, but that those who believed in Jesus had been empowered to destroy sin while still living in the world. Jesus prayed for His disciples (in His Priestly prayer) thus: ‘For those whom you gave me from the world; no more are they of the world; I do not ask that you take them out of the world (because the world hated them) but that you keep them from the evil one. This was the prayer Jesus made on behalf of us all. Specific to Simon Peter, who kept expressing his loyalty to the Lord (that he would accompany Jesus even if it were to the extent of being imprisoned), Jesus prayed that his faith might not fail and that he (Simon) would strengthen his brothers when Jesus was taken away from them. To you and me, what Jesus has to say is just the same – that we are no longer of this world, slaves to sin, that we follow the inclinations of the flesh – responding in anger, in criticism, in negativity, in divisiveness, in discord, in fear, in anxiety……the list is even longer. But we are now to assume our true identity as a Christ follower, which is putting on Christ above and over everything. We are alive in Christ and with Christ to live in a higher realm, with a great sense of freedom (to do good), making every use of each opportunity in the flesh to be like Jesus Christ. Lord Jesus, enlighten our hearts and minds with your Spirit to embrace our true identity of resembling Christ, the beloved of His Father. Amen

Dr. Mini Sherino
